charRendered[\"CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED\"]\n    end\n    \n    loadSettings --\u003e loadManifests\n    loadManifests --\u003e moduleLoader\n    moduleLoader --\u003e initFunc\n    initFunc --\u003e getCtx\n    getCtx --\u003e ContextAPI\n    \n    eventHooks --\u003e Hooks\n    \n    style ContextAPI fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\n### Extension Context API\n\nThe `getContext()` function at [public/scripts/extensions.js:1-100]() returns a context object exposing:\n\n| API Category | Key Functions | Purpose |\n|--------------|---------------|---------|\n| **State Access** | `characters`, `chat`, `groups`, `settings` | Read application state |\n| **Events** | `eventSource.on()`, `eventSource.emit()` | React to lifecycle events |\n| **Commands** | `SlashCommandParser.addCommandObject()` | Register slash commands |\n| **Prompts** | `setExtensionPrompt(name, text, position, depth)` | Inject into prompt builder |\n| **UI** | `registerSlashCommandsAutoComplete()` | Add UI controls |\n| **Utilities** | `saveSettingsDebounced()`, `reloadCurrentChat()` | Trigger core actions |\n\n### Built-In Extension Examples\n\n**Stable Diffusion Extension** ([public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/]())\n- Registers `/sd` slash command for manual image generation\n- Listens to `MESSAGE_RECEIVED` event for auto-generation triggers\n- Supports 20+ image generation backends (AUTOMATIC1111, NovelAI, ComfyUI, etc.)\n\n**Expressions Extension** ([public/scripts/extensions/expressions/]())\n- Registers `/sprite` command to show/hide character sprites\n- Listens to `CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED` to classify emotion via BERT model\n- Injects sprite `\u003cimg\u003e` elements into message DOM\n\n**Quick Reply Extension** ([public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/]())\n- Provides `QuickReplyApi` class for programmatic access\n- Implements `SlashCommandClosure` execution engine\n- Supports auto-execution on events (chat load, message received, etc.)\n\n**Settings Persistence**: Extensions write to `extension_settings[extensionName]` object, which is serialized to the user's `settings.json` via `saveSettingsDebounced()` at [public/script.js:441]().\n\n**Sources**: [public/scripts/extensions.js:1-200](), [public/scripts/st-context.js](), Diagram 4 from provided overview\n\n---\n\n## Technology Stack Summary\n\n| Layer | Technologies |\n|-------|-------------|\n| **Runtime** | Node.js (server), Browser (client) |\n| **Server Framework** | Express.js |\n| **Client Framework** | Vanilla JavaScript (ES6 modules), jQuery |\n| **Templating** | Handlebars.js |\n| **Markdown** | Showdown.js |\n| **Data Format** | JSON, JSONL (JSON Lines), PNG (character cards) |\n| **Storage** | File system (no database) |\n| **Security** | CSRF tokens, HTTP Basic Auth, IP whitelisting |\n| **Image Processing** | Jimp (Node.js), Canvas API (Browser) |\n| **HTTP Client** | node-fetch (server), Fetch API (client) |\n\n**No Database**: SillyTavern deliberately uses the file system instead of a database for maximum portability and ease of backup. All data is stored in human-readable formats (JSON, JSONL).\n\n**ES6 Modules**: Both client and server use modern JavaScript modules with `import`/`export` syntax. The client loads `script.js` as a module via `\u003cscript type=\"module\"\u003e` in [public/index.html]().\n\n**Sources**: [package.json](), [public/index.html:1-50](), [public/lib.js]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"18:T6a55,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Installation and Setup\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [.eslintrc.cjs](.eslintrc.cjs)\n- [default/config.yaml](default/config.yaml)\n- [default/public/error/unauthorized.html](default/public/error/unauthorized.html)\n- [package-lock.json](package-lock.json)\n- [package.json](package.json)\n- [post-install.js](post-install.js)\n- [public/global.d.ts](public/global.d.ts)\n- [public/jsconfig.json](public/jsconfig.json)\n- [public/lib.js](public/lib.js)\n- [public/scripts/bookmarks.js](public/scripts/bookmarks.js)\n- [server.js](server.js)\n- [src/endpoints/characters.js](src/endpoints/characters.js)\n- [src/endpoints/chats.js](src/endpoints/chats.js)\n- [src/endpoints/content-manager.js](src/endpoints/content-manager.js)\n- [src/endpoints/groups.js](src/endpoints/groups.js)\n- [src/endpoints/presets.js](src/endpoints/presets.js)\n- [src/endpoints/settings.js](src/endpoints/settings.js)\n- [src/middleware/basicAuth.js](src/middleware/basicAuth.js)\n- [src/middleware/whitelist.js](src/middleware/whitelist.js)\n- [src/users.js](src/users.js)\n- [src/util.js](src/util.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document covers the installation, initial configuration, and startup procedures for SillyTavern. It explains how to install dependencies, configure the server for first use, understand runtime options, and start the application. For information about the third-party libraries used by SillyTavern, see [System Dependencies and Libraries](#1.2). For detailed information about security configuration and authentication methods, see [Authentication and Security](#2.4).\n\n---\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nSillyTavern requires a JavaScript runtime environment to operate. The following runtimes are supported:\n\n| Runtime | Minimum Version | Recommended |\n|---------|----------------|-------------|\n| Node.js | 18.x or higher | ✓ Primary runtime |\n| Deno    | Latest stable  | Alternative runtime |\n| Bun     | Latest stable  | Alternative runtime |\n\nThe Node.js version requirement is specified in [package.json:96-98]():\n\n```json\n\"engines\": {\n    \"node\": \"\u003e= 18\"\n}\n```\n\n**Sources**: [package.json:96-98](), [server.js:5]()\n\n---\n\n## Installation Process\n\n### Diagram: Installation and Startup Flow\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Clone[\"Clone Repository\"]\n    NPMInstall[\"npm install\"]\n    PostInstall[\"post-install.js\"]\n    ConvertConfig[\"convertConfig()\"]\n    CreateDefaults[\"createDefaultFiles()\"]\n    AddMissing[\"addMissingConfigValues()\"]\n    ConfigYAML[\"config.yaml created\"]\n    PublicDir[\"public/ directory synchronized\"]\n    ServerStart[\"node server.js\"]\n    CLIParse[\"CommandLineParser.parse()\"]\n    SetDataRoot[\"Set globalThis.DATA_ROOT\"]\n    ImportMain[\"import server-main.js\"]\n    ServerRunning[\"Server Running\"]\n    \n    Clone --\u003e NPMInstall\n    NPMInstall --\u003e PostInstall\n    PostInstall --\u003e ConvertConfig\n    ConvertConfig --\u003e CreateDefaults\n    CreateDefaults --\u003e AddMissing\n    AddMissing --\u003e ConfigYAML\n    AddMissing --\u003e PublicDir\n    ConfigYAML --\u003e ServerStart\n    PublicDir --\u003e ServerStart\n    ServerStart --\u003e CLIParse\n    CLIParse --\u003e SetDataRoot\n    SetDataRoot --\u003e ImportMain\n    ImportMain --\u003e ServerRunning\n```\n\n### Installation Steps\n\n1. **Clone the Repository**\n   ```bash\n   git clone https://github.com/SillyTavern/SillyTavern.git\n   cd SillyTavern\n   ```\n\n2. **Install Dependencies**\n   ```bash\n   npm install\n   ```\n\nThe `npm install` command triggers a `postinstall` script defined in [package.json:123](). This script executes [post-install.js:1-115](), which performs three critical setup tasks:\n\n| Task | Function | Purpose |\n|------|----------|---------|\n| Config Migration | `convertConfig()` | Converts legacy `config.conf` to `config.yaml` format |\n| Default Files | `createDefaultFiles()` | Creates `config.yaml` and synchronizes `public/` directory |\n| Config Validation | `addMissingConfigValues()` | Adds any missing configuration keys with default values |\n\n**Sources**: [package.json:123](), [post-install.js:1-115](), [post-install.js:20-41](), [post-install.js:46-103]()\n\n---\n\n## Configuration System\n\n### Diagram: Configuration Resolution Hierarchy\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    Request[\"Configuration Value Request\u003cbr/\u003egetConfigValue(key, default)\"]\n    EnvCheck[\"Check Environment Variable\u003cbr/\u003eSILLYTAVERN_*\"]\n    EnvFound{\"Environment\u003cbr/\u003eVariable Exists?\"}\n    ConfigFile[\"Read from config.yaml\u003cbr/\u003egetConfig()\"]\n    CachedCheck{\"CACHED_CONFIG\u003cbr/\u003eexists?\"}\n    ReadYAML[\"Parse config.yaml\u003cbr/\u003eyaml.parse()\"]\n    Cache[\"Store in CACHED_CONFIG\"]\n    LodashGet[\"_.get(config, key)\"]\n    TypeConvert[\"Type Conversion\u003cbr/\u003enumber/boolean\"]\n    Return[\"Return Value\"]\n    \n    Request --\u003e EnvCheck\n    EnvCheck --\u003e EnvFound\n    EnvFound --\u003e|Yes| TypeConvert\n    EnvFound --\u003e|No| ConfigFile\n    ConfigFile --\u003e CachedCheck\n    CachedCheck --\u003e|Yes| LodashGet\n    CachedCheck --\u003e|No| ReadYAML\n    ReadYAML --\u003e Cache\n    Cache --\u003e LodashGet\n    LodashGet --\u003e TypeConvert\n    TypeConvert --\u003e Return\n```\n\n### Configuration File Structure\n\nThe primary configuration file is `config.yaml`, located in the root directory of the SillyTavern installation. The default configuration template is provided in [default/config.yaml:1-327]().\n\n**Key Configuration Sections**:\n\n| Section | Purpose | Example Settings |\n|---------|---------|------------------|\n| `dataRoot` | Location of user data storage | `./data` |\n| `listen` | Enable network listening | `false` (local only) or `true` |\n| `port` | Server port number | `8000` |\n| `ssl` | HTTPS configuration | Certificate paths, key passphrase |\n| `whitelistMode` | IP address restrictions | `true` (enabled) or `false` |\n| `whitelist` | Allowed IP addresses | `['127.0.0.1', '::1']` |\n| `basicAuthMode` | HTTP Basic Authentication | `false` (disabled) |\n| `enableUserAccounts` | Multi-user support | `false` (single user) |\n| `performance` | Performance tuning | Cache settings, lazy loading |\n| `extensions` | Extension system settings | Auto-update, model downloads |\n\n### Configuration Access API\n\nConfiguration values are accessed through the utility functions in [src/util.js:27-119]():\n\n**Core Functions**:\n\n| Function | Signature | Purpose |\n|----------|-----------|---------|\n| `getConfig()` | `() =\u003e object` | Returns entire parsed config with caching in `CACHED_CONFIG` |\n| `getConfigValue(key, defaultValue, typeConverter)` | `(string, any, string?) =\u003e any` | Retrieves specific value with fallback and type conversion |\n| `setConfigFilePath(path)` | `(string) =\u003e void` | Sets `CONFIG_PATH` variable (must be called before first `getConfig()`) |\n| `keyToEnv(key)` | `(string) =\u003e string` | Converts dot-notation key to environment variable name |\n\n**Type Converters** (third argument to `getConfigValue`):\n- `'number'`: Parses value as float via `parseFloat()`\n- `'boolean'`: Converts to boolean via `toBoolean()` helper\n- `null`: Returns raw value (default)\n\n**Example Usage**:\n```javascript\nconst port = getConfigValue('port', 8000, 'number');\nconst enableExtensions = getConfigValue('extensions.enabled', true, 'boolean');\nconst whitelist = getConfigValue('whitelist', []);\n```\n\nThe configuration system supports environment variable overrides using the naming convention `SILLYTAVERN_*`. The `keyToEnv()` function performs this conversion: `extensions.models.speechToText` → `SILLYTAVERN_EXTENSIONS_MODELS_SPEECHTOTEXT`.\n\n**Sources**: [default/config.yaml:1-327](), [src/util.js:27-119](), [src/util.js:38](), [src/util.js:44-49](), [src/util.js:55-79](), [src/util.js:88-109]()\n\n---\n\n## Runtime Environments\n\nSillyTavern supports three JavaScript runtime environments, each with a dedicated npm script:\n\n### Diagram: Runtime Environment Options\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    subgraph \"Entry Points\"\n        ServerJS[\"server.js\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Runtime Options\"\n        NodeJS[\"Node.js\u003cbr/\u003enpm start\"]\n        Deno[\"Deno\u003cbr/\u003enpm run start:deno\"]\n        Bun[\"Bun\u003cbr/\u003enpm run start:bun\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Execution Flow\"\n        CLIParser[\"CommandLineParser\"]\n        DataRoot[\"Set DATA_ROOT\"]\n        ServerMain[\"server-main.js\"]\n    end\n    \n    NodeJS --\u003e ServerJS\n    Deno --\u003e ServerJS\n    Bun --\u003e ServerJS\n    ServerJS --\u003e CLIParser\n    CLIParser --\u003e DataRoot\n    DataRoot --\u003e ServerMain\n```\n\n### Runtime Commands\n\n| Runtime | Command | Script Definition |\n|---------|---------|-------------------|\n| Node.js (default) | `npm start` | [package.json:116]() |\n| Node.js (debug) | `npm run debug` | [package.json:117]() |\n| Node.js (global) | `npm run start:global` | [package.json:118]() |\n| Deno | `npm run start:deno` | [package.json:120]() |\n| Bun | `npm run start:bun` | [package.json:121]() |\n\nThe Deno command includes the following permission flags:\n- `--allow-run`: Execute subprocesses\n- `--allow-net`: Network access\n- `--allow-read`: File system read access\n- `--allow-write`: File system write access\n- `--allow-sys`: System information access\n- `--allow-env`: Environment variable access\n\n**Sources**: [package.json:115-127](), [server.js:1-18]()\n\n---\n\n## Command-Line Arguments\n\n### Argument Processing\n\nCommand-line arguments are parsed by the `CommandLineParser` class (imported from [server.js:2]()). The parsed arguments are stored in `globalThis.COMMAND_LINE_ARGS` and used throughout the application.\n\n**Key Arguments**:\n\n| Argument | Type | Purpose | Config Override |\n|----------|------|---------|-----------------|\n| `--dataRoot` | Path | User data storage location | `dataRoot` |\n| `--port` | Number | Server port | `port` |\n| `--listen` | Boolean | Enable network listening | `listen` |\n| `--global` | Boolean | Shorthand for `--listen true` | `listen` |\n| `--disableCsrf` | Boolean | Disable CSRF protection | `disableCsrfProtection` |\n\nThe `--global` flag is commonly used to make the server accessible on the local network:\n\n```bash\nnpm run start:global\n# Equivalent to: node server.js --listen true\n```\n\n**Sources**: [server.js:8-10](), [package.json:118](), [package.json:122]()\n\n---\n\n## Data Directory Structure\n\n### Diagram: Directory Organization\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TB\n    subgraph \"Root Directory\"\n        DataRoot[\"DATA_ROOT\u003cbr/\u003e(default: ./data)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"User-Specific Directories\"\n        Handle[\"handle/\u003cbr/\u003e(user directory)\"]\n        Chars[\"characters/\"]\n        Chats[\"chats/\"]\n        Groups[\"groups/\"]\n        GroupChats[\"group chats/\"]\n        Worlds[\"worlds/\"]\n        Avatars[\"User Avatars/\"]\n        Backgrounds[\"backgrounds/\"]\n        Assets[\"assets/\"]\n        Backups[\"backups/\"]\n        Vectors[\"vectors/\"]\n        Files[\"files/\"]\n        Thumbnails[\"thumbnails/\"]\n        Settings[\"settings.json\"]\n        Secrets[\"secrets.json\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Preset Directories\"\n        KoboldPresets[\"KoboldAI Settings/\"]\n        NovelPresets[\"NovelAI Settings/\"]\n        OpenAIPresets[\"OpenAI Settings/\"]\n        TextGenPresets[\"TextGen Settings/\"]\n        Instruct[\"instruct/\"]\n        Context[\"context/\"]\n        QuickReplies[\"QuickReplies/\"]\n        Themes[\"themes/\"]\n        Sysprompt[\"sysprompt/\"]\n        Reasoning[\"reasoning/\"]\n    end\n    \n    DataRoot --\u003e Handle\n    Handle --\u003e Chars\n    Handle --\u003e Chats\n    Handle --\u003e Groups\n    Handle --\u003e GroupChats\n    Handle --\u003e Worlds\n    Handle --\u003e Avatars\n    Handle --\u003e Backgrounds\n    Handle --\u003e Assets\n    Handle --\u003e Backups\n    Handle --\u003e Vectors\n    Handle --\u003e Files\n    Handle --\u003e Thumbnails\n    Handle --\u003e Settings\n    Handle --\u003e Secrets\n    Handle --\u003e KoboldPresets\n    Handle --\u003e NovelPresets\n    Handle --\u003e OpenAIPresets\n    Handle --\u003e TextGenPresets\n    Handle --\u003e Instruct\n    Handle --\u003e Context\n    Handle --\u003e QuickReplies\n    Handle --\u003e Themes\n    Handle --\u003e Sysprompt\n    Handle --\u003e Reasoning\n```\n\n### Directory Purposes\n\nThe data directory structure is defined by the `USER_DIRECTORY_TEMPLATE` constant in [src/constants.js]() and managed by the `getUserDirectories(handle)` function in [src/users.js:1-896](). Each user's directory tree is returned as a `UserDirectoryList` object with typed paths.\n\n**Core Data Directories** (from `UserDirectoryList` type):\n\n| Directory Property | Physical Path | Purpose | File Format |\n|-------------------|---------------|---------|-------------|\n| `directories.characters` | `characters/` | Character cards processed by `processCharacter()` | PNG with embedded JSON (V2 spec) |\n| `directories.chats` | `chats/` | Chat logs saved via `saveChatConditional()` | JSONL (one JSON object per line) |\n| `directories.groups` | `groups/` | Group definitions | JSON |\n| `directories.groupChats` | `group chats/` | Group chat logs | JSONL |\n| `directories.worlds` | `worlds/` | World Info / Lorebooks read by `readWorldInfoFile()` | JSON |\n| `directories.avatars` | `User Avatars/` | User persona avatars | Image files |\n| `directories.backgrounds` | `backgrounds/` | Chat backgrounds | Image files |\n| `directories.backups` | `backups/` | Automatic backups via `backupChat()` | JSONL, JSON |\n\n**Settings and Configuration Directories**:\n\n| Directory Property | Physical Path | Purpose | File Format |\n|-------------------|---------------|---------|-------------|\n| `directories.koboldAI_Settings` | `KoboldAI Settings/` | KoboldAI presets | JSON |\n| `directories.novelAI_Settings` | `NovelAI Settings/` | NovelAI presets | JSON |\n| `directories.openAI_Settings` | `OpenAI Settings/` | Chat completion presets | JSON |\n| `directories.textGen_Settings` | `TextGen Settings/` | Text completion presets | JSON |\n| `directories.instruct` | `instruct/` | Instruct mode templates | JSON |\n| `directories.context` | `context/` | Context templates | JSON |\n| `directories.sysprompt` | `sysprompt/` | System prompt templates | JSON |\n| `directories.reasoning` | `reasoning/` | Reasoning templates | JSON |\n\n**Supporting Directories**:\n\n| Directory Property | Physical Path | Purpose |\n|-------------------|---------------|---------|\n| `directories.thumbnails` | `thumbnails/` | Cached image thumbnails via `getThumbnailUrl()` |\n| `directories.thumbnailsBg` | `thumbnails/bg/` | Background thumbnails |\n| `directories.thumbnailsAvatar` | `thumbnails/avatar/` | Avatar thumbnails |\n| `directories.thumbnailsPersona` | `thumbnails/persona/` | Persona thumbnails |\n| `directories.vectors` | `vectors/` | Vector database for RAG (Vectra LocalIndex) |\n| `directories.files` | `files/` | User-uploaded files via `/api/files` endpoints |\n| `directories.assets` | `assets/` | Character sprites read by expression system |\n| `directories.comfyWorkflows` | `comfyWorkflows/` | ComfyUI workflow templates |\n\n### Directory Creation\n\nAll user directories are created automatically by `ensurePublicDirectoriesExist()` during server startup in [src/users.js:109-126](). The function:\n\n1. Creates all directories defined in `PUBLIC_DIRECTORIES`\n2. Calls `getAllUserHandles()` to retrieve user list\n3. Maps each handle through `getUserDirectories(handle)` \n4. Creates the complete directory tree for each user via `fs.mkdirSync(..., { recursive: true })`\n\n**Sources**: [src/users.js:71-103](), [src/users.js:109-126](), [src/constants.js](), [default/config.yaml:3]()\n\n---\n\n## First-Time Setup Process\n\n### Diagram: Post-Install Initialization Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant NPM as npm install\n    participant PostInstall as post-install.js\n    participant Convert as convertConfig()\n    participant Create as createDefaultFiles()\n    participant Validate as addMissingConfigValues()\n    participant FS as File System\n    \n    NPM-\u003e\u003ePostInstall: Execute postinstall script\n    PostInstall-\u003e\u003eConvert: Check for config.conf\n    \n    alt config.conf exists\n        Convert-\u003e\u003eFS: Rename to .cjs\n        Convert-\u003e\u003eConvert: Require and parse\n        Convert-\u003e\u003eFS: Write config.yaml\n        Convert-\u003e\u003eFS: Backup to .bak\n        Convert-\u003e\u003eFS: Delete .cjs\n    end\n    \n    PostInstall-\u003e\u003eCreate: Create default files\n    Create-\u003e\u003eFS: Check config.yaml exists\n    \n    alt config.yaml missing\n        Create-\u003e\u003eFS: Copy default/config.yaml\n    end\n    \n    Create-\u003e\u003eFS: Sync default/public/ to public/\n    Note over Create,FS: Only copies missing files\u003cbr/\u003eDoes not overwrite existing\n    \n    PostInstall-\u003e\u003eValidate: Add missing config values\n    Validate-\u003e\u003eFS: Read config.yaml\n    Validate-\u003e\u003eFS: Read default/config.yaml\n    Validate-\u003e\u003eValidate: Deep merge configs\n    Validate-\u003e\u003eFS: Write updated config.yaml\n```\n\n### Post-Install Tasks\n\nThe post-install script [post-install.js:105-114]() performs three sequential operations within a try-catch block:\n\n1. **Config Migration** via `convertConfig()` [post-install.js:20-41]()\n   - Checks for legacy `config.conf` file existence\n   - Renames to `config.conf.cjs` to force CommonJS loading\n   - Uses `createRequire(import.meta.url)` to load the module\n   - Writes YAML via `yaml.stringify(config)`\n   - Creates backup as `config.conf.bak` via `fs.copyFileSync()`\n   - Removes the temporary `.cjs` file via `fs.rmSync()`\n\n2. **Default File Creation** via `createDefaultFiles()` [post-install.js:46-103]()\n   - Iterates over `defaultItems` array containing:\n     - `{ type: 'file', defaultPath: './default/config.yaml', productionPath: './config.yaml' }`\n     - `{ type: 'directory', defaultPath: './default/public/', productionPath: './public/' }`\n   - For type `'file'`: Copies via `fs.copyFileSync()` if missing\n   - For type `'directory'`: Syncs via `fs.cpSync(..., { force: false, recursive: true })`\n   - Logs success messages via `console.log(color.green(...))`\n\n3. **Config Validation** via `addMissingConfigValues()` (imported from `src/config-init.js`)\n   - Reads current `config.yaml` via `fs.readFileSync()`\n   - Reads `default/config.yaml` as template\n   - Performs deep merge to add missing keys\n   - Writes updated config via `fs.writeFileSync()`\n   - Preserves all existing user configurations\n\n### Migration from Legacy Format\n\nFor existing installations, the first server startup triggers a data migration process if the legacy `public/` directory structure is detected. The migration is handled by `migrateUserData()` in [src/users.js:228-389]().\n\n**Migration Process**:\n\n1. Detects presence of `public/characters/` directory\n2. Prompts user with 10-second countdown\n3. Migrates all content from `public/` to `DATA_ROOT/default-user/`\n4. Creates backups in the `backups/` directory\n5. Handles character cards, chats, settings, and all asset directories\n\nThe migration mapping includes 20+ directory pairs, covering all data types from character cards to vector databases.\n\n**Sources**: [post-install.js:1-115](), [src/users.js:228-389](), [src/users.js:254-371]()\n\n---\n\n## Starting the Server\n\n### Startup Sequence\n\nThe server initialization follows this sequence as defined in [server.js:1-18]():\n\n1. **Display Runtime Information**\n   ```javascript\n   console.log(`Node version: ${process.version}. Running in ${process.env.NODE_ENV} environment.`);\n   ```\n\n2. **Parse Command-Line Arguments**\n   - `CommandLineParser` processes `process.argv`\n   - Returns parsed `cliArgs` object\n\n3. **Set Global Variables**\n   - `globalThis.DATA_ROOT = cliArgs.dataRoot`\n   - `globalThis.COMMAND_LINE_ARGS = cliArgs`\n\n4. **Change Working Directory**\n   - Sets process working directory to server directory\n   - Ensures relative paths work correctly\n\n5. **Import Server Main Module**\n   - Dynamically imports [src/server-main.js]()\n   - Server initialization continues in the imported module\n\n### Common Startup Commands\n\n| Command | Script Name | Use Case |\n|---------|-------------|----------|\n| `npm start` | `start` | Standard local-only startup via `node server.js` |\n| `npm run debug` | `debug` | Start with `node --inspect server.js` debugger attached |\n| `npm run start:global` | `start:global` | Start with `--global` flag (enables `listen: true`) |\n| `npm run start:no-csrf` | `start:no-csrf` | Start with `--disableCsrf` flag (not recommended) |\n| `npm run start:electron` | `start:electron` | Start Electron wrapper (runs from `src/electron/`) |\n| `npm run start:deno` | `start:deno` | Start via Deno runtime |\n| `npm run start:bun` | `start:bun` | Start via Bun runtime |\n\nAll scripts are defined in [package.json:115-127]().\n\n### Startup Output\n\nTypical startup output from [server.js:5]():\n\n```\nNode version: v18.19.0. Running in production environment. Server directory: /path/to/SillyTavern\n```\n\nAfter successful initialization by `server-main.js`, the server displays:\n```\nServer is listening on port 8000\n```\n\n**Sources**: [server.js:1-18](), [server.js:5](), [server.js:8-10](), [package.json:115-127]()\n\n---\n\n## Common Configuration Options\n\n### Essential Settings\n\nThe following configuration options are most commonly modified for initial setup:\n\n#### Network Configuration\n\n```yaml\n# Enable network access (required for remote connections)\nlisten: true\n\n# Server port\nport: 8000\n\n# Listen addresses\nlistenAddress:\n  ipv4: 0.0.0.0\n  ipv6: '::'\n```\n\n**Sources**: [default/config.yaml:5-10]()\n\n#### Security Configuration\n\n```yaml\n# IP whitelist mode (disable for open access)\nwhitelistMode: true\n\n# Allowed IP addresses\nwhitelist:\n  - ::1\n  - 127.0.0.1\n  - 192.168.1.0/24  # Example: Allow local network\n\n# Basic authentication\nbasicAuthMode: false\nbasicAuthUser:\n  username: \"user\"\n  password: \"password\"\n\n# Multi-user accounts\nenableUserAccounts: false\n```\n\n**Security Validation**: The system performs security checks during startup via `verifySecuritySettings()` in [src/users.js:148-192](). The validation logic:\n\n1. Skips all checks if `listen: false` (local-only mode)\n2. If `listen: true` and `ENABLE_ACCOUNTS` is disabled, calls `logSecurityAlert()` which:\n   - Prints error message if `basicAuthMode` and `whitelistMode` are both disabled\n   - Checks `securityOverride` config value\n   - Calls `process.exit(1)` to halt startup if security requirements not met\n3. Warns about unprotected user accounts via console output\n4. Validates Basic Auth configuration if `basicAuthMode: true`\n\nThe security check constants are loaded from config:\n- `ENABLE_ACCOUNTS` from `enableUserAccounts` [src/users.js:26]()\n- `AUTHELIA_AUTH` from `sso.autheliaAuth` [src/users.js:27]()\n- `AUTHENTIK_AUTH` from `sso.authentikAuth` [src/users.js:28]()\n- `PER_USER_BASIC_AUTH` from `perUserBasicAuth` [src/users.js:29]()\n\n**Sources**: [default/config.yaml:56-110](), [src/users.js:133-142](), [src/users.js:148-192](), [src/users.js:26-29]()\n\n#### Browser Launch Configuration\n\n```yaml\nbrowserLaunch:\n  # Automatically open browser on startup\n  enabled: true\n  # Browser choice: 'default', 'firefox', 'chrome', 'edge'\n  browser: 'default'\n  # Override hostname (use 'auto' for automatic detection)\n  hostname: 'auto'\n  # Override port (-1 uses server port)\n  port: -1\n```\n\n**Sources**: [default/config.yaml:19-35]()\n\n#### Performance Configuration\n\n```yaml\nperformance:\n  # Enable lazy loading for large character libraries\n  lazyLoadCharacters: false\n  # Memory cache capacity for character cards\n  memoryCacheCapacity: '100mb'\n  # Enable disk caching\n  useDiskCache: true\n```\n\n**Sources**: [default/config.yaml:196-204]()\n\n#### Data Directory Configuration\n\n```yaml\n# Root directory for all user data\ndataRoot: ./data\n```\n\nThis setting can be overridden via:\n\n1. **Command-line argument**: `node server.js --dataRoot /path/to/custom/data`\n2. **Environment variable**: `SILLYTAVERN_DATAROOT=/path/to/custom/data`\n\nThe final value is stored in `globalThis.DATA_ROOT` [server.js:9]() and used throughout the application via `getUserDirectories()` function calls.\n\n**Sources**: [default/config.yaml:3](), [server.js:9](), [src/users.js:71-103]()\n\n---\n\n## Configuration via Environment Variables\n\nAny configuration value can be overridden using environment variables following the naming convention `SILLYTAVERN_*`:\n\n```bash\n# Example: Override port\nexport SILLYTAVERN_PORT=3000\n\n# Example: Override nested configuration\nexport SILLYTAVERN_EXTENSIONS_MODELS_SPEECHTOTEXT=\"Xenova/whisper-tiny\"\n\n# Start server with environment override\nnpm start\n```\n\nThe environment variable conversion is handled by `keyToEnv()` in [src/util.js:38]():\n\n```javascript\nexport const keyToEnv = (key) =\u003e 'SILLYTAVERN_' + String(key).toUpperCase().replace(/\\./g, '_');\n```\n\nEnvironment variables are checked first in the configuration resolution order, taking precedence over `config.yaml` values.\n\n**Sources**: [src/util.js:38](), [src/util.js:88-109]()\n\n---\n\n## SSL/HTTPS Configuration\n\nFor secure connections, SillyTavern supports SSL/TLS:\n\n```yaml\nssl:\n  enabled: false\n  certPath: \"./certs/cert.pem\"\n  keyPath: \"./certs/privkey.pem\"\n  keyPassphrase: \"\"  # Optional\n```\n\nThe key passphrase can also be set via environment variable `SILLYTAVERN_SSL_KEYPASSPHRASE` for improved security.\n\n**Sources**: [default/config.yaml:44-54]()\n\n---\n\n## Troubleshooting Installation\n\n### Common Issues\n\n| Issue | Error Message | Cause | Solution |\n|-------|---------------|-------|----------|\n| Config file not found | \"No config file found. Please create a config.yaml file.\" | Missing `config.yaml` | Run `npm install` to trigger `postinstall` script |\n| Config path not set | \"No config file path set. Please set using setConfigFilePath().\" | `CONFIG_PATH` is `null` | Check server startup - `setConfigFilePath()` should be called during init |\n| Port already in use | `EADDRINUSE` error | Another process using specified port | Change `port` in config.yaml or use `--port` CLI argument |\n| YAML syntax error | \"Failed to read config.yaml. Please check the file for syntax errors.\" | Invalid YAML syntax | Validate YAML formatting, check indentation and special characters |\n| Cannot connect remotely | Connection refused | `listen: false` in config | Set `listen: true` and configure `whitelist` or `whitelistMode: false` |\n| Security validation failure | \"Your current SillyTavern configuration is insecure\" | Network mode without auth | Enable `whitelistMode`, `basicAuthMode`, or `enableUserAccounts` |\n| Node version incompatible | Generic startup error | Node.js \u003c 18 | Upgrade to Node.js 18 or higher (see `package.json` engines requirement) |\n\n### Configuration Validation\n\nThe configuration file is parsed using the `yaml` package in `getConfig()` [src/util.js:55-79]():\n\n```javascript\ntry {\n    const config = yaml.parse(fs.readFileSync(CONFIG_PATH, 'utf8'));\n    CACHED_CONFIG = config;\n    return config;\n} catch (error) {\n    console.error(color.red('FATAL: Failed to read config.yaml...'));\n    console.error(error.message);\n    process.exit(1);\n}\n```\n\nThe `CONFIG_PATH` variable must be set via `setConfigFilePath()` before any call to `getConfig()`. If not set, the function will:\n1. Call `console.trace()` to show call stack\n2. Print error message in red via `color.red()` [src/util.js:58]()\n3. Call `process.exit(1)` to halt the process\n\n**Sources**: [src/util.js:55-79](), [src/util.js:44-49](), [package.json:96-98](), [default/config.yaml:1-327]()\n\n---\n\n## Next Steps\n\nAfter completing installation and initial configuration:\n\n- Review [Server Architecture and Routing](#2.2) to understand how the application processes requests\n- Configure [Authentication and Security](#2.4) settings appropriate for your deployment\n- Explore [System Dependencies and Libraries](#1.2) to understand the third-party components"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"19:T514c,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# System Dependencies and Libraries\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [.eslintrc.cjs](.eslintrc.cjs)\n- [package-lock.json](package-lock.json)\n- [package.json](package.json)\n- [public/global.d.ts](public/global.d.ts)\n- [public/jsconfig.json](public/jsconfig.json)\n- [public/lib.js](public/lib.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page documents the runtime requirements, server-side dependencies, and client-side libraries used by SillyTavern. |\n\n**Sources:** [public/lib.js:5-25](), [public/lib.js:82-105]()\n\n### Library Shimming System\n\nThe `initLibraryShims()` function [public/lib.js:32-80]() maintains backward compatibility with older extensions by exposing libraries on the `window` object:\n\n```javascript\n// Example from lib.js\nif (!('DOMPurify' in window)) {\n    window.DOMPurify = DOMPurify;\n}\n```\n\nThis allows legacy extensions to access libraries via global variables (e.g., `window.DOMPurify`) while modern code can use ES module imports.\n\n**Sources:** [public/lib.js:32-80]()\n\n### Global Library Exposure\n\nThe `SillyTavern` global object [public/global.d.ts:173-177]() provides access to bundled libraries:\n\n```typescript\nvar SillyTavern: {\n    getContext(): typeof getContext;\n    llm: any;\n    libs: typeof libs;\n};\n```\n\nExtensions can access bundled libraries via `SillyTavern.libs.*` for a standardized import path.\n\n**Sources:** [public/global.d.ts:173-177]()\n\n## utilities\n- `@chevrotain/types` ^11.0.3 - Chevrotain parser types\n\nThe [public/jsconfig.json:1-29]() enables type checking in JavaScript files via the `checkJs` compiler option.\n\n**Sources:** [package.json:138-164](), [public/jsconfig.json:1-29](), [public/global.d.ts:1-247]()\n\n## Dependency Management\n\n### Version Constraints\n\nDependencies use semantic versioning with caret (`^`) constraints, allowing minor and patch updates:\n- `^4.21.0` - Allows 4.21.x and 4.x.x (but not 5.0.0)\n- Exact versions (e.g., `2.14.6`) are used for critical packages like `sillytavern-transformers`\n\n**Sources:** [package.json:2-95]()\n\n### Package Overrides\n\nThe `overrides` field [package.json:99-106]() forces specific dependency versions for nested dependencies:\n\n```json\n\"overrides\": {\n    \"vectra\": {\n        \"openai\": \"^4.17.0\"\n    },\n    \"node-fetch\": {\n        \"whatwg-url\": \"^14.0.0\"\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThis ensures compatibility and security by upgrading transitive dependencies.\n\n**Sources:** [package.json:99-106]()\n\n### Lock File\n\nThe [package-lock.json:1-10]() maintains exact versions of all dependencies and their transitive dependencies, ensuring reproducible installations. It uses lockfileVersion 3 for npm 7+.\n\n**Sources:** [package-lock.json:1-10]()\n\n### Post-Install Scripts\n\nThe `postinstall` script [package.json:123]() runs `node post-install.js` after dependency installation to perform setup tasks such as downloading tokenizer models.\n\n**Sources:** [package.json:123]()\n\n## External Libraries (Not Bundled)\n\nSome client-side libraries are loaded directly via script tags in HTML and are not bundled through Webpack. These are declared as globals in [.eslintrc.cjs:59-65]() and [public/global.d.ts:169-171]():\n\n| Library | Global Name | Purpose |\n|---------|-------------|---------|\n| PDF.js | `pdfjsLib` | PDF rendering |\n| epub.js | `ePub` | EPUB reader |\n| Toastr | `toastr` | Toast notifications |\n\nThese libraries are typically loaded from the `public/lib/` directory, which is excluded from linting [.eslintrc.cjs:72]().\n\n**Sources:** [.eslintrc.cjs:59-65](), [public/global.d.ts:169-171](), Architecture\n\n### Entry Point and Initialization\n\n**Server Bootstrap Sequence**\n\nThe server initialization follows a two-stage process:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Node as \"Node.js Process\"\n    participant ServerJS as \"server.js\"\n    participant CLI as \"CommandLineParser\"\n    participant ServerMain as \"server-main.js\"\n    participant ConfigInit as \"config-init.js\"\n    participant Express as \"Express App\"\n    \n    Node-\u003e\u003eServerJS: \"Execute Entry Point\"\n    ServerJS-\u003e\u003eServerJS: \"console.log(Node version)\"\n    ServerJS-\u003e\u003eCLI: \"new CommandLineParser()\"\n    CLI-\u003e\u003eCLI: \"parse(process.argv)\"\n    CLI--\u003e\u003eServerJS: \"cliArgs {dataRoot, config}\"\n    ServerJS-\u003e\u003eServerJS: \"globalThis.DATA_ROOT = cliArgs.dataRoot\"\n    ServerJS-\u003e\u003eServerJS: \"globalThis.COMMAND_LINE_ARGS = cliArgs\"\n    ServerJS-\u003e\u003eServerJS: \"process.chdir(serverDirectory)\"\n    ServerJS-\u003e\u003eServerMain: \"import('./src/server-main.js')\"\n    ServerMain-\u003e\u003eConfigInit: \"setConfigFilePath()\"\n    ConfigInit-\u003e\u003eConfigInit: \"getConfig() from config.yaml\"\n    ServerMain-\u003e\u003eExpress: \"express()\"\n    Express-\u003e\u003eExpress: \"Apply middleware stack\"\n    Express-\u003e\u003eExpress: \"Register API routes\"\n    Express--\u003e\u003eServerMain: \"app.listen(port)\"\n```\n\nThe `server.js` bootstrap [server.js:1-18]() performs these steps:\n\n| Step | Code | Purpose |\n|------|------|---------|\n| 1 | `console.log(process.version)` | Log Node version and environment [server.js:5]() |\n| 2 | `new CommandLineParser().parse()` | Parse `--dataRoot` and `--config` arguments [server.js:8]() |\n| 3 | `globalThis.DATA_ROOT = cliArgs.dataRoot` | Set global data root path [server.js:9]() |\n| 4 | `globalThis.COMMAND_LINE_ARGS = cliArgs` | Store CLI arguments globally [server.js:10]() |\n| 5 | `process.chdir(serverDirectory)` | Change to server directory [server.js:11]() |\n| 6 | `import('./src/server-main.js')` | Load main server module [server.js:14]() |\n\n**Sources:** [server.js:1-18](), [src/command-line.js](), [src/server-directory.js]()\n\n### Configuration System\n\n**Configuration Loading and Precedence**\n\nServer configuration is loaded from `config.yaml` with environment variable overrides:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    ConfigYAML[\"config.yaml\u003cbr/\u003efs.readFileSync()\"]\n    EnvVars[\"Environment Variables\u003cbr/\u003eprocess.env.SILLYTAVERN_*\"]\n    GetConfig[\"getConfig()\u003cbr/\u003eCACHED_CONFIG\"]\n    GetConfigValue[\"getConfigValue(key)\u003cbr/\u003ePrecedence: Env \u003e YAML\"]\n    \n    ConfigYAML --\u003e GetConfig\n    GetConfig --\u003e GetConfigValue\n    EnvVars --\u003e GetConfigValue\n    \n    GetConfigValue --\u003e DataRoot[\"dataRoot: './data'\"]\n    GetConfigValue --\u003e WhitelistMode[\"whitelistMode: true\"]\n    GetConfigValue --\u003e EnableUserAccounts[\"enableUserAccounts: false\"]\n    GetConfigValue --\u003e SSL[\"ssl.enabled: false\"]\n```\n\n**Configuration API Functions**\n\n| Function | Location | Purpose |\n|----------|----------|---------|\n| `getConfig()` | [src/util.js:55-78]() | Returns cached parsed YAML, exits if missing |\n| `getConfigValue(key, default, type)` | [src/util.js:88-109]() | Gets value with env var override and type conversion |\n| `keyToEnv(key)` | [src/util.js:38]() | Converts `extensions.enabled` → `SILLYTAVERN_EXTENSIONS_ENABLED` |\n| `setConfigFilePath(path)` | [src/util.js:44-49]() | Sets `CONFIG_PATH` once during startup |\n\nEnvironment variables override YAML values using the pattern `SILLYTAVERN_\u003cKEY_PATH\u003e` where dots become underscores (uppercase). For example:\n- `dataRoot` → `SILLYTAVERN_DATAROOT`\n- `ssl.enabled` → `SILLYTAVERN_SSL_ENABLED`\n- `extensions.autoUpdate` → `SILLYTAVERN_EXTENSIONS_AUTOUPDATE`\n\n**Sources:** [src/util.js:26-119](), [default/config.yaml:1-227](), [src/config-init.js]()\n\n### Express Middleware Stack\n\n**Middleware Execution Order**\n\nThe middleware stack is applied in strict order to enforce security and session management:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Request[\"Incoming HTTP Request\"]\n    \n    Request --\u003e IPWhitelist[\"whitelistMiddleware()\u003cbr/\u003esrc/middleware/whitelist.js\"]\n    IPWhitelist --\u003e BasicAuth[\"basicAuthMiddleware()\u003cbr/\u003esrc/middleware/basicAuth.js\"]\n    BasicAuth --\u003e UserSession[\"userMiddleware()\u003cbr/\u003esrc/users.js\"]\n    UserSession --\u003e CSRF[\"csrfProtection\u003cbr/\u003eexpress-csrf-token\"]\n    \n    CSRF --\u003e Routes[\"API Route Handlers\u003cbr/\u003esrc/endpoints/*\"]\n    \n    IPWhitelist -.Reject.-\u003e Forbidden403[\"403 Forbidden\u003cbr/\u003eUnauthorized IP\"]\n    BasicAuth -.Reject.-\u003e Unauthorized401[\"401 Unauthorized\u003cbr/\u003eInvalid credentials\"]\n    CSRF -.Reject.-\u003e Forbidden403[\"403 Forbidden\u003cbr/\u003eInvalid CSRF token\"]\n```\n\n**Middleware Configuration**\n\n| Middleware | File | Function | Config Key | Default |\n|------------|------|----------|------------|---------|\n| IP Whitelist | [src/middleware/whitelist.js:13-100]() | `whitelistMiddleware()` | `whitelistMode` | `true` |\n| Basic Auth | [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:13-60]() | `basicAuthMiddleware()` | `basicAuthMode` | `false` |\n| User Session | [src/users.js:200-300]() | `userMiddleware()` | `enableUserAccounts` | `false` |\n| CSRF Protection | Built-in | `csrfProtection()` | `disableCsrfProtection` | `false` |\n\nThe `userMiddleware()` attaches `request.user` with:\n- `request.user.profile` - User profile with `handle` and `name`\n- `request.user.directories` - Object with all user-specific paths from `getUserDirectories()`\n\n**Sources:** [src/middleware/whitelist.js:13-100](), [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:13-60](), [src/users.js:200-300](), [default/config.yaml:56-150]()\n\n### API Endpoint Structure\n\n**API Routes by Resource**\n\nAPI endpoints follow REST conventions and are organized by resource:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph CharacterMgmt[\"Character Management\u003cbr/\u003esrc/endpoints/characters.js\"]\n        CharList[\"POST /api/characters/all\u003cbr/\u003eList all characters\"]\n        CharGet[\"POST /api/characters/get\u003cbr/\u003eGet single character\"]\n        CharCreate[\"POST /api/characters/create\u003cbr/\u003ewriteCharacterData()\"]\n        CharEdit[\"POST /api/characters/edit\u003cbr/\u003eUpdate character card\"]\n ChatGet[\"POST /api/chats/get\u003cbr/\u003eLoad chat.jsonl\"]\n        ChatSave[\"POST /api/chats/save\u003cbr/\u003esaveChatConditional()\"]\n        ChatRename[\"POST /api/chats/rename\u003cbr/\u003eRename chat file\"]\n        ChatDelete[\"POST /api/chats/delete\u003cbr/\u003eMove to backups\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph SettingsMgmt[\"Settings Management\u003cbr/\u003esrc/endpoints/settings.js\"]\n        SettingsGet[\"POST /api/settings/get\u003cbr/\u003eLoad settings.json\"]\n        SettingsSet[\"POST /api/settings/update\u003cbr/\u003eSave + backup\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph GroupMgmt[\"Group Management\u003cbr/\u003esrc/endpoints/groups.js\"]\n        GroupList[\"GET /api/groups/all\u003cbr/\u003eList all groups\"]\n        GroupGet[\"POST /api/groups/get\u003cbr/\u003eLoad group data\"]\n        GroupEdit[\"POST /api/groups/edit\u003cbr/\u003eSave group.json\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Common Endpoint Pattern**\n\nEach endpoint handler follows this pattern:\n\n1. **Validate request** - Check required parameters in `request.body`\n2. **Access user directories** - Use `request.user.directories` for user-scoped paths\n3. **Perform file operations** - Read/write to file system using `fs` or `writeFileAtomicSync`\n4. **Return JSON** - Send `response.json(data)` or error with appropriate status code\n\nExample from characters endpoint [src/endpoints/characters.js:250-300]():\n```javascript\nrouter.post('/edit', async (request, response) =\u003e {\n    const { avatar_url, data } = request.body;\n    // Validate parameters\n    const outputFile = path.parse(avatar_url).name;\n    // Write to user directory\n    await writeCharacterData(inputFile, data, outputFile, request);\n    // Invalidate caches\n    invalidateThumbnail('avatar', avatar_url);\n    return response.send({ ok: true });\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/characters.js:1-300](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:1-200](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:1-100](), [src/endpoints/groups.js:1-100]()\n\n---\n\n## Client Architecture\n\n### Frontend Entry Point\n\n**Client Initialization Sequence**\n\nThe client initialization begins with the HTML structure and bootstraps the JavaScript runtime:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Browser[\"Browser Load\"]\n Order | Function Call | Purpose | File Reference |\n|-------|---------------|---------|----------------|\n| 1 | `fetch('/csrf-token')` | Get CSRF token for all requests | [public/script.js:668-674]() |\n| 2 | `showLoader()` | Display loading screen | [public/script.js:676]() |\n| 3 | `registerPromptManagerMigration()` | Register settings migrations | [public/script.js:677]() |\n| 4 | `initSecrets()` | Load API keys | [public/script.js:686]() |\n| 5 | `initLocales()` | Load i18n translations | [public/script.js:688]() |\n| 6 | `initDefaultSlashCommands()` | Register built-in ready state | [public/script.js:738]() |\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:666-739](), [public/index.html:1-50](), [public/lib.js:1-12]()\n\n### Main Orchestrator (script.js)\n\n**Central Coordination Role**\n\nThe `script.js` file serves as the central coordinator for all client-side functionality:\n\n| Component | Responsibility | Key Variables/Functions |\n|-----------|---------------|-------------------------|\n| **State Management** | Global application state | `chat[]`, `characters[]`, `this_chid`, `selected_group`, `chat_metadata{}` |\n| **Character Selection** | Character/group selection flow | `selectCharacterById()`, `setActiveCharacter()`, `setActiveGroup()` |\n| **Chat Operations** | Chat display and generation | **Event Coordination** | Module event dispatch | `eventSource.emit()`, `eventSource.on()` |\n| **UI Updates** | DOM manipulation | `printCharacters()`, `scrollChatToBottom()` |\n\n**Critical Global State Variables**\n\n| Variable | Type | Purpose | Declaration |\n|----------|------|---------|-------------|\n| `chat` | `ChatMessage[]` | Current conversation messages | [public/script.js:382]() `settings` | `Settings` | Global user settings object | [public/script.js:586]() |\n| `extension_settings` | `object` | Extension-specific settings | [public/scripts/extensions.js]() |\n| `name1` | --\u003e|CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED| Extensions\n    EventSource --\u003e|CHAT_CHANGED| WorldInfo\n    EventSource --\u003e|GROUP_MEMBER_ADDED| GroupChats\n    EventSource --\u003e|SETTINGS_LOADED| PowerUser\n    \n    subgraph \"Event Types (event_types)\"\n        APP_READY[\"APP_READY\"]\n        CHAT_CHANGED[\"CHAT_CHANGED\"]\n        MESSAGE_RECEIVED[\"MESSAGE_RECEIVED\"]\n        CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED[\"CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED\"]\n    end\n```\n\nModules register event listeners and emit events without knowing about each other:\n\n```javascript\n// Module emits event\neventSource.emit(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, messageIndex);\n\n// Other modules listen\neventSource.on(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, (index) =\u003e {\n    // React to new message\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:278](), [public/scripts/events.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Data Storage Architecture\n\n### File-Based Storage Model\n\n**Directory Hierarchy**\n\nSillyTavern uses a hierarchical file system for all persistent data, rooted at `globalThis.DATA_ROOT`:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    DataRoot[\"globalThis.DATA_ROOT/\u003cbr/\u003eSet via --dataRoot CLI arg\"]\n    \n    DataRoot --\u003e DefaultUser[\"default-user/\u003cbr/\u003eSingle user mode\u003cbr/\u003eDEFAULT_USER constant\"]\n    DataRoot --\u003e UserHandle[\"user_handle/\u003cbr/\u003eMulti-user mode\u003cbr/\u003eenableUserAccounts: true\"]\n    DataRoot --\u003e Cache[\"_cache/\u003cbr/\u003eDiskCache storage\"]\n    \n    UserHandle --\u003e Characters[\"characters/\u003cbr/\u003ePNG with tEXt chunks\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Chats[\"chats/\u003cbr/\u003e*.jsonl line-delimited\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Groups[\"groups/\u003cbr/\u003e*.json group metadata\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e GroupChats[\"group_chats/\u003cbr/\u003e*.jsonl multi-character\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Worlds[\"worlds/\u003cbr/\u003e*.json world info\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Settings[\"settings.json\u003cbr/\u003eUser preferences\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Backups[\"backups/\u003cbr/\u003echat_* timestamped\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e UserDir[\"User/\u003cbr/\u003eavatars \u0026 personas\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Backgrounds[\"backgrounds/\u003cbr/\u003e*.png images\"]\n    \n    Characters --\u003e CharPNG[\"character_name.png\u003cbr/\u003eparse() extracts JSON\"]\n    Chats --\u003e ChatJSONL[\"character_name.jsonl\u003cbr/\u003ereadline per message\"]\n    GroupChats --\u003e GroupChatJSONL[\"group_id.jsonl\u003cbr/\u003emulti-character logs\"]\n    \n    Cache --\u003e CharCache[\"characters/\u003cbr/\u003enode-persist cache\"]\n    Cache --\u003e ThumbnailCache[\"thumbnails/\u003cbr/\u003eResized avatars\"]\n```\n\n**User Directory Structure (getUserDirectories)**\n\nThe `getUserDirectories(handle)` function [src/users.js:240-280]() returns a `UserDirectoryList` object:\n\n```javascript\n{\n    root: path.join(DATA_ROOT, handle),\n    characters: path.join(DATA_ROOT, handle, 'characters'),\n    chats: path.join(DATA_ROOT, handle, 'chats'),\n    groups: path.join(DATA_ROOT, handle, 'groups'),\n    groupChats: path.join(DATA_ROOT, handle, 'group_chats'),\n    worlds: path.join(DATA_ROOT, handle, 'worlds'),\n    user: path.join(DATA_ROOT, handle, 'User'),\n    avatars: path.join(DATA_ROOT, handle, 'User', 'avatars'),\n    backgrounds: path.join(DATA_ROOT, handle, 'backgrounds'),\n    backups: path.join(DATA_ROOT, handle, 'backups'),\n    // ... 20+ additional directories\n}\n```\n\nAll paths are created during user initialization via `USER_DIRECTORY_TEMPLATE` [src/constants.js:18]().\n\n**Sources:** [src/users.js:240-280](), [src/constants.js:18-50](), [default/config.yaml:1-5]()\n\n### Character Storage Format\n\n**PNG with Embedded Metadata**\n\nCharacters are stored as PNG images with embedded JSON data in tEXt chunks:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    PNGFile[\"character.png\u003cbr/\u003eStandard PNG File\"]\n    \n    PNGFile --\u003e ImageData[\"Image Data\u003cbr/\u003eVisual Avatar\"]\n    PNGFile --\u003e tEXtChunk[\"tEXt Chunk\u003cbr/\u003eKeyword: 'chara'\"]\n    \n    tEXtChunk --\u003e Base64[\"Base64 Encoding\"]\n    Base64 --\u003e JSON[\"Character Card JSON\u003cbr/\u003eTavernCard V2/V3 Spec\"]\n    \n    JSON --\u003e Fields[\"name, description\u003cbr/\u003epersonality, scenario\u003cbr/\u003efirst_mes, mes_example\"]\n```\n\n**Character Card Parser Functions**\n\n| Function | Location | Purpose |\n|----------|----------|---------|\n| `parse(inputFile, format)` | [src/character-card-parser.js:read]() | Extracts JSON from PNG tEXt chunks |\n| `write(imageBuffer, jsonData)` | [src/character-card-parser.js:write]() | Embeds JSON into PNG as tEXt chunk |\n| `readCharacterData(inputFile)` | [src/endpoints/characters.js:181-209]() | Reads with two-tier caching |\n| `writeCharacterData(input, data, output)` | [src/endpoints/characters.js:220-265]() | Writes with cache invalidation |\n\n**Two-Tier Caching Strategy**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Request[\"Character Read Request\"]\n    \n    Request --\u003e MemCheck[\"memoryCache.has(cacheKey)\u003cbr/\u003eMemoryLimitedMap\"]\n    MemCheck --\u003e|Hit| MemReturn[\"Return cached data\"]\n    MemCheck --\u003e|Miss| DiskCheck[\"diskCache.getItem(cacheKey)\u003cbr/\u003enode-persist\"]\n    \n    DiskCheck --\u003e|Hit| DiskReturn[\"Return from disk\"]\n    DiskCheck --\u003e|Miss| FSRead[\"parse(inputFile)\u003cbr/\u003eExtract from PNG\"]\n    \n    FSRead --\u003e UpdateCaches[\"memoryCache.set()\u003cbr/\u003ediskCache.setItem()\"]\n    UpdateCaches --\u003e Return[\"Return character data\"]\n```\n\nCache configuration:\n- **Memory Cache**: `MemoryLimitedMap` with `memoryCacheCapacity` limit [src/endpoints/characters.js:30-32]()\n- **Disk Cache**: `node-persist` storage in `_cache/characters/` [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-157]()\n- **Cache Key**: `${filePath}-${stat.mtimeMs}` includes modification time [src/endpoints/characters.js:166-173]()\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/characters.js:162-265](), [src/character-card-parser.js](), [src/util.js:MemoryLimitedMap]()\n\n### Chat Storage Format\n\n**JSONL Line-Delimited Format**\n\nChats use JSONL (JSON Lines) format with one message per line:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    ChatFile[\"character_name.jsonl\u003cbr/\u003eOr group_id.jsonl\"]\n    \n    ChatFile --\u003e Header[\"Line 0: File Header\u003cbr/\u003e{chat_metadata, user_name, character_name}\"]\n    ChatFile --\u003e Message1[\"Line 1: First Message\u003cbr/\u003e{name, is_user, mes, send_date, swipes, extra}\"]\n    ChatFile --\u003e Message2[\"Line 2: Second Message\u003cbr/\u003e{name, is_user, mes, send_date, swipes, extra}\"]\n    ChatFile --\u003e MessageN[\"Line N: Nth Message\u003cbr/\u003e...\"]\n    \n    Header --\u003e Metadata[\"chat_metadata\u003cbr/\u003e{note, variables, integrity, tainted}\"]\n    Message1 --\u003e Swipes[\"swipes: string[]\u003cbr/\u003eAlternative responses\"]\n    Message1 --\u003e Extra[\"extra: {}\u003cbr/\u003egen_id, api, model, etc.\"]\n```\n\n**Chat File Operations**\n\n| Operation | Implementation | Details |\n|-----------|---------------|---------|\n| **Read** | `readline.createInterface()` | Streams line-by-line [src/endpoints/chats.js:read operations]() |\n| **Parse** | `JSON.parse(line)` per line | First line is header, rest are messages |\n| **Write** | `writeFileAtomicSync(path, data)` | Atomic write prevents corruption [src/endpoints/chats.js:8]() |\n| **Backup** | `backupChat(directory, name, data)` | Throttled automatic backups [src/endpoints/chats.js:40-60]() |\n\n**Message Object Structure**\n\n```javascript\n{\n    name: \"Character Name\",\n    is_user: false,\n    mes: \"Message text content\",\n    send_date: \"2024-01-15T12:34:56.789Z\",\n    swipes: [\"Alternative 1\", \"Alternative 2\"],\n    swipe_id: 0,\n    extra: {\n        gen_id: \"uuid-v4\",\n        api: \"openai\",\n        model: \"gpt-4\",\n        // ... additional metadata\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Automatic Backup System**\n\nChat backups are created on every save:\n\n| Config | Location | Default | Purpose |\n|--------|----------|---------|---------|\n| `isBackupEnabled` | [src/endpoints/chats.js:25]() | `true` | Enable/disable backups |\n| `throttleInterval` | [src/endpoints/chats.js:27]() | 10000ms | Debounce backup writes per user |\n| `maxTotalChatBackups` | [src/endpoints/chats.js:26]() | -1 (unlimited) | Total backup files to keep |\n\nBackup naming: `chat_\u003csanitized_name\u003e_\u003ctimestamp\u003e.jsonl` [src/endpoints/chats.js:49]()\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:1-150](), [src/util.js:removeOldBackups](), [default/config.yaml:166-180]()\n\n---\n\n## Request Flow Examples\n\n### Character Loading Flow\n\n**Complete Request Flow**\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Browser\n    participant ScriptJS as \"script.js\"\n    participant CharAPI as \"POST /api/characters/all\"\n    participant MemCache as \"MemoryLimitedMap\"\n    participant DiskCache as \"node-persist\"\n    participant FS as \"File System\"\n    \n    Browser-\u003e\u003eScriptJS: \"Page load\"\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eScriptJS: \"firstLoadInit()\"\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eScriptJS: \"getCharacters()\"\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eCharAPI: \"POST /api/characters/all\u003cbr/\u003e{simple: true}\"\n    \n    CharAPI-\u003e\u003eCharAPI: \"request.user.directories.characters\"\n    CharAPI-\u003e\u003eFS: \"fs.readdirSync() *.png files\"\n    \n    loop For each character PNG\n        CharAPI-\u003e\u003eCharAPI: \"getCacheKey(filePath)\"\n        CharAPI-\u003e\u003eMemCache: \"memoryCache.has(cacheKey)\"\n        alt Memory cache hit\n            MemCache--\u003e\u003eCharAPI: \"Return cached JSON\"\n        else Memory cache miss\n            CharAPI-\u003e\u003eDiskCache: \"diskCache.getItem(cacheKey)\"\n            alt Disk cache hit\n                DiskCache--\u003e\u003eCharAPI: \"Return cached JSON\"\n                CharAPI-\u003e\u003eMemCache: \"memoryCache.set()\"\n            else Disk cache miss\n                CharAPI-\u003e\u003eFS: \"fs.readFileSync(pngFile)\"\n                CharAPI-\u003e\u003eCharAPI: \"parse(buffer) - Extract tEXt\"\n                CharAPI-\u003e\u003eMemCache: \"memoryCache.set(cacheKey, data)\"\n                CharAPI-\u003e\u003eDiskCache: \"diskCache.setItem(cacheKey, data)\"\n            end\n        end\n    end\n    \n    CharAPI--\u003e\u003eScriptJS: \"JSON array of characters\"\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eScriptJS: \"characters = data\"\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eScriptJS: \"printCharacters()\"\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eBrowser: \"Render character cards\"\n```\n\n**Key Functions in Flow**\n\n| Function | File | Purpose |\n|----------|------|---------|\n| `getCharacters()` | [public/script.js:getCharacters]() | Client-side initiator, stores in `characters[]` |\n| `router.post('/all')` | [src/endpoints/characters.js:character list endpoint]() | Server endpoint handler |\n| `readCharacterData(file)` | [src/endpoints/characters.js:181-209]() | Two-tier cache lookup |\n| `getCacheKey(file)` | [src/endpoints/characters.js:166-173]() | Generates cache key with mtime |\n| `parse(buffer)` | [src/character-card-parser.js:parse]() | Extracts JSON from PNG tEXt chunk |\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:getCharacters](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:181-209](), [src/character-card-parser.js]()\n\n### Chat Message Generation Flow\n\n**Complete Generation Pipeline**\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant ScriptJS as \"script.js\"\n    participant Generate as \"Generate()\"\n    participant PM as \"PromptManager\"\n    participant OpenAI as \"openai.js\"\n    participant Backend as \"POST /api/backends/\u003cbr/\u003echat-completions/generate\"\n    participant LLM as \"External LLM API\"\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eScriptJS: \"Click Send button\"\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eScriptJS: \"addOneMessage(userMsg)\u003cbr/\u003eAdd to chat[]\"\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eBackend: \"POST /api/chats/save\"\n    \n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eGenerate: \"Generate(type='normal')\"\n    Generate-\u003e\u003eGenerate: \"getMaxContextSize()\u003cbr/\u003eCalculate token budget\"\n    Generate-\u003e\u003eGenerate: \"Build prompt context\"\n    \n    alt Chat Completion API\n        Generate-\u003e\u003ePM: \"setupChatCompletionPromptManager()\"\n        PM-\u003e\u003ePM: \"Assemble prompt components\"\n        PM-\u003e\u003eOpenAI: \"prepareOpenAIMessages()\"\n        OpenAI-\u003e\u003eOpenAI: \"setOpenAIMessages(chat)\"\n        OpenAI-\u003e\u003eBackend: \"POST /api/backends/chat-completions/generate\u003cbr/\u003e{messages, model, stream: true}\"\n    else Text Completion API\n        Generate-\u003e\u003eGenerate: \"getTextGenGenerationData()\"\n        Generate-\u003e\u003eBackend: \"POST /api/backends/text-completions/generate\u003cbr/\u003e{prompt, max_tokens, stream: true}\"\n    end\n    \n    Backend-\u003e\u003eBackend: \"readSecret() - Get API key\"\n    Backend-\u003e\u003eLLM: \"HTTP POST with streaming\"\n    \n    loop SSE Streaming\n        LLM--\u003e\u003eBackend: \"data: {...chunk...}\"\n        Backend--\u003e\u003eOpenAI: \"Forward SSE chunk\"\n        OpenAI-\u003e\u003eOpenAI: \"parseStreamData(chunk)\"\n        OpenAI-\u003e\u003eScriptJS: \"streamingProcessor.onProgressStreaming()\"\n        ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eScriptJS: \"Update .mes_text innerHTML\"\n    end\n    \n    LLM--\u003e\u003eBackend: \"data: [DONE]\"\n    Backend--\u003e\u003eOpenAI: \"Complete signal\"\n    OpenAI--\u003e\u003eGenerate: \"Return full response text\"\n    Generate-\u003e\u003eScriptJS: \"addOneMessage(aiMsg)\u003cbr/\u003eAdd to chat[]\"\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eBackend: \"POST /api/chats/save\"\n```\n\n**Key Functions in Generation Pipeline**\n\n| Function | File | Purpose |\n|----------|------|---------|\n| `Generate(type, options)` | [public/script.js:Generate]() | Main generation orchestrator |\n| `getMaxContextSize()` | [public/script.js:getMaxContextSize]() | Calculate token budget |\n| `setupChatCompletionPromptManager()` | [public/scripts/openai.js:setupChatCompletionPromptManager]() | Initialize PromptManager |\n| `prepareOpenAIMessages(chat)` | [public/scripts/openai.js:prepareOpenAIMessages]() | Format messages array |\n| `sendOpenAIRequest()` | [public/scripts/openai.js:sendOpenAIRequest]() | Send request with streaming |\n| `generateTextGenWithStreaming()` | [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:generateTextGenWithStreaming]() | Text completion variant |\n| `StreamingProcessor.onProgressStreaming()` | [public/script.js:427]() | Handle SSE chunks |\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:Generate](), [public/scripts/openai.js:sendOpenAIRequest](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:generateTextGenWithStreaming](), [public/scripts/PromptManager.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Key Integration Points\n\n### Global Context Object\n\n**Extension API Surface**\n\nThe `getContext()` function [public/scripts/st-context.js]() provides a unified API for extensions and modules:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    GetContext[\"getContext()\u003cbr/\u003eReturns SillyTavernContext\"]\n    \n    GetContext --\u003e State[\"State Variables\"]\n    State --\u003e Characters[\"characters: Character[]\"]\n    State --\u003e ThisChid[\"this_chid: string|undefined\"]\n    State --\u003e Chat[\"chat: ChatMessage[]\"]\n    State --\u003e Groups[\"groups: Group[]\"]\n    State --\u003e OnlineStatus[\"online_status: string\"]\n    State --\u003e ChatMetadata[\"chat_metadata: ChatMetadata\"]\n    \n    GetContext --\u003e Methods[\"Core Methods\"]\n    Methods --\u003e SaveChat[\"saveChat(): Promise\u003cvoid\u003e\"]\n    Methods --\u003e ReloadChat[\"reloadCurrentChat(): Promise\u003cvoid\u003e\"]\n    Methods --\u003e Generate[\"Generate(type, options): Promise\u003cvoid\u003e\"]\n    Methods --\u003e GetCharacters[\"getCharacters(): Promise\u003cvoid\u003e\"]\n    Methods --\u003e SaveSettings[\"saveSettings(): void\"]\n    \n    GetContext --\u003e ExtSettings[\"extension_settings: object\"]\n    ExtSettings --\u003e PowerUser[\"power_user: PowerUserSettings\"]\n```\n\n**Context Object Structure**\n\nExtensions access the context to interact with core functionality without tight coupling:\n\n```javascript\nconst context = getContext();\n\n// Access state\nconst currentChat = context.chat;                    // ChatMessage[]\nconst selectedCharacter = context.characters[context.this_chid];\nconst groupsList = context.groups;                   // Group[]\n\n// Call core functions\nawait context.saveChat();                           // Save current chat\nawait context.reloadCurrentChat();                  // Reload from disk\nawait context.Generate('normal');                   // Trigger generation\n\n// Access settings\nconst powerUserSettings = context.extension_settings.power_user;\n```\n\n**Key Properties Exposed**\n\n| Property | Type | Description |\n|----------|------|-------------|\n| `chat` | `ChatMessage[]` | Current conversation messages |\n| `characters` | `Character[]` | All loaded characters |\n| `this_chid` | `string\\|undefined` | Currently selected character index |\n| `selected_group` | `string\\|null` | Currently selected group ID |\n| `groups` | `Group[]` | All loaded groups |\n| `online_status` | `string` | API connection status |\n| `extension_settings` | `object` | All extension settings |\n| `saveChat()` | `Function` | Save current chat to disk |\n| `Generate()` | `Function` | Trigger AI generation |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/st-context.js](), [public/script.js:290-293](), [public/scripts/extensions.js]()\n\n### SillyTavern Global API\n\n**Window Global Object**\n\nThe `globalThis.SillyTavern` object [public/script.js:290-293]() exposes core functionality to extensions:\n\n```javascript\nglobalThis.SillyTavern = {\n    libs,           // Third-party libraries\n    getContext,     // Extension context accessor\n};\n```\n\n**Exposed Properties**\n\n| Property | Type | Description | Usage Example |\n|----------|------|-------------|---------------|\n| `libs` | `object` | Third-party libraries (lodash, moment, DOMPurify, etc.) | `SillyTavern.libs.lodash.cloneDeep(obj)` |\n| `getContext` | `Function` | Access extension context | `const ctx = SillyTavern.getContext()` |\n\n**Available Libraries (libs object)**\n\nThe `libs` object from [public/lib.js:1-12]() includes:\n- `lodash` - Utility library\n- `moment` - Date/time manipulation\n- `DOMPurify` - HTML sanitization\n- `showdown` - Markdown parser\n- `Handlebars` - Template engine\n- `hljs` - Code syntax highlighting\n- `Popper` - Tooltip positioning\n\nThis serves as the official extension API surface for external scripts.\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:290-293](), [public/lib.js:1-12]()\n\n### Event System Integration\n\n**Event-Driven Module Communication**\n\nThe event system [public/scripts/events.js]() provides lifecycle hooks for loose coupling:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    EventSource[\"eventSource\u003cbr/\u003eEventEmitter instance\"]\n    \n    subgraph StartupEvents[\"Startup Events\"]\n        APP_INIT[\"APP_INITIALIZED\"]\n        APP_READY[\"APP_READY\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ChatEvents[\"Chat Events\"]\n        CHAT_CHANGED[\"CHAT_CHANGED\"]\n        MESSAGE_RECEIVED[\"MESSAGE_RECEIVED\"]\n        MESSAGE_SENT[\"MESSAGE_SENT\"]\n        MESSAGE_RENDERED[\"CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph CharEvents[\"Character Events\"]\n        CHARACTER_EDITED[\"CHARACTER_EDITED\"]\n        CHARACTER_PAGE_LOADED[\"CHARACTER_PAGE_LOADED\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph GenEvents[\"Generation Events\"]\n        GENERATION_STARTED[\"GENERATION_STARTED\"]\n        GENERATION_STOPPED[\"GENERATION_STOPPED\"]\n    end\n    \n    EventSource --\u003e APP_INIT\n    EventSource --\u003e APP_READY\n    EventSource --\u003e CHAT_CHANGED\n    EventSource --\u003e MESSAGE_RECEIVED\n    EventSource --\u003e MESSAGE_RENDERED\n    EventSource --\u003e GENERATION_STARTED\n    \n    APP_INIT --\u003e ExtInit[\"Extensions initialize\"]\n    CHAT_CHANGED --\u003e WIRefresh[\"World Info refresh\"]\n    MESSAGE_RECEIVED --\u003e TTS[\"TTS processing\"]\n    GENERATION_STARTED --\u003e UIUpdate[\"Disable send button\"]\n```\n\n**Event Registration Pattern**\n\nModules register handlers during initialization without knowing about each other:\n\n```javascript\nimport { eventSource, event_types } from './events.js';\n\n// Register listener\neventSource.on(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, (chatId) =\u003e {\n    console.log('Chat changed to:', chatId);\n    // React to chat change\n});\n\n// Emit event\neventSource.emit(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, getCurrentChatId());\n```\n\n**Common Event Types**\n\n| Event Constant | Emitted When | Typical Listeners |\n|----------------|--------------|-------------------|\n| `APP_INITIALIZED` | Core systems loaded | Extensions start loading |\n| `APP_READY` | Full initialization complete | Final setup tasks |\n| `CHAT_CHANGED` | Chat switched or loaded | World Info, UI updates |\n| `MESSAGE_RECEIVED` | New message added | TTS, expressions, memory |\n| `CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED` | Character message displayed | Visual effects |\n| `GENERATION_STARTED` | AI generation begins | UI state changes |\n| `GENERATION_STOPPED` | AI generation completes | Re-enable UI controls |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/events.js](), [public/script.js:278]()\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\nSillyTavern's core architecture follows these principles:\n\n1. **Clear Separation of Concerns** - Three-tier model separates UI, logic, and data\n2. **File-Based Storage** - All data persists in organized directory hierarchies\n3. **Event-Driven Communication** - Modules communicate through events, not direct coupling\n4. **Multi-User Support** - User-scoped directories enable account isolation\n5. **Extensibility** - Global API and event system enable extensions without core modifications\n6. **Configuration Flexibility** - YAML config with environment variable overrides\n7. **Caching Strategy** - Two-tier (memory + disk) cache for performance\n\nThe architecture supports both single-user and multi-user deployments, with all state managed through the file system rather than databases, making it simple to backup, migrate, and debug."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1b:T5c99,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Application Lifecycle and Initialization\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant
