[src/endpoints/presets.js](src/endpoints/presets.js)\n- [src/endpoints/settings.js](src/endpoints/settings.js)\n- [src/middleware/basicAuth.js](src/middleware/basicAuth.js)\n- [src/middleware/whitelist.js](src/middleware/whitelist.js)\n- [src/users.js](src/users.js)\n- [src/util.js](src/util.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page documents the bootstrap process and initialization sequence for both the server and client components of SillyTavern. It covers how the application starts up, loads configuration, initializes modules, and prepares for user interaction.\n\nFor information about server architecture and API routing after initialization, see [Server Architecture and Routing](#2.2). For details about the event system used during and after initialization, see [Event System and Module Communication](#2.3).\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nSillyTavern follows a two-phase initialization model:\n\n1. **Server-side initialization**: Node.js process starts, loads configuration, initializes Express server and middleware\n2. **Client-side initialization**: Browser loads HTML, executes JavaScript bootstrap, initializes UI and subsystems\n\nBoth phases must complete successfully before the application is ready for user interaction. The client emits lifecycle events (`APP_INITIALIZED` and `APP_READY`) that extensions and subsystems can listen to.\n\n---\n\n## Server Bootstrap Process\n\n### Entry Point and Command Line Processing\n\nThe server initialization begins at [server.js:1-18](). The entry script performs minimal setup before delegating to the main server module.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Process as Node Process\n    participant server.js\n    participant CommandLineParser\n    participant config.yaml\n    participant server-main.js\n\n    Process-\u003e\u003eserver.js: Execute entry point\n    server.js-\u003e\u003eCommandLineParser: parse(process.argv)\n    CommandLineParser-\u003e\u003eCommandLineParser: Parse CLI arguments\n    CommandLineParser--\u003e\u003eserver.js: Return cliArgs\n    server.js-\u003e\u003eserver.js: Set globalThis.DATA_ROOT\n    server.js-\u003e\u003eserver.js: Set globalThis.COMMAND_LINE_ARGS\n    server.js-\u003e\u003eserver.js: process.chdir(serverDirectory)\n    server.js-\u003e\u003eserver-main.js: Dynamic import\n    server-main.js-\u003e\u003econfig.yaml: Load configuration\n    config.yaml--\u003e\u003eserver-main.js: Config object\n    server-main.js-\u003e\u003eserver-main.js: Initialize Express app\n    server-main.js-\u003e\u003eserver-main.js: Register middleware\n    server-main.js-\u003e\u003eserver-main.js: Register routes\n    server-main.js-\u003e\u003eserver-main.js: Start listening\n```\n\n**Key Global Variables Set:**\n- `globalThis.DATA_ROOT` - Root directory for user data (from CLI or default `./data`)\n- `globalThis.COMMAND_LINE_ARGS` - Parsed command line arguments\n- `process.cwd()` - Set to server directory\n\nSources: [server.js:1-18](), [src/command-line.js](), [src/server-directory.js]()\n\n---\n\n### Configuration Loading\n\nConfiguration is loaded lazily and cached on first access through the `getConfig()` function.\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Start[\"getConfigValue(key)\"] --\u003e CheckCache{\"CACHED_CONFIG\\nexists?\"}\n    CheckCache --\u003e|Yes| GetValue[\"_.get(config, key)\"]\n    CheckCache --\u003e|No| CheckPath{\"CONFIG_PATH\\nset?\"}\n    CheckPath --\u003e|No| Error[\"Error: No config path\"]\n    CheckPath --\u003e|Yes| FileExists{\"config.yaml\\nexists?\"}\n    FileExists --\u003e|No| Exit[\"process.exit(1)\"]\n    FileExists --\u003e|Yes| ParseYAML[\"yaml.parse(file)\"]\n    ParseYAML --\u003e CacheConfig[\"CACHED_CONFIG = config\"]\n    CacheConfig --\u003e GetValue\n    GetValue --\u003e CheckEnv{\"Environment\\nvariable set?\"}\n    CheckEnv --\u003e|Yes| ReturnEnv[\"Return env value\"]\n    CheckEnv --\u003e|No| ReturnConfig[\"Return config value\"]\n    ReturnEnv --\u003e TypeConvert[\"Apply type conversion\"]\n    ReturnConfig --\u003e TypeConvert\n    TypeConvert --\u003e Return[\"Return value\"]\n```\n\n**Configuration Sources (Priority Order):**\n1. Environment variables (prefixed with `SILLYTAVERN_`, e.g., `SILLYTAVERN_PORT`)\n2. `config.yaml` file (specified via CLI or default location)\n3. Default values passed to `getConfigValue()`\n\n**Critical Configuration Values:**\n- `dataRoot` - User data directory\n- `port` - Server port (default: 8000)\n- `listen` - Enable network listening\n- `whitelistMode` - Enable IP whitelist security\n- `enableUserAccounts` - Enable multi-user mode\n- `ssl.enabled` - Enable HTTPS\n\nSources: [src/util.js:52-109](), [default/config.yaml:1-227]()\n\n---\n\n### Middleware Stack Initialization\n\nThe Express middleware stack is registered in a specific order to ensure proper request processing:\n\n| Order | Middleware | Purpose | Config Key |\n|-------|-----------|---------|-----------|\n| 1 | CORS | Handle cross-origin requests | `cors.enabled` |\n| 2 | Host Whitelist | Validate Host header | `hostWhitelist.enabled` |\n| 3 | IP Whitelist | Restrict access by IP | `whitelistMode` |\n| 4 | Basic Auth | HTTP Basic Authentication | `basicAuthMode` |\n| 5 | Session | User session management | `enableUserAccounts` |\n| 6 | CSRF Protection | Prevent cross-site attacks | `disableCsrfProtection` |\n| 7 | Body Parsers | Parse JSON/form data | Always enabled |\n| 8 | Static Files | Serve public directory | Always enabled |\n| 9 | User Middleware | Attach user context | `enableUserAccounts` |\n| 10 | Rate Limiting | Throttle requests | `rateLimiting` |\n\n**Conditional Middleware:**\n- User accounts and sessions are only initialized if `enableUserAccounts` is true\n- Basic auth checks against per-user credentials if `perUserBasicAuth` is true\n- IP whitelist automatically includes Docker hosts if `whitelistDockerHosts` is true\n\nSources: [src/middleware/whitelist.js:1-130](), [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:1-65](), [src/users.js:1-824]()\n\n---\n\n### User Directory Structure\n\nWhen multi-user mode is enabled, each user gets an isolated directory structure:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    DataRoot[\"DATA_ROOT/\"] --\u003e UserDir[\"user_handle/\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Characters[\"characters/\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Chats[\"chats/\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Groups[\"groups/\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e GroupChats[\"group chats/\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Worlds[\"worlds/\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e UserImages[\"User Avatars/\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Backgrounds[\"backgrounds/\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Themes[\"themes/\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Presets[\"...various preset directories\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Backups[\"backups/\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Secrets[\"secrets.json\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Settings[\"settings.json\"]\n    \n    Characters --\u003e CharPNG[\"character.png files\"]\n    Chats --\u003e ChatSubdir[\"character/\"]\n    ChatSubdir --\u003e JSONL[\"chatname.jsonl\"]\n    Backups --\u003e ChatBackups[\"chat_backups/\"]\n    Backups --\u003e SettingsBackups[\"settings_backups/\"]\n```\n\n**Directory Creation:**\nUser directories are created on-demand using the template defined in [src/constants.js]() as `USER_DIRECTORY_TEMPLATE`. The `getUserDirectories()` function returns a `UserDirectoryList` object with paths to all subdirectories.\n\nSources: [src/users.js:140-221](), [src/constants.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Client Bootstrap Process\n\n### HTML Document Load\n\nThe application waits for the DOM to be fully loaded before starting initialization:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Browser\n    participant index.html\n    participant script.js\n    participant firstLoadInit\n\n    Browser-\u003e\u003eindex.html: Load document\n    index.html-\u003e\u003eindex.html: Parse HTML\n    index.html-\u003e\u003escript.js: Load module\n    script.js-\u003e\u003escript.js: Execute top-level code\n    Note over script.js: Lines 334-340\n    script.js-\u003e\u003eBrowser: Check readyState\n    alt readyState === 'complete'\n        Browser-\u003e\u003efirstLoadInit: resolve() immediately\n    else\n        Browser-\u003e\u003eBrowser: Wait for 'load' event\n        Browser-\u003e\u003efirstLoadInit: resolve() on event\n    end\n    firstLoadInit-\u003e\u003efirstLoadInit: Begin initialization\n```\n\n**Wait Pattern:**\nThe bootstrap uses a Promise that resolves when `document.readyState === 'complete'` or the `window.load` event fires, ensuring all resources are loaded before proceeding.\n\nSources: [public/script.js:334-340](), [public/index.html:1-50]()\n\n---\n\n### Client Initialization Sequence\n\nThe `firstLoadInit()` function orchestrates the entire client-side bootstrap process:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Start[\"firstLoadInit()\"] --\u003e GetToken[\"Fetch CSRF token\\nGET /csrf-token\"]\n    GetToken --\u003e ShowLoader[\"showLoader()\"]\n    ShowLoader --\u003e RegPM[\"registerPromptManagerMigration()\"]\n    RegPM --\u003e InitDOM[\"initDomHandlers()\"]\n    InitDOM --\u003e Standalone[\"initStandaloneMode()\"]\n    Standalone --\u003e LibShims[\"initLibraryShims()\"]\n    LibShims --\u003e Markdown[\"reloadMarkdownProcessor()\"]\n    Markdown --\u003e BrowserFix[\"applyBrowserFixes()\"]\n    BrowserFix --\u003e Version[\"getClientVersion()\"]\n    Version --\u003e Secrets[\"initSecrets() + readSecretState()\"]\n    Secrets --\u003e Locales[\"initLocales()\"]\n    Locales --\u003e SlashCmds[\"initDefaultSlashCommands()\"]\n    SlashCmds --\u003e APIs[\"Initialize API modules\\n(OpenAI, TextGen, etc.)\"]\n    APIs --\u003e Extensions[\"initExtensions()\"]\n    Extensions --\u003e PresetMgr[\"initPresetManager()\"]\n    PresetMgr --\u003e GetSettings[\"getSettings()\"]\n    GetSettings --\u003e UIInit[\"Initialize UI systems\\n(keyboard, styles, tags)\"]\n    UIInit --\u003e DataLoad[\"Load data\\n(avatars, characters, backgrounds)\"]\n    DataLoad --\u003e Subsystems[\"Initialize subsystems\\n(tokenizers, world info, etc.)\"]\n    Subsystems --\u003e AppInit[\"eventSource.emit(APP_INITIALIZED)\"]\n    AppInit --\u003e HideLoader[\"hideLoader()\"]\n    HideLoader --\u003e AppReady[\"eventSource.emit(APP_READY)\"]\n    AppReady --\u003e End[\"Initialization complete\"]\n```\n\n**Initialization Phases:**\n\n1. **Security \u0026 Configuration** (Lines 668-688)\n   - Fetch CSRF token from `/csrf-token`\n   - Register prompt manager migrations\n   - Initialize DOM handlers\n   - Detect and configure standalone/PWA mode\n   - Load client version information\n\n2. **Core Systems** (Lines 689-702)\n   - Initialize secrets management\n   - Load localization strings\n   - Initialize chat utilities\n   - Register default slash commands\n   - Initialize text generation models\n   - Initialize API integrations (OpenAI, Kobold, NovelAI, Horde)\n\n3. **Extension Framework** (Lines 697-701)\n   - Initialize system prompts\n   - Load and initialize extensions\n   - Register extension slash commands\n   - Initialize tool calling system\n   - Load preset manager\n   - Initialize system messages\n\n4. **Settings \u0026 UI** (Lines 702-705)\n   - Load user settings via `getSettings()`\n   - Initialize keyboard navigation\n   - Initialize dynamic styles\n   - Initialize tag system\n   - Initialize bookmarks\n\n5. **Data Loading** (Lines 707-710)\n   - Get user avatars\n   - Load character list\n   - Load backgrounds\n   - Initialize tokenizers\n\n6. **Subsystems** (Lines 711-732)\n   - Initialize backgrounds UI\n   - Initialize author's note system\n   - Initialize personas\n   - Initialize slash command autocomplete\n   - Initialize macro autocomplete\n   - Initialize world info\n   - Initialize various features (Horde, stats, CFG, logprobs, etc.)\n\n7. **Finalization** (Lines 733-738)\n   - Emit `APP_INITIALIZED` event\n   - Hide loader\n   - Fix viewport for mobile\n   - Emit `APP_READY` event\n\nSources: [public/script.js:666-739]()\n\n---\n\n### Critical Initialization Functions\n\nThe following table lists key initialization functions called during bootstrap:\n\n| Function | Module | Purpose | Async |\n|----------|--------|---------|-------|\n| `getClientVersion()` | script.js | Fetch version from `/version` endpoint | Yes |\n| `initSecrets()` | secrets.js | Set up secrets storage | Yes |\n| `initLocales()` | i18n.js | Load translation strings | Yes |\n| `initDefaultSlashCommands()` | slash-commands.js | Register built-in commands | No |\n| `initOpenAI()` | openai.js | Initialize OpenAI integration | No |\n| `initExtensions()` | extensions.js | Load extension manifests | No |\n| `initPresetManager()` | preset-manager.js | Load preset definitions | Yes |\n| `getSettings()` | script.js | Load user settings from server | Yes |\n| `getCharacters()` | script.js | Load character list | Yes |\n| `initTokenizers()` | tokenizers.js | Load tokenizer models | Yes |\n| `initWorldInfo()` | world-info.js | Initialize lorebook system | No |\n\n**Async Operations:**\nMany initialization functions are async and must complete before dependent systems can initialize. The bootstrap sequence carefully orders these dependencies to avoid race conditions.\n\nSources: [public/script.js:666-739]()\n\n---\n\n### Settings Loading and Migration\n\nUser settings are loaded from the server and undergo migration if necessary:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client as script.js\n    participant Server as /api/settings/get\n    participant File as settings.json\n    participant Migration as Migration Logic\n\n    Client-\u003e\u003eServer: POST /api/settings/get\n    Server-\u003e\u003eFile: Read user settings\n    File--\u003e\u003eServer: Raw settings JSON\n    Server--\u003e\u003eClient: Settings object\n    Client-\u003e\u003eMigration: Check version/schema\n    alt Settings outdated\n        Migration-\u003e\u003eMigration: Apply migrations\n        Migration-\u003e\u003eClient: Updated settings\n        Client-\u003e\u003eClient: saveSettings()\n    else Settings current\n        Client-\u003e\u003eClient: Use as-is\n    end\n    Client-\u003e\u003eClient: Apply settings to UI\n    Client-\u003e\u003eClient: Trigger dependent inits\n```\n\n**Settings Structure:**\n- `settings.json` - Core application settings\n- `extension_settings` - Extension-specific settings (nested)\n- `power_user` - Advanced user preferences\n- `api_settings` - API connection settings\n\n**Migration Pattern:**\nSettings migrations are performed inline during load. Each subsystem may apply its own migrations (e.g., `migrateInstructModeSettings()` in instruct-mode.js).\n\nSources: [public/script.js:1100-1500](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:1-134]()\n\n---\n\n### Character List Loading\n\nCharacter data can be loaded in two modes depending on configuration:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Start[\"getCharacters()\"] --\u003e CheckMode{\"useShallowCharacters\\n(config)?\"}\n    CheckMode --\u003e|Yes| ShallowLoad[\"Load shallow character data\\n(name, avatar, metadata only)\"]\n    CheckMode --\u003e|No| FullLoad[\"Load full character cards\"]\n    \n    ShallowLoad --\u003e APICall1[\"POST /api/characters/all\"]\n    FullLoad --\u003e APICall2[\"POST /api/characters/all\"]\n    \n    APICall1 --\u003e Server1[\"Server: readShallowCharacters()\"]\n    APICall2 --\u003e Server2[\"Server: readFullCharacters()\"]\n    \n    Server1 --\u003e Cache1{\"Check\\nDiskCache\"}\n    Server2 --\u003e Cache2{\"Check\\nMemoryCache\"}\n    \n    Cache1 --\u003e|Hit| Return1[\"Return cached data\"]\n    Cache1 --\u003e|Miss| ReadPNG1[\"Read PNG metadata only\"]\n    \n    Cache2 --\u003e|Hit| Return2[\"Return cached data\"]\n    Cache2 --\u003e|Miss| ParseCard[\"Parse full character card\"]\n    \n    ReadPNG1 --\u003e CacheWrite1[\"Write to DiskCache\"]\n    ParseCard --\u003e CacheWrite2[\"Write to MemoryCache\"]\n    \n    CacheWrite1 --\u003e Return1\n    CacheWrite2 --\u003e Return2\n    \n    Return1 --\u003e PopulateUI[\"Populate character list UI\"]\n    Return2 --\u003e PopulateUI\n    \n    PopulateUI --\u003e PrintChars[\"printCharacters()\"]\n```\n\n**Performance Optimization:**\n- **Lazy Loading**: When `lazyLoadCharacters` is enabled in config, only minimal metadata is loaded initially\n- **Disk Cache**: Character data is cached on disk in `DATA_ROOT/_cache/characters/` (see `DiskCache` class)\n- **Memory Cache**: Parsed character cards are cached in a `MemoryLimitedMap` (default 100MB capacity)\n- **Unshallowing**: When a lazy-loaded character is selected, `unshallowCharacter()` loads the full card on-demand\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-209](), [public/script.js:1700-2000]()\n\n---\n\n## Lifecycle Events\n\nThe event system provides hooks for extensions and subsystems to react to initialization milestones:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Init as firstLoadInit()\n    participant EventSource as eventSource\n    participant Extensions as Extension Listeners\n    participant UI as UI Systems\n\n    Init-\u003e\u003eEventSource: emit(APP_INITIALIZED)\n    EventSource-\u003e\u003eExtensions: Notify listeners\n    Extensions-\u003e\u003eExtensions: Perform post-init setup\n    Extensions--\u003e\u003eEventSource: Complete\n    \n    Init-\u003e\u003eInit: hideLoader()\n    Init-\u003e\u003eInit: fixViewport()\n    \n    Init-\u003e\u003eEventSource: emit(APP_READY)\n    EventSource-\u003e\u003eUI: Notify listeners\n    UI-\u003e\u003eUI: Enable user interaction\n    EventSource-\u003e\u003eExtensions: Final setup\n```\n\n**Key Lifecycle Events:**\n\n| Event | Timing | Purpose |\n|-------|--------|---------|\n| `APP_INITIALIZED` | After all core systems loaded | Extensions can complete initialization |\n| `APP_READY` | After loader hidden | Application ready for user interaction |\n| `SETTINGS_LOADED_BEFORE` | Before settings applied | Intercept settings loading |\n| `SETTINGS_LOADED_AFTER` | After settings applied | React to settings changes |\n| `CHARACTER_PAGE_LOADED` | After character list rendered | UI customization hooks |\n\nExtensions register listeners via `eventSource.on(event_type, callback)` during their initialization.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/events.js](), [public/script.js:735-738]()\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling During Initialization\n\nThe bootstrap process includes safeguards against initialization failures:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Start[\"firstLoadInit()\"] --\u003e TryCatch[\"try-catch wrapper\"]\n    TryCatch --\u003e GetToken[\"Get CSRF token\"]\n    GetToken --\u003e TokenFail{\"Token\\nfetch fails?\"}\n    TokenFail --\u003e|Yes| ShowError[\"toastr.error()\\n'Couldn't get CSRF token'\"]\n    ShowError --\u003e ThrowError[\"throw new Error('Init failed')\"]\n    TokenFail --\u003e|No| Continue[\"Continue initialization\"]\n    Continue --\u003e AnyFail{\"Any step\\nfails?\"}\n    AnyFail --\u003e|Yes| LogError[\"console.error()\"]\n    LogError --\u003e PartialInit[\"Partial initialization\\n(may be unstable)\"]\n    AnyFail --\u003e|No| Complete[\"Complete successfully\"]\n```\n\n**Critical Failure Points:**\n1. **CSRF Token Failure** (Lines 668-674) - Blocks all initialization, user must refresh\n2. **Settings Load Failure** - Falls back to default settings\n3. **Character Load Failure** - Displays empty character list\n4. **Extension Load Failure** - Logs error but continues (non-blocking)\n\n**Recovery Mechanisms:**\n- Most initialization steps are independent and won't crash the entire application\n- Failed async operations log errors to console but don't propagate exceptions\n- User is notified via toastr notifications for critical failures\n\nSources: [public/script.js:666-674]()\n\n---\n\n## Module Dependency Graph\n\nThe following diagram shows initialization dependencies between major subsystems:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    CSRF[\"CSRF Token\"] --\u003e Settings[\"Settings\"]\n    Secrets[\"Secrets\"] --\u003e APIs[\"API Integrations\"]\n    Locales[\"Locales i18n\"] --\u003e UI[\"UI Systems\"]\n    Settings --\u003e PowerUser[\"Power User Config\"]\n    Settings --\u003e APISettings[\"API Settings\"]\n    \n    APIs --\u003e OpenAI[\"OpenAI\"]\n    APIs --\u003e TextGen[\"Text Generation\"]\n    APIs --\u003e Kobold[\"KoboldAI\"]\n    APIs --\u003e Novel[\"NovelAI\"]\n    APIs --\u003e Horde[\"AI Horde\"]\n    \n    PowerUser --\u003e PromptManager[\"Prompt Manager\"]\n    PowerUser --\u003e InstructMode[\"Instruct Mode\"]\n    PowerUser --\u003e ContextPresets[\"Context Presets\"]\n    \n    Tokenizers[\"Tokenizers\"] --\u003e WorldInfo[\"World Info\"]\n    Tokenizers --\u003e PromptManager\n    \n    Characters[\"Character List\"] --\u003e Tags[\"Tag System\"]\n    Characters --\u003e Groups[\"Group Chats\"]\n    \n    Personas[\"Personas\"] --\u003e Characters\n    \n    Extensions[\"Extension Framework\"] --\u003e ExtModules[\"Extension Modules\"]\n    ExtModules --\u003e Memory[\"Memory Extension\"]\n    ExtModules --\u003e Vectors[\"Vectors Extension\"]\n    ExtModules --\u003e SD[\"Stable Diffusion\"]\n    ExtModules --\u003e Expressions[\"Expressions\"]\n    ExtModules --\u003e QuickReply[\"Quick Reply\"]\n    \n    SlashCommands[\"Slash Commands\"] --\u003e Extensions\n    SlashCommands --\u003e MacroSystem[\"Macro System\"]\n    \n    Settings --\u003e Complete[\"APP_INITIALIZED\"]\n    APIs --\u003e Complete\n    Characters --\u003e Complete\n    Extensions --\u003e Complete\n    \n    Complete --\u003e UIReady[\"hideLoader()\"]\n    UIReady --\u003e AppReady[\"APP_READY\"]\n```\n\n**Initialization Order Constraints:**\n- **Settings** must load before any system that reads configuration\n- **Tokenizers** must initialize before World Info or Prompt Manager\n- **Extensions** must load after core systems (slash commands, APIs) are ready\n- **Character list** can load in parallel with other systems\n\nSources: [public/script.js:666-739]()\n\n---\n\n## Post-Installation Setup\n\nBefore the first server start, `post-install.js` performs one-time setup tasks:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Start[\"npm install\\ncomplete\"] --\u003e RunPost[\"post-install.js\"]\n    RunPost --\u003e ConvertConfig[\"convertConfig()\"]\n    ConvertConfig --\u003e CheckOld{\"config.conf\\nexists?\"}\n    CheckOld --\u003e|Yes| MigrateYAML[\"Convert to config.yaml\"]\n    CheckOld --\u003e|No| SkipConvert[\"Skip conversion\"]\n    \n    MigrateYAML --\u003e CreateFiles[\"createDefaultFiles()\"]\n    SkipConvert --\u003e CreateFiles\n    \n    CreateFiles --\u003e CheckConfig{\"config.yaml\\nexists?\"}\n    CheckConfig --\u003e|No| CopyDefault[\"Copy default/config.yaml\"]\n    CheckConfig --\u003e|Yes| AddMissing[\"addMissingConfigValues()\"]\n    \n    CopyDefault --\u003e CheckSecrets\n    AddMissing --\u003e CheckSecrets{\"secrets.json\\nexists?\"}\n    \n    CheckSecrets --\u003e|No| CreateSecrets[\"Create empty secrets file\"]\n    CheckSecrets --\u003e|Yes| Done[\"Setup complete\"]\n    CreateSecrets --\u003e Done\n```\n\n**Setup Tasks:**\n1. Convert legacy `config.conf` to `config.yaml` format (if present)\n2. Copy default config file if none exists\n3. Merge missing keys from default config into existing config\n4. Create empty `secrets.json` if needed\n5. Create user data directories\n\nSources: [post-install.js:1-69]()\n\n---\n\n## Debug and Development Features\n\nSeveral debug functions are registered during initialization for development purposes:\n\n**Debug Functions** (registered via `registerDebugFunction()`):\n- **Character unshallowing**: Force load full character data\n- **Token cache inspection**: View tokenizer cache contents  \n- **Settings validation**: Check settings schema\n- **Event listener inspection**: View registered event handlers\n\nThese functions are accessible via browser console in development mode.\n\nSources: [public/script.js:733](), [public/scripts/power-user.js:2500-2700]()\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\nThe SillyTavern initialization process follows a carefully orchestrated sequence:\n\n1. **Server starts**, loads configuration, initializes Express middleware stack\n2. **Client loads**, waits for DOM ready, fetches CSRF token\n3. **Core systems initialize** in dependency order (secrets, locales, APIs)\n4. **Extension framework loads**, giving extensions access to core APIs\n5. **Data loads** (characters, settings, backgrounds) with caching for performance\n6. **Lifecycle events fire** (`APP_INITIALIZED`, `APP_READY`) allowing extensions to complete setup\n7. **Application enters ready state**, enabling user interaction\n\nThe modular architecture allows systems to initialize independently while respecting dependencies, with robust error handling to gracefully degrade on partial files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [default/config.yaml](default/config.yaml)\n-
