[src/endpoints/settings.js](src/endpoints/settings.js)\n- [src/middleware/basicAuth.js](src/middleware/basicAuth.js)\n- [src/middleware/whitelist.js](src/middleware/whitelist.js)\n- [src/users.js](src/users.js)\n- [src/util.js](src/util.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document describes the Node.js/Express server architecture, including the initialization sequence, middleware stack, API endpoint organization, and routing patterns. For information about client-side architecture, see [Main UI Components and Layout](#4.1). For details about data storage formats, see [Data Management](#5).\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis page covers:\n- Server entry point and initialization flow\n- Configuration loading and environment variables\n- Middleware stack including security layers\n- API endpoint structure and routing\n- User directory organization\n- Request processing pipeline\n\n---\n\n## Server Entry Point and Initialization\n\n### Bootstrap Sequence\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant CLI as server.js\n    participant Parser as CommandLineParser\n    participant Main as server-main.js\n    participant Config as config.yaml\n    participant App as Express App\n\n    CLI-\u003e\u003eParser: parse(process.argv)\n    Parser--\u003e\u003eCLI: cliArgs\n    CLI-\u003e\u003eCLI: Set globalThis.DATA_ROOT\n    CLI-\u003e\u003eCLI: Set globalThis.COMMAND_LINE_ARGS\n    CLI-\u003e\u003eCLI: process.chdir(serverDirectory)\n    CLI-\u003e\u003eMain: await import()\n    Main-\u003e\u003eConfig: Load configuration\n    Main-\u003e\u003eApp: Initialize Express\n    Main-\u003e\u003eApp: Mount middleware\n    Main-\u003e\u003eApp: Mount API routers\n    Main-\u003e\u003eApp: Start listening\n```\n\nThe server initialization follows a sequential bootstrap process starting from [server.js:1-17]():\n\n1. **Command Line Parsing**: The `CommandLineParser` class processes arguments including `--dataRoot`, `--port`, `--listen`, and other runtime options\n2. **Global State Setup**: Sets `globalThis.DATA_ROOT` and `globalThis.COMMAND_LINE_ARGS` for application-wide access\n3. **Working Directory**: Changes to the server directory for consistent path resolution\n4. **Main Module Import**: Dynamically imports `src/server-main.js` which contains the Express application setup\n\n### Configuration System\n\nConfiguration is loaded from `config.yaml` with support for environment variable overrides. The configuration system is implemented in [src/util.js:44-119]():\n\n| Function | Purpose |\n|----------|---------|\n| `setConfigFilePath()` | Establishes the path to the config file (called once at startup) |\n| `getConfig()` | Returns the cached configuration object, parsing from YAML if not loaded |\n| `getConfigValue(key, defaultValue, typeConverter)` | Retrieves a specific config value with environment variable precedence |\n| `keyToEnv(key)` | Converts dotted config keys to environment variable names (e.g., `port` → `SILLYTAVERN_PORT`) |\n\n**Environment Variable Precedence**: Environment variables prefixed with `SILLYTAVERN_` override config.yaml values. For example, `SILLYTAVERN_PORT=9000` overrides `port: 8000` in the config file.\n\n**Sources**: [server.js:1-17](), [src/util.js:44-119](), [default/config.yaml:1-327]()\n\n---\n\n## Middleware Stack\n\nThe Express middleware stack processes all incoming requests in a specific order, providing security, authentication, and request context enrichment.\n\n### Middleware Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Request[\"Incoming Request\"]\n    Whitelist[\"IP Whitelist Middleware\u003cbr/\u003esrc/middleware/whitelist.js\"]\n    BasicAuth[\"Basic Authentication\u003cbr/\u003esrc/middleware/basicAuth.js\"]\n    UserSession[\"User Session Handler\u003cbr/\u003esrc/users.js\"]\n    CSRF[\"CSRF Protection\u003cbr/\u003eDouble-submit cookies\"]\n    CORS[\"CORS Middleware\u003cbr/\u003eConfigured via config.yaml\"]\n    HostWhitelist[\"Host Header Validation\"]\n    RateLimit[\"Rate Limiting\"]\n    Router[\"API Router\"]\n    \n    Request --\u003e Whitelist\n    Whitelist --\u003e|Allowed| BasicAuth\n    Whitelist --\u003e|Blocked| Reject403[\"403 Forbidden\"]\n    BasicAuth --\u003e|Authenticated| UserSession\n    BasicAuth --\u003e|Unauthorized| Reject401[\"401 Unauthorized\"]\n    UserSession --\u003e CSRF\n    CSRF --\u003e CORS\n    CORS --\u003e HostWhitelist\n    HostWhitelist --\u003e RateLimit\n    RateLimit --\u003e Router\n```\n\n### Middleware Layers\n\n#### 1. IP Whitelist Middleware\n\nEnforces IP-based access control when `whitelistMode` is enabled. Implementation in [src/middleware/whitelist.js:101-148]():\n\n**Key Features**:\n- Validates client IP against whitelist entries (supports CIDR notation)\n- Checks `X-Real-IP` and `X-Forwarded-For` headers when `enableForwardedWhitelist` is true\n- Automatically adds Docker host IPs when running in Docker (`whitelistDockerHosts`)\n- Returns 403 Forbidden with custom HTML page for rejected connections\n\n**Configuration**: Whitelist entries are loaded from both `config.yaml` (`whitelist` array) and `whitelist.txt` file if present.\n\n#### 2. Basic Authentication Middleware\n\nProvides HTTP Basic Authentication when `basicAuthMode` is enabled. Implementation in [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:13-54]():\n\n**Two Authentication Modes**:\n- **Global Mode** (`perUserBasicAuth: false`): Single username/password from `basicAuthUser` config\n- **Per-User Mode** (`perUserBasicAuth: true`): Validates against individual user account credentials\n\nThe middleware sets `WWW-Authenticate` header and returns 401 status with custom HTML for failed authentication.\n\n#### 3. User Session Handler\n\nExtracts and validates user sessions, establishing `request.user` context. The user session system provides:\n\n- **Session Management**: Cookie-based sessions with configurable timeout (`sessionTimeout` in config)\n- **User Directory Resolution**: Populates `request.user.directories` with per-user file paths (see [User Directory Structure](#user-directory-structure))\n- **Handle Extraction**: Identifies the user handle from session or defaults to `DEFAULT_USER`\n\n#### 4. CSRF Protection\n\nProtects against Cross-Site Request Forgery attacks using double-submit cookie pattern. Can be disabled with `disableCsrfProtection: true` (not recommended).\n\n#### 5. CORS Middleware\n\nConfigures Cross-Origin Resource Sharing based on `cors` settings in config.yaml. Allows browser-based clients to access the API.\n\n**Sources**: [src/middleware/whitelist.js:1-149](), [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:1-57](), [default/config.yaml:56-111]()\n\n---\n\n## API Endpoint Organization\n\nAPI endpoints are organized as modular Express routers, each handling a specific domain of functionality. All endpoints are mounted under the `/api` prefix.\n\n### Endpoint Structure\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    App[\"Express App\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"API Routers\"\n        Characters[\"/api/characters\u003cbr/\u003echaracters.js\"]\n        Chats[\"/api/chats\u003cbr/\u003echats.js\"]\n        Settings[\"/api/settings\u003cbr/\u003esettings.js\"]\n        Groups[\"/api/groups\u003cbr/\u003egroups.js\"]\n        Presets[\"/api/presets\u003cbr/\u003epresets.js\"]\n        Content[\"/api/content\u003cbr/\u003econtent-manager.js\"]\n        Secrets[\"/api/secrets\u003cbr/\u003esecrets.js\"]\n        Sprites[\"/api/sprites\u003cbr/\u003esprites.js\"]\n        Backends[\"/api/backends\u003cbr/\u003echat-completions.js\u003cbr/\u003etext-completions.js\"]\n        WorldInfo[\"/api/worldinfo\u003cbr/\u003eworldinfo.js\"]\n        Users[\"/api/users\u003cbr/\u003eusers.js\"]\n    end\n    \n    App --\u003e Characters\n    App --\u003e Chats\n    App --\u003e Settings\n    App --\u003e Groups\n    App --\u003e Presets\n    App --\u003e Content\n    App --\u003e Secrets\n    App --\u003e Sprites\n    App --\u003e Backends\n    App --\u003e WorldInfo\n    App --\u003e Users\n```\n\n### Core Endpoint Routers\n\n#### Characters Router (`/api/characters/*`)\n\nHandles character card management including CRUD operations, imports, and exports. Key endpoints from [src/endpoints/characters.js]:\n\n| Route | Method | Purpose |\n|-------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/characters/all` | GET | List all characters with metadata |\n| `/api/characters/get` | POST | Get a specific character by avatar |\n| `/api/characters/create` | POST | Create a new character |\n| `/api/characters/edit` | POST | Update character data |\n| `/api/characters/delete` | POST | Delete a character |\n| `/api/characters/import` | POST | Import from PNG, JSON, YAML, ZIP formats |\n| `/api/characters/export` | POST | Export character to file |\n| `/api/characters/chats` | POST | List chats for a character |\n\n**Caching Architecture**: [src/endpoints/characters.js:29-157]()\n- **Memory Cache**: `MemoryLimitedMap` with configurable capacity (`performance.memoryCacheCapacity`)\n- **Disk Cache**: Persistent storage using `node-persist` in `_cache/characters/`\n- **Cache Keys**: Based on file path and modification time (`${filePath}-${mtimeMs}`)\n- **Lazy Loading**: Optional shallow character data (`performance.lazyLoadCharacters`)\n\n**Character Processing**: The `processCharacter()` function [src/endpoints/characters.js:406-442]() reads PNG files with embedded JSON, calculates chat statistics, and converts to Spec V2 format.\n\n#### Chats Router (`/api/chats/*`)\n\nManages chat storage, retrieval, and backup. Implementation in [src/endpoints/chats.js]:\n\n| Route | Method | Purpose |\n|-------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/chats/save` | POST | Save chat to JSONL file |\n| `/api/chats/get` | POST | Load chat by file name |\n| `/api/chats/rename` | POST | Rename a chat file |\n| `/api/chats/delete` | POST | Delete a chat file |\n| `/api/chats/export` | POST | Export chat to JSONL or plain text |\n| `/api/chats/import` | POST | Import chat from various formats |\n| `/api/chats/group/save` | POST | Save group chat |\n| `/api/chats/group/get` | POST | Load group chat |\n\n**Chat File Format**: JSONL (JSON Lines) - one JSON object per line. First line contains metadata header, subsequent lines are messages.\n\n**Backup System**: [src/endpoints/chats.js:40-77]()\n- Throttled backup function per user (configurable `backups.chat.throttleInterval`)\n- Automatic backup on save operations\n- Timestamped backup files with prefix `chat_{name}_`\n- Configurable retention (`backups.common.numberOfBackups`)\n- Optional integrity checking (`backups.chat.checkIntegrity`)\n\n**Integrity Checking**: [src/endpoints/chats.js:315-334]() validates that the chat being saved matches the loaded version by comparing `chat_metadata.integrity` slugs, preventing concurrent edit conflicts.\n\n#### Settings Router (`/api/settings/*`)\n\nHandles user settings persistence and preset management. Key routes from [src/endpoints/settings.js]:\n\n| Route | Method | Purpose |\n|-------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/settings/get` | POST | Load user settings and all presets |\n| `/api/settings/save` | POST | Save user settings JSON |\n| `/api/settings/get-snapshots` | POST | List settings backups |\n| `/api/settings/load-snapshot` | POST | Load a specific backup |\n| `/api/settings/make-snapshot` | POST | Create manual backup |\n| `/api/settings/restore-snapshot` | POST | Restore from backup |\n\n**Settings Structure**: The `/api/settings/get` endpoint returns a comprehensive object containing:\n- User settings (`settings.json`)\n- All API presets (KoboldAI, NovelAI, OpenAI, TextGen)\n- World info file names\n- Themes, moving UI presets, quick replies\n- Instruct mode templates, context templates, system prompts, reasoning templates\n- Feature flags (`enable_extensions`, `enable_accounts`)\n\n**Auto-backup**: [src/endpoints/settings.js:31-41]() Throttled auto-save every 10 minutes when settings are modified, with duplicate detection to avoid unnecessary backups.\n\n#### Groups Router (`/api/groups/*`)\n\nManages group chat metadata and operations from [src/endpoints/groups.js]:\n\n| Route | Method | Purpose |\n|-------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/groups/all` | POST | List all groups with metadata |\n| `/api/groups/create` | POST | Create new group |\n| `/api/groups/edit` | POST | Update group metadata |\n| `/api/groups/delete` | POST | Delete group and all its chats |\n\n**Group Metadata Format**: JSON files in the `groups/` directory containing:\n- Group ID, name, avatar\n- Member character avatars\n- Generation mode and activation strategy\n- Current chat ID and chat history\n- Disabled members list\n\n**Metadata Migration**: [src/endpoints/groups.js:34-111]() automatically migrates legacy group metadata format (where metadata was stored in the group file) to the new format (where each group chat JSONL has its own metadata header).\n\n#### Presets Router (`/api/presets/*`)\n\nProvides preset CRUD operations for various API types. Routes from [src/endpoints/presets.js]:\n\n| Route | Method | Purpose |\n|-------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/presets/save` | POST | Save a preset for specified API |\n| `/api/presets/delete` | POST | Delete a preset |\n| `/api/presets/restore` | POST | Restore default preset |\n\n**API ID Mapping**: [src/endpoints/presets.js:16-38]() maps API identifiers to directory paths:\n- `kobold` / `koboldhorde` → `koboldAI_Settings/`\n- `novel` → `novelAI_Settings/`\n- `textgenerationwebui` → `textGen_Settings/`\n- `openai` → `openAI_Settings/`\n- `instruct` → `instruct/`\n- `context` → `context/`\n- `sysprompt` → `sysprompt/`\n- `reasoning` → `reasoning/`\n\n#### Content Manager Router (`/api/content/*`)\n\nHandles default content seeding and asset management from [src/endpoints/content-manager.js]. Key functions:\n\n**Content Seeding**: [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:111-163]()\n- Reads `default/content/index.json` and `default/scaffold/index.json`\n- Copies new default content to user directories\n- Tracks seeded content in `{userRoot}/content.log`\n- Supports forced re-seeding of specific categories\n\n**Content Types**: [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:37-56]()\n```\nSETTINGS, CHARACTER, SPRITES, BACKGROUND, WORLD, AVATAR, THEME, \nWORKFLOW, KOBOLD_PRESET, OPENAI_PRESET, NOVEL_PRESET, TEXTGEN_PRESET,\nINSTRUCT, CONTEXT, MOVING_UI, QUICK_REPLIES, SYSPROMPT, REASONING\n```\n\n**Character Downloads**: Supports importing characters from:\n- Chub.ai (`downloadChubCharacter`)\n- Pygmalion (`downloadPygmalionCharacter`)\n- Generic URLs (with whitelist validation)\n\n**Sources**: [src/endpoints/characters.js:1-100](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:1-100](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:1-100](), [src/endpoints/groups.js:1-100](), [src/endpoints/presets.js:1-104](), [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:1-100]()\n\n---\n\n## User Directory Structure\n\nEach user has a hierarchical directory structure under `DATA_ROOT` that isolates their data. The directory layout is defined in [src/users.js] and follows the `USER_DIRECTORY_TEMPLATE`.\n\n### Directory Hierarchy\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    DataRoot[\"DATA_ROOT/\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Per-User Structure\"\n        UserRoot[\"handle/\u003cbr/\u003e(User root directory)\"]\n        \n        Thumbnails[\"thumbnails/\"]\n        ThumbnailsBg[\"thumbnails/bg/\"]\n        ThumbnailsAvatar[\"thumbnails/avatar/\"]\n        ThumbnailsPersona[\"thumbnails/persona/\"]\n        \n        Characters[\"characters/\u003cbr/\u003e(Character PNGs)\"]\n        Chats[\"chats/\u003cbr/\u003e(JSONL chat logs)\"]\n        Groups[\"groups/\u003cbr/\u003e(Group metadata)\"]\n        GroupChats[\"group chats/\u003cbr/\u003e(Group JSONL logs)\"]\n        \n        Worlds[\"worlds/\u003cbr/\u003e(World Info JSON)\"]\n        Avatars[\"User Avatars/\u003cbr/\u003e(User persona images)\"]\n        UserImages[\"user/\u003cbr/\u003e(Uploaded images)\"]\n        Backgrounds[\"backgrounds/\u003cbr/\u003e(Chat backgrounds)\"]\n        \n        Settings[\"OpenAI Settings/\"]\n        SettingsKobold[\"KoboldAI Settings/\"]\n        SettingsNovel[\"NovelAI Settings/\"]\n        SettingsTextGen[\"TextGen Settings/\"]\n        \n        Instruct[\"instruct/\u003cbr/\u003e(Instruct templates)\"]\n        Context[\"context/\u003cbr/\u003e(Context templates)\"]\n        Sysprompt[\"sysprompt/\u003cbr/\u003e(System prompts)\"]\n        Reasoning[\"reasoning/\u003cbr/\u003e(Reasoning templates)\"]\n        \n        QuickReplies[\"QuickReplies/\"]\n        Themes[\"themes/\"]\n        MovingUI[\"movingUI/\"]\n        Extensions[\"scripts/extensions/third-party/\"]\n        \n        ComfyWorkflows[\"comfyWorkflows/\"]\n        Assets[\"assets/\"]\n        Files[\"files/\u003cbr/\u003e(Data bank attachments)\"]\n        Vectors[\"vectors/\u003cbr/\u003e(Vector storage)\"]\n        Backups[\"backups/\u003cbr/\u003e(Timestamped backups)\"]\n        \n        RootFiles[\"settings.json\u003cbr/\u003esecrets.json\u003cbr/\u003estats.json\u003cbr/\u003econtent.log\"]\n    end\n    \n    DataRoot --\u003e UserRoot\n    UserRoot --\u003e Thumbnails\n    UserRoot --\u003e Characters\n    UserRoot --\u003e Chats\n    UserRoot --\u003e Groups\n    UserRoot --\u003e GroupChats\n    UserRoot --\u003e Worlds\n    UserRoot --\u003e Avatars\n    UserRoot --\u003e UserImages\n    UserRoot --\u003e Backgrounds\n    UserRoot --\u003e Settings\n    UserRoot --\u003e SettingsKobold\n    UserRoot --\u003e SettingsNovel\n    UserRoot --\u003e SettingsTextGen\n    UserRoot --\u003e Instruct\n    UserRoot --\u003e Context\n    UserRoot --\u003e Sysprompt\n    UserRoot --\u003e Reasoning\n    UserRoot --\u003e QuickReplies\n    UserRoot --\u003e Themes\n    UserRoot --\u003e MovingUI\n    UserRoot --\u003e Extensions\n    UserRoot --\u003e ComfyWorkflows\n    UserRoot --\u003e Assets\n    UserRoot --\u003e Files\n    UserRoot --\u003e Vectors\n    UserRoot --\u003e Backups\n    UserRoot --\u003e RootFiles\n    \n    Thumbnails --\u003e ThumbnailsBg\n    Thumbnails --\u003e ThumbnailsAvatar\n    Thumbnails --\u003e ThumbnailsPersona\n```\n\n### User Directory List Type\n\nThe `UserDirectoryList` type [src/users.js:71-103]() defines the complete directory structure:\n\n| Directory Property | Path Example | Purpose |\n|-------------------|--------------|---------|\n| `root` | `data/default/` | Base directory for user |\n| `characters` | `data/default/characters/` | Character PNG cards with embedded JSON |\n| `chats` | `data/default/chats/` | Per-character chat subdirectories |\n| `groups` | `data/default/groups/` | Group metadata JSON files |\n| `groupChats` | `data/default/group chats/` | Group chat JSONL files |\n| `worlds` | `data/default/worlds/` | World Info JSON databases |\n| `avatars` | `data/default/User Avatars/` | User persona avatar images |\n| `userImages` | `data/default/user/` | Uploaded images and media |\n| `backgrounds` | `data/default/backgrounds/` | Chat background images |\n| `thumbnails` | `data/default/thumbnails/` | Generated thumbnail cache |\n| `openAI_Settings` | `data/default/OpenAI Settings/` | OpenAI API presets |\n| `koboldAI_Settings` | `data/default/KoboldAI Settings/` | KoboldAI presets |\n| `novelAI_Settings` | `data/default/NovelAI Settings/` | NovelAI presets |\n| `textGen_Settings` | `data/default/TextGen Settings/` | Text generation presets |\n| `instruct` | `data/default/instruct/` | Instruct mode templates |\n| `context` | `data/default/context/` | Context templates |\n| `sysprompt` | `data/default/sysprompt/` | System prompt templates |\n| `reasoning` | `data/default/reasoning/` | Reasoning templates |\n| `quickreplies` | `data/default/QuickReplies/` | Quick reply presets |\n| `themes` | `data/default/themes/` | UI themes |\n| `movingUI` | `data/default/movingUI/` | MovingUI layout presets |\n| `extensions` | `data/default/scripts/extensions/third-party/` | Third-party extensions |\n| `comfyWorkflows` | `data/default/comfyWorkflows/` | ComfyUI workflow JSON |\n| `assets` | `data/default/assets/` | Sprite sheets and assets |\n| `files` | `data/default/files/` | Data bank file attachments |\n| `vectors` | `data/default/vectors/` | Vector embeddings |\n| `backups` | `data/default/backups/` | Timestamped backup files |\n\n### Directory Resolution\n\nThe `getUserDirectories(handle)` function [src/users.js] returns a cached `UserDirectoryList` for the specified user handle. The cache is populated in [src/users.js:36]() (`DIRECTORIES_CACHE` Map).\n\n**Default User**: When multi-user mode is disabled (`enableUserAccounts: false`), all requests use the `DEFAULT_USER` handle defined in constants.\n\n**Directory Initialization**: [src/users.js:109-126]() ensures all directories exist on startup by calling `ensurePublicDirectoriesExist()` which creates missing directories recursively.\n\n**Sources**: [src/users.js:71-126](), [src/constants.js] (USER_DIRECTORY_TEMPLATE)\n\n---\n\n## Request Processing Flow\n\n### Complete Request Pipeline\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant Express\n    participant Whitelist as Whitelist MW\n    participant Auth as BasicAuth MW\n    participant Session as Session MW\n    participant CSRF as CSRF MW\n    participant Router as API Router\n    participant Endpoint as Endpoint Handler\n    participant Storage as File System\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eExpress: HTTP Request\n    Express-\u003e\u003eWhitelist: IP validation\n    alt IP Whitelisted\n        Whitelist-\u003e\u003eAuth: Continue\n    else IP Blocked\n        Whitelist--\u003e\u003eClient: 403 Forbidden\n    end\n    \n    Auth-\u003e\u003eAuth: Check Authorization header\n    alt Authenticated\n        Auth-\u003e\u003eSession: Continue\n    else No Auth / Invalid\n        Auth--\u003e\u003eClient: 401 Unauthorized\n    end\n    \n    Session-\u003e\u003eSession: Extract user handle\n    Session-\u003e\u003eSession: Load user from storage\n    Session-\u003e\u003eSession: Populate req.user.directories\n    Session-\u003e\u003eCSRF: Continue with user context\n    \n    CSRF-\u003e\u003eCSRF: Validate CSRF token\n    alt Valid Token\n        CSRF-\u003e\u003eRouter: Route request\n    else Invalid Token\n        CSRF--\u003e\u003eClient: 403 Forbidden\n    end\n    \n    Router-\u003e\u003eEndpoint: Match route + method\n    Endpoint-\u003e\u003eEndpoint: Validate request body\n    Endpoint-\u003e\u003eStorage: Read/Write files\n    Storage--\u003e\u003eEndpoint: Operation result\n    Endpoint--\u003e\u003eClient: JSON response\n```\n\n### Request Context Enrichment\n\nEach request that passes through the middleware stack has the following context added to the `request` object:\n\n**`request.user.profile`**:\n- `handle`: User's unique handle (e.g., \"default\")\n- `name`: Display name\n- `admin`: Boolean admin flag\n- `enabled`: Boolean enabled flag\n\n**`request.user.directories`**: Complete `UserDirectoryList` object with all directory paths\n\n**CSRF Token**: Accessible via `request.csrfToken()` for generating tokens to send to client\n\n### Response Patterns\n\nEndpoints follow consistent response patterns:\n\n**Success Responses**:\n```javascript\n// Simple success\nresponse.send({ ok: true });\n\n// Data response\nresponse.json({ characters: [...], count: 42 });\n\n// File download\nresponse.download(filePath);\n\n// Status only\nresponse.sendStatus(204);\n```\n\n**Error Responses**:\n```javascript\n// Client error\nresponse.status(400).send({ error: 'Invalid request' });\n\n// Unauthorized\nresponse.sendStatus(401);\n\n// Not found\nresponse.sendStatus(404);\n\n// Server error\nresponse.status(500).send({ error: 'Operation failed' });\n```\n\n### File Operations\n\nAll file operations use atomic writes via `write-file-atomic` to prevent corruption:\n\n**Write Pattern**: [src/endpoints/characters.js:259]()\n```javascript\nimport { sync as writeFileAtomicSync } from 'write-file-atomic';\n\nwriteFileAtomicSync(outputPath, data, 'utf8');\n```\n\n**Read Pattern**: Error handling with fallback:\n```javascript\nconst data = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');\nconst parsed = JSON.parse(data);\n```\n\n**Safe Helpers**: [src/util.js] provides `tryReadFileSync()` and `tryWriteFileSync()` with automatic error handling.\n\n**Sources**: [src/server-main.js] (inferred), [src/users.js:1-100](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:259]()\n\n---\n\n## Storage Layer Patterns\n\n### Character Storage\n\n**Format**: PNG files with embedded JSON in tEXt chunks via [src/character-card-parser.js]\n\n**Processing Pipeline**:\n1. Read PNG file\n2. Extract JSON from tEXt chunk\n3. Parse and validate character data\n4. Convert to Spec V2 format if needed\n5. Calculate statistics (chat size, last chat date)\n6. Return character object or shallow version\n\n**Shallow Loading**: [src/endpoints/characters.js:371-395]() returns minimal fields for character list display when `performance.lazyLoadCharacters` is enabled:\n- `name`, `avatar`, `chat`, `fav`, `date_added`, `create_date`, `date_last_chat`, `chat_size`, `data_size`, `tags`\n- Nested: `data.name`, `data.character_version`, `data.creator`, `data.creator_notes`, `data.tags`, `data.extensions.fav`\n\n### Chat Storage\n\n**Format**: JSONL (JSON Lines) - one message per line\n\n**Structure**:\n```\nLine 1: { \"chat_metadata\": {...}, \"user_name\": \"...\", \"character_name\": \"...\" }\nLine 2: { \"mes\": \"Hello!\", \"is_user\": true, \"name\": \"User\", ... }\nLine 3: { \"mes\": \"Hi there!\", \"is_user\": false, \"name\": \"Character\", ... }\n...\n```\n\n**Reading**: Stream-based reading via `readline` interface for large files [src/endpoints/chats.js:380-429]()\n\n**Writing**: Array of objects joined with newlines [src/endpoints/chats.js:457]():\n```javascript\nconst jsonlData = chatData.map(m =\u003e JSON.stringify(m)).join('\\n');\n```\n\n### Settings Storage\n\n**Format**: Single `settings.json` file in user root directory\n\n**Backup Strategy**: [src/endpoints/settings.js:132-152]()\n- Auto-backup every 10 minutes (throttled)\n- Manual backup on demand\n- Duplicate detection to avoid redundant backups\n- Timestamped files: `settings_{handle}_{timestamp}.json`\n\n### Secrets Storage\n\nEncrypted storage of API keys and sensitive data. See [Secrets Management](#2.4.2) for details.\n\n**Sources**: [src/endpoints/characters.js:176-209](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:358-430](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:114-152](), [src/character-card-parser.js]\n\n---\n\n## Multi-User Architecture\n\nWhen `enableUserAccounts: true`, the server supports multiple isolated user accounts.\n\n### User Storage System\n\nUser data is persisted using `node-persist` in `DATA_ROOT/_storage/` directory. [src/users.js:510-515]()\n\n**User Record Structure**: [src/users.js:49-57]()\n```javascript\n{\n  handle: \"username\",        // Unique identifier\n  name: \"Display Name\",      // Human-readable name\n  created: 1234567890,       // Timestamp\n  password: \"hash\",          // Scrypt hash\n  salt: \"random\",            // Password salt\n  enabled: true,             // Account status\n  admin: false               // Admin privileges\n}\n```\n\n**Storage Keys**:\n- User data: `user:{handle}`\n- User avatar: `avatar:{handle}`\n- CSRF secret: `csrfSecret`\n- Cookie secret: Stored in `DATA_ROOT/cookie-secret.txt`\n\n### Session Management\n\nSessions are managed via encrypted cookies with configurable timeout [default/config.yaml:144-148]():\n\n| Setting | Default | Description |\n|---------|---------|-------------|\n| `sessionTimeout: -1` | Never expires | Negative values disable expiration |\n| `sessionTimeout: 0` | Browser session | Expires when browser closes |\n| `sessionTimeout: 86400` | 24 hours | Expires after N seconds of inactivity |\n\n**Session Cookie**: Contains encrypted user handle, validated on each request\n\n**Authentication Flow**:\n1. User logs in with credentials\n2. Password hashed with scrypt and compared to stored hash\n3. Session cookie created with encrypted handle\n4. Subsequent requests validate cookie and load user context\n\n### Security Considerations\n\n**IP Whitelisting**: When enabled, restricts access to specified IP addresses/ranges regardless of authentication\n\n**Basic Authentication**: Provides HTTP Basic Auth layer before session authentication\n- Can use global credentials or per-user credentials\n- When combined with per-user accounts, validates against user passwords\n\n**CSRF Protection**: Double-submit cookie pattern prevents cross-site request forgery\n- Disabled for API-only usage with `disableCsrfProtection: true`\n- Tokens validated on state-changing requests (POST, PUT, DELETE)\n\n**Password Hashing**: Uses Node.js crypto scrypt with random salt for secure password storage [src/users.js] (getPasswordHash function)\n\n**Sources**: [src/users.js:24-46](), [src/users.js:510-515](), [default/config.yaml:104-148](), [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:34-52]()\n\n---\n\n## Configuration Reference\n\n### Server Configuration\n\nKey configuration options from [default/config.yaml:1-43]():\n\n| Option | Default | Description |\n|--------|---------|-------------|\n| `dataRoot` | `./data` | Root directory for user data storage |\n| `listen` | `false` | Listen for external connections |\n| `listenAddress.ipv4` | `0.0.0.0` | IPv4 bind address |\n| `listenAddress.ipv6` | `[::]` | IPv6 bind address |\n| `port` | `8000` | HTTP server port |\n| `ssl.enabled` | `false` | Enable HTTPS |\n| `ssl.certPath` | `./certs/cert.pem` | SSL certificate path |\n| `ssl.keyPath` | `./certs/privkey.pem` | SSL private key path |\n\n### Security Configuration\n\nFrom [default/config.yaml:56-152]():\n\n| Option | Default | Description |\n|--------|---------|-------------|\n| `whitelistMode` | `true` | Enable IP whitelisting |\n| `whitelist` | `[::1, 127.0.0.1]` | Allowed IP addresses |\n| `enableForwardedWhitelist` | `true` | Check X-Forwarded-For header |\n| `whitelistDockerHosts` | `true` | Auto-whitelist Docker gateway IPs |\n| `basicAuthMode` | `false` | Enable HTTP Basic Authentication |\n| `enableUserAccounts` | `false` | Enable multi-user mode |\n| `perUserBasicAuth` | `false` | Use per-user credentials for Basic Auth |\n| `sessionTimeout` | `-1` | Session expiration in seconds |\n| `disableCsrfProtection` | `false` | Disable CSRF protection (not recommended) |\n\n### Performance Configuration\n\nFrom [default/config.yaml:196-204]():\n\n| Option | Default | Description |\n|--------|---------|-------------|\n| `performance.lazyLoadCharacters` | `false` | Load minimal character data for list view |\n| `performance.memoryCacheCapacity` | `100mb` | Max memory for character card cache |\n| `performance.useDiskCache` | `true` | Enable persistent disk cache for characters |\n\n### Backup Configuration\n\nFrom [default/config.yaml:166-180]():\n\n| Option | Default | Description |\n|--------|---------|-------------|\n| `backups.common.numberOfBackups` | `50` | Backups to keep per chat/settings file |\n| `backups.chat.enabled` | `true` | Enable automatic chat backups |\n| `backups.chat.checkIntegrity` | `true` | Verify chat integrity before saving |\n| `backups.chat.maxTotalBackups` | `-1` | Max total backups per user (-1 = unlimited) |\n| `backups.chat.throttleInterval` | `10000` | Milliseconds between backups per user |\n\n**Sources**: [default/config.yaml:1-327]()\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling and Logging\n\n### Console Logging\n\nThe server uses color-coded console output via the `chalk` library [src/util.js:512]():\n\n```javascript\nimport { color } from './util.js';\n\nconsole.log(color.green('Success message'));\nconsole.warn(color.yellow('Warning message'));\nconsole.error(color.red('Error message'));\n```\n\n### Access Logging\n\nWhen `logging.enableAccessLog: true`, the server logs all incoming connections with:\n- Timestamp\n- Client IP address\n- User agent string\n\nLogged to both console and `access.log` file.\n\n### Error Pages\n\nCustom HTML error pages are served for common error conditions:\n\n| Status | File | Purpose |\n|--------|------|---------|\n| 401 | `public/error/unauthorized.html` | Basic Auth failure |\n| 403 | `public/error/forbidden-by-whitelist.html` | IP whitelist rejection |\n\nThese pages are loaded using `safeReadFileSync()` from [src/util.js] and rendered with Handlebars templates.\n\n### Critical Error Handling\n\nServer entry point wraps the main import in try-catch [server.js:13-17]():\n\n```javascript\ntry {\n    await import('./src/server-main.js');\n} catch (error) {\n    console.error('A critical error has occurred while starting the server:', error);\n}\n```\n\nThis prevents crashes during initialization and logs the error for debugging.\n\n**Sources**: [server.js:13-17](), [src/util.js:512](), [default/config.yaml:154-159](), [default/public/error/unauthorized.html:1-18]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1d:T415c,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Event System and Module Communication\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [public/index.html](public/index.html)\n- [public/script.js](public/script.js)\n- [public/scripts/group-chats.js](public/scripts/group-chats.js)\n- [public/scripts/instruct-mode.js](public/scripts/instruct-mode.js)\n- [public/scripts/power-user.js](public/scripts/power-user.js)\n- [public/scripts/utils.js](public/scripts/utils.js)\n- [public/scripts/world-info.js](public/scripts/world-info.js)\n- [public/style.css](public/style.css)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n**Purpose**: This document describes the event-driven architecture used for module communication in SillyTavern. It covers the `EventSource` system, `event_types` enum, and patterns for inter-module communication through events.\n\n**Scope**: This page focuses on the event bus mechanism and how modules emit and listen to events. For extension-specific integration patterns, see [Extension Framework](#8). For application initialization sequence, see [Application Lifecycle and Initialization](#2.1).\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nSillyTavern uses a centralized event bus pattern to enable loose coupling between modules. The core components are:\n\n- **EventSource**: A pub-sub event emitter that allows modules to emit and listen to events\n- **event_types**: An enumeration of all standardized event names\n- **Event listeners**: Modules register callbacks to respond to specific events\n- **Event emitters**: Modules trigger events at key points in their execution\n\nThis architecture allows modules and extensions to react to system changes without tight coupling or circular dependencies.\n\nSources: [public/script.js:278](), [public/scripts/events.js:1-50]()\n\n---\n\n## Core Event System Architecture\n\n### EventSource Class\n\nThe `EventSource` class is the central event bus singleton that manages all event subscriptions and emissions. It provides methods for:\n\n- `emit(eventType, ...args)`: Triggers an event with optional arguments\n- `on(eventType, callback)`: Registers a listener for an event\n- `once(eventType, callback)`: Registers a one-time listener\n- `removeListener(eventType, callback)`: Unregisters a listener\n\n**EventSource Singleton Pattern**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Event System Core\"\n        EventSource[\"eventSource\u003cbr/\u003e(singleton instance)\"]\n        EventTypes[\"event_types\u003cbr/\u003e(enum)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Event Producers\"\n        ScriptJS[\"script.js\u003cbr/\u003eMain orchestrator\"]\n        GroupChats[\"group-chats.js\u003cbr/\u003eGroup management\"]\n        WorldInfo[\"world-info.js\u003cbr/\u003eWI scanning\"]\n        PowerUser[\"power-user.js\u003cbr/\u003eSettings\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Event Consumers\"\n        Extensions[\"Extensions\u003cbr/\u003eThird-party code\"]\n        UIModules[\"UI Modules\u003cbr/\u003eTags, personas, etc.\"]\n        CoreSystems[\"Core Systems\u003cbr/\u003eChat, generation\"]\n    end\n    \n    ScriptJS --\u003e|\"emit(event_types.X, data)\"| EventSource\n    GroupChats --\u003e|\"emit(event_types.Y, data)\"| EventSource\n    WorldInfo --\u003e|\"emit(event_types.Z, data)\"| EventSource\n    PowerUser --\u003e|\"emit(event_types.W, data)\"| EventSource\n    \n    EventSource --\u003e|\"on(event_types.X, handler)\"| Extensions\n    EventSource --\u003e|\"on(event_types.Y, handler)\"| UIModules\n    EventSource --\u003e|\"on(event_types.Z, handler)\"| CoreSystems\n    \n    EventTypes -.-\u003e|\"provides constants\"| ScriptJS\n    EventTypes -.-\u003e|\"provides constants\"| Extensions\n```\n\nSources: [public/script.js:278](), [public/scripts/extensions.js:1-100]()\n\n---\n\n## event_types Enumeration\n\nThe `event_types` enum defines all standardized event names used throughout the application. This ensures type safety and prevents typos when emitting or listening to events.\n\n### Event Categories\n\n| Category | Example Events | Purpose |\n|----------|---------------|---------|\n| **Lifecycle** | `APP_INITIALIZED`, `APP_READY` | Application startup sequence |\n| **Chat** | `CHAT_CHANGED`, `MESSAGE_SENT`, `MESSAGE_RECEIVED` | Chat state changes |\n| **Character** | `CHARACTER_EDITED`, `CHARACTER_PAGE_LOADED` | Character management |\n| **Generation** | `GENERATION_STARTED`, `GENERATION_STOPPED` | AI text generation |\n| **Group** | `GROUP_CHAT_CREATED`, `GROUP_UPDATED` | Group chat operations |\n| **World Info** | `WORLDINFO_FORCE_ACTIVATE` | World info activation |\n| **UI** | `SETTINGS_UPDATED`, `THEME_CHANGED` | UI state changes |\n\n**Event Naming Convention**: Events use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE and typically follow the pattern `NOUN_VERB` (e.g., `MESSAGE_SENT`) or `NOUN_VERB_ED` (e.g., `CHARACTER_EDITED`).\n\nSources: [public/scripts/events.js:1-100]()\n\n---\n\n## Event Communication Patterns\n\n### Emit and Listen Pattern\n\n**Event Emission**\n\n```javascript\n// Emitter: script.js or any module\nawait eventSource.emit(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, getCurrentChatId());\n```\n\n**Event Listening**\n\n```javascript\n// Listener: extension or module\neventSource.on(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, (chatId) =\u003e {\n    console.log('Chat changed to:', chatId);\n    // React to chat change\n});\n```\n\n**Event Flow During Chat Generation**\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant ScriptJS as \"script.js\"\n    participant EventSource as \"eventSource\"\n    participant Extensions as \"Extensions\"\n    participant WI as \"WorldInfoBuffer\"\n    participant API as \"OpenAI/TextGen\"\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eScriptJS: Click send message\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eEventSource: emit(GENERATION_STARTED)\n    EventSource-\u003e\u003eExtensions: notify listeners\n    \n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eScriptJS: setupChatCompletionPromptManager()\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eEventSource: emit(PREPARE_CHAT_COMPLETION)\n    EventSource-\u003e\u003eExtensions: allow prompt modification\n    \n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eWI: getWorldInfoPrompt()\n    WI-\u003e\u003eEventSource: emit(WORLDINFO_SCAN_STARTED)\n    WI-\u003e\u003eEventSource: emit(WORLDINFO_ACTIVATED, entries)\n    \n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eAPI: sendOpenAIRequest()\n    \n    API--\u003e\u003eScriptJS: Streaming response chunks\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eEventSource: emit(MESSAGE_STREAMING, chunk)\n    EventSource-\u003e\u003eExtensions: display streaming text\n    \n    API--\u003e\u003eScriptJS: Complete response\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eEventSource: emit(MESSAGE_RECEIVED, index)\n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eEventSource: emit(CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED, index)\n    EventSource-\u003e\u003eExtensions: notify completion\n    \n    ScriptJS-\u003e\u003eEventSource: emit(GENERATION_ENDED)\n    EventSource-\u003e\u003eExtensions: notify listeners\n```\n\nSources: [public/script.js:800-900](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:600-700](), [public/scripts/openai.js:1-100]()\n\n---\n\n## Common Event Types and Usage\n\n### Application Lifecycle Events\n\n**Initialization Sequence**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    A[\"APP_INITIALIZED\"] --\u003e B[\"APP_READY\"]\n    B --\u003e C[\"Modules can interact\"]\n    \n    style A fill:#f9f9f9\n    style B fill:#f9f9f9\n    style C fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\n- **`APP_INITIALIZED`**: Emitted after core systems are loaded but before UI is ready\n  - Location: [public/script.js:735]()\n  - Use: Extensions can initialize their internal state\n  \n- **`APP_READY`**: Emitted when the entire application is ready for user interaction\n  - Location: [public/script.js:738]()\n  - Use: Extensions can perform UI modifications and register event handlers\n\n### Chat Events\n\n| Event Type | Trigger | Arguments | Common Uses |\n|------------|---------|-----------|-------------|\n| `CHAT_CHANGED` | Active chat switches | `chatId` | Refresh extension data for new chat |\n| `MESSAGE_SENT` | User sends message | `messageIndex` | Track user inputs, trigger automations |\n| `MESSAGE_RECEIVED` | AI message received | `messageIndex` | Process AI response, update UI |\n| `MESSAGE_DELETED` | Message removed | `messageIndex` | Clean up extension data |\n| `MESSAGE_EDITED` | Message modified | `messageIndex` | Reprocess message content |\n| `MESSAGE_SWIPED` | User swipes message | `messageIndex` | Update alternative responses |\n\nSources: [public/script.js:1-1000]()\n\n### Character and Group Events\n\n| Event Type | Trigger | Arguments | Common Uses |\n|------------|---------|-----------|-------------|\n| `CHARACTER_EDITED` | Character data modified | `characterId` | Reload character-specific settings |\n| `CHARACTER_PAGE_LOADED` | Character list displayed | none | Update UI elements |\n| `GROUP_CHAT_CREATED` | New group chat started | none | Initialize group-specific features |\n| `GROUP_UPDATED` | Group membership changed | `groupId` | Refresh group state |\n\nSources: [public/scripts/group-chats.js:300-320]()\n\n### Generation Events\n\n| Event Type | Trigger | Arguments | Common Uses |\n|------------|---------|-----------|-------------|\n| `GENERATION_STARTED` | AI generation begins | none | Show loading indicators |\n| `GENERATION_STOPPED` | AI generation ends | none | Hide loading indicators |\n| `TEXT_COMPLETION_SETTINGS_READY` | Settings loaded | none | Configure generation parameters |\n| `GENERATE_BEFORE_COMBINE_PROMPTS` | Before prompt assembly | none | Inject custom prompts |\n| `GENERATE_AFTER_COMBINE_PROMPTS` | After prompt assembly | `{ chatPrompt, quietPrompt }` | Modify final prompt |\n\nSources: [public/script.js:1-2000]()\n\n---\n\n## Module Communication Strategies\n\nSillyTavern modules communicate through three primary patterns:\n\n### 1. Direct Function Imports\n\n**When to Use**: Synchronous operations, utility functions, accessing stable APIs\n\n```javascript\n// Example: Importing utility functions\nimport { substituteParams, saveSettings } from '../script.js';\n```\n\n**Characteristics**:\n- Synchronous execution\n- Type-safe\n- Explicit dependencies\n- Risk of circular dependencies\n\nSources: [public/script.js:1-100]()\n\n### 2. Event Emission (Pub-Sub)\n\n**When to Use**: Notifying multiple listeners, loosely coupled reactions, extension hooks\n\n```javascript\n// Example: Notifying listeners of state change\nawait eventSource.emit(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, chatId);\n```\n\n**Characteristics**:\n- Asynchronous by default\n- One-to-many communication\n- Loose coupling\n- Extensions can hook without modifying core\n\nSources: [public/scripts/events.js:1-100]()\n\n### 3. Shared State Objects\n\n**When to Use**: Global configuration, settings, frequently accessed data\n\n```javascript\n// Example: Accessing global settings\nimport { power_user, chat_metadata } from './power-user.js';\n```\n\n**Characteristics**:\n- Direct access to mutable state\n- Fast read operations\n- Need to emit events when mutating for reactivity\n\nSources: [public/scripts/power-user.js:1-100]()\n\n**Communication Pattern Decision Tree**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Start[\"Need module communication?\"]\n    Start --\u003e|Yes| Q1[\"Multiple modules need to react?\"]\n    Q1 --\u003e|Yes| UseEvents[\"Use Event System\u003cbr/\u003eeventSource.emit()\"]\n    Q1 --\u003e|No| Q2[\"Need synchronous result?\"]\n    Q2 --\u003e|Yes| DirectImport[\"Direct Function Import\"]\n    Q2 --\u003e|No| Q3[\"Frequently accessed data?\"]\n    Q3 --\u003e|Yes| SharedState[\"Shared State Object\u003cbr/\u003e+ emit event on mutation\"]\n    Q3 --\u003e|No| UseEvents2[\"Use Event System\"]\n    \n    style UseEvents fill:#f9f9f9\n    style DirectImport fill:#f9f9f9\n    style SharedState fill:#f9f9f9\n    style UseEvents2 fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\nSources: [public/script.js:1-300]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension Integration via Events\n\nExtensions primarily integrate with the core system through event listeners. This allows third-party code to extend functionality without modifying core files.\n\n### Extension Event Registration Pattern\n\n**Extension Initialization**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    A[\"Extension loads\"] --\u003e B[\"getContext()\"]\n    B --\u003e C[\"Register event listeners\"]\n    C --\u003e D[\"eventSource.on()\"]\n    D --\u003e E[\"Extension ready\"]\n    \n    style A fill:#f9f9f9\n    style B fill:#f9f9f9\n    style C fill:#f9f9f9\n    style D fill:#f9f9f9\n    style E fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\n**Typical Extension Setup**\n\n```javascript\n// Extension: public/scripts/extensions/my-extension/index.js\n\nasync function init() {\n    // Listen to character messages\n    eventSource.on(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, onMessageReceived);\n    \n    // Listen to chat changes\n    eventSource.on(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, onChatChanged);\n    \n    // Listen to generation events\n    eventSource.on(event_types.GENERATION_ENDED, onGenerationEnded);\n}\n\nfunction onMessageReceived(messageIndex) {\n    // React to new message\n    const message = chat[messageIndex];\n    console.log('New message:', message.mes);\n}\n```\n\n### Extension Hook Points\n\n| Hook Point | Event Type | Use Case |\n|------------|------------|----------|\n| **Before prompt generation** | `GENERATE_BEFORE_COMBINE_PROMPTS` | Inject custom system prompts |\n| **After prompt generation** | `GENERATE_AFTER_COMBINE_PROMPTS` | Modify final prompt text |\n| **Message received** | `MESSAGE_RECEIVED` | Process AI responses (sentiment, commands) |\n| **Character rendered** | `CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED` | Update UI elements per message |\n| **Chat changed** | `CHAT_CHANGED` | Load extension-specific chat data |\n| **World Info activated** | `WORLDINFO_ACTIVATED` | Track which entries triggered |\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions.js:1-200]()\n\n---\n\n## Event-Driven Workflow Examples\n\n### Workflow 1: Message Generation with Extension Hooks\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant Core as \"Core (script.js)\"\n    participant Events as \"eventSource\"\n    participant Ext1 as \"Extension A\"\n    participant Ext2 as \"Extension B\"\n    participant API\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eCore: Send message\n    Core-\u003e\u003eEvents: emit(GENERATE_BEFORE_COMBINE_PROMPTS)\n    Events-\u003e\u003eExt1: Inject custom prompt\n    Events-\u003e\u003eExt2: Modify generation settings\n    \n    Core-\u003e\u003eCore: Build prompt with extensions input\n    Core-\u003e\u003eEvents: emit(GENERATE_AFTER_COMBINE_PROMPTS, prompt)\n    Events-\u003e\u003eExt1: Log final prompt\n    \n    Core-\u003e\u003eAPI: Send request\n    API--\u003e\u003eCore: Response\n    \n    Core-\u003e\u003eEvents: emit(MESSAGE_RECEIVED, index)\n    Events-\u003e\u003eExt1: Analyze sentiment\n    Events-\u003e\u003eExt2: Extract keywords\n    \n    Core-\u003e\u003eEvents: emit(CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED, index)\n    Events-\u003e\u003eExt1: Update UI sprites\n    Events-\u003e\u003eExt2: Display metadata\n```\n\nSources: [public/script.js:1500-2000]()\n\n### Workflow 2: Character Switch with State Cleanup\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant Core as \"script.js\"\n    participant Events as \"eventSource\"\n    participant Tags as \"tags.js\"\n    participant WI as \"world-info.js\"\n    participant Ext as \"Extensions\"\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eCore: selectCharacterById(id)\n    Core-\u003e\u003eCore: clearChat()\n    Core-\u003e\u003eCore: setCharacterId(id)\n    \n    Core-\u003e\u003eEvents: emit(CHAT_CHANGED, chatId)\n    Events-\u003e\u003eTags: applyTagsOnCharacterSelect()\n    Events-\u003e\u003eWI: Load character lorebooks\n    Events-\u003e\u003eExt: Load extension data for character\n    \n    Core-\u003e\u003eEvents: emit(CHARACTER_PAGE_LOADED)\n    Events-\u003e\u003eTags: Update tag filters\n    Events-\u003e\u003eExt: Refresh UI elements\n```\n\nSources: [public/script.js:823-856](), [public/scripts/tags.js:1-200]()\n\n### Workflow 3: World Info Activation Chain\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    A[\"Generate() called\"] --\u003e B[\"getWorldInfoPrompt()\"]\n    B --\u003e C[\"WorldInfoBuffer.get()\"]\n    C --\u003e D[\"Scan chat messages\"]\n    D --\u003e E[\"Match keywords\"]\n    E --\u003e F[\"Entry activated\"]\n    F --\u003e G[\"emit(WORLDINFO_ACTIVATED, entries)\"]\n    G --\u003e H[\"Extensions process entries\"]\n    H --\u003e I[\"Return formatted WI string\"]\n    I --\u003e J[\"Continue generation\"]\n    \n    style A fill:#f9f9f9\n    style G fill:#f9f9f9\n    style H fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/world-info.js:200-500]()\n\n---\n\n## Debugging Events\n\n### Logging Event Emissions\n\nTo trace event flow, you can add logging to the EventSource class or use browser developer tools:\n\n```javascript\n// Enable event logging in console\neventSource.on('*', (eventType, ...args) =\u003e {\n    console.log(`[Event] ${eventType}`, args);\n});\n```\n\n### Common Event Issues\n\n| Issue | Cause | Solution |\n|-------|-------|----------|\n| **Event not firing** | Typo in event name | Use `event_types` constants |\n| **Listener called multiple times** | Registered multiple times | Use `once()` or track registration |\n| **Race condition** | Async event ordering | Use `await eventSource.emit()` |\n| **Memory leak** | Listeners not removed | Call `removeListener()` on cleanup |\n\nSources: [public/scripts/events.js:1-100]()\n\n---\n\n## Best Practices\n\n1. **Always use `event_types` constants**: Never use string literals for event names\n2. **Document event arguments**: Specify what data is passed to listeners\n3. **Use `await` for critical events**: Ensure listeners complete before continuing\n4. **Clean up listeners**: Remove event listeners when modules/extensions unload\n5. **Avoid side effects in listeners**: Keep listener callbacks focused and predictable\n6. **Emit events after state changes**: Ensure state is consistent before notifying listeners\n7. **Use specific events over generic ones**: Prefer `MESSAGE_DELETED` over `STATE_CHANGED`\n\nSources: [public/script.js:1-3000](), [public/scripts/extensions.js:1-200]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1e:T5a1c,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Client-Server Data Flow\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [default/config.yaml](default/config.yaml)\n- [default/public/error/unauthorized.html](default/public/error/unauthorized.html)\n- [post-install.js](post-install.js)\n- [public/index.html](public/index.html)\n- [public/script.js](public/script.js)\n- [public/scripts/bookmarks.js](public/scripts/bookmarks.js)\n- [public/scripts/group-chats.js](public/scripts/group-chats.js)\n- [public/scripts/instruct-mode.js](public/scripts/instruct-mode.js)\n- [public/scripts/power-user.js](public/scripts/power-user.js)\n- [public/scripts/utils.js](public/scripts/utils.js)\n- [public/scripts/world-info.js](public/scripts/world-info.js)\n- [public/style.css](public/style.css)\n- [server.js](server.js)\n- [src/endpoints/characters.js](src/endpoints/characters.js)\n- [src/endpoints/chats.js](src/endpoints/chats.js)\n- [src/endpoints/content-manager.js](src/endpoints/content-manager.js)\n- [src/endpoints/groups.js](src/endpoints/groups.js)\n- [src/endpoints/presets.js](src/endpoints/presets.js)\n- [src/endpoints/settings.js](src/endpoints/settings.js)\n- [src/middleware/basicAuth.js](src/middleware/basicAuth.js)\n- [src/middleware/whitelist.js](src/middleware/whitelist.js)\n- [src/users.js](src/users.js)\n- [src/util.js](src/util.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document describes how data flows between the SillyTavern client (browser) and server, including request/response cycles, caching strategies, and persistence mechanisms. It covers the technical implementation of data transfer, storage, and retrieval patterns used throughout the application.\n\nFor information about:\n- Application initialization and startup sequence, see [Application Lifecycle and Initialization](#2.1)\n- Server API endpoint structure and routing, see [Server Architecture and Routing](#2.2)\n- Specific data storage formats and file organization, see [Data Management](#5)\n- Event-driven communication between modules, see [Event System and Module Communication](#2.3)\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\nSillyTavern follows a traditional client-server architecture with clear separation between the browser-based frontend and Node.js backend. The client maintains in-memory state and communicates with the server through RESTful HTTP endpoints. The server manages all file I/O operations and provides secure access to user data.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Browser[\"Browser Process\"]\n        ClientState[\"In-Memory State\u003cbr/\u003echaracters[], chat[], settings\"]\n        ClientCache[\"Client-Side Cache\u003cbr/\u003eAvatars, thumbnails\"]\n        ScriptJS[\"script.js\u003cbr/\u003eMain controller\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Server[\"Node.js Server Process\"]\n        Express[\"Express Router\u003cbr/\u003eAPI endpoints\"]\n        MemCache[\"memoryCache\u003cbr/\u003eMemoryLimitedMap\"]\n        DiskCache[\"diskCache\u003cbr/\u003enode-persist\"]\n        FileSystem[\"File System\u003cbr/\u003ePNG, JSONL, JSON files\"]\n    end\n    \n    ScriptJS --\u003e|\"HTTP POST/GET\u003cbr/\u003egetRequestHeaders()\"| Express\n    Express --\u003e|\"JSON response\"| ScriptJS\n    Express --\u003e|\"Read/Write\"| MemCache\n    MemCache --\u003e|\"Cache miss\"| DiskCache\n    DiskCache --\u003e|\"Cache miss\"| FileSystem\n    FileSystem --\u003e|\"Data\"| DiskCache\n    DiskCache --\u003e|\"Data\"| MemCache\n    MemCache --\u003e|\"Data\"| Express\n    \n    ClientState -.-\u003e|\"Trigger save\"| ScriptJS\n```\n\n**Figure 1: Client-Server Architecture with Multi-Tier Caching**\n\nSources: [public/script.js:1-100](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:28-157](), [server.js:1-18]()\n\n---\n\n## Request/Response Cycle\n\nAll client-server communication uses HTTP requests with CSRF protection. The client includes authentication tokens in request headers, and the server validates the user's identity before processing requests.\n\n### Request Headers\n\nThe client constructs request headers using the `getRequestHeaders()` function:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client as \"Client Code\"\n    participant getRequestHeaders as \"getRequestHeaders()\"\n    participant Server as \"Server Endpoint\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003egetRequestHeaders: Call with options\n    Note over getRequestHeaders: token = CSRF token\u003cbr/\u003eContent-Type = application/json\n    getRequestHeaders--\u003e\u003eClient: Headers object\n    Client-\u003e\u003eServer: fetch() with headers\n    Server-\u003e\u003eServer: Validate CSRF token\n    Server-\u003e\u003eServer: Verify user session\n    Server-\u003e\u003eServer: Check whitelist (if enabled)\n    Server--\u003e\u003eClient: JSON response\n```\n\n**Figure 2: Request Header Construction and Validation**\n\nThe `getRequestHeaders()` function is defined in [public/script.js:619-630]() and constructs an object containing:\n- `Content-Type: application/json` (unless omitted)\n- `X-CSRF-Token: token` (stored globally after initialization)\n\nThe server validates these headers through middleware:\n- CSRF protection: Validates the `X-CSRF-Token` header\n- User authentication: Verifies session cookies\n- IP whitelist: Checks if request originates from allowed IP (if enabled)\n\nSources: [public/script.js:619-642](), [src/middleware/whitelist.js:1-130](), [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:1-65]()\n\n### Response Handling\n\nAll server responses follow a consistent JSON format. The client uses `async/await` patterns with `fetch()` API to handle responses:\n\n| Response Type | Status Code | Handling |\n|--------------|-------------|----------|\n| Success | 200-299 | Parse JSON, update state |\n| Client Error | 400-499 | Display error toastr, log to console |\n| Server Error | 500-599 | Display error toastr, attempt retry |\n| Network Error | N/A | Display connection error |\n\nSources: [public/script.js:647-663]()\n\n---\n\n## Caching Architecture\n\nSillyTavern implements a three-tier caching strategy to optimize performance when loading character cards and other data.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Request[\"Data Request\u003cbr/\u003e(e.g., character card)\"]\n    \n    subgraph Tier1[\"Tier 1: Memory Cache (Client)\"]\n        ClientCache[\"Browser memory\u003cbr/\u003eTemporary storage\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Tier2[\"Tier 2: Memory Cache (Server)\"]\n        MemoryCache[\"memoryCache\u003cbr/\u003eMemoryLimitedMap\u003cbr/\u003e~100MB capacity\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Tier3[\"Tier 3: Disk Cache (Server)\"]\n        DiskCacheStore[\"diskCache\u003cbr/\u003enode-persist\u003cbr/\u003eDATA_ROOT/_cache/characters/\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Storage[\"Tier 4: File System\"]\n        FileStore[\"PNG files\u003cbr/\u003eJSONL files\u003cbr/\u003eJSON files\"]\n    end\n    \n    Request --\u003e ClientCache\n    ClientCache --\u003e|\"Cache miss\"| MemoryCache\n    MemoryCache --\u003e|\"Cache miss\u003cbr/\u003egetCacheKey()\"| DiskCacheStore\n    DiskCacheStore --\u003e|\"Cache miss\"| FileStore\n    \n    FileStore --\u003e|\"parse()\"| DiskCacheStore\n    DiskCacheStore --\u003e|\"Data\"| MemoryCache\n    MemoryCache --\u003e|\"Data\"| ClientCache\n    ClientCache --\u003e|\"Data\"| Response[\"Response\"]\n```\n\n**Figure 3: Multi-Tier Caching Strategy**\n\n### Memory Cache (Server)\n\nThe server maintains a memory-limited LRU cache implemented as `MemoryLimitedMap`:\n\n- **Capacity**: Configurable via `performance.memoryCacheCapacity` in config.yaml (default: 100MB)\n- **Key Format**: `${filePath}-${mtimeMs}` (file path + modification timestamp)\n- **Invalidation**: Automatic on file modification or explicit cache clear\n- **Implementation**: [src/endpoints/characters.js:28-36]()\n\n### Disk Cache (Server)\n\nThe disk cache persists parsed character data between server restarts:\n\n- **Storage Location**: `DATA_ROOT/_cache/characters/`\n- **Implementation**: Uses `node-persist` library with `DiskCache` class\n- **Sync Interval**: 5 minutes (automatic verification and pruning)\n- **Cache Key**: Same as memory cache key format\n- **Configuration**: Enable/disable via `performance.useDiskCache` in config.yaml\n\nThe `DiskCache` class manages cache lifecycle:\n\n```mermaid\nclassDiagram\n    class DiskCache {\n        -instance: LocalStorage\n        -syncInterval: NodeJS.Timeout\n        +syncQueue: Set~string~\n        +cachePath: string\n        +hashedKeys: string[]\n        +instance() Promise~LocalStorage~\n        +verify(directoriesList) Promise~void~\n        -syncCacheEntries() Promise~void~\n        +dispose() void\n    }\n```\n\n**Figure 4: DiskCache Class Structure**\n\nThe disk cache automatically:\n1. Verifies cache integrity every 5 minutes\n2. Removes orphaned cache entries when source files are deleted\n3. Maintains separate cache entries per user\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-157](), [src/util.js:20-24]()\n\n### Cache Invalidation\n\nCache entries are invalidated when:\n- **File Modified**: Detected via `fs.statSync().mtimeMs` change\n- **Explicit Clear**: Through `memoryCache.delete(key)` calls\n- **User-Specific**: When a user is added to `diskCache.syncQueue`\n\n| Event | Memory Cache | Disk Cache |\n|-------|-------------|------------|\n| Character imported | Invalidated immediately | Queued for sync |\n| Character edited | Invalidated immediately | Queued for sync |\n| Character deleted | Invalidated immediately | Synced within 5 minutes |\n| Server restart | Cleared | Persisted |\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:220-234](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:84-97]()\n\n---\n\n## Data Persistence Mechanisms\n\n### Character Card Persistence\n\nCharacter cards are stored as PNG files with embedded JSON metadata in tEXt chunks. This format allows the cards to be both human-readable (as images) and machine-parsable.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client as \"Browser\"\n    participant ServerEndpoint as \"/api/characters/edit\"\n    participant Parser as \"character-card-parser\"\n    participant Cache as \"Caching Layer\"\n    participant FS as \"File System\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eServerEndpoint: POST with character data\n    Note over ServerEndpoint: writeCharacterData()\n    ServerEndpoint-\u003e\u003eParser: write(imageBuffer, data)\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Encode JSON to tEXt chunks\n    Parser--\u003e\u003eServerEndpoint: Modified PNG buffer\n    ServerEndpoint-\u003e\u003eFS: writeFileAtomicSync()\n    Note over FS: Atomic write prevents\u003cbr/\u003ecorruption on crash\n    FS--\u003e\u003eServerEndpoint: Success\n    ServerEndpoint-\u003e\u003eCache: Invalidate cache entries\n    ServerEndpoint--\u003e\u003eClient: {file_name: \"...\"}\n```\n\n**Figure 5: Character Card Write Flow**\n\nThe `writeCharacterData()` function performs:\n1. **Cache invalidation**: Clears memory and marks disk cache for sync\n2. **Image processing**: Reads/resizes image using Jimp\n3. **Metadata encoding**: Embeds JSON in PNG tEXt chunks\n4. **Atomic write**: Uses `write-file-atomic` to prevent corruption\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:220-265](), [src/character-card-parser.js]()\n\n### Character Card Loading\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client as \"getCharacters()\"\n    participant Server as \"/api/characters/all\"\n    participant Cache as \"readCharacterData()\"\n    participant MemCache as \"memoryCache\"\n    participant DiskCache as \"diskCache\"\n    participant Parser as \"parse()\"\n    participant FS as \"File System\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eServer: GET request\n    Server-\u003e\u003eServer: Enumerate PNG files\n    loop For each character\n        Server-\u003e\u003eCache: readCharacterData(filePath)\n        Cache-\u003e\u003eMemCache: Check cache key\n        alt Cache hit\n            MemCache--\u003e\u003eCache: Return cached data\n        else Cache miss\n            Cache-\u003e\u003eDiskCache: getItem(cacheKey)\n            alt Disk cache hit\n                DiskCache--\u003e\u003eCache: Return cached data\n            else Disk cache miss\n                Cache-\u003e\u003eParser: parse(filePath, 'png')\n                Parser-\u003e\u003eFS: Read PNG file\n                FS--\u003e\u003eParser: Buffer\n                Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Extract tEXt chunks\n                Parser--\u003e\u003eCache: JSON string\n                Cache-\u003e\u003eMemCache: set(cacheKey, data)\n                Cache-\u003e\u003eDiskCache: setItem(cacheKey, data)\n            end\n        end\n        Cache--\u003e\u003eServer: Character data\n    end\n    Server--\u003e\u003eClient: Array of characters\n```\n\n**Figure 6: Character Card Read Flow with Caching**\n\nFor performance optimization, the system supports **lazy loading** of character cards:\n- **Enabled via**: `performance.lazyLoadCharacters` in config.yaml\n- **Behavior**: Returns shallow character data (name, avatar URL) without parsing full card\n- **Full Load**: Triggered on-demand when character is selected\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:165-209](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:28-36]()\n\n---\n\n## Chat Persistence\n\nChat messages are stored in JSONL (JSON Lines) format, with one message per line. This format allows efficient appending without re-parsing the entire file.\n\n### Chat File Format\n\n```\n{\"chat_metadata\":{...},\"user_name\":\"User\",\"character_name\":\"Character\"}\n{\"name\":\"User\",\"is_user\":true,\"send_date\":\"2024-01-01T00:00:00.000Z\",\"mes\":\"Hello\",\"extra\":{}}\n{\"name\":\"Character\",\"is_user\":false,\"send_date\":\"2024-01-01T00:00:01.000Z\",\"mes\":\"Hi there!\",\"extra\":{}}\n```\n\nThe first line contains metadata, subsequent lines contain messages.\n\n### Chat Saving Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant UI as \"User Action\"\n    participant Script as \"saveChatConditional()\"\n    participant Debounce as \"chatSaveTimeout\"\n    participant Server as \"/api/chats/save\"\n    participant Backup as \"backupChat()\"\n    participant FS as \"File System\"\n    \n    UI-\u003e\u003eScript: Message sent/edited\n    Script-\u003e\u003eScript: Check isChatSaving flag\n    alt Already saving\n        Script--\u003e\u003eUI: Skip save\n    else Not saving\n        Script-\u003e\u003eScript: Set isChatSaving = true\n        Script-\u003e\u003eDebounce: Clear existing timeout\n        Script-\u003e\u003eDebounce: setTimeout(1000ms)\n        Note over Debounce: Debounce prevents\u003cbr/\u003eexcessive writes\n        Debounce-\u003e\u003eServer: POST with chat data\n        Server-\u003e\u003eServer: Validate integrity\n        alt Integrity check enabled\n            Server-\u003e\u003eServer: Check first line metadata\n            Server-\u003e\u003eServer: Verify UUID\n        end\n        Server-\u003e\u003eBackup: Throttled backup (10s)\n        Backup-\u003e\u003eFS: Write to backups/ directory\n        Note over Backup: Max 50 backups per chat\u003cbr/\u003eby default\n        Server-\u003e\u003eFS: writeFileAtomicSync(chat.jsonl)\n        FS--\u003e\u003eServer: Success\n        Server--\u003e\u003eScript: {file_name: \"...\"}\n        Script-\u003e\u003eScript: Set isChatSaving = false\n    end\n```\n\n**Figure 7: Chat Save Flow with Debouncing and Backups**\n\n### Chat Backup Strategy\n\nBackups are managed with throttling to prevent excessive disk writes:\n\n| Configuration | Default Value | Purpose |\n|--------------|---------------|---------|\n| `backups.chat.enabled` | `true` | Enable/disable backups |\n| `backups.common.numberOfBackups` | `50` | Per-chat backup count |\n| `backups.chat.maxTotalBackups` | `-1` (unlimited) | Total backups per user |\n| `backups.chat.throttleInterval` | `10000` ms | Minimum time between backups |\n| `backups.chat.checkIntegrity` | `true` | Verify UUID before save |\n\nThe backup function is throttled per-user using Lodash's `throttle()`:\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/chats.js:1-100](), [public/script.js:3800-3900]()\n\n### Chat Loading Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client as \"getChat()\"\n    participant Server as \"/api/chats/get\"\n    participant FS as \"File System\"\n    participant Parser as \"JSONL Parser\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eServer: POST {character, chat_id}\n    Server-\u003e\u003eFS: Read chat file\n    FS--\u003e\u003eServer: JSONL content\n    Server-\u003e\u003eParser: Parse line by line\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Extract metadata (line 0)\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Parse messages (lines 1+)\n    Parser--\u003e\u003eServer: Array of messages\n    Server-\u003e\u003eServer: ensureMessageMediaIsArray()\n    Server--\u003e\u003eClient: {chat: [...], metadata: {...}}\n    Client-\u003e\u003eClient: Update chat[] array\n    Client-\u003e\u003eClient: printMessages()\n```\n\n**Figure 8: Chat Load Flow**\n\nThe client loads chats into the global `chat[]` array and renders them to the DOM using `printMessages()`.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/chats.js:195-350](), [public/script.js:4000-4100]()\n\n---\n\n## Settings Persistence\n\nSettings are stored in JSON format and auto-saved with throttling to prevent excessive writes.\n\n### Settings Save Flow\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    UserAction[\"User modifies setting\u003cbr/\u003ein UI\"] --\u003e Debounce[\"saveSettingsDebounced()\u003cbr/\u003e1000ms debounce\"]\n    Debounce --\u003e Validate[\"Validate settings object\"]\n    Validate --\u003e Server[\"/api/settings/save\"]\n    Server --\u003e AutoSave[\"triggerAutoSave()\u003cbr/\u003e10 minute throttle\"]\n    AutoSave --\u003e Backup[\"backupUserSettings()\"]\n    Server --\u003e Write[\"writeFileAtomicSync()\u003cbr/\u003esettings.json\"]\n    Backup --\u003e BackupWrite[\"Write to backups/\u003cbr/\u003esettings_backup_*.json\"]\n```\n\n**Figure 9: Settings Save Flow with Auto-Backup**\n\nThe system implements two types of save operations:\n\n1. **Manual Save**: Triggered by user action, debounced to 1000ms\n2. **Auto-Save**: Triggered every 10 minutes while user is active\n\nBoth operations:\n- Validate settings structure\n- Create timestamped backups\n- Use atomic writes to prevent corruption\n- Store in user-specific directories\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/settings.js:1-200](), [public/script.js:440-442]()\n\n### Settings Structure\n\nSettings are organized hierarchically:\n\n```\n{\n  \"user_name\": \"User\",\n  \"amount_gen\": 80,\n  \"max_context\": 2048,\n  \"extension_settings\": {\n    \"memory\": {...},\n    \"vectors\": {...}\n  },\n  \"power_user\": {\n    \"tokenizer\": 1,\n    \"context\": {...},\n    \"instruct\": {...}\n  }\n}\n```\n\nKey setting categories:\n- **Core settings**: Chat generation parameters (amount_gen, max_context)\n- **Power user settings**: Advanced UI and behavior configuration\n- **Extension settings**: Per-extension configuration objects\n\nSources: [public/script.js:586-598](), [public/scripts/power-user.js:123-350]()\n\n---\n\n## User Data Isolation\n\nAll user data is isolated in per-user directory structures, preventing cross-user data access.\n\n### Directory Structure\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    DataRoot[\"DATA_ROOT/\"]\n    \n    UserDir[\"user_handle/\"]\n    Characters[\"characters/\u003cbr/\u003eCharacter PNG files\"]\n    Chats[\"chats/\u003cbr/\u003ePer-character subdirs\"]\n    ChatFiles[\"chats/character_name/\u003cbr/\u003echat_*.jsonl files\"]\n    Groups[\"groups/\u003cbr/\u003eGroup JSON files\"]\n    GroupChats[\"group_chats/\u003cbr/\u003eGroup JSONL files\"]\n    Worlds[\"worlds/\u003cbr/\u003eWorld info files\"]\n    Backgrounds[\"backgrounds/\u003cbr/\u003eBackground images\"]\n    Settings[\"settings.json\"]\n    Secrets[\"secrets.json\"]\n    Backups[\"backups/\u003cbr/\u003eTimestamped backups\"]\n    \n    DataRoot --\u003e UserDir\n    UserDir --\u003e Characters\n    UserDir --\u003e Chats\n    UserDir --\u003e Groups\n    UserDir --\u003e GroupChats\n    UserDir --\u003e Worlds\n    UserDir --\u003e Backgrounds\n    UserDir --\u003e Settings\n    UserDir --\u003e Secrets\n    UserDir --\u003e Backups\n    Chats --\u003e ChatFiles\n```\n\n**Figure 10: User Directory Structure**\n\nThe `getUserDirectories()` function in [src/users.js:140-200]() returns a `UserDirectoryList` object containing paths to all user-specific directories. This ensures:\n- Data isolation between users (when multi-user mode is enabled)\n- Consistent directory structure across deployments\n- Easy backup and migration of user data\n\nSources: [src/users.js:1-100](), [src/constants.js:1-50]()\n\n### Directory Creation\n\nDirectories are created lazily on first access using the `USER_DIRECTORY_TEMPLATE` constant. The server ensures proper permissions are set on creation.\n\n| Directory | Purpose | File Format |\n|-----------|---------|-------------|\n| `characters/` | Character cards | PNG with embedded JSON |\n| `chats/` | Chat messages | JSONL (subdirectory per character) |\n| `groups/` | Group metadata | JSON |\n| `group_chats/` | Group chat messages | JSONL |\n| `worlds/` | World info / lorebooks | JSON |\n| `backgrounds/` | Background images | PNG, JPG, WebP |\n| `settings.json` | User settings | JSON |\n| `secrets.json` | API keys (encrypted) | JSON |\n| `backups/` | Automated backups | Various |\n\nSources: [src/constants.js:18-40](), [src/users.js:140-200]()\n\n---\n\n## Performance Optimizations\n\n### Debouncing and Throttling\n\nThe system uses strategic debouncing and throttling to optimize performance:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph DebouncingPatterns[\"Debouncing Patterns\"]\n        SaveSettings[\"saveSettingsDebounced()\u003cbr/\u003e1000ms delay\u003cbr/\u003eResets on each call\"]\n        SaveCharacter[\"saveCharacterDebounced()\u003cbr/\u003e1000ms delay\u003cbr/\u003eResets on each call\"]\n        PrintChars[\"printCharactersDebounced()\u003cbr/\u003e100ms delay\u003cbr/\u003eResets on each call\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ThrottlingPatterns[\"Throttling Patterns\"]\n        ChatBackup[\"Chat Backup\u003cbr/\u003e10000ms interval\u003cbr/\u003eFirst call immediate\u003cbr/\u003eLast call trailing\"]\n        SettingsAutoSave[\"Settings Auto-Save\u003cbr/\u003e600000ms interval\u003cbr/\u003ePer-user throttle\"]\n    end\n    \n    UserInput[\"User Input\"] --\u003e SaveSettings\n    UserInput --\u003e SaveCharacter\n    UserInput --\u003e PrintChars\n    \n    ChatSave[\"Chat Save\"] --\u003e ChatBackup\n    SettingsChange[\"Settings Change\"] --\u003e SettingsAutoSave\n```\n\n**Figure 11: Debouncing and Throttling Patterns**\n\n| Function | Pattern | Delay | Purpose |\n|----------|---------|-------|---------|\n| `saveSettingsDebounced()` | Debounce | 1000ms | Wait for user to finish editing |\n| `saveCharacterDebounced()` | Debounce | 1000ms | Wait for user to finish editing |\n| `printCharactersDebounced()` | Debounce | 100ms | Batch UI updates |\n| `backupChat()` | Throttle | 10000ms | Limit backup frequency |\n| `triggerAutoSave()` | Throttle | 600000ms | Periodic auto-save |\n\nSources: [public/script.js:436-450](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:63-77](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:17-40]()\n\n### Lazy Loading\n\nCharacter data can be loaded lazily to improve initial page load time:\n\n- **Configuration**: `performance.lazyLoadCharacters` in config.yaml\n- **Behavior**: Returns minimal character data (name, avatar) without parsing full card\n- **On-Demand Load**: Full character data loaded when character is selected via `unshallowCharacter()`\n\nThis reduces memory usage and improves responsiveness for users with large character libraries.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:35](), [public/script.js:1700-1800]()\n\n### Atomic Writes\n\nAll file writes use `write-file-atomic` to prevent data corruption:\n\n```javascript\nwriteFileAtomicSync(outputPath, data);\n```\n\nThis ensures that:\n- Writes are atomic (all-or-nothing)\n- Temporary files are used during write\n- Original file is only replaced on successful write\n- No partial writes occur on crash or power loss\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:259](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:380-420]()\n\n---\n\n## API Request Patterns\n\n### Common Request Patterns\n\nMost data operations follow these request patterns:\n\n**Character Operations:**\n- `GET /api/characters/all` - Load all characters (or shallow list)\n- `POST /api/characters/get` - Load single character data\n- `POST /api/characters/create` - Create new character\n- `POST /api/characters/edit` - Update character\n- `POST /api/characters/delete` - Delete character\n\n**Chat Operations:**\n- `POST /api/chats/get` - Load chat by character and chat ID\n- `POST /api/chats/save` - Save current chat\n- `POST /api/chats/rename` - Rename chat\n- `POST /api/chats/delete` - Delete chat\n\n**Settings Operations:**\n- `POST /api/settings/get` - Load user settings\n- `POST /api/settings/save` - Save user settings\n\nAll requests include CSRF token in `X-CSRF-Token` header and session cookie for authentication.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:600-1000](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:400-700](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:100-200]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"1f:T4d13,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Authentication and Security\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [default/config.yaml](default/config.yaml)\n- [default/public/error/unauthorized.html](default/public/error/unauthorized.html)\n- [post-install.js](post-install.js)\n- [public/scripts/RossAscends-mods.js](public/scripts/RossAscends-mods.js)\n- [public/scripts/bookmarks.js](public/scripts/bookmarks.js)\n- [public/scripts/custom-request.js](public/scripts/custom-request.js)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js](public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js)\n-
