[src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js](src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js)\n- [src/endpoints/characters.js](src/endpoints/characters.js)\n- [src/endpoints/chats.js](src/endpoints/chats.js)\n- [src/endpoints/content-manager.js](src/endpoints/content-manager.js)\n- [src/endpoints/groups.js](src/endpoints/groups.js)\n- [src/endpoints/openai.js](src/endpoints/openai.js)\n- [src/endpoints/presets.js](src/endpoints/presets.js)\n- [src/endpoints/secrets.js](src/endpoints/secrets.js)\n- [src/endpoints/settings.js](src/endpoints/settings.js)\n- [src/middleware/basicAuth.js](src/middleware/basicAuth.js)\n- [src/middleware/whitelist.js](src/middleware/whitelist.js)\n- [src/prompt-converters.js](src/prompt-converters.js)\n- [src/users.js](src/users.js)\n- [src/util.js](src/util.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document describes the security mechanisms implemented in SillyTavern, including IP whitelisting, authentication methods, CSRF protection, session management, and user account isolation. The security architecture operates as a layered middleware stack that protects server endpoints from unauthorized access.\n\nFor information about secrets storage (API keys), see [Secrets Management](#2.4.2). For user directory structure and multi-user configuration, see [User Management and Multi-User Support](#2.4.1).\n\n---\n\n## Security Architecture Overview\n\nSillyTavern implements a multi-layered security architecture with three primary protection mechanisms that can be enabled independently or in combination:\n\n| Security Layer | Configuration Key | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| IP Whitelisting | `whitelistMode` | Restricts access by client IP address |\n| Basic Authentication | `basicAuthMode` | Requires HTTP Basic Auth credentials |\n| User Accounts | `enableUserAccounts` | Multi-user system with per-user authentication |\n\nAdditional security features include CSRF protection, session management, host header validation, and security validation at startup to prevent insecure configurations.\n\n### Security Middleware Stack\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Request[\"HTTP Request\"]\n    Whitelist[\"IP Whitelist Middleware\u003cbr/\u003esrc/middleware/whitelist.js\"]\n    BasicAuth[\"Basic Auth Middleware\u003cbr/\u003esrc/middleware/basicAuth.js\"]\n    UserSession[\"User Session Handler\u003cbr/\u003esrc/users.js\"]\n    CSRF[\"CSRF Protection\u003cbr/\u003ecsrf tokens\"]\n    Endpoint[\"Protected Endpoint\"]\n    \n    Request --\u003e Whitelist\n    Whitelist --\u003e|\"whitelistMode=true\"| CheckIP{\"IP in\u003cbr/\u003ewhitelist?\"}\n    CheckIP --\u003e|No| Reject403[\"403 Forbidden\"]\n    CheckIP --\u003e|Yes| BasicAuth\n    Whitelist --\u003e|\"whitelistMode=false\"| BasicAuth\n    \n    BasicAuth --\u003e|\"basicAuthMode=true\"| CheckAuth{\"Valid\u003cbr/\u003ecredentials?\"}\n    CheckAuth --\u003e|No| Reject401[\"401 Unauthorized\"]\n    CheckAuth --\u003e|Yes| UserSession\n    BasicAuth --\u003e|\"basicAuthMode=false\"| UserSession\n    \n    UserSession --\u003e CheckAccounts{\"enableUserAccounts?\"}\n    CheckAccounts --\u003e|Yes| ValidateUser{\"Valid user\u003cbr/\u003esession?\"}\n    CheckAccounts --\u003e|No| CSRF\n    ValidateUser --\u003e|No| RedirectLogin[\"Redirect to Login\"]\n    ValidateUser --\u003e|Yes| CSRF\n    \n    CSRF --\u003e CheckCSRF{\"CSRF token\u003cbr/\u003evalid?\"}\n    CheckCSRF --\u003e|No| Reject403B[\"403 Forbidden\"]\n    CheckCSRF --\u003e|Yes| Endpoint\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/middleware/whitelist.js:101-148](), [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:13-56](), [src/users.js:26-30](), [default/config.yaml:56-111]()\n\n---\n\n## IP Whitelisting\n\nIP whitelisting restricts access to SillyTavern based on the client's IP address. When enabled, only clients with IP addresses matching the whitelist can connect to the server.\n\n### Configuration\n\nThe whitelist is configured in `config.yaml` or via a `whitelist.txt` file:\n\n```yaml\n# config.yaml\nwhitelistMode: true\nwhitelist:\n  - ::1\n  - 127.0.0.1\nenableForwardedWhitelist: true\nwhitelistDockerHosts: true\n```\n\n**Whitelist Entry Types:**\n- Single IP addresses: `192.168.1.100`\n- IPv6 addresses: `::1`, `fe80::1`\n- CIDR ranges: `192.168.1.0/24`\n\nThe `whitelist.txt` file (if present) overrides the config file, with one entry per line.\n\n### Forwarded IP Support\n\nWhen SillyTavern runs behind a reverse proxy, `enableForwardedWhitelist` enables checking the client's real IP from headers:\n\n- `X-Real-IP` header (checked first)\n- `X-Forwarded-For` header (uses first IP in the list)\n\n**Security Warning:** Only enable `enableForwardedWhitelist` when using a properly configured reverse proxy, as these headers can be spoofed if the server is directly accessible.\n\n### Docker Host Auto-Whitelisting\n\nWhen running in Docker, `whitelistDockerHosts` automatically resolves and adds these hostnames to the whitelist:\n- `host.docker.internal`\n- `gateway.docker.internal`\n\nThe DNS resolution occurs during server startup in the `addDockerHostsToWhitelist()` function.\n\n### Implementation\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    getWhitelistMiddleware[\"getWhitelistMiddleware()\"]\n    validateWhitelist[\"validateWhitelist()\"]\n    addDockerHosts[\"addDockerHostsToWhitelist()\"]\n    middleware[\"Middleware Function\"]\n    \n    getWhitelistMiddleware --\u003e validateWhitelist\n    getWhitelistMiddleware --\u003e addDockerHosts\n    getWhitelistMiddleware --\u003e middleware\n    \n    middleware --\u003e getIpFromRequest[\"getIpFromRequest()\u003cbr/\u003esocket IP\"]\n    middleware --\u003e getForwardedIp[\"getForwardedIp()\u003cbr/\u003eX-Real-IP or\u003cbr/\u003eX-Forwarded-For\"]\n    middleware --\u003e isIPInWhitelist{\"isIPInWhitelist()\"}\n    \n    isIPInWhitelist --\u003e|Match| next[\"next()\"]\n    isIPInWhitelist --\u003e|No Match| return403[\"403 Forbidden\u003cbr/\u003eforbidden-by-whitelist.html\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/middleware/whitelist.js:1-148](), [default/config.yaml:56-65]()\n\n---\n\n## Basic Authentication\n\nHTTP Basic Authentication provides a simple username/password challenge for accessing SillyTavern. It operates independently of the user accounts system and can be used as an additional security layer.\n\n### Configuration Modes\n\n| Mode | Config Setting | Description |\n|---|---|---|\n| Disabled | `basicAuthMode: false` | No basic auth required |\n| Global | `basicAuthMode: true`\u003cbr/\u003e`perUserBasicAuth: false` | Single username/password for all access |\n| Per-User | `basicAuthMode: true`\u003cbr/\u003e`perUserBasicAuth: true` | Each user account has separate credentials |\n\n**Global Basic Auth Configuration:**\n```yaml\nbasicAuthMode: true\nbasicAuthUser:\n  username: \"user\"\n  password: \"password\"\nperUserBasicAuth: false\n```\n\n**Per-User Basic Auth:**\nWhen `perUserBasicAuth` is enabled, the basic auth credentials must match a valid user account's handle and password from the user accounts system.\n\n### Implementation Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant Middleware as basicAuthMiddleware\n    participant Storage as node-persist\n    participant Config as config.yaml\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eMiddleware: HTTP Request\n    Middleware-\u003e\u003eMiddleware: Check Authorization header\n    \n    alt No Auth Header\n        Middleware-\u003e\u003eClient: 401 + WWW-Authenticate header\n    else Has Auth Header\n        Middleware-\u003e\u003eMiddleware: Decode Base64 credentials\n        Middleware-\u003e\u003eMiddleware: Split username:password\n        \n        alt perUserBasicAuth=false\n            Middleware-\u003e\u003eConfig: Read basicAuthUser config\n            Middleware-\u003e\u003eMiddleware: Compare credentials\n            alt Match\n                Middleware-\u003e\u003eClient: Continue to endpoint\n            else No Match\n                Middleware-\u003e\u003eClient: 401 Unauthorized\n            end\n        else perUserBasicAuth=true\n            Middleware-\u003e\u003eStorage: Get all user handles\n            loop For each user\n                Middleware-\u003e\u003eStorage: getItem(toKey(handle))\n                Middleware-\u003e\u003eMiddleware: Check enabled \u0026\u0026 password match\n                alt Found Match\n                    Middleware-\u003e\u003eClient: Continue to endpoint\n                end\n            end\n            Middleware-\u003e\u003eClient: 401 Unauthorized (no match)\n        end\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:1-56](), [default/config.yaml:66-72](), [default/config.yaml:104-110]()\n\n---\n\n## User Accounts System\n\nThe user accounts system provides multi-user support with isolated data directories and optional password protection. Each user has a separate workspace containing their characters, chats, settings, and other content.\n\n### User Data Structure\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"User Storage\"\n        Storage[\"node-persist storage\u003cbr/\u003eDATA_ROOT/_storage/\"]\n        UserKey[\"user:handle\"]\n        AvatarKey[\"avatar:handle\"]\n        CSRFKey[\"csrfSecret\"]\n        CookieKey[\"cookieSecret\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"User Object\"\n        Handle[\"handle: string\"]\n        Name[\"name: string\"]\n        Created[\"created: timestamp\"]\n        Password[\"password: scrypt hash\"]\n        Salt[\"salt: string\"]\n        Enabled[\"enabled: boolean\"]\n        Admin[\"admin: boolean\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"User Directories\"\n        Root[\"DATA_ROOT/handle/\"]\n        Characters[\"characters/\"]\n        Chats[\"chats/\"]\n        Settings[\"settings.json\"]\n        Secrets[\"secrets.json\"]\n        Backups[\"backups/\"]\n        Extensions[\"extensions/\"]\n        Others[\"... 20+ directories\"]\n    end\n    \n    Storage --\u003e UserKey\n    Storage --\u003e AvatarKey\n    UserKey --\u003e Handle\n    UserKey --\u003e Password\n    UserKey --\u003e Admin\n    \n    Handle --\u003e Root\n    Root --\u003e Characters\n    Root --\u003e Chats\n    Root --\u003e Settings\n    Root --\u003e Secrets\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/users.js:48-103](), [src/users.js:228-428]()\n\n### User Creation and Authentication\n\nUsers are stored in `node-persist` with the key format `user:handle`. Password hashing uses the `scrypt` algorithm:\n\n**Password Hashing:**\n- Algorithm: scrypt\n- Salt: 32-byte random salt per user\n- Storage: Hash and salt stored separately in user object\n\n**User Handle:** A lowercase alphanumeric identifier used for:\n- Storage keys (`user:handle`, `avatar:handle`)\n- Directory names (`DATA_ROOT/handle/`)\n- Login identification\n\n### Security Validation at Startup\n\nThe `verifySecuritySettings()` function performs security checks when the server starts:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Start[\"Server Startup\"]\n    VerifySecurity[\"verifySecuritySettings()\"]\n    \n    Start --\u003e VerifySecurity\n    VerifySecurity --\u003e CheckListen{\"listen mode\u003cbr/\u003eenabled?\"}\n    CheckListen --\u003e|No| Pass[\"Skip all checks\"]\n    CheckListen --\u003e|Yes| CheckMode{\"Security mode\u003cbr/\u003eenabled?\"}\n    \n    CheckMode --\u003e|\"whitelistMode ||\u003cbr/\u003ebasicAuthMode\"| Pass2[\"Security OK\"]\n    CheckMode --\u003e|No Mode| CheckAccounts{\"enableUserAccounts?\"}\n    \n    CheckAccounts --\u003e|No| LogAlert[\"logSecurityAlert()\u003cbr/\u003eINSECURE CONFIG\"]\n    LogAlert --\u003e CheckOverride{\"securityOverride?\"}\n    CheckOverride --\u003e|No| Exit[\"process.exit(1)\"]\n    CheckOverride --\u003e|Yes| Warn[\"Warning + Continue\"]\n    \n    CheckAccounts --\u003e|Yes| GetUsers[\"Get all enabled users\"]\n    GetUsers --\u003e CheckPasswords{\"Any user without\u003cbr/\u003epassword?\"}\n    CheckPasswords --\u003e|Yes| WarnUser[\"Console warning\"]\n    WarnUser --\u003e CheckAdmin{\"Admin without\u003cbr/\u003epassword?\"}\n    CheckAdmin --\u003e|Yes| LogAlert2[\"logSecurityAlert()\u003cbr/\u003eADMIN UNPROTECTED\"]\n    CheckAdmin --\u003e|No| Pass3[\"Continue\"]\n    CheckPasswords --\u003e|No| Pass3\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/users.js:128-192]()\n\n---\n\n## CSRF Protection\n\nCross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) protection prevents unauthorized state-changing requests from malicious websites. SillyTavern implements CSRF protection using double-submit cookies.\n\n### Configuration\n\n```yaml\n# config.yaml\ndisableCsrfProtection: false\n```\n\n**CSRF Token Storage:**\n- For authenticated users: Stored in `node-persist` per user\n- For anonymous sessions: A random 64-byte token is generated at startup\n\n### Implementation Details\n\nThe CSRF protection system uses tokens stored and validated by the server. Each user has a unique CSRF secret that persists across sessions.\n\n**Token Generation:**\n- Format: Base64-encoded random bytes\n- Storage key: `csrfSecret` (per-user in node-persist)\n- Anonymous token: `ANON_CSRF_SECRET` (generated at startup)\n\n**Sources:** [src/users.js:30](), [default/config.yaml:149-150]()\n\n---\n\n## Session Management\n\nSession management controls how long users remain logged in and when their sessions expire.\n\n### Session Configuration\n\n```yaml\n# config.yaml\n# Session timeout in seconds\nsessionTimeout: -1  # -1: never expire, 0: expire on browser close, \u003e0: expire after N seconds of inactivity\n```\n\n| Timeout Value | Behavior |\n|---|---|\n| Negative number (e.g., -1) | Session never expires |\n| 0 | Session expires when browser closes |\n| Positive number | Session expires after N seconds of inactivity |\n\n### Cookie Security\n\nSillyTavern uses a persistent cookie secret for signing session cookies:\n\n**Cookie Secret Storage:**\n1. Check for `cookie-secret.txt` in `DATA_ROOT`\n2. If not found, generate a new random secret\n3. Store the secret persistently\n\n**Cookie Secret Path:** `DATA_ROOT/cookie-secret.txt`\n\nThe cookie secret is read at server startup and used for all session cookie signing operations.\n\n**Sources:** [default/config.yaml:144-148](), [src/users.js:38-46]()\n\n---\n\n## SSO Integration\n\nSillyTavern supports Single Sign-On (SSO) integration with identity providers via header-based authentication.\n\n### Supported SSO Providers\n\n```yaml\n# config.yaml\nsso:\n  autheliaAuth: false\n  authentikAuth: false\n```\n\n**Authelia Integration:**\n- When enabled, reads username from Authelia headers\n- Automatically logs in to matching SillyTavern account\n- Username must match exactly (lowercase)\n\n**Authentik Integration:**\n- Similar header-based authentication\n- Reads username from Authentik headers\n- Requires exact username match (lowercase)\n\n**Security Requirements:**\n- SSO providers must be configured as middleware on reverse proxy\n- Headers should be validated by the reverse proxy\n- Only enable when properly secured with reverse proxy\n\n**Sources:** [default/config.yaml:112-129](), [src/users.js:27-28]()\n\n---\n\n## Host Header Validation\n\nHost header validation prevents host header injection attacks when running in listen mode behind a reverse proxy.\n\n### Configuration\n\n```yaml\n# config.yaml\nhostWhitelist:\n  enabled: false\n  scan: true\n  hosts:\n    - example.com\n    - .trycloudflare.com  # Dot prefix allows all subdomains\n```\n\n**Host Whitelist Features:**\n- `enabled`: Enables/disables host validation\n- `scan`: Scans for potential host header spoofing\n- `hosts`: List of allowed hostnames\n- Subdomain patterns: Use `.domain.com` to allow all subdomains\n- Local access: `localhost` and IP addresses are always allowed\n\n**Sources:** [default/config.yaml:131-142]()\n\n---\n\n## Security Best Practices\n\n### Recommended Security Configuration\n\nFor production deployments, especially when `listen: true`:\n\n**Minimum Security (Choose at least one):**\n- Enable IP whitelisting with restricted range\n- Enable basic authentication\n- Enable user accounts with passwords\n\n**Enhanced Security:**\n```yaml\n# Strong configuration example\nlisten: true\nwhitelistMode: true\nwhitelist:\n  - 192.168.1.0/24  # Local network only\nbasicAuthMode: false  # Using user accounts instead\nenableUserAccounts: true\nperUserBasicAuth: false\ndisableCsrfProtection: false\nsessionTimeout: 86400  # 24 hours\nhostWhitelist:\n  enabled: true\n  scan: true\n  hosts:\n    - yourdomain.com\n```\n\n### Security Warnings and Validation\n\nThe server performs startup validation and will:\n- **Exit on insecure configuration** when `listen: true` with no security enabled (unless `securityOverride: true`)\n- **Warn about unprotected users** when user accounts exist without passwords\n- **Require admin passwords** if no other security layer is active\n\n**Security Override:**\n```yaml\nsecurityOverride: false  # Set to true to bypass security warnings (NOT RECOMMENDED)\n```\n\n### Rate Limiting Configuration\n\n```yaml\nrateLimiting:\n  preferRealIpHeader: false  # Only enable with reverse proxy\n```\n\n**Warning:** Only enable `preferRealIpHeader` when using a properly configured reverse proxy, as it uses `X-Real-IP` header which can be spoofed.\n\n### Access Logging\n\n```yaml\nlogging:\n  enableAccessLog: true  # Records connections with IP, timestamp, and user agent\n  minLogLevel: 0  # DEBUG=0, INFO=1, WARN=2, ERROR=3\n```\n\nAccess logs help monitor unauthorized access attempts and troubleshoot connectivity issues.\n\n**Sources:** [src/users.js:133-192](), [default/config.yaml:153-164]()\n\n---\n\n## API Endpoints and Security\n\nAll API endpoints are protected by the security middleware stack. The middleware is applied in this order:\n\n1. **IP Whitelist** (if enabled)\n2. **Basic Authentication** (if enabled)\n3. **User Session Validation** (if user accounts enabled)\n4. **CSRF Protection** (unless disabled)\n\n### User Session Extraction\n\nThe user session middleware extracts the current user from the request and populates `request.user`:\n\n```javascript\nrequest.user = {\n  profile: {\n    handle: string,\n    name: string,\n    avatar: string,\n    admin: boolean\n  },\n  directories: UserDirectoryList  // See User Management page\n}\n```\n\nAll subsequent endpoints can access user information and directories via `request.user`.\n\n**Sources:** [src/users.js:492-533]()\n\n---\n\n## Directory Isolation and Permissions\n\nEach user has an isolated directory structure under `DATA_ROOT/handle/`:\n\n| Directory | Purpose |\n|---|---|\n| `characters/` | Character cards (PNG with embedded JSON) |\n| `chats/` | Chat logs (JSONL format) |\n| `groups/` | Group metadata |\n| `groupChats/` | Group chat logs |\n| `worlds/` | World Info/Lorebooks |\n| `backgrounds/` | Background images |\n| `themes/` | Custom themes |\n| `extensions/` | User-specific extension data |\n| `backups/` | Chat and settings backups |\n| `secrets.json` | API keys and secrets |\n| `settings.json` | User preferences |\n\n**Directory Caching:**\nUser directories are cached in memory (`DIRECTORIES_CACHE` Map) to avoid repeated path construction. The cache is keyed by user handle.\n\n**Sources:** [src/users.js:71-103](), [src/users.js:109-126]()\n\n---\n\n## Configuration Reference\n\n### Complete Security Configuration Block\n\n```yaml\n# config.yaml - Security Section\nlisten: false\nwhitelistMode: true\nenableForwardedWhitelist: true\nwhitelist:\n  - ::1\n  - 127.0.0.1\nwhitelistDockerHosts: true\nbasicAuthMode: false\nbasicAuthUser:\n  username: \"user\"\n  password: \"password\"\nenableUserAccounts: false\nenableDiscreetLogin: false\nperUserBasicAuth: false\nsso:\n  autheliaAuth: false\n  authentikAuth: false\nhostWhitelist:\n  enabled: false\n  scan: true\n  hosts: []\nsessionTimeout: -1\ndisableCsrfProtection: false\nsecurityOverride: false\nrateLimiting:\n  preferRealIpHeader: false\nlogging:\n  enableAccessLog: true\n  minLogLevel: 0\n```\n\n**Sources:** [default/config.yaml:1-164]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"20:T5f75,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# AI [src/prompt-converters.js](src/prompt-converters.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document provides an overview of how SillyTavern integrates with 30+ Large Language Model (LLM) providers. It covers the two primary integration paradigms (Chat Completion and Text Completion), the provider abstraction layer, authentication mechanisms, and request routing architecture. For detailed information on specific subsystems, see:\n\n- Prompt construction and management: [Prompt Management and Construction](#3.3)\n- Tokenization strategies: [Tokenization System](#3.4)\n- Function calling capabilities: [Tool Calling and Function Execution](#3.5)\n- Generation parameter management: [Preset and Sampler Management](#3.6)\n\n---\n\n## Two Integration Paradigms\n\nSillyTavern supports LLM integration through two distinct API paradigms:\n\n### Chat Completion Paradigm\n\nUsed by modern conversational models that accept structured message arrays with role-based formatting (system, user, assistant). This is the primary paradigm for commercial APIs.\n\n**Key characteristics:**\n- Messages structured as `{ role: string, content: string }` objects\n- Native support for system prompts, tool calling, and multimodal inputs\n- Standardized around OpenAI's Chat Completion format\n- Automatic format conversion for provider-specific requirements\n\n### Text Completion Paradigm\n\nUsed by self-hosted inference servers and legacy models that accept raw text strings. The prompt must be pre-formatted with any special tokens or sequences.\n\n**Key characteristics:**\n- Single concatenated string input\n- Requires manual application of instruct templates and formatting\n- Common for local inference (KoboldAI, Oobaboga, llama.cpp)\n- Direct control over exact tokenization and formatting\n\nSources: [public/scripts/openai.js:175-200](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:1-100](), [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:1-100]()\n\n---\n\n## Supported Providers\n\n### Chat Completion Providers\n\nThe system defines chat completion sources in `chat_completion_sources`:\n\n| Provider | Constant | API Key Required | Notes |\n|----------|----------|------------------|-------|\n| OpenAI | `OPENAI` | Yes | GPT-4, GPT-3.5, o1, o3 models |\n| Claude | `CLAUDE` | Yes | Anthropic Claude models |\n| Google AI Studio | `MAKERSUITE` | Yes | Gemini models |\n| Google Vertex AI | `VERTEXAI` | Yes | Vertex-hosted Gemini |\n| OpenRouter | `OPENROUTER` | Yes | Aggregator for 100+ models |\n| Mistral AI | `MISTRALAI` | Yes | Mistral family |\n| Cohere | `COHERE` | Yes | Command models |\n| Perplexity | `PERPLEXITY` | Yes | Sonar models |\n| Groq | `GROQ` | Yes | Fast inference |\n| AI21 | `AI21` | Yes | Jamba models |\n| xAI | `XAI` | Yes | Grok models |\n| DeepSeek | `DEEPSEEK` | Yes | DeepSeek Chat |\n| Azure OpenAI | `AZURE_OPENAI` | Yes | Enterprise OpenAI |\n| Custom | `CUSTOM` | Optional | OpenAI-compatible endpoints |\n| 10+ more | Various | Yes | See source for complete list |\n\nSources: [public/scripts/openai.js:175-200]()\n\n### Text Completion Providers\n\nDefined in `textgen_types`:\n\n| Provider | Constant | Type | API Key Required |\n|----------|----------|------|------------------|\n| Oobaboga | `OOBA` | Self-hosted | Optional |\n| KoboldCpp | `KOBOLDCPP` | Self-hosted | Optional |\n| llama.cpp | `LLAMACPP` | Self-hosted | Optional |\n| Ollama | `OLLAMA` | Self-hosted | No |\n| TabbyAPI | `TABBY` | Self-hosted | Optional |\n| vLLM | `VLLM` | Self-hosted | Optional |\n| Aphrodite | `APHRODITE` | Self-hosted | Optional |\n| Mancer | `MANCER` | Commercial | Yes |\n| TogetherAI | `TOGETHERAI` | Commercial | Yes |\n| OpenRouter | `OPENROUTER` | Commercial | Yes |\n| DreamGen | `DREAMGEN` | Commercial | Yes |\n| Featherless | `FEATHERLESS` | Commercial | Yes |\n\nSources: [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:31-47]()\n\n---\n\n## System Architecture\n\n### High-Level Request Flow\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Frontend[\"Frontend (public/scripts/)\"]\n        Generate[\"Generate()\u003cbr/\u003escript.js\"]\n        sendOpenAIRequest[\"sendOpenAIRequest()\u003cbr/\u003eopenai.js\"]\n        PromptManager[\"PromptManager\u003cbr/\u003esetupChatCompletionPromptManager()\"]\n        oai_settings[\"oai_settings\u003cbr/\u003echat_completion_source\"]\n        textgen_settings[\"textgenerationwebui_settings\u003cbr/\u003etype\"]\n        countTokensOpenAI[\"countTokensOpenAIAsync()\u003cbr/\u003etokenizers.js\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Backend[\"Backend (src/endpoints/backends/)\"]\n        ChatCompRouter[\"router.post('/generate')\u003cbr/\u003echat-completions.js\"]\n        TextCompRouter[\"router.post('/generate')\u003cbr/\u003etext-completions.js\"]\n        convertClaudeMessages[\"convertClaudeMessages()\u003cbr/\u003eprompt-converters.js\"]\n        convertGooglePrompt[\"convertGooglePrompt()\u003cbr/\u003eprompt-converters.js\"]\n        readSecret[\"readSecret(directories, key)\u003cbr/\u003esecrets.js\"]\n        TokenizerRoutes[\"router.post('/*/encode')\u003cbr/\u003etokenizers.js\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph External[\"External APIs\"]\n        OpenAIAPI[\"api.openai.com/v1\"]\n        ClaudeAPI[\"api.anthropic.com/v1\"]\n        GoogleAPI[\"generativelanguage.googleapis.com\"]\n        LocalServers[\"localhost:* (KoboldCpp/Ooba/llama.cpp)\"]\n    end\n    \n    Generate --\u003e PromptManager\n    Generate --\u003e countTokensOpenAI\n    PromptManager --\u003e sendOpenAIRequest\n    sendOpenAIRequest --\u003e oai_settings\n    \n    oai_settings --\u003e ChatCompRouter\n    textgen_settings --\u003e TextCompRouter\n    \n    countTokensOpenAI --\u003e TokenizerRoutes\n    \n    ChatCompRouter --\u003e readSecret\n    ChatCompRouter --\u003e convertClaudeMessages\n    ChatCompRouter --\u003e convertGooglePrompt\n    TextCompRouter --\u003e readSecret\n    \n    convertClaudeMessages --\u003e ClaudeAPI\n    convertGooglePrompt --\u003e GoogleAPI\n    ChatCompRouter --\u003e OpenAIAPI\n    TextCompRouter --\u003e LocalServers\n```\n\n**Diagram: Core AI Integration Request Flow with Code Entities**\n\nThe system uses a three-tier architecture:\n\n1. **Frontend Layer**: Manages UI, prompt construction, and API selection\n2. **Backend Proxy Layer**: Handles authentication, format conversion, and request routing\n3. **External Services**: The actual LLM providers\n\nSources: [public/scripts/openai.js:1-100](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:1-100](), [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:1-100]()\n\n---\n\n## API Connection Management\n\n### Connection Configuration\n\nThe system uses the `CONNECT_API_MAP` to manage API connections:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph GlobalState[\"Global State (script.js)\"]\n        main_api[\"main_api\u003cbr/\u003e'openai'|'textgenerationwebui'|'kobold'|'novel'\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph OAISettings[\"oai_settings (openai.js)\"]\n        chat_completion_source[\"chat_completion_source\u003cbr/\u003echat_completion_sources.OPENAI|CLAUDE|etc\"]\n        openai_model[\"openai_model\u003cbr/\u003e'gpt-4-turbo'\"]\n        reverse_proxy[\"reverse_proxy\u003cbr/\u003eURL string\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph TextGenSettings[\"textgenerationwebui_settings\"]\n        type[\"type\u003cbr/\u003etextgen_types.OOBA|KOBOLDCPP|OLLAMA|etc\"]\n        server_urls[\"server_urls[type]\u003cbr/\u003eendpoint URL\"]\n        ooba_model[\"*_model\u003cbr/\u003emodel identifier\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ConnectionMap[\"CONNECT_API_MAP (slash-commands.js)\"]\n        MapEntries[\"Key-value mappings:\u003cbr/\u003e- 'openai' → {selected: 'openai', source: OPENAI}\u003cbr/\u003e- 'koboldcpp' → {selected: 'textgenerationwebui', type: KOBOLDCPP}\u003cbr/\u003e- 'claude' → {selected: 'openai', source: CLAUDE}\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph UIElements[\"UI Elements\"]\n        api_button[\"$('#api_button').click()\"]\n        chat_completion_source_select[\"$('#chat_completion_source').change()\"]\n        textgen_type_select[\"$('#textgen_type').change()\"]\n    end\n    \n    main_api --\u003e MapEntries\n    MapEntries --\u003e chat_completion_source\n    MapEntries --\u003e type\n    \n    chat_completion_source --\u003e chat_completion_source_select\n    type --\u003e textgen_type_select\n    \n    api_button --\u003e main_api\n```\n\n**Diagram: API Connection Configuration with Code Symbols**\n\nThe `/api` slash command ([public/scripts/slash-commands.js:237-339]()) uses `CONNECT_API_MAP` to switch between APIs:\n\n```javascript\nconst apiConfig = CONNECT_API_MAP[text?.toString()?.toLowerCase() ?? ''];\nif (apiConfig.source) {\n    oai_settings.chat_completion_source = apiConfig.source;\n}\nif (apiConfig.type) {\n    textgenerationwebui_settings.type = apiConfig.type;\n}\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/slash-commands.js:132-215]()\n\n### Authentication and Secrets\n\nAPI keys are managed through the Secrets system:\n\n| Storage Location | Purpose |\n|------------------|---------|\n| `secrets.json` (server) | Encrypted storage of API keys |\n| `SECRET_KEYS` enum | Standardized key identifiers |\n| `secret_state` (client) | Boolean flags for key existence |\n| `readSecret()` (server) | Secure key retrieval |\n| `writeSecret()` (client/server) | Key storage with encryption |\n\n**Key secret identifiers:**\n- `SECRET_KEYS.OPENAI` - OpenAI API key\n- `SECRET_KEYS.CLAUDE` - Anthropic API key\n- `SECRET_KEYS.MAKERSUITE` - Google AI Studio key\n- `SECRET_KEYS.OPENROUTER` - OpenRouter key\n- 40+ more defined in [public/scripts/secrets.js:17-67]()\n\nSources: [public/scripts/secrets.js:17-67](), [src/endpoints/secrets.js:8-64]()\n\n---\n\n## Request Routing and Format Conversion\n\n### Chat Completion Request Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant UI as \"Frontend UI\"\n    participant OpenAI as \"openai.js\u003cbr/\u003esendOpenAIRequest()\"\n    participant Backend as \"chat-completions.js\u003cbr/\u003erouter\"\n    participant Converter as \"prompt-converters.js\"\n    participant Secrets as \"readSecret()\"\n    participant Provider as \"External API\"\n    \n    UI-\u003e\u003eOpenAI: Generate() called\n    OpenAI-\u003e\u003eOpenAI: setupChatCompletionPromptManager()\n    OpenAI-\u003e\u003eOpenAI: Build MessageCollection\n    OpenAI-\u003e\u003eBackend: POST /api/backends/chat-completions/generate\n    Note over OpenAI,Backend: Payload: {model, messages, source, ...}\n    \n    Backend-\u003e\u003eSecrets: readSecret(directories, SECRET_KEY)\n    Secrets--\u003e\u003eBackend: API key\n    \n    alt Source = OpenAI\n        Backend-\u003e\u003eBackend: Direct pass-through\n    else Source = Claude\n        Backend-\u003e\u003eConverter: convertClaudeMessages()\n        Converter--\u003e\u003eBackend: {messages, systemPrompt}\n    else Source = Google\n        Backend-\u003e\u003eConverter: convertGooglePrompt()\n        Converter--\u003e\u003eBackend: {contents, systemInstruction}\n    else Source = Others\n        Backend-\u003e\u003eConverter: convertXXXMessages()\n    end\n    \n    Backend-\u003e\u003eProvider: HTTP POST with API key\n    \n    alt Streaming Enabled\n        Provider--\u003e\u003eBackend: SSE stream chunks\n        Backend--\u003e\u003eOpenAI: forwardFetchResponse()\n        OpenAI--\u003e\u003eUI: StreamingProcessor\n    else Non-streaming\n        Provider--\u003e\u003eBackend: Complete JSON response\n        Backend--\u003e\u003eOpenAI: Response JSON\n    end\n    \n    OpenAI-\u003e\u003eUI: Update chat with response\n```\n\n**Diagram: Chat Completion Request Sequence**\n\nSources: [public/scripts/openai.js:525-600](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:200-385](), [src/prompt-converters.js:188-285]()\n\n### Provider-Specific Format Conversion\n\nThe [src/prompt-converters.js]() module handles translation between OpenAI's ChatML format and provider-specific formats:\n\n| Provider | Converter Function | Input Format | Output Format |\n|----------|-------------------|--------------|---------------|\n| Claude | `convertClaudeMessages()` | `{role, content}[]` | `{messages[], systemPrompt[]}` with `tool_use` blocks |\n| Google | `convertGooglePrompt()` | `{role, content}[]` | `{contents[], systemInstruction}` with `parts[]` |\n| Cohere | `convertCohereMessages()` | `{role, content}[]` | `{chatHistory[], message}` |\n| Mistral | `convertMistralMessages()` | `{role, content}[]` | Standard with `thinking[]` parts |\n| AI21 | `convertAI21Messages()` | `{role, content}[]` | `{system, messages[]}` flattened |\n| xAI | `convertXAIMessages()` | `{role, content}[]` | `{system, messages[]}` |\n\n**Post-processing via `postProcessPrompt(messages, type, names)`** ([src/prompt-converters.js:85-105]()):\n- `PROMPT_PROCESSING_TYPE.MERGE`: Calls `mergeMessages()` with `{strict: false}`\n- `PROMPT_PROCESSING_TYPE.MERGE_TOOLS`: Merge preserving `tool_calls`\n- `PROMPT_PROCESSING_TYPE.STRICT`: Ensures alternating roles with placeholders\n- `PROMPT_PROCESSING_TYPE.SINGLE`: Combines all into single message\n\nApplied via `oai_settings.custom_prompt_post_processing` setting.\n\nSources: [src/prompt-converters.js:85-105](), [src/prompt-converters.js:188-285](), [src/prompt-converters.js:1-60]()\n\n---\n\n## Text Completion Integration\n\n### Backend Routing\n\nText completion requests route through `/api/backends/text-completions`:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Frontend Configuration\"\n        TextGenSettings[\"\u003ccode\u003etextgenerationwebui_settings\u003c/code\u003e\"]\n        TypeSelector[\"\u003ccode\u003etextgen_settings.type\u003c/code\u003e\"]\n        ServerURL[\"\u003ccode\u003eserver_urls[type]\u003c/code\u003e\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Backend Router\"\n        TextCompRouter[\"\u003ccode\u003e/api/backends/text-completions\u003c/code\u003e\"]\n        StatusCheck[\"\u003ccode\u003ePOST /status\u003c/code\u003e\"]\n        Generate[\"\u003ccode\u003ePOST /generate\u003c/code\u003e\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Type-Specific Handlers\"\n        OobaHandler[\"Oobaboga\u003cbr/\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/api/v1/generate\u003c/code\u003e\"]\n        KoboldHandler[\"KoboldCpp\u003cbr/\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/api/v1/generate\u003c/code\u003e\"]\n        OllamaHandler[\"Ollama\u003cbr/\u003e\u003ccode\u003eparseOllamaStream()\u003c/code\u003e\"]\n        TabbyHandler[\"TabbyAPI\u003cbr/\u003e\u003ccode\u003e/v1/completions\u003c/code\u003e\"]\n    end\n    \n    TypeSelector --\u003e ServerURL\n    TextGenSettings --\u003e TextCompRouter\n    \n    TextCompRouter --\u003e StatusCheck\n    TextCompRouter --\u003e Generate\n    \n    Generate --\u003e OobaHandler\n    Generate --\u003e KoboldHandler\n    Generate --\u003e OllamaHandler\n    Generate --\u003e TabbyHandler\n```\n\n**Diagram: Text Completion Backend Architecture**\n\n### Sampler Configuration\n\nText completion backends expose fine-grained sampler control:\n\n**Oobaboga samplers** (in priority order from `sampler_priority`):\n```\nrepetition_penalty → presence_penalty → frequency_penalty → dry → \ntemperature → dynamic_temperature → top_k → top_p → typical_p → \nepsilon_cutoff → eta_cutoff → tfs → top_a → min_p → mirostat → xtc\n```\n\n**llama.cpp samplers** (from `samplers` array):\n```\npenalties → dry → top_n_sigma → top_k → typ_p → top_p → \nmin_p → xtc → temperature → adaptive_p\n```\n\n**Aphrodite samplers** (from `samplers_priorities`):\n```\ndry → penalties → no_repeat_ngram → temperature → top_nsigma → \ntop_p_top_k → top_a → min_p → tfs → eta_cutoff → epsilon_cutoff → \ntypical_p → quadratic → xtc\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:67-116](), [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:1-100]()\n\n---\n\n## Streaming Response Handling\n\nBoth paradigms support Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming:\n\n### Chat Completion Streaming\n\nFrontend handling via `StreamingProcessor`:\n\n```javascript\n// From sse-stream.js\nconst eventStream = getEventSourceStream(url, {\n    signal: abortController.signal,\n    headers: getRequestHeaders()\n});\n\nfor await (const event of eventStream) {\n    const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n    const text = data.choices[0]?.delta?.content || '';\n    onProgress(text);\n}\n```\n\n### Text Completion Streaming\n\nBackend transforms provider-specific formats to standard SSE:\n\n| Provider | Stream Format | Transformation |\n|----------|---------------|----------------|\n| Oobaboga | JSON chunks with `event: text_stream` | Parse and forward |\n| KoboldCpp | JSON with `token` field | Extract and wrap |\n| Ollama | Custom format | `parseOllamaStream()` converts |\n| llama.cpp | SSE standard | Direct forward |\n\nSources: [public/scripts/sse-stream.js:1-100](), [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:23-72]()\n\n---\n\n## Model Selection and Capabilities\n\n### Dynamic Model Loading\n\nMany providers support dynamic model enumeration via status check endpoints:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant UI as \"$('#api_button').click()\"\n    participant Status as \"getStatusTextgen()\"\n    participant Backend as \"POST /api/backends/text-completions/status\"\n    participant Provider as \"Provider /models or /v1/models\"\n    participant Loader as \"loadOpenRouterModels(data)\"\n    participant Cache as \"openRouterModels[]\"\n    participant Select as \"$('#model_openrouter_select')\"\n    \n    UI-\u003e\u003eStatus: Trigger connection check\n    Status-\u003e\u003eBackend: {api_server, api_type}\n    Backend-\u003e\u003eProvider: GET /models\n    Provider--\u003e\u003eBackend: Model list JSON\n    Backend--\u003e\u003eStatus: {data: models[]}\n    Status-\u003e\u003eLoader: loadOpenRouterModels(data)\n    Loader-\u003e\u003eCache: Store model array\n    Loader-\u003e\u003eSelect: Populate \u003coption\u003e elements\n    Select--\u003e\u003eUI: Updated dropdown\n```\n\n**Diagram: Dynamic Model Discovery Flow**\n\n**Model list caching variables** ([public/scripts/textgen-models.js:11-21]()):\n- `openRouterModels[]` - OpenRouter model metadata with pricing\n- `mancerModels[]` - Mancer available models  \n- `togetherModels[]` - TogetherAI models sorted by ID\n- `ollamaModels[]` - Ollama local models\n- `tabbyModels[]` - TabbyAPI loaded models\n- Refreshed on status check or manual reload\n\n**Loading functions:**\n- `loadOpenRouterModels(data)` - [public/scripts/textgen-models.js:204-400]()\n- `loadOllamaModels(data)` - [public/scripts/textgen-models.js:104-122]()\n- `loadTogetherAIModels(data)` - [public/scripts/textgen-models.js:172-198]()\n\nSources: [public/scripts/textgen-models.js:11-400](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:658-707]()\n\n---\n\n## API-Specific Features\n\n### Claude-Specific Features\n\n- **Prompt caching**: Ephemeral cache for system prompts and tools ([src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:250-268]())\n- **Thinking mode**: Extended reasoning with budget tokens ([src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:310-329]())\n- **Prefill**: Assistant message prefilling ([public/scripts/openai.js:100-103]())\n\n### Google Gemini-Specific Features\n\n- **Thinking configuration**: `thinkingConfig` with budget and level ([src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:531-550]())\n- **Safety settings**: Configurable harm categories ([src/constants.js:141-163]())\n- **Function calling config**: Mode-based tool usage control ([src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:566-591]())\n\n### OpenRouter-Specific Features\n\n- **Provider selection**: Filter by provider with `openrouter_providers` ([public/scripts/openai.js:290-291]())\n- **Fallback models**: Enable with `openrouter_allow_fallbacks` ([public/scripts/openai.js:292]())\n- **Middle-out**: Transform for long contexts ([src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:157-166]())\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:200-600]()\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling and Connection Status\n\n### Status Checking and Connection Management\n\nAPI connectivity is verified through status check endpoints:\n\n**Text Completion Status** - `getStatusTextgen()` ([public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:658-707]()):\n```javascript\nconst response = await fetch('/api/backends/text-completions/status', {\n    method: 'POST',\n    body: JSON.stringify({\n        api_server: getTextGenServer(),\n        api_type: textgenerationwebui_settings.type\n    })\n});\n// Calls loadOllamaModels(), loadMancerModels(), etc. based on type\n```\n\n**Chat Completion Status** - `checkOnlineStatus()` ([public/scripts/openai.js:1-100]()):\n- Checks `/api/backends/chat-completions/status` for model availability\n- Validates API keys via `SECRET_KEYS` presence in `secret_state`\n- Auto-connects if `power_user.auto_connect` is enabled\n\n**Status display** - `setOnlineStatus(value)`:\n- `\"no_connection\"` - Red indicator, disables send button\n- Model name or tier string - Green indicator, enables generation\n- Updates `online_status` global variable\n- Triggers UI updates via `RA_checkOnlineStatus()` ([public/scripts/RossAscends-mods.js:324-353]())\n\n**Bypass for custom endpoints:**\n- `oai_settings.bypass_status_check` - Skip chat completion status check\n- `textgenerationwebui_settings.bypass_status_check` - Skip text completion status check\n- Useful for custom proxies or unsupported API-compatible endpoints\n\nSources: [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:658-707](), [public/scripts/RossAscends-mods.js:324-353](), [public/scripts/openai.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration Points for Extensions\n\nExtensions can interact with AI integration through several mechanisms:\n\n### Extension Integration Points\n\nExtensions integrate with AI providers through several standardized APIs:\n\n**Multimodal Vision Captioning** - `getMultimodalCaption(base64Img, prompt)` ([public/scripts/extensions/shared.js:16-200]()):\n```javascript\nconst caption = await getMultimodalCaption(base64Image, prompt);\n// Routes to appropriate provider based on extension_settings.caption.multimodal_api\n// Supports: openai, anthropic, google, vertexai, mistral, ollama, llamacpp, koboldcpp, etc.\n```\n\n**Generation Lifecycle Hooks:**\n- `event_types.GENERATION_STARTED` - Fired before request sent, allows settings modification\n- `event_types.GENERATION_ENDED` - Fired after response received\n- `event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED` - Fired when message added to chat\n- Extensions can modify `oai_settings`, `textgen_settings`, or request payload\n\n**Prompt Injection** - `setExtensionPrompt(identifier, text, position, depth, role)` ([public/scripts/script.js]()):\n```javascript\nsetExtensionPrompt('my-extension', 'Additional context', \n    extension_prompt_types.IN_CHAT, 0, extension_prompt_roles.SYSTEM);\n// Injects into prompt assembly pipeline at specified depth\n```\n\n**Custom Headers** - Additional Headers system:\n- Extensions can add custom HTTP headers to API requests\n- Configured per-provider via Additional Headers UI\n- Applied in backend request handlers before forwarding\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/shared.js:16-200](), [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js:1-150](), [public/scripts/script.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\nSillyTavern's AI integration system provides:\n\n1. **Unified abstraction** over 30+ LLM providers through two paradigms\n2. **Automatic format conversion** between ChatML and provider-specific formats\n3. **Secure secret management** with encrypted API key storage\n4. **Streaming support** with SSE for real-time responses\n5. **Dynamic model discovery** for providers that support it\n6. **Provider-specific features** (caching, thinking modes, tool calling)\n7. **Robust error handling** with connection status monitoring\n8. **Extension integration** for advanced use cases\n\nThe architecture separates concerns cleanly: the frontend handles user interaction and prompt construction, the backend manages authentication and format conversion, and provider-specific logic is isolated into dedicated modules.\n\nFor implementation details on specific aspects, refer to the subsection pages listed at the beginning of this document.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/openai.js](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js](), [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js](), Purpose and Scope\n\nThe Chat Completion System handles message-based interactions with Large Language Models (LLMs) using the ChatML (Chat Markup Language) message format. This system provides a unified interface for communicating with 20+ chat completion API providers including OpenAI, Claude, Google AI, Mistral, Groq, and many others.\n\n**Related pages:**\n- For text completion APIs (KoboldAI, Oobaboga, etc.), see [Text Generation Backends](#3.2)\n- For provider-specific prompt format conversion, see [Provider Adapters and Prompt Conversion](#3.1.1)\n- For tool calling capabilities, see [Tool Calling and Function Execution](#3.5)\n- For reasoning/thinking content display, see [Reasoning Display and Model Thinking](#3.7)\n\nThe system manages:\n- **Message formatting**: Converting internal chat history to provider-specific formats\n- **Authentication**: Secure API key management via the Secrets system\n- **Request routing**: Backend proxy layer for multiple providers\n- **Response handling**: Both streaming (SSE) and non-streaming responses\n- **Multimodal content**: Image, video, and audio attachments in messages\n\n---\n\n## System Structure\n\nThe system uses a standardized internal message format that is converted to provider-specific formats:\n\n```javascript\n{\n  role: 'user' | 'assistant' | 'system' | 'tool',\n  content: string | Array\u003cContentPart\u003e,\n  name?: string,\n  tool_calls?: ToolCall[],\n  tool_call_id?: string,\n  extra?: {\n    media?: MediaAttachment[],\n    tool_invocations?: ToolInvocation[],\n    reasoning_signature?: string\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Message Construction Pipeline\n\n**Diagram: Message Processing from Raw Chat to Final Prompt**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Chat[\"chat[] PromptConv\n    SendCohere --\u003e OtherAPIs\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:1-1441]()\n\n### Provider Routing Table\n\nThe backend routes requests based on [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:205-1182]()\n\n### Authentication and Secrets Management\n\nAPI keys are managed through the `SecretManager` class:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n other keys\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/secrets.js:109-336](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:1-1441]()\n\n---\n\n## Request Body Construction\n\n### Common Parameters\n\nAll chat completion requests include these base parameters:\n\n| Parameter | Type | Purpose | Source |\n|-----------|------|---------|--------|\n| `messages` | `Array\u003cMessage\u003e` | Chat history in ChatML format | Built by `ChatCompletion.build()` |\n| `model` | `String` | Model identifier | `oai_settings.openai_model` or provider-specific |\n| `max_tokens` | `Number` | Maximum response tokens | `oai_settings.openai_max_tokens` |\n| `temperature` | `Number` | Sampling temperature | `oai_settings.temp_openai` |\n| `stream` | `Boolean` | Enable SSE streaming | `oai_settings.stream_openai` |\n| `stop` | `Array\u003cString\u003e` | Custom stopping strings | `getCustomStoppingStrings()` |\n\n### Provider-Specific Extensions\n\nDifferent providers support additional parameters:\n\n**Claude ([src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:238-299]())**:\n```javascript\n{\n  system: [{ type: 'text', text: '...' }],  // System prompt\n  tools: [...],                              // Function definitions\n  thinking: { type: 'enabled', budget_tokens: 4096 },\n  tool_choice: { type: 'auto' }\n}\n```\n\n**Google AI ([src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:452-595]())**:\n```javascript\n{\n  contents: [...],                    // Messages\n  systemInstruction: {...},          // System prompt\n  generationConfig: {\n    thinkingConfig: { \n      includeThoughts: true,\n      thinkingBudget: 8192\n    }\n  },\n  tools: [{ function_declarations: [...] }]\n}\n```\n\n**OpenAI-compatible ([src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:852-923]())**:\n```javascript\n{\n  messages: [...],\n  tools: [...],\n  tool_choice: 'auto',\n  reasoning_effort: 'medium',    // For o1/o3 models\n  verbosity: 'medium',           // For GPT-5+ models\n  seed: -1,\n  logprobs: true\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:205-1043]()\n\n---\n\n## Streaming System\n\n### SSE (Server-Sent Events) Flow\n\nThe streaming system uses Server-Sent Events for real-time response delivery:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Frontend as Frontend\u003cbr/\u003eopenai.js\n    participant SSE as getEventSourceStream()\u003cbr/\u003esse-stream.js\n    participant Backend as Backend Proxy\n    participant Provider as LLM Provider\n    \n    Frontend-\u003e\u003eBackend: POST /generate\u003cbr/\u003estream: true\n    Backend-\u003e\u003eProvider: POST with stream=true\n    \n    loop For each token\n        Provider--\u003e\u003eBackend: data: {\"choices\":[{\"delta\":{\"content\":\"token\"}}]}\n        Backend--\u003e\u003eSSE: Forward SSE event\n        SSE-\u003e\u003eSSE: Parse event\n        SSE--\u003e\u003eFrontend: Parsed chunk\n        Frontend-\u003e\u003eFrontend: Append to message\n        Frontend-\u003e\u003eFrontend: Update DOM\n    end\n    \n    Provider--\u003e\u003eBackend: data: [DONE]\n    Backend--\u003e\u003eFrontend: Close stream\n    Frontend-\u003e\u003eFrontend: Finalize message\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/sse-stream.js](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:716-1043]()\n\n### StreamingProcessor Implementation\n\nThe frontend uses `StreamingProcessor` to handle incoming chunks. The core loop in `sendOpenAIRequest()` ([public/scripts/openai.js:2381-2450]()):\n\n```javascript\nconst eventStream = getEventSourceStream(response);\nfor await (const event of eventStream) {\n    if (event.data === '[DONE]') break;\n    \n    const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n    const chunk = data?.choices?.[0]?.delta?.content || '';\n    \n    // Append to message text\n    messageText += chunk;\n    \n    // Update DOM with streamed content\n    streamingProcessor.updateMessageBlock(messageId, messageText);\n}\n```\n\nThe `streamingProcessor` instance handles DOM updates, reasoning block rendering, and fade-in effects during streaming.\n\n### Response Forwarding\n\nThe backend uses `forwardFetchResponse()` to pipe provider streams directly to the client with zero buffering:\n\n```javascript\nif (request.body.stream) {\n    // Pipe remote SSE stream directly to Express response\n    // No transformation, minimal latency\n    forwardFetchResponse(generateResponse, response);\n}\n```\n\nExample from Claude handler ([src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:360-362]()):\n```javascript\nif (request.body.stream) {\n    forwardFetchResponse(generateResponse, response);\n}\n```\n\nThis approach:\n- **Minimizes latency**: Zero buffering between provider and client\n- **Reduces memory usage**: Streaming passthrough without accumulation\n- **Preserves SSE format**: Direct forwarding of raw event stream\n- **Handles backpressure**: Node.js streams automatically manage flow control\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:360-362](), [src/util.js:forwardFetchResponse]()\n\n---\n\n## Multimodal Content Support\n\n### Media Attachment Types\n\nThe system supports three types of media attachments:\n\n| Type | MIME Prefix | Supported By |\n|------|-------------|--------------|\n| Image | `image/*` | OpenAI, Claude, Google, Groq, OpenRouter, Custom |\n| Video | `video/*` | Google, Claude (beta), Z.AI |\n| Audio | `audio/*` | Google |\n\n### Image Inlining Flow\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    CheckSupport[\"isImageInliningSupported()\u003cbr/\u003eopenai.js:2698-2730\"]\n    CheckMedia[\"message.extra.media\"]\n    AddImage[\"chatMessage.addImage(url)\u003cbr/\u003eopenai.js:919\"]\n    \n    subgraph MessageContent[\"Message Content Array\"]\n        TextPart[\"{ type: 'text', text: '...' }\"]\n        ImagePart[\"{ type: 'image_url',\u003cbr/\u003eimage_url: { url: 'data:image/...' } }\"]\n    end\n    \n    Convert[\"Provider Conversion\"]\n    \n    subgraph ProviderFormats[\"Provider-Specific Formats\"]\n        ClaudeImg[\"{ type: 'image',\u003cbr/\u003esource: { type: 'base64',\u003cbr/\u003emedia_type: 'image/jpeg',\u003cbr/\u003edata: '...' } }\"]\n        GoogleImg[\"{ inlineData: {\u003cbr/\u003emimeType: 'image/jpeg',\u003cbr/\u003edata: '...' } }\"]\n        OpenAIImg[\"{ type: 'image_url',\u003cbr/\u003eimage_url: { url: 'data:...' } }\"]\n [public/scripts/openai.js:911-927](), [src/prompt-converters.js:270-308]()\n\n### Video Support\n\nVideo inlining is supported by Google AI and Z.AI:\n\n**Google format** ([src/prompt-converters.js:1056-1075]()):\n```javascript\n{\n  inlineData: {\n    mimeType: 'video/mp4',\n    data: base64VideoData\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Z.AI format** ([src/endpoints/openai.js:206-218]()):\n```javascript\n{\n  type: 'video_url',\n  video_url: {\n    url: 'data:video/mp4;base64,...'\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Multimodal Captioning\n\nThe system can generate captions for media using vision models ([public/scripts/extensions/shared.js:16-155]()):\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Image[\"Image/Video Data\"]\n    GetCaption[\"getMultimodalCaption(base64, prompt)\u003cbr/\u003eshared.js:16-155\"]\n    Thumbnail[\"createThumbnail()\u003cbr/\u003eCompress if \u003e 2MB\"]\n    Backend[\"/api/openai/caption-image\u003cbr/\u003eopenai.js:15-270\"]\n    Vision[\"Vision Model\u003cbr/\u003e(GPT-4o, Claude Opus, Gemini, etc.)\"]\n    Caption[\"Caption Text\"]\n    \n    Image --\u003e GetCaption\n    GetCaption --\u003e Thumbnail\n    Thumbnail --\u003e Backend\n    Backend --\u003e Vision\n    Vision --\u003e Caption\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/shared.js:16-155](), [src/endpoints/openai.js:15-270](), [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js:1-600]()\n\n---\n\n## Tool Calling Support\n\nChat completion APIs support function/tool calling for models that provide it. The system:\n\n1. **Defines tools** in OpenAI format\n2. **Converts to provider format** (e.g., Claude's `input_schema`, Google's `function_declarations`)\n3. **Receives tool calls** in responses\n4. **Executes locally** via `ToolManager`\n5. **Submits results** back to the model\n\nSee [Tool Calling and Function Execution](#3.5) for detailed documentation.\n\n**Key integration points:**\n- Tool definitions: [public/scripts/openai.js:2243-2246]()\n- Claude conversion: [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:260-269]()\n- Google conversion: [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:499-522]()\n- Tool result handling: [public/scripts/openai.js:2460-2502]()\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:2243-2502](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:260-522]()\n\n---\n\n## Reasoning and Thinking Display\n\nModels with reasoning capabilities (o1, Claude Opus 4, Gemini 2.5+) can output thinking/reasoning content:\n\n### Thinking Signature System\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Message[\"Message with extra.reasoning_signature\"]\n    Check[\"Check if same API + model\u003cbr/\u003eopenai.js:578-582\"]\n    Keep[\"Keep signature\u003cbr/\u003eSend with message\"]\n    Remove[\"Remove signature\u003cbr/\u003eDon't send\"]\n    Display[\"Display thinking blocks\u003cbr/\u003ewith special styling\"]\n    \n    Message --\u003e Check\n    Check --\u003e|Same| Keep\n    Check --\u003e|Different| Remove\n    Keep --\u003e Display\n```\n\nThe system validates that thinking content was generated by the same API and model before sending it back, preventing cross-model contamination ([public/scripts/openai.js:578-595]()).\n\n### Provider-Specific Reasoning\n\n**Claude** ([src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:310-329]()):\n```javascript\nif (useThinking) {\n  requestBody.thinking = {\n    type: 'enabled',\n    budget_tokens: budgetTokens\n  };\n}\n```\n\n**Google** ([src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:531-550]()):\n```javascript\ngenerationConfig.thinkingConfig = {\n  includeThoughts: true,\n  thinkingBudget: 8192  // or thinkingLevel: 'medium'\n};\n```\n\n**OpenAI** ([src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:1004-1009]()):\n```javascript\n{\n  reasoning_effort: 'medium',  // For o1/o3 models\n  verbosity: 'high'            // For GPT-5 models\n}\n```\n\nSee [Reasoning Display and Model Thinking](#3.7) for UI rendering details.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:578-595](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:310-550](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:1004-1009]()\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling and Validation\n\n### API Key Validation\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Request[\"Request to chat completion\"]\n    CheckSource[\"Determine provider\u003cbr/\u003echat_completion_source\"]\n    CheckKey[\"readSecret(directories, key)\"]\n    \n    HasKey{Key exists?}\n    CheckRP{Reverse\u003cbr/\u003eproxy set?}\n    \n    UseKey[\"Use API key\u003cbr/\u003ein Authorization header\"]\n    UseProxy[\"Use reverse proxy\u003cbr/\u003ewith proxy_password\"]\n    Error[\"Return 400\u003cbr/\u003e'API key missing'\"]\n    \n    Request --\u003e CheckSource\n    CheckSource --\u003e CheckKey\n    CheckKey --\u003e HasKey\n    HasKey --\u003e|Yes| UseKey\n    HasKey --\u003e|No| CheckRP\n    CheckRP --\u003e|Yes| UseProxy\n    CheckRP --\u003e|No| Error\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:207-213](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:716-780]()\n\n### Response Error Handling\n\nThe backend logs errors and returns appropriate status codes:\n\n```javascript\nif (!generateResponse.ok) {\n    const generateResponseText = await generateResponse.text();\n    console.warn(color.red(`Claude API returned error: ${generateResponse.status}`));\n    return response.status(500).send({ error: true });\n}\n```\n\nCommon error scenarios:\n- **400**: Missing API key or invalid request\n- **401**: Invalid API key\n- **429**: Rate limit exceeded\n- **500**: Provider API error or internal server error\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:364-368](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:1035-1040]()\n\n---\n\n## Configuration and Settings\n\n### Settings Structure\n\nChat completion settings are stored in `oai_settings` ([public/scripts/openai.js:372-472]()):\n\n| Setting | Type | Purpose |\n|---------|------|---------|\n| `chat_completion_source` | `String` | Provider selection (e.g., 'openai', 'claude') |\n| `openai_model` | `String` | OpenAI model name |\n| `claude_model` | `String` | Claude model name |\n| `google_model` | `String` | Google model name |\n| `temp_openai` | `Number` | Temperature (0.0-2.0) |\n| `top_p_openai` | `Number` | Top-P sampling |\n| `top_k_openai` | `Number` | Top-K sampling |\n| `frequency_penalty` | `Number` | Frequency penalty |\n| `presence_penalty` | `Number` | Presence penalty |\n| `openai_max_context` | `Number` | Context window size |\n| `openai_max_tokens` | `Number` | Max response tokens |\n| `stream_openai` | `Boolean` | Enable streaming |\n| `reverse_proxy` | `String` | Custom API endpoint URL |\n| `proxy_password` | `String` | Password for reverse proxy |\n| `use_sysprompt` | `Boolean` | Use system prompt format |\n| `function_calling` | `Boolean` | Enable tool calling |\n| `show_thoughts` | `Boolean` | Display reasoning content |\n| `reasoning_effort` | `String` | Reasoning effort level |\n| `verbosity` | `String` | Response verbosity level |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:372-472]()\n\n### Model Context Limits\n\nContext limits are defined as constants ([public/scripts/openai.js:123-142]()):\n\n```javascript\nconst max_2k = 2047;\nconst max_4k = 4095;\nconst max_8k = 8191;\nconst max_16k = 16383;\nconst max_32k = 32767;\nconst max_64k = 65535;\nconst max_128k = 128 * 1000;\nconst max_200k = 200 * 1000;\nconst claude_100k_max = 99000;\nconst unlocked_max = max_2mil;\n```\n\nThese are used for token budget calculations in `ChatCompletion.build()`.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:123-142](), [public/scripts/openai.js:1075-1667]()\n\n---\n\n## Code Entity Reference\n\n### Key Classes and Functions\n\n| Entity | Scope\n\nThis document covers SillyTavern's **text completion** backend system, which supports 15+ self-hosted and commercial APIs that use the text completion paradigm (prompt → text continuation). For information about **chat completion** backends (message-based APIs like OpenAI, Claude), see [Chat Completion System](#3.1). For prompt construction and management, see [Prompt Management and Construction](#3.3).\n\nText completion backends are characterized by:\n- Single prompt string input (no structured message arrays)\n- Direct text continuation output\n- Often used with local/self-hosted models\n- Support for legacy and specialized inference engines\n\n---\n\n## Backend Types and Providers\n\nSillyTavern supports 15 distinct text completion backend types through a unified configuration system.\n\n### Supported Backend Types\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    TextGenAPI[\"textgen_types\u003cbr/\u003e(Enum Definition)\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Self-Hosted Backends\"\n        OOBA[\"OOBA\u003cbr/\u003etext-generation-webui\"]\n        KOBOLDCPP[\"KOBOLDCPP\u003cbr/\u003ekoboldcpp\"]\n        LLAMACPP[\"LLAMACPP\u003cbr/\u003ellama.cpp server\"]\n        TABBY[\"TABBY\u003cbr/\u003eTabbyAPI\"]\n        OLLAMA[\"OLLAMA\u003cbr/\u003eOllama\"]\n        VLLM[\"VLLM\u003cbr/\u003evLLM inference\"]\n        APHRODITE[\"APHRODITE\u003cbr/\u003eAphrodite engine\"]\n        GENERIC[\"GENERIC\u003cbr/\u003eOpenAI-compatible\"]\n        HUGGINGFACE[\"HUGGINGFACE\u003cbr/\u003eHF Inference\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Commercial APIs\"\n        MANCER[\"MANCER\u003cbr/\u003eMancer.tech\"]\n        TOGETHERAI[\"TOGETHERAI\u003cbr/\u003eTogether AI\"]\n        INFERMATICAI[\"INFERMATICAI\u003cbr/\u003eInfermaticAI\"]\n        DREAMGEN[\"DREAMGEN\u003cbr/\u003eDreamGen.com\"]\n        OPENROUTER[\"OPENROUTER\u003cbr/\u003eOpenRouter\"]\n        FEATHERLESS[\"FEATHERLESS\u003cbr/\u003eFeatherless.ai\"]\n    end\n    \n    TextGenAPI --\u003e OOBA\n    TextGenAPI --\u003e KOBOLDCPP\n    TextGenAPI --\u003e LLAMACPP\n    TextGenAPI --\u003e TABBY\n    TextGenAPI --\u003e OLLAMA\n    TextGenAPI --\u003e VLLM\n    TextGenAPI --\u003e APHRODITE\n    TextGenAPI --\u003e GENERIC\n    TextGenAPI --\u003e HUGGINGFACE\n    TextGenAPI --\u003e FEATHERLESS\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:31-47]()\n\n### Backend Type Definitions\n\n| Backend Type | Identifier | Primary Use Case | Endpoint Pattern |\n|-------------|-----------|------------------|------------------|\n| `OOBA` | `'ooba'` | Text Generation WebUI | `/v1/completions` |\n| `KOBOLDCPP` | `'koboldcpp'` | KoboldCpp quantized models | `/v1/completions` |\n| `LLAMACPP` | `'llamacpp'` | llama.cpp native server | `/completion` |\n| `TABBY` | `'tabby'` | TabbyAPI (Exllamav2) | `/v1/completions` |\n| `OLLAMA` | `'ollama'` | Ollama local models | `/api/generate` |\n| `VLLM` | `'vllm'` | vLLM production inference | `/v1/completions` |\n| `APHRODITE` | `'aphrodite'` | Aphrodite engine | `/v1/completions` |\n| `MANCER` | `'mancer'` | Mancer cloud service | `/oai/v1/completions` |\n| `TOGETHERAI` | `'togetherai'` | Together AI API | `/v1/completions` |\n| `INFERMATICAI` | `'infermaticai'` | InfermaticAI service | `/v1/completions` `/v1/completions` |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:31-47](), [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:121-150]()\n\n---\n\n## Configuration System\n\n### Settings Object Streaming\n    Settings --\u003e Presets\n    Settings --\u003e SamplerOrder\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:143-236]()\n\n### Key Configuration Properties\n\n| Property | Type | Purpose |\n|----------|------|---------|\n| `type` | string | Active backend type from `textgen_types` |\n| `temp` | number | Temperature sampling parameter (0.0-2.0+) |\n| `top_p` | number | |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:143-236]()\n\n### Sampler Order Systems\n\nDifferent backends use different sampler ordering mechanisms:\n\n**KoboldCpp Sampler Order** (numeric indices):\n```javascript\n// Default order: [6, 0, 1, 3, 4, 2, 5]\n// Mapped to samplers: [temperature, top_k, top_p, tfs, top_a, typical_p, tail_free]\nconst KOBOLDCPP_ORDER = [6, 0, 1, 3, 4, 2, 5];\n```\n\n**llama.cpp Samplers** (named samplers):\n```javascript\n// Default order\nconst LLAMACPP_DEFAULT_ORDER = [\n    'penalties', 'dry', 'top_n_sigma', 'top_k',\n    'typ_p', 'top_p', 'min_p', 'xtc',\n    'temperature', 'adaptive_p'\n];\n```\n\n**Ooba Sampler Priority** (named samplers):\n```javascript\nconst OOBA_DEFAULT_ORDER = [\n    'repetition_penalty', 'presence_penalty', 'frequency_penalty',\n    'dry', 'temperature', 'dynamic_temperature', 'quadratic_sampling',\n    'top_n_sigma', 'top_k', 'top_p', 'typical_p', 'epsilon_cutoff',\n    'eta_cutoff', 'tfs', 'top_a', 'min_p', 'mirostat', 'xtc',\n    'encoder_repetition_penalty', 'no_repeat_ngram'\n];\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:67-116]()\n\n---\n\n## API Routing and Request Flow\n\n### Client-to-Server Request Pipeline\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client as \"textgen-settings.js\u003cbr/\u003eClient\"\n    participant Validator as \"validateTextGenUrl()\u003cbr/\u003eURL Validation\"\n    participant Status as \"/api/backends/text-completions/status\u003cbr/\u003eStatus Endpoint\"\n    participant Generate as \"/api/backends/text-completions/generate\u003cbr/\u003eGeneration Endpoint\"\n    participant Backend as \"External Backend\u003cbr/\u003e(OOBA/LLAMACPP/etc)\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eValidator: Format and validate URL\n    Validator-\u003e\u003eClient: Formatted URL\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eStatus: POST api_server, api_type\n    Status-\u003e\u003eBackend: GET /v1/models (or equivalent)\n    Backend-\u003e\u003eStatus: Model list JSON\n    Status-\u003e\u003eClient: {result, data: models[]}\n    \n    Note over Client: Load models, update UI\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eGenerate: POST with prompt + settings\n    Generate-\u003e\u003eGenerate: Transform payload by api_type\n    Generate-\u003e\u003eBackend: POST /v1/completions (or equivalent)\n    \n    alt Streaming Enabled\n        Backend--\u003e\u003eGenerate: SSE stream chunks\n        Generate--\u003e\u003eClient: Forward SSE stream\n        Client-\u003e\u003eClient: Update UI incrementally\n    else Non-streaming\n        Backend-\u003e\u003eGenerate: Complete JSON response\n        Generate-\u003e\u003eClient: {choices: [{text: \"...\"}]}\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:326-401](), [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:100-442]()\n\n### Status Check Endpoint\n\nThe status endpoint probes backend availability and retrieves model information:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    StatusReq[\"POST /api/backends/text-completions/status\"]\n    \n    ValidateURL[\"Validate and format URL\u003cbr/\u003etrimV1(api_server)\"]\n    \n    DetermineEndpoint[\"Determine endpoint by api_type\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Endpoint Mapping\"\n        OobaEndpoint[\"/v1/models\"]\n        LlamacppEndpoint[\"/v1/models\"]\n        TabbyEndpoint[\"/v1/model/list\"]\n        TogetherEndpoint[\"/api/models?\u0026info\"]\n        OllamaEndpoint[\"/api/tags\"]\n        DreamGenEndpoint[\"/api/openai/v1/models\"]\n    end\n    \n --\u003e LlamacppEndpoint\n    DetermineEndpoint --\u003e TabbyEndpoint\n    DetermineEndpoint --\u003e TogetherEndpoint\n    DetermineEndpoint --\u003e OllamaEndpoint\n    DetermineEndpoint --\u003e DreamGenEndpoint\n    OobaEndpoint --\u003e FetchModels\n    LlamacppEndpoint --\u003e FetchModels\n    TabbyEndpoint --\u003e FetchModels\n    TogetherEndpoint --\u003e FetchModels\n    OllamaEndpoint --\u003e FetchModels\n    DreamGenEndpoint --\u003e FetchModels\n    FetchModels --\u003e ParseResponse\n    ParseResponse --\u003e AdditionalInfo\n    AdditionalInfo --\u003e Response\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:100-232]()\n\n### Generation Endpoint Payload Transformation\n\nThe generation endpoint transforms requests based on backend type:\n\n| Backend Type | Payload Transformation | Notes |\n|-------------|----------------------|-------|\n| `OOBA`, `KOBOLDCPP`, `VLLM`, `APHRODITE`, `TABBY`, `GENERIC`, `FEATHERLESS`, `HUGGINGFACE` | Standard `/v1/completions` format | OpenAI-compatible |\n| `LLAMACPP` | Native llama.cpp format | Uses `/completion` endpoint |\n| `OLLAMA` | Wrapped in `{model, prompt, raw: true, options: {...}}` | Special format |\n| `TOGETHERAI` | Filtered to `TOGETHERAI_KEYS` allowlist | Removes unsupported params |\n| `INFERMATICAI` | Filtered to `INFERMATICAI_KEYS` allowlist | Maps response format |\n| `OPENROUTER` | Wrapped in chat format with provider config | Uses `/v1/chat/completions` |\n| `DREAMGEN` | Standard format to `/api/openai/v1/completions` | Custom endpoint path |\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:272-432]()\n\n### Server URL Management\n\nEach backend type can have its own configured server URL:\n\n```javascript\nexport const SERVER_INPUTS = {\n 'https://dreamgen.com';\nexport let OPENROUTER_SERVER = 'https://openrouter.ai/api';\nexport let FEATHERLESS_SERVER = 'https://api.featherless.ai/v1';\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:130-140](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:121-128]()\n\n---\n\n## Model Loading System\n\n### Model Discovery and Selection\n\nDifferent backends expose model [public/scripts/textgen-models.js:364-581]()\n\n### Model Card Structure\n\nEach Featherless model card displays:\n- Model ID (with zero-width spaces for wrapping)\n- Model class (architecture type)\n- Context length\n- Date added\n\nSelection updates `textgen_settings.featherless_model` and triggers a status check.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-models.js:415-460]()\n\n---\n\n## Streaming Implementation\n\n### Ollama Special Streaming\n\nOllama uses a non-standard streaming format that requires special parsing:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant Generate as \"/api/backends/text-completions/generate\"\n    participant Parser as \"parseOllamaStream()\"\n    participant Ollama as \"Ollama Backend\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eGenerate: POST with stream: true\n    Generate-\u003e\u003eOllama: POST /api/generate\n    Ollama--\u003e\u003eParser: Raw JSON stream (not SSE)\n    \n    loop For each chunk\n        Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Accumulate partialData\n        Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Try JSON.parse()\n        alt Valid JSON found\n            Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Extract response + thinking\n            Parser-\u003e\u003eClient: SSE: data: {choices: [{text, thinking}]}\n            Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Clear partialData\n        else Incomplete JSON\n            Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Continue accumulating\n        end\n    end\n    \n    Parser-\u003e\u003eClient: data: [DONE]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:29-72]()\n\n### Standard SSE Streaming\n\nMost backends use standard Server-Sent Events:\n\n```javascript\nif (request.body.stream) {\n    const completionsStream = await fetch(url, args);\n    // Pipe remote SSE stream to Express response\n    forwardFetchResponse(completionsStream, response);\n}\n```\n\nThe `forwardFetchResponse()` utility (from [src/util.js]()) pipes the backend's SSE stream directly to the client.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:404-408]()\n\n---\n\n## Backend-Specific Features\n\n### KoboldCpp Generation Abortion\n\nKoboldCpp supports mid-generation abortion via a special endpoint:\n\n```javascript\nasync function abortKoboldCppRequest(request, url) {\n    const args = {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: {},\n    };\n    setAdditionalHeaders(request, args, url);\n    const abortResponse = await fetch(`${url}/api/extra/abort`, args);\n}\n```\n\nThis is called when the client socket closes during a KoboldCpp generation.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:74-97]()\n\n### llama.cpp Properties Endpoint\n\nllama.cpp servers expose model properties including chat templates:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    PropsReq[\"POST /api/backends/text-completions/props\"]\n    \n    FetchProps[\"GET {baseUrl}/props?model=...\"]\n    \n    ParseTemplate[\"Parse chat_template\u003cbr/\u003eHash with SHA-256\"]\n    \n    DeriveTemplates[\"deriveTemplatesFromChatTemplate()\u003cbr/\u003eGenerate instruct/context presets\"]\n    \n    UpdateSettings[\"Update power_user settings\u003cbr/\u003eAuto-select presets\"]\n    \n    PropsReq --\u003e FetchProps\n    FetchProps --\u003e ParseTemplate\n    ParseTemplate --\u003e DeriveTemplates\n    DeriveTemplates --\u003e UpdateSettings\n```\n\nThe hash is used to cache derived templates and avoid re-parsing identical chat templates.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:234-270](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:746-794]()\n\n### llama.cpp Slot Management\n\nllama.cpp servers support slot-based state management:\n\n```javascript\nllamacpp.post('/slots', async function (request, response) {\n    // Actions: info, erase, restore, save\n    const action = request.body.action;\n    const id_slot = request.body.id_slot;\n    const filename = request.body.filename;\n    \n    if (action === 'info') {\n        // GET /slots\n    } else {\n        // POST /slots/{id_slot}?action={action}\n        // body: { filename: \"...\" }\n    }\n});\n```\n\nThis allows saving/restoring slot state to named files on the llama.cpp server.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:551-599]()\n\n### Ollama Image Captioning\n\nOllama supports multimodal models for image captioning:\n\n```javascript\nollama.post('/caption-image', async function (request, response) {\n    const fetchResponse = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/api/generate`, {\n        method: 'POST',\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n            model: request.body.model,\n            prompt: request.body.prompt,\n            images: [request.body.image], // Base64 image\n            stream: false,\n        }),\n    });\n    const data = await fetchResponse.json();\n    return response.send({ caption: data.response });\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:476-518]()\n\n### Ollama Model Download\n\nOllama can pull models on-demand:\n\n```javascript\nollama.post('/download', async function (request, response) {\n    const fetchResponse = await fetch(`${url}/api/pull`, {\n        method: 'POST',\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n            name: request.body.name,\n            stream: false,\n        }),\n    });\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:446-474]()\n\n### TabbyAPI Model Download\n\nTabbyAPI supports downloading models with admin permission check:\n\n```javascript\ntabby.post('/download', async function (request, response) {\n    // 1. Check /v1/auth/permission\n    const permissionJson = await permissionResponse.json();\n    if (permissionJson.permission !== 'admin') {\n        return response.status(403).send({ error: true });\n    }\n    \n    // 2. POST /v1/download\n    const fetchResponse = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/v1/download`, args);\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:603-645]()\n\n### OpenRouter Provider Selection\n\nOpenRouter allows specifying preferred providers and quantizations:\n\n```javascript\nif (request.body.api_type === TEXTGEN_TYPES.OPENROUTER) {\n    if (Array.isArray(request.body.provider) \u0026\u0026 request.body.provider.length \u003e 0) {\n        request.body.provider = {\n            allow_fallbacks: request.body.allow_fallbacks ?? true,\n            order: request.body.provider,\n        };\n    }\n    \n    if (Array.isArray(request.body.quantizations) \u0026\u0026 request.body.quantizations.length \u003e 0) {\n        request.body.provider ??= {};\n        request.body.provider.quantizations = request.body.quantizations;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:361-378]()\n\n### Ollama Batch Size Configuration\n\nOllama supports batch size configuration:\n\n```javascript\nif (request.body.api_type === TEXTGEN_TYPES.OLLAMA) {\n    const numBatch = Number(getConfigValue('ollama.batchSize', -1, 'number'));\n    if (numBatch \u003e 0) {\n        request.body.num_batch = numBatch;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:385-399]()\n\n---\n\n## Tokenization Support\n\n### Remote Tokenization\n\nSome backends support remote tokenization for accurate token counting:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    TokenizerBestMatch[\"getTokenizerBestMatch('textgenerationwebui')\"]\n    \n    CheckAPI[\"Check online_status\u003cbr/\u003eCheck TOKENIZER_SUPPORTED_KEY\"]\n
