TextGenAPI[\"tokenizers.API_TEXTGENERATIONWEBUI\u003cbr/\u003eTEXTGEN_TOKENIZERS.includes(type)\"]\n        OpenRouterMap[\"OpenRouter → Model tokenizer\u003cbr/\u003egetCurrentOpenRouterModelTokenizer()\"]\n        DreamGenMap[\"DreamGen → Model tokenizer\u003cbr/\u003egetCurrentDreamGenModelTokenizer()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Fallback: Local Tokenizers\"\n        ModelName[\"Parse model name string\"]\n        \n        Llama3[\"llama3/llama-3 → LLAMA3\"]\n        Mistral[\"mistral/mixtral → MISTRAL\"]\n        Gemma[\"gemma → GEMMA\"]\n        Nemo[\"nemo/pixtral → NEMO\"]\n        DeepSeek[\"deepseek → DEEPSEEK\"]\n        Yi[\"yi → YI\"]\n        Jamba[\"jamba → JAMBA\"]\n        CommandR[\"command-r → COMMAND_R\"]\n        CommandA[\"command-a → COMMAND_A\"]\n        Qwen2[\"qwen2 → QWEN2\"]\n        DefaultLlama[\"Default → LLAMA\"]\n    end\n    \n    TokenizerBestMatch --\u003e CheckAPI\n    CheckAPI --\u003e KoboldAPI\n    CheckAPI --\u003e TextGenAPI\n    CheckAPI --\u003e OpenRouterMap\n    CheckAPI --\u003e DreamGenMap\n    \n    CheckAPI --\u003e ModelName\n    ModelName --\u003e Llama3\n    ModelName --\u003e Mistral\n    ModelName --\u003e Gemma\n    ModelName --\u003e Nemo\n    ModelName --\u003e DeepSeek\n    ModelName --\u003e Yi\n    ModelName --\u003e Jamba\n    ModelName --\u003e CommandR\n    ModelName --\u003e CommandA\n    ModelName --\u003e Qwen2\n    ModelName --\u003e DefaultLlama\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:282-362]()\n\n### Textgen-Supported Remote Tokenization\n\nBackends that support remote tokenization (populated in `TEXTGEN_TOKENIZERS`):\n\n```javascript\nexport const TEXTGEN_TOKENIZERS = [\n    textgen_types.OOBA,\n    textgen_types.KOBOLDCPP,\n    textgen_types.LLAMACPP,\n    textgen_types.TABBY,\n    textgen_types.APHRODITE,\n    textgen_types.VLLM,\n    textgen_types.MANCER,\n    textgen_types.TOGETHERAI,\n    textgen_types.INFERMATICAI,\n    textgen_types.DREAMGEN,\n    textgen_types.OLLAMA,\n];\n```\n\nRemote tokenization endpoints:\n- **Ooba**: Requires `/v1/internal/model/info` endpoint with `x-supports-tokenization: true` header\n- **KoboldAI**: Requires KoboldCpp version ≥ 1.41 (sets `kai_flags.can_use_tokenization`)\n- **Other backends**: Use `/tokenize` or `/v1/token/encode` endpoints if available\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:62-63](), [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js:188-204](), [public/scripts/kai-settings.js:72-73]()\n\n### Remote Tokenization Endpoint\n\nServer-side tokenization proxy:\n\n```javascript\nrouter.post('/remote/textgenerationwebui/encode', async function (req, res) {\n    const text = req.body.text || '';\n    const baseUrl = getTextGenServer();\n    \n    const response = await fetch(`${baseUrl}/v1/token/encode`, {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n        body: JSON.stringify({ text }),\n    });\n    \n    const data = await response.json();\n    return res.send({ ids: data.tokens, count: data.length });\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:950-986]()\n\n---\n\n## Preset Management\n\n### Preset Storage and Selection\n\nText completion presets are managed through the preset system (see [Preset and Sampler Management](#3.6)):\n\n```javascript\n$('#settings_preset_textgenerationwebui').on('change', async function () {\n    const presetName = $(this).val();\n    await selectPreset(presetName);\n    await eventSource.emit(event_types.PRESET_CHANGED, {\n        apiId: 'textgenerationwebui',\n        name: presetName\n    });\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:954-958]()\n\n### Preset Application\n\nWhen a preset is selected:\n\n1. All `setting_names` properties are updated from preset\n2. UI controls (sliders, inputs) are synchronized\n3. Logit bias is recalculated and displayed\n4. Sampler order is applied to sortable UI elements\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:370-386]()\n\n### Settings Filtering for Presets\n\nWhen saving presets, certain properties are excluded:\n\n```javascript\nconst filteredKeys = [\n    'api_server', 'preset', 'streaming', 'truncation_length',\n    'n', 'streaming_url', 'stopping_strings', 'can_use_tokenization',\n    'can_use_streaming', 'preset_settings_novel', 'preset_settings',\n    'streaming_novel', 'nai_preamble', 'model_novel', 'streaming_kobold',\n    'enabled', 'bind_to_context', 'seed', 'legacy_api',\n    'mancer_model', 'togetherai_model', 'ollama_model', 'vllm_model',\n    'aphrodite_model', 'llamacpp_model', 'server_urls', 'type',\n    'custom_model', 'bypass_status_check', 'infermaticai_model',\n    'dreamgen_model', 'openrouter_model', 'featherless_model',\n    'max_tokens_second', 'openrouter_providers', 'openrouter_quantizations',\n    'openrouter_allow_fallbacks', 'tabby_model', 'derived', 'generic_model',\n    'include_reasoning', 'global_banned_tokens', 'send_banned_tokens',\n];\n```\n\nThese keys are connection-specific or UI state and should not be part of reusable presets.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/preset-manager.js:679-723]()\n\n---\n\n## Token Banning System\n\n### Ban Token Configuration\n\nToken banning supports multiple formats:\n\n```javascript\nfunction getCustomTokenBans(settings = null) {\n    const sequences = []\n        .concat(settings.banned_tokens.split('\\n'))\n        .concat(settings.global_banned_tokens.split('\\n'))\n        .concat(textgenerationwebui_banned_in_macros);\n    \n    for (const line of sequences) {\n        // Format 1: Raw token IDs as JSON array\n        if (line.startsWith('[') \u0026\u0026 line.endsWith(']')) {\n            const tokens = JSON.parse(line);\n            banned_tokens.push(...tokens);\n        }\n        // Format 2: Quoted string (verbatim)\n        else if (line.startsWith('\"') \u0026\u0026 line.endsWith('\"')) {\n            banned_strings.push(line.slice(1, -1));\n        }\n        // Format 3: Raw text (tokenize)\n        else {\n            const tokens = getTextTokens(tokenizer, line);\n            banned_tokens.push(...tokens);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Format examples:**\n- `[123, 456, 789]` - Direct token IDs\n- `\"fuck\"` - Verbatim string (sent as-is)\n- `hello` - Text to tokenize\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:438-498]()\n\n### Ban Token Kill Switch\n\nA toggle controls whether banned tokens are sent:\n\n```javascript\n$('#send_banned_tokens_textgenerationwebui').on('change', function () {\n    const checked = !!$(this).prop('checked');\n    toggleBannedStringsKillSwitch(checked,\n        checked\n            ? t`Banned tokens/strings are being sent in the request.`\n            : t`Banned tokens/strings are NOT being sent in the request.`);\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:814-820]()\n\n---\n\n## Logit Bias System\n\n### Logit Bias Calculation\n\nLogit bias is calculated from a list of bias entries:\n\n```javascript\nfunction calculateLogitBias(settings = null) {\n    if (!Array.isArray(settings.logit_bias) || settings.logit_bias.length === 0) {\n        return {};\n    }\n    \n    const tokenizer = getTokenizerForTokenIds();\n    const result = {};\n    \n    function addBias(bias, sequence) {\n        for (const logit of sequence) {\n            const key = String(logit);\n            result[key] = bias;\n        }\n    }\n    \n    getLogitBiasListResult(settings.logit_bias, tokenizer, addBias);\n    return result;\n}\n```\n\nThe result is a map of `{tokenId: biasValue}` sent to the backend.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:517-549]()\n\n### Bias Cache\n\nLogit bias is cached to avoid recalculation:\n\n```javascript\nBIAS_CACHE.delete(BIAS_KEY);\ndisplayLogitBias(textgenerationwebui_settings.logit_bias, BIAS_KEY);\n```\n\nThe cache key is `'#textgenerationwebui_api-settings'`.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:117](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:383-384]()\n\n---\n\n## Advanced Sampling Features\n\n### DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) Sampling\n\nDRY sampling prevents repetition by penalizing recently used token sequences:\n\n| Parameter | Purpose |\n|-----------|---------|\n| `dry_multiplier` | Penalty strength (0 = disabled) |\n| `dry_base` | Exponential base for sequence length penalty |\n| `dry_allowed_length` | Minimum sequence length to penalize |\n| `dry_sequence_breakers` | JSON array of strings that reset sequences |\n| `dry_penalty_last_n` | Context window for penalty (0 = entire context) |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:175-179]()\n\n### XTC (Exclude Top Choices) Sampling\n\nXTC excludes top token choices to increase diversity:\n\n| Parameter | Purpose |\n|-----------|---------|\n| `xtc_threshold` | Probability threshold for exclusion |\n| `xtc_probability` | Probability of applying XTC (0 = disabled) |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:227-228]()\n\n### Dynamic Temperature\n\nDynamic temperature adjusts temperature based on entropy:\n\n| Parameter | Purpose |\n|-----------|---------|\n| `dynatemp` | Enable/disable dynamic temperature |\n| `min_temp` | Minimum temperature value |\n| `max_temp` | Maximum temperature value |\n| `dynatemp_exponent` | Exponent for entropy scaling |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:169-172]()\n\n### Mirostat Sampling\n\nMirostat dynamically adjusts sampling to target a specific perplexity:\n\n| Parameter | Purpose |\n|-----------|---------|\n| `mirostat_mode` | 0 = disabled, 1 = Mirostat v1, 2 = Mirostat v2 |\n| `mirostat_tau` | Target perplexity (typical: 5.0) |\n| `mirostat_eta` | Learning rate (typical: 0.1) |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:191-193]()\n\n### Adaptive Sampling\n\nAdaptive sampling adjusts probabilities based on entropy:\n\n| Parameter | Purpose |\n|-----------|---------|\n| `adaptive_target` | Target entropy (-0.01 = adaptive) |\n| `adaptive_decay` | Decay factor (0.9 typical) |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:234-235]()\n\n---\n\n## Backend Comparison Matrix\n\n| Feature | OOBA | KoboldCpp | llama.cpp | TabbyAPI | Ollama | vLLM | Aphrodite | Commercial pipeline for constructing prompts from individual components and assembling them into the final context sent to language models. The pipeline handles prompt collection, ordering, injection, formatting for specific APIs, and token budget enforcement.\n\nFor detailed information about the PromptManager UI and configuration, see [Prompt Manager System](#3.3.1). For instruct mode formatting details, see [Instruct Mode and Context Formatting](#3.3.2). For World Info activation and scanning, see [World Info System](#6.1).\n\n---\n\n## Pipeline Overview\n\nThe prompt assembly pipeline transforms individual components (system prompts, character data, chat history, World Info, extension prompts) into a structured message array suitable for the target API.\n\n### Chat Completion vs Text Completion\n\nSillyTavern supports two distinct prompt assembly flows:\n\n**Title: API-Specific Assembly Flows**\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    GenerateRequest[\"Generate() called\"]\n    CheckAPI{\"main_api check\"}\n    \n    CheckAPI --\u003e|\"openai\"| ChatFlow[\"Chat Completion Flow\"]\n    CheckAPI --\u003e|\"kobold, textgenerationwebui,\u003cbr/\u003enovel, koboldhorde\"| TextFlow[\"Text Completion Flow\"]\n    \n    subgraph ChatCompletionPipeline [\"Chat Completion Pipeline\"]\n        SetupPM[\"setupChatCompletionPromptManager()\"]\n        CollectPrompts[\"promptManager.preparePrompts()\"]\n        FormatMessages[\"setOpenAIMessages()\"]\n        ApplyInstruct[\"formatInstructModeChat()\"]\n        PostProcess[\"postProcessPrompt()\"]\n        SendChat[\"sendOpenAIRequest()\"]\n        \n        SetupPM --\u003e CollectPrompts\n        CollectPrompts --\u003e FormatMessages\n        FormatMessages --\u003e ApplyInstruct\n        ApplyInstruct --\u003e PostProcess\n        PostProcess --\u003e SendChat\n    end\n    \n    subgraph TextCompletionPipeline [\"Text Completion Pipeline\"]\n        BuildString[\"Build single string\"]\n        AssembleComponents[\"Assemble story string,\u003cbr/\u003eexamples, chat history\"]\n        ApplyInstructText[\"formatInstructModePrompt()\"]\n        SendText[\"Send to API\"]\n        \n        BuildString --\u003e AssembleComponents\n        AssembleComponents --\u003e ApplyInstructText\n        ApplyInstructText --\u003e SendText\n    end\n    \n    ChatFlow --\u003e SetupPM\n    TextFlow --\u003e BuildString\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:7769-7800](), [public/scripts/openai.js:626-672](), [public/scripts/openai.js:1000-1200]()\n\n### Component Sources\n\n**Title: Prompt Component Sources and Collection**\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph ComponentSources [\"Component Sources\"]\n        SystemPrompts[\"System Prompts\u003cbr/\u003e(via PromptManager)\"]\n        CharacterData[\"Character Data\u003cbr/\u003e(description, personality,\u003cbr/\u003escenario, examples)\"]\n        PersonaDesc[\"Persona Description\u003cbr/\u003e(user_avatar descriptor)\"]\n        StoryString[\"Story String\u003cbr/\u003e(context.story_string)\"]\n        WorldInfo[\"World Info\u003cbr/\u003e(getWorldInfoPrompt())\"]\n        ExtensionPrompts[\"Extension Prompts\u003cbr/\u003e(extension_prompts object)\"]\n        ChatHistory[\"Chat History\u003cbr/\u003e(chat array)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Assembly [\"Assembly Process\"]\n SystemPrompts --\u003e Collect\n    CharacterData --\u003e Collect\n    PersonaDesc --\u003e Collect\n    StoryString --\u003e Collect\n    WorldInfo --\u003e InjectDepth\n    ExtensionPrompts --\u003e InjectDepth\n    ChatHistory --\u003e FormatRoles\n    \n    Collect --\u003e Order\n    Order --\u003e InjectDepth\n    InjectDepth --\u003e FormatRoles\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:626-672](), [public/scripts/openai.js:525-600](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:1-500]()\n\n---\n\n## Chat Completion Assembly Pipeline\n\nThe chat completion pipeline assembles prompts into an array of message objects with roles (`system`, `user`, `assistant`).\n\n### Step 1: PromptManager Setup\n\nThe `setupChatCompletionPromptManager()` function initializes the `PromptManager` instance if it doesn't exist, configuring it with default prompts and settings.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    CheckPM{\"promptManager\u003cbr/\u003eexists?\"}\n    CreatePM[\"new PromptManager()\"]\n    LoadConfig[\"Load configuration:\u003cbr/\u003e- prefix: 'completion_'\u003cbr/\u003e- defaultPrompts\u003cbr/\u003e- promptOrder strategy\"]\n    InitHandlers[\"Set handlers:\u003cbr/\u003e- saveServiceSettings()\u003cbr/\u003e- tryGenerate()\u003cbr/\u003e- tokenHandler\"]\n    Render[\"promptManager.render()\"]\n    \n    CheckPM --\u003e|No| CreatePM\n    CheckPM --\u003e|Yes| Render\n    CreatePM --\u003e LoadConfig\n    LoadConfig --\u003e InitHandlers\n    InitHandlers --\u003e Render\n```\n\n**Key Configuration:**\n\n| Property | Value | Description |\n|----------|-------|-------------|\n| `prefix` | `'completion_'` | ID prefix for UI elements |\n| `containerIdentifier` | `'completion_prompt_manager'` | Main container ID |\n| `listIdentifier` | `'completion_prompt_manager_list'` | Prompt list container ID |\n| `defaultPrompts` | Object | Default prompt content (main, nsfw, jailbreak, enhanceDefinitions) |\n| `promptOrder.strategy` | `'global'` | Order strategy (global vs per-character) |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:626-672](), [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:432-500]()\n\n---\n\n### Step 2: Prompt Collection\n\nThe `PromptManager.preparePrompts()` method collects all active prompts from configured sources.\n\n**Title: PromptManager.preparePrompts() Flow**\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    PreparePrompts[\"preparePrompts(messages)\"]\n    LoadActive[\"Load activeCharacter\"]\n    GetPrompts[\"Get prompts from:\u003cbr/\u003e- serviceSettings.prompts\u003cbr/\u003e- character overrides\"]\n    FilterEnabled[\"Filter enabled prompts\"]\n    FilterTriggers[\"Filter by injection_trigger\"]\n    ApplyOrder[\"Sort by prompt_order\"]\n    CreateMessages[\"Create Message instances\"]\n    \n    PreparePrompts --\u003e LoadActive\n    LoadActive --\u003e GetPrompts\n    GetPrompts --\u003e FilterEnabled\n    FilterEnabled --\u003e FilterTriggers\n    FilterTriggers --\u003e ApplyOrder\n    ApplyOrder --\u003e CreateMessages\n    \n    subgraph PromptStructure [\"Prompt Structure\"]\n        Identifier[\"identifier: string\"]\n        Role[\"role: 'system'|'user'|'assistant'\"]\n        Content[\"content: string\"]\n        InjectionDepth[\"injection_depth: number\"]\n        InjectionPosition[\"injection_position: 0|1\"]\n        InjectionOrder[\"injection_order: number\"]\n    end\n    \n    GetPrompts --\u003e PromptStructure\n```\n\n**Prompt Filtering:**\n\n1. **Enabled Check**: Only prompts with `enabled: true` are included\n2. **Trigger Check**: If `injection_trigger` array is non-empty, the current generation type must be included\n3. **Override Check**: Character-specific overrides replace default prompts\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:650-750](), [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:79-196]()\n\n### Step 3: Chat History Formatting\n\nThe `setOpenAIMessages()` function converts the `chat` array into message objects with proper roles and content.\n\n**Title: Chat Message Formatting (setOpenAIMessages)**\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    ChatArray[\"chat array (ChatMessage[])\"]\n    Iterate[\"Iterate messages\"]\n    CheckIgnore{\"extra.IGNORE_SYMBOL?\"}\n    DetermineRole[\"Determine role:\u003cbr/\u003e- is_user → 'user'\u003cbr/\u003e- else → 'assistant'\u003cbr/\u003e- extra.type === NARRATOR → 'system'\"]\n    ApplyNamesBehavior[\"Apply names_behavior\"]\n    CollectMedia[\"Collect extra.media\"]\n    CollectInvocations[\"Collect extra.tool_invocations\"]\n    CollectSignature[\"Collect extra.reasoning_signature\"]\n    CreateMessage[\"Create message object\"]\n    \n    ChatArray --\u003e Iterate\n    Iterate --\u003e CheckIgnore\n    CheckIgnore --\u003e|Yes| Iterate\n    CheckIgnore --\u003e|No| DetermineRole\n    DetermineRole --\u003e ApplyNamesBehavior\n    ApplyNamesBehavior --\u003e CollectMedia\n    CollectMedia --\u003e CollectInvocations\n    CollectInvocations --\u003e CollectSignature\n    CollectSignature --\u003e CreateMessage\n    \n    subgraph NamesBehaviorEnum [\"names_behavior Settings\"]\n        NONE[\"-1: NONE\u003cbr/\u003e(no names added)\"]\n        DEFAULT[\"0: DEFAULT\u003cbr/\u003e(names in groups only)\"]\n        COMPLETION[\"1: COMPLETION\u003cbr/\u003e(names in role field)\"]\n        CONTENT[\"2: CONTENT\u003cbr/\u003e(prepend to content)\"]\n    end\n    \n    ApplyNamesBehavior --\u003e NamesBehaviorEnum\n```\n\n**Message Object Structure:**\n\n```typescript\n{\n  role: 'user' | 'assistant' | 'system',\n  content: string,\n  name?: string,                    // Character name\n  media?: Array,                    // Attached media\n  mediaDisplay?: string,            // Display mode\n  mediaIndex?: number,              // First media index\n  invocations?: Array,              // Tool invocations\n  signature?: string                // Reasoning signature\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:525-600](), [public/scripts/openai.js:202-207]()\n\n### Step 4: Example Message Formatting\n\nThe `setOpenAIMessageExamples()` function parses example dialogues into individual message blocks.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    ExampleString[\"\u003cSTART\u003e Example string\"]\n    RemoveStart[\"Remove \u003cSTART\u003e tag\"]\n    ReplaceNewlines[\"Replace \\\\r with ''\"]\n    ParseIndividual[\"parseExampleIntoIndividual()\"]\n    SplitMessages[\"Split by user/bot names\"]\n    CreateBlocks[\"Create message blocks\"]\n    \n    ExampleString --\u003e RemoveStart\n    RemoveStart --\u003e ReplaceNewlines\n    ReplaceNewlines --\u003e ParseIndividual\n    ParseIndividual --\u003e SplitMessages\n    SplitMessages --\u003e CreateBlocks\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:607-618](), [public/scripts/openai.js:680-750]()\n\n---\n\n### Step 5: World Info Integration\n\nThe `getWorldInfoPrompt()` function activates and formats World Info entries based on keyword matching.\n\n**Title: World Info Activation and Injection**\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    GetWIPrompt[\"getWorldInfoPrompt(chat, maxContext)\"]\n    CreateBuffer[\"new WorldInfoBuffer(messages, globalScanData)\"]\n    ActivateEntries[\"Activate entries by scanning buffer\"]\n    ApplyBudget[\"Apply world_info_budget\"]\n    GroupByDepth[\"Group by injection_depth\"]\n    FormatEntries[\"Format with wi_format template\"]\n    \n    GetWIPrompt --\u003e CreateBuffer\n    CreateBuffer --\u003e ActivateEntries\n    ActivateEntries --\u003e ApplyBudget\n    ApplyBudget --\u003e GroupByDepth\n    GroupByDepth --\u003e FormatEntries\n    \n    subgraph WIEntry [\"World Info Entry Structure\"]\n        EntryContent[\"content: string\"]\n        Keys[\"key: string[]\"]\n        SecondaryKeys[\"keysecondary: string[]\"]\n        Depth[\"depth: number\"]\n        Position[\"position: number\"]\n        Order[\"order: number\"]\n    end\n    \n    ActivateEntries --\u003e WIEntry\n    \n    subgraph WIResult [\"Return Object (WIPromptResult)\"]\n        WIBefore[\"worldInfoBefore: string\"]\n        WIAfter[\"worldInfoAfter: string\"]\n Global WI takes priority |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:1000-1200](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:199-430](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:27-31]()\n\n### Step 6: Extension Prompts\n\nExtension prompts are collected from the `extension_prompts` object and injected at specified positions.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    ExtPrompts[\"extension_prompts object\"]\n    FilterType{\"extension_prompt_type\"}\n    \n    FilterType --\u003e|\"IN_PROMPT (0)\"| StoryString[\"Add to story string\"]\n    FilterType --\u003e|\"IN_CHAT (1)\"| InjectChat[\"Inject at depth\"]\n FilterType\n```\n\n**Extension Prompt Types:**\n\n| Type | Value | Description |\n|------|-------|-------------|\n| `NONE` | -1 | Not an extension prompt |\n| `IN_PROMPT` | 0 | Included in story string |\n| `IN_CHAT` | 1 | Injected as chat message at depth |\n| `BEFORE_PROMPT` | 2 | Before story string |\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:452-460](), [public/scripts/extensions.js]() (extension_prompts)\n\n---\n\n### Step 7: Component Assembly\n\nThe `prepareOpenAIMessages()` function in the `MessageCollection` class assembles all components into the final message array.\n\n**Title: Message Assembly in prepareOpenAIMessages()**\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    PrepareMessages[\"prepareOpenAIMessages(name, type, quietPrompt)\"]\n    CollectPrompts[\"Collect from promptManager\"]\n    AddSystemPrompts[\"Add system prompts\"]\n    AddStoryString[\"Add story string\"]\n    AddCharDescription[\"Add character description\"]\n    AddPersona[\"Add persona description\"]\n    AddExamples[\"Add example messages\"]\n    AddNewChatPrompt[\"Add new_chat_prompt\"]\n    AddWI[\"Inject World Info at depths\"]\n    AddExtPrompts[\"Inject extension prompts\"]\n    AddChatHistory[\"Add formatted chat history\"]\n    AddQuietPrompt[\"Add quietPrompt if present\"]\n    \n    PrepareMessages --\u003e CollectPrompts\n    CollectPrompts --\u003e AddSystemPrompts\n    AddSystemPrompts --\u003e AddStoryString\n    AddStoryString --\u003e AddCharDescription\n    AddCharDescription --\u003e AddPersona\n    AddPersona --\u003e AddExamples\n    AddExamples --\u003e AddNewChatPrompt\n    AddNewChatPrompt --\u003e AddWI\n    AddWI --\u003e AddExtPrompts\n    AddExtPrompts --\u003e AddChatHistory\n    AddChatHistory --\u003e AddQuietPrompt\n    \n    subgraph AssemblyOrder [\"Standard Assembly Order\"]\n        Order1[\"1. System prompts\"]\n        Order2[\"2. Story string\"]\n        Order3[\"3. Character data\"]\n        Order4[\"4. Persona description\"]\n        Order5[\"5. Example messages\"]\n        Order6[\"6. New chat marker\"]\n        Order7[\"7. World Info (depth-based)\"]\n        Order8[\"8. Extension prompts (depth-based)\"]\n        Order9[\"9. Chat history\"]\n        Order10[\"10. Quiet prompt (generation-specific)\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Component Ordering:**\n\nThe order of components is controlled by `prompt_order` array in `oai_settings`. The default order is:\n\n```javascript\n[\n  { identifier: 'main', enabled: true },\n  { identifier: 'worldInfoBefore', enabled: true },\n  { identifier: 'charDescription', enabled: true },\n  { identifier: 'charPersonality', enabled: true },\n  { identifier: 'scenario', enabled: true },\n  { identifier: 'personaDescription', enabled: true },\n  { identifier: 'enhanceDefinitions', enabled: false },\n  { identifier: 'nsfw', enabled: false },\n  { identifier: 'worldInfoAfter', enabled: true },\n  { identifier: 'dialogueExamples', enabled: true },\n  { identifier: 'jailbreak', enabled: true }\n]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:1000-1200](), [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:760-880](), [public/scripts/openai.js:372-386]()\n\n### Step 8: Depth-Based Injection\n\nPrompts with `injection_position: INJECTION_POSITION.RELATIVE` are injected relative to the end of the chat history.\n\n**Title: Depth-Based Prompt Injection**\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    ChatHistory[\"Chat History (N messages)\"]\n    \n    subgraph DepthCalculation [\"Depth Calculation\"]\n        RelativeDepth[\"injection_position = 0\u003cbr/\u003e(RELATIVE)\"]\n        CalcPosition[\"Position = N - injection_depth\"]\n        Example[\"Example: N=50, depth=4\u003cbr/\u003e→ Insert at position 46\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph MultipleAtDepth [\"Multiple Prompts at Same Depth\"]\n        SortByOrder[\"Sort by injection_order\"]\n        Order50[\"order=50 (first)\"]\n        Order100[\"order=100\"]\n        Order200[\"order=200 (last)\"]\n    end\n    \n    ChatHistory --\u003e RelativeDepth\n    RelativeDepth --\u003e CalcPosition\n    CalcPosition --\u003e Example\n    \n    CalcPosition --\u003e SortByOrder\n    SortByOrder --\u003e Order50\n    Order50 --\u003e Order100\n    Order100 --\u003e Order200\n```\n\n**Injection Rules:**\n\n1. **Relative Depth**: Position calculated as `chatLength - injection_depth`\n2. **Absolute Position**: Direct insertion at `injection_depth` index\n3. **Order Sorting**: Prompts at same depth sorted by `injection_order` (ascending)\n4. **Trigger Filtering**: Only prompts matching current generation type are injected\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:31-40](), [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:760-850]()\n\n---\n\n### Step 9: Instruct Mode Application\n\nIf instruct mode is enabled, the `formatInstructModeChat()` function wraps messages with instruction sequences.\n\n**Title: Instruct Mode Sequence Application**\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    MessageArray[\"Message array\"]\n --\u003e|\"user\"| ApplyInput[\"Wrap with:\u003cbr/\u003einput_sequence\u003cbr/\u003e+ input_suffix\"]\n    CheckRole --\u003e|\"assistant\"| ApplyOutput[\"Wrap with:\u003cbr/\u003eoutput_sequence\u003cbr/\u003e+ output_suffix\"]\n    \n    ApplySystem --\u003e CheckFirst{\"First message?\"}\n    ApplyInput --\u003e CheckFirst\n    ApplyOutput --\u003e CheckFirst\n    \n    CheckFirst --\u003e|Yes| UseFirst[\"Use first_*_sequence\"]\n    CheckFirst --\u003e|No| CheckLast{\"Last message?\"}\n    \n    CheckLast --\u003e|Yes| UseLast[\"Use
