--\u003e Formatted\n    \n    subgraph InstructSettings [\"Instruct Mode Settings\"]\n        InputSeq[\"input_sequence: string\"]\n        InputSuf[\"input_suffix: string\"]\n        OutputSeq[\"output_sequence: string\"]\n        OutputSuf[\"output_suffix: string\"]\n        SysSeq[\"system_sequence: string\"]\n        SysSuf[\"system_suffix: string\"]\n        StopSeq[\"stop_sequence: string\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Instruct Sequence Variables:**\n\n| Setting | Description | Example |\n|---------|-------------|---------|\n| `input_sequence` | Prefix before user messages | `### Instruction:` |\n| `input_suffix` | Suffix after user messages | `\\n` |\n| `output_sequence` | Prefix before AI messages | `### Response:` |\n| `output_suffix` | Suffix after AI messages | `\\n` |\n| `system_sequence` | Prefix before system messages | `### System:` |\n| `system_suffix` | Suffix after system messages | `\\n` |\n| `first_input_sequence` | Override for first user message | `### First Input:` |\n| `last_output_sequence` | Override for last AI message | `### Final Response:` |\n| `stop_sequence` | Stop string for generation | `\\n###` |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/instruct-mode.js:100-300](), [public/scripts/power-user.js:225-252]()\n\n### Step 10: Names Behavior\n\nThe `names_behavior` setting controls how character names are added to messages.\n\n**Title: Names Behavior Application**\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Message[\"Message object\"]\n    CheckBehavior{\"names_behavior\"}\n    \n    CheckBehavior --\u003e|\"-1 (NONE)\"| NoNames[\"No names added\"]\n    CheckBehavior --\u003e|\"0 (DEFAULT)\"| CheckGroup{\"Is group or\u003cbr/\u003eforce_avatar?\"}\n    CheckBehavior --\u003e|\"1 (COMPLETION)\"| AddToRole[\"Add to role field\"]\n    CheckBehavior --\u003e|\"2 (CONTENT)\"| PrependContent[\"Prepend to content\"]\n    \n    CheckGroup --\u003e|Yes| PrependGroupName[\"content = name + ': ' + content\"]\n    CheckGroup --\u003e|No| NoNames\n    \n    subgraph NamesBehaviorEnum [\"names_behavior Enum\"]\n        NONE[\"-1: NONE\"]\n        DEFAULT[\"0: DEFAULT\"]\n        COMPLETION[\"1: COMPLETION\"]\n        CONTENT[\"2: CONTENT\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Examples [\"Examples\"]\n        Ex1[\"DEFAULT in group:\u003cbr/\u003e'Alice: Hello there'\"]\n        Ex2[\"COMPLETION:\u003cbr/\u003erole: 'Alice'\"]\n        Ex3[\"CONTENT:\u003cbr/\u003e'Alice: Hello there'\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Names Behavior Settings:**\n\n| Value | Name | Description |\n|-------|------|-------------|\n| `-1` | `NONE` | Never add character names |\n| `0` | `DEFAULT` | Add names only in group chats or when `force_avatar` is true |\n| `1` | `COMPLETION` | Add name to the `role` field (for APIs that support it) |\n| `2` | `CONTENT` | Always prepend `name: ` to message content |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:202-207](), [public/scripts/openai.js:550-568]()\n\n---\n\n### Step 11: Post-Processing and Message Merging\n\nThe `postProcessPrompt()` function applies merging strategies to ensure messages conform to API requirements.\n\n**Title: Post-Processing Pipeline (postProcessPrompt)**\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    MessageArray[\"Message array\"]\n    CheckType{\"custom_prompt_post_processing\"}\n    \n    CheckType --\u003e|\"NONE\"| NoMerge[\"Return unchanged\"]\n    CheckType --\u003e|\"MERGE\"| BasicMerge[\"mergeMessages()\u003cbr/\u003estrict=false\"]\n    CheckType --\u003e|\"MERGE_TOOLS\"| MergeTools[\"mergeMessages()\u003cbr/\u003etools=true\"]\n    CheckType --\u003e|\"STRICT\"| StrictMerge[\"mergeMessages()\u003cbr/\u003estrict=true\"]\n    CheckType --\u003e|\"SINGLE\"| SingleMerge[\"Merge all to single\"]\n    \n    BasicMerge --\u003e MergeConsecutive[\"Merge consecutive\u003cbr/\u003esame-role messages\"]\n    MergeTools --\u003e MergeWithTools[\"Merge but preserve\u003cbr/\u003etool messages\"]\n    StrictMerge --\u003e EnsureAlternating[\"Ensure alternating\u003cbr/\u003euser/assistant\"]\n    SingleMerge --\u003e CombineAll[\"Combine all into\u003cbr/\u003esingle message\"]\n    \n    subgraph MergingStrategies [\"Merging Strategies\"]\n        Strategy1[\"NONE: No merging\"]\n        Strategy2[\"MERGE: Basic merging\"]\n        Strategy3[\"MERGE_TOOLS: Preserve tools\"]\n        Strategy4[\"STRICT: Enforce alternation\"]\n        Strategy5[\"SINGLE: Single message\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Merging Strategies:**\n\n| Strategy | Description | Use Case |\n|----------|-------------|----------|\n| `NONE` | No post-processing | APIs that allow consecutive same-role messages |\n| `MERGE` | Merge consecutive messages with same role | Basic compatibility |\n| `MERGE_TOOLS` | Merge but preserve tool calls/responses | Tool-calling APIs |\n| `STRICT` | Ensure strict user/assistant alternation | Claude, some OpenAI models |\n| `STRICT_TOOLS` | Strict alternation with tool preservation | Claude with tools |\n| `SINGLE` | Merge all into single message | Legacy text completion |\n\n**Sources:** [src/prompt-converters.js:85-150](), [src/prompt-converters.js:200-400](), [public/scripts/openai.js:216-227]()\n\n### Step 12: Token Budget Enforcement\n\nThe final step ensures the assembled prompt fits within the model's context window.\n\n**Title: Token Budget Enforcement and Trimming**\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    FinalPrompt[\"Final message array\"]\n    CountTokens[\"Count total tokens\"]\n    CheckBudget{\"Tokens \u003e max_context?\"}\n    \n    CheckBudget --\u003e|No| SendToAPI[\"Send to API\"]\n    CheckBudget --\u003e|Yes| ApplyTrimming[\"Apply trimming strategy\"]\n    \n    ApplyTrimming --\u003e CheckStrategy{\"Trimming strategy\"}\n    \n    CheckStrategy --\u003e|\"Middle-out\"| TrimMiddle[\"Remove messages\u003cbr/\u003efrom middle\"]\n    CheckStrategy --\u003e|\"Truncate\"| TrimOldest[\"Remove oldest\u003cbr/\u003emessages\"]\n    CheckStrategy --\u003e|\"Smart\"| SmartTrim[\"Preserve system\u003cbr/\u003eand recent messages\"]\n    \n    TrimMiddle --\u003e RecountTokens[\"Recount tokens\"]\n    TrimOldest --\u003e RecountTokens\n    SmartTrim --\u003e RecountTokens\n    \n    RecountTokens --\u003e CheckBudget\n    \n    subgraph TokenCounting [\"Token Counting\"]\n        GetTokenizer[\"getTokenizerModel()\"]\n        CountAsync[\"getTokenCountAsync()\"]\n        CacheResult[\"Cache token counts\"]\n    end\n    \n    CountTokens --\u003e GetTokenizer\n    GetTokenizer --\u003e CountAsync\n    CountAsync --\u003e CacheResult\n```\n\n**Trimming Process:**\n\n1. **Count Tokens**: Use `getTokenCountAsync()` to count all message tokens\n2. **Check Budget**: Compare against `openai_max_context`\n3. **Apply Trimming**: Remove messages based on strategy\n4. **Preserve System**: Always keep system prompts and recent context\n5. **Iterate**: Repeat until within budget\n\n**Token Reservations:**\n\n- **System Prompts**: Always preserved\n- **Recent Messages**: Last N messages preserved (configurable)\n- **World Info**: Budget allocated as percentage of context\n- **Response Buffer**: Reserved for model's response (`openai_max_tokens`)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:234](), [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:760-850](), [public/scripts/openai.js:1200-1400]()\n\n---\n\n## Text Completion Assembly Pipeline\n\nThe text completion pipeline assembles prompts into a single string rather than a structured message array.\n\n**Title: Text Completion Assembly Flow**\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Generate[\"Generate() called\"]\n    BuildString[\"Build single string\"]\n    \n    subgraph Components [\"Component Assembly\"]\n        StoryString[\"Story string template\"]\n        CharExamples[\"Character examples\"]\n        ExampleSep[\"example_separator\"]\n        ChatStart[\"chat_start marker\"]\n        ChatHistory[\"Chat history as text\"]\n        LastMessage[\"Last message incomplete?\"]\n    end\n    \n    BuildString --\u003e StoryString\n    StoryString --\u003e CharExamples\n    CharExamples --\u003e ExampleSep\n    ExampleSep --\u003e ChatStart\n    ChatStart --\u003e ChatHistory\n    ChatHistory --\u003e LastMessage\n    \n    LastMessage --\u003e ApplyInstruct[\"formatInstructModePrompt()\"]\n    ApplyInstruct --\u003e AddStopStrings[\"Add stopping strings\"]\n    AddStopStrings --\u003e SendToAPI[\"Send to text completion API\"]\n    \n    subgraph TextAPIs [\"Text Completion APIs\"]\n        Kobold[\"KoboldAI\"]\n        NovelAI[\"NovelAI\"]\n        TextGenWebUI[\"Text Generation WebUI\"]\n        Horde[\"AI Horde\"]\n        LlamaCpp[\"llama.cpp\"]\n        Ollama[\"Ollama\"]\n    end\n    \n    SendToAPI --\u003e TextAPIs\n```\n\n**Key Differences from Chat Completion:**\n\n1. **Single String**: All components concatenated into one string\n2. **No Roles**: No system/user/assistant distinction\n3. **Instruct Mode**: Applied to entire string, not per-message\n4. **Stopping Strings**: Character names often used as stop strings\n5. **Simpler Assembly**: Linear concatenation vs. structured injection\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:1-500](), [public/scripts/kai-settings.js:1-300](), [public/scripts/instruct-mode.js:200-300]()\n\n---\n\n## API-Specific Formatting\n\nDifferent APIs require different message formats. The `sendOpenAIRequest()` function handles API-specific transformations.\n\n**Title: API-Specific Message Transformation**\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    StandardMessages[\"Standard message array\"]\n    CheckSource{\"chat_completion_source\"}\n    \n    CheckSource --\u003e|\"openai\"| OpenAIFormat[\"OpenAI format:\u003cbr/\u003eDirect use\"]\n    CheckSource --\u003e|\"claude\"| ClaudeFormat[\"convertClaudeMessages()\"]\n    CheckSource --\u003e|\"google\"| GoogleFormat[\"convertGooglePrompt()\"]\n    CheckSource --\u003e|\"mistral\"| MistralFormat[\"convertMistralMessages()\"]\n    CheckSource --\u003e|\"cohere\"| CohereFormat[\"convertCohereMessages()\"]\n    CheckSource --\u003e|\"openrouter\"| OpenRouterFormat[\"OpenRouter format:\u003cbr/\u003e+ embedOpenRouterMedia()\"]\n    \n    ClaudeFormat --\u003e ClaudeSystem[\"Separate system prompt\"]\n    GoogleFormat --\u003e GoogleParts[\"Convert to parts array\"]\n    MistralFormat --\u003e MistralStandard[\"Mistral standard\"]\n    CohereFormat --\u003e CohereRole[\"Convert role names\"]\n    OpenRouterFormat --\u003e MediaEmbed[\"Embed media inline\"]\n    \n    subgraph ConversionFunctions [\"Conversion Functions\"]\n        F1[\"convertClaudeMessages()\"]\n        F2[\"convertGooglePrompt()\"]\n        F3[\"convertMistralMessages()\"]\n        F4[\"convertCohereMessages()\"]\n        F5[\"embedOpenRouterMedia()\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**API Conversion Functions:**\n\n| API | Function | Key Transformations |\n|-----|----------|---------------------|\n| OpenAI | None (native) | Direct use of message array |\n| Claude | `convertClaudeMessages()` | Separate system prompt, merge consecutive user messages |\n| Google | `convertGooglePrompt()` | Convert to `parts` array with `text` objects |\n| Mistral | `convertMistralMessages()` | Standard format, specific tool handling |\n| Cohere | `convertCohereMessages()` | Convert `assistant` → `chatbot` role |\n| OpenRouter | `embedOpenRouterMedia()` | Embed media as base64 in content |\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:205-280](), [src/prompt-converters.js:60-500]()\n\n---\n\n## Context Settings\n\nContext settings control how character information and chat structure are assembled into prompts.\n\n### Story String Template\n\nThe `story_string` is a Handlebars template that assembles character information into the context.\n\n**Default Template:**\n```handlebars\n{{#if system}}{{system}}\\n{{/if}}\n{{#if description}}{{description}}\\n{{/if}}\n{{#if personality}}{{char}}'s personality: {{personality}}\\n{{/if}}\n{{#if scenario}}Scenario: {{scenario}}\\n{{/if}}\n{{#if persona}}{{persona}}\\n{{/if}}\n```\n\n**Available Template Variables:**\n\n| Variable | Source | Description |\n|----------|--------|-------------|\n| `{{system}}` | System prompt | Primary instructions |\n| `{{description}}` | Character card | Character description |\n| `{{personality}}` | Character card | Character personality |\n| `{{scenario}}` | Character card | Current scenario |\n| `{{persona}}` | User persona | User/persona description |\n| `{{char}}` | Character name | Character display name |\n| `{{user}}` | User name | User display name |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/power-user.js:87](), [public/scripts/power-user.js:254-264]()\n\n### Story String Position\n\nThe story string can be injected at different positions:\n\n| Position | Value | Description |\n|----------|-------|-------------|\n| `IN_PROMPT` | 0 | Included in system prompt (default) |\n| `IN_CHAT` | 1 | Deprecated |\n| `BEFORE_PROMPT` | 2 | Before all other prompts |\n\n**Related Settings:**\n\n- **`story_string_depth`**: Injection depth (default: 1)\n- **`story_string_role`**: Message role (default: `SYSTEM`)\n- **`story_string_position`**: Position type (default: `IN_PROMPT`)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/power-user.js:261-263](), [public/scripts/power-user.js:254-264]()\n\n### Example Separator and Chat Start\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    CharExamples[\"Character Examples\"]\n    ExampleSep[\"example_separator\u003cbr/\u003e(default: ***)\"]\n    ChatStart[\"chat_start\u003cbr/\u003e(default: ***)\"]\n    ChatHistory[\"Chat History\"]\n    \n    CharExamples --\u003e ExampleSep\n    ExampleSep --\u003e ChatStart\n    ChatStart --\u003e ChatHistory\n```\n\n- **`example_separator`**: Delimiter between example messages (default: `***`)\n- **`chat_start`**: Marker indicating start of actual chat (default: `***`)\n\nThese are primarily used in text completion to separate training examples from the actual conversation.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/power-user.js:88-89](), [public/scripts/power-user.js:256-258]()\n\n### Persona Description Position\n\nThe persona description can be positioned in multiple ways:\n\n| Position | Value | Description |\n|----------|-------|-------------|\n| `IN_PROMPT` | 0 | Included in story string template |\n| `AFTER_CHAR` | 1 | Deprecated |\n| `TOP_AN` | 2 | Top of Author's Note position |\n| `BOTTOM_AN` | 3 | Bottom of Author's Note position |\n| `AT_DEPTH` | 4 | Injected at `persona_description_depth` |\n| `NONE` | 9 | Not included |\n\n**Related Settings:**\n\n- **`persona_description_depth`**: Injection depth when using `AT_DEPTH`\n- **`persona_description_role`**: Message role (system/user/assistant)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/power-user.js:112-121](), [public/scripts/power-user.js:297-302]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration Points\n\n### Message Class and MessageCollection\n\nThe `Message` class represents individual messages in the prompt, and `MessageCollection` manages the collection.\n\n```mermaid\nclassDiagram\n    class Message {\n        +string identifier\n        +string role\n        +string content\n        +string name\n        +number getTokens()\n        +string getText()\n    }\n    \n    class MessageCollection {\n        +Message[] collection\n        +PromptManager promptManager\n        +add(Message)\n        +getChat()\n        +getItemizedIndex(index)\n        +preparePrompts()\n        +prepareOpenAIMessages()\n    }\n    \n    class TokenHandler {\n        +countTokens(string)\n        +resetCounts()\n    }\n    \n    MessageCollection --\u003e Message\n    MessageCollection --\u003e TokenHandler\n```\n\n**Key Methods:**\n\n- **`Message.getTokens()`**: Returns cached token count for this message\n- **`MessageCollection.add()`**: Adds a message to the collection\n- **`MessageCollection.prepareOpenAIMessages()`**: Assembles all messages into final array\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:300-450](), [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:300-400]()\n\n### Character Overrides\n\nCharacters can override prompt settings on a per-character basis.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    DefaultPrompts[\"oai_settings.prompts\"]\n    CharOverrides[\"characters[id].data.extensions.prompts\"]\n    CheckOverride{\"Has override?\"}\n    \n    DefaultPrompts --\u003e CheckOverride\n    CharOverrides --\u003e CheckOverride\n    \n    CheckOverride --\u003e|Yes| UseOverride[\"Use character prompt\"]\n    CheckOverride --\u003e|No| UseDefault[\"Use default prompt\"]\n    \n    UseOverride --\u003e FinalPrompt[\"Final prompt collection\"]\n    UseDefault --\u003e FinalPrompt\n```\n\n**Override Priority:**\n\n1. Character-specific prompt settings (highest)\n2. Chat-specific prompt settings\n3. Global prompt settings (lowest)\n\nPrompts with `forbid_overrides: true` cannot be overridden.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:650-750](), [public/script.js:1-100]()\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\nSillyTavern's prompt management system provides a flexible framework for:\n\n1. **Defining prompts** with rich metadata (role, depth, order, triggers)\n2. **Collecting prompts** from multiple sources (system, character, extensions, World Info)\n3. **Ordering prompts** using injection depths and sort orders\n4. **Managing tokens** with real-time counting and budget warnings\n5. **Integrating** with instruct mode, context settings, and other systems\n\nThis architecture enables fine-grained control over prompt construction while remaining accessible through a visual UI.\n\n**Primary Implementation Files:**\n- [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:1-1000]() - Core prompt management\n- [public/scripts/power-user.js:254-370]() - Context settings\n- [public/scripts/instruct-mode.js:1-300]() - Instruct mode formatting\n- [public/scripts/world-info.js:1-500]() - World Info integration"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"24:T6f23,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Tokenization and Token Management\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [public/css/promptmanager.css](public/css/promptmanager.css)\n- [public/scripts/PromptManager.js](public/scripts/PromptManager.js)\n- [public/scripts/kai-settings.js](public/scripts/kai-settings.js)\n- [public/scripts/nai-settings.js](public/scripts/nai-settings.js)\n- [public/scripts/preset-manager.js](public/scripts/preset-manager.js)\n- [public/scripts/templates/promptManagerFooter.html](public/scripts/templates/promptManagerFooter.html)\n- [public/scripts/templates/promptManagerHeader.html](public/scripts/templates/promptManagerHeader.html)\n- [public/scripts/templates/promptManagerListHeader.html](public/scripts/templates/promptManagerListHeader.html)\n- [public/scripts/textgen-models.js](public/scripts/textgen-models.js)\n- [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js](public/scripts/textgen-settings.js)\n- [public/scripts/tokenizers.js](public/scripts/tokenizers.js)\n- [src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js](src/endpoints/backends/text-completions.js)\n- [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js](src/endpoints/tokenizers.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThe Tokenization and Token Management system converts text to tokens and manages token budgets across SillyTavern's AI integrations. This system provides three core functions:\n\n1. **Token Counting**: Determining the token count of text strings for context budget calculations\n2. **Token Encoding/Decoding**: Converting between text and token IDs for logit bias, banned tokens, and API-level control\n3. **Context Budget Management**: Tracking and enforcing token limits during prompt assembly\n\nThe system supports both local tokenizers (server-side SentencePiece, Web Tokenizers, Tiktoken) and remote tokenizers (querying the AI backend's tokenization endpoint).\n\nFor how token counts are used during prompt construction, see page 3.3 (Prompt Assembly Pipeline). For how World Info manages token budgets, see page 6.1 (World Info System).\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\nSillyTavern's tokenization system operates on three levels:\n\n1. **Frontend Token Counting** - Provides synchronous and asynchronous token counting with caching ([public/scripts/tokenizers.js:1-1000]())\n2. **Backend Tokenization** - Server-side tokenizer implementations using SentencePiece, Web Tokenizers, and Tiktoken ([src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:1-1000]())\n3. **Remote Tokenization** - Queries the active AI backend for tokenization when supported ([public/scripts/tokenizers.js:365-432]())\n\nThe system automatically selects the appropriate tokenizer based on the active API and model, falling back to estimation when exact tokenization is unavailable.\n\n**Tokenization System Architecture**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Client[\"Client (tokenizers.js)\"]\n        getTokenCount[\"getTokenCount(str, padding)\"]\n        getTokenCountAsync[\"getTokenCountAsync(str, padding)\"]\n        tokenCache[\"tokenCache\u003cbr/\u003e{cacheKey: count}\"]\n        bestMatch[\"getTokenizerBestMatch(forApi)\"]\n        callTokenizer[\"callTokenizer(type, str)\"]\n        counterWrapperOpenAI[\"counterWrapperOpenAI(text)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Selection[\"Tokenizer Selection\"]\n        tokenizerEnum[\"tokenizers enum\u003cbr/\u003eLLAMA=3, OPENAI=2,\u003cbr/\u003eCLAUDE=11, LLAMA3=12,\u003cbr/\u003eAPI_CURRENT=6, etc.\"]\n        currentRemote[\"currentRemoteTokenizerAPI()\"]\n        bestMatchAlgo[\"Model Name Pattern Matching\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Backend[\"Server (src/endpoints/tokenizers.js)\"]\n        sppLlama[\"spp_llama\u003cbr/\u003eSentencePieceTokenizer\"]\n        sppMistral[\"spp_mistral\u003cbr/\u003eSentencePieceTokenizer\"]\n        llama3Web[\"llama3_tokenizer\u003cbr/\u003eWebTokenizer\"]\n        claudeWeb[\"claude_tokenizer\u003cbr/\u003eWebTokenizer\"]\n        getTiktoken[\"getTiktokenTokenizer(model)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Remote[\"Remote API Endpoints\"]\n        textgenEncode[\"/v1/internal/encode\"]\n        koboldCount[\"/api/extra/tokencount\"]\n    end\n    \n    getTokenCount --\u003e tokenCache\n    getTokenCountAsync --\u003e tokenCache\n    tokenCache --\u003e|\"miss\"| callTokenizer\n    \n    callTokenizer --\u003e bestMatch\n    bestMatch --\u003e tokenizerEnum\n    tokenizerEnum --\u003e currentRemote\n    \n    currentRemote --\u003e|\"API_TEXTGENERATIONWEBUI\"| textgenEncode\n    currentRemote --\u003e|\"API_KOBOLD\"| koboldCount\n    \n    tokenizerEnum --\u003e|\"POST /api/tokenizers/llama/encode\"| sppLlama\n    tokenizerEnum --\u003e|\"POST /api/tokenizers/mistral/encode\"| sppMistral\n    tokenizerEnum --\u003e|\"POST /api/tokenizers/llama3/encode\"| llama3Web\n    tokenizerEnum --\u003e|\"POST /api/tokenizers/claude/encode\"| claudeWeb\n    tokenizerEnum --\u003e|\"POST /api/tokenizers/openai/encode\"| getTiktoken\n    \n    counterWrapperOpenAI --\u003e getTiktoken\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:15-37](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:282-362](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:365-432](), [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:242-258]()\n\n## Tokenizer Types\n\nSillyTavern supports multiple tokenizer types, each corresponding to different model families:\n\n| Tokenizer ID | Name | Type | Use Case |\n|--------------|------|------|----------|\n| `tokenizers.NONE` | None | Estimation | Fallback (3.35 chars/token) |\n| `tokenizers.GPT2` | GPT-2 | Tiktoken | Legacy GPT models |\n| `tokenizers.OPENAI` | OpenAI | Tiktoken | GPT-3.5, GPT-4, GPT-4o |\n| `tokenizers.LLAMA` | LLaMA | SentencePiece | LLaMA, LLaMA 2 |\n| `tokenizers.LLAMA3` | LLaMA 3 | Web Tokenizer | LLaMA 3 family |\n| `tokenizers.MISTRAL` | Mistral | SentencePiece | Mistral, Mixtral |\n| `tokenizers.CLAUDE` | Claude | Web Tokenizer | Anthropic Claude models |\n| `tokenizers.GEMMA` | Gemma | SentencePiece | Google Gemma |\n| `tokenizers.QWEN2` | Qwen2 | Web Tokenizer | Qwen2 family |\n| `tokenizers.COMMAND_R` | Command-R | Web Tokenizer | Cohere Command-R |\n| `tokenizers.NERD` | NerdStash | SentencePiece | NovelAI Clio |\n| `tokenizers.NERD2` | NerdStash v2 | SentencePiece | NovelAI Kayra |\n| `tokenizers.API_CURRENT` | API (Current) | Remote | Delegates to remote API |\n| `tokenizers.API_TEXTGENERATIONWEBUI` | API (Text Gen) | Remote | Text Gen WebUI tokenization |\n| `tokenizers.API_KOBOLD` | API (KoboldAI) | Remote | KoboldAI/KoboldCpp tokenization |\n| `tokenizers.BEST_MATCH` | Best Match | Auto-select | Automatic selection based on API/model |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:15-37](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:40-55](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:62-63]()\n\n## Best Match Algorithm\n\nThe `getTokenizerBestMatch()` function automatically selects the most appropriate tokenizer based on the active API and model name:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    Start[\"getTokenizerBestMatch(forApi)\"]\n    CheckAPI{\"API Type?\"}\n    \n    Start --\u003e CheckAPI\n    \n    CheckAPI --\u003e|\"novel\"| NovelCheck{\"Model Name?\"}\n    NovelCheck --\u003e|\"clio\"| ReturnNerd[\"Return NERD\"]\n    NovelCheck --\u003e|\"kayra\"| ReturnNerd2[\"Return NERD2\"]\n    NovelCheck --\u003e|\"erato\"| ReturnLlama3[\"Return LLAMA3\"]\n    \n    CheckAPI --\u003e|\"kobold/textgen\"| ConnCheck{\"Connected?\"}\n    ConnCheck --\u003e|\"Yes\"| APICheck{\"API Supports\u003cbr/\u003eTokenization?\"}\n    APICheck --\u003e|\"Yes (Kobold)\"| ReturnAPIKobold[\"Return API_KOBOLD\"]\n    APICheck --\u003e|\"Yes (TextGen)\"| ReturnAPITextgen[\"Return API_TEXTGENERATIONWEBUI\"]\n    APICheck --\u003e|\"No\"| ModelCheck{\"Model Name?\"}\n    \n    ConnCheck --\u003e|\"No\"| ModelCheck\n    \n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"llama3/llama-3\"| ReturnLlama3\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"mistral/mixtral\"| ReturnMistral[\"Return MISTRAL\"]\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"gemma\"| ReturnGemma[\"Return GEMMA\"]\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"qwen2\"| ReturnQwen2[\"Return QWEN2\"]\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"command-r\"| ReturnCommandR[\"Return COMMAND_R\"]\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"default\"| ReturnLlama[\"Return LLAMA\"]\n    \n    CheckAPI --\u003e|\"other\"| ReturnNone[\"Return NONE\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:282-362]()\n\n### Remote Tokenization Support\n\nRemote tokenization is available for specific backends when they expose tokenization endpoints:\n\n- **KoboldAI Classic**: Requires version 1.41+ ([public/scripts/kai-settings.js:72]())\n- **Text Generation WebUI (Ooba)**: Available when `x-supports-tokenization` header is present ([public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:724-725]())\n- **Supported Text Gen Types**: Ooba, Tabby, KoboldCpp, LlamaCpp, and similar backends ([public/scripts/tokenizers.js:306-316]())\n\nThe system uses session storage to track tokenization support and errors to avoid repeated failed requests:\n\n- `TOKENIZER_SUPPORTED_KEY`: Indicates the backend supports tokenization\n- `TOKENIZER_WARNING_KEY`: Marks that the backend reported a tokenization error\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:13-14](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:304-322]()\n\n## Token Counting and Caching\n\n### Counting Functions\n\nThe system provides two token counting functions:\n\n**`getTokenCount(str, padding)`** - Synchronous token counting (deprecated)\n- Returns token count immediately\n- Uses cached results when available\n- Falls back to API-specific logic for OpenAI\n- **Deprecated**: Use `getTokenCountAsync` for new code\n\n**`getTokenCountAsync(str, padding)`** - Asynchronous token counting\n- Returns a Promise resolving to token count\n- Supports all tokenizer types including remote APIs\n- Implements intelligent caching\n- Recommended for all new code\n\nBoth functions accept an optional `padding` parameter to add extra tokens to the count for system/formatting overhead.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:440-487](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:496-543]()\n\n### Token Cache Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Cache Key Generation\"\n        tokenizerType[\"Tokenizer Type ID\"]\n        textHash[\"String Hash (getStringHash)\"]\n        modelHash[\"Model Hash (for API_TEXTGENERATIONWEBUI)\"]\n        padding[\"Padding Value\"]\n        \n        tokenizerType --\u003e cacheKey[\"Cache Key:\u003cbr/\u003etokenizerType-hash[modelHash]+padding\"]\n        textHash --\u003e cacheKey\n        modelHash --\u003e cacheKey\n        padding --\u003e cacheKey\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Cache Storage\"\n        tokenCache[\"tokenCache object\u003cbr/\u003e(in-memory)\"]\n        objectStore[\"objectStore\u003cbr/\u003e(IndexedDB via localforage)\"]\n        \n        tokenCache --\u003e objectStore\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Cache Operations\"\n        lookup[\"Cache Lookup\"]\n        store[\"Store Result\"]\n        save[\"saveTokenCache()\u003cbr/\u003e(periodic)\"]\n        load[\"loadTokenCache()\u003cbr/\u003e(on init)\"]\n    end\n    \n    cacheKey --\u003e lookup\n    lookup --\u003e|\"Hit\"| returnCached[\"Return Cached Value\"]\n    lookup --\u003e|\"Miss\"| callTokenizer[\"callTokenizer()\"]\n    callTokenizer --\u003e store\n    store --\u003e tokenCache\n    \n    load --\u003e objectStore\n    objectStore --\u003e tokenCache\n    tokenCache --\u003e save\n    save --\u003e objectStore\n```\n\n**Cache Key Format**: `{tokenizerId}-{stringHash}[{modelHash}]+{padding}`\n\nThe cache key combines:\n- Tokenizer type ID (e.g., `3` for LLAMA)\n- String hash of the input text\n- Model hash (for `API_TEXTGENERATIONWEBUI` to handle model changes)\n- Padding value\n\n**Example**: `3-1234567890+0` (LLAMA tokenizer, hash 1234567890, no padding)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:157-196](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:470-486](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:518-542]()\n\n### Cache Persistence\n\nThe token cache is persisted to IndexedDB using LocalForage:\n\n- **Store Name**: `SillyTavern_ChatCompletions`\n- **Key**: `tokenCache`\n- **Load**: On initialization via `loadTokenCache()`\n- **Save**: Periodic via `saveTokenCache()`\n- **Reset**: Available via `resetTokenCache()` debug function\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:155-156](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:168-196]()\n\n## Backend Tokenizer Implementation\n\n### Tokenizer Classes\n\nThe backend implements three types of tokenizers:\n\n```mermaid\nclassDiagram\n    class SentencePieceTokenizer {\n        -SentencePieceProcessor instance\n        -string model\n        -string fallbackModel\n        +constructor(model, fallbackModel)\n        +get() Promise~SentencePieceProcessor~\n    }\n    \n    class WebTokenizer {\n        -Tokenizer instance\n        -string model\n        -string fallbackModel\n        +constructor(model, fallbackModel)\n        +get() Promise~Tokenizer~\n    }\n    \n    class TiktokenWrapper {\n        +getTiktokenTokenizer(model) Tiktoken\n        +tokenizersCache object\n    }\n    \n    note for SentencePieceTokenizer \"Uses @agnai/sentencepiece-js\\nModels: llama, mistral, yi, gemma, jamba\"\n    note for WebTokenizer \"Uses @agnai/web-tokenizers\\nModels: claude, llama3, command-r, qwen2, nemo\"\n    note for TiktokenWrapper \"Uses tiktoken npm package\\nModels: GPT-2, GPT-3.5, GPT-4, GPT-4o\"\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:147-190](), [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:196-240](), [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:520-529]()\n\n### Model Download and Caching\n\nThe backend can download tokenizer models from URLs when enabled:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    getPath[\"getPathToTokenizer(model, fallback)\"]\n    isURL{\"Is URL?\"}\n    \n    getPath --\u003e isURL\n    isURL --\u003e|\"No\"| returnPath[\"Return Path\"]\n    isURL --\u003e|\"Yes\"| checkCache{\"Cached?\"}\n    \n    checkCache --\u003e|\"Yes\"| checkCompressed{\"Compressed (.gz)?\"}\n    checkCompressed --\u003e|\"Yes\"| decompress[\"Decompress and Cache\"]\n    checkCompressed --\u003e|\"No\"| returnCached[\"Return Cached Path\"]\n    decompress --\u003e returnCached\n    \n    checkCache --\u003e|\"No\"| downloadEnabled{\"Download\u003cbr/\u003eEnabled?\"}\n    downloadEnabled --\u003e|\"No\"| useFallback[\"Use Fallback Model\"]\n    downloadEnabled --\u003e|\"Yes\"| download[\"Fetch from URL\"]\n    download --\u003e saveCache[\"Save to _cache/\"]\n    saveCache --\u003e returnCached\n    \n    useFallback --\u003e returnPath\n```\n\n**Configuration**: `enableDownloadableTokenizers` in `config.yaml` (default: `true`)\n\n**Cache Location**: `DATA_ROOT/_cache/`\n\n**Compressed Models**: Models with `.gz` extension are automatically decompressed after download\n\n**Examples**:\n- Command-R: `https://github.com/SillyTavern/SillyTavern-Tokenizers/raw/main/command-r.json.gz`\n- Qwen2: `https://github.com/SillyTavern/SillyTavern-Tokenizers/raw/main/qwen2.json.gz`\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:61-142](), [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:251-255]()\n\n### Backend API Endpoints\n\nThe backend exposes encoding and decoding endpoints for each tokenizer:\n\n| Endpoint Pattern | Method | Purpose |\n|------------------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/tokenizers/{model}/encode` | POST | Encode text to token IDs and chunks |\n| `/api/tokenizers/{model}/decode` | POST | Decode token IDs to text and chunks |\n\n**Request Body (Encode)**:\n```json\n{\n  \"text\": \"string to tokenize\"\n}\n```\n\n**Response Body (Encode)**:\n```json\n{\n  \"ids\": [1234, 5678, ...],\n  \"count\": 42,\n  \"chunks\": [\"token\", \"text\", ...]\n}\n```\n\n**Request Body (Decode)**:\n```json\n{\n  \"ids\": [1234, 5678, ...]\n}\n```\n\n**Response Body (Decode)**:\n```json\n{\n  \"text\": \"decoded string\",\n  \"chunks\": [\"token\", \"text\", ...]\n}\n```\n\n**Available Models**: `llama`, `nerdstash`, `nerdstash_v2`, `mistral`, `yi`, `gemma`, `jamba`, `gpt2`, `claude`, `llama3`, `qwen2`, `command-r`, `command-a`, `nemo`, `deepseek`\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:731-760](), [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:554-727]()\n\n### Special Endpoint: OpenAI\n\nThe `/api/tokenizers/openai/encode` endpoint performs model-specific routing:\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart LR\n    Request[\"POST /api/tokenizers/openai/encode\u003cbr/\u003e?model=MODEL_NAME\"]\n    \n    ModelCheck{\"Model Name?\"}\n    \n    Request --\u003e ModelCheck\n    \n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"llama3/llama-3\"| Llama3Handler[\"llama3_tokenizer\"]\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"llama\"| LlamaHandler[\"spp_llama\"]\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"mistral\"| MistralHandler[\"spp_mistral\"]\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"yi\"| YiHandler[\"spp_yi\"]\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"gemma/gemini\"| GemmaHandler[\"spp_gemma\"]\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"claude\"| ClaudeHandler[\"claude_tokenizer\"]\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"deepseek\"| DeepseekHandler[\"deepseek_tokenizer\"]\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"qwen2\"| Qwen2Handler[\"qwen2Tokenizer\"]\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"command-r\"| CommandRHandler[\"commandRTokenizer\"]\n    ModelCheck --\u003e|\"default\"| TiktokenHandler[\"getTiktokenTokenizer(model)\"]\n```\n\nThis allows OpenAI API users to use model-appropriate tokenizers without manual selection.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:762-862]()\n\n## Integration with Text Generation\n\n### Custom Token Bans\n\nThe tokenization system integrates with text generation to support custom token bans through the `getCustomTokenBans()` function:\n\n**Token Ban Processing Flow**\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    getCustomTokenBans[\"getCustomTokenBans(settings)\"]\n    \n    sources[\"Token Ban Sources\"]\n    sources --\u003e|\"1\"| settingsBanned[\"settings.banned_tokens\u003cbr/\u003e(per-preset bans)\"]\n    sources --\u003e|\"2\"| globalBanned[\"settings.global_banned_tokens\u003cbr/\u003e(global bans)\"]\n    sources --\u003e|\"3\"| macroBanned[\"textgenerationwebui_banned_in_macros\u003cbr/\u003e({{banned}} macro)\"]\n    \n    settingsBanned --\u003e combine[\"Split by newlines and combine\"]\n    globalBanned --\u003e combine\n    macroBanned --\u003e combine\n    \n    combine --\u003e substituteParams[\"substituteParams(sequence)\"]\n    substituteParams --\u003e parse[\"Parse Each Line\"]\n    \n    parse --\u003e rawTokens{\"Format?\"}\n    rawTokens --\u003e|\"[1,2,3]\"| jsonParse[\"JSON.parse() token IDs\"]\n    rawTokens --\u003e|'\"text\"'| addString[\"Add to banned_strings array\"]\n    rawTokens --\u003e|\"text\"| tokenize[\"getTextTokens(tokenizer, line)\"]\n    \n    jsonParse --\u003e addToList[\"Add to banned_tokens array\"]\n    tokenize --\u003e addToList\n    \n    addToList --\u003e result[\"Return {\u003cbr/\u003ebanned_tokens: '1,2,3,...',\u003cbr/\u003ebanned_strings: [...]\u003cbr/\u003e}\"]\n    addString --\u003e result\n```\n\n**Tokenizer Selection**: Uses `getTokenizerForTokenIds()` which prefers:\n1. `API_CURRENT` if available (for Text Completion APIs)\n2. User-selected tokenizer if it supports encoding (`ENCODE_TOKENIZERS`)\n3. Model-specific tokenizers (OpenRouter, DreamGen)\n4. Defaults to `LLAMA`\n\n**Format Examples**:\n- Raw token IDs: `[1234, 5678]` → parsed as integer array\n- Quoted strings: `\"specific phrase\"` → sent as string to backend\n- Plain text: `word` → tokenized locally to IDs\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:438-498](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:407-430]()\n\n### Logit Bias Calculation\n\nLogit bias uses tokenization to convert text sequences into token IDs for controlling model output probability:\n\n**Logit Bias Processing Flow**\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    calculateLogitBias[\"calculateLogitBias(settings)\"]\n    \n    getBias[\"settings.logit_bias array\u003cbr/\u003e[{text: 'word', value: -5}, ...]\"]\n    getBias --\u003e checkEmpty{\"Array empty?\"}\n    checkEmpty --\u003e|\"Yes\"| returnEmpty[\"Return {}\"]\n    checkEmpty --\u003e|\"No\"| getTokenizer[\"getTokenizerForTokenIds()\"]\n    \n    getTokenizer --\u003e iterate[\"For each bias entry\"]\n    \n    iterate --\u003e getLogitBiasListResult[\"getLogitBiasListResult(logit_bias, tokenizer, addBias)\"]\n    \n    getLogitBiasListResult --\u003e parseEntry{\"Entry Type?\"}\n    \n    parseEntry --\u003e|\"Text String\"| tokenizeText[\"getTextTokens(tokenizer, text)\"]\n    parseEntry --\u003e|\"Token IDs Array\"| useTokens[\"Use token IDs directly\"]\n    \n    tokenizeText --\u003e addBias[\"addBias(biasValue, tokenIds)\u003cbr/\u003eresult[tokenId] = biasValue\"]\n    useTokens --\u003e addBias\n    \n    addBias --\u003e buildResult[\"Accumulate in result object\"]\n    buildResult --\u003e returnResult[\"Return {\u003cbr/\u003e  '1234': -5,\u003cbr/\u003e  '5678': 2.5,\u003cbr/\u003e  ...\u003cbr/\u003e}\"]\n    \n    returnEmpty --\u003e End\n    returnResult --\u003e End\n```\n\n**Key Components**:\n\n- **`getTokenizerForTokenIds()`**: Selects encoding tokenizer\n  - Prefers `API_CURRENT` for Text Completion APIs\n  - Falls back to user-selected tokenizer if in `ENCODE_TOKENIZERS`\n  - Uses model-specific tokenizers (OpenRouter, DreamGen)\n  - Defaults to `LLAMA`\n\n- **`getLogitBiasListResult()`**: Processes bias entries\n  - Handles both text and token ID formats\n  - Calls `addBias()` callback for each token\n  - Shared with NovelAI banned words system\n\n- **Result Format**: `{ tokenId: biasValue }` object\n  - Positive values increase probability\n  - Negative values decrease probability\n  - Sent to backend in generation request\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:517-549](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:407-430](), [public/scripts/logit-bias.js]()\n\n### Remote Tokenization Endpoints\n\nFor backends that support remote tokenization, the system makes requests to:\n\n**Text Generation WebUI**:\n```\nPOST {baseUrl}/v1/internal/encode\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\n  \"text\": \"string to encode\"\n}\n```\n\n**KoboldAI**:\n```\nPOST {baseUrl}/api/extra/tokencount\nContent-Type: application/json\n\n{\n  \"prompt\": \"string to count\"\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:864-933](), [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:935-983]()\n\n## Context Budget Management\n\nThe tokenization system integrates with the Prompt Manager to enforce context limits and manage token budgets.\n\n### Token Handler in PromptManager\n\nThe `PromptManager` class uses a `TokenHandler` instance to manage token counting across all prompt components:\n\n**TokenHandler Integration Flow**\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    PM[\"PromptManager\"]\n    TH[\"TokenHandler\u003cbr/\u003e(openai.js)\"]\n    getCounts[\"getCounts()\u003cbr/\u003eReturns token counts per prompt\"]\n    \n    PM --\u003e|\"has-a\"| TH\n    PM --\u003e|\"calls\"| getCounts\n    \n    getCounts --\u003e|\"returns\"| countsObject[\"{\u003cbr/\u003epromptId: tokenCount,\u003cbr/\u003e...\u003cbr/\u003e}\"]\n    \n    countsObject --\u003e render[\"render()\u003cbr/\u003eDisplay token counts in UI\"]\n    countsObject --\u003e validate[\"Validate against max_context\"]\n```\n\nThe `TokenHandler` maintains a `counts` object mapping prompt identifiers to token counts:\n\n```javascript\ncounts = {\n  'main': 150,\n  'charDescription': 342,\n  'worldInfoBefore': 1200,\n  // ... etc\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:364-366](), [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:446-448]()\n\n### Token Budget Display\n\nThe Prompt Manager UI displays token counts for each active prompt component:\n\n**Token Count Display Architecture**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph UI[\"Prompt Manager UI\"]\n        promptList[\"#completion_prompt_manager_list\"]\n        promptItem[\".completion_prompt_manager_prompt\"]\n        tokenSpan[\".prompt_manager_prompt_tokens\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Data[\"Token Data Flow\"]\n        TH[\"TokenHandler.getCounts()\"]\n        counts[\"counts object\"]\n        render[\"render() / renderDebounced()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Visual[\"Visual Indicators\"]\n        normalColor[\"Normal: default color\"]\n        warningColor[\"Warning: warningTokenThreshold\"]\n        dangerColor[\"Danger: dangerTokenThreshold\"]\n    end\n    \n    TH --\u003e counts\n    counts --\u003e render\n    render --\u003e tokenSpan\n    \n    tokenSpan --\u003e normalColor\n    tokenSpan --\u003e warningColor\n    tokenSpan --\u003e dangerColor\n    \n    promptItem --\u003e tokenSpan\n    promptList --\u003e promptItem\n```\n\nThe token display uses color-coded thresholds:\n- **Warning Threshold**: `warningTokenThreshold` (default: 1500 tokens)\n- **Danger Threshold**: `dangerTokenThreshold` (default: 500 tokens)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/PromptManager.js:338-340](), [public/css/promptmanager.css:45-49]()\n\n### Token Budget Calculation\n\nToken budgets are calculated during prompt assembly in `openai.js`:\n\n**Budget Calculation Flow**\n\n```mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    preparePrompts[\"preparePrompts(messages, ...)\"]\n    \n    maxTokens[\"max_context - max_length\"]\n    \n    tokenBudget[\"Calculate tokenBudget\"]\n    \n    preparePrompts --\u003e maxTokens\n    maxTokens --\u003e tokenBudget\n    \n    tokenBudget --\u003e countPrompts[\"Count all prompt tokens\"]\n    countPrompts --\u003e checkOverage{\"tokenCount \u003e tokenBudget?\"}\n    \n    checkOverage --\u003e|\"Yes\"| trimMessages[\"Trim messages from middle\"]\n    checkOverage --\u003e|\"No\"| returnMessages[\"Return final messages\"]\n    \n    trimMessages --\u003e returnMessages\n```\n\nThe budget calculation accounts for:\n- **Context Window Size** (`max_context`): Maximum tokens the model can process\n- **Response Length** (`max_length`): Tokens reserved for model response\n- **Fixed Overhead**: Tokens used by chat template formatting\n- **Variable Content**: User messages, character card, World Info, etc.\n\n**Token Budget Formula**:\n```\navailable_tokens = max_context - max_length - template_overhead\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js]() (in `preparePrompts` function)\n\n### World Info Token Budgets\n\nWorld Info (Lorebook) entries use dedicated token budget controls:\n\n**World Info Budget Types**:\n\n| Budget Type | Configuration | Default |\n|-------------|---------------|---------|\n| **Percentage** | `world_info_budget` | 25% of context |\n| **Absolute Cap** | `world_info_budget_cap` | 1500 tokens |\n\n**Budget Calculation**:\n```javascript\nconst available = max_context * (world_info_budget / 100);\nconst actualBudget = Math.min(available, world_info_budget_cap);\n```\n\nEach World Info entry also has individual token limits:\n- **Max Tokens**: Per-entry limit (e.g., 500 tokens max)\n- **Budget Priority**: Entries with higher priority get tokens first\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js]() (budget management functions)\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n### Token Count Estimation\n\nWhen exact tokenization is unavailable or fails, the system falls back to character-based estimation:\n\n**Ratio**: `CHARACTERS_PER_TOKEN_RATIO = 3.35`\n\n**Formula**: `Math.ceil(text.length / 3.35)`\n\nThis provides a conservative estimate for context management when precise counting isn't possible.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:11](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:164-166]()\n\n### Caching Strategy\n\nThe token cache significantly improves performance by avoiding repeated tokenization:\n\n1. **Cache on First Use**: Every tokenization result is cached with a composite key\n2. **Model-Specific Keys**: Different models use different cache entries to avoid conflicts\n3. **Persistent Storage**: Cache survives browser reloads via IndexedDB\n4. **Manual Reset**: Debug function `resetTokenCache()` available for troubleshooting\n\n**Cache Hit Benefits**:\n- Instant token counts for repeated text (e.g., character descriptions, system prompts)\n- Reduced API calls for remote tokenization\n- Lower CPU usage for local tokenization\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:168-196](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:470-486]()\n\n### Asynchronous Operation\n\nThe `getTokenCountAsync()` function is recommended because:\n\n- Non-blocking for large texts\n- Supports remote API calls naturally\n- Consistent interface across all tokenizer types\n- Better performance in UI-heavy operations\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:440-487]()\n\n## Tokenization in Different APIs\n\n### NovelAI\n\nNovelAI uses custom tokenizers for its models and has special handling for banned words:\n\n- **Clio**: Uses `NERD` (NerdStash) tokenizer\n- **Kayra**: Uses `NERD2` (NerdStash v2) tokenizer\n- **Erato**: Uses `LLAMA3` tokenizer\n\nThe `getBadWordIds()` function generates permutations of banned words to handle capitalization and spacing:\n\n**Permutations Generated**:\n1. Original text\n2. Original + leading space\n3. Capitalized first letter\n4. Capitalized + leading space\n5. Lowercase first letter\n6. Lowercase + leading space\n7. All uppercase\n8. All uppercase + leading space\n9. All lowercase\n10. All lowercase + leading space\n\n**Special Formats**:\n- `{verbatim text}`: Tokenized exactly as-is\n- `[1234, 5678]`: Raw token IDs (JSON array)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/nai-settings.js:435-518](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:287-296]()\n\n### OpenAI / Chat Completion APIs\n\nOpenAI tokenization uses Tiktoken with model-specific encodings:\n\n**Model Selection** (via `getTokenizerModel()`):\n- `o1`, `o3`, `gpt-5`: Use `o1` encoding\n- `gpt-4o`, `chatgpt-4o-latest`: Use `gpt-4o` encoding\n- `gpt-4-32k`: Use `gpt-4-32k` encoding\n- `gpt-4`: Use `gpt-4` encoding\n- `gpt-3.5-turbo`: Use model-specific encoding\n\nFor token counting with padding, OpenAI uses `NONE` tokenizer (estimation) for shadow prompt building, and delegates to `counterWrapperOpenAIAsync()` for extensions and World Info.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/tokenizers.js:431-518](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:448-456](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:504-512]()\n\n### Text Completion Backends\n\nText completion backends (KoboldAI, Ooba, etc.) prefer remote tokenization when available:\n\n**Feature Detection**:\n- KoboldAI: Version check for `1.41+` ([public/scripts/kai-settings.js:72]())\n- Ooba: `x-supports-tokenization` header ([public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:724]())\n\n**Fallback Chain**:\n1. Try remote API tokenization\n2. Use OpenRouter model-specific tokenizer (if OpenRouter)\n3. Use DreamGen model-specific tokenizer (if DreamGen)\n4. Parse model name for tokenizer hints (llama3, mistral, gemma, etc.)\n5. Default to `LLAMA` tokenizer\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tokenizers.js:306-322](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:401-419]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"25:T5050,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Tool Calling and Function Execution\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [public/scripts/RossAscends-mods.js](public/scripts/RossAscends-mods.js)\n- [public/scripts/custom-request.js](public/scripts/custom-request.js)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js](public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/caption/settings.html](public/scripts/extensions/caption/settings.html)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/shared.js](public/scripts/extensions/shared.js)\n- [public/scripts/openai.js](public/scripts/openai.js)\n- [public/scripts/reasoning.js](public/scripts/reasoning.js)\n- [public/scripts/secrets.js](public/scripts/secrets.js)\n- [public/scripts/slash-commands.js](public/scripts/slash-commands.js)\n- [public/scripts/st-context.js](public/scripts/st-context.js)\n- [public/scripts/tool-calling.js](public/scripts/tool-calling.js)\n- [src/constants.js](src/constants.js)\n- [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js](src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js)\n- [src/endpoints/openai.js](src/endpoints/openai.js)\n- [src/endpoints/secrets.js](src/endpoints/secrets.js)\n- [src/prompt-converters.js](src/prompt-converters.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Overview\n\nTool calling (also known as function calling) allows Large Language Models to invoke external functions during chat generation. When enabled, the model can request execution of predefined tools by name with specific parameters, receive the results, and incorporate them into its response. This enables capabilities such as web search, image generation, data retrieval, and custom workflow automation.\n\nThis document covers:\n- The `ToolManager` singleton that registers and manages tools\n- Tool definition schemas and registration API\n- Execution flow from model request to result injection\n- API-specific tool calling implementations (OpenAI, Claude, Google, etc.)\n- Integration with the chat generation pipeline\n\nFor information about slash commands and scripting, see [7.1](#7.1). For extension development, see [8.1](#8.1).\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Client[\"Client (public/scripts/)\"]\n        ToolManager[\"ToolManager\u003cbr/\u003e(tool-calling.js)\"]\n        ToolDefinition[\"ToolDefinition\u003cbr/\u003eclass\"]\n        Extensions[\"Extensions\u003cbr/\u003e(SD, Memory, etc.)\"]\n        OpenAIJS[\"openai.js\u003cbr/\u003eChat Generation\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Server[\"Server (src/)\"]\n        ChatCompletions[\"chat-completions.js\u003cbr/\u003e/api/backends/chat-completions\"]\n        PromptConverters[\"prompt-converters.js\u003cbr/\u003eFormat conversion\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph External[\"External LLM APIs\"]\n        OpenAI[\"OpenAI API\u003cbr/\u003etools array\"]\n        Claude[\"Claude API\u003cbr/\u003etools + tool_use\"]\n        Google[\"Google API\u003cbr/\u003efunction_declarations\"]\n    end\n    \n    Extensions --\u003e|registerFunctionTool| ToolManager\n    ToolManager --\u003e|stores| ToolDefinition\n    OpenAIJS --\u003e|getToolArray| ToolManager\n    OpenAIJS --\u003e|POST /api/backends/chat-completions| ChatCompletions\n    ChatCompletions --\u003e|convertToProviderFormat| PromptConverters\n    PromptConverters --\u003e|OpenAI format| OpenAI\n    PromptConverters --\u003e|Claude format| Claude\n    PromptConverters --\u003e|Gemini format| Google\n    OpenAI --\u003e|tool_calls response| ChatCompletions\n    Claude --\u003e|tool_use response| ChatCompletions\n    Google --\u003e|functionCall response| ChatCompletions\n    ChatCompletions --\u003e|tool_calls| OpenAIJS\n    OpenAIJS --\u003e|invokeFunctionTools| ToolManager\n    ToolManager --\u003e|execute| ToolDefinition\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:1-1248](), [public/scripts/openai.js:79-79](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:1-1441]()\n\n## Tool Manager Singleton\n\nThe `ToolManager` class is a singleton that manages all registered tools. It provides the central API for tool registration, retrieval, and execution.\n\n### Key Methods\n\n| Method | Purpose | Returns |\n|--------|---------|---------|\n| `registerFunctionTool()` | Register a new function tool | `void` |\n| `unregisterFunctionTool()` | Remove a registered tool | `void` |\n| `getFunctionTool()` | Get a tool by name | `ToolRegistration \\| undefined` |\n| `getToolArray()` | Get all tools in OpenAI format | `ToolDefinitionOpenAI[]` |\n| `invokeFunctionTools()` | Execute tool calls from LLM | `Promise\u003cToolInvocationResult\u003e` |\n| `isToolCallingSupported()` | Check if current API supports tools | `boolean` |\n\n### Singleton Access\n\n```javascript\nimport { ToolManager } from './tool-calling.js';\n\n// Access the singleton instance\nconst toolManager = ToolManager.instance;\n\n// Or use static methods\nToolManager.registerFunctionTool(...);\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:114-1248]()\n\n## Tool Registration\n\nTools are registered via the `registerFunctionTool()` static method. Each tool requires a unique name, description, parameter schema, and action function.\n\n### Registration Interface\n\n```typescript\ntype ToolRegistration = {\n    name: string;                    // Unique tool identifier\n    displayName: string;             // Human-readable name\n    description: string;             // What the tool does\n    parameters: object;              // JSON Schema for parameters\n    action: (args: object) =\u003e any;   // Execution function\n    formatMessage?: (invocation: ToolInvocation) =\u003e string;\n    shouldRegister?: () =\u003e boolean;  // Conditional registration\n    stealth?: boolean;               // Hide from chat output\n}\n```\n\n### Example Registration\n\n```javascript\nToolManager.registerFunctionTool({\n    name: 'get_current_weather',\n    displayName: 'Weather Lookup',\n    description: 'Get the current weather for a location',\n    parameters: {\n        type: 'object',\n        properties: {\n            location: {\n                type: 'string',\n                description: 'City name, e.g. San Francisco'\n            },\n            unit: {\n                type: 'string',\n                enum: ['celsius', 'fahrenheit']\n            }\n        },\n        required: ['location']\n    },\n    action: async (args) =\u003e {\n        const weather = await fetchWeather(args.location, args.unit);\n        return JSON.stringify(weather);\n    }\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:733-878]()\n\n## Tool Definition Format\n\nTools are converted to API-specific formats using the `ToolDefinition` class. The base format follows OpenAI's function calling specification.\n\n### OpenAI Format\n\n```javascript\n{\n    type: 'function',\n    function: {\n        name: 'tool_name',\n        description: 'Tool description',\n        parameters: {\n            type: 'object',\n            properties: { /* ... */ },\n            required: [ /* ... */ ]\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Claude Format Conversion\n\nClaude requires tools in a different structure with `input_schema` instead of `parameters`:\n\n```javascript\n{\n    name: 'tool_name',\n    description: 'Tool description',\n    input_schema: {\n        type: 'object',\n        properties: { /* ... */ },\n        required: [ /* ... */ ]\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Google Format Conversion\n\nGoogle AI uses `function_declarations`:\n\n```javascript\n{\n    function_declarations: [\n        {\n            name: 'tool_name',\n            description: 'Tool description',\n            parameters: { /* ... */ }\n        }\n    ]\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:113-229](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:262-279](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:499-522]()\n\n## Tool Execution Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant UI as Chat UI\n    participant OpenAI as openai.js\n    participant Server as chat-completions.js\n    participant LLM as External LLM\n    participant TM as ToolManager\n    participant Tool as ToolDefinition\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eUI: Send message\n    UI-\u003e\u003eOpenAI: Generate()\n    OpenAI-\u003e\u003eTM: getToolArray()\n    TM--\u003e\u003eOpenAI: tools[]\n    OpenAI-\u003e\u003eServer: POST /api/backends/chat-completions\n    Note over Server: tools: [...]\u003cbr/\u003etool_choice: \"auto\"\n    Server-\u003e\u003eLLM: Convert to provider format\n    LLM--\u003e\u003eServer: Response with tool_calls\n    Server--\u003e\u003eOpenAI: tool_calls in message\n    \n    OpenAI-\u003e\u003eTM: invokeFunctionTools(tool_calls)\n    \n    loop For each tool_call\n        TM-\u003e\u003eTool: execute(parameters)\n        Tool--\u003e\u003eTM: result\n        TM-\u003e\u003eTM: Store invocation in message.extra\n    end\n    \n    TM--\u003e\u003eOpenAI: ToolInvocationResult\n    \n    alt If not stealth\n        OpenAI-\u003e\u003eUI: Display tool call message\n        OpenAI-\u003e\u003eServer: Continue generation with tool results\n        Server-\u003e\u003eLLM: Messages + tool results\n        LLM--\u003e\u003eServer: Final response\n        Server--\u003e\u003eOpenAI: Final message\n        OpenAI-\u003e\u003eUI: Display final response\n    else If stealth\n        Note over OpenAI,UI: No message shown, no follow-up\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:889-891](), [public/scripts/openai.js:1255-1378](), [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:879-1051]()\n\n## API Support Detection\n\nNot all APIs support tool calling. The `ToolManager.isToolCallingSupported()` method checks compatibility based on the current API and settings.\n\n### Supported APIs\n\n| API Source | Support | Notes |\n|------------|---------|-------|\n| OpenAI | ✅ | Full support, native format |\n| Claude (Anthropic) | ✅ | Requires conversion to `tool_use` format |\n| Google AI Studio | ✅ | Uses `function_declarations` |\n| Google Vertex AI | ✅ | Uses `function_declarations` |\n| OpenRouter | ✅ | Proxies to underlying models |\n| Groq | ✅ | OpenAI-compatible |\n| MistralAI | ✅ | OpenAI-compatible |\n| Cohere | ✅ | Custom format |\n| Custom (OpenAI-compatible) | ✅ | If `function_calling` enabled |\n| Text Completion APIs | ❌ | No tool calling support |\n\n### Support Check Implementation\n\n```javascript\nstatic isToolCallingSupported() {\n    if (main_api !== 'openai') {\n        return false;\n    }\n    \n    if (!oai_settings.function_calling) {\n        return false;\n    }\n    \n    const supportedSources = [\n        chat_completion_sources.OPENAI,\n        chat_completion_sources.CLAUDE,\n        chat_completion_sources.MAKERSUITE,\n        chat_completion_sources.VERTEXAI,\n        chat_completion_sources.OPENROUTER,\n        chat_completion_sources.MISTRALAI,\n        chat_completion_sources.CUSTOM,\n        chat_completion_sources.GROQ,\n        chat_completion_sources.COHERE,\n        // ... more sources\n    ];\n    \n    return supportedSources.includes(oai_settings.chat_completion_source);\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:231-299]()\n\n## Tool Invocation Storage\n\nTool invocations are stored in the chat message's `extra` field as `tool_invocations` array. Each invocation contains the call details and result.\n\n### ToolInvocation Structure\n\n```typescript\ntype ToolInvocation = {\n    id: string;           // Unique identifier (from LLM)\n    displayName: string;  // Human-readable tool name\n    name: string;         // Actual tool name\n    parameters: string;   // JSON string of parameters\n    result: string;       // JSON string of result\n    signature?: string;   // Optional reasoning signature\n}\n```\n\n### Storage in Chat Message\n\n```javascript\nchat[messageIndex] = {\n    mes: 'Assistant message text',\n    extra: {\n        tool_invocations: [\n            {\n                id: 'call_abc123',\n                displayName: 'Weather Lookup',\n                name: 'get_current_weather',\n                parameters: '{\"location\":\"Tokyo\",\"unit\":\"celsius\"}',\n                result: '{\"temp\":22,\"condition\":\"sunny\"}'\n            }\n        ]\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Prompt Context Inclusion\n\nWhen messages with tool invocations are included in subsequent prompts, they are converted to the API-specific format:\n\n**OpenAI:** Messages with `role: \"assistant\"` and `tool_calls` array, followed by `role: \"tool\"` messages with results.\n\n**Claude:** Assistant messages with `tool_use` content blocks, followed by user messages with `tool_result` blocks.\n\n**Google:** Assistant messages with `functionCall` parts, followed by user messages with `functionResponse` parts.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:576-593](), [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:879-1051]()\n\n## Integration with Chat Generation\n\nTool calling integrates into the standard chat generation pipeline. The process is controlled by the `function_calling` setting in OpenAI settings.\n\n### Settings Control\n\n```javascript\n// In oai_settings\n{\n    function_calling: true,  // Enable/disable tool calling\n    tool_choice: 'auto',     // 'auto' | 'none' | 'required' | {name: string}\n}\n```\n\n### Generation Pipeline Integration\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    A[\"setupChatCompletionPromptManager\"] --\u003e B{function_calling enabled?}\n    B --\u003e|No| C[\"Generate without tools\"]\n    B --\u003e|Yes| D[\"ToolManager.getToolArray()\"]\n    D --\u003e E[\"Add tools to request body\"]\n    E --\u003e F[\"Send to API\"]\n    F --\u003e G{Response has tool_calls?}\n    G --\u003e|No| H[\"Normal message handling\"]\n    G --\u003e|Yes| I[\"ToolManager.invokeFunctionTools()\"]\n    I --\u003e J{stealth tools?}\n    J --\u003e|Yes| K[\"Silent execution, end\"]\n    J --\u003e|No| L[\"Add tool message to chat\"]\n    L --\u003e M[\"Trigger follow-up generation\"]\n    M --\u003e F\n```\n\n### Message Flow with Tools\n\n1. **Initial Request:** User message sent with `tools` array attached\n2. **Tool Call Response:** LLM responds with `tool_calls` in assistant message\n3. **Tool Execution:** `invokeFunctionTools()` executes each tool\n4. **Result Messages:** Tool results added as separate messages (format varies by API)\n5. **Follow-up Generation:** New generation request includes tool results\n6. **Final Response:** LLM generates final answer using tool results\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:1255-1378](), [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:879-1051]()\n\n## Provider-Specific Conversion\n\nDifferent LLM providers require different tool call formats. The server-side `prompt-converters.js` handles these conversions.\n\n### OpenAI → Claude Conversion\n\n```javascript\n// Convert OpenAI tool_calls to Claude tool_use\nif (message.role === 'assistant' \u0026\u0026 message.tool_calls) {\n    message.content = message.tool_calls.map((tc) =\u003e ({\n        type: 'tool_use',\n        id: tc.id,\n        name: tc.function.name,\n        input: JSON.parse(tc.function.arguments),\n    }));\n}\n\n// Convert OpenAI tool results to Claude tool_result\nif (message.role === 'tool') {\n    message.role = 'user';\n    message.content = [{\n        type: 'tool_result',\n        tool_use_id: message.tool_call_id,\n        content: message.content,\n    }];\n}\n```\n\n### OpenAI → Google Conversion\n\n```javascript\n// Convert tool_calls to functionCall\nif (message.tool_calls) {\n    message.parts = message.tool_calls.map(tc =\u003e ({\n        functionCall: {\n            name: tc.function.name,\n            args: JSON.parse(tc.function.arguments)\n        }\n    }));\n}\n\n// Convert tool results to functionResponse\nif (message.role === 'tool') {\n    message.parts = [{\n        functionResponse: {\n            name: message.tool_call_id,\n            response: { result: message.content }\n        }\n    }];\n}\n```\n\n### Schema Flattening\n\nSome APIs (like Claude) don't support nested `$ref` or `$defs` in JSON schemas. The `flattenSchema()` function resolves these:\n\n```javascript\nfunction flattenSchema(schema, source) {\n    // Resolve $ref and $defs\n    // Inline definitions\n    // Remove unsupported keywords\n    return flattenedSchema;\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/prompt-converters.js:234-314](), [src/prompt-converters.js:726-828](), [src/util.js:579-689]()\n\n## Built-in Tool Registrations\n\nSeveral extensions register tools automatically when tool calling is enabled:\n\n### Image Generation (Stable Diffusion Extension)\n\nRegisters `generate_image` tool when SD extension is active and tool calling is enabled.\n\n```javascript\nToolManager.registerFunctionTool({\n    name: 'generate_image',\n    displayName: 'Image Generation',\n    description: 'Generate an image based on a text prompt',\n    parameters: {\n        type: 'object',\n        properties: {\n            prompt: { type: 'string' }\n        },\n        required: ['prompt']\n    },\n    action: async (args) =\u003e {\n        const result = await generatePicture('sd', args.prompt, null);\n        return JSON.stringify({ success: true, url: result });\n    }\n});\n```\n\n### Tool Registration Hooks\n\nExtensions listen for `OAI_SETTINGS_UPDATED` event to register/unregister tools based on settings:\n\n```javascript\neventSource.on(event_types.OAI_SETTINGS_UPDATED, () =\u003e {\n    if (ToolManager.isToolCallingSupported()) {\n        registerMyTool();\n    } else {\n        unregisterMyTool();\n    }\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js]() (referenced but not in provided files)\n\n## Stealth Tools\n\nStealth tools execute silently without showing results in chat or triggering follow-up generation. Useful for logging, analytics, or state updates.\n\n### Stealth Tool Registration\n\n```javascript\nToolManager.registerFunctionTool({\n    name: 'log_event',\n    displayName: 'Event Logger',\n    description: 'Log an event for analytics',\n    parameters: {\n        type: 'object',\n        properties: {\n            event_type: { type: 'string' },\n            data: { type: 'object' }\n        }\n    },\n    stealth: true,  // Mark as stealth\n    action: async (args) =\u003e {\n        await sendAnalytics(args.event_type, args.data);\n        return JSON.stringify({ logged: true });\n    }\n});\n```\n\n### Stealth Execution Behavior\n\nWhen a stealth tool is invoked:\n1. Tool executes normally\n2. Result is stored in message `extra.tool_invocations`\n3. **No tool call message is added to chat**\n4. **No follow-up generation occurs**\n5. Generation ends silently\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:1006-1026]()\n\n## Tool Call Message Formatting\n\nTool invocations can have custom message formatting via `formatMessage` callback.\n\n### Default Formatting\n\n```javascript\nfunction defaultFormatMessage(invocation) {\n    const resultObj = tryParse(invocation.result);\n    const result = resultObj?.result ?? resultObj?.return ?? invocation.result;\n    \n    return `[Invoked tool: **${invocation.displayName}**]\n**Parameters:**\n\\`\\`\\`json\n${invocation.parameters}\n\\`\\`\\`\n**Result:**\n\\`\\`\\`json\n${stringify(result)}\n\\`\\`\\``;\n}\n```\n\n### Custom Formatting\n\n```javascript\nToolManager.registerFunctionTool({\n    name: 'weather_lookup',\n    // ... other properties\n    formatMessage: (invocation) =\u003e {\n        const params = JSON.parse(invocation.parameters);\n        const result = JSON.parse(invocation.result);\n        return `Weather in ${params.location}: ${result.temp}°${params.unit}, ${result.condition}`;\n    }\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:301-322](), [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:1028-1051]()\n\n## Error Handling\n\nTool execution errors are captured and returned in the `ToolInvocationResult`:\n\n```typescript\ntype ToolInvocationResult = {\n    invocations: ToolInvocation[];  // Successful invocations\n    errors: Error[];                 // Errors that occurred\n    stealthCalls: string[];          // Names of stealth tools invoked\n}\n```\n\n### Error Recovery\n\n```javascript\nasync invokeFunctionTools(toolCalls) {\n    const invocations = [];\n    const errors = [];\n    \n    for (const toolCall of toolCalls) {\n        try {\n            const result = await this.executeTool(toolCall);\n            invocations.push(result);\n        } catch (error) {\n            console.error('Tool execution failed:', error);\n            errors.push(error);\n            // Still add a failed invocation record\n            invocations.push({\n                id: toolCall.id,\n                name: toolCall.function.name,\n                result: JSON.stringify({ error: error.message })\n            });\n        }\n    }\n    \n    return { invocations, errors, stealthCalls: [] };\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:879-1051]()\n\n## Slash Command Integration\n\nTool calling can be triggered via slash commands for testing:\n\n```stscript\n/tool-call name=\"get_weather\" args={\"location\":\"Tokyo\"}\n```\n\nThis allows manual invocation of registered tools without LLM involvement.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:1053-1248]() (implementation details for slash commands)\n\n## Configuration UI\n\nTool calling is controlled in the OpenAI API settings panel:\n\n- **Function Calling Checkbox** (`#openai_function_calling`): Master toggle at [public/scripts/openai.js:354]()\n- **Tool Choice Dropdown**: Controls when tools are invoked ('auto', 'none', 'required')\n- **Settings saved to** `oai_settings.function_calling` and `oai_settings.tool_choice`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:354](), [public/scripts/openai.js:459]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"26:T4f85,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Streaming and Response
