[public/scripts/slash-commands.js](public/scripts/slash-commands.js)\n- [public/scripts/sse-stream.js](public/scripts/sse-stream.js)\n- [public/scripts/st-context.js](public/scripts/st-context.js)\n- [public/scripts/tags.js](public/scripts/tags.js)\n- [public/scripts/tool-calling.js](public/scripts/tool-calling.js)\n- [src/constants.js](src/constants.js)\n- [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js](src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js)\n- [src/endpoints/openai.js](src/endpoints/openai.js)\n- [src/endpoints/secrets.js](src/endpoints/secrets.js)\n- [src/prompt-converters.js](src/prompt-converters.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document explains how SillyTavern handles streaming responses from AI providers and processes them for display. It covers the Server-Sent Events (SSE) infrastructure, response parsing, smooth streaming animation, and special response types like reasoning content and tool calls.\n\nFor information about prompt assembly before generation, see [Prompt Assembly Pipeline](#3.3). For information about tokenization, see [Tokenization and Token Management](#3.4). For tool calling setup, see [Tool Calling and Function Execution](#3.5).\n\n---\n\n## Streaming Architecture Overview\n\nSillyTavern uses a client-server streaming architecture where the Node.js server proxies streaming responses from external AI providers to the browser client using Server-Sent Events (SSE).\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client as \"Client\u003cbr/\u003e(openai.js)\"\n    participant SSE as \"SSE Handler\u003cbr/\u003e(sse-stream.js)\"\n    participant Server as \"Server\u003cbr/\u003e(chat-completions.js)\"\n    participant LLM as \"External LLM API\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eServer: \"POST /api/backends/chat-completions\u003cbr/\u003e{stream: true}\"\n    Server-\u003e\u003eLLM: \"Streaming request\"\n    \n    loop \"For each chunk\"\n        LLM--\u003e\u003eServer: \"SSE chunk\u003cbr/\u003edata: {...}\"\n        Server-\u003e\u003eServer: \"forwardFetchResponse()\u003cbr/\u003eConvert format if needed\"\n        Server--\u003e\u003eSSE: \"SSE event\"\n        SSE-\u003e\u003eSSE: \"parseStreamData()\u003cbr/\u003eExtract text\"\n        SSE-\u003e\u003eClient: \"onProgress callback\"\n        Client-\u003e\u003eClient: \"Update message DOM\"\n    end\n    \n    LLM--\u003e\u003eServer: \"[DONE]\"\n    Server--\u003e\u003eSSE: \"Stream complete\"\n    SSE-\u003e\u003eClient: \"onDone callback\"\n    Client-\u003e\u003eClient: \"Finalize message\"\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:48](), [public/scripts/sse-stream.js](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:1-100]()\n\n---\n\n## Server-Sent Events Infrastructure\n\n### Client-Side SSE Setup\n\nThe `getEventSourceStream()` function in `sse-stream.js` establishes the SSE connection and manages the streaming lifecycle.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Start[\"Client calls\u003cbr/\u003esendOpenAIRequest()\"]\n    Setup[\"getEventSourceStream()\u003cbr/\u003eCreates EventSource\"]\n    Listen[\"Attach listeners:\u003cbr/\u003eonopen, onmessage, onerror\"]\n    Process[\"parseStreamData()\u003cbr/\u003eParse incoming chunks\"]\n    Progress[\"onProgress callback\u003cbr/\u003eUpdate UI\"]\n    Done[\"onDone callback\u003cbr/\u003eFinalize response\"]\n    \n    Start --\u003e Setup\n    Setup --\u003e Listen\n    Listen --\u003e Process\n    Process --\u003e Progress\n    Process --\u003e Done\n    \n    style Setup fill:#f9f9f9\n    style Process fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\nKey components:\n- **`EventSource`** - Browser API for SSE connections\n- **`onopen`** - Fired when connection established\n- **`onmessage`** - Fired for each data chunk\n- **`onerror`** - Fired on connection errors\n- **`AbortController`** - Used to cancel streaming\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/sse-stream.js:1-300](), [public/scripts/openai.js:1800-2100]()\n\n### Server-Side SSE Proxying\n\nThe server proxies streaming responses from external APIs using `forwardFetchResponse()`.\n\n| Component | Location | Purpose |\n|-----------|----------|---------|\n| `forwardFetchResponse()` | [src/util.js]() | Pipes fetch response to Express response |\n| `sendClaudeRequest()` | [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:205-385]() | Handles Claude streaming |\n| `sendMakerSuiteRequest()` | [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:392-700]() | Handles Google streaming |\n| `sendOpenAIRequest()` | [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:702-1000]() | Handles OpenAI streaming |\n\nThe server transforms provider-specific streaming formats into a consistent SSE format for the client.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:200-1000](), [src/util.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Streaming Pipeline\n\n### Stream Initialization\n\nWhen generation starts, the client sends a request with `stream: true` and sets up streaming infrastructure.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Request[\"sendOpenAIRequest()\u003cbr/\u003estream: true\"]\n    Controller[\"Create AbortController\"]\n    EventSource[\"getEventSourceStream()\"]\n    Handlers[\"Setup callbacks:\u003cbr/\u003eonProgress, onDone\"]\n    \n    Request --\u003e Controller\n    Controller --\u003e EventSource\n    EventSource --\u003e Handlers\n    \n    style Request fill:#f9f9f9\n    style EventSource fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\nKey functions:\n- **`sendOpenAIRequest()`** - Initiates streaming request ([public/scripts/openai.js:1800-2000]())\n- **`Generate()`** - Main generation orchestrator ([public/scripts/script.js]())\n- Socket close listener - Aborts stream when user navigates away\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:1800-2100](), [public/scripts/script.js]()\n\n### Chunk Processing\n\nThe `parseStreamData()` function extracts text from each SSE chunk based on the provider format.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Chunk[\"SSE chunk arrives\"]\n    Parse[\"parseStreamData()\"]\n    Check{Provider?}\n    OpenAI[\"Extract from\u003cbr/\u003echoices[0].delta.content\"]\n    Claude[\"Extract from\u003cbr/\u003edelta.text or content_block.text\"]\n    Google[\"Extract from\u003cbr/\u003ecandidates[0].content.parts\"]\n    Cohere[\"Extract from\u003cbr/\u003etext\"]\n    \n    Chunk --\u003e Parse\n    Parse --\u003e Check\n    Check --\u003e|OpenAI| OpenAI\n    Check --\u003e|Claude| Claude\n    Check --\u003e|Google| Google\n    Check --\u003e|Cohere| Cohere\n    \n    OpenAI --\u003e Return[\"Return text chunk\"]\n    Claude --\u003e Return\n    Google --\u003e Return\n    Cohere --\u003e Return\n    \n    style Parse fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\nProvider-specific parsing logic handles:\n- **OpenAI format** - `{\"choices\":[{\"delta\":{\"content\":\"text\"}}]}`\n- **Claude format** - `{\"type\":\"content_block_delta\",\"delta\":{\"text\":\"text\"}}`\n- **Google format** - `{\"candidates\":[{\"content\":{\"parts\":[{\"text\":\"text\"}]}}]}`\n- **Cohere format** - `{\"text\":\"text\"}`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/sse-stream.js:100-400]()\n\n### Message Accumulation\n\nThe streaming processor accumulates chunks into a complete message while updating the UI in real-time.\n\n| Component | Purpose |\n|-----------|---------|\n| `StreamingProcessor` | Manages streaming state and accumulation |\n| `generatedPromptCache` | Accumulates the generated text |\n| `messageChunk` | Current chunk being processed |\n| `onProgress()` callback | Updates message block with accumulated text |\n\nThe accumulation process:\n1. **Append chunk** to `generatedPromptCache`\n2. **Extract special content** (reasoning, tool calls)\n3. **Update DOM** with current accumulated text\n4. **Handle formatting** (markdown, code blocks, etc.)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:2000-2200](), [public/scripts/sse-stream.js:200-500]()\n\n---\n\n## Response Parsing\n\n### Provider-Specific Formats\n\nDifferent AI providers return responses in different formats. The system normalizes these into a consistent structure.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Response[\"Raw provider response\"]\n    Router{API Source}\n    \n    Router --\u003e|OpenAI| OAIParse[\"Parse OpenAI format\u003cbr/\u003echoices[0].message.content\"]\n    Router --\u003e|Claude| ClaudeParse[\"Parse Claude format\u003cbr/\u003econtent[0].text\"]\n    Router --\u003e|Google| GoogleParse[\"Parse Google format\u003cbr/\u003ecandidates[0].content.parts\"]\n    Router --\u003e|Cohere| CohereParse[\"Parse Cohere format\u003cbr/\u003etext\"]\n    \n    OAIParse --\u003e Extract[\"extractMessageFromData()\"]\n    ClaudeParse --\u003e Extract\n    GoogleParse --\u003e Extract\n    CohereParse --\u003e Extract\n    \n    Extract --\u003e Normalize[\"Normalized message object\"]\n    \n    style Router fill:#f9f9f9\n    style Extract fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\nThe `extractMessageFromData()` function handles format conversion:\n\n**OpenAI-compatible APIs:**\n```javascript\n// public/scripts/script.js\nmessage = data?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content || ''\n```\n\n**Claude API:**\n```javascript\n// Wrapped to OpenAI format by server\nmessage = data?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content || ''\n// Original content available in data.content\n```\n\n**Google API:**\n```javascript\n// Server converts to OpenAI-compatible format\nmessage = data?.choices?.[0]?.message?.content || ''\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/script.js:extractMessageFromData](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:200-1500]()\n\n### Special Content Extraction\n\nThe system extracts special content types beyond plain text:\n\n| Content Type | Extraction Function | Location |\n|--------------|-------------------|----------|\n| Reasoning/Thinking | `extractReasoningFromData()` | [public/scripts/reasoning.js:92-145]() |\n| Reasoning Signature | `extractReasoningSignatureFromData()` | [public/scripts/reasoning.js:156-192]() |\n| Tool Calls | Extracted from `tool_calls` field | [public/scripts/openai.js:2300-2500]() |\n| Logprobs | `saveLogprobsForActiveMessage()` | [public/scripts/logprobs.js]() |\n\n**Reasoning Content Extraction:**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Data[\"Response data\"]\n    Check{Has reasoning?}\n    Extract[\"extractReasoningFromData()\"]\n    DeepSeek[\"reasoning_content field\"]\n    Claude[\"thinking content block\"]\n    Google[\"thought parts\"]\n    Store[\"Store in message.extra\"]\n    \n    Data --\u003e Check\n    Check --\u003e|Yes| Extract\n    Extract --\u003e DeepSeek\n    Extract --\u003e Claude\n    Extract --\u003e Google\n    DeepSeek --\u003e Store\n    Claude --\u003e Store\n    Google --\u003e Store\n    \n    style Extract fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\nThe reasoning content is stored separately from the main message text and can be displayed in expandable UI blocks.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/reasoning.js:92-192](), [public/scripts/openai.js:2200-2400]()\n\n---\n\n## Smooth Streaming\n\nSmooth streaming provides character-by-character animation for a more natural reading experience.\n\n### Animation System\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Enable[\"power_user.smooth_streaming = true\"]\n    Stream[\"Text chunk arrives\"]\n    Check{Smooth enabled?}\n    Queue[\"Add to animation queue\"]\n    Animate[\"Animate character-by-character\"]\n    Display[\"Update DOM incrementally\"]\n    FadeIn[\"applyStreamFadeIn()\u003cbr/\u003eFade animation\"]\n    \n    Enable --\u003e Stream\n    Stream --\u003e Check\n    Check --\u003e|Yes| Queue\n    Check --\u003e|No| DirectDisplay[\"Display immediately\"]\n    Queue --\u003e Animate\n    Animate --\u003e Display\n    Display --\u003e FadeIn\n    \n    style Queue fill:#f9f9f9\n    style Animate fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\nConfiguration options:\n\n| Setting | Default | Purpose |\n|---------|---------|---------|\n| `smooth_streaming` | `false` | Enable smooth streaming |\n| `smooth_streaming_speed` | `30` | Characters per second (10-100) |\n\n**Implementation details:**\n\nThe smooth streaming system uses:\n1. **Character queue** - Buffers incoming text\n2. **Animation timer** - Controls display rate\n3. **Fade-in effect** - Applied to newly visible text via `applyStreamFadeIn()`\n4. **State management** - Tracks animation progress\n\n```javascript\n// Simplified concept (not actual code)\nfunction smoothStreamAnimation(text, speed) {\n    const chars = text.split('');\n    const delay = 1000 / speed; // ms per character\n    \n    for (const char of chars) {\n        setTimeout(() =\u003e {\n            appendChar(char);\n            applyStreamFadeIn();\n        }, delay);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:2100-2300](), [public/scripts/util/stream-fadein.js]()\n\n### Performance Optimization\n\nFor long responses, smooth streaming includes optimizations:\n\n- **Batch processing** - Groups characters into small batches\n- **Skip ahead** - Can skip animation on user interaction\n- **Memory management** - Clears processed chunks from queue\n- **Debouncing** - Limits DOM updates to prevent lag\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:2100-2300](), [public/scripts/sse-stream.js:300-500]()\n\n---\n\n## Special Response Types\n\n### Reasoning/Thinking Content\n\nThe `ReasoningHandler` class manages the display and processing of model reasoning.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Generate[\"Message generation starts\"]\n    Extract[\"extractReasoningFromData()\"]\n    Check{Has reasoning?}\n    Handler[\"ReasoningHandler\"]\n    State[\"Set ReasoningState\"]\n    Thinking[\"State = THINKING\u003cbr/\u003eShow spinner\"]\n    Done[\"State = DONE\u003cbr/\u003eShow content\"]\n    Hidden[\"State = HIDDEN\u003cbr/\u003eHide completely\"]\n    Display[\"updateReasoningUI()\"]\n    \n    Generate --\u003e Extract\n    Extract --\u003e Check\n    Check --\u003e|Yes| Handler\n    Check --\u003e|No| NoReasoning[\"Normal message\"]\n    Handler --\u003e State\n    State --\u003e Thinking\n    State --\u003e Done\n    State --\u003e Hidden\n    Thinking --\u003e Display\n    Done --\u003e Display\n    Hidden --\u003e Display\n    \n    style Handler fill:#f9f9f9\n    style Display fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\n**Reasoning states:**\n\n| State | Symbol | Description |\n|-------|--------|-------------|\n| None | `ReasoningState.None` | No reasoning content |\n| Thinking | `ReasoningState.Thinking` | Reasoning in progress (streaming) |\n| Done | `ReasoningState.Done` | Reasoning complete |\n| Hidden | `ReasoningState.Hidden` | Reasoning available but hidden |\n\n**Reasoning types:**\n\n| Type | Source |\n|------|--------|\n| `ReasoningType.Model` | Directly from model output |\n| `ReasoningType.Parsed` | Extracted via template parsing |\n| `ReasoningType.Manual` | User-added |\n| `ReasoningType.Edited` | User-modified |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/reasoning.js:230-500]()\n\n### Tool Invocations\n\nTool calls are extracted and processed during streaming.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Response[\"Response with tool_calls\"]\n    Extract[\"Extract tool_call objects\"]\n    Parse[\"Parse tool name \u0026 parameters\"]\n    Execute[\"ToolManager.invokeTool()\"]\n    Result[\"Tool execution result\"]\n    Append[\"Append to message.extra\"]\n    Display[\"Display in chat\"]\n    \n    Response --\u003e Extract\n    Extract --\u003e Parse\n    Parse --\u003e Execute\n    Execute --\u003e Result\n    Result --\u003e Append\n    Append --\u003e Display\n    \n    style Execute fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\n**Tool invocation structure:**\n\n```javascript\n{\n    id: \"call_123abc\",\n    displayName: \"Web Search\",\n    name: \"web_search\",\n    parameters: \"{\\\"query\\\":\\\"example\\\"}\",\n    result: \"Search results...\",\n    signature: \"...\" // Optional encrypted signature\n}\n```\n\nTool calls are stored in `message.extra.tool_invocations` and displayed as expandable blocks in the chat UI.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:1-200](), [public/scripts/openai.js:2300-2500]()\n\n### Multimodal Responses\n\nResponses can include images, audio, or video content.\n\n| Media Type | Extraction | Display |\n|------------|-----------|---------|\n| Images | `message.extra.media` with `type: MEDIA_TYPE.IMAGE` | Inline or gallery |\n| Audio | `message.extra.media` with `type: MEDIA_TYPE.AUDIO` | Audio player |\n| Video | `message.extra.media` with `type: MEDIA_TYPE.VIDEO` | Video player |\n\nThe media attachments are processed in parallel with text streaming and added to the message structure.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:2400-2600](), [public/script.js:appendMediaToMessage]()\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling and Recovery\n\n### Stream Interruption\n\nUsers can interrupt streaming by:\n\n1. **Stop button** - Sets `is_send_press = false`, aborts controller\n2. **Navigation** - Socket close event triggers abort\n3. **New generation** - Previous stream automatically cancelled\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Streaming[\"Response streaming\"]\n    Event{Interrupt event?}\n    Stop[\"User clicks stop\"]\n    Navigate[\"User navigates away\"]\n    NewGen[\"New generation starts\"]\n    Abort[\"controller.abort()\"]\n    Cleanup[\"Clean up state\"]\n    Finalize[\"Finalize partial response\"]\n    \n    Streaming --\u003e Event\n    Event --\u003e|Stop| Stop\n    Event --\u003e|Navigate| Navigate\n    Event --\u003e|New| NewGen\n    Stop --\u003e Abort\n    Navigate --\u003e Abort\n    NewGen --\u003e Abort\n    Abort --\u003e Cleanup\n    Cleanup --\u003e Finalize\n    \n    style Abort fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:1900-2000](), [public/scripts/sse-stream.js:100-200]()\n\n### Error Recovery\n\nThe system handles various error conditions:\n\n| Error Type | Handling |\n|------------|----------|\n| Network failure | Show error message, preserve partial response |\n| API error | Parse error details, display to user |\n| Timeout | Abort stream, save what was generated |\n| Invalid JSON | Continue processing, log warning |\n| Rate limit | Show specific error, suggest retry |\n\n**Error detection:**\n\n```javascript\n// SSE error handler\neventSource.onerror = (event) =\u003e {\n    if (event.error) {\n        console.error('Stream error:', event.error);\n        abortStatusCheck(); // Cleanup\n        showError(event.error);\n    }\n};\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/sse-stream.js:150-250](), [public/scripts/openai.js:2000-2100]()\n\n### Retry Logic\n\nFor transient failures, the system can retry:\n\n1. **Exponential backoff** - Increasing delay between retries\n2. **Max attempts** - Configurable retry limit\n3. **Partial preservation** - Saves successfully streamed content before retry\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:1800-2000](), [public/scripts/sse-stream.js:200-300]()\n\n---\n\n## Processing Pipeline Summary\n\nThe complete streaming and processing pipeline:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Start[\"User sends message\"]\n    Request[\"sendOpenAIRequest()\"]\n    Server[\"Server proxies to LLM\"]\n    Stream[\"SSE stream established\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Per-Chunk Processing\"\n        Chunk[\"Chunk arrives\"]\n        Parse[\"parseStreamData()\"]\n        Reasoning[\"Extract reasoning\"]\n        Tools[\"Extract tool calls\"]\n        Text[\"Extract text content\"]\n        Accumulate[\"Append to cache\"]\n        Update[\"Update DOM\"]\n    end\n    \n    Complete[\"Stream complete\"]\n    Extract[\"Extract final data\"]\n    Format[\"Apply formatting\"]\n    Save[\"Save to chat\"]\n    Display[\"Final display\"]\n    \n    Start --\u003e Request\n    Request --\u003e Server\n    Server --\u003e Stream\n    Stream --\u003e Chunk\n    Chunk --\u003e Parse\n    Parse --\u003e Reasoning\n    Parse --\u003e Tools\n    Parse --\u003e Text\n    Text --\u003e Accumulate\n    Accumulate --\u003e Update\n    Update --\u003e Chunk\n    Stream --\u003e Complete\n    Complete --\u003e Extract\n    Extract --\u003e Format\n    Format --\u003e Save\n    Save --\u003e Display\n    \n    style Parse fill:#f9f9f9\n    style Update fill:#f9f9f9\n    style Format fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\n**Key processing stages:**\n\n1. **Request initiation** - Setup streaming infrastructure\n2. **Chunk reception** - Receive SSE events from server\n3. **Data parsing** - Extract content from provider format\n4. **Special handling** - Process reasoning, tools, media\n5. **Accumulation** - Build complete response\n6. **UI updates** - Display progressively or smoothly\n7. **Finalization** - Apply formatting, save to database\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:1800-2600](), [public/scripts/sse-stream.js](), [src/prompt-converters.js](src/prompt-converters.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document covers the preset and sampler management system in SillyTavern. Presets store collections of generation parameters (samplers) that control how language models generate text. The system supports multiple API backends, each with their own sampler configurations, and provides unified management through the `PresetManager` class.\n\nFor information about prompt construction and injection, see [Prompt Management and Construction](#3.3). For context-specific settings, see [Instruct Mode and Context Formatting](#3.3.2).\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nThe preset system manages collections of sampler parameters that control text generation behavior. Each preset contains settings like temperature, repetition penalty, top-p, and other parameters that influence the randomness and creativity of model outputs.\n\n### Preset Types\n\nSillyTavern supports multiple preset categories:\n\n| Preset Type | Storage Location | API Scope |\n|-------------|-----------------|-----------|\n| **Text Completion** | `textgenerationwebui_presets` | Text Generation backends |\n| **KoboldAI** | `koboldai_settings` | KoboldAI, Horde |\n| **NovelAI** | `novelai_settings` | NovelAI |\n| **OpenAI** | `openai_settings` | Chat Completion APIs |\n| **Context Template** | `context_presets` | All APIs |\n| **Instruct Template** | `instruct_presets` | All APIs |\n| **System Prompt** | `system_prompts` | Chat Completion APIs |\n| **Reasoning Template** | `reasoning_templates` | All APIs |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/preset-manager.js:44-596]()\n\n---\n\n## Sampler Parameters\n\nSamplers are algorithms that control how the model selects tokens during generation. Each parameter influences different aspects of the output.\n\n### Common Sampler Parameters\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Temperature Family\"\n        temp[\"Temperature\u003cbr/\u003e(temp)\"]\n        dynatemp[\"Dynamic Temperature\u003cbr/\u003e(dynatemp)\"]\n        mintemp[\"Min Temperature\u003cbr/\u003e(min_temp)\"]\n        maxtemp[\"Max Temperature\u003cbr/\u003e(max_temp)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Probability Filters\"\n        topp[\"Top P\u003cbr/\u003e(top_p)\"]\n        minp[\"Min P\u003cbr/\u003e(min_p)\"]\n        topk[\"Top K\u003cbr/\u003e(top_k)\"]\n        topa[\"Top A\u003cbr/\u003e(top_a)\"]\n        typicalp[\"Typical P\u003cbr/\u003e(typical_p)\"]\n        tfs[\"Tail Free Sampling\u003cbr/\u003e(tfs)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Repetition Control\"\n        reppen[\"Repetition Penalty\u003cbr/\u003e(rep_pen)\"]\n        reprange[\"Rep Pen Range\u003cbr/\u003e(rep_pen_range)\"]\n        freqpen[\"Frequency Penalty\u003cbr/\u003e(freq_pen)\"]\n        prespen[\"Presence Penalty\u003cbr/\u003e(presence_pen)\"]\n        dry[\"DRY Sampling\u003cbr/\u003e(dry_multiplier)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Advanced Samplers\"\n        mirostat[\"Mirostat\u003cbr/\u003e(mirostat_mode)\"]\n        xtc[\"XTC\u003cbr/\u003e(xtc_probability)\"]\n        nsigma[\"N-Sigma\u003cbr/\u003e(nsigma)\"]\n    end\n    \n    temp --\u003e topp\n    topp --\u003e minp\n    minp --\u003e reppen\n```\n\n### Parameter Definitions\n\n| Parameter | Range | Default | Description |\n|-----------|-------|---------|-------------|\n| `temp` | 0.0-5.0 | 0.7-1.0 | Higher = more random, Lower = more deterministic |\n| `top_p` | 0.0-1.0 | 0.5-1.0 | Nucleus sampling: cumulative probability threshold |\n| `min_p` | 0.0-1.0 | 0.0 | Minimum probability relative to max token probability |\n| `top_k` | 0-100+ | 0-40 | Limits selection to K most likely tokens (0 = disabled) |\n| `top_a` | 0.0-1.0 | 0.0 | Removes tokens below adaptive threshold |\n| `typical_p` | 0.0-1.0 | 1.0 | Locally typical sampling |\n| `tfs` | 0.0-1.0 | 1.0 | Tail free sampling (1.0 = disabled) |\n| `rep_pen` | 1.0-1.5 | 1.0-1.2 | Penalty for repeated tokens |\n| `rep_pen_range` | 0-4096 | 0-2048 | How far back to check for repetitions |\n| `freq_pen` | 0.0-2.0 | 0.0 | OpenAI-style frequency penalty |\n| `presence_pen` | 0.0-2.0 | 0.0 | OpenAI-style presence penalty |\n| `dry_multiplier` | 0.0-5.0 | 0.0 | DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) penalty strength |\n| `mirostat_mode` | 0-2 | 0 | Adaptive sampling mode (0 = disabled) |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:143-236](), [public/scripts/nai-settings.js:36-62](), [public/scripts/kai-settings.js:28-50]()\n\n---\n\n## Preset Manager System\n\nThe `PresetManager` class provides a unified interface for managing presets across all API types.\n\n### PresetManager Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"PresetManager Class\"\n        pm[\"PresetManager\"]\n        select[\"select element\"]\n        apiId[\"apiId: string\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Core Methods\"\n        getAllPresets[\"getAllPresets()\"]\n        findPreset[\"findPreset(name)\"]\n        selectPreset[\"selectPreset(value)\"]\n        getSelectedPreset[\"getSelectedPreset()\"]\n        savePreset[\"savePreset(name, settings)\"]\n        updatePreset[\"updatePreset()\"]\n        deletePreset[\"deletePreset(name)\"]\n        renamePreset[\"renamePreset(newName)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Preset Operations\"\n        getPresetList[\"getPresetList(api)\"]\n        getPresetSettings[\"getPresetSettings(name)\"]\n        updateList[\"updateList(name, preset)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Import/Export\"\n        performMasterImport[\"performMasterImport(data)\"]\n        performMasterExport[\"performMasterExport()\"]\n    end\n    \n    pm --\u003e getAllPresets\n    pm --\u003e findPreset\n    pm --\u003e selectPreset\n    pm --\u003e savePreset\n    pm --\u003e updatePreset\n    pm --\u003e deletePreset\n    \n    savePreset --\u003e getPresetSettings\n    savePreset --\u003e updateList\n    updatePreset --\u003e getPresetSettings\n    \n    getPresetSettings --\u003e getPresetList\n```\n\n### Preset Storage Structure\n\nText completion presets are stored as JSON files on the server and referenced by name:\n\n```javascript\n// textgenerationwebui_settings object\n{\n  preset: \"Default\",              // Selected preset name\n  temp: 0.7,\n  top_p: 0.5,\n  top_k: 40,\n  rep_pen: 1.2,\n  // ... other sampler settings\n}\n\n// textgenerationwebui_presets array\n[\n  { /* preset 0 settings */ },\n  { /* preset 1 settings */ },\n  // ...\n]\n\n// textgenerationwebui_preset_names array\n[\"Default\", \"Creative\", \"Precise\", ...]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/preset-manager.js:111-637](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:143-236]()\n\n---\n\n## CRUD Operations\n\n### Creating and Saving Presets\n\nThe `savePreset` method handles both creating new presets and updating existing ones:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant UI as User Interface\n    participant PM as PresetManager\n    participant Server as /api/presets/save\n    participant FS as File System\n    \n    UI-\u003e\u003ePM: savePreset(name, settings)\n    PM-\u003e\u003ePM: getPresetSettings(name)\n    PM-\u003e\u003eServer: POST preset data\n    Server-\u003e\u003eFS: Write JSON file\n    FS--\u003e\u003eServer: Success\n    Server--\u003e\u003ePM: {name: sanitizedName}\n    PM-\u003e\u003ePM: updateList(name, preset)\n    PM-\u003e\u003eUI: Update select element\n```\n\nThe preset save endpoint validates and sanitizes preset names:\n\n- Special handling for instruct templates checks for embedded system prompts\n- NovelAI presets are converted from v3 format if needed\n- File names are sanitized to prevent path traversal\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/preset-manager.js:466-498]()\n\n### Loading Presets\n\nPresets are loaded at startup and when the API type changes:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    load[\"loadTextGenSettings()\"]\n    convert[\"convertPresets(data)\"]\n    apply[\"selectPreset(name)\"]\n    update[\"setSettingByName()\"]\n    save[\"setGenerationParamsFromPreset()\"]\n    \n    load --\u003e convert\n    convert --\u003e apply\n    apply --\u003e update\n    apply --\u003e save\n```\n\nThe loading process:\n1. Fetches preset data from server via `/api/settings` on startup\n2. Parses JSON strings in the presets array\n3. Populates the preset dropdown (`#settings_preset_textgenerationwebui`)\n4. Applies the selected preset to current settings\n5. Updates UI sliders and controls\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:551-607]()\n\n### Updating Presets\n\nThe `updatePreset` method saves current settings to the selected preset:\n\n```javascript\n// Called when user clicks update button\nasync updatePreset(option = { skipUpdate: false }) {\n    const selected = $(this.select).find('option:selected');\n    \n    if (selected.val() == 'gui') {\n        toastr.info('Cannot update GUI preset');\n        return;\n    }\n    \n    const name = selected.text();\n    await this.savePreset(name, null, option);\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/preset-manager.js:424-438]()\n\n### Deleting Presets\n\nDeletion removes the preset from both the UI and server storage:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant PM as PresetManager\n    participant UI as Select Element\n    participant Server as /api/presets/delete\n    \n    PM-\u003e\u003eUI: Find and remove option\n    PM-\u003e\u003ePM: Update preset arrays\n    PM-\u003e\u003eUI: Select first remaining preset\n    PM-\u003e\u003eServer: POST delete request\n    Server--\u003e\u003ePM: Success\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/preset-manager.js:781-819]()\n\n---\n\n## API-Specific Implementations\n\nEach API backend has its own sampler configuration and preset management:\n\n### Text Generation WebUI (Ooba, TabbyAPI, etc.)\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"textgen_settings object\"\n        ts_preset[\"preset: string\"]\n        ts_type[\"type: textgen_types\"]\n        ts_temp[\"temp: 0.7\"]\n        ts_topp[\"top_p: 0.5\"]\n        ts_topk[\"top_k: 40\"]\n        ts_sampler[\"sampler_priority: array\"]\n        ts_more[\"... 50+ parameters\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Backend Types\"\n        ooba[\"OOBA\"]\n        mancer[\"MANCER\"]\n        vllm[\"VLLM\"]\n        aphrodite[\"APHRODITE\"]\n        tabby[\"TABBY\"]\n        koboldcpp[\"KOBOLDCPP\"]\n        llamacpp[\"LLAMACPP\"]\n        ollama[\"OLLAMA\"]\n        openrouter[\"OPENROUTER\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Sampler Orders\"\n        ooba_order[\"OOBA_DEFAULT_ORDER\"]\n        llama_order[\"LLAMACPP_DEFAULT_ORDER\"]\n        aphro_order[\"APHRODITE_DEFAULT_ORDER\"]\n    end\n    \n    ts_type --\u003e ooba\n    ts_type --\u003e mancer\n    ts_type --\u003e vllm\n    ts_type --\u003e tabby\n    \n    ooba --\u003e ooba_order\n    llamacpp --\u003e llama_order\n    aphrodite --\u003e aphro_order\n```\n\nThe text generation settings support 15+ backend types with varying sampler support. Key differences:\n\n- **Ooba/TextGenWebUI**: Full sampler suite with custom priority order\n- **vLLM/Aphrodite**: Limited samplers, Top K uses `-1` for disabled state\n- **llama.cpp**: Custom sampler order with DRY and XTC support\n- **Ollama**: Wrapped in `options` object, separate API structure\n- **OpenRouter**: Converted to chat completion format with provider filtering\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:31-236](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:917-952]()\n\n### KoboldAI Backend\n\nKoboldAI presets include sampler ordering with version-gated features:\n\n```javascript\n// kai_flags determine available features\nexport const kai_flags = {\n    can_use_tokenization: false,        // \u003e= v1.41\n    can_use_stop_sequence: false,       // \u003e= v1.2.2\n    can_use_streaming: false,           // \u003e= v1.30\n    can_use_default_badwordsids: false, // \u003e= v1.2.4\n    can_use_mirostat: false,            // \u003e= v1.35\n    can_use_grammar: false,             // \u003e= v1.44\n    can_use_min_p: false,               // \u003e= v1.48\n};\n```\n\nFeature detection is performed via version comparison during status checks:\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/kai-settings.js:52-66](), [public/scripts/kai-settings.js:379-389]()\n\n### NovelAI Backend\n\nNovelAI uses a different preset format with model-specific sampling:\n\n```javascript\nconst nai_settings = {\n    temperature: 1.5,\n    repetition_penalty: 2.25,\n    repetition_penalty_range: 2048,\n    tail_free_sampling: 0.975,\n    top_k: 10,\n    top_p: 0.75,\n    top_a: 0.08,\n    typical_p: 0.975,\n    min_p: 0,\n    model_novel: 'clio-v1',  // or 'kayra-v1', 'llama-3-erato-v1'\n    preamble: '[ Style: chat, complex, sensory, visceral ]',\n    order: [1, 5, 0, 2, 3, 4],  // Sampler order\n};\n```\n\nNovelAI v3 presets are auto-converted on import using `convertNovelPreset()`.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/nai-settings.js:36-62](), [public/scripts/nai-settings.js:118-148]()\n\n---\n\n## Sampler Ordering\n\nDifferent backends apply samplers in different orders, which significantly affects output quality.\n\n### Sampler Order Visualization\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"llama.cpp Default Order\"\n        l1[\"penalties\"] --\u003e l2[\"dry\"]\n        l2 --\u003e l3[\"top_n_sigma\"]\n        l3 --\u003e l4[\"top_k\"]\n        l4 --\u003e l5[\"typ_p\"]\n        l5 --\u003e l6[\"top_p\"]\n        l6 --\u003e l7[\"min_p\"]\n        l7 --\u003e l8[\"xtc\"]\n        l8 --\u003e l9[\"temperature\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Ooba Default Order\"\n        o1[\"rep_penalty\"] --\u003e o2[\"presence_pen\"]\n        o2 --\u003e o3[\"frequency_pen\"]\n        o3 --\u003e o4[\"dry\"]\n        o4 --\u003e o5[\"temperature\"]\n        o5 --\u003e o6[\"dyn_temp\"]\n        o6 --\u003e o7[\"top_k\"]\n        o7 --\u003e o8[\"top_p\"]\n        o8 --\u003e o9[\"typical_p\"]\n    end\n```\n\n### Configurable Sampler Orders\n\nUsers can reorder samplers via drag-and-drop UI:\n\n```javascript\n// llama.cpp sampler reordering\n$('#llamacpp_samplers_sortable').sortable({\n    delay: getSortableDelay(),\n    stop: function () {\n        const order = [];\n        $('#llamacpp_samplers_sortable').children().each(function () {\n            order.push($(this).data('name'));\n        });\n        textgenerationwebui_settings.samplers = order;\n        saveSettingsDebounced();\n    },\n});\n```\n\nDefault orders are provided for each backend:\n- **llama.cpp**: `LLAMACPP_DEFAULT_ORDER` - 10 samplers\n- **Ooba**: `OOBA_DEFAULT_ORDER` - 20 samplers  \n- **Aphrodite**: `APHRODITE_DEFAULT_ORDER` - 15 samplers\n- **KoboldCPP**: `KOBOLDCPP_ORDER` - 7 samplers (numeric IDs)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:67-116](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:841-915]()\n\n---\n\n## Auto-Selection System\n\nThe preset manager can automatically select presets based on character or group names:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Chat as Chat System\n    participant Auto as autoSelectPreset()\n    participant PM as PresetManager\n    participant Fuse as Fuzzy Search\n    \n    Chat-\u003e\u003eAuto: Character/Group changed\n    Auto-\u003e\u003ePM: getPresetManager(main_api)\n    Auto-\u003e\u003ePM: findPreset(name)\n    PM-\u003e\u003eFuse: Search preset names\n    Fuse--\u003e\u003ePM: Best match\n    PM--\u003e\u003eAuto: Preset found\n    Auto-\u003e\u003ePM: selectPreset(preset)\n    PM-\u003e\u003ePM: Apply preset settings\n```\n\nThe auto-selection algorithm:\n1. Triggered on character or group selection change\n2. Extracts name from `characters[this_chid]` or `groups[selected_group]`\n3. Uses fuzzy matching to find preset with matching name\n4. Only switches if different from current preset\n5. Logs selection to console\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/preset-manager.js:49-76]()\n\n---\n\n## Master Import/Export\n\nThe master import/export system allows bundling multiple preset types into a single file:\n\n### Master Preset Structure\n\n```javascript\n{\n  \"instruct\": {\n    \"name\": \"Custom Instruct\",\n    \"input_sequence\": \"\\n### Instruction:\",\n    \"output_sequence\": \"\\n### Response:\",\n    // ... more instruct settings\n  },\n  \"context\": {\n    \"name\": \"Custom Context\",\n    \"story_string\": \"{{description}}\",\n    // ... more context settings\n  },\n  \"preset\": {\n    \"name\": \"Custom Samplers\",\n    \"temp\": 0.8,\n    \"top_p\": 0.9,\n    // ... sampler settings\n  },\n  \"reasoning\": {\n    \"name\": \"Custom Reasoning\",\n    \"prefix\": \"\u003cthinking\u003e\",\n    \"suffix\": \"\u003c/thinking\u003e\",\n    // ...\n  },\n  \"srw\": {\n    \"value\": \"Sure, here is\",\n    \"show\": true\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### Import Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant UI as Import UI\n    participant PM as PresetManager\n    participant Validate as Validation\n    participant Save as Individual Savers\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eUI: Select JSON file\n    UI-\u003e\u003ePM: performMasterImport(data)\n    PM-\u003e\u003eValidate: Check each section\n    Validate--\u003e\u003ePM: Valid sections list\n    PM-\u003e\u003eUI: Show selection popup\n    User-\u003e\u003eUI: Confirm sections\n    UI-\u003e\u003eSave: setData() for each section\n    Save--\u003e\u003ePM: Import complete\n    PM-\u003e\u003eUser: Show success toast\n```\n\nThe import process:\n1. Validates JSON structure\n2. Detects legacy single-preset imports (instruct/context/preset only)\n3. Presents checkboxes for available sections\n4. Calls appropriate manager's `setData()` for each selected section\n5. Reports imported section names in toast notification\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/preset-manager.js:207-329]()\n\n### Export Flow\n\nMaster export collects data from all preset types:\n\n```javascript\nstatic async performMasterExport() {\n    const sectionNames = {\n        'instruct': { name: 'Instruct Template', checked: true },\n        'context': { name: 'Context Template', checked: true },\n        'sysprompt': { name: 'System Prompt', checked: true },\n        'preset': { name: 'Text Completion Preset', checked: false },\n        'reasoning': { name: 'Reasoning Formatting', checked: true },\n        'srw': { name: 'Start Reply With', checked: false },\n    };\n    \n    // Show selection UI\n    const confirmedSections = /* ... user selection ... */;\n    \n    const data = {};\n    for (const section of confirmedSections) {\n        data[section] = masterSections[section].getData();\n    }\n    \n    return JSON.stringify(data, null, 4);\n}\n```\n\nEach section's `getData()` method retrieves the current settings from the appropriate manager.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/preset-manager.js:335-370]()\n\n---\n\n## UI Integration\n\n### Preset Selection Controls\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Text Generation UI\"\n        select[\"#settings_preset_textgenerationwebui\"]\n        update[\"Update Preset Button\"]\n        saveas[\"Save As Button\"]\n        delete[\"Delete Button\"]\n        reset[\"Reset Samplers Button\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Sampler Controls\"\n        sliders[\"Slider Inputs\"]\n        counters[\"Counter Displays\"]\n        order[\"Sortable Sampler Order\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Actions\"\n        change[\"onChange → selectPreset()\"]\n        click_update[\"onClick → updatePreset()\"]\n        click_save[\"onClick → savePresetAs()\"]\n        click_delete[\"onClick → deletePreset()\"]\n        drag[\"onDrag → reorder samplers\"]\n    end\n    \n    select --\u003e change\n    update --\u003e click_update\n    saveas --\u003e click_save\n    delete --\u003e click_delete\n    order --\u003e drag\n    \n    sliders --\u003e counters\n```\n\n### Dynamic UI Elements\n\nSampler controls are shown/hidden based on backend type:\n\n```javascript\nfunction showSamplerControls(type) {\n    // Hide all sampler controls\n    $('.sampler_options').each((i, el) =\u003e $(el).hide());\n    \n    // Show controls based on backend type\n    switch (type) {\n        case OOBA:\n            $('#sampler_priority_container').show();\n            $('#dynatemp_block_ooba').show();\n            break;\n        case LLAMACPP:\n            $('#llamacpp_samplers_sortable').show();\n            break;\n        case KOBOLDCPP:\n            $('#koboldcpp_order').show();\n            break;\n        // ... other cases\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:1358-1542]()\n\n---\n\n## Preset Storage Backend\n\nServer-side preset management is handled through REST endpoints:\n\n### Preset API Endpoints\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |\n|----------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/presets/save` | POST | Save or update preset |\n| `/api/presets/delete` | POST | Delete preset by name |\n| `/api/presets/restore` | POST | Get default preset |\n\nThe save endpoint handles:\n- Name sanitization with `getSanitizedFilename()`\n- API type-specific subdirectories\n- JSON file writing with atomic writes\n- Error handling for file system operations\n\n**Sources:** Server implementation at [src/endpoints/presets.js]() (not provided in files, but referenced)\n\n---\n\n## Extension Points\n\nPresets support an `extensions` field for custom data:\n\n```javascript\n// In preset object\n{\n  temp: 0.7,\n  top_p: 0.9,\n  // ... standard fields\n  extensions: {\n    // Extension-specific data\n    my_extension: {\n      custom_setting: \"value\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\nExtensions can read/write preset extension data:\n\n```javascript\n// Read extension data from preset\nconst manager = getPresetManager('textgenerationwebui');\nconst extensionData = await manager.readPresetExtensionField({\n    name: 'Current Preset',\n    path: 'my_extension.custom_setting'\n});\n\n// Write extension data to preset\nawait manager.writePresetExtensionField({\n    name: 'Current Preset', \n    path: 'my_extension.custom_setting',\n    value: 'new_value'\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/preset-manager.js:843-894]()\n\n---\n\n## Token Budget and Sampler Interaction\n\nSamplers interact with tokenization for features like repetition penalty range:\n\n```javascript\n// Repetition penalty applies to last N tokens\nconst repPenRange = textgenerationwebui_settings.rep_pen_range;\n\n// Token counting for banned tokens\nfunction getCustomTokenBans(settings) {\n    const tokenizer = getTokenizerForTokenIds();\n    const banned_tokens = [];\n    \n    for (const line of sequences) {\n        const tokens = getTextTokens(tokenizer, line);\n        banned_tokens.push(...tokens);\n    }\n    \n    return banned_tokens.filter(onlyUnique).map(x =\u003e String(x)).join(',');\n}\n```\n\nDRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) sampling uses token-level sequence matching:\n\n```javascript\n{\n    dry_multiplier: 0.0,           // Strength of penalty\n    dry_base: 1.75,                // Base for penalty calculation\n    dry_allowed_length: 2,         // Min sequence length to penalize\n    dry_sequence_breakers: '[\"\\\\n\", \":\", \"\\\\\"\", \"*\"]',\n    dry_penalty_last_n: 0,         // How far back to check\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:438-498](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:175-179]()\n\n---\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### Preset Naming Convention\n\n- Use descriptive names that indicate purpose: `\"Creative Writing\"`, `\"Precise Answers\"`\n- Name presets after characters/groups for auto-selection\n- Avoid special characters that might cause file system issues\n\n### Sampler Configuration Guidelines\n\n**For creative writing:**\n- Higher temperature (1.0-1.5)\n- Higher top-p (0.9-0.95)\n- Lower repetition penalty (1.0-1.1)\n\n**For factual responses:**\n- Lower temperature (0.3-0.7)\n- Lower top-p (0.5-0.8)\n- Moderate repetition penalty (1.1-1.2)\n\n**For code generation:**\n- Very low temperature (0.1-0.3)\n- Low top-p (0.5-0.7)\n- Higher repetition penalty (1.2-1.3)\n\n### Sampler Order Recommendations\n\nTemperature should generally come late in the order to preserve the probability distribution shaped by other samplers. The recommended orders provided by the system have been tuned for quality.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js:960-1009]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"28:T59ed,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Reasoning Display System\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [public/scripts/RossAscends-mods.js](public/scripts/RossAscends-mods.js)\n- [public/scripts/custom-request.js](public/scripts/custom-request.js)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js](public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/caption/settings.html](public/scripts/extensions/caption/settings.html)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/shared.js](public/scripts/extensions/shared.js)\n- [public/scripts/openai.js](public/scripts/openai.js)\n- [public/scripts/reasoning.js](public/scripts/reasoning.js)\n- [public/scripts/secrets.js](public/scripts/secrets.js)\n- [public/scripts/slash-commands.js](public/scripts/slash-commands.js)\n- [public/scripts/st-context.js](public/scripts/st-context.js)\n- [public/scripts/tool-calling.js](public/scripts/tool-calling.js)\n- [src/constants.js](src/constants.js)\n- [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js](src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js)\n- [src/endpoints/openai.js](src/endpoints/openai.js)\n- [src/endpoints/secrets.js](src/endpoints/secrets.js)\n- [src/prompt-converters.js](src/prompt-converters.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThe Reasoning Display System manages the extraction, storage, formatting, and display of model reasoning/thinking content during AI generation. This includes native model reasoning (e.g., Claude's thinking blocks, DeepSeek's reasoning content), parsed reasoning from response text using templates, and manually-entered reasoning annotations.\n\nFor information about the broader AI request flow and prompt assembly, see [AI Integration](#3) and [Prompt Assembly Pipeline](#3.3). For streaming response processing, see [Streaming and Response Processing](#3.6).\n\n## System Overview\n\nThe reasoning system operates across three layers: extraction from API responses, storage in chat messages, and UI rendering. It supports multiple reasoning sources and maintains state throughout the generation lifecycle.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Extraction Layer\"\n        APIResp[\"API Response\"]\n        ExtractFunc[\"extractReasoningFromData()\"]\n        ExtractSig[\"extractReasoningSignatureFromData()\"]\n        ParseTemplate[\"parseReasoningFromString()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Storage Layer\"\n        ChatMessage[\"chat[messageId]\"]\n        ReasoningField[\"extra.reasoning\"]\n        ReasoningType[\"extra.reasoning_type\"]\n        ReasoningSig[\"extra.reasoning_signature\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"UI Layer\"\n        ReasoningHandler[\"ReasoningHandler\"]\n        ReasoningState[\"state: None|Thinking|Done|Hidden\"]\n        DetailsDom[\"\u003cdetails class='mes_reasoning_details'\u003e\"]\n        ContentDom[\"\u003cdiv class='mes_reasoning'\u003e\"]\n    end\n    \n    APIResp --\u003e ExtractFunc\n    APIResp --\u003e ExtractSig\n    APIResp --\u003e ParseTemplate\n    \n    ExtractFunc --\u003e ReasoningField\n    ExtractSig --\u003e ReasoningSig\n    ParseTemplate --\u003e ReasoningField\n    \n    ReasoningField --\u003e ReasoningHandler\n    ReasoningType --\u003e ReasoningHandler\n    ReasoningSig --\u003e ReasoningHandler\n    \n    ReasoningHandler --\u003e ReasoningState\n    ReasoningHandler --\u003e DetailsDom\n    ReasoningHandler --\u003e ContentDom\n```\n\n**Diagram: Reasoning System Architecture**\n\nSources: [public/scripts/reasoning.js:89-145](), [public/scripts/reasoning.js:251-292](), [public/scripts/openai.js:525-600]()\n\n## Reasoning Types and States\n\nThe system distinguishes between different origins of reasoning content and tracks the current state of reasoning during generation.\n\n### Reasoning Types\n\n| Type | Enum Value | Description | Storage Field |\n|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|\n| Model-native | `ReasoningType.Model` | Extracted from API response (Claude thinking, DeepSeek reasoning_content) | `extra.reasoning_type = 'model'` |\n| Parsed | `ReasoningType.Parsed` | Extracted from message text using templates | `extra.reasoning_type = 'parsed'` |\n| Manual | `ReasoningType.Manual` | User-entered annotation | `extra.reasoning_type = 'manual'` |\n| Edited | `ReasoningType.Edited` | Model/parsed reasoning that was edited | `extra.reasoning_type = 'edited'` |\n\n### Reasoning States\n\n| State | Enum Value | Description | When Active |\n|-------|-----------|-------------|-------------|\n| None | `ReasoningState.None` | No reasoning present or expected | Before reasoning starts |\n| Thinking | `ReasoningState.Thinking` | Reasoning in progress | During streaming of reasoning content |\n| Done | `ReasoningState.Done` | Reasoning complete | After reasoning finishes |\n| Hidden | `ReasoningState.Hidden` | Model supports reasoning but doesn't expose it | Models like o1, gpt-4.5 |\n\nSources: [public/scripts/reasoning.js:56-62](), [public/scripts/reasoning.js:238-247]()\n\n## API-Specific Reasoning Extraction\n\nDifferent AI providers return reasoning content in different formats. The extraction system handles these variations transparently.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    ExtractFunc[\"extractReasoningFromData(data, options)\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Chat Completion Sources\"\n        DeepSeek[\"DEEPSEEK\u003cbr/\u003echoices[0].message.reasoning_content\"]\n        XAI[\"XAI\u003cbr/\u003echoices[0].message.reasoning_content\"]\n        OpenRouter[\"OPENROUTER\u003cbr/\u003echoices[0].message.reasoning\"]\n        Claude[\"CLAUDE\u003cbr/\u003econtent.find(type='thinking').thinking\"]\n        Gemini[\"MAKERSUITE/VERTEXAI\u003cbr/\u003eresponseContent.parts[].text (if thought=true)\"]\n        Mistral[\"MISTRALAI\u003cbr/\u003echoices[0].message.content[0].thinking[].text\"]\n        Generic[\"AIMLAPI/POLLINATIONS/etc\u003cbr/\u003ereasoning_content || reasoning\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Text Completion Sources\"\n        OpenRouterText[\"OPENROUTER (text)\u003cbr/\u003echoices[0].reasoning\"]\n        Ollama[\"OLLAMA\u003cbr/\u003ethinking\"]\n    end\n    \n    ExtractFunc --\u003e DeepSeek\n    ExtractFunc --\u003e XAI\n    ExtractFunc --\u003e OpenRouter\n    ExtractFunc --\u003e Claude\n    ExtractFunc --\u003e Gemini\n    ExtractFunc --\u003e Mistral\n    ExtractFunc --\u003e Generic\n    ExtractFunc --\u003e OpenRouterText\n    ExtractFunc --\u003e Ollama\n    \n    ShowThoughts[\"oai_settings.show_thoughts\"]\n    ShowThoughts -.checks.-\u003e ExtractFunc\n```\n\n**Diagram: API Response Extraction Paths**\n\nThe `extractReasoningFromData()` function serves as the central extraction point, with logic branches for each provider:\n\n```javascript\n// Example extraction logic for different sources\nswitch (chatCompletionSource ?? oai_settings.chat_completion_source) {\n    case chat_completion_sources.DEEPSEEK:\n        return data?.choices?.[0]?.message?.reasoning_content ?? '';\n    case chat_completion_sources.CLAUDE:\n        return data?.content?.find(part =\u003e part.type === 'thinking')?.thinking ?? '';\n    case chat_completion_sources.MAKERSUITE:\n    case chat_completion_sources.VERTEXAI:\n        return data?.responseContent?.parts?.filter(part =\u003e part.thought)\n            ?.map(part =\u003e part.text)?.join('\\n\\n') ?? '';\n    // ... other sources\n}\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/reasoning.js:88-145](), [public/scripts/openai.js:529-600](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:205-384]()\n\n### Hidden Reasoning Models\n\nSome models perform reasoning internally but do not expose the reasoning content in the response. The system detects these models to adjust UI expectations:\n\n```javascript\nconst hiddenReasoningModels = [\n    { name: 'gpt-4.5', func: FUNCS.startsWith },\n    { name: 'o1', func: FUNCS.startsWith },\n    { name: 'o3', func: FUNCS.startsWith },\n    { name: 'gemini-2.0-flash-thinking-exp', func: FUNCS.startsWith },\n    { name: 'gemini-2.0-pro-exp', func: FUNCS.startsWith },\n];\n```\n\nWhen a hidden reasoning model is detected, the UI shows a \"Model is thinking...\" indicator without displaying reasoning content.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/reasoning.js:193-223](), [public/scripts/openai.js:525-600]()\n\n## Reasoning Templates and Manual Parsing\n\nUsers can define templates to extract reasoning from message text that doesn't natively separate reasoning from response content. Templates use prefix, suffix, and separator patterns.\n\n### Template Structure\n\n```typescript\ninterface ReasoningTemplate {\n    name: string;           // Display name (e.g., \"DeepSeek\")\n    prefix: string;         // Opening delimiter (e.g., \"\")\n    separator: string;      // Separator between reasoning and response\n}\n```\n\nDefault templates are loaded from `public/reasoning/*.json` files. The system includes built-in templates for common formats:\n\n| Template | Prefix | Suffix | Separator |\n|----------|--------|--------|-----------|\n| DeepSeek | `` | (empty) |\n| Custom | (user-defined) | (user-defined) | (user-defined) |\n\n### Parsing Process\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant UI as \"Reasoning UI\"\n    participant Parser as \"parseReasoningFromString()\"\n    participant Message as \"chat[messageId]\"\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eUI: Click \"Parse Reasoning\"\n    UI-\u003e\u003eUI: Get selected template\n    UI-\u003e\u003eParser: parseReasoningFromString(text, template)\n    \n    Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Build regex from template\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Execute regex.exec(text)\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Extract reasoning \u0026 clean message\n    \n    Parser--\u003e\u003eUI: {reasoning, message, found}\n    \n    alt Reasoning found\n        UI-\u003e\u003eMessage: Set extra.reasoning\n        UI-\u003e\u003eMessage: Set extra.reasoning_type = 'parsed'\n        UI-\u003e\u003eMessage: Update mes (cleaned text)\n        UI-\u003e\u003eUI: updateReasoningUI()\n    else Not found\n        UI-\u003e\u003eUser: Show \"No reasoning found\"\n    end\n```\n\n**Diagram: Manual Reasoning Parsing Flow**\n\nThe parsing function uses regular expressions to identify and extract reasoning blocks:\n\n```javascript\nfunction parseReasoningFromString(text, template) {\n    const prefix = escapeRegex(template.prefix);\n    const suffix = escapeRegex(template.suffix);\n    const separator = escapeRegex(template.separator);\n    \n    const pattern = `${prefix}([\\\\s\\\\S]*?)${suffix}(?:${separator})?([\\\\s\\\\S]*)`;\n    const regex = new RegExp(pattern);\n    const match = regex.exec(text);\n    \n    if (match) {\n        return {\n            reasoning: match[1].trim(),\n            message: match[2].trim(),\n            found: true\n        };\n    }\n    return { reasoning: '', message: text, found: false };\n}\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/reasoning.js:21-37](), [public/scripts/reasoning.js:548-621]()\n\n### Auto-Parse Feature\n\nWhen `reasoning.auto_parse` is enabled, the system automatically attempts to parse reasoning from new messages using the selected template during message streaming:\n\n```javascript\n// Auto-parse during streaming\nif (power_user.reasoning.auto_parse) {\n    const template = getReasoningTemplateByName(power_user.reasoning.template);\n    const { reasoning, message, found } = parseReasoningFromString(text, template);\n    \n    if (found \u0026\u0026 reasoning) {\n        this.reasoning = reasoning;\n        this.type = ReasoningType.Parsed;\n        // Update streamed text to exclude reasoning\n        return message;\n    }\n}\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/reasoning.js:38-50](), [public/scripts/reasoning.js:487-547]()\n\n## Reasoning Handler and UI Updates\n\nThe `ReasoningHandler` class manages the lifecycle of reasoning display for individual messages, handling state transitions during streaming and final rendering.\n\n### ReasoningHandler Class\n\n```mermaid\nclassDiagram\n    class ReasoningHandler {\n        +ReasoningState state\n        +ReasoningType type\n        +string reasoning\n        +string reasoningDisplayText\n        +Date startTime\n        +Date endTime\n        \n        +initHandleMessage(messageIdOrElement, options)\n        +initContinue(promptReasoning)\n        +onStreamStart()\n        +onStreamChunk(chunk, complete)\n        +onStreamEnd()\n        +updateReasoningUI()\n        +removeReasoningUI()\n        -parseReasoningManually(messageId, template)\n    }\n    \n    class StreamingProcessor {\n        +ReasoningHandler reasoningHandler\n    }\n    \n    StreamingProcessor --\u003e ReasoningHandler : owns\n```\n\n**Diagram: ReasoningHandler Class Structure**\n\n### State Transitions During Generation\n\n```mermaid\nstateDiagram-v2\n    [*] --\u003e None: Generation starts\n    \n    None --\u003e Thinking: Reasoning chunk received\n    None --\u003e Hidden: Hidden reasoning model detected\n    \n    Thinking --\u003e Thinking: More reasoning chunks\n    Thinking --\u003e Done: Reasoning complete, message starts\n    \n    Hidden --\u003e Hidden: Generation continues\n    Hidden --\u003e [*]: Generation ends\n    \n    Done --\u003e Done: Message chunks received\n    Done --\u003e [*]: Generation ends\n    \n    None --\u003e [*]: No reasoning detected\n```\n\n**Diagram: Reasoning State Transitions**\n\nSources: [public/scripts/reasoning.js:251-292](), [public/scripts/reasoning.js:293-351]()\n\n### DOM Structure\n\nThe reasoning UI is rendered as a `\u003cdetails\u003e` element within the message block:\n\n```html\n\u003cdiv class=\"mes\" mesid=\"123\"\u003e\n    \u003cdetails class=\"mes_reasoning_details\" open\u003e\n        \u003csummary class=\"mes_reasoning_header\"\u003e\n            \u003cdiv class=\"mes_reasoning_header_title\"\u003e\n                \u003c!-- Title with timer, duration, etc --\u003e\n            \u003c/div\u003e\n            \u003cdiv class=\"mes_reasoning_controls\"\u003e\n                \u003c!-- Edit, copy, delete buttons --\u003e\n            \u003c/div\u003e\n        \u003c/summary\u003e\n        \u003cdiv class=\"mes_reasoning\" contenteditable=\"false\"\u003e\n            \u003c!-- Reasoning content --\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n    \u003c/details\u003e\n    \u003c!-- Rest of message content --\u003e\n\u003c/div\u003e\n```\n\nKey UI features:\n- **Auto-expand**: Controlled by `power_user.reasoning.auto_expand` setting\n- **Duration display**: Shows time spent on reasoning (from `startTime` to `endTime`)\n- **Edit mode**: Click edit button to enable `contenteditable=\"true\"`\n- **Copy/Delete**: Action buttons for reasoning manipulation\n- **Translation**: Reasoning can be translated like regular message content\n\nSources: [public/scripts/reasoning.js:352-486](), [public/scripts/reasoning.js:622-756]()\n\n### Update Function Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Caller\n    participant updateReasoningUI\n    participant ReasoningHandler\n    participant DOM\n    \n    Caller-\u003e\u003eupdateReasoningUI: updateReasoningUI(messageId)\n    updateReasoningUI-\u003e\u003eReasoningHandler: new ReasoningHandler()\n    updateReasoningUI-\u003e\u003eReasoningHandler: initHandleMessage(messageId, {reset})\n    \n    ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eDOM: Find message block\n    ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eDOM: messageBlock.attr('mesid')\n    \n    alt Reasoning exists\n        ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eReasoningHandler: Read from chat[messageId].extra\n        ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eReasoningHandler: Set state = Done\n        ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eDOM: Render/update \u003cdetails\u003e\n    else No reasoning\n        ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eReasoningHandler: Set state = None\n        ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eDOM: Remove \u003cdetails\u003e if exists\n    end\n    \n    ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eDOM: Update header (timer, type badge)\n    ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eDOM: Set contenteditable state\n    ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eDOM: Apply auto_expand setting\n```\n\n**Diagram: updateReasoningUI Execution Flow**\n\nSources: [public/scripts/reasoning.js:227-234](), [public/scripts/reasoning.js:352-486]()\n\n## Reasoning Signatures (Context Preservation)\n\nGemini models (via MakerSuite/VertexAI and OpenRouter) use encrypted reasoning signatures to maintain reasoning context across multi-turn conversations without exposing the actual reasoning content in subsequent prompts.\n\n### Signature Extraction\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Response[\"API Response\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"OpenRouter Format\"\n        OR_Details[\"reasoning_details[]\"]\n        OR_Type[\"type = 'reasoning.encrypted'\"]\n        OR_Data[\"data (encrypted signature)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Direct Gemini Format\"\n        G_Parts[\"responseContent.parts[]\"]\n        G_Sig[\"thoughtSignature\"]\n        G_Text[\"text (only if text part)\"]\n    end\n    \n    Response --\u003e OR_Details\n    OR_Details --\u003e OR_Type\n    OR_Type --\u003e OR_Data\n    \n    Response --\u003e G_Parts\n    G_Parts --\u003e G_Sig\n    G_Parts --\u003e G_Text\n    \n    OR_Data --\u003e Signature[\"Signature String\"]\n    G_Sig --\u003e Signature\n```\n\n**Diagram: Signature Extraction Paths**\n\nThe `extractReasoningSignatureFromData()` function extracts these signatures:\n\n```javascript\n// OpenRouter format\nif (isOpenRouter \u0026\u0026 Array.isArray(data?.choices?.[0]?.message?.reasoning_details)) {\n    for (const detail of data.choices[0].message.reasoning_details) {\n        // Exclude tool call signatures\n        if (!/^tool_/.test(detail.id) \u0026\u0026 detail.type === 'reasoning.encrypted' \u0026\u0026 detail.data) {\n            return detail.data;\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Direct Gemini format\nif (isGemini \u0026\u0026 Array.isArray(data?.responseContent?.parts)) {\n    data.responseContent.parts.forEach((part) =\u003e {\n        if (part.thoughtSignature \u0026\u0026 typeof part.text === 'string') {\n            return part.thoughtSignature;\n        }\n    });\n}\n```\n\n### Signature in Chat Messages\n\nSignatures are stored in the message's `extra.reasoning_signature` field and included when sending messages back to the API:\n\n```javascript\n// In setOpenAIMessages()\nconst signature = isSameModel ? chat[j]?.extra?.reasoning_signature : null;\nmessages[i] = { \n    'role': role, \n    'content': content, \n    'signature': signature,\n    // ... other fields\n};\n```\n\nSignatures are only included when the current API and model match the ones that generated the reasoning, preventing signature mismatches across different models.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/reasoning.js:147-192](), [public/scripts/openai.js:577-595](), [src/prompt-converters.js:1029-1078]()\n\n### Signature Handling in Prompt Converters\n\nServer-side code adds signatures to messages before sending to APIs:\n\n```javascript\n// In addOpenRouterSignatures() for OpenRouter\nif (message.signature) {\n    if (!message.reasoningDetails) {\n        message.reasoningDetails = [];\n    }\n    message.reasoningDetails.push({\n        id: uuidv4(),\n        type: 'reasoning.encrypted',\n        data: message.signature,\n    });\n}\n\n// In convertGooglePrompt() for direct Gemini\nif (message.signature) {\n    message.parts.push({\n        thoughtSignature: message.signature,\n    });\n}\n```\n\nSources: [src/prompt-converters.js:1029-1078](), [src/prompt-converters.js:1080-1122]()\n\n## Streaming Integration\n\nThe reasoning system integrates with the streaming response processor to update reasoning content in real-time during generation.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant API\n    participant SSE as \"SSE Stream\"\n    participant StreamProc as \"StreamingProcessor\"\n    participant ReasoningHandler\n    participant UI\n    \n    API-\u003e\u003eSSE: Start streaming\n    StreamProc-\u003e\u003eReasoningHandler: new ReasoningHandler(timeStarted)\n    StreamProc-\u003e\u003eReasoningHandler: onStreamStart()\n    \n    loop For each chunk\n        SSE-\u003e\u003eStreamProc: SSE event with data\n        StreamProc-\u003e\u003eStreamProc: parseStreamData(data)\n        \n        alt Reasoning chunk\n            StreamProc-\u003e\u003eReasoningHandler: onStreamChunk(chunk, complete=false)\n            ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eReasoningHandler: state = Thinking\n            ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eReasoningHandler: reasoning += chunk\n            ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eUI: Update reasoning display\n        else Message chunk\n            StreamProc-\u003e\u003eReasoningHandler: onStreamChunk('', complete=true)\n            ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eReasoningHandler: state = Done\n            ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eUI: Finalize reasoning display\n            StreamProc-\u003e\u003eUI: Update message text\n        end\n    end\n    \n    SSE-\u003e\u003eStreamProc: Stream complete\n    StreamProc-\u003e\u003eReasoningHandler: onStreamEnd()\n    ReasoningHandler-\u003e\u003eUI: Final UI update\n```\n\n**Diagram: Streaming Reasoning Flow**\n\nThe `ReasoningHandler` provides hooks for the streaming lifecycle:\n\n- `onStreamStart()`: Called when streaming begins, initializes state\n- `onStreamChunk(chunk, complete)`: Called for each chunk, updates reasoning content\n- `onStreamEnd()`: Called when streaming finishes, finalizes display\n\nSources: [public/scripts/reasoning.js:293-351](), [public/scripts/reasoning.js:487-547]()\n\n## Settings and Configuration\n\nThe reasoning system is controlled through several user-configurable settings stored in `power_user.reasoning`:\n\n| Setting | Type | Default | Description |\n|---------|------|---------|-------------|\n| `template` | string | `'DeepSeek'` | Active reasoning template name |\n| `auto_parse` | boolean | `false` | Auto-parse reasoning from responses |\n| `auto_expand` | boolean | `false` | Auto-expand reasoning blocks in UI |\n| `show_hidden` | boolean | `false` | Show reasoning UI for hidden reasoning models |\n| `add_to_prompts` | boolean | `false` | Include reasoning in prompt context |\n| `max_additions` | number | `5` | Max number of reasoning blocks to include in prompts |\n\nAdditionally, API-specific settings control reasoning behavior:\n\n| Setting | Location | Description |\n|---------|----------|-------------|\n| `show_thoughts` | `oai_settings.show_thoughts` | Enable/disable reasoning display for chat completion APIs |\n| `reasoning_effort` | `oai_settings.reasoning_effort` | Control reasoning depth (auto/low/medium/high/min/max) |\n| `verbosity` | `oai_settings.verbosity` | Control reasoning verbosity (auto/low/medium/high) |\n\nSources: [public/scripts/reasoning.js:38-50](), [public/scripts/openai.js:354-357]()\n\n## Budget Token Calculation\n\nSome APIs (Claude, Gemini) support configurable reasoning budgets to control the amount of tokens dedicated to reasoning:\n\n```javascript\n// Claude budget calculation\nfunction calculateClaudeBudgetTokens(maxTokens, reasoningEffort, isStreaming) {\n    const effortMap = {\n        'auto': null,\n        'low': 0.2,    // 20% of max_tokens\n        'medium': 0.5, // 50%\n        'high': 0.8,   // 80%\n        'min': 1024,   // Fixed minimum\n        'max': null,   // No limit\n    };\n    \n    const multiplier = effortMap[reasoningEffort];\n    if (multiplier === null) return null;\n    if (typeof multiplier === 'number' \u0026\u0026 multiplier \u003c 1) {\n        return Math.floor(maxTokens * multiplier);\n    }\n    return multiplier;\n}\n\n// Applied in request body\nif (useThinking \u0026\u0026 Number.isInteger(budgetTokens)) {\n    requestBody.thinking = {\n        type: 'enabled',\n        budget_tokens: budgetTokens,\n    };\n}\n```\n\nSources: [src/prompt-converters.js:175-233](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:308-329]()\n\n## Slash Commands\n\nThe reasoning system provides several slash commands for programmatic control:\n\n```javascript\n// Get reasoning from a message\n/reasoning-get (mesid=\u003cnumber\u003e)\n// Returns: reasoning text or empty string\n\n// Set reasoning for a message\n/reasoning-set (mesid=\u003cnumber\u003e) (type=model|parsed|manual|edited) \u003creasoning text\u003e\n// Returns: empty string\n\n// Delete reasoning from a message\n/reasoning-delete (mesid=\u003cnumber\u003e)\n// Returns: empty string\n\n// List available templates\n/reasoning-templates\n// Returns: JSON array of template names\n\n// Parse reasoning from message text\n/reasoning-parse (mesid=\u003cnumber\u003e) (template=\u003cname\u003e)\n// Returns: parsed reasoning or empty string\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/reasoning.js:757-1029]()\n\n---\n\nThis system enables SillyTavern to transparently handle reasoning from any supported AI model, providing a consistent user experience regardless of the underlying API's native reasoning format."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"29:T9b15,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# User Interface\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source
