Organization\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [public/css/backgrounds.css](public/css/backgrounds.css)\n- [public/css/mobile-styles.css](public/css/mobile-styles.css)\n- [public/css/select2-overrides.css](public/css/select2-overrides.css)\n- [public/css/st-tailwind.css](public/css/st-tailwind.css)\n- [public/css/tags.css](public/css/tags.css)\n- [public/css/toggle-dependent.css](public/css/toggle-dependent.css)\n- [public/css/world-info.css](public/css/world-info.css)\n- [public/scripts/a11y.js](public/scripts/a11y.js)\n- [public/scripts/backgrounds.js](public/scripts/backgrounds.js)\n- [public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js](public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js)\n- [public/scripts/filters.js](public/scripts/filters.js)\n- [public/scripts/keyboard.js](public/scripts/keyboard.js)\n- [public/scripts/personas.js](public/scripts/personas.js)\n- [public/scripts/sse-stream.js](public/scripts/sse-stream.js)\n- [public/scripts/tags.js](public/scripts/tags.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page documents SillyTavern's tag system and organizational features. Tags are used to categorize and filter characters, groups, and personas. The system includes standard tags for classification, special \"actionable\" filter tags (Favorites, Groups, Folders), and \"bogus folders\" that provide hierarchical organization within a flat tag structure.\n\nFor information about the broader filtering system that integrates with tags, see [Filtering System](#4.3). For persona-specific tagging and organization, see [Personas and User Profiles](#4.4).\n\n---\n\n## Tag Data Model\n\n### Core Data Structures\n\nThe tag system maintains two primary data structures that are persisted in user settings:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Tag System Data Model\"\n        Tags[\"tags Array\u003cbr/\u003e(settings.tags)\"]\n        TagMap[\"tag_map Object\u003cbr/\u003e(settings.tag_map)\"]\n        \n        TagObj[\"Tag Object\"]\n        TagObj --\u003e |properties| TagId[\"id: string (UUID)\"]\n        TagObj --\u003e |properties| TagName[\"name: string\"]\n        TagObj --\u003e |properties| FolderType[\"folder_type: TAG_FOLDER_TYPES\"]\n        TagObj --\u003e |properties| SortOrder[\"sort_order: number\"]\n        TagObj --\u003e |properties| Color[\"color/color2: string\"]\n        TagObj --\u003e |properties| CreateDate[\"create_date: number\"]\n        TagObj --\u003e |properties| Hidden[\"is_hidden_on_character_card: boolean\"]\n        \n        Tags --\u003e |contains| TagObj\n        \n        MapEntry[\"Map Entry\"]\n        MapEntry --\u003e |key| EntityKey[\"Entity Key\u003cbr/\u003e(avatar URL or group ID)\"]\n        MapEntry --\u003e |value| TagIdArray[\"Array of Tag IDs\"]\n        \n        TagMap --\u003e |contains| MapEntry\n    end\n    \n    style Tags fill:#f9f9f9\n    style TagMap fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:345-351](), [public/scripts/tags.js:342-351]()\n\nThe `Tag` object structure (lines 323-339 in tags.js):\n- **id**: Unique UUID identifier used for referencing\n- **name**: Display name shown in UI\n- **folder_type**: One of `TAG_FOLDER_TYPES` (OPEN, CLOSED, NONE) for bogus folder behavior\n- **filter_state**: Saved filter state (SELECTED, EXCLUDED, UNDEFINED)\n- **sort_order**: Custom sort position when manually ordered\n- **color/color2**: Background and foreground colors for tag display\n- **create_date**: Timestamp of tag creation\n- **is_hidden_on_character_card**: Whether tag appears on character cards\n\nThe `tag_map` object maps entity identifiers to arrays of tag IDs. For characters, the key is the avatar URL (e.g., `\"character.png\"`). For groups, the key is the group ID (e.g., `\"group_12345\"`).\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:323-339](), [public/scripts/tags.js:345-351]()\n\n### Storage and Persistence\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Settings[\"power_user settings\"]\n    \n    Settings --\u003e TagsArray[\"settings.tags[]\u003cbr/\u003eGlobal tag definitions\"]\n    Settings --\u003e TagMapObj[\"settings.tag_map{}\u003cbr/\u003eEntity → Tag mappings\"]\n    \n    SaveTrigger1[\"addTagsToEntity()\"]\n    SaveTrigger2[\"removeTagFromEntity()\"]\n    SaveTrigger3[\"createNewTag()\"]\n    SaveTrigger4[\"deleteTag()\"]\n    \n    SaveTrigger1 --\u003e |calls| SaveDebounced[\"saveSettingsDebounced()\"]\n    SaveTrigger2 --\u003e |calls| SaveDebounced\n    SaveTrigger3 --\u003e |calls| SaveDebounced\n    SaveTrigger4 --\u003e |calls| SaveDebounced\n    \n    SaveDebounced --\u003e |persists| Settings\n```\n\nTags are loaded via `loadTagsSettings(settings)` which populates the global `tags` array and `tag_map` object. Any modifications trigger `saveSettingsDebounced()` to persist changes.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:614-617](), [public/scripts/tags.js:782-810](), [public/scripts/tags.js:821-843]()\n\n---\n\n## Tag Types and Special Behaviors\n\n### Regular Tags\n\nRegular tags are user-created labels for organizing entities. They support:\n- Custom names and colors\n- Manual or automatic sorting\n- Import/export functionality\n- Visibility control on character cards\n\nDefault tags are created on first run (lines 292-299):\n```\nPlain Text, OpenAI, W++, Boostyle, PList, AliChat\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:292-299]()\n\n### Actionable Tags (Filter Tags)\n\nActionable tags are special system-defined filter controls that appear as circular buttons in the tag filter UI:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"ACTIONABLE_TAGS Constants\"\n        FAV[\"FAV (id: '1')\u003cbr/\u003efilterByFav()\u003cbr/\u003eStar icon\"]\n        GROUP[\"GROUP (id: '0')\u003cbr/\u003efilterByGroups()\u003cbr/\u003eUsers icon\"]\n        FOLDER[\"FOLDER (id: '4')\u003cbr/\u003efilterByFolder()\u003cbr/\u003eFolder icon\"]\n        VIEW[\"VIEW (id: '2')\u003cbr/\u003eonViewTagsListClick()\u003cbr/\u003eGear icon\"]\n        HINT[\"HINT (id: '3')\u003cbr/\u003eonTagListHintClick()\u003cbr/\u003eTags icon\"]\n        UNFILTER[\"UNFILTER (id: '5')\u003cbr/\u003eonClearAllFiltersClick()\u003cbr/\u003eFilter-X icon\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Filter Integration\"\n        FAV --\u003e |maps to| FavFilter[\"FILTER_TYPES.FAV\"]\n        GROUP --\u003e |maps to| GroupFilter[\"FILTER_TYPES.GROUP\"]\n        FOLDER --\u003e |maps to| FolderFilter[\"FILTER_TYPES.FOLDER\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"State Persistence\"\n        FavFilter --\u003e FavStorage[\"TagFilterState_FAV\"]\n        GroupFilter --\u003e GroupStorage[\"TagFilterState_GROUP\"]\n        FolderFilter --\u003e FolderStorage[\"TagFilterState_FOLDER\"]\n        \n        FavStorage --\u003e AccountStorage[\"accountStorage\"]\n        GroupStorage --\u003e AccountStorage\n        FolderStorage --\u003e AccountStorage\n    end\n```\n\nEach actionable tag with a `filter_state` property maintains persistent state per context (character list, group candidates, group members). The state is stored in `accountStorage` with context-specific keys.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:268-275](), [public/scripts/tags.js:281-285](), [public/scripts/tags.js:165-169](), [public/scripts/tags.js:534-551]()\n\n### Bogus Folders\n\nBogus folders are tags that act as hierarchical containers while maintaining a flat tag structure. Each folder tag has a `folder_type` property:\n\n| Folder Type | Icon | Behavior | CSS Class |\n|-------------|------|----------|-----------|\n| `OPEN` | ✔ (fa-folder-open) | Shows all tagged entities even if not selected | `folder_open` |\n| `CLOSED` | 👁 (fa-eye-slash) | Hides tagged entities unless folder is selected | `folder_closed` |\n| `NONE` | ✕ | Not a folder (standard tag) | `no_folder` |\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Bogus Folder States\"\n        FolderTag[\"Tag with folder_type\"]\n        \n        FolderTag --\u003e |OPEN| OpenBehavior[\"All entities visible\u003cbr/\u003eActs as category label\"]\n        FolderTag --\u003e |CLOSED| ClosedBehavior[\"Entities hidden\u003cbr/\u003eMust click folder to view\"]\n        FolderTag --\u003e |NONE| NoneBehavior[\"Standard tag behavior\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Folder Filtering Logic\"\n        FilterByTagState[\"filterByTagState()\"]\n        FilterByTagState --\u003e CheckClosed[\"Check closedFolders\"]\n        CheckClosed --\u003e HideEntities[\"Hide entities in\u003cbr/\u003eclosed folders unless\u003cbr/\u003efolder is selected\"]\n        CheckClosed --\u003e HideEmpty[\"Hide empty folders\u003cbr/\u003e(unless searching)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Folder Selection\"\n        ClickFolder[\"Click folder tag\"]\n        ClickFolder --\u003e ToggleState[\"toggleTagThreeState()\"]\n        ToggleState --\u003e UpdateFilter[\"Update TAG filter\u003cbr/\u003ein FilterHelper\"]\n        UpdateFilter --\u003e Drilldown[\"Update drilldown UI\"]\n    end\n```\n\nThe `isBogusFolder(tag)` function checks if a tag is configured as a folder (lines 447-449). `isBogusFolderOpen()` checks if any folders are currently selected. The `chooseBogusFolder()` function handles folder selection/deselection.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:315-320](), [public/scripts/tags.js:447-449](), [public/scripts/tags.js:467-469](), [public/scripts/tags.js:478-494](), [public/scripts/tags.js:369-408]()\n\n---\n\n## Tag Operations\n\n### Creating and Managing Tags\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant UI as \"Tag Input UI\"\n    participant TagSystem as \"tags.js\"\n    participant Settings as \"Settings Storage\"\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eUI: Enter tag name\n    UI-\u003e\u003eTagSystem: createNewTag(tagName)\n    TagSystem-\u003e\u003eTagSystem: Generate UUID\n    TagSystem-\u003e\u003eTagSystem: Create Tag object\n    TagSystem-\u003e\u003eTagSystem: tags.push(newTag)\n    TagSystem-\u003e\u003eSettings: saveSettingsDebounced()\n    TagSystem-\u003e\u003eUI: Return tag\n    UI-\u003e\u003eUI: Render new tag\n```\n\nKey functions:\n\n| Function | Purpose | Location |\n|----------|---------|----------|\n| `createNewTag(tagName, color?, color2?)` | Creates a new tag with UUID | [tags.js:928-941]() |\n| `deleteTag(tagId)` | Removes tag and all mappings | [tags.js:1126-1153]() |\n| `renameTagKey(oldKey, newKey)` | Updates tag_map keys | [tags.js:619-624]() |\n| `getTag(tagName)` | Finds tag by name | [tags.js:943-945]() |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:928-945](), [public/scripts/tags.js:1126-1153](), [public/scripts/tags.js:619-624]()\n\n### Adding and Removing Tags from Entities\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Entity Tagging API\"\n        AddAPI[\"addTagsToEntity(tag, entityId, options)\"]\n        RemoveAPI[\"removeTagFromEntity(tag, entityId, options)\"]\n        \n        AddAPI --\u003e AddToMap[\"addTagToMap(tagId, characterId)\"]\n        RemoveAPI --\u003e RemoveFromMap[\"removeTagFromMap(tagId, characterId)\"]\n        \n        AddToMap --\u003e MapUpdate[\"tag_map[key].push(tagId)\"]\n        RemoveFromMap --\u003e MapRemove[\"tag_map[key].splice(index, 1)\"]\n        \n        MapUpdate --\u003e Save[\"saveSettingsDebounced()\"]\n        MapRemove --\u003e Save\n        \n        Save --\u003e Redraw[\"printCharactersDebounced()\"]\n        Redraw --\u003e UpdateUI[\"Update tag lists\u003cbr/\u003eprintTagList()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Entity Key Resolution\"\n        GetKey[\"getTagKeyForEntity(entityOrKey)\"]\n        GetKey --\u003e CheckObject[\"Is object with id?\"]\n        CheckObject --\u003e CheckCharacter[\"Is character/character id?\"]\n        CheckCharacter --\u003e ReturnAvatar[\"Return character.avatar\"]\n        CheckObject --\u003e CheckGroup[\"Is group/group id?\"]\n        CheckGroup --\u003e ReturnGroup[\"Return group id\"]\n    end\n```\n\nThe `addTagsToEntity()` function (lines 782-810) accepts either single tags/entities or arrays, and optionally updates specific tag lists in the UI. The `removeTagFromEntity()` function (lines 821-843) similarly handles removal and UI updates.\n\nEntity keys are resolved by `getTagKeyForEntity()` (lines 691-722), which handles character objects, character IDs, character avatar URLs, and group IDs, converting them all to the appropriate tag_map key.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:782-810](), [public/scripts/tags.js:821-843](), [public/scripts/tags.js:691-722](), [public/scripts/tags.js:851-870](), [public/scripts/tags.js:878-894]()\n\n### Tag Import and Export\n\nTags can be imported when importing characters or groups. The import behavior is controlled by `power_user.tag_import_setting`:\n\n| Setting | Value | Behavior |\n|---------|-------|----------|\n| `ASK` | 1 | Prompt user for each tag |\n| `NONE` | 2 | Don't import any tags |\n| `ALL` | 3 | Import all tags |\n| `ONLY_EXISTING` | 4 | Only import tags that already exist |\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Import[\"importTags(item, importSetting)\"]\n    \n    Import --\u003e ExtractTags[\"Extract tags from\u003cbr/\u003eitem.tags array\"]\n    \n    ExtractTags --\u003e CheckSetting{\"import_setting\"}\n    \n    CheckSetting --\u003e |NONE| SkipAll[\"Return empty array\"]\n    CheckSetting --\u003e |ALL| CreateAll[\"Create all missing tags\"]\n    CheckSetting --\u003e |ONLY_EXISTING| FilterExisting[\"Only use existing tags\"]\n    CheckSetting --\u003e |ASK| PromptUser[\"Show tag selection popup\"]\n    \n    CreateAll --\u003e AddToEntity[\"Add tags to entity\"]\n    FilterExisting --\u003e AddToEntity\n    PromptUser --\u003e |selected tags| AddToEntity\n```\n\nThe `importTags()` function (lines 1184-1286) handles the import logic, creating new tags as needed and adding them to the imported entity's tag_map entry.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:245-250](), [public/scripts/tags.js:1184-1286]()\n\n---\n\n## FilterHelper Architecture\n\n### Overview\n\nThe `FilterHelper` class ([public/scripts/filters.js:78-330]()) provides a unified filtering system that coordinates multiple filter types. Each filterable list (main character list, group candidates, group members, personas) has its own `FilterHelper` instance.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"FilterHelper Instances\"\n        entitiesFilter[\"entitiesFilter\u003cbr/\u003e(main character list)\"]\n        groupCandidatesFilter[\"groupCandidatesFilter\u003cbr/\u003e(add members to group)\"]\n        groupMembersFilter[\"groupMembersFilter\u003cbr/\u003e(current group members)\"]\n        personasFilter[\"personasFilter\u003cbr/\u003e(persona list)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"FilterHelper Class Structure\"\n        FilterHelper[\"FilterHelper class\"]\n        \n        FilterHelper --\u003e onDataChanged[\"onDataChanged: Function\u003cbr/\u003e(callback on filter change)\"]\n        FilterHelper --\u003e filterData[\"filterData: Object\u003cbr/\u003e(current filter states)\"]\n        FilterHelper --\u003e filterFunctions[\"filterFunctions: Object\u003cbr/\u003e(filter implementations)\"]\n        FilterHelper --\u003e scoreCache[\"scoreCache: Map\u003cbr/\u003e(fuzzy search scores)\"]\n        FilterHelper --\u003e fuzzySearchCaches[\"fuzzySearchCaches: Object\u003cbr/\u003e(search result caching)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Filter Types (FILTER_TYPES)\"\n        SEARCH[\"SEARCH: 'search'\"]\n        TAG[\"TAG: 'tag'\"]\n        FAV[\"FAV: 'fav'\"]\n        GROUP[\"GROUP: 'group'\"]\n        FOLDER[\"FOLDER: 'folder'\"]\n        WI_SEARCH[\"WORLD_INFO_SEARCH\"]\n        PERSONA_SEARCH[\"PERSONA_SEARCH\"]\n    end\n    \n    FilterHelper --\u003e |manages| SEARCH\n    FilterHelper --\u003e |manages| TAG\n    FilterHelper --\u003e |manages| FAV\n    FilterHelper --\u003e |manages| GROUP\n    FilterHelper --\u003e |manages| FOLDER\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/filters.js:78-166](), [public/scripts/filters.js:15-23]()\n\n### Filter Application Pipeline\n\nWhen `applyFilters(data)` is called, the FilterHelper sequentially applies each active filter:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Caller\n    participant FilterHelper\n    participant searchFilter\n    participant tagFilter\n    participant favFilter\n    participant groupFilter\n    participant folderFilter\n    \n    Caller-\u003e\u003eFilterHelper: applyFilters(entities)\n    \n    FilterHelper-\u003e\u003eFilterHelper: Check hasAnyFilter()\n    \n    FilterHelper-\u003e\u003esearchFilter: filterFunctions[FILTER_TYPES.SEARCH]\n    searchFilter-\u003e\u003esearchFilter: fuzzySearchCharacters(data, term)\n    searchFilter-\u003e\u003eFilterHelper: Filtered by search\n    \n    FilterHelper-\u003e\u003etagFilter: filterFunctions[FILTER_TYPES.TAG]\n    tagFilter-\u003e\u003etagFilter: Check selected/excluded tags\n    tagFilter-\u003e\u003etagFilter: isElementTagged(entity, tagId)\n    tagFilter-\u003e\u003eFilterHelper: Filtered by tags\n    \n    FilterHelper-\u003e\u003efavFilter: filterFunctions[FILTER_TYPES.FAV]\n    favFilter-\u003e\u003efavFilter: Check entity.item.fav\n    favFilter-\u003e\u003eFilterHelper: Filtered by favorite\n    \n    FilterHelper-\u003e\u003egroupFilter: filterFunctions[FILTER_TYPES.GROUP]\n    groupFilter-\u003e\u003egroupFilter: Check entity.type === 'group'\n    groupFilter-\u003e\u003eFilterHelper: Filtered by group\n    \n    FilterHelper-\u003e\u003efolderFilter: filterFunctions[FILTER_TYPES.FOLDER]\n    folderFilter-\u003e\u003efolderFilter: isBogusFolder(entity.item)\n    folderFilter-\u003e\u003eFilterHelper: Filtered by folder\n    \n    FilterHelper-\u003e\u003eCaller: Final filtered data\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/filters.js:265-294](), [public/scripts/filters.js:144-152](), [public/scripts/filters.js:225-258]()\n\n### Three-State Tag Filter Logic\n\nTags in filter contexts support three distinct states managed through `toggleTagThreeState()`:\n\n```mermaid\nstateDiagram-v2\n    [*] --\u003e UNDEFINED: Initial state\n    UNDEFINED --\u003e SELECTED: Click once\n    SELECTED --\u003e EXCLUDED: Click twice\n    EXCLUDED --\u003e UNDEFINED: Click third time\n    \n    note right of UNDEFINED: No filtering\u003cbr/\u003eFILTER_STATES.UNDEFINED\u003cbr/\u003eGrey appearance\n    note right of SELECTED: Show only tagged\u003cbr/\u003eFILTER_STATES.SELECTED\u003cbr/\u003eFull opacity\n    note right of EXCLUDED: Hide tagged\u003cbr/\u003eFILTER_STATES.EXCLUDED\u003cbr/\u003eRed X overlay\n```\n\nThe `toggleTagThreeState()` function ([public/scripts/tags.js:1060-1103]()) cycles through states:\n\n1. **UNDEFINED** (`FILTER_STATES.UNDEFINED`): Tag has no effect on filtering (default state)\n2. **SELECTED** (`FILTER_STATES.SELECTED`): Only show entities with this tag (`filterData[FILTER_TYPES.TAG].selected[]`)\n3. **EXCLUDED** (`FILTER_STATES.EXCLUDED`): Hide entities with this tag (`filterData[FILTER_TYPES.TAG].excluded[]`)\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Tag Filter State Management\"\n        ToggleFunc[\"toggleTagThreeState(tagElement, options)\"]\n        \n        ToggleFunc --\u003e ReadCurrent[\"Read current state\u003cbr/\u003efrom filterData\"]\n        ReadCurrent --\u003e Cycle[\"Cycle to next state\u003cbr/\u003eUNDEFINED → SELECTED → EXCLUDED\"]\n        Cycle --\u003e UpdateClass[\"Update CSS classes\u003cbr/\u003e(selected, excluded)\"]\n        UpdateClass --\u003e UpdateHelper[\"filterHelper.setFilterData(\u003cbr/\u003e  FILTER_TYPES.TAG,\u003cbr/\u003e  {selected: [], excluded: []}\u003cbr/\u003e)\"]\n        UpdateHelper --\u003e Trigger[\"filterHelper.onDataChanged()\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:1060-1103](), [public/scripts/filters.js:35-42](), [public/scripts/filters.js:225-258]()\n\n### Tag Filter Implementation\n\nThe tag filtering logic in `FilterHelper.tagFilter()` handles both inclusion and exclusion:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    tagFilter[\"FilterHelper.tagFilter(data)\"]\n    \n    tagFilter --\u003e GetFilterData[\"filterData = this.filterData[FILTER_TYPES.TAG]\"]\n    GetFilterData --\u003e CheckEmpty{\"selected.length || excluded.length\"}\n    CheckEmpty --\u003e |false| ReturnAll[\"Return data unchanged\"]\n    \n    CheckEmpty --\u003e |true| IterateEntities[\"For each entity in data\"]\n    \n    IterateEntities --\u003e IsTag{\"entity.type === 'tag'?\"}\n    IsTag --\u003e |yes| IncludeTag[\"Include tag entity\"]\n    \n    IsTag --\u003e |no| CheckSelected[\"Check if tagged with\u003cbr/\u003eselected tags\"]\n    CheckSelected --\u003e LogicMode{\"TAG_LOGIC_AND\"}\n    \n    LogicMode --\u003e |true| AndLogic[\"Must have ALL\u003cbr/\u003eselected tags\"]\n    LogicMode --\u003e |false| OrLogic[\"Must have ANY\u003cbr/\u003eselected tag\"]\n    \n    AndLogic --\u003e CheckExcluded[\"Check excluded tags\"]\n    OrLogic --\u003e CheckExcluded\n    \n    CheckExcluded --\u003e HasExcluded{\"Has excluded tag?\"}\n    HasExcluded --\u003e |yes| Exclude[\"Exclude entity\"]\n    HasExcluded --\u003e |no| Include[\"Include entity\"]\n    \n    Include --\u003e ReturnFiltered[\"Return filtered array\"]\n    Exclude --\u003e ReturnFiltered\n```\n\nThe `isElementTagged()` helper ([public/scripts/filters.js:212-218]()) checks tag membership:\n- For characters: looks up `tag_map[entity.item.avatar]`\n- For groups: looks up `tag_map[String(entity.id)]`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/filters.js:225-258](), [public/scripts/filters.js:212-218]()\n\n### Filter Context Isolation\n\nEach FilterHelper instance maintains independent state and supports context-specific persistence:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Filter Context Architecture\"\n        MainContext[\"entitiesFilter\u003cbr/\u003e(CHARACTER_FILTER_SELECTOR)\"]\n        CandContext[\"groupCandidatesFilter\u003cbr/\u003e(GROUP_FILTER_SELECTOR)\"]\n        MembContext[\"groupMembersFilter\u003cbr/\u003e(GROUP_MEMBERS_FILTER_SELECTOR)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Context-Specific Functions\"\n        GetContext[\"getFilterContext(filterHelper)\"]\n        GetHelper[\"getFilterHelper(listSelector)\"]\n        GetStorage[\"getFilterStorageKey(filterHelper)\"]\n        \n        GetContext --\u003e |returns| ContextInfo[\"{ selector, searchInput }\"]\n        GetHelper --\u003e |identifies by DOM| ReturnHelper[\"Appropriate FilterHelper\"]\n        GetStorage --\u003e |returns| StorageKey[\"Storage key prefix\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Persistence Keys\"\n        MainContext --\u003e CharListKeys[\"CharacterList_TagFilterState_FAV\u003cbr/\u003eCharacterList_TagFilterState_GROUP\u003cbr/\u003eCharacterList_TagFilterState_FOLDER\"]\n        CandContext --\u003e CandKeys[\"GroupCandidates_TagFilterState_FAV\u003cbr/\u003eGroupCandidates_TagFilterState_GROUP\u003cbr/\u003eGroupCandidates_TagFilterState_FOLDER\"]\n        MembContext --\u003e MembKeys[\"GroupMembers_TagFilterState_FAV\u003cbr/\u003eGroupMembers_TagFilterState_GROUP\u003cbr/\u003eGroupMembers_TagFilterState_FOLDER\"]\n        \n        CharListKeys --\u003e AccountStorage[\"accountStorage\"]\n        CandKeys --\u003e AccountStorage\n        MembKeys --\u003e AccountStorage\n    end\n```\n\nHelper functions for context management:\n- `getFilterContext(filterHelper)` ([public/scripts/tags.js:69-87]()): Returns selector and search input for a context\n- `getFilterHelper(listSelector)` ([public/scripts/tags.js:94-109]()): Determines which FilterHelper to use based on DOM location\n- `getFilterStorageKey(filterHelper)` ([public/scripts/tags.js:176-185]()): Returns persistence key prefix\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:69-87](), [public/scripts/tags.js:94-109](), [public/scripts/tags.js:176-185](), [public/scripts/tags.js:534-551]()\n\n### Actionable Tag Persistence\n\nActionable tag filters (FAV, GROUP, FOLDER) use the `applyActionableTagFilter()` helper:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant TagElement as \"Actionable Tag Element\"\n    participant Apply as \"applyActionableTagFilter()\"\n    participant Toggle as \"toggleTagThreeState()\"\n    participant Storage as \"accountStorage\"\n    participant Helper as \"FilterHelper\"\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eTagElement: Click FAV/GROUP/FOLDER tag\n    TagElement-\u003e\u003eApply: Call with (filterHelper, tag, filterType, storageKey)\n    Apply-\u003e\u003eToggle: toggleTagThreeState(this)\n    Toggle--\u003e\u003eApply: Returns new state\n    \n    Apply-\u003e\u003eStorage: setItem(contextStorageKey, state)\n    Note over Storage: Key: CharacterList_TagFilterState_FAV\n    \n    Apply-\u003e\u003etag: tag.filter_state = state\u003cbr/\u003e(if main character list)\n    \n    Apply-\u003e\u003eHelper: filterHelper.setFilterData(filterType, state)\n    Helper-\u003e\u003eHelper: Trigger onDataChanged()\n```\n\nThe persistence strategy:\n- **Per-context storage**: Each context stores actionable tag states independently\n- **Backward compatibility**: Main character list also updates `tag.filter_state` for legacy support\n- **Storage keys**: Combine context prefix + actionable filter key (e.g., `CharacterList_TagFilterState_FAV`)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:534-551](), [public/scripts/tags.js:165-169](), [public/scripts/tags.js:587-612]()\n\n### Filter State Reading\n\nWhen rendering tag filters, the system reads persisted state through `determineTagFilterState()`:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Determine[\"determineTagFilterState(\u003cbr/\u003e  filterHelper,\u003cbr/\u003e  tag,\u003cbr/\u003e  isFilterActionable\u003cbr/\u003e)\"]\n    \n    Determine --\u003e CheckActionable{\"isFilterActionable?\"}\n    \n    CheckActionable --\u003e |yes| GetFilterType[\"Get filterType from\u003cbr/\u003eTAG_ID_TO_FILTER_TYPE\"]\n    GetFilterType --\u003e ReadHelper[\"filterHelper.getFilterData(filterType)\"]\n    ReadHelper --\u003e ReturnState1[\"Return state or\u003cbr/\u003eDEFAULT_FILTER_STATE\"]\n    \n    CheckActionable --\u003e |no| GetTagData[\"Get filterHelper.getFilterData(\u003cbr/\u003e  FILTER_TYPES.TAG\u003cbr/\u003e)\"]\n    GetTagData --\u003e CheckExcluded{\"tag.id in excluded?\"}\n    CheckExcluded --\u003e |yes| ReturnExcluded[\"Return 'EXCLUDED'\"]\n    \n    CheckExcluded --\u003e |no| CheckSelected{\"tag.id in selected?\"}\n    CheckSelected --\u003e |yes| ReturnSelected[\"Return 'SELECTED'\"]\n    CheckSelected --\u003e |no| ReturnUndefined[\"Return DEFAULT_FILTER_STATE\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:562-581](), [public/scripts/tags.js:281-285]()\n\n---\n\n## Tag Organization Features\n\n### Bogus Folder Drilldown Navigation\n\nWhen a bogus folder is selected, a \"breadcrumb\" navigation appears showing the folder hierarchy:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Folder Drilldown UI\"\n        Drilldown[\"rm_tag_bogus_drilldown\"]\n        \n        Drilldown --\u003e BackButton[\"Back button\u003cbr/\u003e(tag with 'back' id)\"]\n        Drilldown --\u003e FolderChain[\"Folder tags chain\u003cbr/\u003e(separated by \u003e icon)\"]\n        \n        BackButton --\u003e ClickBack[\"chooseBogusFolder(source, 'back', true)\"]\n        ClickBack --\u003e RemoveLast[\"Remove last folder\u003cbr/\u003efrom filter\"]\n        \n        FolderChain --\u003e ClickFolder[\"chooseBogusFolder(source, tagId)\"]\n        ClickFolder --\u003e SimulateClick[\"Simulate click on\u003cbr/\u003efilter tag element\"]\n        SimulateClick --\u003e UpdateFilter[\"toggleTagThreeState()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Folder Display Logic\"\n        GetOpen[\"getOpenBogusFolders()\"]\n        GetOpen --\u003e FilterSelected[\"Get selected tags\u003cbr/\u003efrom FILTER_TYPES.TAG\"]\n        FilterSelected --\u003e FilterFolders[\"Filter for isBogusFolder()\"]\n        FilterFolders --\u003e BuildUI[\"Build drilldown UI\u003cbr/\u003ein printTagFilters()\"]\n    end\n```\n\nThe drilldown UI is rendered in `printTagFilters()` (lines 1441-1522) and provides:\n- A \"back\" button to navigate up one level\n- Clickable folder tags showing the current navigation path\n- CSS styling with chevron separators between folder levels\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:456-460](), [public/scripts/tags.js:478-494](), [public/scripts/tags.js:1441-1522]()\n\n### Tag Sorting\n\nTags support multiple sort modes:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Sort Modes\"\n        SortMode[\"power_user.tags_sort_mode\"]\n        \n        SortMode --\u003e |MANUAL| Manual[\"Custom sort_order\u003cbr/\u003eUser-defined positions\"]\n        SortMode --\u003e |ALPHABETICAL| Alpha[\"Sort by name\u003cbr/\u003eCase-insensitive\"]\n        SortMode --\u003e |BY_ENTRIES| ByCount[\"Sort by count\u003cbr/\u003eMost used first\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Sort Implementation\"\n        CompareFunc[\"compareTagsForSort()\"]\n        \n        CompareFunc --\u003e CheckManual{\"mode === MANUAL?\"}\n        CheckManual --\u003e |yes| CompareSortOrder[\"Compare sort_order\u003cbr/\u003ethen name\"]\n        CheckManual --\u003e |no| CheckAlpha{\"mode === ALPHABETICAL?\"}\n        \n        CheckAlpha --\u003e |yes| CompareName[\"localeCompare names\"]\n        CheckAlpha --\u003e |no| CompareCount[\"Compare count\u003cbr/\u003ethen name\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Sort Application\"\n        SortTags[\"sortTags(tags)\"]\n        GetTagsList[\"getTagsList(key, sort=true)\"]\n        PrintTagList[\"printTagList(selector, options)\"]\n        \n        SortTags --\u003e Apply[\"Apply compareTagsForSort()\"]\n        GetTagsList --\u003e Apply\n        PrintTagList --\u003e Apply\n    end\n```\n\nThe `compareTagsForSort()` function (lines 2034-2051) implements the comparison logic. Tags can be manually reordered in the tag view popup, which updates their `sort_order` property.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:252-257](), [public/scripts/tags.js:2034-2051](), [public/scripts/tags.js:2019-2032](), [public/scripts/tags.js:640-655]()\n\n### Folder Block Rendering\n\nWhen displaying the character list with folders, each folder appears as a card showing:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Bogus Folder Block (getTagBlock)\"\n        FolderTemplate[\"FOLDER_TEMPLATE.clone()\"]\n        \n        FolderTemplate --\u003e Avatar[\"Avatar with folder icon\u003cbr/\u003eColor-coded background\"]\n        FolderTemplate --\u003e Name[\"Folder name\"]\n        FolderTemplate --\u003e Counter[\"Entity count\u003cbr/\u003e'X characters'\"]\n        FolderTemplate --\u003e HiddenCounter[\"Hidden count\u003cbr/\u003e'X hidden' (if any)\"]\n        FolderTemplate --\u003e AvatarList[\"Inline avatar previews\u003cbr/\u003ebuildAvatarList()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Folder States\"\n        OpenFolder[\"folder_open class\u003cbr/\u003eGreen folder-open icon\"]\n        ClosedFolder[\"folder_closed class\u003cbr/\u003eYellow eye-slash icon\"]\n    end\n```\n\nThe `getTagBlock()` function (lines 505-524) builds the HTML for folder display cards. It includes:\n- Icon indicating folder state (open/closed)\n- Count of visible entities\n- Count of hidden entities (in closed folders)\n- Inline avatar preview of contained entities via `buildAvatarList()`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:505-524]()\n\n---\n\n## UI Components and Rendering\n\n### Tag Rendering Pipeline\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Tag List Rendering\"\n        PrintTagList[\"printTagList(listSelector, options)\"]\n        \n        PrintTagList --\u003e GetTags[\"Get tags via\u003cbr/\u003egetTagsList(key, sort)\"]\n        PrintTagList --\u003e Sort[\"Sort tags\u003cbr/\u003ecompareTagsForSort()\"]\n        PrintTagList --\u003e ClearList[\"Clear existing UI\u003cbr/\u003e(if not appending)\"]\n        PrintTagList --\u003e IterateTags[\"For each tag\"]\n        \n        IterateTags --\u003e CreateTemplate[\"Clone TAG_TEMPLATE\"]\n        CreateTemplate --\u003e SetId[\"Set tag id attribute\"]\n        CreateTemplate --\u003e SetColors[\"Apply color/color2\"]\n        CreateTemplate --\u003e SetName[\"Set tag_name text\"]\n        CreateTemplate --\u003e AddRemove[\"Add tag_remove button\u003cbr/\u003e(if removable)\"]\n        \n        AddRemove --\u003e ExpandLogic{\"Need expansion?\"}\n        ExpandLogic --\u003e |yes| AddExpander[\"Add 'more tags' indicator\"]\n        ExpandLogic --\u003e |no| AppendTag[\"Append to list\"]\n        \n        AppendTag --\u003e AttachHandlers[\"Attach click handlers\"]\n    end\n```\n\nThe `printTagList()` function (lines 1321-1439) is the primary rendering function. It accepts options for:\n- `removable`: Whether to show remove buttons\n- `selectable`: Whether tags are clickable\n- `addable`: Whether to show an \"add tag\" input\n- `reorderable`: Whether tags can be drag-reordered\n- `skipExisting`: Whether to skip tags already in the list\n- `maxTag`: Maximum tags to show before truncation\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:1321-1439]()\n\n### Inline Tag Display\n\nInline tags appear on character/group cards:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Inline Tag Rendering\"\n        TagsInline[\"tags.tags_inline container\"]\n        \n        TagsInline --\u003e PrintInline[\"printTagList(selector, {\u003cbr/\u003e  removable: false,\u003cbr/\u003e  selectable: false\u003cbr/\u003e})\"]\n        \n        PrintInline --\u003e SmallSize[\"Smaller font size\u003cbr/\u003e(mainFontSize - 15%)\"]\n        PrintInline --\u003e MinimalPadding[\"Minimal padding\u003cbr/\u003e(0 0.15rem)\"]\n        PrintInline --\u003e OverflowHidden[\"max-height: 66%\u003cbr/\u003eoverflow: hidden\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Expansion Behavior\"\n        CheckCache[\"Check expanded_tags_cache\"]\n        CheckCache --\u003e |cached| ShowAll[\"Show all tags\"]\n        CheckCache --\u003e |not cached| CheckLimit[\"Check tag count vs maxTags\"]\n        \n        CheckLimit --\u003e |exceeds| ShowExpander[\"Show 'more tags' button\"]\n        ShowExpander --\u003e ClickExpand[\"Click expander\"]\n        ClickExpand --\u003e AddToCache[\"Add to expanded_tags_cache\"]\n        AddToCache --\u003e Rerender[\"Re-render with all tags\"]\n    end\n```\n\nInline tags use the `.tags_inline` CSS class (tags.css lines 134-142) and have reduced size and padding. If there are more than `MAX_TAGS` (default 7), a \"show more\" indicator appears.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:1321-1439](), [public/css/tags.css:134-156]()\n\n### Tag Filter Controls\n\nThe tag filter UI appears in character lists and group member lists:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Filter UI (printTagFilters)\"\n        FilterControls[\"rm_tag_filter container\"]\n        \n        FilterControls --\u003e Actionable[\"Render actionable tags\u003cbr/\u003e(FAV, GROUP, FOLDER, etc.)\"]\n        FilterControls --\u003e Drilldown[\"Render bogus folder drilldown\"]\n        FilterControls --\u003e Regular[\"Render regular tag filters\"]\n        \n        Actionable --\u003e ActionableIcon[\"Circular icon buttons\"]\n        Regular --\u003e ThreeState[\"Three-state toggleable tags\"]\n        \n        ThreeState --\u003e Undefined[\"UNDEFINED: grey, opacity 0.6\"]\n        ThreeState --\u003e Selected[\"SELECTED: full opacity, 'selected' class\"]\n        ThreeState --\u003e Excluded[\"EXCLUDED: red border, X overlay\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"State Visual Indicators\"\n        CSS_Undefined[\"opacity: 0.6\u003cbr/\u003efilter: brightness(0.8)\"]\n        CSS_Selected[\"opacity: 1\u003cbr/\u003efilter: none\"]\n        CSS_Excluded[\"opacity: 1\u003cbr/\u003efilter: saturate(0.4)\u003cbr/\u003eborder: 1px solid red\u003cbr/\u003e::after X overlay\"]\n    end\n```\n\nThe `printTagFilters()` function (lines 1441-1522) renders the filter controls. It:\n1. Gets visible tags that have associated entities\n2. Filters out actionable tags based on context (group contexts hide GROUP/FOLDER)\n3. Adds actionable tags as circular icon buttons\n4. Adds regular tags as three-state toggleable elements\n5. Renders the bogus folder drilldown navigation if folders are open\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:1441-1522](), [public/css/tags.css:169-216]()\n\n### Tag View Management Popup\n\nThe tag management popup (`#tag_view_list`) provides full CRUD operations:\n\n| Feature | Implementation | Function |\n|---------|----------------|----------|\n| Create tag | Input field + Add button | `onTagCreateClick()` |\n| Rename tag | Click name, edit inline | `onTagRenameClick()` |\n| Delete tag | Trash icon button | `onTagDeleteClick()` |\n| Set colors | Color picker (background/foreground) | `onTagColorPicker2Change()` |\n| Set as folder | Folder type buttons | `onTagAsFolderClick()` |\n| Reorder | Drag and drop | Sortable with `onTagsSorted()` |\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    OpenPopup[\"Click 'Manage tags' button\u003cbr/\u003e(ACTIONABLE_TAGS.VIEW)\"]\n    \n    OpenPopup --\u003e Render[\"onViewTagsListClick()\"]\n    Render --\u003e BuildList[\"Build tag_view_item elements\"]\n    \n    BuildList --\u003e NameButton[\"tag_view_name (clickable)\"]\n    BuildList --\u003e Counter[\"tag_view_counter (entity count)\"]\n    BuildList --\u003e ColorPicker[\"Color pickers (bg/fg)\"]\n    BuildList --\u003e FolderButtons[\"Folder type buttons\u003cbr/\u003e(OPEN/CLOSED/NONE)\"]\n    BuildList --\u003e DeleteButton[\"Delete button\"]\n    \n    NameButton --\u003e RenameInput[\"Inline text edit\"]\n    FolderButtons --\u003e UpdateFolderType[\"Update tag.folder_type\"]\n    DeleteButton --\u003e ConfirmDelete[\"Popup confirmation\"]\n    \n    UpdateFolderType --\u003e SaveSettings[\"saveSettingsDebounced()\"]\n    ConfirmDelete --\u003e RemoveTag[\"deleteTag(tagId)\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:1589-1748](), [public/scripts/tags.js:1766-1801](), [public/scripts/tags.js:1803-1818](), [public/scripts/tags.js:1820-1903]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration Points\n\n### Character List Integration\n\nThe character list filtering pipeline coordinates FilterHelper with tag-specific logic:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"printCharacters() Pipeline\"\n        Start[\"printCharacters()\"]\n        \n        Start --\u003e BuildEntities[\"Build entities array\u003cbr/\u003e(characters + groups + tags)\"]\n        BuildEntities --\u003e ApplyFilters[\"entitiesFilter.applyFilters(entities)\"]\n        \n        ApplyFilters --\u003e SearchFilter[\"FILTER_TYPES.SEARCH\u003cbr/\u003efuzzySearchCharacters()\"]\n        SearchFilter --\u003e TagFilter[\"FILTER_TYPES.TAG\u003cbr/\u003etagFilter()\"]\n        TagFilter --\u003e FavFilter[\"FILTER_TYPES.FAV\u003cbr/\u003efavFilter()\"]\n        FavFilter --\u003e GroupFilter[\"FILTER_TYPES.GROUP\u003cbr/\u003egroupFilter()\"]\n        GroupFilter --\u003e FolderFilter[\"FILTER_TYPES.FOLDER\u003cbr/\u003efolderFilter()\"]\n        \n        FolderFilter --\u003e FilterByState[\"filterByTagState(entities, {\u003cbr/\u003e  globalDisplayFilters: true\u003cbr/\u003e})\"]\n        \n        FilterByState --\u003e CheckClosedFolders[\"Find closedFolders\u003cbr/\u003e(folder_type === CLOSED)\"]\n        CheckClosedFolders --\u003e HideInClosed[\"Hide entities in\u003cbr/\u003eclosed folders unless\u003cbr/\u003efolder is selected\"]\n        HideInClosed --\u003e HideEmpty[\"Hide empty folders\u003cbr/\u003e(unless searching)\"]\n        \n        HideEmpty --\u003e Render[\"Render filtered entities\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Character Card Tags\"\n        CharCard[\"character_select element\"]\n        CharCard --\u003e InlineTags[\".tags.tags_inline\"]\n        \n        InlineTags --\u003e GetCharTags[\"getTagsList(character.avatar)\"]\n        GetCharTags --\u003e PrintInline[\"printTagList(selector, {\u003cbr/\u003e  removable: false,\u003cbr/\u003e  selectable: false\u003cbr/\u003e})\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Tag Filter UI\"\n        FilterPanel[\"#rm_characters_block .rm_tag_filter\"]\n        FilterPanel --\u003e PrintFilters[\"printTagFilters(type, entities, options)\"]\n        PrintFilters --\u003e RenderActionable[\"Render ACTIONABLE_TAGS\"]\n        PrintFilters --\u003e RenderDrilldown[\"Render bogus folder drilldown\"]\n        PrintFilters --\u003e RenderRegular[\"Render regular tag filters\"]\n        \n        RenderRegular --\u003e AttachClick[\"Attach click handler\"]\n        AttachClick --\u003e ToggleState[\"toggleTagThreeState()\"]\n        ToggleState --\u003e UpdateHelper[\"filterHelper.setFilterData()\"]\n        UpdateHelper --\u003e Callback[\"onDataChanged callback\"]\n        Callback --\u003e Refilter[\"Re-run printCharacters()\"]\n    end\n```\n\nThe `filterByTagState()` function ([public/scripts/tags.js:369-408]()) applies tag-specific display logic:\n- Removes already-filtered folders from the entity list\n- Hides entities in closed folders (unless the folder is selected in `filterData.selected`)\n- Hides empty folders (unless `FILTER_TYPES.SEARCH` has a value)\n- When displaying a folder's contents (`subForEntity` option), calls `filterTagSubEntities()`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:369-408](), [public/scripts/tags.js:56](), [public/scripts/tags.js:1441-1522](), [public/scripts/tags.js:1060-1103]()\n\n### Group Integration\n\nGroups use separate FilterHelper instances for member management, with context-specific UI adaptations:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Group Tag Storage\"\n        GroupObj[\"groups[i]\"]\n        GroupObj --\u003e GroupId[\"id: String (e.g., 'group_12345')\"]\n        \n        TagMap[\"tag_map Object\"]\n        TagMap --\u003e Entry[\"tag_map['group_12345'] = [tagId1, tagId2]\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Group Member Filter Contexts\"\n        GroupChatsJS[\"group-chats.js exports\"]\n        GroupChatsJS --\u003e CandFilter[\"groupCandidatesFilter\u003cbr/\u003e(FilterHelper instance)\"]\n        GroupChatsJS --\u003e MembFilter[\"groupMembersFilter\u003cbr/\u003e(FilterHelper instance)\"]\n        \n        CandFilter --\u003e CandUI[\"#rm_group_add_members\u003cbr/\u003e#unaddedCharList\"]\n        MembFilter --\u003e MembUI[\"#rm_group_members\u003cbr/\u003e#currentGroupMembers\"]\n        \n        CandUI --\u003e CandSelector[\"GROUP_FILTER_SELECTOR\u003cbr/\u003e#rm_group_filter\"]\n        MembUI --\u003e MembSelector[\"GROUP_MEMBERS_FILTER_SELECTOR\u003cbr/\u003e#rm_group_members_filter\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Context Detection\"\n        GetHelper[\"getFilterHelper(listSelector)\"]\n        GetHelper --\u003e CheckParent[\"Check .closest() for:\u003cbr/\u003e#currentGroupMembers\u003cbr/\u003e#unaddedCharList\"]\n        CheckParent --\u003e Return[\"Return appropriate\u003cbr/\u003eFilterHelper instance\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Actionable Tag Filtering\"\n        FilterTags[\"filterActionableTagsForGroupContext()\"]\n        FilterTags --\u003e KeepFav[\"Keep ACTIONABLE_TAGS.FAV\"]\n        FilterTags --\u003e KeepUtil[\"Keep VIEW/HINT/UNFILTER\"]\n        FilterTags --\u003e HideGrpFolder[\"Hide GROUP and FOLDER\u003cbr/\u003e(not relevant in group contexts)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Visible Avatars\"\n        GetVisible[\"getVisibleAvatarsForGroupContext(\u003cbr/\u003e  type,\u003cbr/\u003e  currentGroup\u003cbr/\u003e)\"]\n        GetVisible --\u003e |group_members_list| ReturnMembers[\"currentGroup.members\"]\n        GetVisible --\u003e |group_candidates_list| ReturnNonMembers[\"characters.filter(\u003cbr/\u003e  !in currentGroup.members\u003cbr/\u003e)\"]\n    end\n```\n\nKey differences for group contexts:\n- `isGroupContext(type)` ([public/scripts/tags.js:116-118]()): Checks if type is `group_candidates_list` or `group_members_list`\n- `filterActionableTagsForGroupContext()` ([public/scripts/tags.js:150-163]()): Hides GROUP and FOLDER actionable tags\n- `getVisibleAvatarsForGroupContext()` ([public/scripts/tags.js:126-142]()): Returns visible character avatars based on group membership\n- Each context maintains independent filter state that persists to `accountStorage` with context-specific keys\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:94-109](), [public/scripts/tags.js:116-118](), [public/scripts/tags.js:126-142](), [public/scripts/tags.js:150-163]()\n\n### Persona Integration\n\nPersonas use avatar filenames as tag map keys, similar to characters:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Persona Tag Keys\"\n        PersonaAvatar[\"user_avatar value\u003cbr/\u003e(e.g., 'persona.png')\"]\n        \n        TagMapEntry[\"tag_map['persona.png']\u003cbr/\u003e= [tagId1, tagId2]\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Persona Display\"\n        PersonaBlock[\"Persona avatar block\"]\n        PersonaBlock --\u003e GetPersonaTags[\"getTagsList('persona.png')\"]\n        GetPersonaTags --\u003e RenderTags[\"printTagList(selector)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Persona Filtering\"\n        PersonaList[\"User avatar list\"]\n        PersonaList --\u003e PersonasFilter[\"personasFilter\u003cbr/\u003e(FilterHelper instance)\"]\n        PersonasFilter --\u003e ApplyFilters[\"Apply tag filters\"]\n        ApplyFilters --\u003e FilteredList[\"Filtered persona list\"]\n    end\n```\n\nPersonas have a dedicated `FilterHelper` instance (`personasFilter`) defined in personas.js that applies tag filtering to the persona list independently from character/group filtering.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/personas.js:72](), [public/scripts/tags.js:691-722]()\n\n### Search Integration\n\nTag filtering integrates with text search filtering:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Combined Filtering\"\n        FilterHelper[\"FilterHelper.applyFilters(data)\"]\n        \n        FilterHelper --\u003e SearchFilter[\"searchFilter()\u003cbr/\u003e(fuzzy search)\"]\n        FilterHelper --\u003e TagFilter[\"tagFilter()\u003cbr/\u003e(tag matching)\"]\n        FilterHelper --\u003e FavFilter[\"favFilter()\"]\n        FilterHelper --\u003e GroupFilter[\"groupFilter()\"]\n        FilterHelper --\u003e FolderFilter[\"folderFilter()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Filter Order\"\n        Search[\"1. FILTER_TYPES.SEARCH\"]\n        Search --\u003e Tag[\"2. FILTER_TYPES.TAG\"]\n        Tag --\u003e Fav[\"3. FILTER_TYPES.FAV\"]\n        Fav --\u003e Group[\"4. FILTER_TYPES.GROUP\"]\n        Group --\u003e Folder[\"5. FILTER_TYPES.FOLDER\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Special Search Behavior\"\n        EmptyFolders[\"Empty folders hidden\"]\n        SearchActive{\"Search term active?\"}\n        SearchActive --\u003e |yes| ShowEmptyFolders[\"Show matching\u003cbr/\u003eempty folders\"]\n        SearchActive --\u003e |no| HideEmptyFolders[\"Hide empty folders\"]\n    end\n```\n\nWhen a search term is active, the system shows empty folders that match the search term, even though they contain no visible characters. This allows users to find folders by name.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:369-408](), [public/scripts/filters.js:144-152]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"2c:T4653,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Personas and User Profiles\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [public/css/backgrounds.css](public/css/backgrounds.css)\n- [public/css/mobile-styles.css](public/css/mobile-styles.css)\n- [public/css/select2-overrides.css](public/css/select2-overrides.css)\n- [public/css/st-tailwind.css](public/css/st-tailwind.css)\n- [public/css/tags.css](public/css/tags.css)\n- [public/css/toggle-dependent.css](public/css/toggle-dependent.css)\n- [public/css/world-info.css](public/css/world-info.css)\n- [public/scripts/a11y.js](public/scripts/a11y.js)\n- [public/scripts/backgrounds.js](public/scripts/backgrounds.js)\n- [public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js](public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js)\n- [public/scripts/filters.js](public/scripts/filters.js)\n- [public/scripts/keyboard.js](public/scripts/keyboard.js)\n- [public/scripts/personas.js](public/scripts/personas.js)\n- [public/scripts/sse-stream.js](public/scripts/sse-stream.js)\n- [public/scripts/tags.js](public/scripts/tags.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page documents the persona system in SillyTavern, which allows users to define multiple user profiles (personas) with distinct names, avatars, and descriptions. Personas can be selected globally or locked to specific chats or characters, enabling role-playing scenarios where the user represents different characters in different contexts.\n\nFor character-side profile information, see Character Management ([5.1](#5.1)). For World Info entries that can be associated with personas, see World Info System ([6.1](#6.1)).\n\n---\n\n## Core Concepts\n\nA **persona** in SillyTavern consists of:\n\n- **Avatar ID**: A filename reference to an image file in the `User Avatars/` directory\n- **Display Name**: The name shown in chats (stored in `power_user.personas`)\n- **Description**: Optional text injected into the prompt context (stored in `power_user.persona_descriptions`)\n- **Metadata**: Position, depth, role, lorebook association, and title\n\nPersonas support three locking mechanisms:\n- **Default Persona**: Applied to all new chats unless overridden\n- **Character Lock**: Specific persona used when chatting with a character/group\n- **Chat Lock**: Specific persona locked to the current chat session\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:44-59](), [public/scripts/power-user.js:293-301]()\n\n---\n\n## Persona State Management\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Persona Storage\"\n        UA[\"user_avatar\u003cbr/\u003e(current selection)\"]\n        PP[\"power_user.personas\u003cbr/\u003e{avatarId: name}\"]\n        PD[\"power_user.persona_descriptions\u003cbr/\u003e{avatarId: descriptor}\"]\n        DP[\"power_user.default_persona\u003cbr/\u003e(avatar ID)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Persona Descriptor\"\n        Desc[\"description: string\"]\n        Pos[\"position: enum\"]\n        Depth[\"depth: number\"]\n        Role[\"role: enum\"]\n        LB[\"lorebook: string\"]\n        Title[\"title: string\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Lock State\"\n        ChatLock[\"chat_metadata.persona\"]\n        CharLock[\"character.data.extensions.persona\"]\n        GroupLock[\"group.persona\"]\n    end\n    \n    UA --\u003e|\"references\"| PP\n    UA --\u003e|\"references\"| PD\n    PD --\u003e|\"contains\"| Desc\n    PD --\u003e|\"contains\"| Pos\n    PD --\u003e|\"contains\"| Depth\n    PD --\u003e|\"contains\"| Role\n    PD --\u003e|\"contains\"| LB\n    PD --\u003e|\"contains\"| Title\n    \n    ChatLock --\u003e|\"overrides\"| CharLock\n    CharLock --\u003e|\"overrides\"| DP\n    DP --\u003e|\"fallback\"| UA\n```\n\n**Diagram: Persona State Structure**\n\nThe active persona is resolved through a hierarchy:\n1. Chat-specific lock (`chat_metadata.persona`)\n2. Character/group-specific lock (`extensions.persona`)\n3. Default persona (`power_user.default_persona`)\n4. Current selection (`user_avatar`)\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:52-59](), [public/scripts/personas.js:577-650]()\n\n---\n\n## Persona Selection Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant UI as Persona UI\n    participant Core as personas.js\n    participant PU as power_user\n    participant Chat as Chat System\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eUI: Click persona avatar\n    UI-\u003e\u003eCore: setUserAvatar(avatarId)\n    Core-\u003e\u003ePU: Update user_avatar\n    Core-\u003e\u003eUI: reloadUserAvatar()\n    UI-\u003e\u003eChat: Update .mes avatars\n    Core-\u003e\u003eUI: updatePersonaUIStates()\n    UI-\u003e\u003eUI: Highlight selected persona\n    Core-\u003e\u003eCore: selectCurrentPersona()\n    Core-\u003e\u003eChat: retriggerFirstMessageOnEmptyChat()\n    Core-\u003e\u003ePU: saveSettingsDebounced()\n```\n\n**Diagram: Persona Selection Sequence**\n\nThe `setUserAvatar` function [public/scripts/personas.js:115-127]() orchestrates the persona change, updating the global `user_avatar` variable, refreshing all user message avatars in the chat, and potentially retriggering the first message if the chat is empty.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:115-127](), [public/scripts/personas.js:129-140](), [public/scripts/personas.js:656-693]()\n\n---\n\n## Persona Description Injection\n\nPersona descriptions are injected into the prompt context based on their configured position:\n\n| Position | Enum Value | Description |\n|----------|-----------|-------------|\n| `IN_PROMPT` | 0 | Injected into story string/main prompt |\n| `TOP_AN` | 2 | Before Author's Note |\n| `BOTTOM_AN` | 3 | After Author's Note |\n| `AT_DEPTH` | 4 | At specified depth in chat history |\n| `NONE` | 9 | Not injected |\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Persona Description Flow\"\n        Get[\"getPersonaDescription(name)\"]\n        Check[\"Check position setting\"]\n        Inject[\"Inject into prompt\"]\n        \n        Get --\u003e Check\n        Check --\u003e|\"IN_PROMPT\"| SP[\"Story String\"]\n        Check --\u003e|\"TOP_AN\"| TAN[\"Before AN\"]\n        Check --\u003e|\"BOTTOM_AN\"| BAN[\"After AN\"]\n        Check --\u003e|\"AT_DEPTH\"| Depth[\"At depth N\"]\n        Check --\u003e|\"NONE\"| Skip[\"Skip injection\"]\n        \n        SP --\u003e Inject\n        TAN --\u003e Inject\n        BAN --\u003e Inject\n        Depth --\u003e Inject\n    end\n```\n\n**Diagram: Persona Description Injection Points**\n\nThe `getPersonaDescription` function [public/scripts/personas.js:695-739]() retrieves the description and expands macros. The description is then injected by the prompt construction system based on the `position` field.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:695-739](), [public/scripts/power-user.js:110-120](), [public/scripts/personas.js:741-778]()\n\n---\n\n## Persona Connections\n\nPersonas can be linked to specific characters or groups, creating automatic persona switches when those entities are selected.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Connection Structure\"\n        PC[\"PersonaConnection\"]\n        Type[\"type: 'character'|'group'\"]\n        ID[\"id: string\"]\n        \n        PC --\u003e|\"contains\"| Type\n        PC --\u003e|\"contains\"| ID\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Storage Locations\"\n        Char[\"character.data.extensions\u003cbr/\u003e.persona_connections\"]\n        Meta[\"chat_metadata\u003cbr/\u003e.persona_connections\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Lookup Process\"\n        Select[\"Character/Group Selected\"]\n        Find[\"findPersonaConnection()\"]\n        Switch[\"Switch to connected persona\"]\n        \n        Select --\u003e Find\n        Find --\u003e|\"query\"| Char\n        Find --\u003e|\"query\"| Meta\n        Find --\u003e Switch\n    end\n    \n    Char -.-\u003e|\"stored as\"| PC\n    Meta -.-\u003e|\"stored as\"| PC\n```\n\n**Diagram: Persona Connection Architecture**\n\nConnections are stored as arrays in `character.data.extensions.persona_connections` or `chat_metadata.persona_connections`. The `findPersonaConnection` function [public/scripts/personas.js:780-811]() searches these arrays when a character or group is selected.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:44-48](), [public/scripts/personas.js:780-811](), [public/scripts/personas.js:813-882]()\n\n---\n\n## Persona Management UI\n\nThe persona management interface is accessed via the \"User Settings\" button in the top bar and consists of several key components:\n\n| Component | Element ID | Function |\n|-----------|-----------|----------|\n| Persona Panel | `#persona-management-button` | Main drawer container |\n| Avatar Grid | `#user_avatar_block` | Displays all available personas |\n| Search Bar | `.personas_search` | Filters personas by name |\n| Sort Dropdown | `#persona_sort_order` | Controls display order |\n| Create Button | `#create_dummy_persona` | Creates new persona |\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Persona UI Components\"\n        Panel[\"#persona-management-button\u003cbr/\u003e(Drawer)\"]\n        Grid[\"#user_avatar_block\u003cbr/\u003e(Avatar Container)\"]\n        Template[\"#user_avatar_template\u003cbr/\u003e(Clone Source)\"]\n        Pagination[\"#persona_pagination_container\"]\n        \n        Panel --\u003e Grid\n        Grid --\u003e|\"clones\"| Template\n        Grid --\u003e|\"controlled by\"| Pagination\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Persona Block Structure\"\n        Block[\"div.avatar-container\"]\n        Avatar[\"img (avatar image)\"]\n        Name[\"div.ch_name\"]\n        Desc[\"div.ch_description\"]\n        Title[\"div.ch_additional_info\"]\n        \n        Block --\u003e Avatar\n        Block --\u003e Name\n        Block --\u003e Desc\n        Block --\u003e Title\n    end\n    \n    Grid --\u003e|\"appends\"| Block\n```\n\n**Diagram: Persona UI Component Hierarchy**\n\nThe `getUserAvatars` function [public/scripts/personas.js:234-309]() populates the grid by cloning the template for each persona. The `getUserAvatarBlock` function [public/scripts/personas.js:191-214]() configures each cloned element with the persona's data.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:191-214](), [public/scripts/personas.js:234-309]()\n\n---\n\n## Persona Operations\n\n### Creating Personas\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant Upload as Avatar Upload\n    participant Create as createPersona()\n    participant Init as initPersona()\n    participant API as /api/avatars/upload\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eUpload: Upload avatar image\n    Upload-\u003e\u003eAPI: POST with FormData\n    API--\u003e\u003eUpload: {path: avatarId}\n    Upload-\u003e\u003eCreate: createPersona(avatarId)\n    Create-\u003e\u003eUser: Prompt for name\n    Create-\u003e\u003eUser: Prompt for description\n    Create-\u003e\u003eInit: initPersona(avatarId, name, desc)\n    Init-\u003e\u003eInit: Add to power_user.personas\n    Init-\u003e\u003eInit: Add to persona_descriptions\n```\n\n**Diagram: Persona Creation Flow**\n\nTwo creation paths exist:\n1. **Avatar Upload**: User uploads an image via `changeUserAvatar` [public/scripts/personas.js:345-413](), which calls `createPersona` [public/scripts/personas.js:421-435]()\n2. **Dummy Persona**: User creates a persona without custom avatar via `createDummyPersona` [public/scripts/personas.js:437-458]()\n\nBoth paths converge at `initPersona` [public/scripts/personas.js:468-480](), which adds the persona to `power_user` settings.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:345-413](), [public/scripts/personas.js:421-458](), [public/scripts/personas.js:468-480]()\n\n### Editing Personas\n\nThe persona editor is a modal popup that allows editing all persona fields:\n\n| Field | Input Type | Storage Path |\n|-------|-----------|--------------|\n| Name | Text input | `power_user.personas[avatarId]` |\n| Description | Textarea | `power_user.persona_descriptions[avatarId].description` |\n| Title | Text input | `power_user.persona_descriptions[avatarId].title` |\n| Position | Dropdown | `power_user.persona_descriptions[avatarId].position` |\n| Depth | Number input | `power_user.persona_descriptions[avatarId].depth` |\n| Role | Dropdown | `power_user.persona_descriptions[avatarId].role` |\n| Lorebook | File selector | `power_user.persona_descriptions[avatarId].lorebook` |\n\nThe `openPersonaEditor` function [public/scripts/personas.js:884-1051]() renders the editor using the template at `#persona_description_template`.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:884-1051]()\n\n### Converting Characters to Personas\n\nUsers can convert existing characters into personas via `convertCharacterToPersona` [public/scripts/personas.js:494-551](). This:\n1. Extracts the character's avatar and description\n2. Swaps `{{char}}` and `{{user}}` macros if present\n3. Creates a new persona with the character's data\n4. Uploads the avatar with suffix `(Persona).png`\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:494-551]()\n\n---\n\n## Backend Integration\n\n### API Endpoints\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |\n|----------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/avatars/get` | POST | Retrieves list of all user avatars |\n| `/api/avatars/upload` | POST | Uploads a new avatar image |\n| `/api/avatars/delete` | POST | Deletes an avatar file |\n\nThe upload endpoint accepts an optional `crop` query parameter for avatar cropping and an `overwrite_name` form field to update existing avatars.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:235-244](), [public/scripts/personas.js:318-343]()\n\n### Storage Structure\n\nPersona data is stored in multiple locations:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"settings.json (power_user object)\"\n        Personas[\"personas: {avatarId: name}\"]\n        Descriptions[\"persona_descriptions: {avatarId: PersonaDescriptor}\"]\n        Default[\"default_persona: avatarId\"]\n        Current[\"persona_description: string\u003cbr/\u003e(legacy)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"User Avatars/ Directory\"\n        Files[\"*.png avatar files\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Chat/Character Metadata\"\n        ChatPersona[\"chat_metadata.persona\"]\n        CharPersona[\"character.data.extensions.persona\"]\n        Connections[\"*.persona_connections[]\"]\n    end\n    \n    Personas -.-\u003e|\"references\"| Files\n    Default -.-\u003e|\"references\"| Files\n    ChatPersona -.-\u003e|\"references\"| Files\n    CharPersona -.-\u003e|\"references\"| Files\n```\n\n**Diagram: Persona Data Storage Locations**\n\nThe `personas` and `persona_descriptions` objects in `power_user` provide the master registry. Individual chats and characters can override the active persona through their metadata.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/power-user.js:293-301](), [public/scripts/personas.js:68-69]()\n\n---\n\n## Persona Locking and Auto-Selection\n\n### Lock Hierarchy\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Start[\"Chat/Character Selected\"]\n    \n    CheckChat{\"chat_metadata.persona\u003cbr/\u003eexists?\"}\n    CheckChar{\"character/group\u003cbr/\u003e.persona exists?\"}\n    CheckDefault{\"default_persona\u003cbr/\u003eset?\"}\n    \n    ChatLock[\"Use chat-locked persona\"]\n    CharLock[\"Use character-locked persona\"]\n    DefaultLock[\"Use default persona\"]\n    NoChange[\"Keep current persona\"]\n    \n    Start --\u003e CheckChat\n    CheckChat --\u003e|\"Yes\"| ChatLock\n    CheckChat --\u003e|\"No\"| CheckChar\n    CheckChar --\u003e|\"Yes\"| CharLock\n    CheckChar --\u003e|\"No\"| CheckDefault\n    CheckDefault --\u003e|\"Yes\"| DefaultLock\n    CheckDefault --\u003e|\"No\"| NoChange\n```\n\n**Diagram: Persona Lock Resolution**\n\nThe `autoSelectPersona` function [public/scripts/personas.js:577-650]() implements this hierarchy. It's called on chat load and character selection events.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:577-650](), [public/scripts/personas.js:652-693]()\n\n### Setting Locks\n\n| Lock Type | Function | Storage Location |\n|-----------|----------|------------------|\n| Default | `setDefaultPersona()` | `power_user.default_persona` |\n| Character | `lockPersonaToEntity()` | `character.data.extensions.persona` |\n| Chat | `lockPersonaToChat()` | `chat_metadata.persona` |\n\nThe UI provides buttons to set/unset each lock type. The `updatePersonaUIStates` function [public/scripts/personas.js:1053-1132]() updates button states based on current locks.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:1134-1172](), [public/scripts/personas.js:1174-1203](), [public/scripts/personas.js:1053-1132]()\n\n---\n\n## Token Counting and Validation\n\nPersona descriptions contribute to the token budget. The system provides real-time token counting:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Edit[\"Edit persona description\"]\n    Debounce[\"Debounced handler\"]\n    Count[\"countPersonaDescriptionTokens()\"]\n    Display[\"Update token display\"]\n    \n    Edit --\u003e Debounce\n    Debounce --\u003e Count\n    Count --\u003e|\"getTokenCountAsync()\"| Display\n```\n\n**Diagram: Persona Description Token Counting**\n\nThe `countPersonaDescriptionTokens` function [public/scripts/personas.js:556-575]() uses the tokenizer system to count tokens and displays the result in the editor UI.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:556-575]()\n\n---\n\n## Slash Commands\n\nThe persona system registers several slash commands:\n\n| Command | Function | Description |\n|---------|----------|-------------|\n| `/persona` | `setPersonaCommand` | Sets active persona by name |\n| `/persona-description` | `getPersonaDescriptionCommand` | Retrieves persona description |\n\nThese are registered in `registerPersonaSlashCommands` [public/scripts/personas.js:1205-1299]().\n\nExample usage:\n```\n/persona Alice\n/persona-description name=Alice\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:1205-1299]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration with World Info\n\nPersonas can have associated World Info (lorebook) files. The `lorebook` field in `PersonaDescriptor` specifies the filename of a World Info file to load when the persona is active.\n\nThe `getOrCreatePersonaDescriptor` function [public/scripts/personas.js:1301-1318]() ensures a descriptor exists before attempting to modify it, creating default values if needed.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:1301-1318](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:1-26]()\n\n---\n\n## Event-Driven Architecture\n\nThe persona system emits and listens to several events:\n\n| Event | Trigger | Purpose |\n|-------|---------|---------|\n| `CHAT_CHANGED` | Chat loaded | Auto-select persona for chat |\n| `CHARACTER_SELECTED` | Character picked | Auto-select persona for character |\n| `USER_MESSAGE_RENDERED` | User message displayed | Update avatar in DOM |\n\nThe `autoSelectPersona` function subscribes to these events during `initPersonas` [public/scripts/personas.js:1320-1348]().\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:1320-1348](), [public/scripts/personas.js:577-650]()\n\n---\n\n## Default Values\n\n| Setting | Default Value | Constant |\n|---------|--------------|----------|\n| Description Position | `IN_PROMPT` (0) | `persona_description_positions.IN_PROMPT` |\n| Description Depth | 2 | `DEFAULT_DEPTH` |\n| Description Role | 0 | `DEFAULT_ROLE` |\n| Description Text | Empty string | - |\n| Title | Empty string | - |\n| Lorebook | Empty string | - |\n\nSources: [public/scripts/personas.js:65-66](), [public/scripts/power-user.js:110-120]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"2d:T813b,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Backgrounds and Theming\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [public/css/backgrounds.css](public/css/backgrounds.css)\n- [public/css/mobile-styles.css](public/css/mobile-styles.css)\n- [public/css/select2-overrides.css](public/css/select2-overrides.css)\n- [public/css/st-tailwind.css](public/css/st-tailwind.css)\n- [public/css/tags.css](public/css/tags.css)\n- [public/css/toggle-dependent.css](public/css/toggle-dependent.css)\n- [public/css/world-info.css](public/css/world-info.css)\n- [public/scripts/a11y.js](public/scripts/a11y.js)\n- [public/scripts/backgrounds.js](public/scripts/backgrounds.js)\n- [public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js](public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js)\n- [public/scripts/filters.js](public/scripts/filters.js)\n- [public/scripts/keyboard.js](public/scripts/keyboard.js)\n- [public/scripts/personas.js](public/scripts/personas.js)\n- [public/scripts/sse-stream.js](public/scripts/sse-stream.js)\n- [public/scripts/tags.js](public/scripts/tags.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document describes the background management system and theming architecture in SillyTavern. The background system (`backgrounds.js`) manages custom images displayed behind the chat interface, supporting both global and chat-specific backgrounds with lazy loading, thumbnail caching, and multiple fitting modes. The theming system controls visual appearance through CSS custom properties (`--SmartTheme*` variables), UI mode toggles (`toggle-dependent.css`), and dynamic focus style generation (`dynamic-styles.js`).\n\nFor UI component structure, see [Main UI Components and Layout](#4.1). For character-specific visual elements, see [Character Expressions System](#8.3). For power user settings, see [Power User Settings](page reference not provided in context).\n\n---\n\n## Background System Architecture\n\nThe background system manages images displayed behind the chat interface, supporting two distinct scopes: global backgrounds that apply across all chats, and chat-specific backgrounds that override the global setting for individual conversations.\n\n### Background Sources and Metadata\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Background State Management\"\n        BGSettings[\"background_settings\u003cbr/\u003e{name, url, fitting, animation, sortOrder}\"]\n        ChatMeta[\"chat_metadata\u003cbr/\u003e[BG_METADATA_KEY]\u003cbr/\u003eCustom background URL\"]\n        ListMeta[\"chat_metadata\u003cbr/\u003e[LIST_METADATA_KEY]\u003cbr/\u003eArray of chat background paths\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Background Sources\"\n        Global[\"BG_SOURCES.GLOBAL (0)\u003cbr/\u003eSystem backgrounds\u003cbr/\u003ebackgrounds/ directory\"]\n        Chat[\"BG_SOURCES.CHAT (1)\u003cbr/\u003eChat-specific backgrounds\u003cbr/\u003eCustom uploads\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Metadata Cache\"\n        MetaCache[\"METADATA_CACHE\u003cbr/\u003eMap\u003cstring, ImageMetadata\u003e\"]\n        MetaData[\"ImageMetadata:\u003cbr/\u003e- dominantColor\u003cbr/\u003e- aspectRatio\u003cbr/\u003e- addedTimestamp\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Thumbnail Cache\"\n        ThumbStorage[\"THUMBNAIL_STORAGE\u003cbr/\u003elocalforage instance\"]\n        ThumbBlobs[\"THUMBNAIL_BLOBS\u003cbr/\u003eMap\u003cstring, blobUrl\u003e\"]\n    end\n    \n    Global --\u003e BGSettings\n    Chat --\u003e ChatMeta\n    Chat --\u003e ListMeta\n    \n    Global --\u003e MetaCache\n    Chat --\u003e MetaCache\n    MetaCache --\u003e MetaData\n    \n    Global --\u003e ThumbStorage\n    Chat --\u003e ThumbStorage\n    ThumbStorage --\u003e ThumbBlobs\n    \n    BGSettings -.-\u003e|\"displayed as\"| BG1[\"#bg1\u003cbr/\u003eDOM element\u003cbr/\u003ebackground-image\"]\n    ChatMeta -.-\u003e|\"overrides\"| BG1\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/backgrounds.js:14-59](), [public/scripts/backgrounds.js:95-101]()\n\nThe system uses three storage mechanisms:\n\n| Storage | Purpose | Scope |\n|---------|---------|-------|\n| `background_settings` | Global background state (name, URL, fitting mode) | Global |\n| `chat_metadata[BG_METADATA_KEY]` | Chat-specific locked background URL | Per-chat |\n| `chat_metadata[LIST_METADATA_KEY]` | Array of custom backgrounds uploaded to chat | Per-chat |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/backgrounds.js:14-15]()\n\n### Background Selection Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant UI as \"Background Drawer UI\"\n    participant BGSystem as \"backgrounds.js\"\n    participant ChatMeta as \"chat_metadata\"\n    participant DOM as \"#bg1 Element\"\n    participant Server as \"Backend API\"\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eUI: Click background thumbnail\n    UI-\u003e\u003eBGSystem: onSelectBackgroundClick()\n    \n    alt Is Chat Background or Locked\n        BGSystem-\u003e\u003eChatMeta: saveBackgroundMetadata(url)\n        BGSystem-\u003e\u003eDOM: Set background-image\n        Note over BGSystem: Updates locked background\n    else Is Global Background\n        BGSystem-\u003e\u003eBGSystem: setBackground(file, url)\n        BGSystem-\u003e\u003eServer: Save settings\n        BGSystem-\u003e\u003eDOM: Set background-image\n        Note over BGSystem: Updates global background\n    end\n    \n    BGSystem-\u003e\u003eUI: highlightSelectedBackground()\n    BGSystem-\u003e\u003eUI: highlightLockedBackground()\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/backgrounds.js:327-345]()\n\n---\n\n## Background Storage and Persistence\n\n### File System Organization\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Server File Structure\"\n        DataRoot[\"DATA_ROOT/\"]\n        BgDir[\"backgrounds/\"]\n        UserDir[\"user_data/\u003chandle\u003e/\"]\n        ChatBg[\"Custom chat backgrounds\u003cbr/\u003e(paths in chat_metadata)\"]\n        \n        DataRoot --\u003e BgDir\n        DataRoot --\u003e UserDir\n        UserDir --\u003e ChatBg\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Frontend Storage\"\n        LocalForage[\"localforage\u003cbr/\u003eTHUMBNAIL_STORAGE\"]\n        ThumbCache[\"Thumbnail blobs\u003cbr/\u003e160x90 compressed\"]\n        MetaCache[\"METADATA_CACHE\u003cbr/\u003eImage metadata\"]\n        \n        LocalForage --\u003e ThumbCache\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Background URLs\"\n        SystemURL[\"backgrounds/\u003cfilename\u003e\"]\n        CustomURL[\"user_data/\u003chandle\u003e/\u003cpath\u003e\"]\n        CSSURL[\"url('...')\"]\n        \n        BgDir -.-\u003e|generates| SystemURL\n        ChatBg -.-\u003e|generates| CustomURL\n        SystemURL --\u003e CSSURL\n        CustomURL --\u003e CSSURL\n    end\n    \n    CSSURL -.-\u003e|applied to| DOM[\"#bg1 background-image\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/backgrounds.js:257-259](), [public/scripts/backgrounds.js:31-42]()\n\n### Background Loading and Caching\n\nThe system implements three-tier caching for backgrounds:\n\n1. **Blob URL Cache** (`THUMBNAIL_BLOBS`): In-memory cache of blob URLs for immediate reuse\n2. **LocalForage Storage** (`THUMBNAIL_STORAGE`): Persistent IndexedDB storage of thumbnail images\n3. **Metadata Cache** (`METADATA_CACHE`): Image metadata including dominant colors and aspect ratios\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant UI as \"Background Grid\"\n    participant Lazy as \"LazyLoadObserver\"\n    participant Cache as \"getThumbnailFromStorage()\"\n    participant BlobCache as \"THUMBNAIL_BLOBS\"\n    participant Storage as \"THUMBNAIL_STORAGE\"\n    participant Server as \"Server\"\n    \n    UI-\u003e\u003eLazy: Thumbnail enters viewport\n    Lazy-\u003e\u003eCache: Request thumbnail\n    \n    Cache-\u003e\u003eBlobCache: Check blob URL cache\n    alt Blob URL exists\n        BlobCache--\u003e\u003eUI: Return cached blob URL\n    else Check LocalForage\n        Cache-\u003e\u003eStorage: getItem(bg)\n        alt Blob exists in storage\n            Storage--\u003e\u003eCache: Return saved blob\n            Cache-\u003e\u003eBlobCache: Cache blob URL\n            Cache--\u003e\u003eUI: Return blob URL\n        else Fetch and generate\n            Cache-\u003e\u003eServer: fetch(imageUrl)\n            Server--\u003e\u003eCache: Image blob\n            Cache-\u003e\u003eCache: createThumbnail(160x90)\n            Cache-\u003e\u003eStorage: setItem(bg, thumbnail)\n            Cache-\u003e\u003eBlobCache: Cache blob URL\n            Cache--\u003e\u003eUI: Return blob URL\n        end\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/backgrounds.js:383-418]()\n\n### Thumbnail Configuration\n\n| Property | Value | Purpose |\n|----------|-------|---------|\n| `THUMBNAIL_CONFIG.width` | 160 | Thumbnail width in pixels |\n| `THUMBNAIL_CONFIG.height` | 90 | Thumbnail height (16:9 aspect ratio) |\n| `THUMBNAIL_COLUMNS_DEFAULT_DESKTOP` | 5 | Default grid columns on desktop |\n| `THUMBNAIL_COLUMNS_DEFAULT_MOBILE` | 3 | Default grid columns on mobile |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/backgrounds.js:22-25](), [public/scripts/backgrounds.js:39-42]()\n\n---\n\n## Background UI
