[public/scripts/world-info.js](public/scripts/world-info.js)\n- [public/style.css](public/style.css)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document describes SillyTavern's localization and internationalization (i18n) system, which provides multi-language support for the user interface. The system enables translation of both static UI elements and dynamically generated content through a combination of HTML attribute-based translation and runtime translation functions.\n\nFor information about text formatting and markdown processing, see [4.1](#4.1). For details about UI theming and styling, see [4.4](#4.4).\n\n---\n\n## System Architecture Overview\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Initialization[\"Application Initialization\"]\n        InitLocales[\"initLocales()\"]\n        LoadLocale[\"Load Language File\"]\n        ParseJSON[\"Parse locale JSON\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Sources[\"Translation Sources\"]\n        LocaleFiles[\"public/locales/*.json\u003cbr/\u003e14+ Language Files\"]\n        EnglishBase[\"English Base Text\u003cbr/\u003e(Embedded in HTML)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph TranslationMethods[\"Translation Methods\"]\n        DataI18n[\"data-i18n Attributes\u003cbr/\u003e(Static HTML Elements)\"]\n        TFunction[\"t Template Literal\u003cbr/\u003e(Runtime Translation)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Application[\"Application Layer\"]\n        HTMLElements[\"HTML Elements\u003cbr/\u003eButtons, Labels, etc.\"]\n        JSCode[\"JavaScript Code\u003cbr/\u003eDynamic Messages\"]\n        ToastMessages[\"Toast Notifications\"]\n        Popups[\"Popup Dialogs\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Storage[\"User Preferences\"]\n        LocalStorage[\"Browser LocalStorage\u003cbr/\u003eSelected Language\"]\n    end\n    \n    InitLocales --\u003e LoadLocale\n    LoadLocale --\u003e LocaleFiles\n    LoadLocale --\u003e ParseJSON\n    \n    ParseJSON --\u003e DataI18n\n    ParseJSON --\u003e TFunction\n    \n    DataI18n --\u003e HTMLElements\n    TFunction --\u003e JSCode\n    TFunction --\u003e ToastMessages\n    TFunction --\u003e Popups\n    \n    LocalStorage --\u003e InitLocales\n    InitLocales --\u003e LocalStorage\n    \n    EnglishBase --\u003e HTMLElements\n    \n    style LocaleFiles fill:#f9f9f9\n    style TranslationMethods fill:#f0f0f0\n```\n\n**System Architecture Overview**\n\nThe i18n system operates through a two-phase translation process: static HTML elements are translated via `data-i18n` attributes during page load, while dynamic content uses the `t` template literal function for runtime translation.\n\nSources: [public/script.js:233](), [public/script.js:688](), [public/index.html:56-363]()\n\n---\n\n## Translation File Structure\n\n### Locale File Organization\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    LocalesDir[\"public/locales/\"]\n    \n    LocalesDir --\u003e zhCN[\"zh-cn.json\u003cbr/\u003eSimplified Chinese\"]\n    LocalesDir --\u003e zhTW[\"zh-tw.json\u003cbr/\u003eTraditional Chinese\"]\n    LocalesDir --\u003e ruRU[\"ru-ru.json\u003cbr/\u003eRussian\"]\n    LocalesDir --\u003e frFR[\"fr-fr.json\u003cbr/\u003eFrench\"]\n    LocalesDir --\u003e jaJP[\"ja-jp.json\u003cbr/\u003eJapanese\"]\n    LocalesDir --\u003e esES[\"es-es.json\u003cbr/\u003eSpanish\"]\n    LocalesDir --\u003e koKR[\"ko-kr.json\u003cbr/\u003eKorean\"]\n    LocalesDir --\u003e nlNL[\"nl-nl.json\u003cbr/\u003eDutch\"]\n    LocalesDir --\u003e ukUA[\"uk-ua.json\u003cbr/\u003eUkrainian\"]\n    LocalesDir --\u003e viVN[\"vi-vn.json\u003cbr/\u003eVietnamese\"]\n    LocalesDir --\u003e arSA[\"ar-sa.json\u003cbr/\u003eArabic\"]\n    LocalesDir --\u003e itIT[\"it-it.json\u003cbr/\u003eItalian\"]\n    LocalesDir --\u003e ptPT[\"pt-pt.json\u003cbr/\u003ePortuguese\"]\n    LocalesDir --\u003e isIS[\"is-is.json\u003cbr/\u003eIcelandic\"]\n    \n    style LocalesDir fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\n**Supported Languages Directory Structure**\n\nTranslation files are stored in `public/locales/` with ISO 639-1 language codes and ISO 3166-1 country codes in lowercase, separated by hyphens.\n\n### Translation File Format\n\nEach locale file is a flat JSON object mapping translation keys to localized strings:\n\n| Key Pattern | Example | Purpose |\n|------------|---------|---------|\n| Simple key | `\"Favorite\": \"星标\"` | Direct text replacement |\n| Multi-word key | `\"AI Response Configuration\"` | Phrase translation |\n| Descriptive key | `\"clickslidertips\"` | Longer instructions |\n| Template key | `\"${hidden} characters hidden.\"` | Interpolated strings |\n\n**Example Translation Entries:**\n\n```json\n{\n    \"Favorite\": \"星标\",\n    \"Tag\": \"标签\",\n    \"Delete\": \"删除\",\n    \"clickslidertips\": \"单击滑块以手动输入值。\",\n    \"Temperature\": \"温度\",\n    \"AI Response Configuration\": \"AI响应配置\"\n}\n```\n\nSources: [public/locales/zh-cn.json:1-10](), [public/locales/zh-tw.json:1-10](), [public/locales/ru-ru.json:1-10]()\n\n---\n\n## HTML Attribute-Based Translation\n\n### The `data-i18n` Attribute System\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    HTMLElement[\"HTML Element with\u003cbr/\u003edata-i18n attribute\"]\n    \n    HTMLElement --\u003e TextContent[\"data-i18n='key'\u003cbr/\u003eTranslates text content\"]\n    HTMLElement --\u003e TitleAttr[\"data-i18n='[title]key'\u003cbr/\u003eTranslates title attribute\"]\n    HTMLElement --\u003e PlaceholderAttr[\"data-i18n='[placeholder]key'\u003cbr/\u003eTranslates placeholder\"]\n    \n    TextContent --\u003e Example1[\"\u003cbutton data-i18n='Favorite'\u003eFavorite\u003c/button\u003e\"]\n    TitleAttr --\u003e Example2[\"\u003cdiv data-i18n='[title]AI Response Configuration'\u003cbr/\u003etitle='AI Response Configuration'\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\"]\n    PlaceholderAttr --\u003e Example3[\"\u003cinput data-i18n='[placeholder]Enter text'\u003cbr/\u003eplaceholder='Enter text'\u003e\"]\n    \n    Example1 --\u003e Result1[\"\u003cbutton\u003e星标\u003c/button\u003e\u003cbr/\u003e(Chinese)\"]\n    Example2 --\u003e Result2[\"\u003cdiv title='AI响应配置'\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\u003cbr/\u003e(Chinese)\"]\n    Example3 --\u003e Result3[\"\u003cinput placeholder='输入文字'\u003e\u003cbr/\u003e(Chinese)\"]\n```\n\n**HTML Translation Attribute Patterns**\n\nThe `data-i18n` attribute supports multiple translation targets through a prefix syntax. Without a prefix, it translates text content; with `[attribute]` prefix, it translates the specified attribute.\n\n### Attribute Translation Patterns\n\n| Pattern | Effect | Example |\n|---------|--------|---------|\n| `data-i18n=\"key\"` | Translates element text content | `\u003cbutton data-i18n=\"Delete\"\u003eDelete\u003c/button\u003e` |\n| `data-i18n=\"[title]key\"` | Translates `title` attribute | `\u003cdiv title=\"Help\" data-i18n=\"[title]Help\"\u003e\u003c/div\u003e` |\n| `data-i18n=\"[placeholder]key\"` | Translates `placeholder` attribute | `\u003cinput placeholder=\"Search\" data-i18n=\"[placeholder]Search\"\u003e` |\n| `data-i18n=\"[aria-label]key\"` | Translates `aria-label` for accessibility | `\u003cbutton aria-label=\"Close\" data-i18n=\"[aria-label]Close\"\u003e` |\n\n**Common HTML Elements Using Translation:**\n\n- Navigation buttons and menu items\n- Form labels and input placeholders\n- Tooltip titles and help text\n- Dialog titles and messages\n- Settings panel labels\n\nSources: [public/index.html:56](), [public/index.html:70](), [public/index.html:82](), [public/index.html:94-112]()\n\n---\n\n## Runtime Translation Functions\n\n### The `t` Template Literal Function\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph Import[\"Import Statement\"]\n        ImportT[\"import { t } from './scripts/i18n.js'\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph UsagePatterns[\"Usage Patterns\"]\n        Simple[\"t`Simple text`\"]\n        Interpolation[\"t`${variable} text`\"]\n        Plural[\"t`${count} item${count \u003e 1 ? 's' : ''}`\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph TargetContexts[\"Target Contexts\"]\n        Toasts[\"toastr.info(t`message`)\"]\n        Popups[\"callGenericPopup(t`title`)\"]\n        DynamicHTML[\"element.text(t`label`)\"]\n        Validation[\"console.warn(t`error`)\"]\n    end\n    \n    ImportT --\u003e Simple\n    ImportT --\u003e Interpolation\n    ImportT --\u003e Plural\n    \n    Simple --\u003e Toasts\n    Interpolation --\u003e Popups\n    Interpolation --\u003e DynamicHTML\n    Simple --\u003e Validation\n```\n\n**Runtime Translation Function Usage Patterns**\n\nThe `t` function is imported from `i18n.js` and used with template literal syntax for translating dynamically generated strings.\n\n### Translation Function Usage Patterns\n\n| Usage Pattern | Example | Use Case |\n|--------------|---------|----------|\n| Simple string | `t\\`Tap to close\\`` | Static runtime text |\n| Variable interpolation | `t\\`${name} deleted successfully\\`` | Dynamic messages |\n| Conditional pluralization | `t\\`${count} character${count \u003e 1 ? 's' : ''} hidden.\\`` | Count-based text |\n| Multi-line text | `t\\`Line 1\\nLine 2\\`` | Longer messages |\n\n**Code Examples:**\n\n```javascript\n// Toast notifications\ntoastr.warning(msg, t`Group Validation`);\n\n// Element attributes\n$(this).attr('title', t`Tap to close`);\n\n// Console messages\nconsole.warn(t`Warning: Configuration invalid`);\n\n// Popup dialogs\ncallGenericPopup(t`Confirm deletion`, POPUP_TYPE.CONFIRM);\n\n// Dynamic content with interpolation\nconst msg = t`${hidden} character${hidden \u003e 1 ? 's' : ''} hidden.`;\n```\n\nSources: [public/script.js:363](), [public/scripts/utils.js:18](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:23](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:866](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:881](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:227-228]()\n\n---\n\n## Initialization and Locale Loading\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant App as Application\n    participant Init as initLocales()\n    participant Storage as LocalStorage\n    participant Fetch as Fetch API\n    participant Parser as JSON Parser\n    participant DOM as DOM Elements\n    \n    App-\u003e\u003eInit: await initLocales()\n    Init-\u003e\u003eStorage: Read saved locale preference\n    \n    alt Locale preference exists\n        Storage--\u003e\u003eInit: Return locale code (e.g., \"zh-cn\")\n    else No preference\n        Init-\u003e\u003eInit: Use default (English)\n    end\n    \n    Init-\u003e\u003eFetch: GET /locales/{locale}.json\n    Fetch--\u003e\u003eInit: JSON file content\n    \n    Init-\u003e\u003eParser: Parse translation data\n    Parser--\u003e\u003eInit: Translation map object\n    \n    Init-\u003e\u003eDOM: Find all [data-i18n] elements\n    \n    loop For each element\n        DOM-\u003e\u003eInit: Element with data-i18n\n        Init-\u003e\u003eInit: Extract translation key\n        Init-\u003e\u003eInit: Lookup translation\n        Init-\u003e\u003eDOM: Update element content/attribute\n    end\n    \n    Init--\u003e\u003eApp: Initialization complete\n```\n\n**Localization Initialization Sequence**\n\nThe `initLocales()` function is called early in the application bootstrap process to load and apply translations before the main UI renders.\n\n### Initialization Process\n\nThe locale system initializes during the application startup sequence:\n\n1. **Locale Detection**: Check LocalStorage for saved language preference\n2. **File Loading**: Fetch the appropriate JSON locale file from `public/locales/`\n3. **Parsing**: Parse JSON into a translation map\n4. **DOM Translation**: Process all elements with `data-i18n` attributes\n5. **Function Setup**: Make `t` function available for runtime translations\n\nSources: [public/script.js:688](), [public/script.js:233]()\n\n---\n\n## Helper Functions and Utilities\n\n### The `getCurrentLocale()` Function\n\n```javascript\n// Import pattern\nimport { getCurrentLocale, t } from './i18n.js';\n\n// Usage in code\nconst locale = getCurrentLocale(); // Returns e.g., \"zh-cn\", \"ja-jp\"\n```\n\nThe `getCurrentLocale()` function returns the currently active locale code, useful for locale-specific logic or formatting.\n\n### Pagination Localization\n\nThe utility functions provide specialized translation support for pagination UI components:\n\n```javascript\n// From utils.js\nexport const localizePagination = function (container) {\n    container.find('[title=\"Next page\"]').attr('title', t`Next page`);\n    container.find('[title=\"Previous page\"]').attr('title', t`Previous page`);\n    container.find('[title=\"First page\"]').attr('title', t`First page`);\n    container.find('[title=\"Last page\"]').attr('title', t`Last page`);\n};\n```\n\nThis function specifically handles pagination control translations after pagination components are rendered.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/utils.js:18](), [public/scripts/utils.js:30-35]()\n\n---\n\n## Translation Coverage Areas\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph UI[\"User Interface Components\"]\n        TopBar[\"Top Bar Navigation\"]\n        Settings[\"Settings Panels\"]\n        CharacterUI[\"Character Management\"]\n        ChatUI[\"Chat Interface\"]\n        ContextMenu[\"Context Menus\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Dynamic[\"Dynamic Content\"]\n        Toasts[\"Toast Notifications\"]\n        Popups[\"Popup Dialogs\"]\n        Validation[\"Validation Messages\"]\n        Errors[\"Error Messages\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Advanced[\"Advanced Features\"]\n        WorldInfo[\"World Info Labels\"]\n        GroupChats[\"Group Chat Messages\"]\n        Presets[\"Preset Names/Descriptions\"]\n        Tooltips[\"Tooltips \u0026 Help Text\"]\n    end\n    \n    TopBar --\u003e DataI18n[\"data-i18n attributes\"]\n    Settings --\u003e DataI18n\n    CharacterUI --\u003e DataI18n\n    ChatUI --\u003e DataI18n\n    ContextMenu --\u003e DataI18n\n    \n    Toasts --\u003e TFunc[\"t function\"]\n    Popups --\u003e TFunc\n    Validation --\u003e TFunc\n    Errors --\u003e TFunc\n    \n    WorldInfo --\u003e TFunc\n    GroupChats --\u003e TFunc\n    Presets --\u003e Mixed[\"Mixed: both methods\"]\n    Tooltips --\u003e Mixed\n```\n\n**Application Areas Using Translation System**\n\nDifferent parts of the application use different translation methods depending on whether content is static (HTML) or dynamic (JavaScript-generated).\n\n| Component | Translation Method | Coverage |\n|-----------|-------------------|----------|\n| Main menu buttons | `data-i18n` | Complete |\n| Settings labels | `data-i18n` | Complete |\n| AI configuration panel | `data-i18n` | Complete |\n| Toast messages | `t` function | Complete |\n| Validation errors | `t` function | Extensive |\n| Console warnings | `t` function | Partial |\n| Character context menus | `data-i18n` | Complete |\n\nSources: [public/index.html:54-363](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:227-228](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:866](), [public/scripts/utils.js:30-35]()\n\n---\n\n## Translation Key Naming Conventions\n\n### Key Structure Patterns\n\n| Pattern Type | Example Key | English Text | Purpose |\n|-------------|-------------|--------------|---------|\n| Single word | `\"Favorite\"` | \"Favorite\" | Simple nouns/verbs |\n| Phrase | `\"AI Response Configuration\"` | \"AI Response Configuration\" | Multi-word labels |\n| Descriptive | `\"clickslidertips\"` | \"Click slider numbers...\" | Instructions |\n| Parameter name | `\"Temperature\"` | \"Temperature\" | Technical terms |\n| Action + object | `\"Delete the preset\"` | \"Delete the preset\" | Button labels |\n| Abbreviated | `\"rep.pen\"` | \"Repetition Penalty\" | Short labels |\n| Descriptive suffix | `\"Temperature_desc\"` | \"Temperature controls...\" | Tooltip descriptions |\n| Template syntax | `\"${count} characters hidden.\"` | \"3 characters hidden.\" | Dynamic text |\n\n### Special Key Patterns\n\n**Description Keys**: Many technical parameters have companion description keys:\n- `\"Temperature\"` → Label\n- `\"Temperature_desc\"` → Detailed explanation\n\n**Interpolated Keys**: Keys containing `${}` syntax support variable substitution:\n```json\n{\n    \"${hidden} characters hidden.\": \"${hidden} 个角色已隐藏。\"\n}\n```\n\nSources: [public/locales/zh-cn.json:1-50](), [public/locales/zh-cn.json:119-125]()\n\n---\n\n## Adding New Translations\n\n### Process for Adding a New Language\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Start[\"Start: Add New Language\"]\n    \n    CreateFile[\"1. Create new JSON file\u003cbr/\u003epublic/locales/{lang-code}.json\"]\n    CopyBase[\"2. Copy English base text\u003cbr/\u003efrom HTML/existing locale\"]\n    TranslateKeys[\"3. Translate all key values\u003cbr/\u003eKeep keys unchanged\"]\n    TestLoad[\"4. Test locale loading\u003cbr/\u003eVerify file loads correctly\"]\n    TestUI[\"5. Test UI elements\u003cbr/\u003eCheck translations display\"]\n    \n    Start --\u003e CreateFile\n    CreateFile --\u003e CopyBase\n    CopyBase --\u003e TranslateKeys\n    TranslateKeys --\u003e TestLoad\n    TestLoad --\u003e TestUI\n    \n    TestUI --\u003e Complete[\"Complete: New Language Added\"]\n    \n    style CreateFile fill:#f9f9f9\n    style TranslateKeys fill:#fff9e6\n    style TestUI fill:#e6f9ff\n```\n\n**New Language Addition Workflow**\n\nTo add support for a new language, create a locale file following the naming convention and translate all keys while preserving the key structure.\n\n### Step-by-Step Guide\n\n1. **Create Locale File**: \n   - File: `public/locales/{language-code}.json`\n   - Naming: Use ISO 639-1 language + ISO 3166-1 country (lowercase, hyphen-separated)\n   - Example: `de-de.json` for German (Germany)\n\n2. **Copy Key Structure**:\n   - Use an existing locale file (e.g., `zh-cn.json`) as template\n   - Copy all keys, preserving structure\n   - Or extract English text from HTML `data-i18n` attributes\n\n3. **Translate Values**:\n   - Translate only the values, never the keys\n   - Preserve placeholders: `${variable}` syntax\n   - Maintain formatting: `\\n` for newlines\n   - Keep technical terms consistent\n\n4. **Test Coverage**:\n   - Load the application with new locale\n   - Verify all UI elements translate\n   - Check dynamic messages (toasts, popups)\n   - Test special characters and long text\n\n5. **Add Locale to Selector**:\n   - Update locale selection UI (if exists)\n   - Test locale switching\n\nSources: [public/locales/zh-cn.json:1-2787](), [public/locales/zh-tw.json:1-2841]()\n\n---\n\n## Handling Special Cases\n\n### Pluralization\n\nThe system handles pluralization through conditional expressions in translation keys:\n\n```javascript\n// In JavaScript code\nconst hidden = 3;\nconst message = t`${hidden} character${hidden \u003e 1 ? 's' : ''} hidden.`;\n\n// In locale file\n{\n    \"${hidden} character hidden.\": \"${hidden} 个角色已隐藏。\",\n    \"${hidden} characters hidden.\": \"${hidden} 个角色已隐藏。\"\n}\n```\n\nFor languages with complex plural rules, the conditional logic appears in the `t` function call.\n\n### Right-to-Left (RTL) Languages\n\nArabic (`ar-sa.json`) is supported, indicating RTL layout capability. The system likely uses CSS directionality attributes based on the selected locale.\n\n### Long Text and Truncation\n\nSome translations may be longer than the English source text. The UI layout system must accommodate varying text lengths through:\n- Flexible CSS layouts\n- Text wrapping\n- Tooltip overflow handling\n- Responsive design patterns\n\nSources: [public/scripts/world-info.js:881](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:227](), [public/locales/ar-sa.json:1-10]()\n\n---\n\n## Translation File Statistics\n\n| Locale Code | Language | Approximate Keys | Coverage Level |\n|-------------|----------|------------------|----------------|\n| zh-cn | Chinese (Simplified) | ~2,787 | Very High |\n| zh-tw | Chinese (Traditional) | ~2,841 | Very High |\n| ru-ru | Russian | ~2,500+ | High |\n| fr-fr | French | ~1,800+ | Medium-High |\n| ja-jp | Japanese | ~1,200+ | Medium |\n| es-es | Spanish | ~1,300+ | Medium |\n| ko-kr | Korean | ~1,200+ | Medium |\n| nl-nl | Dutch | ~1,100+ | Medium |\n| uk-ua | Ukrainian | ~1,100+ | Medium |\n| vi-vn | Vietnamese | ~1,100+ | Medium |\n| ar-sa | Arabic | ~1,000+ | Medium |\n| it-it | Italian | ~1,100+ | Medium |\n| pt-pt | Portuguese | ~1,100+ | Medium |\n| is-is | Icelandic | ~1,100+ | Medium |\n\n**Note**: Key counts are approximate based on file size. Chinese locales have the highest coverage, serving as reference implementations for comprehensive translation.\n\nSources: [public/locales/zh-cn.json:1-2787](), [public/locales/zh-tw.json:1-2841](), [public/locales/ru-ru.json:1-2500]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration Points with Other Systems\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    I18nSystem[\"i18n System\u003cbr/\u003e(initLocales, t)\"]\n    \n    PowerUser[\"power-user.js\u003cbr/\u003eSettings UI\"]\n    WorldInfo[\"world-info.js\u003cbr/\u003eLorebook Messages\"]\n    GroupChats[\"group-chats.js\u003cbr/\u003eGroup Management\"]\n    Utils[\"utils.js\u003cbr/\u003eUtility Functions\"]\n    InstructMode[\"instruct-mode.js\u003cbr/\u003eMode Configuration\"]\n    \n    I18nSystem --\u003e PowerUser\n    I18nSystem --\u003e WorldInfo\n    I18nSystem --\u003e GroupChats\n    I18nSystem --\u003e Utils\n    I18nSystem --\u003e InstructMode\n    \n    PowerUser --\u003e SettingsLabels[\"Settings Panel Labels\u003cbr/\u003eTooltips, Descriptions\"]\n    WorldInfo --\u003e DynamicMessages[\"Dynamic Messages\u003cbr/\u003eValidation, Warnings\"]\n    GroupChats --\u003e Notifications[\"Toast Notifications\u003cbr/\u003eUser Feedback\"]\n    Utils --\u003e PaginationUI[\"Pagination Controls\u003cbr/\u003eNavigation Labels\"]\n    InstructMode --\u003e PresetLabels[\"Preset Names\u003cbr/\u003eMode Descriptions\"]\n```\n\n**System Integration Points**\n\nThe i18n system is imported and used across all major subsystems for consistent multi-language support.\n\n### Key Integration Areas\n\n- **Settings System** ([power-user.js:68]()): All settings labels, tooltips, and descriptions\n- **World Info** ([world-info.js:23]()): Entry validation messages and UI labels\n- **Group Chats** ([group-chats.js:87]()): Group management messages and notifications\n- **Utilities** ([utils.js:18]()): Pagination and common UI elements\n- **Instruct Mode** ([instruct-mode.js]()): Configuration UI labels\n\nThe `t` function and `data-i18n` attributes provide a consistent translation interface across all modules.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/power-user.js:68](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:23](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:87](), [public/scripts/utils.js:18]()\n\n---\n\n## Best Practices for Developers\n\n### Adding Translatable Content\n\n**For Static HTML Elements:**\n```html\n\u003c!-- DO: Use data-i18n with clear key --\u003e\n\u003cbutton data-i18n=\"Delete Character\"\u003eDelete Character\u003c/button\u003e\n\n\u003c!-- DO: Translate attributes --\u003e\n\u003cinput placeholder=\"Search\" data-i18n=\"[placeholder]Search characters\"\u003e\n\n\u003c!-- DON'T: Hardcode text without data-i18n --\u003e\n\u003cbutton\u003eDelete Character\u003c/button\u003e\n```\n\n**For Dynamic JavaScript Content:**\n```javascript\n// DO: Use t function for runtime strings\ntoastr.success(t`Character deleted successfully`);\n\n// DO: Use interpolation for dynamic values\nconst message = t`Deleted ${count} characters`;\n\n// DON'T: Use raw English strings\ntoastr.success('Character deleted successfully');\n```\n\n### Translation Key Guidelines\n\n1. **Use descriptive keys**: Prefer readable keys over cryptic abbreviations\n2. **Maintain consistency**: Use existing key patterns for similar content\n3. **Preserve placeholders**: Keep `${}` syntax in translations\n4. **Document context**: Add comments for ambiguous keys\n5. **Test thoroughly**: Verify translations display correctly in all contexts\n\n### Performance Considerations\n\n- Translations are loaded once at initialization\n- DOM translations occur during page load\n- Runtime `t` function calls are lightweight lookups\n- Large locale files (~100KB+) load quickly but can be split if needed\n\nSources: [public/index.html:56-363](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:866-881](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:227-228]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"2f:T5c5b,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Accessibility and Keyboard Navigation\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [public/css/backgrounds.css](public/css/backgrounds.css)\n- [public/css/mobile-styles.css](public/css/mobile-styles.css)\n- [public/css/select2-overrides.css](public/css/select2-overrides.css)\n- [public/css/st-tailwind.css](public/css/st-tailwind.css)\n- [public/css/tags.css](public/css/tags.css)\n- [public/css/toggle-dependent.css](public/css/toggle-dependent.css)\n- [public/css/world-info.css](public/css/world-info.css)\n- [public/scripts/a11y.js](public/scripts/a11y.js)\n- [public/scripts/backgrounds.js](public/scripts/backgrounds.js)\n- [public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js](public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js)\n- [public/scripts/filters.js](public/scripts/filters.js)\n- [public/scripts/keyboard.js](public/scripts/keyboard.js)\n- [public/scripts/personas.js](public/scripts/personas.js)\n- [public/scripts/sse-stream.js](public/scripts/sse-stream.js)\n- [public/scripts/tags.js](public/scripts/tags.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page documents SillyTavern's accessibility and keyboard navigation systems. These systems ensure that the application is usable with keyboard-only input and follows WCAG accessibility guidelines through proper ARIA attributes and focus management.\n\nFor UI theming and visual customization, see [Backgrounds and Theming](#4.4). For localization features, see [Localization and Internationalization](#4.5).\n\n## System Overview\n\nSillyTavern's accessibility infrastructure consists of three coordinated subsystems:\n\n1. **Keyboard Navigation System** ([public/scripts/keyboard.js]()) - Manages focus states, tabindex assignment, and keyboard interaction patterns\n2. **ARIA Attribute System** ([public/scripts/a11y.js]()) - Applies semantic roles and accessibility attributes to UI elements\n3. **Dynamic Focus Styling** ([public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js]()) - Automatically generates `:focus-visible` styles from existing `:hover` styles\n\nThese systems work together to provide a consistent, accessible experience across the entire application without requiring manual ARIA or focus styling for each component.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Accessibility Infrastructure\"\n        KeyboardJS[\"keyboard.js\u003cbr/\u003eFocus \u0026 Tabindex Management\"]\n        A11yJS[\"a11y.js\u003cbr/\u003eARIA Attributes\"]\n        DynamicStyles[\"dynamic-styles.js\u003cbr/\u003eFocus Style Generation\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"DOM Observation\"\n        MutationObs[\"MutationObserver\u003cbr/\u003eMonitors DOM Changes\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"UI Components\"\n        Buttons[\"Buttons \u0026 Controls\u003cbr/\u003e.menu_button, .right_menu_button\"]\n        Lists[\"Lists \u0026 Grids\u003cbr/\u003e#rm_print_characters_block\"]\n        Inputs[\"Form Elements\u003cbr/\u003eselect, textarea, input\"]\n        Custom[\"Custom Controls\u003cbr/\u003e.custom_interactable\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Stylesheets\"\n        MainCSS[\"Core Stylesheets\u003cbr/\u003estyle.css, tags.css, etc.\"]\n        ExtCSS[\"Extension Stylesheets\u003cbr/\u003escripts/extensions/**/*.css\"]\n        DynamicSheet[\"Dynamic Stylesheet\u003cbr/\u003e#dynamic-styles\"]\n    end\n    \n    MutationObs --\u003e|\"Detects new elements\"| KeyboardJS\n    MutationObs --\u003e|\"Detects new elements\"| A11yJS\n    MutationObs --\u003e|\"Detects new stylesheets\"| DynamicStyles\n    \n    KeyboardJS --\u003e|\"Sets tabindex\"| Buttons\n    KeyboardJS --\u003e|\"Sets tabindex\"| Lists\n    KeyboardJS --\u003e|\"Sets tabindex\"| Custom\n    \n    A11yJS --\u003e|\"Sets role attributes\"| Buttons\n    A11yJS --\u003e|\"Sets role attributes\"| Lists\n    \n    DynamicStyles --\u003e|\"Processes hover rules\"| MainCSS\n    DynamicStyles --\u003e|\"Processes hover rules\"| ExtCSS\n    DynamicStyles --\u003e|\"Inserts focus-visible rules\"| DynamicSheet\n    \n    DynamicSheet --\u003e|\"Styles focus states\"| Buttons\n    DynamicSheet --\u003e|\"Styles focus states\"| Lists\n    DynamicSheet --\u003e|\"Styles focus states\"| Custom\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/keyboard.js:1-250](), [public/scripts/a11y.js:1-131](), [public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js:1-199]()\n\n## Keyboard Navigation System\n\nThe keyboard navigation system is implemented in [public/scripts/keyboard.js](). It automatically identifies interactive elements and manages their keyboard accessibility state.\n\n### Interactable Types\n\nThe system recognizes specific CSS selectors as keyboard-interactable controls. These are defined in the `interactableSelectors` array:\n\n| Selector Pattern | Purpose | Examples |\n|-----------------|---------|----------|\n| `.menu_button` | General menu buttons | Settings, API selection |\n| `.right_menu_button` | Drawer menu buttons | Character management |\n| `.drawer-icon` | Main menu bar icons | Extensions, personas |\n| `.character_select`, `.group_select` | Entity selection cards | Character list items |\n| `.tag .tag_remove` | Tag removal buttons | Tag management |\n| `.bg_example` | Background thumbnails | Background selection |\n| `.mes_buttons .mes_button` | Message inline buttons | Edit, delete, regenerate |\n| `.swipe_left`, `.swipe_right` | Swipe navigation | Message variations |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/keyboard.js:2-23]()\n\n### CSS Classes for State Management\n\nThe system uses four primary CSS classes to manage interactable state:\n\n- **`interactable`** (`INTERACTABLE_CONTROL_CLASS`) - Applied to all keyboard-navigable controls\n- **`custom_interactable`** (`CUSTOM_INTERACTABLE_CONTROL_CLASS`) - Manually registered controls\n- **`not_focusable`** (`NOT_FOCUSABLE_CONTROL_CLASS`) - Temporarily excludes from keyboard navigation\n- **`disabled`** (`DISABLED_CONTROL_CLASS`) - Marks control as disabled\n\nThese constants are exported and used throughout the codebase:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Import[\"Import Constants\u003cbr/\u003eINTERACTABLE_CONTROL_CLASS\"]\n    \n    TagsJS[\"tags.js:27\u003cbr/\u003eTag filter controls\"]\n    PersonasJS[\"personas.js\u003cbr/\u003ePersona selection\"]\n    BackgroundsJS[\"backgrounds.js\u003cbr/\u003eBackground thumbnails\"]\n    \n    Import --\u003e TagsJS\n    Import --\u003e PersonasJS\n    Import --\u003e BackgroundsJS\n    \n    TagsJS --\u003e|\"Adds class to\"| TagControls[\"Tag Elements\u003cbr/\u003e.rm_tag_filter .tag\"]\n    PersonasJS --\u003e|\"Adds class to\"| PersonaControls[\"Persona Blocks\u003cbr/\u003e.avatar-container\"]\n    BackgroundsJS --\u003e|\"Adds class to\"| BGControls[\"Background Tiles\u003cbr/\u003e.bg_example\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/keyboard.js:30-34](), [public/scripts/tags.js:27]()\n\n### Interactable Registration Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant DOM as \"DOM Element Added\"\n    participant Observer as \"MutationObserver\"\n    participant Check as \"isKeyboardInteractable()\"\n    participant Make as \"makeKeyboardInteractable()\"\n    participant State as \"State Check\"\n    participant TabIndex as \"Tabindex Assignment\"\n    \n    DOM-\u003e\u003eObserver: Element inserted\n    Observer-\u003e\u003eCheck: Check if matches selector\n    Check-\u003e\u003eCheck: Test against interactableSelectors[]\n    \n    alt Matches selector\n        Check--\u003e\u003eMake: true\n        Make-\u003e\u003eState: Check for disabled/not_focusable\n        \n        alt Not disabled/not_focusable\n            State-\u003e\u003eTabIndex: Set tabindex=\"0\"\n            TabIndex-\u003e\u003eDOM: Element focusable\n        else Disabled or not_focusable\n            State-\u003e\u003eTabIndex: Remove tabindex\n            TabIndex-\u003e\u003eDOM: Element not focusable\n        end\n        \n        Make-\u003e\u003eDOM: Add .interactable class\n    else Does not match\n        Check--\u003e\u003eObserver: false\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/keyboard.js:40-73](), [public/scripts/keyboard.js:99-153]()\n\n### Registering Custom Interactables\n\nExtensions can register custom interactable types using `registerInteractableType()`:\n\n```javascript\n// From extension code\nimport { registerInteractableType } from './keyboard.js';\n\n// Register custom buttons\nregisterInteractableType('.my-extension-button', {\n    disabledByDefault: false,\n    notFocusableByDefault: false\n});\n```\n\nThis function:\n1. Adds the selector to `interactableSelectors` array\n2. Queries existing matching elements\n3. Optionally applies `disabled` or `not_focusable` classes\n4. Calls `makeKeyboardInteractable()` on all matches\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/keyboard.js:74-96]()\n\n### Focus Management and Tabindex\n\nThe `makeKeyboardInteractable()` function manages `tabindex` attributes based on element state:\n\n| Element State | Tabindex Value | Behavior |\n|--------------|----------------|----------|\n| Normal interactable | `0` | Included in tab order |\n| Has `disabled` class | Removed | Excluded from keyboard navigation |\n| Has `not_focusable` class | Removed | Excluded from keyboard navigation |\n| Parent has `disabled` | Removed | Inherited exclusion |\n| Custom tabindex | Preserved in `data-original-tabindex` | Restored when re-enabled |\n\nThe system checks ancestors for `disabled` or `not_focusable` classes using `hasDisabledOrNotFocusableAncestor()`, ensuring child elements respect parent state.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/keyboard.js:115-153]()\n\n### Scroll Reset Behavior\n\nElements with the `scroll-reset-container` class automatically reset their scroll position when focus leaves:\n\n```javascript\n// Applied to containers like tag filters, dropdown menus\ncontainer.addEventListener('focusout', (e) =\u003e {\n    setTimeout(() =\u003e {\n        if (!container.contains(document.activeElement)) {\n            container.scrollTop = 0;\n            container.scrollLeft = 0;\n        }\n    }, 0);\n});\n```\n\nThis prevents confusion when navigating away from scrollable regions and returning later.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/keyboard.js:176-201]()\n\n### Keyboard Event Handling\n\nThe system handles `Enter` key presses on focused interactables to trigger click events:\n\n```javascript\ndocument.addEventListener('keydown', (event) =\u003e {\n    if (event.key === 'Enter' \u0026\u0026 \n        event.target.classList.contains('interactable')) {\n        event.target.click();\n    }\n});\n```\n\nThis provides consistent activation behavior for all keyboard-navigable controls.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/keyboard.js:203-250]()\n\n## ARIA Attributes and Semantic Roles\n\nThe [public/scripts/a11y.js]() module applies WCAG-compliant ARIA roles to UI elements. Unlike the keyboard navigation system which manages focus, this system ensures screen readers correctly interpret the semantic structure.\n\n### ARIA Role Mappings\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Selector Groups\"\n        ButtonSel[\"buttonSelectors\u003cbr/\u003e.menu_button, .right_menu_button\u003cbr/\u003e.drawer-icon, etc.\"]\n        ListSel[\"listSelectors\u003cbr/\u003e.options-content, .list-group\u003cbr/\u003e#rm_print_characters_block\"]\n        ListItemSel[\"listItemSelectors\u003cbr/\u003e.entity_block, .group_member\u003cbr/\u003e.tag_view_item\"]\n        ToolbarSel[\"toolbarSelectors\u003cbr/\u003e.jg-menu\"]\n        TabListSel[\"tabListSelectors\u003cbr/\u003e#bg_tabs .bg_tabs_list\"]\n        TabItemSel[\"tabItemSelectors\u003cbr/\u003e.bg_tab_button\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Applied ARIA Roles\"\n        ButtonRole[\"role='button'\"]\n        ListRole[\"role='list'\"]\n        ListItemRole[\"role='listitem'\"]\n        ToolbarRole[\"role='toolbar'\"]\n        TabListRole[\"role='tablist'\"]\n        TabRole[\"role='tab'\"]\n    end\n    \n    ButtonSel --\u003e|\"setAttribute\"| ButtonRole\n    ListSel --\u003e|\"setAttribute\"| ListRole\n    ListItemSel --\u003e|\"setAttribute\"| ListItemRole\n    ToolbarSel --\u003e|\"setAttribute\"| ToolbarRole\n    TabListSel --\u003e|\"setAttribute\"| TabListRole\n    TabItemSel --\u003e|\"setAttribute\"| TabRole\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/a11y.js:6-84]()\n\n### Role Application Process\n\nThe `a11yRules` object maps selectors to functions that apply appropriate ARIA roles:\n\n```javascript\nconst a11yRules = {\n    [buttonSelectors]: (element) =\u003e {\n        element.setAttribute('role', 'button');\n    },\n    [listSelectors]: (element) =\u003e {\n        element.setAttribute('role', 'list');\n    },\n    // ... etc\n};\n```\n\nThe `applyA11yRules()` function processes each rule for an element and its descendants:\n\n1. Check if element matches selector\n2. Apply rule function\n3. Query descendants matching selector\n4. Apply rule to each descendant\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/a11y.js:61-103]()\n\n### Initialization and Observation\n\nThe system initializes on page load and observes for dynamic content:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Init as \"initAccessibility()\"\n    participant Body as \"document.body\"\n    participant Observer as \"MutationObserver\"\n    participant Apply as \"applyA11yRules()\"\n    participant Element as \"DOM Element\"\n    \n    Init-\u003e\u003eBody: Apply rules to existing elements\n    Body-\u003e\u003eApply: Process all children\n    Apply-\u003e\u003eElement: Set ARIA attributes\n    \n    Init-\u003e\u003eObserver: Start observing\n    \n    loop On mutation\n        Observer-\u003e\u003eApply: Process added nodes\n        Apply-\u003e\u003eElement: Set ARIA attributes\n    end\n```\n\nThe observer watches for `childList` mutations with `subtree: true`, ensuring all dynamically added content receives proper ARIA attributes.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/a11y.js:105-131]()\n\n### Specific ARIA Patterns\n\n| UI Component | ARIA Role | Selector |\n|-------------|-----------|----------|\n| Character list | `list` | `#rm_print_characters_block` |\n| Character card | `listitem` | `.entity_block` |\n| Background grid | `list` | `.bg_list` |\n| Background tile | `listitem` | `.bg_example` |\n| Tag list | `list` | `.tag_view_list_tags` |\n| Tag item | `listitem` | `.tag_view_item` |\n| Background menu | `toolbar` | `.jg-menu` |\n| Background tabs | `tablist` | `.bg_tabs_list` |\n| Tab button | `tab` | `.bg_tab_button` |\n| Toast notification | `status` | `#toast-container .toast` |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/a11y.js:21-84]()\n\n## Dynamic Focus Styling System\n\nThe [public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js]() module automatically generates `:focus-visible` styles from existing `:hover` styles, ensuring visual consistency between mouse and keyboard interactions without manual CSS duplication.\n\n### Focus Style Generation Algorithm\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Start[\"Start: Process Stylesheet\"] --\u003e ProcessRules[\"processRules()\u003cbr/\u003eIterate CSSRuleList\"]\n    \n    ProcessRules --\u003e CheckType{Rule Type?}\n    \n    CheckType --\u003e|\"CSSImportRule\"| Import[\"Process Imported Sheet\u003cbr/\u003eRecursively\"]\n    CheckType --\u003e|\"CSSStyleRule\"| Style[\"Parse Selectors\"]\n    CheckType --\u003e|\"CSSMediaRule\"| Media[\"Recurse with @media wrapper\"]\n    CheckType --\u003e|\"CSSSupportsRule\"| Supports[\"Recurse with @supports wrapper\"]\n    CheckType --\u003e|\"CSSContainerRule\"| Container[\"Recurse with @container wrapper\"]\n    \n    Import --\u003e ProcessRules\n    Media --\u003e ProcessRules\n    Supports --\u003e ProcessRules\n    Container --\u003e ProcessRules\n    \n    Style --\u003e HasHover{Contains :hover?}\n    HasHover --\u003e|\"Yes\"| StoreHover[\"Store hover rule with\u003cbr/\u003ewrappers chain\"]\n    HasHover --\u003e|\"No\"| CheckFocus{Contains :focus?}\n    \n    CheckFocus --\u003e|\"Yes\"| StoreFocus[\"Mark as existing focus rule\"]\n    CheckFocus --\u003e|\"No\"| ProcessRules\n    \n    StoreHover --\u003e ProcessRules\n    StoreFocus --\u003e ProcessRules\n    \n    ProcessRules --\u003e Done{More rules?}\n    Done --\u003e|\"Yes\"| ProcessRules\n    Done --\u003e|\"No\"| Generate[\"Generate Missing Focus Rules\"]\n    \n    Generate --\u003e ForEach[\"For each hover rule\"]\n    ForEach --\u003e HasMatch{Matching focus\u003cbr/\u003erule exists?}\n    \n    HasMatch --\u003e|\"No\"| Create[\"Create :focus-visible rule\u003cbr/\u003efrom hover cssText\"]\n    HasMatch --\u003e|\"Yes\"| Skip[\"Skip\"]\n    \n    Create --\u003e Wrap{Has wrappers?}\n    Wrap --\u003e|\"Yes\"| WrapRule[\"Nest in @media/@supports/\u003cbr/\u003e@container blocks\"]\n    Wrap --\u003e|\"No\"| Insert[\"Insert into dynamic stylesheet\"]\n    \n    WrapRule --\u003e Insert\n    Skip --\u003e ForEach\n    Insert --\u003e ForEach\n    \n    ForEach --\u003e End[\"End\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js:35-151]()\n\n### Wrapper Chain Handling\n\nThe system preserves conditional contexts (media queries, feature queries, container queries) when generating focus styles:\n\n```css\n/* Source CSS */\n@media (min-width: 768px) {\n    @supports (backdrop-filter: blur(10px)) {\n        .button:hover {\n            background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1);\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/* Generated CSS */\n@media (min-width: 768px) {\n    @supports (backdrop-filter: blur(10px)) {\n        .button:focus-visible {\n            background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThe `wrapperSignature()` function creates unique keys for wrapper chains to distinguish identical selectors in different contexts:\n\n```javascript\nfunction wrapperSignature(wrappers) {\n    return wrappers.map(w =\u003e `${w.type}:${w.conditionText}`).join(';');\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js:43-52](), [public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js:86-97]()\n\n### Stylesheet Processing\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Input Stylesheets\"\n        Main[\"Core CSS\u003cbr/\u003estyle.css, tags.css\"]\n        Extensions[\"Extension CSS\u003cbr/\u003escripts/extensions/**/*.css\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Processing\"\n        Processor[\"applyDynamicFocusStyles()\"]\n        HoverCache[\"Hover Rules Collection\"]\n        FocusCache[\"Existing Focus Rules\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Output Stylesheets\"\n        DynamicCore[\"#dynamic-styles\u003cbr/\u003eCore focus rules\"]\n        DynamicExt[\"#dynamic-extension-styles\u003cbr/\u003eExtension focus rules\"]\n    end\n    \n    Main --\u003e Processor\n    Extensions --\u003e Processor\n    \n    Processor --\u003e HoverCache\n    Processor --\u003e FocusCache\n    \n    HoverCache --\u003e DynamicCore\n    HoverCache --\u003e DynamicExt\n    \n    FocusCache --\u003e Processor\n```\n\nThe system maintains two dynamic stylesheets:\n- `#dynamic-styles` - Focus rules for core CSS\n- `#dynamic-extension-styles` - Focus rules for extension CSS\n\nThis separation prevents conflicts and allows extensions to be loaded/unloaded independently.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js:153-178]()\n\n### Initialization and Observation\n\nThe system initializes by processing all existing stylesheets and observes for new stylesheet additions:\n\n```javascript\nexport function initDynamicStyles() {\n    // Start observing head for new stylesheets\n    observer.observe(document.head, {\n        childList: true,\n        subtree: true,\n    });\n    \n    // Process all stylesheets on initial load\n    Array.from(document.styleSheets).forEach(sheet =\u003e {\n        try {\n            applyDynamicFocusStyles(sheet, {\n                fromExtension: sheet.href?.toLowerCase()\n                    .includes('scripts/extensions')\n            });\n        } catch (e) {\n            console.warn('Failed to process stylesheet:', e);\n        }\n    });\n}\n```\n\nThe `MutationObserver` watches for new `\u003clink rel=\"stylesheet\"\u003e` elements and processes them when loaded.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js:8-26](), [public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js:183-198]()\n\n## Mobile and Touch Considerations\n\nThe accessibility system adapts to mobile devices and touch interfaces through conditional behavior and responsive CSS.\n\n### Mobile Detection\n\nThe system uses `window.matchMedia()` to detect narrow screens and adjust defaults:\n\n```javascript\n// From backgrounds.js - thumbnail column defaults\nconst isNarrowScreen = window.matchMedia('(max-width: 480px)').matches;\ncolumns = isNarrowScreen ? \n    THUMBNAIL_COLUMNS_DEFAULT_MOBILE : \n    THUMBNAIL_COLUMNS_DEFAULT_DESKTOP;\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/backgrounds.js:212-216]()\n\n### Touch-Specific UI Patterns\n\nMobile styles ([public/css/mobile-styles.css]()) provide touch-friendly alternatives:\n\n| Pattern | Desktop | Mobile |\n|---------|---------|--------|\n| Background menu buttons | Visible on hover | Explicit toggle button |\n| Drawer sizing | Resizable | Fixed 100dvw |\n| Focus indicators | Keyboard-driven | Tap-driven |\n| Scroll containers | Mouse wheel | Touch gestures |\n\nThe `.mobile-only-menu-toggle` class provides a tap target for accessing menus on touch devices:\n\n```css\n.bg_example .mobile-only-menu-toggle {\n    display: none; /* Hidden on desktop */\n}\n\n@media screen and (max-width: 1000px) {\n    .bg_example .mobile-only-menu-toggle {\n        display: flex; /* Shown on mobile */\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/css/mobile-styles.css:69-84](), [public/css/backgrounds.css:110-112]()\n\n### Viewport Units\n\nThe mobile stylesheet uses dynamic viewport units (`dvh`, `dvw`) for consistent sizing across devices with dynamic UI elements (e.g., mobile browser chrome):\n\n```css\n#sheld {\n    width: 100dvw;\n    height: calc(100dvh - 36px);\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/css/mobile-styles.css:221-244]()\n\n## CSS Focus Styling Patterns\n\nThe codebase follows consistent patterns for focus styling across components.\n\n### Focus-Visible vs Focus\n\nThe system prefers `:focus-visible` over `:focus` to avoid unwanted focus rings on mouse clicks:\n\n```css\n/* Good - only shows for keyboard navigation */\n.button:focus-visible {\n    outline: 2px solid var(--interactable-outline-color);\n}\n\n/* Avoid - shows for all focus including mouse clicks */\n.button:focus {\n    outline: 2px solid blue;\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/css/backgrounds.css:128-131]()\n\n### Outline Color Variable\n\nThe application uses `--interactable-outline-color` CSS custom property for consistent focus indicators:\n\n```css\n.bg_example:focus-visible {\n    outline-offset: inherit;\n    outline-color: var(--interactable-outline-color);\n}\n```\n\nThis variable is defined in the theme system and adapts to light/dark modes.\n\n**Sources:** [public/css/backgrounds.css:128-131]()\n\n### Complex Focus States\n\nSome components require complex focus-within behavior for nested controls:\n\n```css\n/* Show menu when background tile has focus within */\n.bg_example:hover .jg-menu,\n.bg_example:focus-within .jg-menu {\n    opacity: 1;\n    visibility: visible;\n    transform: scale(1);\n}\n```\n\nThe `.jg-menu` becomes visible when the parent `.bg_example` contains a focused element, ensuring toolbar buttons are accessible via keyboard.\n\n**Sources:** [public/css/backgrounds.css:198-203]()\n\n## Extension Integration\n\nExtensions can integrate with the accessibility system through exported functions and CSS patterns.\n\n### Registering Interactables\n\nExtensions should import and use `registerInteractableType()`:\n\n```javascript\n// In extension's index.js\nimport { registerInteractableType } from '../../keyboard.js';\n\n// After creating custom UI\nregisterInteractableType('.my-extension-button');\n```\n\nAlternatively, add the `INTERACTABLE_CONTROL_CLASS` to elements:\n\n```javascript\nimport { INTERACTABLE_CONTROL_CLASS } from '../../keyboard.js';\n\nconst button = document.createElement('button');\nbutton.classList.add(INTERACTABLE_CONTROL_CLASS);\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/keyboard.js:30-31](), [public/scripts/keyboard.js:74-96]()\n\n### Extension Stylesheets\n\nExtension stylesheets are automatically processed for dynamic focus generation:\n\n```javascript\n// In dynamic-styles.js\napplyDynamicFocusStyles(sheet, {\n    fromExtension: sheet.href?.toLowerCase()\n        .includes('scripts/extensions')\n});\n```\n\nFocus rules are inserted into `#dynamic-extension-styles` separately from core styles.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/dynamic-styles.js:191-195]()\n\n### ARIA Best Practices for Extensions\n\nExtensions should follow the established ARIA patterns:\n\n1. Use semantic HTML where possible (`\u003cbutton\u003e`, `\u003cinput\u003e`, etc.)\n2. Apply `role` attributes for custom controls\n3. Use `aria-label` for controls without visible text\n4. Provide `aria-describedby` for complex controls\n5. Maintain `aria-expanded` state for toggles\n\nThe core ARIA system will automatically apply roles for standard selector patterns, but extensions may need to manually set attributes for complex custom controls.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/a11y.js:61-84]()\n\n---\n\n**Page Summary:** SillyTavern's accessibility system provides comprehensive keyboard navigation, ARIA semantics, and dynamic focus styling through three coordinated modules. Extensions integrate by registering custom interactables and following established CSS and ARIA patterns. The system automatically adapts to mobile devices and ensures consistent user experience across input methods."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"30:T8c0e,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Data Management\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [default/config.yaml](default/config.yaml)\n- [default/public/error/unauthorized.html](default/public/error/unauthorized.html)\n- [post-install.js](post-install.js)\n- [public/index.html](public/index.html)\n- [public/script.js](public/script.js)\n- [public/scripts/bookmarks.js](public/scripts/bookmarks.js)\n- [public/scripts/group-chats.js](public/scripts/group-chats.js)\n- [public/scripts/instruct-mode.js](public/scripts/instruct-mode.js)\n- [public/scripts/power-user.js](public/scripts/power-user.js)\n- [public/scripts/utils.js](public/scripts/utils.js)\n- [public/scripts/world-info.js](public/scripts/world-info.js)\n- [public/style.css](public/style.css)\n- [server.js](server.js)\n- [src/endpoints/characters.js](src/endpoints/characters.js)\n- [src/endpoints/chats.js](src/endpoints/chats.js)\n- [src/endpoints/content-manager.js](src/endpoints/content-manager.js)\n- [src/endpoints/groups.js](src/endpoints/groups.js)\n- [src/endpoints/presets.js](src/endpoints/presets.js)\n- [src/endpoints/settings.js](src/endpoints/settings.js)\n- [src/middleware/basicAuth.js](src/middleware/basicAuth.js)\n- [src/middleware/whitelist.js](src/middleware/whitelist.js)\n- [src/users.js](src/users.js)\n- [src/util.js](src/util.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document covers the data persistence, storage, and caching systems in SillyTavern. It explains how character cards, chat messages, settings, and other user data are stored, organized, and managed across the filesystem.\n\nFor information about specific character card formats and import/export operations, see [Character Management](#5.1). For chat-specific features like backup scheduling and integrity checking, see [Chat Storage and Persistence](#5.2). For user-level settings hierarchy, see [Settings and Configuration Management](#5.3).\n\n---\n\n## Overview and Data Root Directory\n\nSillyTavern organizes all user data under a configurable `DATA_ROOT` directory. This root is set during server initialization and determines where all characters, chats, settings, and assets are stored.\n\n**Configuration and Initialization**\n\nThe data root is determined by command-line arguments and stored globally:\n\n```javascript\nglobalThis.DATA_ROOT = cliArgs.dataRoot\n```\n\nThe default data root is configured in [default/config.yaml:3]() as `./data`.\n\n**Directory Structure Template**\n\nEach user (or the default user in single-user mode) receives an isolated directory structure based on the `USER_DIRECTORY_TEMPLATE`:\n\n### User Directory Layout\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    DataRoot[\"DATA_ROOT/\"]\n    \n    DataRoot --\u003e UserHandle[\"user_handle/\"]\n    \n    UserHandle --\u003e Characters[\"characters/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Chats[\"chats/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Groups[\"groups/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e GroupChats[\"group chats/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Worlds[\"worlds/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Backgrounds[\"backgrounds/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e UserImages[\"User Avatars/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Sprites[\"characters/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Themes[\"themes/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Presets[\"OpenAI Settings/\\nKoboldAI Settings/\\nTextGen Settings/\\nNovelAI Settings/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Instruct[\"instruct/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Context[\"context/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e SysPrompt[\"sysprompt/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Reasoning[\"reasoning/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e QuickReplies[\"QuickReplies/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Backups[\"backups/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Uploads[\"uploads/\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Settings[\"settings.json\"]\n    UserHandle --\u003e Secrets[\"secrets.json\"]\n    \n    Chats --\u003e ChatFiles[\"character_name/\\n  chat1.jsonl\\n  chat2.jsonl\"]\n    GroupChats --\u003e GroupFiles[\"group_id/\\n  chat_id.jsonl\"]\n    Backups --\u003e ChatBackups[\"chat_backups/\"]\n    Backups --\u003e SettingsBackups[\"settings_backups/\"]\n    \n    DataRoot --\u003e Cache[\"_cache/\\n  characters/\\n  thumbnails/\"]\n```\n\n**User Directory Management**\n\nThe `getUserDirectories` function in [src/users.js:136-167]() builds the complete directory structure for each user. This function is called whenever user-scoped operations occur.\n\nSources: [src/users.js:136-167](), [src/constants.js](), [default/config.yaml:3](), [server.js:9]()\n\n---\n\n## File System Organization\n\n### Multi-User vs Single-User Mode\n\nSillyTavern supports two operational modes:\n\n| Mode | Configuration | User Handle | Directory Isolation |\n|------|--------------|-------------|---------------------|\n| **Single-User** | `enableUserAccounts: false` | `default` | All data under `DATA_ROOT/default/` |\n| **Multi-User** | `enableUserAccounts: true` | Per-user unique handle | Data under `DATA_ROOT/{handle}/` |\n\n**User Directory Retrieval**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Request[\"HTTP Request\"] --\u003e Middleware[\"requireLoginMiddleware\"]\n    Middleware --\u003e UserCheck{\"User\\nEnabled?\"}\n    UserCheck --\u003e|Yes| GetDirs[\"getUserDirectories(handle)\"]\n    UserCheck --\u003e|No| Error403[\"403 Forbidden\"]\n    GetDirs --\u003e CacheCheck{\"In\\nDIRECTORIES_CACHE?\"}\n    CacheCheck --\u003e|Yes| Return[\"Return Cached\"]\n    CacheCheck --\u003e|No| Build[\"Build from Template\"]\n    Build --\u003e Cache[\"Store in Cache\"]\n    Cache --\u003e Return\n```\n\nThe directories cache [src/users.js:36]() stores `UserDirectoryList` objects keyed by user handle to avoid repeated filesystem operations.\n\n**Directory Creation**\n\nDirectories are created lazily when first accessed. The `ensureDirectoriesExist` function [src/users.js:169-189]() creates all required subdirectories with appropriate permissions.\n\nSources: [src/users.js:36](), [src/users.js:136-167](), [src/users.js:169-189](), [default/config.yaml:104]()\n\n---\n\n## Character Card Storage Format\n\n### PNG + JSON Embedded Format\n\nCharacter cards in SillyTavern use a PNG image file format with JSON metadata embedded in PNG chunks. This allows a single `.png` file to contain both the character's avatar image and all associated data.\n\n**Character Card Structure**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    PNGFile[\"character.png\"]\n    \n    PNGFile --\u003e ImageData[\"PNG Image Data\\n(Visual Avatar)\"]\n    PNGFile --\u003e TextChunk[\"tEXt Chunk: 'chara'\"]\n    \n    TextChunk --\u003e Base64[\"Base64 Encoded String\"]\n    Base64 --\u003e JSONData[\"Character JSON\\n{\\n  name, description,\\n  first_mes, personality,\\n  scenario, mes_example,\\n  extensions: {...}\\n}\"]\n    \n    JSONData --\u003e CoreFields[\"Core Fields:\\nname, description,\\nfirst_mes, personality,\\nscenario\"]\n    JSONData --\u003e CharBook[\"Character Book:\\nworld_info\"]\n    JSONData --\u003e Extensions[\"Extensions:\\ndepth_prompt, expressions,\\nvector_index\"]\n```\n\n**Reading Character Data**\n\nThe `readCharacterData` function [src/endpoints/characters.js:181-209]() implements a multi-level caching strategy:\n\n1. Check memory cache (`MemoryLimitedMap`)\n2. Check disk cache (`DiskCache`)\n3. Parse PNG file using `parse()` from character-card-parser\n4. Store in both caches\n\n**Writing Character Data**\n\nThe `writeCharacterData` function [src/endpoints/characters.js:220-265]() handles character card creation and updates:\n\n1. Process input image (resize, crop if needed)\n2. Embed JSON data into PNG tEXt chunk using `write()`\n3. Write atomically to filesystem\n4. Invalidate caches\n\n**Character Card Parser**\n\nThe parser module [src/character-card-parser.js]() handles the low-level PNG chunk manipulation:\n\n- `parse(inputFile, inputFormat)` - Extracts JSON from PNG chunks\n- `read(buffer)` - Reads chunks from Buffer\n- `write(buffer, data)` - Writes JSON to PNG chunks\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:181-209](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:220-265](), [src/character-card-parser.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Caching Architecture\n\n### Two-Tier Cache System\n\nSillyTavern implements a sophisticated two-tier caching system for character data to minimize filesystem I/O and improve performance.\n\n**Cache Hierarchy**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Request[\"Character Data Request\"]\n    \n    Request --\u003e MemCheck{\"MemoryLimitedMap\\nhas data?\"}\n    MemCheck --\u003e|Hit| ReturnMem[\"Return from Memory\"]\n    MemCheck --\u003e|Miss| DiskCheck{\"DiskCache\\nhas data?\"}\n    \n    DiskCheck --\u003e|Hit| LoadDisk[\"Load from Disk Cache\"]\n    DiskCheck --\u003e|Miss| ParsePNG[\"Parse PNG File\"]\n    \n    LoadDisk --\u003e StoreMem[\"Store in Memory Cache\"]\n    ParsePNG --\u003e StoreBoth[\"Store in Both Caches\"]\n    \n    StoreMem --\u003e ReturnDisk[\"Return Data\"]\n    StoreBoth --\u003e ReturnParse[\"Return Data\"]\n    \n    ReturnMem --\u003e End[\"End\"]\n    ReturnDisk --\u003e End\n    ReturnParse --\u003e End\n```\n\n### Memory Cache (MemoryLimitedMap)\n\nThe memory cache [src/endpoints/characters.js:33]() is a size-limited in-memory store:\n\n```javascript\nconst memoryCacheCapacity = getConfigValue('performance.memoryCacheCapacity', '100mb');\nconst memoryCache = new MemoryLimitedMap(memoryCacheCapacity);\n```\n\n**Configuration**\n\n| Setting | Default | Purpose |\n|---------|---------|---------|\n| `performance.memoryCacheCapacity` | `'100mb'` | Maximum memory for cached character data |\n\n**Cache Key Format**\n\nCache keys include both file path and modification time to automatically invalidate stale entries:\n\n```javascript\nfunction getCacheKey(inputFile) {\n    if (fs.existsSync(inputFile)) {\n        const stat = fs.statSync(inputFile);\n        return `${inputFile}-${stat.mtimeMs}`;\n    }\n    return inputFile;\n}\n```\n\n**Memory Management**\n\nOn Android devices, the memory cache is bypassed [src/endpoints/characters.js:199]() to conserve resources:\n\n```javascript\n!isAndroid \u0026\u0026 memoryCache.set(cacheKey, result);\n```\n\n### Disk Cache (DiskCache)\n\nThe disk cache provides persistent caching across server restarts using the `node-persist` library.\n\n**DiskCache Implementation**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    DC[\"DiskCache Instance\"]\n    \n    DC --\u003e Storage[\"node-persist Storage\\nDATA_ROOT/_cache/characters/\"]\n    DC --\u003e SyncQueue[\"syncQueue: Set\u003cstring\u003e\"]\n    DC --\u003e Interval[\"Sync Interval\\n5 minutes\"]\n    \n    Interval --\u003e ProcessQueue[\"Process Sync Queue\"]\n    ProcessQueue --\u003e Verify[\"verify(directories)\"]\n    Verify --\u003e ValidKeys[\"Build Valid Keys Set\"]\n    ValidKeys --\u003e Prune[\"Remove Invalid Entries\"]\n    \n    SyncQueue --\u003e AddHandle[\"Add User Handle\\non Write\"]\n```\n\n**Key Methods**\n\n| Method | Purpose |\n|--------|---------|\n| `instance()` | Returns or initializes the node-persist storage |\n| `verify(directoriesList)` | Validates cache entries against actual files |\n| `syncQueue.add(handle)` | Queues a user for cache verification |\n\n**Cache Location**\n\nDisk cache is stored at `DATA_ROOT/_cache/characters/` [src/endpoints/characters.js:69](). Each cached item is hashed by `node-persist`.\n\n**Verification and Pruning**\n\nThe `verify` method [src/endpoints/characters.js:126-150]() runs periodically to:\n\n1. Build a set of valid cache keys from actual character files\n2. Remove any cache entries that no longer correspond to files\n3. Handle character deletions automatically\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-157](), [src/util.js]() (MemoryLimitedMap), [default/config.yaml:196-204]()\n\n---\n\n## Chat Storage Format\n\n### JSONL (JSON Lines) Structure\n\nChats are stored in the JSONL format, where each line is a separate JSON object representing either metadata or a single message.\n\n**Chat File Structure**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    ChatFile[\"chat_name.jsonl\"]\n    \n    ChatFile --\u003e Line0[\"Line 0: Metadata Header\\n{\\n  chat_metadata: {...},\\n  user_name,\\n  character_name\\n}\"]\n    \n    ChatFile --\u003e Line1[\"Line 1: Message 1\\n{\\n  mes, name, is_user,\\n  send_date, extra: {...}\\n}\"]\n    \n    ChatFile --\u003e Line2[\"Line 2: Message 2\"]\n    ChatFile --\u003e LineN[\"Line N: Message N\"]\n    \n    Line0 --\u003e Metadata[\"Metadata:\\n- tainted\\n- note_prompt\\n- note_depth\\n- world_info\\n- integrity (UUID)\"]\n    \n    Line1 --\u003e MessageFields[\"Message Fields:\\n- mes (text)\\n- name (speaker)\\n- is_user (boolean)\\n- send_date (ISO)\\n- swipes (array)\\n- swipe_id (number)\\n- extra (object)\"]\n```\n\n**Metadata Header (Line 0)**\n\nThe first line of every chat file contains the metadata header [src/endpoints/chats.js:147-164]():\n\n```javascript\n{\n  \"chat_metadata\": {\n    \"tainted\": false,\n    \"note_prompt\": \"\",\n    \"note_depth\": 4,\n    \"world_info\": \"...\",\n    \"integrity\": \"uuid-v4-string\"\n  },\n  \"user_name\": \"User\",\n  \"character_name\": \"Character\"\n}\n```\n\n**Integrity Checking**\n\nThe `integrity` field [default/config.yaml:176]() contains a UUID generated when the chat is created. This is verified on load to detect corruption:\n\n```javascript\nif (checkIntegrity) {\n    const valid = await checkChatIntegrity(chatPath);\n    if (!valid) {\n        console.warn(`Chat integrity check failed: ${chatPath}`);\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Chat File Locations\n\n**Character Chats**\n\nCharacter chats are stored at: `DATA_ROOT/{handle}/chats/{character_name}/{chat_name}.jsonl`\n\n**Group Chats**\n\nGroup chats are stored at: `DATA_ROOT/{handle}/group chats/{group_id}/{chat_id}.jsonl`\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/chats.js:147-164](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:176-235](), [default/config.yaml:176]()\n\n---\n\n## Backup System Architecture\n\n### Chat Backups\n\nSillyTavern implements an automatic backup system for chat files with throttling and rotation.\n\n**Backup Flow**\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant SaveEndpoint as \"/api/chats/save\"\n    participant BackupFunc as \"getBackupFunction()\"\n    participant Throttle as \"Throttled backupChat\"\n    participant FS as \"File System\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eSaveEndpoint: POST chat data\n    SaveEndpoint-\u003e\u003eSaveEndpoint: Write chat.jsonl\n    SaveEndpoint-\u003e\u003eBackupFunc: Get backup function\n    BackupFunc--\u003e\u003eSaveEndpoint: Throttled function\n    SaveEndpoint-\u003e\u003eThrottle: backupChat(dir, name, data)\n    \n    Note over Throttle: Throttle Interval:\\n10 seconds (default)\n    \n    Throttle-\u003e\u003eFS: Write to backups/chat_backups/\n    FS--\u003e\u003eThrottle: Success\n    Throttle-\u003e\u003eThrottle: removeOldBackups()\n    \n    Note over Throttle: Keep N backups per chat\\nand M total backups\n```\n\n**Backup Configuration**\n\n| Setting | Default | Description |\n|---------|---------|-------------|\n| `backups.chat.enabled` | `true` | Enable automatic chat backups |\n| `backups.chat.checkIntegrity` | `true` | Verify chat integrity before saving |\n| `backups.chat.maxTotalBackups` | `-1` | Max backups across all chats (-1 = unlimited) |\n| `backups.chat.throttleInterval` | `10000` | Milliseconds between backups per user |\n| `backups.common.numberOfBackups` | `50` | Backups to keep per individual chat |\n\n**Backup File Naming**\n\nBackup files follow the pattern: `chat_{sanitized_name}_{timestamp}.jsonl`\n\nExample: `chat_my_character_20240315_143022.jsonl`\n\n**Throttling Implementation**\n\nEach user has a separate throttled backup function [src/endpoints/chats.js:64-77]():\n\n```javascript\nconst backupFunctions = new Map();\n\nfunction getBackupFunction(handle) {\n    if (!backupFunctions.has(handle)) {\n        backupFunctions.set(handle, \n            _.throttle(backupChat, throttleInterval, \n                { leading: true, trailing: true }\n            )\n        );\n    }\n    return backupFunctions.get(handle);\n}\n```\n\nThis ensures:\n- Leading edge: First save triggers immediately\n- Trailing edge: Last save in a burst is not lost\n- Per-user isolation: One user's rapid saves don't affect others\n\n**Backup Rotation**\n\nTwo rotation strategies run in sequence:\n\n1. **Per-Chat Rotation**: Keep only the most recent N backups for each specific chat\n2. **Global Rotation**: Limit total backups across all chats to M files\n\n```javascript\nremoveOldBackups(directory, `${backupPrefix}${name}_`);\nif (maxTotalBackups \u003e= 0) {\n    removeOldBackups(directory, backupPrefix, maxTotalBackups);\n}\n```\n\n### Settings Backups\n\nSettings are also backed up automatically with a separate autosave mechanism [src/endpoints/settings.js:18-39]():\n\n**Autosave Mechanism**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    SettingsSave[\"POST /api/settings/save\"] --\u003e TriggerAuto[\"triggerAutoSave(handle)\"]\n    TriggerAuto --\u003e CheckFunc{\"Has autosave\\nfunction?\"}\n    CheckFunc --\u003e|No| CreateFunc[\"Create throttled function\\n10 minute interval\"]\n    CheckFunc --\u003e|Yes| GetFunc[\"Get existing function\"]\n    CreateFunc --\u003e Call[\"Call function\"]\n    GetFunc --\u003e Call\n    Call --\u003e BackupSettings[\"backupUserSettings()\"]\n    BackupSettings --\u003e WriteBackup[\"Write to\\nbackups/settings_backups/\"]\n```\n\n**Settings Backup Interval**\n\nSettings autosave is throttled to once every 10 minutes [src/endpoints/settings.js:18]():\n\n```javascript\nconst AUTOSAVE_INTERVAL = 10 * 60 * 1000;\n```\n\nThis prevents excessive backup file creation from frequent setting changes.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/chats.js:40-100](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:18-75](), [src/util.js]() (removeOldBackups), [default/config.yaml:166-180]()\n\n---\n\n## Atomic File Operations\n\n### Write Atomicity\n\nSillyTavern uses `write-file-atomic` to ensure file writes are atomic and crash-safe.\n\n**Atomic Write Pattern**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    WriteRequest[\"Write Operation\"]\n    \n    WriteRequest --\u003e TempFile[\"Create .tmp file\\nin same directory\"]\n    TempFile --\u003e WriteData[\"Write complete data\\nto temp file\"]\n    WriteData --\u003e Fsync[\"fsync() to disk\"]\n    Fsync --\u003e Rename[\"Atomic rename\\n.tmp -\u003e target\"]\n    Rename --\u003e Success[\"Success\"]\n    \n    WriteData --\u003e Error{\"Error?\"}\n    Error --\u003e|Yes| Cleanup[\"Delete .tmp file\"]\n    Cleanup --\u003e Fail[\"Operation Failed\"]\n```\n\n**Usage Throughout Codebase**\n\nAtomic writes are used for all critical data:\n\n- Character cards: [src/endpoints/characters.js:259]()\n- Chat files: [src/endpoints/chats.js]()\n- Settings: [src/endpoints/settings.js]()\n- Group metadata: [src/endpoints/groups.js:7]()\n\n```javascript\nimport { sync as writeFileAtomicSync } from 'write-file-atomic';\n\nwriteFileAtomicSync(outputImagePath, outputImage);\n```\n\n**Crash Safety**\n\nThis approach ensures:\n- **Durability**: Data is synced to disk before rename\n- **Atomicity**: The rename operation is atomic at the filesystem level\n- **Consistency**: Old file remains intact if write fails\n- **No Corruption**: Readers never see partial writes\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:259](), [src/endpoints/chats.js](), [src/endpoints/settings.js](), [src/util.js]() (tryWriteFileSync wrapper)\n\n---\n\n## Data Import and Export\n\n### Character Import Flow\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Import[\"Character Import Request\"] --\u003e DetectFormat{\"Detect Format\"}\n    \n    DetectFormat --\u003e PNG[\"PNG with embedded JSON\"]\n    DetectFormat --\u003e JSON[\"Raw JSON file\"]\n    DetectFormat --\u003e CharX[\"CharX Archive (.charx)\"]\n    DetectFormat --\u003e BYAF[\"BYAF Archive (.byaf)\"]\n    \n    PNG --\u003e ExtractPNG[\"extract PNG data\"]\n    JSON --\u003e ParseJSON[\"parse JSON\"]\n    CharX --\u003e UnzipCharX[\"unzip archive\"]\n    BYAF --\u003e UnzipBYAF[\"unzip archive\"]\n    \n    ExtractPNG --\u003e ValidateCard[\"TavernCardValidator.validate()\"]\n    ParseJSON --\u003e ValidateCard\n    UnzipCharX --\u003e ParseCharX[\"CharXParser.parse()\"]\n    UnzipBYAF --\u003e ParseBYAF[\"ByafParser.parse()\"]\n    \n    ParseCharX --\u003e ExtractAssets[\"Extract assets:\\n- sprites\\n- audio\\n- attachments\"]\n    ParseBYAF --\u003e ExtractAssets\n    \n    ValidateCard --\u003e CheckOverwrite{\"Overwrite\\ncheck\"}\n    ExtractAssets --\u003e CheckOverwrite\n    \n    CheckOverwrite --\u003e WritePNG[\"writeCharacterData()\"]\n    WritePNG --\u003e PersistAssets[\"Persist assets to\\nuser directories\"]\n    PersistAssets --\u003e UpdateCache[\"Invalidate caches\"]\n    UpdateCache --\u003e Complete[\"Import Complete\"]\n```\n\n**Character Archive Formats**\n\n| Format | Extension | Structure | Parser |\n|--------|-----------|-----------|--------|\n| **Standard** | `.png` | PNG with tEXt chunks | character-card-parser |\n| **CharX** | `.charx` | ZIP with assets | CharXParser |\n| **BYAF** | `.byaf` | ZIP with backyard.ai format | ByafParser |\n| **JSON** | `.json` | Plain character object | JSON.parse |\n\n**CharX Format**\n\nThe CharX format [src/charx.js]() extends the standard format with additional assets:\n\n```\narchive.charx (ZIP)\n├── card.png          # Character card\n├── assets/\n│   ├── sprites/      # Expression sprites\n│   ├── audio/        # Voice samples\n│   └── attachments/  # Other files\n└── metadata.json     # Asset manifest\n```\n\nAfter import, `persistCharXAssets` [src/charx.js]() extracts assets to appropriate user directories.\n\n### Export Operations\n\n**Character Export**\n\nThe `/api/characters/export` endpoint [src/endpoints/characters.js]() handles character export:\n\n1. Read character PNG file\n2. Optionally bundle with assets (CharX format)\n3. Stream to client\n\n**Chat Export**\n\nChat export can produce multiple formats:\n\n- **JSONL**: Native format, preserves all metadata\n- **Plain Text**: Human-readable conversation\n- **Markdown**: Formatted with speaker names\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js](), [src/charx.js](), [src/byaf.js](), [src/validator/TavernCardValidator.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Secrets Storage\n\n### Encrypted API Key Storage\n\nAPI keys and sensitive credentials are stored in `secrets.json` with optional encryption.\n\n**Secrets Storage Location**\n\nPer-user secrets file: `DATA_ROOT/{handle}/secrets.json`\n\n**Storage Structure**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    SecretsFile[\"secrets.json\"]\n    \n    SecretsFile --\u003e APIKeys[\"API Keys:\\n- api_key_novel\\n- api_key_openai\\n- api_key_claude\"]\n    SecretsFile --\u003e Endpoints[\"Custom Endpoints:\\n- api_url_scale\\n- sd_base\"]\n    SecretsFile --\u003e Models[\"Model Settings:\\n- chat_completion_source\\n- model_*\"]\n    \n    APIKeys --\u003e ReadOp[\"readSecret(key)\"]\n    APIKeys --\u003e WriteOp[\"writeSecret(key, value)\"]\n    \n    ReadOp --\u003e Decrypt[\"Decrypt if needed\"]\n    WriteOp --\u003e Encrypt[\"Encrypt if needed\"]\n```\n\n**Read/Write Operations**\n\nThe secrets API [src/endpoints/secrets.js]() provides:\n\n- `readSecret(user, key)` - Retrieve a secret value\n- `writeSecret(user, key, value)` - Store a secret value\n- Automatic encryption/decryption based on configuration\n\n**Endpoints**\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |\n|----------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/secrets/read` | POST | Read a specific secret |\n| `/api/secrets/write` | POST | Write a secret value |\n| `/api/secrets/find` | POST | List all secret keys |\n\n**Security Considerations**\n\n- Secrets are stored per-user in multi-user mode\n- Optional encryption at rest\n- Never exposed in logs\n- `allowKeysExposure` setting [default/config.yaml:216]() controls API access\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/secrets.js](), [public/scripts/secrets.js](), [default/config.yaml:216]()\n\n---\n\n## Settings Hierarchy\n\n### Multi-Level Settings System\n\nSillyTavern implements a hierarchical settings system with three levels:\n\n**Settings Levels**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    GlobalSettings[\"Global Settings\\nsettings.json\\n(User-wide defaults)\"]\n    \n    GlobalSettings --\u003e CharSettings[\"Character Settings\\n(Overrides for specific character)\"]\n    GlobalSettings --\u003e ChatSettings[\"Chat Settings\\nchat_metadata\\n(Overrides for specific chat)\"]\n    \n    CharSettings --\u003e Runtime[\"Runtime Merged Settings\"]\n    ChatSettings --\u003e Runtime\n    \n    Runtime --\u003e ContextTemplate[\"Context Template\"]\n    Runtime --\u003e InstructMode[\"Instruct Mode\"]\n    Runtime --\u003e SysPrompt[\"System Prompt\"]\n    Runtime --\u003e APISettings[\"API Settings\"]\n```\n\n**Settings File Location**\n\nGlobal settings: `DATA_ROOT/{handle}/settings.json`\n\n**Settings Scope**\n\n| Level | Scope | Storage Location | Priority |\n|-------|-------|------------------|----------|\n| **Global** | All chats | `settings.json` | Lowest |\n| **Character** | Specific character | Character card `extensions` | Medium |\n| **Chat** | Specific conversation | `chat_metadata` in JSONL | Highest |\n\n**Settings Save Flow**\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant Endpoint as \"/api/settings/save\"\n    participant Validate as \"Validation\"\n    participant FS as \"File System\"\n    participant Backup as \"Backup System\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eEndpoint: POST settings JSON\n    Endpoint-\u003e\u003eValidate: Check structure\n    Validate--\u003e\u003eEndpoint: Valid\n    Endpoint-\u003e\u003eFS: Atomic write to settings.json\n    FS--\u003e\u003eEndpoint: Success\n    Endpoint-\u003e\u003eBackup: triggerAutoSave(handle)\n    Backup--\u003e\u003eEndpoint: Scheduled\n    Endpoint--\u003e\u003eClient: 200 OK\n    \n    Note over Backup: Backup created after\\n10 minute throttle\n```\n\n**Debounced Saving**\n\nClient-side saving is debounced [public/script.js:441]():\n\n```javascript\nexport const saveSettingsDebounced = debounce(\n    (loopCounter) =\u003e saveSettings(loopCounter), \n    DEFAULT_SAVE_EDIT_TIMEOUT\n);\n```\n\nThis prevents excessive writes during rapid setting changes.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/settings.js](), [public/script.js:441](), [public/scripts/power-user.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Asset Management\n\n### Image and File Storage\n\nSillyTavern manages various types of assets across the user directory structure.\n\n**Asset Type Directory Mapping**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Assets[\"User Assets\"]\n    \n    Assets --\u003e Avatars[\"Character Avatars\\ncharacters/*.png\"]\n    Assets --\u003e UserAvatars[\"User Avatars\\nUser Avatars/*.png\"]\n    Assets --\u003e Backgrounds[\"Backgrounds\\nbackgrounds/*.png,jpg,webp\"]\n    Assets --\u003e Sprites[\"Character Sprites\\ncharacters/{name}/*.png\"]\n    Assets --\u003e Themes[\"UI Themes\\nthemes/*.css\"]\n    Assets --\u003e Uploads[\"File Uploads\\nuploads/*\"]\n    \n    Avatars --\u003e AvatarThumb[\"Thumbnail\\nthumbnails/avatar/\"]\n    Backgrounds --\u003e BgThumb[\"Thumbnail\\nthumbnails/bg/\"]\n    UserAvatars --\u003e PersonaThumb[\"Thumbnail\\nthumbnails/persona/\"]\n```\n\n**Thumbnail Generation**\n\nThumbnails are automatically generated 144]\n```\n\n**Thumbnail Flow**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Upload[\"Asset Upload\"] --\u003e CheckThumb{\"Thumbnails\\nenabled?\"}\n    CheckThumb --\u003e|Yes| Generate[\"Generate thumbnail\"]\n
