[src/endpoints/presets.js](src/endpoints/presets.js)\n- [src/endpoints/settings.js](src/endpoints/settings.js)\n- [src/middleware/basicAuth.js](src/middleware/basicAuth.js)\n- [src/middleware/whitelist.js](src/middleware/whitelist.js)\n- [src/users.js](src/users.js)\n- [src/util.js](src/util.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document covers the management of character cards in SillyTavern: their storage format, import/export mechanisms, caching strategies, and API endpoints for character operations. Character cards contain the personality, description, and metadata for AI characters that users interact with.\n\nFor information about chat message storage, see [Chat Storage and Persistence](#5.2). For character sprite assets and expressions, see [Asset and File Management](#5.4).\n\n---\n\n## Character Card Specification\n\n### Spec V2 Format\n\nSillyTavern uses the Character Card V2 specification as its primary format. Character data is embedded as JSON metadata within PNG image files.\n\n**Core Structure:**\n\n```javascript\n{\n  \"spec\": \"chara_card_v2\",\n  \"spec_version\": \"2.0\",\n  \"data\": {\n    \"name\": \"Character Name\",\n    \"description\": \"Physical description and traits\",\n    \"personality\": \"Personality traits\",\n    \"scenario\": \"Current scenario or context\",\n    \"first_mes\": \"First message from character\",\n    \"mes_example\": \"Example dialogue\",\n    \"creator_notes\": \"Notes from the character creator\",\n    \"system_prompt\": \"System-level instructions\",\n    \"post_history_instructions\": \"Instructions applied after history\",\n    \"alternate_greetings\": [\"Greeting 1\", \"Greeting 2\"],\n    \"character_version\": \"1.0\",\n    \"tags\": [\"tag1\", \"tag2\"],\n    \"creator\": \"Creator name\",\n    \"character_book\": { /* embedded lorebook */ },\n    \"extensions\": {\n      \"talkativeness\": 0.5,\n      \"fav\": false,\n      \"world\": \"worldinfo_file.json\",\n      \"depth_prompt\": {\n        \"prompt\": \"Depth prompt text\",\n        \"depth\": 4,\n        \"role\": \"system\"\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:445-658]()\n\n### Spec V1 (Legacy) Format\n\nLegacy V1 format uses flat structure at the root level. When loading V1 cards, they are automatically converted to V2 format.\n\n```javascript\n{\n  \"name\": \"Character Name\",\n  \"description\": \"Description text\",\n  \"personality\": \"Personality text\",\n  \"scenario\": \"Scenario text\",\n  \"first_mes\": \"First message\",\n  \"mes_example\": \"Example messages\",\n  \"creatorcomment\": \"Creator notes\",\n  \"avatar\": \"none\",\n  \"chat\": \"Character Name - 2024-01-01@12h00m00s000ms\",\n  \"talkativeness\": 0.5,\n  \"fav\": false,\n  \"create_date\": \"2024-01-01T12:00:00.000Z\"\n}\n```\n\nThe conversion process merges V1 fields into the V2 `data` structure and preserves any unknown fields for compatibility.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:470-494](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:505-558]()\n\n### Format Conversion Pipeline\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Input[\"Character Input\u003cbr/\u003e(PNG/JSON/YAML)\"]\n    Parse[\"parse()\u003cbr/\u003eExtract JSON from PNG\"]\n    Detect[\"Format Detection\"]\n    V1Check{\"Spec V1?\"}\n    V2Check{\"Spec V2?\"}\n    ConvertV1[\"convertToV2()\u003cbr/\u003eTransform structure\"]\n    ReadV2[\"readFromV2()\u003cbr/\u003eMap fields\"]\n    Output[\"Standardized V2 Format\"]\n    \n    Input --\u003e Parse\n    Parse --\u003e Detect\n    Detect --\u003e V1Check\n    Detect --\u003e V2Check\n    V1Check --\u003e|\"spec undefined\"| ConvertV1\n    V2Check --\u003e|\"spec === 'chara_card_v2'\"| ReadV2\n    ConvertV1 --\u003e Output\n    ReadV2 --\u003e Output\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:451-462](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:470-494](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:505-558]()\n\n---\n\n## File System Organization\n\n### Directory Structure\n\nCharacter data is stored in per-user directory structures:\n\n```\ndata/\n└── {user_handle}/\n    ├── characters/           # Character card PNG files\n    │   ├── character1.png\n    │   ├── character2.png\n    │   └── {character_name}/ # Character-specific assets\n    │       └── expressions/  # Expression sprites (see 8.3)\n    ├── chats/               # Chat logs per character (see 5.2)\n    │   ├── character1/\n    │   └── character2/\n    └── worlds/              # World Info files (see 6.1)\n```\n\nEach character is stored as a PNG file containing embedded JSON metadata. The filename serves as the unique identifier for the character.\n\nSources: [src/users.js:71-103](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:408-442]()\n\n### Character Metadata\n\nWhen loading the character list, the system calculates additional metadata:\n\n| Field | Type | Description |\n|-------|------|-------------|\n| `avatar` | string | Filename of the character PNG |\n| `date_added` | number | File creation timestamp (ms) |\n| `create_date` | string | ISO 8601 creation date |\n| `date_last_chat` | number | Most recent chat modification time (ms) |\n| `chat_size` | number | Total size of all chat files in bytes |\n| `data_size` | number | Sum of string lengths in character data |\n| `json_data` | string | Serialized character JSON |\n| `shallow` | boolean | If true, only core metadata loaded (lazy loading) |\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:366-395](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:406-442]()\n\n---\n\n## Character Card Reading and Processing\n\n### Read Pipeline\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Request[\"Client Request\"]\n    CheckCache[\"Check MemoryCache\"]\n    CacheHit{\"Cache Hit?\"}\n    CheckDisk[\"Check DiskCache\"]\n    DiskHit{\"Disk Hit?\"}\n    ReadFile[\"parse()\u003cbr/\u003eRead PNG + Extract JSON\"]\n    WriteDisk[\"Write to DiskCache\"]\n    WriteMem[\"Write to MemoryCache\"]\n    Return[\"Return Character Data\"]\n    \n    Request --\u003e CheckCache\n    CheckCache --\u003e CacheHit\n    CacheHit --\u003e|Yes| Return\n    CacheHit --\u003e|No| CheckDisk\n    CheckDisk --\u003e DiskHit\n    DiskHit --\u003e|Yes| WriteMem\n    DiskHit --\u003e|No| ReadFile\n    ReadFile --\u003e WriteDisk\n    WriteDisk --\u003e WriteMem\n    WriteMem --\u003e Return\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:176-209]()\n\n### Reading Character Data\n\nThe `readCharacterData()` function implements a two-tier caching strategy:\n\n```javascript\nasync function readCharacterData(inputFile, inputFormat = 'png') {\n    const cacheKey = getCacheKey(inputFile);\n    \n    // Check memory cache\n    if (memoryCache.has(cacheKey)) {\n        return memoryCache.get(cacheKey);\n    }\n    \n    // Check disk cache\n    if (useDiskCache) {\n        const cache = await diskCache.instance();\n        const cachedData = await cache.getItem(cacheKey);\n        if (cachedData) {\n            return cachedData;\n        }\n    }\n    \n    // Read from file\n    const result = await parse(inputFile, inputFormat);\n    \n    // Populate caches\n    !isAndroid \u0026\u0026 memoryCache.set(cacheKey, result);\n    if (useDiskCache) {\n        const cache = await diskCache.instance();\n        await cache.setItem(cacheKey, result);\n    }\n    \n    return result;\n}\n```\n\nCache keys include the file modification time to automatically invalidate stale entries: `{filePath}-{mtimeMs}`.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:176-209](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:162-173]()\n\n### Character Processing\n\nThe `processCharacter()` function loads a character and calculates its metadata:\n\n1. **Read Image**: Load PNG file and extract embedded JSON\n2. **Parse Format**: Convert to V2 format if needed via `getCharaCardV2()`\n3. **Calculate Stats**: Compute chat size, last chat date, and data size\n4. **Shallow Mode**: If lazy loading enabled, return only core fields via `toShallow()`\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:406-442](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:371-395]()\n\n---\n\n## Caching Architecture\n\n### DiskCache\n\nThe `DiskCache` class provides persistent storage for parsed character data using the `node-persist` library.\n\n**Key Features:**\n\n| Property | Value | Description |\n|----------|-------|-------------|\n| Cache Directory | `{DATA_ROOT}/_cache/characters` | Persistent storage location |\n| Sync Interval | 5 minutes | Periodic verification of cache validity |\n| TTL | false | No automatic expiration |\n| Max File Descriptors | 100 | Concurrent file handle limit |\n\n**Cache Verification:**\n\nThe cache automatically prunes entries for deleted character files every 5 minutes:\n\n```javascript\nasync verify(directoriesList) {\n    const cache = await this.instance();\n    const validKeys = new Set();\n    \n    // Collect valid cache keys from existing files\n    for (const dir of directoriesList) {\n        const files = fs.readdirSync(dir.characters, { withFileTypes: true });\n        for (const file of files.filter(f =\u003e f.isFile() \u0026\u0026 path.extname(f.name) === '.png')) {\n            const filePath = path.join(dir.characters, file.name);\n            const cacheKey = getCacheKey(filePath);\n            validKeys.add(path.parse(cache.getDatumPath(cacheKey)).base);\n        }\n    }\n    \n    // Remove orphaned cache entries\n    for (const key of this.hashedKeys) {\n        if (!validKeys.has(key)) {\n            await cache.removeItem(key);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-157](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:122-150]()\n\n### MemoryLimitedMap\n\nIn-memory cache with automatic eviction when capacity is exceeded. Default capacity is 100MB, configurable via `performance.memoryCacheCapacity` in `config.yaml`.\n\nAndroid devices automatically skip memory caching to manage tight memory constraints.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:29-31](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:199]()\n\n### Cache Invalidation\n\nCaches are invalidated in two ways:\n\n1. **Automatic**: Cache keys include file modification time, so updates automatically create new cache entries\n2. **Manual**: When writing character data, relevant cache entries are explicitly deleted and the user handle is added to `diskCache.syncQueue` for verification\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:220-234]()\n\n---\n\n## Character Import Mechanisms\n\n### Import Format Detection\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Upload[\"File Upload\"]\n    ExtCheck{\"File Extension\"}\n    \n    PNG[\"PNG File\"]\n    JSON[\"JSON File\"]\n    YAML[\"YAML/YML File\"]\n    ZIP[\"ZIP File\"]\n    \n    PNGImport[\"Standard PNG Import\u003cbr/\u003eRead embedded JSON\"]\n    JSONCheck{\"Has 'spec' field?\"}\n    V2Import[\"importFromJson()\u003cbr/\u003eSpec V2 Card\"]\n    V1Import[\"importFromJson()\u003cbr/\u003eSpec V1 Card\"]\n    YAMLImport[\"importFromYaml()\u003cbr/\u003eTavern YAML format\"]\n    ZIPCheck{\"ZIP Type?\"}\n    CharXImport[\"importFromCharX()\u003cbr/\u003eCharX format\"]\n    BYAFImport[\"importFromByaf()\u003cbr/\u003eBYAF format\"]\n    \n    Success[\"writeCharacterData()\u003cbr/\u003eSave to disk\"]\n    \n    Upload --\u003e ExtCheck\n    ExtCheck --\u003e|\".png\"| PNG\n    ExtCheck --\u003e|\".json\"| JSON\n    ExtCheck --\u003e|\".yaml/.yml\"| YAML\n    ExtCheck --\u003e|\".zip\"| ZIP\n    \n    PNG --\u003e PNGImport\n    PNGImport --\u003e Success\n    \n    JSON --\u003e JSONCheck\n    JSONCheck --\u003e|\"spec exists\"| V2Import\n    JSONCheck --\u003e|\"name exists\"| V1Import\n    V2Import --\u003e Success\n    V1Import --\u003e Success\n    \n    YAML --\u003e YAMLImport\n    YAMLImport --\u003e Success\n    \n    ZIP --\u003e ZIPCheck\n    ZIPCheck --\u003e|\"CharX\"| CharXImport\n    ZIPCheck --\u003e|\"BYAF\"| BYAFImport\n    CharXImport --\u003e Success\n    BYAFImport --\u003e Success\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:1062-1261]()\n\n### PNG Import\n\nStandard character card import. The PNG file contains embedded JSON metadata in tEXt chunks.\n\n**Process:**\n1. Parse PNG with `parse()` from character-card-parser\n2. Extract JSON from tEXt chunk (key: `chara`)\n3. Convert to V2 format if needed\n4. Write to characters directory\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:1062-1083]()\n\n### JSON Import\n\nImports character data from JSON files. Supports both V1 and V2 formats.\n\n**V2 Detection**: Presence of `spec` field with value `\"chara_card_v2\"`\n\n**V1 Detection**: Presence of `name` field without `spec`\n\nAdditional handling:\n- RisuAI sprite extraction via `importRisuSprites()`\n- Private fields removed (`fav`, `chat`) via `unsetPrivateFields()`\n- Default avatar applied if no image provided\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:882-923]()\n\n### YAML Import\n\nImports from Tavern YAML format (simplified character definition):\n\n```yaml\nname: Character Name\ncontext: Description text\ngreeting: First message\n```\n\nThe importer converts this minimal format to full V2 specification with default values.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:733-757]()\n\n### CharX Import\n\nCharX is a ZIP-based format containing:\n- Character card JSON\n- Avatar image\n- Auxiliary assets (sprites, backgrounds, misc files)\n\n**Import Process:**\n1. Parse ZIP buffer with `CharXParser`\n2. Extract card JSON and avatar image\n3. Process and sanitize character data\n4. Persist auxiliary assets via `persistCharXAssets()`\n5. Write character card with avatar\n\nAssets are organized by character name:\n```\ncharacters/{character_name}/  # Sprites\nbackgrounds/                   # Backgrounds\nassets/                       # Misc files\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:767-800]()\n\n### BYAF Import\n\nBYAF (Build Your AI Friend) format includes:\n- Character card\n- Multiple chat scenarios\n- Chat backgrounds\n- Alternate character icons\n\n**Import Process:**\n1. Parse BYAF ZIP with `ByafParser`\n2. Extract character card and primary icon\n3. Import chat backgrounds to user images\n4. Create chat files for each scenario\n5. Save alternate icons to character subdirectory\n\n**Note**: BYAF does not support character expressions, so alternate icons are stored but not mapped to expression system.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:802-873]()\n\n---\n\n## Character Export Operations\n\n### Export Format\n\nCharacters can be exported in two formats:\n\n**1. PNG Format (Default)**\n- Character card with embedded JSON metadata\n- Preserves full V2 specification\n- Includes embedded character book if present\n\n**2. JSON Format**\n- Plain JSON file with character data\n- Used for backup or external editing\n\n### Export Process\n\n```javascript\n// Client requests export\nPOST /api/characters/export\n{\n  \"avatar_url\": \"character.png\",\n  \"format\": \"png\" // or \"json\"\n}\n\n// Server responds with file download\n```\n\nThe export process:\n1. Reads character data from file\n2. Removes private fields (`fav`, `chat`) via `unsetPrivateFields()`\n3. Serializes to requested format\n4. Returns as downloadable file\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:499-503]()\n\n---\n\n## Character Writing and Image Processing\n\n### Write Pipeline\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Input[\"Character Data + Image\"]\n    ImageSource{\"Image Source\"}\n    Buffer[\"Buffer\u003cbr/\u003e(in-memory)\"]\n    File[\"File Path\u003cbr/\u003e(disk)\"]\n    \n    ParseBuffer[\"parseImageBuffer()\u003cbr/\u003eDecode + Resize\"]\n    ReadFile[\"tryReadImage()\u003cbr/\u003eLoad + Resize\"]\n    Fallback[\"Use DEFAULT_AVATAR_PATH\"]\n    \n    Crop[\"applyAvatarCropResize()\u003cbr/\u003e400x600 standard size\"]\n    Embed[\"write()\u003cbr/\u003eEmbed JSON in PNG tEXt\"]\n    Save[\"writeFileAtomicSync()\u003cbr/\u003eSave to disk\"]\n    InvalidateCache[\"Invalidate Cache\u003cbr/\u003eQueue sync\"]\n    \n    Input --\u003e ImageSource\n    ImageSource --\u003e|\"Buffer\"| Buffer\n    ImageSource --\u003e|\"File\"| File\n    \n    Buffer --\u003e ParseBuffer\n    File --\u003e ReadFile\n    ParseBuffer --\u003e|\"Error\"| Fallback\n    ReadFile --\u003e|\"Error\"| Fallback\n    \n    ParseBuffer --\u003e Crop\n    ReadFile --\u003e Crop\n    Fallback --\u003e Crop\n    \n    Crop --\u003e Embed\n    Embed --\u003e Save\n    Save --\u003e InvalidateCache\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:220-265]()\n\n### Image Processing\n\nThe `applyAvatarCropResize()` function processes character avatars:\n\n**Standard Dimensions**: 400x600 pixels (AVATAR_WIDTH × AVATAR_HEIGHT)\n\n**Operations**:\n1. **Crop**: Apply user-defined crop rectangle if specified\n2. **Resize**: If `want_resize` enabled, scale to standard dimensions\n3. **Cover**: Use cover mode to maintain aspect ratio\n4. **Encode**: Output as PNG buffer\n\n```javascript\nasync function applyAvatarCropResize(jimp, crop) {\n    let finalWidth = image.bitmap.width;\n    let finalHeight = image.bitmap.height;\n    \n    // Apply crop if defined\n    if (crop \u0026\u0026 [crop.x, crop.y, crop.width, crop.height].every(x =\u003e typeof x === 'number')) {\n        image.crop({ x: crop.x, y: crop.y, w: crop.width, h: crop.height });\n        \n        if (crop.want_resize) {\n            finalWidth = AVATAR_WIDTH;\n            finalHeight = AVATAR_HEIGHT;\n        } else {\n            finalWidth = crop.width;\n            finalHeight = crop.height;\n        }\n    }\n    \n    image.cover({ w: finalWidth, h: finalHeight });\n    return await image.getBuffer(JimpMime.png);\n}\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:283-306]()\n\n---\n\n## API Endpoints\n\n### Character CRUD Operations\n\n#### GET /api/characters/all\n\nReturns list of all characters for the authenticated user.\n\n**Response Format**:\n```javascript\n[\n  {\n    \"avatar\": \"character.png\",\n    \"name\": \"Character Name\",\n    \"chat\": \"Character Name - 2024-01-01@12h00m00s000ms\",\n    \"date_added\": 1704110400000,\n    \"create_date\": \"2024-01-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\n    \"date_last_chat\": 1704196800000,\n    \"chat_size\": 524288,\n    \"data_size\": 1024,\n    \"fav\": false,\n    \"tags\": [\"tag1\", \"tag2\"],\n    \"data\": { /* V2 data object */ },\n    \"shallow\": false\n  }\n]\n```\n\n**Shallow Mode**: If `performance.lazyLoadCharacters` is enabled, only core metadata is returned (`shallow: true`), reducing memory usage for large character libraries.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:1337-1405]()\n\n#### POST /api/characters/create\n\nCreates a new character or imports from file upload.\n\n**Request**:\n```javascript\n// Form-data multipart upload\n{\n  \"avatar\": File,           // Character image\n  \"ch_name\": \"Name\",\n  \"description\": \"...\",\n  \"personality\": \"...\",\n  \"scenario\": \"...\",\n  \"first_mes\": \"...\",\n  \"mes_example\": \"...\",\n  // ... other V2 fields\n}\n```\n\n**Response**:\n```javascript\n{\n  \"file_name\": \"character.png\"\n}\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:1062-1261]()\n\n#### POST /api/characters/edit\n\nUpdates an existing character.\n\n**Request**:\n```javascript\n{\n  \"avatar_url\": \"character.png\",\n  \"ch_name\": \"Updated Name\",\n  \"description\": \"Updated description\",\n  // ... other fields to update\n}\n```\n\n**Crop Support**: Optionally accepts `crop` parameters for avatar image:\n```javascript\n{\n  \"crop\": {\n    \"x\": 0,\n    \"y\": 0,\n    \"width\": 400,\n    \"height\": 600,\n    \"want_resize\": true\n  }\n}\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:1282-1335]()\n\n#### POST /api/characters/delete\n\nDeletes a character and optionally its associated data.\n\n**Request**:\n```javascript\n{\n  \"avatar_url\": \"character.png\",\n  \"delete_chats\": true  // Also delete chat history\n}\n```\n\nThe endpoint removes:\n- Character PNG file\n- Character asset directory (sprites, etc.)\n- Chat files (if `delete_chats` is true)\n- Thumbnail cache entry\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:1407-1475]()\n\n#### POST /api/characters/chats\n\nReturns list of chat files for a character.\n\n**Request**:\n```javascript\n{\n  \"avatar_url\": \"character.png\"\n}\n```\n\n**Response**:\n```javascript\n[\n  {\n    \"file_name\": \"chat1.jsonl\",\n    \"file_id\": \"chat1\",\n    \"file_size\": \"512 KB\",\n    \"chat_items\": 150,\n    \"mes\": \"Last message preview...\",\n    \"last_mes\": \"2024-01-01T12:00:00.000Z\"\n  }\n]\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:1477-1545]()\n\n### Character Duplication\n\n#### POST /api/characters/duplicate\n\nCreates a copy of an existing character with a unique name.\n\n**Request**:\n```javascript\n{\n  \"avatar_url\": \"original.png\"\n}\n```\n\n**Process**:\n1. Read original character data\n2. Generate unique name via `getUniqueName()`\n3. Parse and modify character data\n4. Write as new character file\n5. Return new filename\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:1547-1592]()\n\n---\n\n## Character Name Generation\n\nWhen importing characters, the system ensures unique filenames using `getPngName()`:\n\n```javascript\nfunction getPngName(name, directories) {\n    const baseName = sanitize(name);\n    const existingFiles = fs.readdirSync(directories.characters)\n        .filter(f =\u003e path.extname(f) === '.png')\n        .map(f =\u003e path.parse(f).name);\n    \n    return getUniqueName(\n        baseName,\n        (x) =\u003e existingFiles.includes(x),\n        { startIndex: 0 }\n    ) + '.png';\n}\n```\n\nThe function:\n1. Sanitizes the character name for filesystem compatibility\n2. Checks existing character files\n3. Appends ` (N)` suffix if name conflicts exist\n4. Returns safe filename with `.png` extension\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:1594-1616]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration with Other Systems\n\n### World Info Integration\n\nCharacters can have associated World Info files through the `data.extensions.world` field. When converting V1 to V2 format or importing characters with world info:\n\n1. Read world info file from `worlds` directory\n2. If file has `originalData`, embed as `character_book` in V2 format\n3. Otherwise, convert entries to character book format via `convertWorldInfoToCharacterBook()`\n\nThis embeds lorebook data directly in the character card for portability.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:629-646](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:665-724]()\n\n### Chat History Integration\n\nCharacter metadata includes chat statistics:\n- `chat_size`: Total bytes of all chat files\n- `date_last_chat`: Most recent chat modification time\n\nCalculated by scanning the `chats/{character_name}/` directory.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:338-359]()\n\n### Thumbnail Generation\n\nCharacter avatars are automatically processed for thumbnail generation (see configuration in `config.yaml`). Thumbnails are invalidated when characters are updated via `invalidateThumbnail()`.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:21]()\n\n---\n\n## Configuration Options\n\nCharacter management behavior is controlled by `config.yaml`:\n\n| Setting | Type | Default | Description |\n|---------|------|---------|-------------|\n| `performance.lazyLoadCharacters` | boolean | false | Enable shallow character loading (metadata only) |\n| `performance.memoryCacheCapacity` | string | '100mb' | Maximum memory cache size for character data |\n| `performance.useDiskCache` | boolean | true | Enable persistent disk caching |\n\n**Memory Management**:\n- Android devices always skip memory caching\n- Disk cache is verified every 5 minutes\n- Cache capacity is enforced via `MemoryLimitedMap`\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:35-36](), [default/config.yaml:196-204]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"32:T6425,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Chat Storage and Persistence\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [default/config.yaml](default/config.yaml)\n- [default/public/error/unauthorized.html](default/public/error/unauthorized.html)\n- [post-install.js](post-install.js)\n- [public/scripts/bookmarks.js](public/scripts/bookmarks.js)\n- [server.js](server.js)\n- [src/endpoints/characters.js](src/endpoints/characters.js)\n- [src/endpoints/chats.js](src/endpoints/chats.js)\n- [src/endpoints/content-manager.js](src/endpoints/content-manager.js)\n- [src/endpoints/groups.js](src/endpoints/groups.js)\n- [src/endpoints/presets.js](src/endpoints/presets.js)\n- [src/endpoints/settings.js](src/endpoints/settings.js)\n- [src/middleware/basicAuth.js](src/middleware/basicAuth.js)\n- [src/middleware/whitelist.js](src/middleware/whitelist.js)\n- [src/users.js](src/users.js)\n- [src/util.js](src/util.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document explains how SillyTavern stores, saves, loads, and manages chat conversation data on disk. It covers the JSONL file format, storage directory structure, save/load operations, automatic backup system, integrity checking, and bookmark/branch functionality. For information about character card storage, see [Character Management](#5.1). For configuration and settings persistence, see [Settings and Configuration Management](#5.3).\n\n## JSONL File Format\n\nSillyTavern stores all chat conversations in **JSONL** (JSON Lines) format, where each line is a complete, self-contained JSON object. The first line contains metadata and header information, while subsequent lines represent individual messages.\n\n### Chat Header Structure\n\nThe first line of every chat file is a header object:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"chat_metadata\": {\n    \"main_chat\": \"Character Name - 2024-01-15@14h30m00s\",\n    \"integrity\": \"unique-chat-identifier\",\n    \"note_prompt\": \"Custom system note\",\n    \"...\": \"other metadata fields\"\n  },\n  \"user_name\": \"unused\",\n  \"character_name\": \"unused\"\n}\n```\n\nThe `user_name` and `character_name` fields in the header are legacy placeholders and are not actively used. The actual message metadata is stored within each message object.\n\n### Message Structure\n\nEach subsequent line represents a message:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"name\": \"Character Name\",\n  \"is_user\": false,\n  \"is_system\": false,\n  \"send_date\": \"2024-01-15T14:30:00.000Z\",\n  \"mes\": \"The actual message text goes here\",\n  \"swipes\": [\"Alternative response 1\", \"Alternative response 2\"],\n  \"swipe_id\": 0,\n  \"swipe_info\": [],\n  \"gen_started\": 1705329000000,\n  \"gen_finished\": 1705329005000,\n  \"extra\": {\n    \"api\": \"openai\",\n    \"model\": \"gpt-4\",\n    \"token_count\": 150,\n    \"bookmark_link\": \"checkpoint_name\",\n    \"branches\": [\"branch1\", \"branch2\"]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:1-660]()\n\n## Storage Directory Structure\n\nChat files are stored in user-specific directories with separate locations for character chats and group chats.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    DataRoot[\"DATA_ROOT\u003cbr/\u003e(configurable data directory)\"]\n    UserRoot[\"user_handle/\u003cbr/\u003e(per-user directory)\"]\n    ChatsDir[\"chats/\u003cbr/\u003e(character conversations)\"]\n    GroupChatsDir[\"group chats/\u003cbr/\u003e(multi-character conversations)\"]\n    BackupsDir[\"backups/\u003cbr/\u003e(automatic backups)\"]\n    \n    DataRoot --\u003e UserRoot\n    UserRoot --\u003e ChatsDir\n    UserRoot --\u003e GroupChatsDir\n    UserRoot --\u003e BackupsDir\n    \n    ChatsDir --\u003e CharDir[\"character_name/\u003cbr/\u003e(one folder per character)\"]\n    CharDir --\u003e ChatFile1[\"chat_name1.jsonl\"]\n    CharDir --\u003e ChatFile2[\"chat_name2.jsonl\"]\n    \n    GroupChatsDir --\u003e GroupFile1[\"group_chat_id1.jsonl\"]\n    GroupChatsDir --\u003e GroupFile2[\"group_chat_id2.jsonl\"]\n    \n    BackupsDir --\u003e BackupFile[\"chat_sanitized_name_timestamp.jsonl\"]\n```\n\n| Directory | Purpose | Example Path |\n|-----------|---------|--------------|\n| `chats/` | Character-specific chat folders | `data/default/chats/` |\n| `chats/{character}/` | All chats with a specific character | `data/default/chats/Alice/` |\n| `chats/{character}/*.jsonl` | Individual chat files | `data/default/chats/Alice/Alice - 2024-01-15@14h30m00s.jsonl` |\n| `group chats/` | Group conversation storage | `data/default/group chats/` |\n| `group chats/*.jsonl` | Group chat files | `data/default/group chats/1234567890.jsonl` |\n| `backups/` | Automatic chat backups | `data/default/backups/` |\n\n**Sources:** [src/users.js:71-103](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:469-491](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:513-537]()\n\n## Save Operations Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client as Client (script.js)\n    participant SaveCond as saveChatConditional()\n    participant API as POST /api/chats/save\n    participant TrySave as trySaveChat()\n    participant IntCheck as checkChatIntegrity()\n    participant Backup as getBackupFunction()\n    participant FileSystem as File System\n\n    Client-\u003e\u003eSaveCond: Save chat request\n    Note over SaveCond: Debounced (user action)\u003cbr/\u003eor immediate (auto-save)\n    SaveCond-\u003e\u003eAPI: POST with chat array\n    API-\u003e\u003eAPI: Validate request body\n    API-\u003e\u003eTrySave: Call with chat data\n    \n    alt Integrity Check Enabled\n        TrySave-\u003e\u003eIntCheck: Verify integrity slug\n        IntCheck-\u003e\u003eFileSystem: Read first line of existing file\n        IntCheck-\u003e\u003eIntCheck: Compare integrity metadata\n        alt Integrity Mismatch\n            IntCheck--\u003e\u003eTrySave: Throw IntegrityMismatchError\n            TrySave--\u003e\u003eAPI: Return error\n            API--\u003e\u003eClient: 400 { error: 'integrity' }\n        end\n    end\n    \n    TrySave-\u003e\u003eTrySave: Convert to JSONL\n    TrySave-\u003e\u003eFileSystem: writeFileAtomicSync()\n    TrySave-\u003e\u003eBackup: Trigger throttled backup\n    \n    alt Backup Throttle Window Passed\n        Backup-\u003e\u003eFileSystem: Write to backups/ directory\n        Backup-\u003e\u003eBackup: removeOldBackups()\n    end\n    \n    API--\u003e\u003eClient: 200 { ok: true }\n```\n\n### Client-Side Save Functions\n\nThe client initiates saves through several pathways:\n\n- **`saveChat()`**: Direct save operation with optional parameters\n- **`saveChatConditional()`**: Debounced save triggered by user actions\n- **Auto-save**: Periodic saves during generation or idle time\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/bookmarks.js:1-50]() (client-side references), [src/endpoints/chats.js:469-491]()\n\n### Server-Side Save Processing\n\nThe server endpoint `POST /api/chats/save` handles the save request:\n\n1. **Validation**: Ensures the request body contains a valid chat array\n2. **Path Construction**: Builds the file path from user directories and character name\n3. **Integrity Check**: Optionally verifies the chat hasn't been modified externally\n4. **Atomic Write**: Uses `write-file-atomic` to prevent corruption during write\n5. **Backup Trigger**: Calls the throttled backup function for the user\n\n```javascript\n// Example save endpoint logic (simplified)\nrouter.post('/save', validateAvatarUrlMiddleware, async function (request, response) {\n    const cardName = String(request.body.avatar_url).replace('.png', '');\n    const chatData = request.body.chat;\n    const chatFileName = `${String(request.body.file_name)}.jsonl`;\n    const chatFilePath = path.join(request.user.directories.chats, cardName, sanitize(chatFileName));\n    \n    await trySaveChat(chatData, chatFilePath, request.body.force, handle, cardName, backupDirectory);\n    return response.send({ ok: true });\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:469-491](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:456-467]()\n\n## Load Operations\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Client[\"Client Request\"]\n    Endpoint[\"POST /api/chats/get\"]\n    GetData[\"getChatData()\"]\n    FileRead[\"fs.readFileSync()\"]\n    Parse[\"Parse JSONL\u003cbr/\u003eline-by-line\"]\n    Return[\"Return chat array\"]\n    \n    Client --\u003e Endpoint\n    Endpoint --\u003e GetData\n    GetData --\u003e FileRead\n    FileRead --\u003e Parse\n    Parse --\u003e Return\n    Return --\u003e Client\n    \n    style GetData fill:#f9f9f9\n    style Parse fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\n### Loading Process\n\nThe `POST /api/chats/get` endpoint retrieves a chat file:\n\n1. **Directory Check**: Ensures the character's chat directory exists\n2. **File Path Construction**: Builds the full path to the JSONL file\n3. **File Read**: Reads the entire file as a string\n4. **JSONL Parsing**: Splits by newlines and parses each line as JSON\n5. **Filtering**: Removes any malformed lines that fail to parse\n6. **Return**: Sends the array of message objects to the client\n\n```javascript\n// Example from getChatData function\nexport function getChatData(chatFilePath) {\n    let chatData = [];\n    const chatJSON = tryReadFileSync(chatFilePath) ?? '';\n    if (chatJSON.length \u003e 0) {\n        const lines = chatJSON.split('\\n');\n        chatData = lines.map(line =\u003e tryParse(line)).filter(x =\u003e x);\n    }\n    return chatData;\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:498-511](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:513-537]()\n\n## Backup System\n\nSillyTavern implements an automatic backup system with configurable throttling to prevent excessive disk writes while ensuring data safety.\n\n### Backup Configuration\n\nBackup behavior is controlled via `config.yaml`:\n\n| Configuration Key | Default | Description |\n|-------------------|---------|-------------|\n| `backups.chat.enabled` | `true` | Enable automatic chat backups |\n| `backups.chat.checkIntegrity` | `true` | Verify chat integrity before saving |\n| `backups.chat.maxTotalBackups` | `-1` | Maximum total backups per user (-1 = unlimited) |\n| `backups.chat.throttleInterval` | `10000` | Milliseconds between backup writes per user |\n| `backups.common.numberOfBackups` | `50` | Number of backups to keep per chat |\n\n**Sources:** [default/config.yaml:166-181](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:25-28]()\n\n### Backup Mechanism\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    SaveTrigger[\"Chat Save Triggered\"]\n    BackupFunc[\"getBackupFunction(handle)\"]\n    Throttle[\"Throttled Function\u003cbr/\u003e(per user)\"]\n    CheckExists[\"Check if backup\u003cbr/\u003edirectory exists\"]\n    Sanitize[\"Sanitize chat name\"]\n    Timestamp[\"Generate timestamp\"]\n    WriteBackup[\"Write backup file\"]\n    RemoveOld[\"removeOldBackups()\"]\n    \n    SaveTrigger --\u003e BackupFunc\n    BackupFunc --\u003e Throttle\n    \n    Throttle --\u003e|First call or\u003cbr/\u003einterval passed| CheckExists\n    Throttle --\u003e|Within throttle\u003cbr/\u003ewindow| Queue[\"Queue for later\"]\n    \n    CheckExists --\u003e Sanitize\n    Sanitize --\u003e Timestamp\n    Timestamp --\u003e WriteBackup\n    WriteBackup --\u003e RemoveOld\n    \n    RemoveOld --\u003e CleanupPerChat[\"Clean per-chat backups\u003cbr/\u003e(keep last N)\"]\n    RemoveOld --\u003e CleanupTotal[\"Clean total backups\u003cbr/\u003e(if limit set)\"]\n    \n    style Throttle fill:#f9f9f9\n    style RemoveOld fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\n### Per-User Throttling\n\nEach user has their own throttled backup function to prevent cross-user interference:\n\n```javascript\nconst backupFunctions = new Map(); // Map\u003cstring, DebouncedFunc\u003ctypeof backupChat\u003e\u003e\n\nfunction getBackupFunction(handle) {\n    if (!backupFunctions.has(handle)) {\n        backupFunctions.set(handle, _.throttle(backupChat, throttleInterval, { \n            leading: true, \n            trailing: true \n        }));\n    }\n    return backupFunctions.get(handle);\n}\n```\n\nThe throttling ensures:\n- **Leading edge**: First save creates a backup immediately\n- **Trailing edge**: Last save in a burst creates a backup after the interval\n- **Minimum interval**: 10 seconds (default) between backups per user\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:62-77](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:40-60]()\n\n### Backup Cleanup\n\nTwo levels of backup cleanup occur:\n\n1. **Per-Chat Cleanup**: Removes old backups for a specific chat, keeping only the most recent N backups (default: 50)\n2. **Total Cleanup**: If `maxTotalBackups` is set, removes oldest backups across all chats when the total exceeds the limit\n\n**Sources:** [src/util.js:642-660](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:52-56]()\n\n## Integrity Checking\n\nThe integrity system prevents data loss when multiple clients or processes attempt to modify the same chat file.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client1 as Client 1\n    participant Client2 as Client 2\n    participant Server as Server\n    participant File as Chat File\n\n    Note over File: Initial state\u003cbr/\u003eintegrity: \"abc123\"\n    \n    Client1-\u003e\u003eServer: Load chat\n    Server-\u003e\u003eFile: Read first line\n    File--\u003e\u003eServer: { chat_metadata: { integrity: \"abc123\" } }\n    Server--\u003e\u003eClient1: Chat array + metadata\n    \n    Client2-\u003e\u003eServer: Load chat\n    Server-\u003e\u003eFile: Read first line\n    File--\u003e\u003eServer: { chat_metadata: { integrity: \"abc123\" } }\n    Server--\u003e\u003eClient2: Chat array + metadata\n    \n    Note over Client1: User edits chat\u003cbr/\u003eintegrity still \"abc123\"\n    \n    Client1-\u003e\u003eServer: Save chat (integrity: \"abc123\")\n    Server-\u003e\u003eFile: Check integrity\n    File--\u003e\u003eServer: Matches \"abc123\"\n    Server-\u003e\u003eFile: Write new data\u003cbr/\u003eintegrity: \"def456\"\n    Server--\u003e\u003eClient1: 200 OK\n    \n    Note over Client2: User also edits\u003cbr/\u003eintegrity still \"abc123\"\n    \n    Client2-\u003e\u003eServer: Save chat (integrity: \"abc123\")\n    Server-\u003e\u003eFile: Check integrity\n    File--\u003e\u003eServer: Now \"def456\" - MISMATCH!\n    Server--\u003e\u003eClient2: 400 { error: 'integrity' }\n    \n    Note over Client2: Client must reload\u003cbr/\u003eand merge changes\n```\n\n### Integrity Slug Generation\n\nWhen a chat is loaded, the client generates a unique integrity slug that is stored in `chat_metadata.integrity`. This slug is sent back with every save request. The server compares this slug with the current file's integrity metadata before writing.\n\n### Integrity Check Logic\n\n```javascript\nasync function checkChatIntegrity(filePath, integritySlug) {\n    if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {\n        return true; // New file, always valid\n    }\n    \n    const firstLine = await readFirstLine(filePath);\n    const jsonData = tryParse(firstLine);\n    const chatIntegrity = jsonData?.chat_metadata?.integrity;\n    \n    if (!chatIntegrity) {\n        return true; // No integrity metadata, skip check\n    }\n    \n    return chatIntegrity === integritySlug;\n}\n```\n\n### Bypassing Integrity Check\n\nThe `force` parameter in the save request can bypass the integrity check:\n\n```javascript\nawait trySaveChat(chatData, chatFilePath, request.body.force, handle, cardName, backupDirectory);\n```\n\nThis is useful for:\n- Administrative operations\n- Automated backups\n- Import operations\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:315-334](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:456-467](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:435-445]()\n\n## Bookmarks and Branches\n\nSillyTavern supports creating alternate versions of chats through bookmarks (checkpoints) and branches.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    MainChat[\"Main Chat\u003cbr/\u003eAlice - 2024-01-15@14h30m00s\"]\n    \n    Msg1[\"Message 1\"]\n    Msg2[\"Message 2\"]\n    Msg3[\"Message 3\"]\n    Msg4[\"Message 4\"]\n    Msg5[\"Message 5\"]\n    \n    Bookmark1[\"Checkpoint #1\u003cbr/\u003eAlice - 2024-01-15@14h30m00s - Checkpoint #1\"]\n    Bookmark2[\"Checkpoint #2\u003cbr/\u003eAlice - 2024-01-15@14h30m00s - Checkpoint #2\"]\n    \n    Branch1[\"Branch #1\u003cbr/\u003eAlice - 2024-01-15@14h30m00s - Branch #1\"]\n    Branch2[\"Branch #2\u003cbr/\u003eAlice - 2024-01-15@14h30m00s - Branch #2\"]\n    \n    MainChat --\u003e Msg1\n    Msg1 --\u003e Msg2\n    Msg2 --\u003e Msg3\n    Msg3 --\u003e Msg4\n    Msg4 --\u003e Msg5\n    \n    Msg3 --\u003e|bookmark_link| Bookmark1\n    Msg5 --\u003e|bookmark_link| Bookmark2\n    \n    Msg2 -.-\u003e|branches[0]| Branch1\n    Msg4 -.-\u003e|branches[0]| Branch2\n    \n    Bookmark1 --\u003e B1Msg1[\"Message 1\"]\n    Bookmark1 --\u003e B1Msg2[\"Message 2\"]\n    Bookmark1 --\u003e B1Msg3[\"Message 3\"]\n    \n    Branch1 --\u003e BR1Msg1[\"Message 1\"]\n    Branch1 --\u003e BR1Msg2[\"Message 2\"]\n    Branch1 --\u003e BR1Msg3[\"New Message 3\"]\n```\n\n### Bookmarks (Checkpoints)\n\nBookmarks create a snapshot of the chat up to a specific message, allowing users to return to that point later.\n\n**Creation Process:**\n\n1. User clicks the bookmark icon on a message\n2. System prompts for a name (or auto-generates one)\n3. A new chat file is created containing messages up to that point\n4. The message's `extra.bookmark_link` field stores the bookmark name\n5. The bookmark's `chat_metadata.main_chat` field references the original chat\n\n**Bookmark Metadata:**\n\n```json\n{\n  \"chat_metadata\": {\n    \"main_chat\": \"Alice - 2024-01-15@14h30m00s\",\n    \"...\": \"other metadata\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Message Linking:**\n\n```json\n{\n  \"mes\": \"This is the checkpointed message\",\n  \"extra\": {\n    \"bookmark_link\": \"Alice - 2024-01-15@14h30m00s - Checkpoint #1\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/bookmarks.js:222-267](), [public/scripts/bookmarks.js:84-108]()\n\n### Branches\n\nBranches create diverging conversation paths from a specific message, useful for exploring different scenarios.\n\n**Creation Process:**\n\n1. User selects \"Create Branch\" on a message\n2. System creates a new chat with messages up to that point\n3. The message's `extra.branches` array stores all branch names\n4. Users can continue the conversation differently in each branch\n\n**Branch Tracking:**\n\n```json\n{\n  \"mes\": \"Message where branch occurs\",\n  \"extra\": {\n    \"branches\": [\n      \"Alice - 2024-01-15@14h30m00s - Branch #1\",\n      \"Alice - 2024-01-15@14h30m00s - Branch #2\"\n    ]\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/bookmarks.js:167-212](), [public/scripts/bookmarks.js:416-432]()\n\n### Chat Name Generation\n\nBoth bookmarks and branches use `getUniqueName()` to ensure no filename collisions:\n\n```javascript\nfunction buildCheckpointName(name, i) {\n    let cleanName = name.replace(new RegExp(` - ${bookmarkNameToken}\\\\d+$`), '');\n    cleanName = cleanName.replace(new RegExp(`^${bookmarkNameToken}\\\\d+ - `), '');\n    return `${cleanName} - ${bookmarkNameToken}${i}`;\n}\n\nconst suggestedName = getUniqueName(mainChatName, (x) =\u003e existingChats.includes(x), { \n    nameBuilder: buildCheckpointName \n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/bookmarks.js:84-108]()\n\n## Import and Export\n\nSillyTavern supports importing chats from multiple formats and exporting to JSONL or plain text.\n\n### Supported Import Formats\n\n| Format | Detection Method | Import Function |\n|--------|------------------|-----------------|\n| **Ooba** | JSON with `data_visible` array | `importOobaChat()` |\n| **Agnai** | JSON with `messages` array | `importAgnaiChat()` |\n| **CAI Tools** | JSON with `histories` object | `importCAIChat()` |\n| **Kobold Lite** | JSON with `savedsettings` and `actions` | `importKoboldLiteChat()` |\n| **Chub** | Special flattening of nested messages | `flattenChubChat()` |\n| **RisuAI** | JSON with `data.message` array | `importRisuChat()` |\n\n### Import Process\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Upload[\"Upload File\"]\n    Detect[\"Detect Format\"]\n    Convert[\"Convert to ST Format\"]\n    Validate[\"Validate Structure\"]\n    Save[\"Save as JSONL\"]\n    \n    Upload --\u003e Detect\n    Detect --\u003e|Ooba| ConvertOoba[\"importOobaChat()\"]\n    Detect --\u003e|Agnai| ConvertAgnai[\"importAgnaiChat()\"]\n    Detect --\u003e|CAI| ConvertCAI[\"importCAIChat()\"]\n    Detect --\u003e|Kobold| ConvertKobold[\"importKoboldLiteChat()\"]\n    Detect --\u003e|Chub| ConvertChub[\"flattenChubChat()\"]\n    Detect --\u003e|Risu| ConvertRisu[\"importRisuChat()\"]\n    \n    ConvertOoba --\u003e Convert\n    ConvertAgnai --\u003e Convert\n    ConvertCAI --\u003e Convert\n    ConvertKobold --\u003e Convert\n    ConvertChub --\u003e Convert\n    ConvertRisu --\u003e Convert\n    \n    Convert --\u003e Validate\n    Validate --\u003e Save\n```\n\n### Import Normalization\n\nAll import functions normalize to the standard SillyTavern message format:\n\n```javascript\n{\n    name: characterName,\n    is_user: false,\n    send_date: new Date().toISOString(),\n    mes: messageText,\n    extra: {}\n}\n```\n\nThe first line always includes the header:\n\n```javascript\n{\n    chat_metadata: {},\n    user_name: 'unused',\n    character_name: 'unused'\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:109-307]()\n\n### Export Formats\n\nThe `POST /api/chats/export` endpoint supports two export formats:\n\n**1. JSONL (Native Format)**\n\nReturns the raw JSONL file content, preserving all metadata:\n\n```javascript\nif (request.body.format === 'jsonl') {\n    const rawFile = fs.readFileSync(filename, 'utf8');\n    return response.status(200).json({\n        message: `Chat saved to ${exportfilename}`,\n        result: rawFile\n    });\n}\n```\n\n**2. Plain Text**\n\nConverts to human-readable format:\n\n```\nCharacter Name: Message text here\n\nUser Name: User response here\n\nCharacter Name: Another message\n```\n\nSystem messages (`is_system: true`) are excluded from text exports.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:591-658]()\n\n## Chat Information and Metadata\n\nSillyTavern provides functions to retrieve chat metadata without loading the entire chat file.\n\n### Chat Info Retrieval\n\nThe `getChatInfo()` function reads a chat file and extracts key information:\n\n```javascript\nexport async function getChatInfo(pathToFile, additionalData = {}, withMetadata = false, matcher = null) {\n    const chatData = {\n        match: false,\n        file_id: parsedPath.name,\n        file_name: parsedPath.base,\n        file_size: formatBytes(stats.size),\n        chat_items: 0,\n        mes: '[The chat is empty]',\n        last_mes: stats.mtimeMs,\n        ...additionalData\n    };\n    \n    // Stream file line-by-line\n    const rl = readline.createInterface({ input: fileStream });\n    \n    rl.on('line', (line) =\u003e {\n        if (withMetadata \u0026\u0026 itemCounter === 0) {\n            chatData.chat_metadata = jsonData.chat_metadata;\n        }\n        itemCounter++;\n        lastLine = line;\n    });\n    \n    return chatData;\n}\n```\n\nThis function:\n- Returns basic statistics without loading the full chat\n- Optionally extracts metadata from the first line\n- Optionally matches messages against a search function\n- Uses streaming to handle large files efficiently\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:348-430](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:338-359]()\n\n## Group Chat Specifics\n\nGroup chats have a different storage structure and metadata format.\n\n### Group Chat Storage\n\n- **Location**: `{user_directories.groupChats}/{chat_id}.jsonl`\n- **Metadata**: Stored in chat file header (migrated from group file)\n- **Message Format**: Includes `original_avatar` and `force_avatar` fields\n\n### Group Chat Metadata Migration\n\nSillyTavern migrated from storing metadata in the group JSON file to storing it per-chat:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    OldFormat[\"Old Format\u003cbr/\u003egroup.json\u003cbr/\u003e{ chat_metadata: {...} }\"]\n    Migration[\"migrateGroupChatsMetadataFormat()\"]\n    NewFormat[\"New Format\u003cbr/\u003echat.jsonl\u003cbr/\u003eLine 1: { chat_metadata: {...} }\"]\n    \n    OldFormat --\u003e Migration\n    Migration --\u003e Backup[\"Create backup in\u003cbr/\u003ebackups/_group_metadata_update/\"]\n    Migration --\u003e Extract[\"Extract metadata\u003cbr/\u003efrom group.json\"]\n    Migration --\u003e Update[\"Update each chat file\u003cbr/\u003ewith its metadata\"]\n    Migration --\u003e Clean[\"Remove metadata\u003cbr/\u003efrom group.json\"]\n    Update --\u003e NewFormat\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/groups.js:34-111]()\n\n## Rename and Delete Operations\n\n### Chat Rename\n\nThe `POST /api/chats/rename` endpoint:\n\n1. Validates the original and new file names\n2. Copies the original file to the new name\n3. Deletes the original file\n4. Returns the sanitized new filename\n\n```javascript\nfs.copyFileSync(pathToOriginalFile, pathToRenamedFile);\nfs.unlinkSync(pathToOriginalFile);\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:539-567]()\n\n### Chat Delete\n\nThe `POST /api/chats/delete` endpoint:\n\n1. Constructs the full path to the chat file\n2. Attempts to delete the file using `tryDeleteFile()`\n3. Returns success or error status\n\n```javascript\nconst chatFilePath = path.join(request.user.directories.chats, dirName, sanitize(chatFileName));\nif (tryDeleteFile(chatFilePath)) {\n    return response.send({ ok: true });\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:569-589]()\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n### Streaming for Large Files\n\nThe `getChatInfo()` function uses Node.js readline interface to stream chat files line-by-line, preventing memory exhaustion with large chats:\n\n```javascript\nconst fileStream = fs.createReadStream(pathToFile);\nconst rl = readline.createInterface({\n    input: fileStream,\n    crlfDelay: Infinity\n});\n\nrl.on('line', (line) =\u003e {\n    // Process one line at a time\n});\n```\n\n### Atomic Writes\n\nAll write operations use `write-file-atomic` to ensure:\n- Writes complete fully or not at all\n- No partially-written files during crashes\n- Safe concurrent access on most file systems\n\n```javascript\nimport { sync as writeFileAtomicSync } from 'write-file-atomic';\n\nwriteFileAtomicSync(filePath, jsonlData);\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:358-430](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:465]()\n\n## Error Handling\n\n### Integrity Mismatch Error\n\nWhen integrity checking fails, a custom error is thrown:\n\n```javascript\nclass IntegrityMismatchError extends Error {\n    constructor(...params) {\n        super(...params);\n        if (Error.captureStackTrace) {\n            Error.captureStackTrace(this, IntegrityMismatchError);\n        }\n        this.date = new Date();\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThe save endpoint catches this error and returns a specific status:\n\n```javascript\ncatch (error) {\n    if (error instanceof IntegrityMismatchError) {\n        return response.status(400).send({ error: 'integrity' });\n    }\n    // ... other error handling\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:435-445](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:483-490]()\n\n## Related Endpoints Reference\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |\n|----------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/chats/save` | POST | Save chat data with integrity check |\n| `/api/chats/get` | POST | Load chat data for a character |\n| `/api/chats/rename` | POST | Rename an existing chat file |\n| `/api/chats/delete` | POST | Delete a chat file |\n| `/api/chats/export` | POST | Export chat to JSONL or text format |\n| `/api/chats/group/import` | POST | Import a group chat file |\n| `/api/chats/group/save` | POST | Save group chat data |\n| `/api/chats/group/get` | POST | Load group chat data |\n| `/api/chats/group/delete` | POST | Delete a group chat |\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:432-660]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"33:T4c9a,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Settings and Configuration Management\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [default/config.yaml](default/config.yaml)\n- [default/public/error/unauthorized.html](default/public/error/unauthorized.html)\n- [post-install.js](post-install.js)\n- [public/index.html](public/index.html)\n- [public/script.js](public/script.js)\n- [public/scripts/bookmarks.js](public/scripts/bookmarks.js)\n- [public/scripts/group-chats.js](public/scripts/group-chats.js)\n- [public/scripts/instruct-mode.js](public/scripts/instruct-mode.js)\n- [public/scripts/power-user.js](public/scripts/power-user.js)\n- [public/scripts/utils.js](public/scripts/utils.js)\n- [public/scripts/world-info.js](public/scripts/world-info.js)\n- [public/style.css](public/style.css)\n- [server.js](server.js)\n- [src/endpoints/characters.js](src/endpoints/characters.js)\n- [src/endpoints/chats.js](src/endpoints/chats.js)\n- [src/endpoints/content-manager.js](src/endpoints/content-manager.js)\n- [src/endpoints/groups.js](src/endpoints/groups.js)\n- [src/endpoints/presets.js](src/endpoints/presets.js)\n- [src/endpoints/settings.js](src/endpoints/settings.js)\n- [src/middleware/basicAuth.js](src/middleware/basicAuth.js)\n- [src/middleware/whitelist.js](src/middleware/whitelist.js)\n- [src/users.js](src/users.js)\n- [src/util.js](src/util.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page documents the configuration and settings management systems in SillyTavern, covering both server-level configuration and user-specific settings. This includes the loading mechanisms, persistence strategies, settings hierarchy, and preset management.\n\nFor information about character-specific data storage, see [Character Management](#5.1). For chat-specific metadata, see [Chat Storage and Persistence](#5.2).\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nSillyTavern employs a two-tier configuration system:\n\n1. **Server Configuration** (`config.yaml`): Read-only runtime configuration that controls server behavior, security settings, and system-wide features.\n2. **User Settings** (`settings.json`): Per-user mutable settings that store UI preferences, API configurations, extension settings, and application state.\n\nAdditionally, the system supports **presets** for various AI backends and prompt configurations, along with a **settings hierarchy** that allows chat-level and character-level overrides of global settings.\n\n---\n\n## Server Configuration System\n\n### Configuration File Structure\n\nThe server configuration is stored in `config.yaml` at the application root. The configuration is parsed once at startup and cached in memory.\n\n```yaml\n# Key configuration sections\ndataRoot: ./data\nport: 8000\nlisten: false\nwhitelistMode: true\nenableUserAccounts: false\nbasicAuthMode: false\nssl:\n  enabled: false\nbackups:\n  chat:\n    enabled: true\n    numberOfBackups: 50\nthumbnails:\n  enabled: true\n  format: \"jpg\"\nperformance:\n  lazyLoadCharacters: false\n  memoryCacheCapacity: '100mb'\n  useDiskCache: true\n```\n\n**Sources:** [default/config.yaml:1-227]()\n\n### Configuration Loading Mechanism\n\n#### Server Config Loading Flow\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Start[\"Application Start\"] --\u003e SetPath[\"setConfigFilePath()\"]\n    SetPath --\u003e CheckCache{\"CACHED_CONFIG exists?\"}\n    CheckCache --\u003e|Yes| Return[\"Return cached config\"]\n    CheckCache --\u003e|No| CheckFile{\"config.yaml exists?\"}\n    CheckFile --\u003e|No| Error[\"Exit with error\"]\n    CheckFile --\u003e|Yes| Parse[\"yaml.parse()\"]\n    Parse --\u003e Cache[\"Store in CACHED_CONFIG\"]\n    Cache --\u003e Return\n    \n    GetValue[\"getConfigValue(key)\"] --\u003e CheckEnv{\"Environment variable?\"}\n    CheckEnv --\u003e|Yes| EnvValue[\"Return env value\"]\n    CheckEnv --\u003e|No| LodashGet[\"_.get(config, key)\"]\n    LodashGet --\u003e TypeConvert[\"Apply type converter\"]\n    TypeConvert --\u003e ReturnValue[\"Return value\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/util.js:29-109]()\n\nThe configuration system uses these key functions:\n\n- **`setConfigFilePath(configFilePath)`**: Sets the path to the config file, called once during initialization [src/util.js:44-49]()\n- **`getConfig()`**: Returns the parsed and cached configuration object [src/util.js:55-79]()\n- **`getConfigValue(key, defaultValue, typeConverter)`**: Retrieves a specific configuration value with environment variable override support [src/util.js:88-109]()\n\n### Environment Variable Overrides\n\nConfiguration values can be overridden using environment variables. The naming convention is:\n\n```\nSILLYTAVERN_\u003cSECTION\u003e_\u003cSUBSECTION\u003e_\u003cKEY\u003e\n```\n\nFor example:\n- `config.yaml`: `extensions.models.speechToText`\n- Environment: `SILLYTAVERN_EXTENSIONS_MODELS_SPEECHTOTEXT`\n\nThe `keyToEnv()` function performs this transformation [src/util.js:38]().\n\n**Sources:** [src/util.js:26-38](), [src/util.js:88-109]()\n\n---\n\n## User Settings System\n\n### Settings File Structure\n\nUser settings are stored in `settings.json` within each user's data directory. The file contains:\n\n```javascript\n{\n  \"power_user\": {\n    \"tokenizer\": 1,\n    \"token_padding\": 64,\n    \"font_scale\": 1,\n    \"instruct\": { /* instruct mode config */ },\n    \"context\": { /* context template config */ },\n    \"personas\": { /* persona definitions */ },\n    // ... many more settings\n  },\n  \"extension_settings\": {\n    \"expressions\": { /* expression settings */ },\n    \"memory\": { /* memory extension settings */ },\n    \"vectors\": { /* RAG settings */ },\n    // ... per-extension namespaces\n  },\n  \"amount_gen\": 80,\n  \"max_context\": 2048,\n  \"main_api\": \"kobold\",\n  // ... global settings\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/power-user.js:123-350](), [public/script.js:586-589]()\n\n### Settings Categories\n\n| Category | Purpose | Storage Location |\n|----------|---------|------------------|\n| Global Settings | API selection, generation parameters | Root level in `settings.json` |\n| Power User Settings | UI preferences, advanced features | `power_user` object |\n| Extension Settings | Per-extension configuration | `extension_settings.\u003cextension_name\u003e` |\n| Instruct Mode | Prompt formatting templates | `power_user.instruct` |\n| Context Templates | Story string and chat formatting | `power_user.context` |\n| Personas | User identity configurations | `power_user.personas` |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/power-user.js:123-350](), [public/script.js:586-589]()\n\n### Settings Persistence Architecture\n\n#### Settings Save Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant UI as \"UI Controls\"\n    participant Script as \"script.js\"\n    participant Debounce as \"saveSettingsDebounced\"\n    participant API as \"/api/settings/save\"\n    participant Server as \"settings.js endpoint\"\n    participant FS as \"File System\"\n    participant Backup as \"Backup System\"\n    \n    UI-\u003e\u003eScript: User modifies setting\n    Script-\u003e\u003eScript: Update in-memory settings object\n    Script-\u003e\u003eDebounce: Trigger save (1000ms debounce)\n    \n    Note over Debounce: Wait for debounce period\u003cbr/\u003eCoalesce multiple changes\n    \n    Debounce-\u003e\u003eAPI: POST settings object\n    API-\u003e\u003eServer: Request with settings JSON\n    Server-\u003e\u003eServer: triggerAutoSave(handle)\n    Server-\u003e\u003eFS: writeFileAtomicSync(settings.json)\n    Server-\u003e\u003eBackup: backupUserSettings() (throttled 10min)\n    Backup-\u003e\u003eFS: Write backup with timestamp\n    Server--\u003e\u003eAPI: Success response\n    API--\u003e\u003eScript: Settings saved confirmation\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:441](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:1-182]()\n\nThe settings persistence system uses several mechanisms to ensure data integrity:\n\n1. **Debounced Saving**: Settings saves are debounced to prevent excessive disk writes [public/script.js:441]()\n2. **Atomic Writes**: Uses `write-file-atomic` to prevent corruption [src/endpoints/settings.js:94-101]()\n3. **Automatic Backups**: Settings are backed up every 10 minutes (throttled) [src/endpoints/settings.js:18-40]()\n4. **Backup Rotation**: Old backups are pruned based on configured limits [src/util.js:430-457]()\n\n### Settings Loading\n\nSettings are loaded during application initialization:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Init[\"firstLoadInit()\"] --\u003e GetSettings[\"getSettings()\"]\n    GetSettings --\u003e Fetch[\"GET /api/settings/get\"]\n    Fetch --\u003e Server[\"settings.js endpoint\"]\n    Server --\u003e ReadFile[\"Read settings.json\"]\n    ReadFile --\u003e Parse[\"Parse JSON\"]\n    Parse --\u003e Apply[\"Apply to client state\"]\n    Apply --\u003e LoadExtensions[\"loadExtensionSettings()\"]\n    LoadExtensions --\u003e LoadPowerUser[\"loadPowerUserSettings()\"]\n    LoadPowerUser --\u003e LoadPresets[\"Load presets\"]\n    LoadPresets --\u003e Ready[\"settingsReady = true\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:666-738](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:120-137]()\n\nThe `getSettings()` function loads all user settings and broadcasts them to the appropriate subsystems [public/script.js:3806-3906]().\n\n---\n\n## Settings Hierarchy\n\nSillyTavern implements a three-tier settings hierarchy that allows fine-grained control over behavior:\n\n### Hierarchy Levels\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Global[\"Global Settings\u003cbr/\u003e(settings.json)\"] --\u003e Chat[\"Chat-Level Settings\u003cbr/\u003e(chat_metadata)\"]\n    Chat --\u003e Character[\"Character-Level Settings\u003cbr/\u003e(character data)\"]\n    \n    Global -.-\u003e|\"Default values\"| Result[\"Effective Settings\"]\n    Chat -.-\u003e|\"Override global\"| Result\n    Character -.-\u003e|\"Override chat \u0026 global\"| Result\n    \n    style Result fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:424-426](), [public/scripts/power-user.js:361-376]()\n\n### Setting Override Examples\n\n| Setting | Global | Chat Override | Character Override |\n|---------|--------|---------------|-------------------|\n| `instruct.enabled` | `power_user.instruct.enabled` | `chat_metadata.instruct.enabled` | `character.data.extensions.instruct.enabled` |\n| `temperature` | `oai_settings.temp_openai` | `chat_metadata.api_settings.temp_openai` | Character-specific preset binding |\n| `story_string` | `power_user.context.story_string` | `chat_metadata.context.story_string` | N/A |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/power-user.js:361-376](), [public/scripts/instruct-mode.js:23-48]()\n\n### Character-Specific Setting Overrides\n\nCharacter cards can specify overrides for global settings through the `extensions` field:\n\n```javascript\ncharacter.data = {\n  name: \"Character Name\",\n  description: \"...\",\n  // ...\n  extensions: {\n    depth_prompt: {\n      prompt: \"...\",\n      depth: 4,\n      role: \"system\"\n    },\n    instruct: {\n      enabled: true,\n      preset: \"Custom\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:542-565]()\n\nThe function `setCharacterSettingsOverrides()` applies character-specific overrides when a character is selected [public/script.js:4085-4111]().\n\n---\n\n## Preset Management System\n\nPresets are reusable configuration templates for different AI backends and prompt formatting systems.\n\n### Preset Types\n\n| Type | Directory Path | Extension | Purpose |\n|------|----------------|-----------|---------|\n| KoboldAI | `koboldAI_Settings` | `.json` | KoboldAI API parameters |\n| NovelAI | `novelAI_Settings` | `.json` | NovelAI API parameters |\n| OpenAI | `openAI_Settings` | `.json` | Chat completion parameters |\n| TextGen | `textGen_Settings` | `.json` | Text completion parameters |\n| Instruct | `instruct` | `.json` | Instruct mode templates |\n| Context | `context` | `.json` | Context/story string templates |\n| System Prompt | `sysprompt` | `.json` | System prompt templates |\n| Reasoning | `reasoning` | `.json` | Reasoning display templates |\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/presets.js:16-37](), [src/constants.js:60-78]()\n\n### Preset Storage Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"User Data Directory\"\n        UserRoot[\"data/\u003chandle\u003e/\"] --\u003e Kobold[\"koboldAI_Settings/\"]\n        UserRoot --\u003e Novel[\"novelAI_Settings/\"]\n        UserRoot --\u003e OpenAI[\"openAI_Settings/\"]\n        UserRoot --\u003e TextGen[\"textGen_Settings/\"]\n        UserRoot --\u003e Instruct[\"instruct/\"]\n        UserRoot --\u003e Context[\"context/\"]\n        UserRoot --\u003e SysPrompt[\"sysprompt/\"]\n        UserRoot --\u003e Reasoning[\"reasoning/\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Default Presets\"\n        ContentDir[\"default/content/\"] --\u003e DefaultKobold[\"*.settings\"]\n        ContentDir --\u003e DefaultOpenAI[\"*.json\"]\n        ContentDir --\u003e DefaultInstruct[\"*.json\"]\n    end\n    \n    DefaultKobold -.-\u003e|\"Copy on first use\"| Kobold\n    DefaultOpenAI -.-\u003e|\"Copy on first use\"| OpenAI\n    DefaultInstruct -.-\u003e|\"Copy on first use\"| Instruct\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:63-81](), [src/constants.js:15-96]()\n\n### Preset Management Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant UI as \"Preset Selector\"\n    participant PM as \"PresetManager\"\n    participant API as \"Preset API\"\n    participant FS as \"File System\"\n    participant Content as \"Content Manager\"\n    \n    UI-\u003e\u003ePM: Select preset\n    PM-\u003e\u003eAPI: GET /api/presets/get\n    API-\u003e\u003eFS: Read preset file\n    FS--\u003e\u003eAPI: Preset JSON\n    API--\u003e\u003ePM: Preset data\n    PM-\u003e\u003eUI: Apply preset settings\n    \n    Note over UI,PM: User modifies settings\n    \n    UI-\u003e\u003ePM: Update preset\n    PM-\u003e\u003eAPI: POST /api/presets/save\n    API-\u003e\u003eFS: writeFileAtomicSync(preset.json)\n    FS--\u003e\u003eAPI: Success\n    API--\u003e\u003ePM: Saved\n    \n    Note over UI,Content: First-time user\n    \n    Content-\u003e\u003eContent: getDefaultPresets()\n    Content-\u003e\u003eFS: Copy from default/content/\n    FS--\u003e\u003eContent: Preset installed\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/presets.js:1-90](), [public/scripts/preset-manager.js]()\n\n### Preset Binding\n\nPresets can be bound to specific API connections when `bind_preset_to_connection` is enabled. This creates a mapping between chat completion sources and preferred presets.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:106-113]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension Settings Management\n\nExtension settings are stored within the main `settings.json` file under the `extension_settings` namespace. Each extension has its own sub-object.\n\n### Extension Settings Structure\n\n```javascript\nextension_settings = {\n  \"expressions\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"api\": \"extras\",\n    \"localUrl\": \"http://localhost:5100\"\n  },\n  \"memory\": {\n    \"enabled\": true,\n    \"summary_prompt\": \"...\",\n    \"position\": 0\n  },\n  \"vectors\": {\n    \"enabled\": false,\n    \"source\": \"openai\",\n    \"chunk_size\": 512\n  }\n  // ... other extensions\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions.js:190]()\n\n### Extension Settings Persistence\n\nExtension settings are saved through the main settings save mechanism. Extensions can trigger saves using:\n\n```javascript\nsaveSettingsDebounced(); // Debounced save\n```\n\nExtensions can also listen for settings changes via the event system:\n\n```javascript\neventSource.on(event_types.SETTINGS_LOADED, () =\u003e {\n  // Apply extension settings\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions.js:190](), [public/script.js:441]()\n\n---\n\n## Advanced Settings Features\n\n### Context Template System\n\nContext templates define how the story string and chat format are constructed. These are managed through the `power_user.context` object.\n\n**Key Settings:**\n- `story_string`: Template for assembling character/scenario info\n- `chat_start`: Separator at chat beginning\n- `example_separator`: Separator between example dialogues\n- `story_string_position`: Where to inject the story string\n- `use_stop_strings`: Whether to use stop strings from context\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/power-user.js:254-264](), [public/scripts/power-user.js:361-376]()\n\n### Instruct Mode Configuration\n\nInstruct mode settings define prompt wrapping sequences for instruction-following models:\n\n```javascript\npower_user.instruct = {\n  enabled: false,\n  preset: \"Alpaca\",\n  input_sequence: \"### Instruction:\",\n  output_sequence: \"### Response:\",\n  system_sequence: \"\",\n  wrap: true,\n  macro: true,\n  names_behavior: \"force\"\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/instruct-mode.js:23-48](), [public/scripts/power-user.js:225-252]()\n\n### Model Template Bindings\n\nThe system supports automatic derivation of instruct/context templates based on the loaded model's chat template. Mappings are stored in `power_user.model_templates_mappings`:\n\n```javascript\nmodel_templates_mappings = {\n  \"llama-3\": {\n    instruct: \"Llama 3\",\n    context: \"Llama 3\"\n  },\n  \"mistral\": {\n    instruct: \"Mistral\",\n    context: \"Default\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/power-user.js:269-272]()\n\n---\n\n## Settings Import and Export\n\n### Settings Export\n\nUsers can export their entire configuration through the UI. The export includes:\n- Main settings\n- Power user settings\n- Extension settings\n- Presets\n- Personas\n- Themes\n\nThe export is packaged as a JSON file that can be shared or backed up.\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:4719-4744]()\n\n### Settings Import\n\nSettings can be imported from a previously exported JSON file. The import process:\n1. Validates the imported data structure\n2. Merges with existing settings (configurable)\n3. Triggers a reload of affected subsystems\n4. Saves the merged settings\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:4746-4789]()\n\n### Tag Import Settings\n\nWhen importing characters or other entities with tags, the system supports multiple import modes via `power_user.tag_import_setting`:\n\n- `ASK`: Prompt user for each new tag\n- `IMPORT`: Automatically import all tags\n- `IGNORE`: Don't import tags\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/tags.js:208]()\n\n---\n\n## Settings Validation and Migration\n\n### Settings Migration\n\nThe system includes migration logic to handle settings format changes across versions:\n\n```javascript\n// Example: Instruct mode migration\nfunction migrateInstructModeSettings(settings) {\n  // separator_sequence =\u003e output_suffix\n  if (settings.separator_sequence !== undefined) {\n    settings.output_suffix = settings.separator_sequence || '';\n    delete settings.separator_sequence;\n  }\n  \n  // Apply defaults for new fields\n  const defaults = {\n    system_same_as_user: false,\n    sequences_as_stop_strings: true\n  };\n  \n  for (let key in defaults) {\n    if (settings[key] === undefined) {\n      settings[key] = defaults[key];\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/instruct-mode.js:55-105]()\n\n### Settings Integrity\n\nThe settings system includes several integrity mechanisms:\n\n1. **Atomic writes**: Prevents corruption during save operations [src/endpoints/settings.js:94-101]()\n2. **JSON validation**: Malformed JSON is rejected\n3. **Backup system**: Regular backups allow recovery from corruption [src/endpoints/settings.js:42-57]()\n4. **Type coercion**: Settings are coerced to expected types on load [src/util.js:100-108]()\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/settings.js:1-182](), [src/util.js:88-109]()\n\n---\n\n## Settings API Endpoints\n\n### Server Endpoints\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |\n|----------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/settings/get` | GET | Retrieve user settings |\n| `/api/settings/save` | POST | Save modified settings |\n| `/api/settings/backups` | GET | List available backups |\n| `/api/settings/restore` | POST | Restore from backup |\n| `/api/presets/get` | POST | Get preset by API and name |\n| `/api/presets/save` | POST | Save/update preset |\n| `/api/presets/delete` | POST | Delete preset |\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/settings.js:138-182](), [src/endpoints/presets.js:39-90]()\n\n### Client-Side Functions\n\n| Function | Purpose |\n|----------|---------|\n| `saveSettings()` | Synchronously save settings to server |\n| `saveSettingsDebounced()` | Debounced version of saveSettings |\n| `getSettings()` | Load settings from server |\n| `loadPowerUserSettings()` | Apply power user settings to UI
