[src/util.js](src/util.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis page documents SillyTavern's asset and file management systems, including:\n- **Sprite storage**: Character expression images and visual novel mode sprites\n- **Background management**: Chat background images\n- **Asset system**: Audio files (BGM, ambient, blip sounds) and 3D models (Live2D, VRM)\n- **File attachments**: User-uploaded files through the Data Bank interface\n- **Character asset integration**: Importing and exporting sprites with character cards\n\nFor character card data formats and character-specific metadata, see [Character Management](#5.1). For general data persistence patterns, see [Data Management](#5).\n\n---\n\n## Directory Structure\n\nEach user has an isolated directory tree for storing assets. The asset-related directories are defined in the `UserDirectoryList` type.\n\n**User Asset Directory Layout:**\n\n```\ndata/\n└── {user_handle}/\n    ├── characters/          # Character cards (PNG files)\n    │   └── {character_name}/  # Sprite folders (one per character)\n    │       ├── joy.png\n    │       ├── anger.png\n    │       └── subfolder/     # Optional sprite subfolders\n    ├── backgrounds/         # Chat background images\n    ├── assets/              # Categorized asset files\n    │   ├── bgm/             # Background music\n    │   ├── ambient/         # Ambient sounds\n    │   ├── blip/            # UI sound effects\n    │   ├── live2d/          # Live2D models\n    │   ├── vrm/             # VRM 3D models\n    │   ├── character/       # Character-specific assets\n    │   └── temp/            # Temporary uploads\n    └── files/               # User-uploaded file attachments\n```\n\nSources: [src/users.js:71-103](), [src/endpoints/assets.js:14]()\n\n---\n\n## Asset Type System\n\nSillyTavern categorizes assets into distinct types with dedicated storage locations and API endpoints.\n\n### Asset Categories\n\n| Category | Purpose | Storage Path | File Types |\n|----------|---------|--------------|------------|\n| `bgm` | Background music | `assets/bgm/` | Audio files |\n| `ambient` | Ambient sounds | `assets/ambient/` | Audio files |\n| `blip` | UI sound effects | `assets/blip/` | Audio files |\n| `live2d` | Live2D models | `assets/live2d/` | JSON models + textures |\n| `vrm` | VRM 3D avatars | `assets/vrm/model/`, `assets/vrm/animation/` | VRM files, animations |\n| `character` | Character-specific assets | `assets/character/` | Various |\n| `temp` | Temporary uploads | `assets/temp/` | Various |\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/assets.js:14](), [src/endpoints/assets.js:84-96]()\n\n---\n\n## Sprite Management System\n\nSprites are character expression images stored in per-character folders within the `characters/` directory. The sprite system powers both the single-expression display mode and Visual Novel mode with multiple characters.\n\n### Sprite Storage Structure\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    CharDir[\"characters/ directory\"]\n    CharCard[\"character.png\u003cbr/\u003e(Character card file)\"]\n    SpriteDir[\"character/\u003cbr/\u003e(Sprite folder)\"]\n    \n    CharDir --\u003e CharCard\n    CharDir --\u003e SpriteDir\n    \n    SpriteDir --\u003e Default[\"joy.png\u003cbr/\u003eanger.png\u003cbr/\u003eneutral.png\u003cbr/\u003e(Default sprites)\"]\n    SpriteDir --\u003e Multi[\"joy-1.png\u003cbr/\u003ejoy-2.png\u003cbr/\u003e(Multiple sprites)\"]\n    SpriteDir --\u003e Subfolder[\"subfolder/\u003cbr/\u003e(Override folder)\"]\n    \n    Subfolder --\u003e SubSprites[\"joy.png\u003cbr/\u003eanger.png\"]\n    \n    style CharDir fill:#f9f9f9\n    style SpriteDir fill:#e8f5e9\n    style Subfolder fill:#e8f5e9\n```\n\n**Sprite Naming Convention:**\n- Basic: `{expression}.png` (e.g., `joy.png`, `anger.png`)\n- Multiple sprites per expression: `{expression}-{index}.png` or `{expression}.{suffix}.png`\n- Subfolder override: `{character_name}/{subfolder}/{expression}.png`\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/sprites.js:18-38](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:812-820]()\n\n### Sprite API Endpoints\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant Server as \"Server\u003cbr/\u003e/api/sprites\"\n    participant FS as \"File System\u003cbr/\u003echaracters/{name}/\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eServer: POST /get\u003cbr/\u003e{name: \"Alice\"}\n    Server-\u003e\u003eFS: Read directory\n    FS--\u003e\u003eServer: List of sprite files\n    Server-\u003e\u003eServer: Group by expression\n    Server--\u003e\u003eClient: ExpressionImage[]\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eServer: POST /upload\u003cbr/\u003e{name, label, file}\n    Server-\u003e\u003eServer: Validate filename\n    Server-\u003e\u003eFS: Write sprite file\n    Server-\u003e\u003eFS: invalidate cache\n    Server--\u003e\u003eClient: Success\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eServer: POST /delete\u003cbr/\u003e{name, expression}\n    Server-\u003e\u003eFS: Delete file\n    Server--\u003e\u003eClient: Success\n```\n\n**Key API Routes:**\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |\n|----------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/sprites/get` | POST | List all sprites for a character, grouped by expression label |\n| `/api/sprites/upload` | POST | Upload a new sprite image |\n| `/api/sprites/delete` | POST | Delete a sprite image |\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/sprites.js:136-257]()\n\n### Expression Override System\n\nUsers can override the sprite folder for a character using the `expressionOverrides` setting. This allows multiple sprite sets for the same character.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Char[\"Character:\u003cbr/\u003eAlice.png\"]\n    \n    Default[\"Default sprites:\u003cbr/\u003eAlice/joy.png\"]\n    Override[\"Override sprites:\u003cbr/\u003eAlice/formal/joy.png\"]\n    \n    Setting[\"expressionOverride:\u003cbr/\u003epath: 'Alice/formal'\"]\n    \n    Char --\u003e Default\n    Char --\u003e Override\n    Setting -.-\u003e|\"Redirects to\"| Override\n    \n    style Setting fill:#fff3cd\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:614-626](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:677-698]()\n\n---\n\n## Character Asset Integration\n\nSprites can be embedded in character card files during import/export, enabling portable character packages with their visual assets.\n\n### CharX Format Integration\n\nThe CharX format (ZIP-based) supports embedding auxiliary assets including sprites, backgrounds, and miscellaneous files.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    CharX[\"CharX Archive\u003cbr/\u003e(ZIP file)\"]\n    \n    CharX --\u003e Card[\"card.json\u003cbr/\u003e(Character data)\"]\n    CharX --\u003e Avatar[\"avatar.png\u003cbr/\u003e(Character image)\"]\n    CharX --\u003e Assets[\"assets/\u003cbr/\u003e(Auxiliary files)\"]\n    \n    Assets --\u003e Sprites[\"sprites/\u003cbr/\u003e(Expression images)\"]\n    Assets --\u003e Backgrounds[\"backgrounds/\u003cbr/\u003e(Background images)\"]\n    Assets --\u003e Misc[\"misc/\u003cbr/\u003e(Other files)\"]\n    \n    Import[\"Import Process\"]\n    Import --\u003e ParseZIP[\"Parse ZIP archive\"]\n    ParseZIP --\u003e ExtractCard[\"Extract card.json\"]\n    ParseZIP --\u003e ExtractAssets[\"Extract auxiliary assets\"]\n    ExtractAssets --\u003e PersistAssets[\"persistCharXAssets()\"]\n    PersistAssets --\u003e SaveSprites[\"Save to\u003cbr/\u003echaracters/{name}/\"]\n    PersistAssets --\u003e SaveBg[\"Save to\u003cbr/\u003ebackgrounds/\"]\n    \n    style CharX fill:#e1f5ff\n    style Assets fill:#f0e1ff\n```\n\n**Asset Persistence Logic:**\n\nThe `persistCharXAssets()` function processes the auxiliary assets from a CharX archive:\n\n1. **Sprite assets**: Files in `assets/sprites/` are saved to `characters/{character_name}/`\n2. **Background assets**: Files in `assets/backgrounds/` are saved to `backgrounds/`\n3. **Other assets**: Files in `assets/misc/` are saved to `assets/character/{character_name}/`\n\nSources: [src/charx.js:182-268](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:767-800]()\n\n### RisuAI Sprite Import\n\nCharacters exported from RisuAI may contain base64-encoded sprites in the `data.extensions.risuai` field. These are automatically extracted during import.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Import as \"Character Import\"\n    participant Parser as \"importRisuSprites()\"\n    participant Decoder as \"Base64 Decoder\"\n    participant FS as \"File System\"\n    \n    Import-\u003e\u003eParser: Character data\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Check for risuai.additionalAssets\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Check for risuai.emotions\n    \n    loop For each sprite\n        Parser-\u003e\u003eDecoder: Decode base64 data\n        Decoder--\u003e\u003eParser: Buffer\n        Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Generate filename\n        Parser-\u003e\u003eFS: Write sprite file\n    end\n    \n    Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Clean risuai data\n    Parser--\u003e\u003eImport: Modified character data\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/sprites.js:48-114](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:22]()\n\n---\n\n## Asset Management System\n\nThe asset management system provides structured storage for non-sprite files including audio, 3D models, and other resources.\n\n### Asset API Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Client[\"Client UI\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Asset Endpoints\u003cbr/\u003e/api/assets\"\n        Get[\"/get\u003cbr/\u003eList assets by category\"]\n        Upload[\"/upload\u003cbr/\u003eUpload new asset\"]\n        Delete[\"/delete\u003cbr/\u003eDelete asset\"]\n        Download[\"/download\u003cbr/\u003eDownload from URL\"]\n        Character[\"/character\u003cbr/\u003eGet character assets\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Storage System\"\n        Validate[\"validateAssetFileName()\"]\n        Categories[\"Asset Categories\u003cbr/\u003ebgm, ambient, blip, etc.\"]\n        FileSystem[\"File System\u003cbr/\u003eassets/{category}/\"]\n    end\n    \n    Client --\u003e Get\n    Client --\u003e Upload\n    Client --\u003e Delete\n    Client --\u003e Download\n    \n    Upload --\u003e Validate\n    Delete --\u003e Validate\n    \n    Validate --\u003e Categories\n    Categories --\u003e FileSystem\n    \n    Get --\u003e FileSystem\n    Character --\u003e FileSystem\n    \n    style Validate fill:#fff3cd\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/assets.js:98-259]()\n\n### Asset Filename Validation\n\nAll asset filenames must pass validation to prevent security issues:\n\n```javascript\n// Validation rules (src/endpoints/assets.js:21-52)\n- Alphanumeric, underscore, dash only: /^[a-zA-Z0-9_\\-.]+$/\n- No forbidden extensions (.exe, .dll, .sh, etc.)\n- Cannot start with '.'\n- Must pass sanitize-filename check\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/assets.js:21-52]()\n\n### Live2D and VRM Asset Handling\n\n3D model assets require special handling due to their multi-file structure.\n\n**Live2D Model Discovery:**\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Scan[\"Scan assets/live2d/\"]\n    Find[\"Find *.json files\u003cbr/\u003econtaining 'model'\"]\n    Return[\"Return model paths\"]\n    \n    Scan --\u003e Find\n    Find --\u003e Return\n```\n\n**VRM Model Structure:**\n```\nassets/vrm/\n├── model/               # VRM model files\n│   └── character.vrm\n└── animation/           # Animation files\n    └── idle.vrma\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/assets.js:125-137](), [src/endpoints/assets.js:140-167]()\n\n---\n\n## Background Management\n\nBackground images are stored in the `backgrounds/` directory and can be uploaded, listed, and deleted through dedicated endpoints.\n\n### Background Endpoints\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |\n|----------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/backgrounds/get` | POST | List all background images |\n| `/api/backgrounds/upload` | POST | Upload a background image |\n| `/api/backgrounds/delete` | POST | Delete a background image |\n| `/api/backgrounds/rename` | POST | Rename a background image |\n\nThe background system shares infrastructure with asset management but maintains a separate storage location for organizational purposes.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:272](), [src/users.js:83]()\n\n---\n\n## File Attachments System\n\nThe file attachments system (Data Bank) allows users to upload arbitrary files that can be referenced in chats or used by extensions.\n\n### File Upload Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant API as \"/api/files\"\n    participant Validator as \"validateAssetFileName()\"\n    participant FS as \"files/ directory\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eAPI: POST /upload\u003cbr/\u003e{name, data (base64)}\n    API-\u003e\u003eValidator: Check filename\n    Validator--\u003e\u003eAPI: Validation result\n    \n    alt Valid filename\n        API-\u003e\u003eFS: Write file (base64 decoded)\n        API-\u003e\u003eAPI: Generate client path\n        API--\u003e\u003eClient: {path: \"/user/files/name\"}\n    else Invalid filename\n        API--\u003e\u003eClient: 400 Bad Request\n    end\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eAPI: POST /list\n    API-\u003e\u003eFS: Read directory\n    FS--\u003e\u003eAPI: File list\n    API--\u003e\u003eClient: [{name, mtime, size}]\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eAPI: POST /download\u003cbr/\u003e{name}\n    API-\u003e\u003eFS: Read file\n    FS--\u003e\u003eAPI: File data\n    API--\u003e\u003eClient: File content\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/files.js:28-64]()\n\n### File Operations\n\n**Upload Validation:**\n```javascript\n// Files must pass validateAssetFileName() checks:\n// - Alphanumeric, underscore, dash, dot only\n// - No executable extensions\n// - No leading dot\n// - Must be a valid sanitized filename\n```\n\n**File Listing:**\nFiles are returned with metadata including:\n- `name`: Filename\n- `mtime`: Last modified timestamp\n- `size`: File size in bytes\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/files.js:66-88]()\n\n---\n\n## Content Import and URL Download\n\nAssets and backgrounds can be imported from external URLs if the source domain is whitelisted.\n\n### URL Download System\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Request[\"Client Request\u003cbr/\u003eURL + asset type\"]\n    \n    Request --\u003e Validate[\"Validate URL\"]\n    Validate --\u003e Whitelist[\"Check domain whitelist\"]\n    \n    Whitelist --\u003e|Allowed| Fetch[\"Fetch from URL\"]\n    Whitelist --\u003e|Blocked| Reject[\"Return 403\"]\n    \n    Fetch --\u003e Stream[\"Stream to file\"]\n    Stream --\u003e Save[\"Save to storage\"]\n    Save --\u003e Response[\"Return success\"]\n    \n    style Whitelist fill:#fff3cd\n    style Reject fill:#ffe1e1\n```\n\n**Whitelisted Domains (default):**\n- `localhost`\n- `cdn.discordapp.com`\n- `files.catbox.moe`\n- `raw.githubusercontent.com`\n\nAdditional domains can be added via the `whitelistImportDomains` configuration setting.\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:22-23](), [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:519-612]()\n\n---\n\n## Asset Caching Strategy\n\nWhile this page focuses on storage, it's worth noting that character sprites benefit from a multi-tier caching system documented in [Caching Architecture](#5.5). The cache keys are based on file paths and modification times:\n\n```javascript\n// Cache key format (src/endpoints/characters.js:166-173)\nfunction getCacheKey(inputFile) {\n    if (fs.existsSync(inputFile)) {\n        const stat = fs.statSync(inputFile);\n        return `${inputFile}-${stat.mtimeMs}`;\n    }\n    return inputFile;\n}\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:166-173]()\n\n---\n\n## Security Considerations\n\n### Filename Validation\n\nAll uploaded assets undergo strict filename validation to prevent directory traversal and code execution attacks:\n\n1. **Character whitelist**: Only `[a-zA-Z0-9_\\-.]` allowed\n2. **Extension blacklist**: Executable extensions rejected\n3. **Path traversal prevention**: No leading dots or path separators\n4. **Sanitization**: Passed through `sanitize-filename` library\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/assets.js:21-52]()\n\n### File Size Limits\n\nFile uploads are subject to the Express body parser limits configured at the server level. Large asset files should be uploaded through chunked transfer mechanisms.\n\n### User Isolation\n\nAll asset storage paths are user-specific. The middleware layer ensures users can only access their own asset directories through `request.user.directories`.\n\nSources: [src/users.js:109-126]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"35:T5806,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Caching Architecture\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [default/config.yaml](default/config.yaml)\n- [default/public/error/unauthorized.html](default/public/error/unauthorized.html)\n- [post-install.js](post-install.js)\n- [public/scripts/bookmarks.js](public/scripts/bookmarks.js)\n- [server.js](server.js)\n- [src/endpoints/characters.js](src/endpoints/characters.js)\n- [src/endpoints/chats.js](src/endpoints/chats.js)\n- [src/endpoints/content-manager.js](src/endpoints/content-manager.js)\n- [src/endpoints/groups.js](src/endpoints/groups.js)\n- [src/endpoints/presets.js](src/endpoints/presets.js)\n- [src/endpoints/settings.js](src/endpoints/settings.js)\n- [src/middleware/basicAuth.js](src/middleware/basicAuth.js)\n- [src/middleware/whitelist.js](src/middleware/whitelist.js)\n- [src/users.js](src/users.js)\n- [src/util.js](src/util.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document describes the multi-tier caching systems used in SillyTavern to optimize data access and reduce I/O operations. It covers character data caching (memory and disk), backup throttling, and directory path caching. For information about the underlying data storage formats and persistence mechanisms, see [Chat Storage and Persistence](#5.2) and [Character Management](#5.1).\n\n---\n\n## Overview\n\nSillyTavern implements several caching strategies to improve performance:\n\n- **Two-level character cache**: In-memory (L1) and disk-based (L2) caching for parsed character cards\n- **Throttled backups**: Rate-limited backup operations to prevent filesystem thrashing\n- **Directory caching**: Cached user directory path resolution\n- **Configurable capacity**: Memory and disk cache sizes configurable via `config.yaml`\n\nThe caching architecture is designed to handle large character libraries (thousands of cards) while managing memory constraints, with special optimizations for Android devices.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/characters.js:29-36](), [default/config.yaml:196-204]()\n\n---\n\n## Character Data Caching\n\n### Cache Hierarchy\n\nCharacter card data flows through a three-tier hierarchy when being read:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Request[\"readCharacterData(inputFile)\"]\n    \n    subgraph L1[\"L1 Cache - Memory\"]\n        MemCache[\"memoryCache\u003cbr/\u003e(MemoryLimitedMap)\"]\n        MemCheck{\"Key exists?\"}\n    end\n    \n    subgraph L2[\"L2 Cache - Disk\"]\n        DiskCache[\"diskCache.instance()\u003cbr/\u003e(node-persist)\"]\n        DiskCheck{\"Key exists?\"}\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Source[\"Source Data\"]\n        ParseFile[\"parse(inputFile)\u003cbr/\u003eExtract PNG metadata\"]\n    end\n    \n    Request --\u003e CacheKey[\"getCacheKey(inputFile)\u003cbr/\u003eformat: path-mtimeMs\"]\n    CacheKey --\u003e MemCheck\n    \n    MemCheck --\u003e|\"Hit\"| ReturnMem[\"Return cached data\"]\n    MemCheck --\u003e|\"Miss (useDiskCache=true)\"| DiskCheck\n    MemCheck --\u003e|\"Miss (useDiskCache=false)\"| ParseFile\n    \n    DiskCheck --\u003e|\"Hit\"| UpdateMem[\"Update memoryCache\"]\n    DiskCheck --\u003e|\"Miss\"| ParseFile\n    \n    ParseFile --\u003e UpdateBoth[\"Update both caches\u003cbr/\u003e(unless Android)\"]\n    UpdateMem --\u003e ReturnDisk[\"Return cached data\"]\n    UpdateBoth --\u003e ReturnParsed[\"Return parsed data\"]\n    \n    style L1 fill:#f9f9f9\n    style L2 fill:#f0f0f0\n    style Source fill:#e8e8e8\n```\n\n**Cache Key Format:** `${filePath}-${mtimeMs}` — combines the full file path with the modification timestamp, ensuring cache invalidation when files are modified.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/characters.js:166-209]()\n\n---\n\n### DiskCache Class\n\nThe `DiskCache` class provides persistent caching of parsed character card JSON data using the `node-persist` library.\n\n#### Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph DiskCacheClass[\"DiskCache Instance\"]\n        Instance[\"#instance\u003cbr/\u003e(LocalStorage)\"]\n        SyncQueue[\"syncQueue\u003cbr/\u003e(Set\u0026lt;string\u0026gt;)\"]\n        SyncInterval[\"#syncInterval\u003cbr/\u003e(NodeJS.Timeout)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph FileSystem[\"File System\"]\n        CacheDir[\"DATA_ROOT/_cache/characters/\"]\n        HashedFiles[\"Hashed key files\u003cbr/\u003e(node-persist format)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Operations[\"Operations\"]\n        GetInstance[\"instance()\u003cbr/\u003eInitialize storage\"]\n        Verify[\"verify(directoriesList)\u003cbr/\u003ePrune stale entries\"]\n        Sync[\"#syncCacheEntries()\u003cbr/\u003eEvery 5 minutes\"]\n    end\n    \n    GetInstance --\u003e Instance\n    Instance --\u003e CacheDir\n    CacheDir --\u003e HashedFiles\n    \n    SyncQueue --\u003e Sync\n    SyncInterval --\u003e Sync\n    Sync --\u003e Verify\n    \n    style DiskCacheClass fill:#f9f9f9\n    style Operations fill:#e8f5e9\n```\n\n#### Key Properties and Methods\n\n| Property/Method | Type | Description |\n|----------------|------|-------------|\n| `DIRECTORY` | `static string` | Cache directory name: `'characters'` |\n| `SYNC_INTERVAL` | `static number` | Sync interval: `300000` ms (5 minutes) |\n| `syncQueue` | `Set\u003cstring\u003e` | User handles pending cache verification |\n| `cachePath` | `getter` | Returns `DATA_ROOT/_cache/characters` |\n| `instance()` | `async` | Initializes and returns node-persist storage |\n| `verify(directoriesList)` | `async` | Validates cache entries against actual files |\n| `dispose()` | `void` | Cleans up sync interval timer |\n\n#### Cache Verification Process\n\nThe `verify()` method prunes stale cache entries by:\n\n1. Scanning all user character directories for `.png` files\n2. Computing expected cache keys: `getCacheKey(filePath)`\n3. Comparing against actual hashed keys in cache directory\n4. Removing cache entries that no longer have corresponding source files\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-159]()\n\n---\n\n### MemoryLimitedMap\n\nThe `MemoryLimitedMap` class provides an in-memory cache with a configurable size limit measured in bytes. It wraps a standard JavaScript `Map` and tracks the total size of stored values.\n\n#### Configuration\n\n```javascript\n// From characters.js:30-31\nconst memoryCacheCapacity = getConfigValue('performance.memoryCacheCapacity', '100mb');\nconst memoryCache = new MemoryLimitedMap(memoryCacheCapacity);\n```\n\nThe capacity is specified as a human-readable string (e.g., `'100mb'`, `'50mb'`) and parsed by the `bytes` library.\n\n#### Platform-Specific Behavior\n\n```javascript\n// From characters.js:33\nconst isAndroid = process.platform === 'android';\n\n// From characters.js:199\n!isAndroid \u0026\u0026 memoryCache.set(cacheKey, result);\n```\n\nOn Android devices, the memory cache is **not populated** to avoid memory pressure on resource-constrained devices. The disk cache is still used.\n\n#### Cache Operations\n\n| Operation | Description |\n|-----------|-------------|\n| `memoryCache.has(cacheKey)` | Check if key exists in memory |\n| `memoryCache.get(cacheKey)` | Retrieve cached value |\n| `memoryCache.set(cacheKey, result)` | Store value (if not Android) |\n| `memoryCache.delete(key)` | Remove single entry |\n| `memoryCache.keys()` | Iterate over all keys |\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/characters.js:29-31](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:184-199](), [src/util.js:17]()\n\n---\n\n### Read Path: readCharacterData()\n\nThe `readCharacterData()` function implements the cache hierarchy for reading character cards:\n\n```javascript\nasync function readCharacterData(inputFile, inputFormat = 'png') {\n    const cacheKey = getCacheKey(inputFile);\n    \n    // L1: Check memory cache\n    if (memoryCache.has(cacheKey)) {\n        return memoryCache.get(cacheKey);\n    }\n    \n    // L2: Check disk cache\n    if (useDiskCache) {\n        try {\n            const cache = await diskCache.instance();\n            const cachedData = await cache.getItem(cacheKey);\n            if (cachedData) {\n                return cachedData;\n            }\n        } catch (error) {\n            console.warn('Error while reading from disk cache:', error);\n        }\n    }\n    \n    // Source: Parse from file\n    const result = await parse(inputFile, inputFormat);\n    \n    // Update caches\n    !isAndroid \u0026\u0026 memoryCache.set(cacheKey, result);\n    if (useDiskCache) {\n        try {\n            const cache = await diskCache.instance();\n            await cache.setItem(cacheKey, result);\n        } catch (error) {\n            console.warn('Error while writing to disk cache:', error);\n        }\n    }\n    \n    return result;\n}\n```\n\n**Cache Miss Handling:** On a complete cache miss, the function parses the PNG file using the `parse()` function from the character card parser, then populates both cache levels for future requests.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/characters.js:181-209]()\n\n---\n\n### Write Path: writeCharacterData()\n\nWhen character data is modified, the cache must be invalidated to prevent serving stale data:\n\n```javascript\nasync function writeCharacterData(inputFile, data, outputFile, request, crop) {\n    try {\n        // Reset memory cache\n        for (const key of memoryCache.keys()) {\n            if (Buffer.isBuffer(inputFile)) {\n                break;\n            }\n            if (key.startsWith(inputFile)) {\n                memoryCache.delete(key);\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n        \n        // Schedule disk cache verification\n        if (useDiskCache \u0026\u0026 !Buffer.isBuffer(inputFile)) {\n            diskCache.syncQueue.add(request.user.profile.handle);\n        }\n        \n        // ... write operations ...\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Invalidation Strategy:**\n\n1. **Memory cache**: Immediately invalidated by deleting matching keys\n2. **Disk cache**: User handle added to `syncQueue` for deferred verification\n3. **Verification**: Runs every 5 minutes via `setInterval`, triggered by `#syncCacheEntries()`\n\nThis deferred approach prevents blocking the write operation while still ensuring eventual consistency.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/characters.js:220-265]()\n\n---\n\n### Cache Key Generation\n\nCache keys incorporate both the file path and modification timestamp:\n\n```javascript\nfunction getCacheKey(inputFile) {\n    if (fs.existsSync(inputFile)) {\n        const stat = fs.statSync(inputFile);\n        return `${inputFile}-${stat.mtimeMs}`;\n    }\n    return inputFile;\n}\n```\n\n**Automatic Invalidation:** When a character file is modified, its `mtimeMs` changes, resulting in a different cache key. This provides automatic cache invalidation without explicit management.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/characters.js:166-173]()\n\n---\n\n## Backup Throttling\n\nChat and settings backups use throttling to prevent excessive filesystem writes while ensuring data safety.\n\n### Chat Backup Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    SaveRequest[\"saveChatConditional()\"]\n    \n    subgraph BackupSystem[\"Backup Throttling System\"]\n        BackupMap[\"backupFunctions\u003cbr/\u003e(Map\u0026lt;handle, throttledFunc\u0026gt;)\"]\n        GetFunc[\"getBackupFunction(handle)\"]\n        Throttled[\"_.throttle(backupChat, 10s)\u003cbr/\u003eleading: true\u003cbr/\u003etrailing: true\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph BackupOps[\"Backup Operations\"]\n        BackupFile[\"backupChat(directory, name, data)\"]\n        WriteFile[\"Write to backups/\u003cbr/\u003echat_name_timestamp.jsonl\"]\n        Prune[\"removeOldBackups(directory, prefix)\"]\n    end\n    \n    SaveRequest --\u003e GetFunc\n    GetFunc --\u003e BackupMap\n    BackupMap --\u003e|\"New user\"| CreateThrottled[\"Create throttled function\"]\n    BackupMap --\u003e|\"Exists\"| Throttled\n    CreateThrottled --\u003e Throttled\n    \n    Throttled --\u003e BackupFile\n    BackupFile --\u003e WriteFile\n    WriteFile --\u003e Prune\n    \n    style BackupSystem fill:#fff3cd\n```\n\n#### Throttle Configuration\n\n| Parameter | Value | Effect |\n|-----------|-------|--------|\n| `throttleInterval` | `10000` ms (10s) | Maximum backup frequency per user |\n| `leading` | `true` | Execute immediately on first call |\n| `trailing` | `true` | Execute after wait time if called during throttle |\n\nThe `leading: true, trailing: true` configuration ensures that:\n- The first save triggers an immediate backup\n- Subsequent saves within 10 seconds are buffered\n- A final backup occurs after the throttle period if saves occurred during it\n\n#### Per-User Throttling\n\n```javascript\nconst backupFunctions = new Map();\n\nfunction getBackupFunction(handle) {\n    if (!backupFunctions.has(handle)) {\n        backupFunctions.set(handle, _.throttle(backupChat, throttleInterval, \n            { leading: true, trailing: true }));\n    }\n    return backupFunctions.get(handle);\n}\n```\n\nEach user gets their own throttled backup function, preventing one user's frequent saves from blocking another user's backups.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/chats.js:62-77](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:25-28]()\n\n---\n\n### Settings Autosave Throttling\n\nUser settings use a similar throttling mechanism with a longer interval:\n\n```javascript\n// From settings.js:18\nconst AUTOSAVE_INTERVAL = 10 * 60 * 1000; // 10 minutes\n\nconst AUTOSAVE_FUNCTIONS = new Map();\n\nfunction triggerAutoSave(handle) {\n    if (!AUTOSAVE_FUNCTIONS.has(handle)) {\n        const throttledAutoSave = _.throttle(\n            () =\u003e backupUserSettings(handle, true), \n            AUTOSAVE_INTERVAL\n        );\n        AUTOSAVE_FUNCTIONS.set(handle, throttledAutoSave);\n    }\n    \n    const functionToCall = AUTOSAVE_FUNCTIONS.get(handle);\n    if (functionToCall \u0026\u0026 typeof functionToCall === 'function') {\n        functionToCall();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Duplicate Prevention:** Settings backups include a `preventDuplicates` flag that compares the current settings file against the most recent backup to avoid creating identical backups.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/settings.js:18-41](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:132-166]()\n\n---\n\n## Directory Caching\n\nUser directory paths are cached to avoid repeated filesystem traversal and path construction.\n\n### DIRECTORIES_CACHE\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph Cache[\"DIRECTORIES_CACHE Map\"]\n        Key[\"Key: user handle (string)\"]\n        Value[\"Value: UserDirectoryList\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph DirectoryList[\"UserDirectoryList Structure\"]\n        Root[\"root: DATA_ROOT/handle\"]\n        Characters[\"characters: root/characters\"]\n        Chats[\"chats: root/chats\"]\n        Groups[\"groups: root/groups\"]\n        Worlds[\"worlds: root/worlds\"]\n        Etc[\"... 30+ directory paths\"]\n    end\n    \n    GetUserDirs[\"getUserDirectories(handle)\"]\n    \n    GetUserDirs --\u003e CacheCheck{\"handle in\u003cbr/\u003eDIRECTORIES_CACHE?\"}\n    CacheCheck --\u003e|\"Hit\"| ReturnCached[\"Return cached value\"]\n    CacheCheck --\u003e|\"Miss\"| BuildDirs[\"Build directory structure\"]\n    BuildDirs --\u003e UpdateCache[\"Cache result\"]\n    UpdateCache --\u003e ReturnNew[\"Return new value\"]\n    \n    Key --\u003e Value\n    Value --\u003e DirectoryList\n    \n    style Cache fill:#f9f9f9\n```\n\n### Cache Population\n\nThe cache is populated on-demand:\n\n```javascript\nexport function getUserDirectories(handle) {\n    if (DIRECTORIES_CACHE.has(handle)) {\n        return DIRECTORIES_CACHE.get(handle);\n    }\n    \n    const directories = { /* construct 30+ directory paths */ };\n    DIRECTORIES_CACHE.set(handle, directories);\n    return directories;\n}\n```\n\n**Cache Lifetime:** The cache persists for the lifetime of the server process. There is no eviction policy, as the number of users is typically small and directory structures are immutable once created.\n\n**Sources:** [src/users.js:34-36](), [src/users.js:492-503]()\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\nCache behavior is controlled through `config.yaml` and environment variables.\n\n### Performance Configuration\n\n```yaml\n# From config.yaml:196-204\nperformance:\n  # Enables lazy loading of character cards. Improves performances with large card libraries.\n  # May have compatibility issues with some extensions.\n  lazyLoadCharacters: false\n  \n  # The maximum amount of memory that parsed character cards can use. \n  # Set to 0 to disable memory caching.\n  memoryCacheCapacity: '100mb'\n  \n  # Enables disk caching for character cards. Improves performances with large card libraries.\n  useDiskCache: true\n```\n\n### Configuration Table\n\n| Setting | Type | Default | Description |\n|---------|------|---------|-------------|\n| `performance.lazyLoadCharacters` | `boolean` | `false` | Load only essential character metadata initially |\n| `performance.memoryCacheCapacity` | `string` | `'100mb'` | Maximum memory for character cache (supports `'kb'`, `'mb'`, `'gb'`) |\n| `performance.useDiskCache` | `boolean` | `true` | Enable persistent disk caching |\n| `backups.chat.throttleInterval` | `number` | `10000` | Chat backup throttle interval in milliseconds |\n\n### Environment Variable Overrides\n\nAny configuration value can be overridden via environment variables:\n\n```bash\n# Format: SILLYTAVERN_PERFORMANCE_MEMORYCACHECAPACITY\nexport SILLYTAVERN_PERFORMANCE_MEMORYCACHECAPACITY='200mb'\nexport SILLYTAVERN_PERFORMANCE_USEDISKCACHE='false'\n```\n\n**Sources:** [default/config.yaml:196-204](), [src/util.js:33-109](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:25-28]()\n\n---\n\n## Cache Performance Characteristics\n\n### Memory Cache\n\n| Aspect | Behavior |\n|--------|----------|\n| **Lookup Time** | O(1) — HashMap lookup |\n| **Storage Overhead** | ~2x data size (key + value + Map overhead) |\n| **Eviction Policy** | Size-based, tracks total byte count |\n| **Persistence** | None — lost on server restart |\n| **Ideal For** | Frequently accessed characters, small-medium libraries |\n\n### Disk Cache\n\n| Aspect | Behavior |\n|--------|----------|\n| **Lookup Time** | O(1) + disk I/O latency (~1-10ms) |\n| **Storage Overhead** | Minimal — JSON files with hashed names |\n| **Eviction Policy** | Manual via `verify()` — removes entries for deleted source files |\n| **Persistence** | Survives server restarts |\n| **Ideal For** | Large character libraries (1000+ cards), reducing parse overhead |\n\n### Trade-offs\n\n**With both caches enabled (default):**\n- First access: ~20-50ms (parse PNG + update both caches)\n- Subsequent access (memory): \u003c1ms\n- Subsequent access (disk): ~1-5ms\n- Restart recovery: 1-5ms (disk cache survives)\n\n**With only memory cache:**\n- First access: ~20-50ms (parse PNG)\n- Subsequent access: \u003c1ms\n- Restart recovery: ~20-50ms per character (full reparse)\n\n**With caches disabled:**\n- Every access: ~20-50ms (parse PNG every time)\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/characters.js:181-209]()\n\n---\n\n## Cache Lifecycle Events\n\n### Server Startup\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Main as server.js\n    participant Users as users.js\n    participant Characters as characters.js\n    participant Disk as DiskCache\n    \n    Main-\u003e\u003eUsers: ensurePublicDirectoriesExist()\n    Users-\u003e\u003eUsers: getUserDirectoriesList()\n    Users--\u003e\u003eMain: directoriesList[]\n    \n    Main-\u003e\u003eDisk: diskCache.instance()\n    Disk-\u003e\u003eDisk: storage.init({ dir: cachePath })\n    Disk-\u003e\u003eDisk: setInterval(syncCacheEntries, 5min)\n    Disk--\u003e\u003eMain: Initialized\n    \n    Note over Disk: Disk cache ready for use\n    Note over Characters: Memory cache empty, will populate on demand\n```\n\n### Character Import/Edit\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant Server as /api/characters/edit\n    participant Write as writeCharacterData()\n    participant MemCache as memoryCache\n    participant DiskCache as diskCache.syncQueue\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eServer: POST character edit\n    Server-\u003e\u003eWrite: writeCharacterData(...)\n    \n    Write-\u003e\u003eMemCache: Iterate keys, delete matches\n    MemCache--\u003e\u003eWrite: Cache invalidated\n    \n    Write-\u003e\u003eDiskCache: syncQueue.add(userHandle)\n    DiskCache--\u003e\u003eWrite: Scheduled for verification\n    \n    Write-\u003e\u003eWrite: write PNG file\n    Write--\u003e\u003eServer: Success\n    Server--\u003e\u003eClient: 200 OK\n    \n    Note over DiskCache: Actual verification occurs\u003cbr/\u003ewithin next 5 minutes\n```\n\n### Server Shutdown\n\n```javascript\n// From characters.js process exit handler\nprocess.on('exit', () =\u003e {\n    diskCache.dispose();\n});\n```\n\nThe `dispose()` method clears the sync interval timer to allow graceful shutdown.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/characters.js:152-156](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:220-265]()\n\n---\n\n## Memory Management\n\n### Size Calculation for MemoryLimitedMap\n\nThe `MemoryLimitedMap` tracks the byte size of stored values to enforce the capacity limit. When the limit is exceeded, it evicts entries to free space.\n\n```javascript\n// Conceptual implementation\nclass MemoryLimitedMap {\n    constructor(maxSize) {\n        this.maxSize = parseBytes(maxSize); // e.g., '100mb' -\u003e 104857600\n        this.map = new Map();\n        this.currentSize = 0;\n    }\n    \n    set(key, value) {\n        const valueSize = Buffer.byteLength(JSON.stringify(value));\n        \n        // Evict if needed\n        while (this.currentSize + valueSize \u003e this.maxSize \u0026\u0026 this.map.size \u003e 0) {\n            const firstKey = this.map.keys().next().value;\n            this.delete(firstKey);\n        }\n        \n        this.map.set(key, value);\n        this.currentSize += valueSize;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Eviction Strategy:** First-In-First-Out (FIFO) — oldest entries are evicted first when the size limit is reached.\n\n**Sources:** [src/util.js:17](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:30-31]()\n\n---\n\n## Advanced Topics\n\n### Lazy Loading Integration\n\nWhen `performance.lazyLoadCharacters` is enabled, the system uses the `toShallow()` function to return minimal character data:\n\n```javascript\nconst toShallow = (character) =\u003e {\n    return {\n        shallow: true,\n        name: character.name,\n        avatar: character.avatar,\n        chat: character.chat,\n        fav: character.fav,\n        date_added: character.date_added,\n        create_date: character.create_date,\n        date_last_chat: character.date_last_chat,\n        chat_size: character.chat_size,\n        data_size: character.data_size,\n        tags: character.tags,\n        data: {\n            name: _.get(character, 'data.name', ''),\n            character_version: _.get(character, 'data.character_version', ''),\n            creator: _.get(character, 'data.creator', ''),\n            creator_notes: _.get(character, 'data.creator_notes', ''),\n            tags: _.get(character, 'data.tags', []),\n            extensions: {\n                fav: _.get(character, 'data.extensions.fav', false),\n            },\n        },\n    };\n};\n```\n\nThis reduces memory footprint for the character list view, with full character data loaded only when a character is opened.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/characters.js:371-395](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:35]()\n\n### Cache Warming Strategy\n\nThere is no explicit cache warming mechanism. Caches are populated on-demand as characters are accessed. For large libraries, the first character list load will populate the disk cache, benefiting subsequent server restarts.\n\n### Cache Statistics\n\nThe current implementation does not expose cache statistics (hit rate, miss rate, eviction count). These could be added by instrumenting the `MemoryLimitedMap` and `DiskCache` classes with counters.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-209]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"36:T5937,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Context and Memory [src/endpoints/backends/kobold.js](src/endpoints/backends/kobold.js)\n- [src/endpoints/files.js](src/endpoints/files.js)\n- [src/endpoints/sprites.js](src/endpoints/sprites.js)\n- [src/endpoints/vectors.js](src/endpoints/vectors.js)\n- [src/vectors/cohere-vectors.js](src/vectors/cohere-vectors.js)\n- [src/vectors/extras-vectors.js](src/vectors/extras-vectors.js)\n- [src/vectors/nomicai-vectors.js](src/vectors/nomicai-vectors.js)\n- [src/vectors/openai-vectors.js](src/vectors/openai-vectors.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page provides an overview of SillyTavern's context and memory management systems. These systems augment AI prompts with additional information to improve response quality and consistency. Three main subsystems work together to provide different types of context:\n\n- **World Info/Lorebooks**: Keyword-triggered lore injection (detailed in [World Info System](#6.1))\n- **Chat Summarization**: Periodic conversation summaries (detailed in [Chat Summarization Extension](#6.2))\n- **Vector Storage/RAG**: Semantic retrieval of relevant information (detailed in [Vector Storage and RAG System](#6.3))\n\nThis page focuses on how these systems integrate into the prompt assembly pipeline and interact with each other. For detailed information about each subsystem's internal workings, see their respective pages.\n\n## Purpose in the Generation Pipeline\n\nContext and memory systems solve a fundamental problem: AI models have limited context windows, but conversations and character lore can be extensive. These systems intelligently select and inject the most relevant information into each generation request.\n\n**Context Systems in Generation Pipeline**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    UserMsg[\"User sends message\"]\n    Generate[\"Generate() called\"]\n    PromptMgr[\"setupChatCompletionPromptManager()\"]\n    \n    subgraph ContextSystems[\"Context \u0026 Memory Systems\"]\n        WI[\"World Info System\u003cbr/\u003egetWorldInfoPrompt()\"]\n        Memory[\"Memory Extension\u003cbr/\u003eModule: '1_memory'\"]\n        Vectors[\"Vectors Extension\u003cbr/\u003eTags: '3_vectors', '4_vectors_data_bank'\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph PromptAssembly[\"Prompt Assembly\"]\n        ExtPrompts[\"extension_prompts object\u003cbr/\u003eKeyed by position \u0026 identifier\"]\n        BuildPrompt[\"Build messages array\"]\n        InjectWI[\"Inject WI at scan depth\"]\n        InjectExt[\"Inject extension prompts\u003cbr/\u003eat configured positions\"]\n    end\n    \n    API[\"sendOpenAIRequest()\u003cbr/\u003eor text completion\"]\n    \n    UserMsg --\u003e Generate\n    Generate --\u003e PromptMgr\n    PromptMgr --\u003e WI\n    PromptMgr --\u003e Memory\n    PromptMgr --\u003e Vectors\n    \n    WI --\u003e ExtPrompts\n    Memory --\u003e ExtPrompts\n    Vectors --\u003e ExtPrompts\n    \n    ExtPrompts --\u003e BuildPrompt\n    BuildPrompt --\u003e InjectWI\n    BuildPrompt --\u003e InjectExt\n    InjectExt --\u003e API\n```\n\nSources: [public/script.js:100-114](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:1-52](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:1-40](), [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:1-55]()\n\n## System Architecture Overview\n\nEach context system operates independently but coordinates through the extension prompt mechanism. They run at different stages of the generation process and inject content at configurable positions.\n\n**Context Systems Architecture**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph WorldInfo[\"World Info System\"]\n        WIEntries[\"World Info Entries\u003cbr/\u003e{world}.json files\"]\n        WIScanner[\"WorldInfoBuffer\u003cbr/\u003eKeyword scanning\"]\n        WIActivation[\"Activation Logic\u003cbr/\u003eAND/OR/NOT\"]\n        WIBudget[\"Token Budget Manager\u003cbr/\u003efilterByScoreAndBudget()\"]\n        WITimed[\"WorldInfoTimedEffects\u003cbr/\u003esticky, cooldown, delay\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph MemoryExt[\"Memory Extension\"]\n        ChatHistory[\"Chat Messages\u003cbr/\u003egetContext().chat\"]\n        Summarizer[\"summarizeChat()\u003cbr/\u003eCalls etc.\"]\n        VecQuery[\"queryCollection()\u003cbr/\u003eSimilarity search\"]\n        VecPrompt[\"Extension Prompts\u003cbr/\u003e'3_vectors', '4_vectors_data_bank'\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph System Overview\n\nThe World Info system (also called \"Lorebooks\") provides keyword-activated lore injection. When specific keywords appear in the chat, corresponding entries are inserted into the prompt.\n\n### Key Components\n\n- **Entries**: Individual lore pieces with activation keys, content, and metadata\n- **Scanning**: `WorldInfoBuffer` scans recent messages for keyword matches\n- **Activation Logic**: Supports `AND_ANY`, `AND_ALL`, `NOT_ANY`, `NOT_ALL` for complex triggers\n- **Budget Management**: Token-based limits prevent context overflow\n- **Timed Effects**: Sticky entries persist, cooldowns prevent repeated activation, delays defer activation\n\n**World Info Entry Fields**\n\n| Field | Type | Purpose |\n|-------|------|---------|\n| `key` | string[] | Primary activation keywords |\n| `keysecondary` | string[] | Secondary keywords for logic operations |\n| `content` | string | Text to inject when activated |\n| `selectiveLogic` | number | Logic type (AND/OR/NOT) |\n| `constant` | boolean | Always inject if true |\n| `order` | number | Priority for injection order |\n| `position` | number | Where to inject (before_char, after_char, etc.) |\n| `depth` | number | At which message depth to inject |\n| `sticky` | number | Messages to remain active after trigger |\n| `cooldown` | number | Messages to wait before re-activation |\n| `delay` | number | Messages to wait before initial activation |\n\nSources: [public/scripts/world-info.js:102-183]()\n\n### Integration Points\n\nWorld Info integrates at multiple points in the generation flow:\n\n```javascript\n// Called during prompt assembly\ngetWorldInfoPrompt(allMessages, maxContext, isDryRun)\n// Returns: { worldInfoString, worldInfoBefore, worldInfoAfter, ... }\n```\n\nThe system scans the chat buffer, activates matching entries, applies budget constraints, and returns formatted strings for injection at various positions.\n\n**Detailed architecture and behavior is documented in [World Info System](#6.1).**\n\nSources: [public/scripts/world-info.js:1282-1600]()\n\n## Chat Summarization Extension\n\nThe Memory extension periodically generates summaries of the conversation to preserve important context while staying within token limits.\n\n### Key Components\n\n- **Summarization Sources**: Uses Main API, Extras API (deprecated), or WebLLM Extension\n- **Trigger Mechanism**: Runs every N messages (configurable interval)\n- **Caching**: Stores summaries by message hash to avoid redundant generation\n- **Injection**: Adds summary to prompt via extension prompt system\n\n**Memory Extension Flow**\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Chat as Chat System\n    participant Module as moduleWorker\n    participant Mem as Memory Extension\n    participant LLM as LLM Provider\n    participant Prompt as Prompt System\n    \n    Chat-\u003e\u003eModule: CHAT_CHANGED event\n    Module-\u003e\u003eMem: onChatEvent()\n    Mem-\u003e\u003eMem: Check interval threshold\n    \n    alt Should summarize\n        Mem-\u003e\u003eMem: Get last N messages\n        Mem-\u003e\u003eMem: Check cachedSummaries\n        alt Cache miss\n            Mem-\u003e\u003eLLM: summarizeChat()\n            Note over LLM: Uses configured source\u003cbr/\u003eMain API/Extras/WebLLM\n            LLM--\u003e\u003eMem: Summary text\n            Mem-\u003e\u003eMem: Store in cachedSummaries\n        end\n        Mem-\u003e\u003eMem: formatMemoryValue()\n        Mem-\u003e\u003ePrompt: setExtensionPrompt()\u003cbr/\u003eidentifier: '1_memory'\n    end\n    \n    Prompt-\u003e\u003eChat: Summary injected at\u003cbr/\u003econfigured position\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:183-435]()\n\n### Configuration\n\nKey settings control summarization behavior:\n\n| Setting | Purpose | Default |\n|---------|---------|---------|\n| `prompt` | LLM instruction for summarization | \"Summarize the most important facts...\" |\n| `template` | How to format summary in prompt | \"[Summary: {{summary}}]\" |\n| `promptInterval` | Messages between summaries | 10 |\n| `promptWords` | Target summary length | 200 |\n| `position` | Where to inject summary | `IN_PROMPT` |\n| `role` | Message role for summary | `SYSTEM` |\n| `depth` | Scan depth for messages | 2 |\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:99-138]()\n\n### Summarization Methods\n\n```javascript\n// Main API source - uses current chat completion API\nasync function summarizeMain(text, targetWords)\n\n// Extras API source (deprecated)\nasync function summarizeExtra(element)\n\n// WebLLM source - uses local browser model\nasync function summarizeWebLlm(text, targetWords)\n```\n\nEach method formats the request appropriately for its target API and returns the summary text.\n\n**Detailed configuration and behavior is documented in [Chat Summarization Extension](#6.2).**\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:437-743]()\n\n## Vector Storage and RAG System\n\nThe Vectors extension uses embedding-based retrieval (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) to find semantically relevant past messages, file content, and World Info entries.\n\n### Key Components\n\n- **Vectorization**: Converts text to embeddings using various providers\n- **Storage**: Uses `vectra.LocalIndex` for local vector database\n- **Collections**: Separate indices for chats, files, and World Info\n- **Retrieval**: Queries by semantic similarity to current context\n\n**Vector Storage Architecture**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Sources[\"Content Sources\"]\n        ChatMsgs[\"Chat Messages\"]\n        Files[\"Attached Files\"]\n        WIEntries[\"World Info Entries\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Vectorization[\"Vectorization Pipeline\"]\n        Chunk[\"splitByChunks()\u003cbr/\u003eSplit into chunks\"]\n        Summarize[\"summarizeExtra()\u003cbr/\u003eOptional summarization\"]\n        Embed[\"getVector()\u003cbr/\u003eGenerate embeddings\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Providers[\"Embedding Providers\"]\n        OpenAI[\"OpenAI API\"]\n        Transform[\"Transformers (local)\"]\n        Ollama[\"Ollama\"]\n        Cohere[\"Cohere\"]\n        Others[\"Others...\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Storage[\"Vector Storage\"]\n        ChatIdx[\"Chat Collection\u003cbr/\u003egetCurrentChatId()\"]\n        FileIdx[\"File Collections\u003cbr/\u003efile_${hash}\"]\n        WIIdx[\"World Info Collection\u003cbr/\u003eworld_info_${name}\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Retrieval[\"Retrieval System\"]\n        Query[\"queryCollection()\u003cbr/\u003eSimilarity search\"]\n        Format[\"Format results\"]\n        Inject[\"setExtensionPrompt()\u003cbr/\u003e'3_vectors', '4_vectors_data_bank'\"]\n    end\n    \n    ChatMsgs --\u003e Chunk\n    Files --\u003e Chunk\n    WIEntries --\u003e Chunk\n    \n    Chunk --\u003e Summarize\n    Summarize --\u003e Embed\n    \n    Embed --\u003e OpenAI\n    Embed --\u003e Transform\n    Embed --\u003e Ollama\n    Embed --\u003e Cohere\n    Embed --\u003e Others\n    \n    OpenAI --\u003e ChatIdx\n    OpenAI --\u003e FileIdx\n    OpenAI --\u003e WIIdx\n    \n    ChatIdx --\u003e Query\n    FileIdx --\u003e Query\n    WIIdx --\u003e Query\n    \n    Query --\u003e Format\n    Format --\u003e Inject\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:42-230](), [src/endpoints/vectors.js:22-98]()\n\n### Vectorization Process\n\n```javascript\n// Main synchronization function\nasync function synchronizeChat(batchSize = 5)\n// Returns: Number of messages remaining to vectorize\n\n// For each message:\n// 1. Hash the message text\n// 2. Check if already vectorized\n// 3. Split into chunks if needed\n// 4. Generate embeddings\n// 5. Insert into vector index\n```\n\nThe system processes messages incrementally to avoid blocking the UI. A progress indicator shows vectorization status.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:279-471]()\n\n### Query and Retrieval\n\nWhen generating a response, the system queries the vector database:\n\n```javascript\nasync function retrieveFromVectorIndex(source)\n// 1. Get recent messages as query\n// 2. Query vector collections\n// 3. Filter by score threshold\n// 4. Return top-K results\n```\n\nResults are formatted and injected into the prompt at the configured position.\n\n### Supported Embedding Sources\n\nThe system supports multiple embedding providers, each with specific configuration:\n\n| Source | API Endpoint | Models |\n|--------|--------------|--------|\n| `transformers` | Local (browser) | Built-in transformer models |\n| `openai` | api.openai.com | text-embedding-ada-002, etc. |\n| `cohere` | api.cohere.ai | embed-english-v3.0, etc. |\n| `ollama` | Local server | User-downloaded models |\n| `llamacpp` | Local server | User models |\n| `palm` | Google AI Studio | text-embedding-005 |\n| `nomicai` | api-atlas.nomic.ai | nomic-embed-text |\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/vectors.js:22-98](), [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:56-74]()\n\n**Detailed architecture and configuration is documented in [Vector Storage and RAG System](#6.3).**\n\n## Integration with Prompt Assembly\n\nAll three systems inject content through the extension prompt mechanism. This provides a unified interface for adding context at specific positions in the prompt.\n\n**Extension Prompt Integration**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph ExtensionPrompts[\"extension_prompts Object\"]\n        direction TB\n        InPrompt[\"extension_prompt_types.IN_PROMPT\"]\n        BeforePrompt[\"extension_prompt_types.BEFORE_PROMPT\"]\n Assembly[\"Prompt Assembly\"]\n        Builder[\"Message Builder\"]\n        Inject[\"Injection Logic\"]\n        Final[\"Final Prompt\"]\n    end\n    \n    WI --\u003e|\"setExtensionPrompt()\"| InPrompt\n    WI --\u003e|\"worldInfoBefore\"| BeforePrompt\n    WI --\u003e|\"worldInfoAfter\"| AfterPrompt\n    WI --\u003e|\"worldInfoDepth\"| AtDepth\n    \n    Mem --\u003e|\"setExtensionPrompt()\u003cbr/\u003e'1_memory'\"| InPrompt\n    Vec --\u003e|\"setExtensionPrompt()\u003cbr/\u003e'3_vectors'\"| InPrompt\n    Vec [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:375-385](), [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:844-873]()\n\n### Extension Prompt Types\n\nThe `extension_prompt_types` enum defines injection positions:\n\n```javascript\nexport const extension_prompt_types = {\n    NONE: -1,\n    IN_PROMPT: 0,      // Story string position\n    IN_CHAT: 1,        // Not commonly used\n    BEFORE_PROMPT: 2,  // Before all prompt components\n}\n```\n\nFor depth-based injection, entries specify a `depth` value indicating how many messages from the end to inject before.\n\nSources: [public/script.js:452-461]()\n\n### Injection Order\n\nDuring prompt assembly, the PromptManager processes extension prompts in a specific order:\n\n1. **BEFORE_PROMPT**: Injected at the very beginning\n2. **Story String Position**: Based on `context.story_string_position` setting\n3. **IN_PROMPT**: Typically at story string position\n4. **Depth-based**: Injected at specific message depths\n5. **AFTER_PROMPT**: Not commonly used, but available\n\nWorld Info has special handling - it can inject at multiple positions simultaneously (before char, after char, at depth, etc.).\n\nSources: [public/script.js:3308-3440]()\n\n## Configuration and Coordination\n\n### Global Settings\n\nContext systems have global settings that affect all three subsystems:\n\n**Budget Management**\n\n```javascript\n// World Info budget (percentage of context)\nworld_info_budget = 25\n\n// Maximum tokens for WI entries\nworld_info_budget_cap = 0  // 0 = unlimited\n\n// Scan depth for keyword matching\nworld_info_depth = 2\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/world-info.js:66-82]()\n\n**Extension Prompt Settings**\n\nEach system can configure its prompt position and role:\n\n```javascript\n// Memory extension\nextension_settings.memory.position = extension_prompt_types.IN_PROMPT\nextension_settings.memory.role = extension_prompt_roles.SYSTEM\n\n// Vectors extension\nextension_settings.vectors.position = extension_prompt_types.IN_PROMPT\nextension_settings.vectors.file_position_db = extension_prompt_types.IN_PROMPT\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:108-115](), [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:84-110]()\n\n### Per-Chat Metadata\n\nChat-specific state is stored in `chat_metadata`:\n\n```javascript\n// World Info timed effects\nchat_metadata.timedWorldInfo = {\n    sticky: {},    // Entries with sticky effect\n    cooldown: {},  // Entries on cooldown\n    delay: {}      // Entries with delayed activation\n}\n\n// Vector embeddings cache\nchat_metadata.vectors = {\n    // Hashed message embeddings\n}\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/world-info.js:480-543](), [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:473-520]()\n\n### Performance Considerations\n\n**Debouncing and Throttling**\n\nAll three systems use debouncing to prevent excessive processing:\n\n```javascript\n// World Info saves\nconst saveWorldDebounced = debounce(async (name, data) =\u003e \n    await _save(name, data), debounce_timeout.relaxed);\n\n// Chat save after memory update\nconst saveChatDebounced = debounce(() =\u003e \n    getContext().saveChat(), debounce_timeout.relaxed);\n\n// Vector synchronization\nconst moduleWorker = new ModuleWorkerWrapper(synchronizeChat);\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/world-info.js:83](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:91](), [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:117]()\n\n**Batch Processing**\n\nVector embeddings support batch processing to reduce API calls:\n\n```javascript\n// Batch size depends on provider\nconst getBatchSize = () =\u003e \n    ['transformers', 'ollama'].includes(settings.source) ? 1 : 5;\n\n// Process multiple messages at once\nasync function getVectorBatch(source, sourceSettings, texts, isQuery, directories)\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:55-56](), [src/endpoints/vectors.js:93-172]()\n\n## Data Flow Example\n\n**Complete Context Assembly Flow**\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant Generate as Generate()\n    participant PM as PromptManager\n    participant WI as World Info\n    participant Mem as Memory\n    participant Vec as Vectors\n    participant Ext as extension_prompts\n    participant API as LLM API\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eGenerate: Send message\n    Generate-\u003e\u003ePM: summary\n        API--\u003e\u003eMem: Summary text\n        Mem-\u003e\u003eExt: setExtensionPrompt('1_memory')\n    end\n    deactivate Mem\n    \n    PM-\u003e\u003eVec: Retrieve relevant vectors\n This system allows users to define entries that are automatically inserted into the AI prompt when specific conditions are met, enabling rich world-building without manually managing context.\n\nFor advanced World Info features such as timed effects, sticky entries, and automation triggers, see [World Info Advanced Features](#6.2). For semantic search capabilities, see [Vector Search and Embeddings](#6.3).\n\n---\n\n## System Overview\n\nWorld Info (also called \"lorebooks\") consists of entries that contain:\n- **Keys**: Primary and secondary keywords that trigger the entry\n- **Content**: Text to inject into the prompt when activated\n- **Configuration**: Settings controlling when and how the entry activates\n\nThe system scans recent chat messages and other context sources to determine which entries should be active, then allocates token budget to include as many relevant entries as possible within constraints.\n\n**World Info Activation Flow: getWorldInfoPrompt() Pipeline**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph GetWIPrompt[\"getWorldInfoPrompt(scanApi, maxContext, isDryRun)\"]\n needle, entry)\u003cbr/\u003eUses: world_info_logic enum\u003cbr/\u003eUses: entry.selectiveLogic\"]\n        CallMatch --\u003e CallScore[\"buffer.getScore(entry, scanState)\u003cbr/\u003eReturns: [public/scripts/world-info.js:1470-1613](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:337-367](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:429-474](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:1643-1727](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:480-838](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:1729-2044]()\n\n---\n\n## Core Architecture\n\n### WorldInfoBuffer Class\n\nThe `WorldInfoBuffer` class manages the scanning buffer for one evaluation cycle. It maintains three types of buffers:\n\n| Buffer Type | Purpose | Populated By |\n|------------|---------|--------------|\n| Depth Buffer | Recent chat messages ordered by depth | Constructor initialization |\n| Recurse Buffer | Content added by recursive scanning | Entry activation with `scan_depth` |\n| Inject Buffer | Valid prompt injections for current scan | Extension system |\n\nThe buffer provides methods to:\n- `get(entry, scanState)` - Returns the scan buffer respecting depth and context settings [world-info.js:279-328]()\n- `matchKeys(haystack, needle, entry)` - Performs key matching with regex/case-sensitivity support [world-info.js:337-367]()\n- `getScore(entry, scanState)` - Calculates activation score based on matched keys [world-info.js:429-474]()\n- `addRecurse(message)` - Adds content to recursion buffer [world-info.js:373-375]()\n- `addInject(message)` - Adds prompt injection to buffer [world-info.js:381-383]()\n- `getDepth()` - Returns current effective scan depth (base + skew) [world-info.js:401-404]()\n- `advanceScan()` - Increments skew to advance scan range [world-info.js:396-398]()\n\n**WorldInfoBuffer Class buildGlobalScanData()\n\nThe `buildGlobalScanData()` function at [public/scripts/world-info.js:1058-1132]() constructs a `WIGlobalScanData` object containing character-independent context. This data is scanned alongside chat messages when entries have matching flags enabled.\n\n```typescript\n// WIGlobalScanData typedef [world-info.js:104-113]\ninterface WIGlobalScanData {\n    trigger: string;                // From GENERATION_TYPE_TRIGGERS, e.g., 'normal', 'continue'\n    personaDescription: string;     // From power_user.persona_descriptions[user_avatar]\n    characterDescription: string;   // From characters[this_chid].description\n    characterPersonality: string;   // From characters[this_chid].personality\n    characterDepthPrompt: string;   // From characters[this_chid].data.extensions.depth_prompt.prompt\n    scenario: string;               // From characters[this_chid].scenario\n    creatorNotes: string;           // From characters[this_chid].data.creator_notes\n}\n```\n\n**Entry Matching Flags:**\n- `entry.matchPersonaDescription` - Include user persona in scan\n- `entry.matchCharacterDescription` - Include character description\n- `entry.matchCharacterPersonality` - Include character personality\n- `entry.matchCharacterDepthPrompt` - Include character notes\n- `entry.matchScenario` - Include character scenario\n- `entry.matchCreatorNotes` - Include creator notes\n\nThese flags are checked in `WorldInfoBuffer.get()` at [public/scripts/world-info.js:299-316]() to conditionally append global data to the scan buffer.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:1058-1132](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:104-113](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:186-194](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:279-328]()\n\n---\n\n## Entry Structure and Fields\n\nEach World Info entry is a JavaScript object with the following field definition structure:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"WI Entry Core Fields\"\n        UID[\"uid: number\u003cbr/\u003e(unique identifier)\"]\n        Keys[\"key: string[]\u003cbr/\u003e(primary keywords)\"]\n        KeySecondary[\"keysecondary: string[]\u003cbr/\u003e(secondary keywords)\"]\n        Comment[\"comment: string\u003cbr/\u003e(user note, max 100 chars)\"]\n        Content[\"content: string\u003cbr/\u003e(text to inject)\"]\n        \n        Enabled[\"enabled: boolean\"]\n        Position[\"position: number\u003cbr/\u003e(injection position)\"]\n        Depth[\"depth: number\u003cbr/\u003e(scan depth, default 4)\"]\n        Order[\"order: number\u003cbr/\u003e(display order)\"]\n        Weight[\"weight: number\u003cbr/\u003e(priority, default 100)\"]\n    end\n     WholeWords[\"matchWholeWords: boolean\"]\n        UseGroupScoring[\"useGroupScoring: boolean\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Advanced Fields\"\n        ScanDepth[\"scanDepth: number\u003cbr/\u003e(custom scan depth)\"]\n        Constant[\"constant: boolean\u003cbr/\u003e(always active)\"]\n        Excludes[\"excludeRecursion: boolean\"]\n        Probability[\"probability: number\u003cbr/\u003e(0-100, activation chance)\"]\n        Group[\"group: string\u003cbr/\u003e(grouping identifier)\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Entry Field Definitions:**\n\nThe entry schema is defined in `newEntryTemplate` object at [public/scripts/world-info.js:2683-2747]() and as typedef `WIScanEntry` at [public/scripts/world-info.js:116-137]().\n\n| Field | Type | Default | Description | Reference |\n|-------|------|---------|-------------|-----------|\n| `uid` | number | auto-incremented | Unique identifier within the book | [world-info.js:2685]() |\n| `key` | string[] | `[]` | Primary keywords that trigger activation | [world-info.js:2694]() |\n| `keysecondary` | string[] | `[]` | Secondary keywords for logic operations | [world-info.js:2696]() |\n| `content` | string | `''` | Text content to inject into prompt | [world-info.js:2686]() |\n| `comment` | string | `''` | User note (max `MAX_COMMENT_LENGTH=100`) | [world-info.js:2687]() |\n| `enabled` | boolean | `true` | Whether entry can activate | [world-info.js:2693]() |\n| `position` | number | `0` | Injection position (uses `wi_anchor_position`) | [world-info.js:2697]() |\n| `depth` | number | `DEFAULT_DEPTH=4` | Depth for position-based injection | [world-info.js:2688]() |\n| `order` | number | `0` | Display order (higher = top) | [world-info.js:2689]() |\n| `weight` | number | `DEFAULT_WEIGHT=100` | Budget active regardless of keys | [world-info.js:2692]() |\n| `excludeRecursion` | boolean | `false` | Exclude from recursive scans | [world-info.js:2706]() |\n| `scanDepth` | number | `null` | Custom scan whole word matching | [world-info.js:2701]() |\n| `useGroupScoring` | boolean | `false` | Enable group-based scoring | [world-info.js:2702]() |\n| `sticky` | number | `null` | Sticky duration (timed effect) | [world-info.js:2710]() |\n| `cooldown` | number | `null` | Cooldown duration (timed effect) | [world-info.js:2711]() |\n| `delay` | number | `null` | Delay before activation | [world-info.js:2712]() |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:2683-2747](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:116-137](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:96-99]()\n\n---\n\n## Scanning and Matching System\n\n### Scan States\n\nThe system operates in different scan states:\n\n```javascript\nexport const scan_state = {\n    NONE: 0,        // Scanning stopped\n    INITIAL: 1,     // First scan pass\n    RECURSION: 2,   // Triggered by recursion\n    MIN_ACTIVATIONS: 3  // Seeking minimum activations\n};\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:43-60]()\n\n### Key Matching Logic\n\nKeys can be plain text or regular expressions (format: `/pattern/flags`). The `matchKeys()` method in `WorldInfoBuffer` implements the following logic:\n\n1. **Regex Detection**: If `needle` matches `/regex/flags` format, use `parseRegexFromString(needle)` [world-info.js:2865-2909]() and test with `RegExp.test()`\n2. **Case Transformation**: Apply `#transformString(str, entry)` [world-info.js:268-271]() based on `entry.caseSensitive ?? world_info_case_sensitive`\n3. **Whole Word Matching**: If `entry.matchWholeWords ?? world_info_match_whole_words` is enabled:\n   - Multi-word phrases: Use simple `includes()` check\n   - Single words: Use regex pattern `(?:^|\\\\W)(escapedKeyword)(?:$|\\\\W)` with word boundaries\n\n**matchKeys() Flow**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Start[\"matchKeys(haystack, needle, entry)\u003cbr/\u003e[world-info.js:337-367]\"]\n    \n    Start --\u003e CheckRegex{\"parseRegexFromString(needle)\u003cbr/\u003e[world-info.js:2865-2909]\"}\n    CheckRegex --\u003e|\"keyRegex != null\"| TestRegex[\"keyRegex.test(haystack)\u003cbr/\u003eReturn boolean\"]\n    \n    CheckRegex --\u003e|\"keyRegex == null\"| Transform[\"haystack = #transformString(haystack, entry)\u003cbr/\u003eneedle = #transformString(needle, entry)\u003cbr/\u003e[world-info.js:268-271]\"]\n    Transform --\u003e CheckWhole{\"entry.matchWholeWords ??\u003cbr/\u003eworld_info_match_whole_words\u003cbr/\u003e[world-info.js:78]\"}\n    \n    CheckWhole --\u003e|true| CheckMulti{\"needle contains\u003cbr/\u003ewhitespace?\"}\n    CheckMulti --\u003e|\"Yes (multi-word)\"| SimpleIncludes[\"haystack.includes(needle)\"]\n    CheckMulti --\u003e|\"No (single word)\"| WordBoundary[\"new RegExp('(?:^|\\\\\\\\W)(' +\u003cbr/\u003eescapeRegex(needle) + ')(?:$|\\\\\\\\W)')\u003cbr/\u003e.test(haystack)\u003cbr/\u003e[utils.js:escapeRegex]\"]\n    \n    CheckWhole --\u003e|false| SimpleIncludes2[\"haystack.includes(needle)\"]\n    \n    SimpleIncludes --\u003e Return[\"Return boolean\"]\n    WordBoundary --\u003e Return\n    SimpleIncludes2 --\u003e Return\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:337-367](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:268-271](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:2865-2909](), [public/scripts/utils.js]()\n\n### Logic Operators\n\nSecondary keys use logic operators to refine activation:\n\n```javascript\nexport const world_info_logic = {\n    AND_ANY: 0,   // Primary keys OR any secondary key\n    NOT_ALL: 1,   // Primary keys AND NOT all secondary keys\n    NOT_ANY: 2,   // Primary keys AND NOT any secondary key\n    AND_ALL: 3    // Primary keys AND all secondary keys\n};\n```\n\n**Scoring Calculation:**\n- Each matched primary key adds 1 to score\n- Secondary keys affect final score based on logic type\n- `AND_ANY`: Add secondary score to primary score\n- `AND_ALL`: Only add secondary score if all secondary keys match\n- `NOT_*`: Negative logic reduces or blocks activation\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:33-38](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:429-474]()\n\n### Activation Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Prompt as Prompt Assembly\n    participant WI as getWorldInfoPrompt()\n    participant Buffer as WorldInfoBuffer\n    participant Scanner as Entry Scanner\n    participant Budget as Budget Allocator\n    participant Result as WIPromptResult\n    \n    Prompt-\u003e\u003eWI: Request WI prompt\n    WI-\u003e\u003eWI: Prepare messages (depth order)\n    WI-\u003e\u003eWI: Build globalScanData\n    WI-\u003e\u003eBuffer: new WorldInfoBuffer(messages, globalScanData)\n    \n    loop For each scan pass (INITIAL, RECURSION, MIN_ACTIVATIONS)\n        WI-\u003e\u003eScanner: Scan all entries\n        Scanner-\u003e\u003eBuffer: get(entry, scanState)\n        Buffer--\u003e\u003eScanner: Scan buffer string\n        \n        loop For each entry\n            Scanner-\u003e\u003eBuffer: getScore(entry, scanState)\n            Buffer-\u003e\u003eBuffer: matchKeys() for each key\n            Buffer--\u003e\u003eScanner: Score value\n        end\n        \n        Scanner--\u003e\u003eWI: Scored entries\n        WI-\u003e\u003eBudget: allocateBudget(scoredEntries)\n        Budget--\u003e\u003eWI: Active entries within budget\n        \n        alt Has recursion entries\n            WI-\u003e\u003eBuffer: addRecurse(entry.content)\n            WI-\u003e\u003eWI: Trigger RECURSION scan\n        end\n    end\n    \n    WI-\u003e\u003eWI: Build prompt strings by position\n    WI--\u003e\u003ePrompt: WIPromptResult\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:1134-1430](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:1470-1613]()\n\n---\n\n## Budget Management\n\nThe budget system controls how many entries can be included based on token limits.\n\n### Budget Configuration\n\n| Setting | Global Variable | Default | Description |\n|---------|----------------|---------|-------------|\n| Budget % | `world_info_budget` | 25 | Percentage of context to allocate |\n| Budget Cap | `world_info_budget_cap` | 0 | Absolute token limit (0 = unlimited) |\n| Min Activations | `world_info_min_activations` | 0 | Minimum entries to activate |\n| Min Act. Depth | `world_info_min_activations_depth_max` | 0 | Max depth to search for min activations |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:69-75]()\n\n### Budget Allocation Process\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Start[\"Scored Entries\"]\n    \n    Start --\u003e Sort[\"Sort by:\u003cbr/\u003e1. Weight (desc)\u003cbr/\u003e2. Order (desc)\u003cbr/\u003e3. UID (asc)\"]\n    \n    Sort --\u003e Loop[\"For each entry\"]\n    \n    Loop --\u003e GetTokens[\"getTokenCountAsync(content)\"]\n    GetTokens --\u003e CheckBudget{\"Remaining\u003cbr/\u003ebudget?\"}\n    \n    CheckBudget --\u003e|Yes| Include[\"Include entry\"]\n    CheckBudget --\u003e|No| Exclude[\"Exclude entry\"]\n    \n    Include --\u003e UpdateBudget[\"budget -= tokens\"]\n    UpdateBudget --\u003e CheckNext{\"More\u003cbr/\u003eentries?\"}\n    \n    Exclude --\u003e CheckNext\n    \n    CheckNext --\u003e|Yes| Loop\n    CheckNext --\u003e|No| CheckMin{\"Met min\u003cbr/\u003eactivations?\"}\n    \n    CheckMin --\u003e|No| IncreaseDepth[\"Increase scan depth\u003cbr/\u003e(buffer.advanceScan)\"]\n    IncreaseDepth --\u003e RescanCheck{\"Depth \u003cbr/\u003emax limit?\"}\n    RescanCheck --\u003e|No| Rescan[\"Rescan with MIN_ACTIVATIONS state\"]\n    RescanCheck --\u003e|Yes| Done[\"Return active entries\"]\n    \n    CheckMin --\u003e|Yes| Done\n    Rescan --\u003e Loop\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:1643-1727]()\n\n---\n\n## Insertion Strategies and Positions\n\n### Character vs. Global Strategy\n\n```javascript\nexport const world_info_insertion_strategy = {\n    evenly: 0,           // Distribute character and global entries evenly\n    character_first: 1,  // Prioritize character entries\n    global_first: 2      // Prioritize global entries\n};\n```\n\nThe strategy (`world_info_character_strategy`) determines the order when allocating budget between character-specific lorebooks and globally selected ones.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:27-31](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:80]()\n\n### Entry Positions\n\nEntries can be inserted at various positions in the prompt using the `wi_anchor_position` enum:\n\n| Position Value | Constant | Location | Result Property |\n|---------------|----------|----------|-----------------|\n| 0 | `before_char` | Before character definitions | `worldInfoBefore` |\n| 1 | `after_char` | After character definitions | `worldInfoAfter` |\n| 2-101 | `at_depth` | At specific depth (2-101) | `worldInfoDepth[depth]` |\n| 3 | N/A | In chat examples | `worldInfoExamples` |\n| 996 | `after_an` | After Author's Note | `anAfter` |\n| 997 | `before_an` | Before Author's Note | `anBefore` |\n| 998 | `an_depth` | At Author's Note depth | `anDepth` |\n| 999 | `char_depth_prompt` | In character depth prompt | `charDepthPrompt` |\n| 1000+ | Custom outlets | Named outlets | `outletEntries[name]` |\n\n**Prompt Assembly with WI Positions**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"buildPromptByPosition() Output\"\n        Start[\"Activated Entries\"]\n        \n        Start --\u003e Group[\"Group by entry.position\"]\n        \n        Before[\"position === 0\u003cbr/\u003eworldInfoBefore string\"]\n        After[\"position === 1\u003cbr/\u003eworldInfoAfter string\"]\n        Examples[\"position === 3\u003cbr/\u003eworldInfoExamples array\"]\n        Depth[\"position 2-101\u003cbr/\u003eworldInfoDepth array\"]\n        ANBefore[\"position === 997\u003cbr/\u003eanBefore array\"]\n        ANAfter[\"position === 996\u003cbr/\u003eanAfter array\"]\n        ANDepth[\"position === 998\u003cbr/\u003eanDepth array\"]\n        CharDepth[\"position === 999\u003cbr/\u003echarDepthPrompt array\"]\n        Outlets[\"position \u003e= 1000\u003cbr/\u003eoutletEntries map\"]\n        \n        Group --\u003e Before\n        Group --\u003e After\n        Group --\u003e Examples\n        Group --\u003e Depth\n        Group --\u003e ANBefore\n        Group --\u003e ANAfter\n        Group --\u003e ANDepth\n        Group --\u003e CharDepth\n        Group --\u003e Outlets\n        \n Result\n        ANAfter --\u003e Result\n        Result --\u003e Integration[\"Integrated by\u003cbr/\u003esetupChatCompletionPromptManager()\u003cbr/\u003eor getPromptManager()\"]\n    end\n```\n\nThe `position` field uses constants defined at [public/scripts/world-info.js:2683-2747]() and is processed by `buildPromptByPosition()` at [public/scripts/world-info.js:1729-2044]().\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:1729-2044](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:2683-2747]()\n\n---\n\n## Recursion System\n\nRecursive scanning allows activated entries to trigger additional entries by including their content in subsequent scan passes.\n\n### Recursion Configuration\n\n- **Enable Recursion**: `world_info_recursive` (global setting)\n- **Max Steps**: `world_info_max_recursion_steps` (0 = unlimited, limited to prevent infinite loops)\n- **Per-Entry Control**: `entry.excludeRecursion` (prevents entry from being scanned)\n\n### Recursion Flow\n\n**Configuration Variables:**\n- `world_info_recursive` [world-info.js:75]() - Enable/disable recursion globally\n- `world_info_max_recursion_steps` [world-info.js:82]() - Maximum recursion depth (0 = unlimited, limited to 1000 by scan buffer)\n- `entry.excludeRecursion` [world-info.js:2706]() - Per-entry exclusion flag\n- `entry.scanDepth` [world-info.js:2699]() - Custom scan depth for recursive entries\n\n**Recursion Process:**\n\n1. **Initial Scan** (`scan_state.INITIAL`): Activate entries based on chat messages\n2. **Recurse Buffer Population**: If `world_info_recursive === true`, call `buffer.addRecurse(entry.content)` for activated entries with `entry.scanDepth \u003e 0` and `entry.excludeRecursion === false`\n3. **Recursion Scan** (`scan_state.RECURSION`): Perform additional scan pass where `buffer.get(entry, scanState)` includes `#recurseBuffer` contents\n4. **Iteration**: Continue until no new activations or `world_info_max_recursion_steps` reached\n\n**Example:**\n```\nEntry A: key=[\"dragon\"], content=\"Dragons breathe fire breath\", scanDepth=4\nEntry B: key=[\"fire breath\"], content=\"Fire breath is hot\", scanDepth=4\n\nChat: \"I saw a dragon\"\n→ Entry A activates (matched \"dragon\")\n→ Entry A content added to recurseBuffer\n→ Recursion scan matches \"fire breath\" in recurseBuffer\n→ Entry B activates\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:75](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:82](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:2699](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:2706](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:373-375](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:1470-1613]()\n\n---\n\n## UI and Management\n\n### World Info Editor Structure\n\nThe editor is rendered in `#WorldInfo` div and
