data, fromTemplate)` | [world-info.js:2231-2317]() | Creates new entry in `world_info[name].entries` |\n| `deleteWorldInfoEntry(data, uid)` | [world-info.js:2431-2460]() | Removes entry by UID |\n| `saveWorldInfoEntry(name, data, immediately)` | [world-info.js:2157-2230]() | Persists entry changes to file |\n| `createNewWorldInfo(worldInfoName)` | [world-info.js:3045-3096]() | Creates new world info book |\n| `importWorldInfo(file)` | [world-info.js:2920-3032]() | Imports book from JSON file |\n| `deleteWorldInfo(worldInfoName)` | [world-info.js:3098-3163]() | Deletes entire world info book |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:62-63](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:89](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:92](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:948-1132](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:2157-2230](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:2231-2317](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:2431-2460](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:2920-3032](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:3045-3096](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:3098-3163](), [public/index.html]()\n\n### Entry Management Functions\n\n| Function | Purpose |\n|----------|---------|\n| `createWorldInfoEntry(name, data, fromTemplate)` | Creates new entry |\n| `deleteWorldInfoEntry(data, uid)` | Deletes entry |\n| `clearWorldInfo(worldInfoName)` | Removes all entries from book |\n| `duplicateWorldInfoEntry(data, uid)` | Clones entry |\n| `setWorldInfoSettings(settings, data)` | Updates book settings |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:2231-2317](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:2431-2460](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:2399-2429]()\n\n### Filter System\n\nThe World Info filter (`worldInfoFilter`) uses the `FilterHelper` class:\n\n```javascript\nexport const worldInfoFilter = new FilterHelper(() =\u003e updateEditor());\n```\n\nSupported filter types:\n- `FILTER_TYPES.WORLD_INFO_SEARCH`: Text search across entry fields\n- State filters for enabled/disabled/constant entries\n- Group-based filtering\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:92](), [public/scripts/filters.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Storage and Persistence\n\n### File Structure\n\nWorld Info books are stored as JSON files:\n\n```\ndata/\n  [user]/\n    worlds/\n      [book_name].json\n```\n\n**Book File Format:**\n```json\n{\n    \"name\": \"Book Name\",\n    \"entries\": {\n        \"0\": { \"uid\": 0, \"key\": [\"keyword\"], \"content\": \"...\", ... },\n        \"1\": { \"uid\": 1, ... }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Save Operations\n\n| Function | Debounce | Description |\n|----------|----------|-------------|\n| `saveWorldDebounced(name, data)` | 1000ms | Saves world info book |\n| `saveWorldInfo(name, data, immediately)` | Optional | Direct save with optional immediate flag |\n\nThe debounced save prevents excessive file writes during rapid editing.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:83](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:2157-2230]()\n\n### Character-Bound Lorebooks\n\nWorld Info books can be bound to characters via:\n- **Embedded**: Book data stored directly in character card JSON (`data.character_book`)\n- **Linked**: Reference to external book file stored in character data\n\n**Embedded Format Detection:**\n```javascript\nexport function checkEmbeddedWorld(data) {\n    // Check for CharacterBook format\n    if (data?.character_book?.entries) {\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for V1 embedded format\n    if (data?.entries?.[0]?.keys) {\n        return true;\n    }\n    return false;\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:2072-2118](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:2320-2397]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration with Chat System\n\n### Activation Trigger Points\n\nWorld Info scanning is triggered at multiple integration points in the chat generation pipeline:\n\n**World Info Trigger Point Mapping**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph MainTriggers[\"Primary Generation Triggers\"]\n        GenerateMain[\"Generate() [script.js]\u003cbr/\u003eMain generation entry point\"]\n        \n        GenerateMain --\u003e ChatCompPath[\"Chat Completion Path\"]\n        GenerateMain --\u003e TextGenPath[\"Text Generation Path\"]\n        GenerateMain --\u003e NovelPath[\"NovelAI Path\"]\n        GenerateMain --\u003e KoboldPath[\"KoboldAI Path\"]\n        \n        ChatCompPath --\u003e SetupChatComp[\"setupChatCompletionPromptManager(input, charId, groupId)\u003cbr/\u003e[openai.js]\u003cbr/\u003eReturns: PromptManager instance\"]\n        TextGenPath --\u003e GetTextGen[\"getTextGenGenerationData(generationType, quietPrompt, skipWIAN, quietImage)\u003cbr/\u003e[textgen-settings.js]\u003cbr/\u003eReturns: {prompt, ...}\"]\n        NovelPath --\u003e GetNovel[\"getNovelGenerationData(finalPrompt, model, isImpersonate, quiet, isContinue)\u003cbr/\u003e[nai-settings.js]\u003cbr/\u003eReturns: {input, ...}\"]\n        KoboldPath --\u003e GetKobold[\"getKoboldGenerationData(finalPrompt, api_server, api_type, isImpersonate, isContinue)\u003cbr/\u003e[kai-settings.js]\u003cbr/\u003eReturns: {prompt, ...}\"]\n        \n        SetupChatComp --\u003e CallGetWI[\"Calls: getWorldInfoPrompt(scanApi, maxContext, isDryRun)\"]\n        GetTextGen --\u003e CallGetWI\n        GetNovel --\u003e CallGetWI\n        GetKobold --\u003e CallGetWI\n    end\n    \n    subgraph WIPromptFunction[\"getWorldInfoPrompt() [world-info.js:1134-1430]\"]\n        CallGetWI --\u003e ExecuteWI[\"1. new WorldInfoBuffer(messages, globalScanData)\u003cbr/\u003e2. Scan entries with checkWorldInfoActivation()\u003cbr/\u003e3. Allocate budget\u003cbr/\u003e4. new WorldInfoTimedEffects()\u003cbr/\u003e5. buildPromptByPosition()\"]\n        \n        ExecuteWI --\u003e ReturnWIResult[\"Returns: WIPromptResult\u003cbr/\u003e{worldInfoString, worldInfoBefore, worldInfoAfter,\u003cbr/\u003eworldInfoDepth, worldInfoExamples, anBefore,\u003cbr/\u003eanAfter, outletEntries}\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph PromptIntegration[\"Prompt Assembly Integration\"]\n        ReturnWIResult --\u003e PMInject[\"PromptManager consumes WIPromptResult\u003cbr/\u003eInserts at: position-specific locations\"]\n        ReturnWIResult --\u003e StringConcat[\"Legacy APIs: String concatenation\u003cbr/\u003eInserts: worldInfoBefore + prompt + worldInfoAfter\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph AlternateTriggers[\"Other Trigger Points\"]\n        GetChatFunc[\"getChat() [script.js]\u003cbr/\u003eChat initialization, may pre-scan WI\"]\n        SlashWIScan[\"/wi-scan slash command [world-info.js:3768-3791]\u003cbr/\u003eManual scan trigger, dry-run mode\"]\n        ExtensionEvt[\"Extensions calling getWorldInfoPrompt() directly\u003cbr/\u003eVia: import from './scripts/world-info.js'\"]\n        \n        GetChatFunc -.-\u003e|\"May call\"| CallGetWI\n        SlashWIScan --\u003e CallGetWI\n        ExtensionEvt --\u003e CallGetWI\n    end\n```\n\n### Main Integration Function\n\nThe `getWorldInfoPrompt()` function is the primary entry point at [public/scripts/world-info.js:1134-1430]():\n\n```typescript\n/**\n * Gets the world info prompt for the current context\n * @param {WIScanEntry[]} scanApi - Entries for recursive scanning\n * @param {number} maxContext - Maximum context size in tokens\n * @param {boolean} isDryRun - Whether this is a dry run (no side effects)\n * @returns {Promise\u003cWIPromptResult\u003e}\n */\nasync function getWorldInfoPrompt(scanApi, maxContext, isDryRun = false)\n```\n\nThis function orchestrates:\n1. Building `WorldInfoBuffer` from chat messages via `new WorldInfoBuffer()` [world-info.js:240-243]()\n2. Building global scan data via `buildGlobalScanData()` [world-info.js:1058-1132]()\n3. Scanning all entries in `selected_world_info` [world-info.js:67]() and character lorebooks\n4. Checking activation via `checkWorldInfoActivation()` [world-info.js:1470-1613]()\n5. Managing timed effects via `WorldInfoTimedEffects` class [world-info.js:480-838]()\n6. Allocating token budget via internal allocation logic [world-info.js:1643-1727]()\n7. Building position-sorted output via `buildPromptByPosition()` [world-info.js:1729-2044]()\n\nThe result `WIPromptResult` [world-info.js:152-162]() is consumed by:\n- `setupChatCompletionPromptManager()` in [public/scripts/openai.js]() for chat completion APIs\n- `getTextGenGenerationData()` in [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js]() for text completion APIs\n- `getNovelGenerationData()` in [public/scripts/nai-settings.js]() for NovelAI\n- `getKoboldGenerationData()` in [public/scripts/kai-settings.js]() for KoboldAI\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:1134-1430](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:240-243](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:1058-1132](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:1470-1613](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:480-838](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:1729-2044](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:152-162](), [public/scripts/openai.js](), [public/scripts/textgen-settings.js](), [public/scripts/nai-settings.js](), [public/scripts/kai-settings.js]()\n\n### Result Structure\n\n```typescript\ninterface WIPromptResult {\n    worldInfoString: string;        // Complete concatenated WI\n    worldInfoBefore: string;        // Position 0 entries\n    worldInfoAfter: string;         // Position 1 entries\n    worldInfoExamples: Array;       // Position 3 entries\n    worldInfoDepth: Array;          // Position 2+ entries\n    anBefore: Array;                // Position 997 entries\n    anAfter: Array;                 // Position 996 entries\n    outletEntries: Record\u003cstring, string[]\u003e; // Custom outlet positions\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:152-162]()\n\n---\n\n## Slash Commands\n\nThe World Info system registers slash commands via `registerWorldInfoSlashCommands()` at [public/scripts/world-info.js:3571-3835](). Commands are registered using `SlashCommandParser.addCommandObject()`.\n\n**Basic World Info Commands:**\n\n| Command | Arguments | Returns | Implementation |\n|---------|-----------|---------|----------------|\n| `/world` | `name: string` | Selected book name | Adds to `selected_world_info[]` array, calls `saveSettingsDebounced()` [world-info.js:3579-3608]() |\n| `/world-off` | `name: string` | Deactivated book name | Removes from `selected_world_info[]`, calls `saveSettingsDebounced()` [world-info.js:3610-3639]() |\n| `/getchatbook` | - | Character's lorebook name | Returns `characters[this_chid].data?.extensions?.world` [world-info.js:3641-3655]() |\n| `/setchatbook` | `name: string` | Updated book name | Calls `charUpdatePrimaryWorld(worldName, saveImmediate=true)` [world-info.js:3657-3671]() |\n| `/wi-list-books` | - | JSON array of book names | Returns `world_names[]` global variable [world-info.js:3673-3688]() |\n| `/wi-list-entries` | `bookName?: string` | JSON array of entry objects | Iterates `selected_world_info[]` or specified book [world-info.js:3690-3739]() |\n| `/wi-scan` | - | WIPromptResult as pipe | Calls `getWorldInfoPrompt([], maxContext, isDryRun=true)` [world-info.js:3768-3791]() |\n\n**Character Lorebook Management:**\n\n| Function | Location | Purpose |\n|----------|----------|---------|\n| `charUpdatePrimaryWorld(worldName, saveImmediate)` | [world-info.js:2568-2624]() | Sets character's primary lorebook (`data.extensions.world`) |\n| `charSetAuxWorlds(bookNames, saveImmediate)` | [world-info.js:2568-2624]() | Sets character's auxiliary lorebooks (`data.extensions.worlds`) |\n| `setWorldInfoButtonClass(chid, forceValue, prevValue)` | [world-info.js:3362-3408]() | Updates UI button state for character's lorebook |\n\n**Advanced Commands** (for entry manipulation, see Advanced Features page):\n- `/wi-activate`, `/wi-deactivate` - External activation control\n- `/wi-create`, `/wi-update`, `/wi-delete` - Entry CRUD operations\n- Commands reference `WorldInfoBuffer.externalActivations` static map\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:3571-3835](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:3579-3608](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:3610-3639](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:3641-3655](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:3657-3671](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:3673-3688](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:3690-3739](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:3768-3791](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:2568-2624](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:3362-3408]()\n\n---\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n### Optimization Strategies\n\n1. **Debounced Saves**: 1000ms debounce prevents excessive disk writes\n2. **Token Caching**: Token counts cached per entry content hash\n3. **Early Exit**: Constant entries skip scanning entirely\n4. **Budget Limits**: Hard token cap prevents runaway activation\n5. **Scan Depth Limits**: `MAX_SCAN_DEPTH = 1000` prevents excessive iteration\n\n### Token Budget Formula\n\nThe budget calculation in `getWorldInfoPrompt()` implements the following logic:\n\n```javascript\n// Calculate budget from maxContext parameter\nconst budgetPercent = world_info_budget;  // Global setting [world-info.js:73]\nconst budgetCap = world_info_budget_cap;  // Global setting [world-info.js:81]\n\n// Available budget based on percentage\nconst availableBudget = Math.round(maxContext * (budgetPercent / 100));\n\n// Apply cap if configured (0 = unlimited)\nconst effectiveBudget = budgetCap \u003e 0 \n    ? Math.min(availableBudget, budgetCap)\n    : availableBudget;\n\n// Budget is consumed during allocation loop\n// Each entry: budget -= await getTokenCountAsync(entry.content)\n```\n\n**Budget Allocation Process:**\n\n1. **Sorting**: Entries sorted by `weight` (desc), `order` (desc), `uid` (asc) - see `sortFn` at [world-info.js:88]()\n2. **Token Counting**: Each entry's content tokenized via `getTokenCountAsync(content)` from [tokenizers.js]()\n3. **Budget Tracking**: Running budget decremented for each included entry\n4. **Cutoff**: Allocation stops when `budget \u003c entry_tokens` unless `world_info_min_activations` forces more scanning\n\n**Global Variables:**\n- `world_info_budget` [world-info.js:73]() - Default: 25 (percent)\n- `world_info_budget_cap` [world-info.js:81]() - Default: 0 (no cap)\n- `world_info_min_activations` [world-info.js:70]() - Minimum entries to activate\n- `world_info_min_activations_depth_max` [world-info.js:71]() - Max depth for min activations\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:73](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:81](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:70-71](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:88](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:1643-1727](), [public/scripts/tokenizers.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Event System Integration\n\nWorld Info integrates with the global `eventSource` system at [public/scripts/events.js]() to allow extensions to hook into activation lifecycle:\n\n**Event Emission Points**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Event Lifecycle\"\n        Start[\"getWorldInfoPrompt() start\"]\n        \n        Scan[\"Scan and activate entries\"]\n        \n        Emit[\"eventSource.emit(event_types.WORLD_INFO_ACTIVATED,\u003cbr/\u003e{entries: activatedEntries, scan: scanState})\"]\n        \n        Build[\"Build WIPromptResult\"]\n        \n        Return[\"Return to caller\"]\n        \n        Start --\u003e Scan\n        Scan --\u003e Emit\n        Emit --\u003e Build\n        Build --\u003e Return\n    end\n    \n    Extensions[\"Extensions subscribe:\u003cbr/\u003eeventSource.on(event_types.WORLD_INFO_ACTIVATED,\u003cbr/\u003ecallback)\"]\n    \n    Extensions -.-\u003e|\"Listen\"| Emit\n```\n\n**External Activation API**\n\nExtensions can force-activate entries via the static `WorldInfoBuffer.externalActivations` Map:\n\n```javascript\n// Static property defined at [world-info.js:202-203]\nWorldInfoBuffer.externalActivations = new Map();\n\n// Add external activation\nconst key = `${worldName}.${uid}`;\nWorldInfoBuffer.externalActivations.set(key, {\n    world: worldName,\n    uid: entryUid,\n    // Optional: entry field overrides (e.g., content, position)\n});\n\n// Check during scanning [world-info.js:412-414]\nconst externalEntry = buffer.getExternallyActivated(entry);\nif (externalEntry) {\n    // Entry force-activated, bypass normal key matching\n}\n\n// Reset after scan [world-info.js:419-421]\nbuffer.resetExternalEffects();\n```\n\n**Integration with Slash Commands:**\n\nThe `/wi-activate` and `/wi-deactivate` commands use this API:\n- `/wi-activate` adds to `externalActivations` Map\n- `/wi-deactivate` removes from `externalActivations` Map\n- Entries remain force-activated until explicitly deactivated or buffer reset\n\n**Event Emission:**\n\nAfter activation, `eventSource.emit(event_types.WORLD_INFO_ACTIVATED, { entries, scan })` is called, allowing extensions to react to WI changes.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/world-info.js:202-203](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:412-414](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:419-421](), [public/scripts/events.js](), [public/scripts/event_types.js]()\n\n---\n\n**Key Takeaways:**\n\n- The `WorldInfoBuffer` class manages all scanning and matching operations\n- Entries are activated based on keyword matches using configurable logic operators\n- Budget allocation prioritizes entries by weight and enforces token limits  \n- Multiple insertion positions allow fine control over prompt structure\n- Recursive scanning enables cascading activation of related entries\n- The system integrates tightly with the chat generation pipeline via `getWorldInfoPrompt()`\n\nFor details on advanced features like sticky entries, cooldowns, and automation, see [World Info Advanced Features](#6.2)."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"38:T6816,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Chat Summarization [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/templates/upload-expression.html](public/scripts/extensions/expressions/templates/upload-expression.html)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js](public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/memory/settings.html](public/scripts/extensions/memory/settings.html)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/memory/style.css](public/scripts/extensions/memory/style.css)\n- [src/endpoints/assets.js](src/endpoints/assets.js)\n- [src/endpoints/backends/kobold.js](src/endpoints/backends/kobold.js)\n- [src/endpoints/files.js](src/endpoints/files.js)\n- [src/endpoints/sprites.js](src/endpoints/sprites.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThe Chat Summarization Extension (also known as the Memory Extension) automatically generates and maintains summaries of chat conversations, allowing AI models to retain context about earlier parts of long conversations that would otherwise exceed the context window. This document covers the summarization system's architecture, trigger mechanisms, storage, and prompt injection strategies.\n\nFor information about the Vector Storage system (RAG), see [Vector Storage and RAG System](#6.3). For World Info context management, see [World Info System](#6.1).\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:1-1004]()\n\n---\n\n## System Overview\n\nThe Memory Extension monitors chat conversations and periodically generates condensed summaries of message history. These summaries are:\n- Stored as metadata on chat messages\n- Injected into the prompt at configurable positions\n- Incrementally updated based on message count or word count thresholds\n- Generated using one of three backends: Extras API (deprecated), Main API, or WebLLM\n\nThe extension operates automatically in the background, triggered by the `CHAT_CHANGED` and `MESSAGE_RECEIVED` events.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Events[\"Event System\"]\n        ChatChanged[\"CHAT_CHANGED event\"]\n        MessageReceived[\"MESSAGE_RECEIVED event\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Worker[\"moduleWorker\"]\n        EventHandler[\"onChatEvent()\"]\n        ConditionCheck[\"getSummaryPromptForNow()\"]\n        Summarize[\"summarizeChat()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Sources[\"Summarization Sources\"]\n        ExtrasAPI[\"summarizeChatExtras()\"]\n        MainAPI[\"summarizeChatMain()\"]\n        WebLLM[\"summarizeChatWebLLM()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Storage[\"Storage Layer\"]\n        ChatMessage[\"chat[i].extra.memory\"]\n        GetLatest[\"getLatestMemoryFromChat()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Injection[\"Prompt Injection\"]\n        SetContext[\"setMemoryContext()\"]\n        Format[\"formatMemoryValue()\"]\n        ExtensionPrompt[\"setExtensionPrompt()\"]\n    end\n    \n    ChatChanged --\u003e EventHandler\n    MessageReceived --\u003e EventHandler\n    EventHandler --\u003e ConditionCheck\n    ConditionCheck --\u003e|\"Conditions met\"| Summarize\n    \n    Summarize --\u003e ExtrasAPI\n    Summarize --\u003e MainAPI\n    Summarize --\u003e WebLLM\n    \n    ExtrasAPI --\u003e SetContext\n    MainAPI --\u003e SetContext\n    WebLLM --\u003e SetContext\n    \n    SetContext --\u003e ChatMessage\n    GetLatest --\u003e ChatMessage\n    SetContext --\u003e Format\n    Format --\u003e ExtensionPrompt\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:411-471](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:541-556](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:357-414]()\n\n---\n\n## Architecture and Data Flow\n\n### Module Registration\n\nThe extension registers as `MODULE_NAME = '1_memory'`, with the numeric prefix controlling load order. It integrates with the core event system and extension prompt mechanism.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph Init[\"Initialization\"]\n        LoadSettings[\"loadSettings()\"]\n        EventRegistration[\"eventSource.on()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph EventLoop[\"Event Loop\"]\n        Debounce[\"Debounced handler\"]\n        StateCheck[\"Check frozen/streaming\"]\n        HashCheck[\"Check lastMessageHash\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Generation[\"Generation Pipeline\"]\n        BuildPrompt[\"Build summarization prompt\"]\n        APICall[\"Call selected API\"]\n        ParseResponse[\"Parse and validate\"]\n        SaveToChat[\"Save to chat message\"]\n    end\n    \n    Init --\u003e EventLoop\n    EventLoop --\u003e Generation\n    \n    style Init fill:#f9f9f9\n    style EventLoop fill:#f0f0f0\n    style Generation fill:#e8e8e8\n```\n\nKey state variables:\n- `lastMessageHash` - Hash of last processed message (prevents duplicate processing)\n- `lastMessageId` - Index of last processed message\n- `inApiCall` - Flag to prevent concurrent summarization requests\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:36-60](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:141-168]()\n\n---\n\n## Summarization Sources\n\nThe extension supports three backend APIs for generating summaries:\n\n### Source Comparison\n\n| Source | Constant | Supported Operations | Token Counting | Context Size |\n|--------|----------|---------------------|----------------|--------------|\n| Extras API | `summary_sources.extras` | Auto-summarize only | GPT-2 tokenizer | 1024 - 64 tokens |\n| Main API | `summary_sources.main` | Auto + forced | Main tokenizer | `getMaxContextSize()` |\n| WebLLM | `summary_sources.webllm` | Auto + forced | WebLLM tokenizer | `getWebLlmContextSize()` |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:93-97](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:48-75]()\n\n### Extras API Flow (Deprecated)\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Event as onChatEvent\n    participant Sum as summarizeChatExtras\n    participant Build as getMemoryString\n    participant API as callExtrasSummarizeAPI\n    participant Store as setMemoryContext\n    \n    Event-\u003e\u003eSum: Trigger summarization\n    Sum-\u003e\u003eBuild: Build memory buffer\n    Note over Build: Reverses chat\u003cbr/\u003eCollects messages\u003cbr/\u003eUntil token limit\n    Build--\u003e\u003eSum: Concatenated string\n    Sum-\u003e\u003eAPI: POST /api/summarize\n    API--\u003e\u003eSum: Summary text\n    Sum-\u003e\u003eStore: Save summary\n    Store-\u003e\u003eStore: Inject via setExtensionPrompt\n```\n\nThe Extras API implementation:\n1. Reverses the chat history\n2. Concatenates messages with `\\n\\n` delimiter\n3. Includes existing summary as context\n4. Stops when token budget is reached\n5. Sends the entire string to `/api/summarize`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:827-891](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:893-933]()\n\n### Main API Flow\n\nThe Main API offers three prompt building strategies controlled by `extension_settings.memory.prompt_builder`:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    MainAPI[\"summarizeChatMain()\"]\n    \n    Builder{prompt_builder}\n    \n    DEFAULT[\"DEFAULT (0)\u003cbr/\u003egenerateQuietPrompt()\"]\n    RAW_BLOCKING[\"RAW_BLOCKING (1)\u003cbr/\u003egetRawSummaryPrompt()\u003cbr/\u003edeactivateSendButtons()\"]\n    RAW_NON_BLOCKING[\"RAW_NON_BLOCKING (2)\u003cbr/\u003egetRawSummaryPrompt()\u003cbr/\u003eNo blocking\"]\n    \n    MainAPI --\u003e Builder\n    Builder --\u003e|0| DEFAULT\n    Builder --\u003e|1| RAW_BLOCKING\n    Builder --\u003e|2| RAW_NON_BLOCKING\n    \n    DEFAULT --\u003e Response[\"removeReasoningFromString()\"]\n    RAW_BLOCKING --\u003e Response\n    RAW_NON_BLOCKING --\u003e Response\n    \n    Response --\u003e Save[\"setMemoryContext()\"]\n```\n\n**Default Builder (0):**\n- Uses `generateQuietPrompt()` with the summary prompt as system message\n- Leverages full prompt assembly pipeline (WI, examples, etc.)\n- Blocks during generation\n\n**Raw Blocking (1):**\n- Uses `getRawSummaryPrompt()` to build custom prompt from unsummarized messages\n- Calls `generateRaw()` with system prompt and raw messages\n- Deactivates send buttons during generation\n\n**Raw Non-Blocking (2):**\n- Same as Raw Blocking but doesn't lock UI\n- Allows user to continue chatting during summarization\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:681-753](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:99-103]()\n\n### Raw Prompt Construction\n\nThe `getRawSummaryPrompt()` function builds a prompt from messages not yet summarized:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Start[\"getRawSummaryPrompt()\"]\n    \n    GetLatest[\"Get latest summary index\"]\n    BuildBuffer[\"Build chat buffer\"]\n    \n    Loop[\"For each message after last summary\"]\n    TokenCheck{Tokens \u003e PROMPT_SIZE}\n    AddMessage[\"Add to buffer\"]\n    CheckLimit{maxMessagesPerRequest\u003cbr/\u003ereached?}\n    \n    Return[\"Return rawPrompt,\u003cbr/\u003elastUsedIndex\"]\n    \n    Start --\u003e GetLatest\n    GetLatest --\u003e BuildBuffer\n    BuildBuffer --\u003e Loop\n    Loop --\u003e TokenCheck\n    TokenCheck --\u003e|No| AddMessage\n    TokenCheck --\u003e|Yes| Return\n    AddMessage --\u003e CheckLimit\n    CheckLimit --\u003e|Yes| Return\n    CheckLimit --\u003e|No| Loop\n```\n\nThe buffer format is: `{name}:\\n{message}` for each message, joined with `\\n\\n`. The latest summary (if any) is prepended to provide continuity.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:755-825]()\n\n### WebLLM Flow\n\nWebLLM uses a messages array format similar to Main API but with WebLLM-specific token counting:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Event as onChatEvent\n    participant Check as getSummaryPromptForNow\n    participant Sum as summarizeChatWebLLM\n    participant Raw as getRawSummaryPrompt\n    participant Gen as generateWebLlmChatPrompt\n    participant Store as setMemoryContext\n    \n    Event-\u003e\u003eCheck: Check conditions\n    Check--\u003e\u003eSum: Prompt text\n    Sum-\u003e\u003eRaw: Build raw prompt\n    Raw--\u003e\u003eSum: rawPrompt, lastUsedIndex\n    Sum-\u003e\u003eGen: [system, user] messages\n    Note over Gen: max_tokens override\u003cbr/\u003eif configured\n    Gen--\u003e\u003eSum: Summary response\n    Sum-\u003e\u003eStore: Save with index\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:628-679]()\n\n---\n\n## Update Triggers and Conditions\n\n### Trigger Logic\n\nSummarization is triggered by `onChatEvent()` when:\n1. A new message is added to chat\n2. `extension_settings.memory.memoryFrozen` is false\n3. Not currently streaming (`streamingProcessor.isFinished`)\n4. Not already in an API call\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Event[\"onChatEvent()\"]\n    \n    ModuleCheck{Module enabled?}\n    StreamCheck{Streaming?}\n    FrozenCheck{Memory frozen?}\n    InCallCheck{inApiCall?}\n    \n    HashCheck{Message hash\u003cbr/\u003echanged?}\n    DeleteCheck{Messages\u003cbr/\u003edeleted?}\n    \n    Summarize[\"summarizeChat()\"]\n    Restore[\"Restore latest memory\"]\n    Skip[\"Skip\"]\n    \n    Event --\u003e ModuleCheck\n    ModuleCheck --\u003e|No| Skip\n    ModuleCheck --\u003e|Yes| StreamCheck\n    StreamCheck --\u003e|Yes| Skip\n    StreamCheck --\u003e|No| FrozenCheck\n    FrozenCheck --\u003e|Yes| Skip\n    FrozenCheck --\u003e|No| InCallCheck\n    InCallCheck --\u003e|Yes| Skip\n    InCallCheck --\u003e|No| HashCheck\n    \n    HashCheck --\u003e|Same| Skip\n    HashCheck --\u003e|Changed| DeleteCheck\n    DeleteCheck --\u003e|Yes| Restore\n    DeleteCheck --\u003e|No| Summarize\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:417-471]()\n\n### Condition Evaluation\n\n`getSummaryPromptForNow()` determines if summarization should proceed based on:\n\n| Condition | Setting | Behavior |\n|-----------|---------|----------|\n| Interval disabled | `promptInterval === 0` | Skip unless forced |\n| Too few messages | `chat.length \u003c promptInterval` | Skip unless forced |\n| Message threshold | `messagesSinceLastSummary \u003e= promptInterval` | Trigger |\n| Word threshold | `wordsSinceLastSummary \u003e= promptForceWords` | Trigger |\n\nThe function scans backwards from the latest message, counting messages and words until it finds an existing summary.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:559-626]()\n\n### Auto-Adjustment\n\nTwo helper functions provide automatic configuration:\n\n**`onPromptIntervalAutoClick()`:**\n- Calculates average message token count\n- Computes how many messages fit in context after summary\n- Sets `promptInterval` to trigger before context overflow\n\n**`onPromptForceWordsAutoClick()`:**\n- Calculates average message word count\n- Determines target summary size to maintain context utilization\n- Sets `promptForceWords` to maintain balance\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:208-244](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:170-206]()\n\n---\n\n## Storage and Persistence\n\n### Message-Level Storage\n\nSummaries are stored in the `extra.memory` property of chat messages:\n\n```javascript\nchat[index].extra = {\n    memory: \"Summary text here...\",\n    // other extra data\n}\n```\n\nThis design allows:\n- Multiple summaries throughout a conversation\n- Incremental summarization (each summary builds on previous)\n- Restoration to earlier states by deleting summaries\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:357-371](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:373-392]()\n\n### Retrieval Functions\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph Retrieval[\"Summary Retrieval\"]\n        GetLatest[\"getLatestMemoryFromChat()\"]\n        GetIndex[\"getIndexOfLatestChatSummary()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Scan[\"Backward Scan\"]\n        Reverse[\"Reverse chat array\"]\n        SkipLast[\"Skip last message\"]\n        FindMem[\"Find first with extra.memory\"]\n    end\n    \n    GetLatest --\u003e Scan\n    GetIndex --\u003e Scan\n    Scan --\u003e|Found| Return[\"Return summary/index\"]\n    Scan --\u003e|Not found| Empty[\"Return ''/−1\"]\n```\n\nBoth functions skip the last message (the one being generated) and scan backwards until finding a message with `extra.memory` set.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:352-392]()\n\n### Context Change Detection\n\n`isContextChanged()` validates that the chat context hasn't changed during a long-running summarization:\n\n```javascript\nfunction isContextChanged(context) {\n    const newContext = getContext();\n    if (newContext.groupId !== context.groupId\n        || newContext.chatId !== context.chatId\n        || (!newContext.groupId \u0026\u0026 (newContext.characterId !== context.characterId))) {\n        return true;\n    }\n    return false;\n}\n```\n\nIf context changed, the summary is discarded.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:394-409]()\n\n---\n\n## Injection System\n\n### Formatting and Templates\n\nThe `formatMemoryValue()` function transforms the raw summary text using the configured template:\n\n```javascript\nfunction formatMemoryValue(value) {\n    if (!value) return '';\n    value = value.trim();\n    \n    if (extension_settings.memory.template) {\n        return substituteParamsExtended(extension_settings.memory.template, { summary: value });\n    } else {\n        return `Summary: ${value}`;\n    }\n}\n```\n\nDefault template: `[Summary: {{summary}}]`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:77-89]()\n\n### Injection via Extension Prompts\n\nThe `setMemoryContext()` function injects the summary into the prompt system:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    SetMem[\"setMemoryContext(summary, save, index)\"]\n    \n    Save{save === true}\n    Store[\"Store in chat[index].extra.memory\"]\n    Skip1[\"Skip save\"]\n    \n    Format[\"formatMemoryValue(summary)\"]\n    \n    Inject[\"setExtensionPrompt(MODULE_NAME, ...)\"]\n    \n    SetMem --\u003e Save\n    Save --\u003e|Yes| Store\n    Save --\u003e|No| Skip1\n    Store --\u003e Format\n    Skip1 --\u003e Format\n    \n    Format --\u003e Inject\n    \n    Note1[\"Position: extension_settings.memory.position\"]\n    Note2[\"Role: extension_settings.memory.role\"]\n    Note3[\"Depth: extension_settings.memory.depth\"]\n    Note4[\"Scan: extension_settings.memory.scan\"]\n    \n    Inject -.-\u003e Note1\n    Inject -.-\u003e Note2\n    Inject -.-\u003e Note3\n    Inject -.-\u003e Note4\n```\n\nThe `setExtensionPrompt()` call uses:\n- `position`: Where to inject (see Position Modes below)\n- `role`: Message role (system/user/assistant)\n- `depth`: How many messages from end (for in-chat injection)\n- `scan`: Whether to include in World Info scanning\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:935-971]()\n\n### Position Modes\n\n| Value | Constant | Behavior |\n|-------|----------|----------|\n| -1 | None | Not injected (accessible via `{{summary}}` macro) |\n| 0 | `IN_PROMPT` | After Main Prompt / Story String |\n| 1 | `IN_CHAT` | In-chat at specified depth |\n| 2 | `BEFORE_PROMPT` | Before Main Prompt / Story String |\n\nFor `IN_CHAT` mode, depth is measured from the end of the chat. Depth 2 means \"2 messages before the current end.\"\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:317-322](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/settings.html:114-143]()\n\n### Role Assignment\n\nWhen injecting with `IN_CHAT` position, the role determines how the summary appears in the message array:\n\n- **System (0):** Injected as system message\n- **User (1):** Injected as user message  \n- **Assistant (2):** Injected as assistant message\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:310-315]()\n\n---\n\n## Settings and Configuration\n\n### Settings Schema\n\nThe default settings object defines all configurable parameters:\n\n```javascript\nconst defaultSettings = {\n    memoryFrozen: false,           // Pause auto-updates\n    SkipWIAN: false,               // Omit WI/AN from summary (Main API only)\n    source: summary_sources.extras, // Which API to use\n    prompt: defaultPrompt,         // Summarization system prompt\n    template: defaultTemplate,     // Injection template\n    position: IN_PROMPT,           // Where to inject\n    role: SYSTEM,                  // Message role\n    scan: false,                   // Include in WI scan\n    depth: 2,                      // Depth for in-chat injection\n    promptWords: 200,              // Target word count\n    promptInterval: 10,            // Update every N messages\n    promptForceWords: 0,           // Update every N words (0=disabled)\n    overrideResponseLength: 0,     // Max tokens (0=default)\n    maxMessagesPerRequest: 0,      // Limit messages per summary (0=unlimited)\n    prompt_builder: DEFAULT,       // Which prompt builder\n};\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:108-139]()\n\n### Settings UI Bindings\n\nThe `loadSettings()` function binds UI elements to settings:\n\n```javascript\n$('#summary_source').val(extension_settings.memory.source).trigger('change');\n$('#memory_frozen').prop('checked', extension_settings.memory.memoryFrozen).trigger('input');\n$('#memory_prompt').val(extension_settings.memory.prompt).trigger('input');\n// ... etc\n```\n\nEach input triggers a corresponding handler that updates `extension_settings.memory` and calls `saveSettingsDebounced()`.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:141-168](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:260-350]()\n\n### Dynamic UI Control\n\n`switchSourceControls()` shows/hides settings based on selected source:\n\n```javascript\nfunction switchSourceControls(value) {\n    $('#summaryExtensionDrawerContents [data-summary-source], #memory_settings [data-summary-source]')\n        .each((_, element) =\u003e {\n            const source = element.dataset.summarySource.split(',').map(s =\u003e s.trim());\n            $(element).toggle(source.includes(value));\n        });\n}\n```\n\nElements with `data-summary-source=\"main,webllm\"` are only visible when those sources are selected.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:253-258]()\n\n---\n\n## Slash Commands and Macros\n\n### `/summarize` Command\n\nThe `/summarize` command supports both chat summarization and arbitrary text summarization:\n\n```\n/summarize          - Summarize current chat\n/summarize quiet=true - Summarize without toast\n/summarize source=main prompt=\"...\" [text] - Summarize arbitrary text\n```\n\nParameters:\n- `quiet`: Suppress UI notifications\n- `source`: Override API source (extras/main/webllm)\n- `prompt`: Override system prompt\n\n**Implementation:**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Cmd[\"/summarize command\"]\n    \n    Check{Text provided?}\n    \n    ChatSum[\"forceSummarizeChat(quiet)\"]\n    TextSum[\"Summarize arbitrary text\"]\n    \n    SourceSwitch{Source?}\n    \n    Extras[\"callExtrasSummarizeAPI()\"]\n    Main[\"generateRaw()\"]\n    WebLLM[\"generateWebLlmChatPrompt()\"]\n    \n    Cmd --\u003e Check\n    Check --\u003e|No| ChatSum\n    Check --\u003e|Yes| TextSum\n    \n    TextSum --\u003e SourceSwitch\n    SourceSwitch --\u003e Extras\n    SourceSwitch --\u003e Main\n    SourceSwitch --\u003e WebLLM\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:502-539]()\n\n### `/summary-restore` Command\n\nRestores a previous summary by deleting the latest one:\n\n```javascript\nSlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(SlashCommand.fromProps({\n    name: 'summary-restore',\n    callback: onRestoreSummaryClick,\n    helpString: 'Restore a previous summary; use repeatedly to clear summarization state.',\n}));\n```\n\nThis allows users to \"undo\" a bad summary or reset the summarization state.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:973-1004]()\n\n### `{{summary}}` Macro\n\nThe extension registers a `{{summary}}` macro that resolves to the current summary text:\n\n```javascript\nmacros.registerMacro(MacroCategory.METAPROMPT, 'summary', () =\u003e {\n    const context = getContext();\n    const chat = context.chat;\n    const latestMemory = getLatestMemoryFromChat(chat);\n    return latestMemory;\n});\n```\n\nThis allows summaries to be referenced in prompts even when injection position is set to \"None.\"\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:973-1004]()\n\n---\n\n## API Integration Details\n\n### Extras API Endpoint\n\nThe Extras API `/api/summarize` endpoint expects:\n- `text`: String to summarize\n- Optional `params` for generation settings\n\nResponse: `{ summary: \"...\" }`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:893-933]()\n\n### Main API Integration\n\nUses two core generation functions:\n\n**`generateQuietPrompt(params)`:**\n- Adds summary request as system message to existing prompt\n- Includes full context (WI, examples, etc.) unless `skipWIAN=true`\n- Blocks until complete\n\n**`generateRaw(params)`:**\n- Bypasses normal prompt assembly\n- Direct system prompt + user content\n- Used for raw prompt builders\n\nBoth support `responseLength` parameter for token limit override.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:681-753]()\n\n### WebLLM Integration\n\nWebLLM uses the `generateWebLlmChatPrompt()` function from shared utilities:\n\n```javascript\nconst messages = [\n    { role: 'system', content: prompt },\n    { role: 'user', content: rawPrompt }\n].filter(m =\u003e m.content);\n\nconst params = {};\nif (extension_settings.memory.overrideResponseLength \u003e 0) {\n    params.max_tokens = extension_settings.memory.overrideResponseLength;\n}\n\nconst summary = await generateWebLlmChatPrompt(messages, params);\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:628-679]()\n\n---\n\n## Settings HTML Structure\n\nThe settings UI is organized into collapsible sections:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Drawer[\"inline-drawer\"]\n    \n    Header[\"Header: Summarize + Popout button\"]\n    Content[\"inline-drawer-content\"]\n    \n    Source[\"Source selector\"]\n    MemoryContents[\"Memory textarea + controls\"]\n    SettingsBlock[\"Summary Settings (hidden by default)\"]\n    \n    Drawer --\u003e Header\n    Drawer --\u003e Content\n    Content --\u003e Source\n    Content --\u003e MemoryContents\n    Content --\u003e SettingsBlock\n    \n    SettingsBlock --\u003e PromptBuilder[\"Prompt builder options\"]\n    SettingsBlock --\u003e PromptConfig[\"Prompt + word count\"]\n    SettingsBlock --\u003e Frequency[\"Update frequency sliders\"]\n    SettingsBlock --\u003e Template[\"Injection template\"]\n    SettingsBlock --\u003e Position[\"Injection position\"]\n```\n\nKey controls:\n- **Pause checkbox:** Sets `memoryFrozen`\n- **No WI/AN checkbox:** Sets `SkipWIAN` (Main API only)\n- **Summarize now button:** Calls `forceSummarizeChat()` (Main/WebLLM only)\n- **Restore Previous button:** Deletes latest summary\n- **Settings toggle:** Shows/hides advanced settings\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/settings.html:1-149]()\n\n---\n\n## Token Counting Strategy\n\nThe extension uses different tokenizers based on source:\n\n```javascript\nasync function countSourceTokens(text, padding = 0) {\n    if (extension_settings.memory.source === summary_sources.webllm) {\n        const count = await countWebLlmTokens(text);\n        return count + padding;\n    }\n    \n    if (extension_settings.memory.source === summary_sources.extras) {\n        const count = getTextTokens(tokenizers.GPT2, text).length;\n        return count + padding;\n    }\n    \n    return await getTokenCountAsync(text, padding);\n}\n```\n\nContext size calculation:\n\n```javascript\nasync function getSourceContextSize() {\n    const overrideLength = extension_settings.memory.overrideResponseLength;\n    \n    if (extension_settings.memory.source === summary_sources.webllm) {\n        const maxContext = await getWebLlmContextSize();\n        return overrideLength \u003e 0 ? (maxContext - overrideLength) : Math.round(maxContext * 0.75);\n    }\n    \n    if (extension_settings.source === summary_sources.extras) {\n        return 1024 - 64;\n    }\n    \n    return getMaxContextSize(overrideLength);\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:42-75]()\n\n---\n\n## Example Workflow\n\n### Typical Auto-Summarization Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant Chat as Chat UI\n    participant Event as eventSource\n    participant Mem as Memory Extension\n    participant API as Main API\n    participant Prompt as Prompt System\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eChat: Send message 10\n    Chat-\u003e\u003eEvent: MESSAGE_RECEIVED\n    Event-\u003e\u003eMem: onChatEvent()\n    \n    Mem-\u003e\u003eMem: Check lastMessageHash\n    Mem-\u003e\u003eMem: getSummaryPromptForNow()\n    Note over Mem: messagesSinceLastSummary=10\u003cbr/\u003epromptInterval=10\u003cbr/\u003e✓ Trigger\n    \n    Mem-\u003e\u003eMem: getRawSummaryPrompt()\n    Note over Mem: Collect messages 1-9\u003cbr/\u003e(message 10 excluded)\n    \n    Mem-\u003e\u003eAPI: generateRaw({\u003cbr/\u003e  prompt: \"Summarize...\",\u003cbr/\u003e  systemPrompt: \"...\",\u003cbr/\u003e  responseLength: 0\u003cbr/\u003e})\n    \n    API--\u003e\u003eMem: \"Summary of conversation...\"\n    \n    Mem-\u003e\u003eMem: setMemoryContext(summary, true, 9)\n    Note over Mem: Save to chat[9].extra.memory\n    \n    Mem-\u003e\u003ePrompt: setExtensionPrompt(\u003cbr/\u003e  '1_memory',\u003cbr/\u003e  formatted,\u003cbr/\u003e  position,\u003cbr/\u003e  depth,\u003cbr/\u003e  scan,\u003cbr/\u003e  role\u003cbr/\u003e)\n    \n    Note over Prompt: Summary now available\u003cbr/\u003efor next generation\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:417-471](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:681-753](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:935-971]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"39:T772b,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Vector Storage and RAG System\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js](public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/settings.html](public/scripts/extensions/vectors/settings.html)\n- [src/endpoints/vectors.js](src/endpoints/vectors.js)\n- [src/vectors/cohere-vectors.js](src/vectors/cohere-vectors.js)\n- [src/vectors/extras-vectors.js](src/vectors/extras-vectors.js)\n- [src/vectors/nomicai-vectors.js](src/vectors/nomicai-vectors.js)\n- [src/vectors/openai-vectors.js](src/vectors/openai-vectors.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document covers SillyTavern's vector storage and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) system, which enables semantic search and context retrieval through text embeddings. The system vectorizes chat messages, file attachments, and World Info entries, storing them in local vector databases for similarity-based retrieval during chat generation.\n\nFor information about World Info keyword-based activation, see [6.1](#6.1). For chat summarization features, see [6.2](#6.2).\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThe Vector Storage system provides three primary capabilities:\n\n1. **Chat Message RAG**: Retrieves semantically relevant past messages and injects them into prompts\n2. **File Vectorization**: Chunks and indexes large text files for semantic search\n3. **Vector-Based World Info**: Activates World Info entries based on semantic similarity rather than keywords\n\nThe system supports 20+ embedding providers and uses the `vectra` library for local vector storage with cosine similarity search.\n\n## System Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph ClientExtension[\"Extension: public/scripts/extensions/vectors/\"]\n        IndexJS[\"index.js\u003cbr/\u003eMain extension logic\"]\n        Settings[\"settings.html\u003cbr/\u003eConfiguration UI\"]\n        WebLLM[\"webllm.js\u003cbr/\u003eLocal embedding provider\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ServerEndpoints[\"Server: src/endpoints/vectors.js\"]\n        QueryRoute[\"/api/vector/query\"]\n        InsertRoute[\"/api/vector/insert\"]\n        ListRoute[\"/api/vector/list\"]\n        DeleteRoute[\"/api/vector/delete\"]\n        PurgeRoute[\"/api/vector/purge\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph VectorProviders[\"Embedding Providers: src/vectors/\"]\n [src/endpoints/vectors.js:1-579]()\n\n## Core Components\n\n### Extension Module\n\nThe vectors extension is registered as a client-side module that hooks into the chat generation lifecycle.\n\n| Component | File | Purpose |\n|-----------|------|---------|\n| Main Extension | `public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js` | Orchestrates vectorization and RAG retrieval |\n| Settings Object | [index.js:57-115]() | Configuration for all vector features |\n| Module Worker | [index.js:117]() | Debounced chat synchronization |\n| WebLLM Provider | `public/scripts/extensions/vectors/webllm.js` | Browser-based embeddings |\n\n### Server API\n\nThe server provides RESTful endpoints for vector operations:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph Client[\"Client Operations\"]\n        Insert[\"insertVectorItems()\"]\n        Query[\"queryCollection()\"]\n        List[\"getSavedHashes()\"]\n        Delete[\"deleteVectorItems()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Server[\"Server Routes\"]\n        PostInsert[\"/api/vector/insert\"]\n        PostQuery[\"/api/vector/query\"]\n        PostList[\"/api/vector/list\"]\n        PostDelete[\"/api/vector/delete\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph VectraOps[\"Vectra Operations\"]\n        StoreInsert[\"store.upsertItem()\"]\n        StoreQuery[\"store.queryItems()\"]\n        StoreList[\"store.listItems()\"]\n        StoreDelete[\"store.deleteItem()\"]\n    end\n    \n    Insert --\u003e|POST| PostInsert\n    Query --\u003e|POST| PostQuery\n    List --\u003e|POST| PostList\n    Delete --\u003e|POST| PostDelete\n    \n    PostInsert --\u003e StoreInsert\n    PostQuery --\u003e StoreQuery\n    PostList --\u003e StoreList\n    PostDelete --\u003e StoreDelete\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/vectors.js:443-579](), [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:873-985]()\n\n## Embedding Providers\n\nThe system abstracts 20+ embedding providers through a unified interface:\n\n### Provider Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Dispatcher[\"Provider Dispatcher\"]\n        GetVector[\"getVector(source, settings, text)\"]\n        GetBatch[\"getBatchVector(source, settings, texts[])\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph CloudProviders[\"Cloud API Providers\"]\n        OpenAI[\"OpenAI\u003cbr/\u003etext-embedding-3-*\"]\n        Cohere[\"Cohere\u003cbr/\u003eembed-v4.0\"]\n        Together[\"TogetherAI\u003cbr/\u003em2-bert-*\"]\n        Mistral[\"MistralAI\u003cbr/\u003emistral-embed\"]\n        Google[\"Google AI\u003cbr/\u003etext-embedding-005\"]\n        Nomic[\"NomicAI\u003cbr/\u003enomic-embed-text\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph LocalProviders[\"Local Providers\"]\n        Transformers[\"Transformers\u003cbr/\u003epipeline('feature-extraction')\"]\n        LlamaCpp[\"llama.cpp\u003cbr/\u003e/embedding endpoint\"]\n        Ollama[\"Ollama\u003cbr/\u003e/api/embeddings\"]\n        KoboldCpp[\"KoboldCpp\u003cbr/\u003e/api/extra/embeddings\"]\n        WebLLM[\"WebLLM\u003cbr/\u003eBrowser-based\"]\n    end\n    \n    GetVector --\u003e CloudProviders\n    GetVector --\u003e LocalProviders\n    GetBatch --\u003e CloudProviders\n    GetBatch --\u003e LocalProviders\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/vectors.js:52-165](), [src/endpoints/vectors.js:22-41]()\n\n### Provider Implementation\n\nEach provider implements two functions:\n\n1. **Single Text**: `getProviderVector(text, ...params) -\u003e number[]`\n2. **Batch**: `getProviderBatchVector(texts[], ...params) -\u003e number[][]`\n\nExample provider structure from [src/vectors/openai-vectors.js:67-122]():\n\n```javascript\n// Batch vectorization with retry logic and batching\nasync function getOpenAIBatchVector(texts, source, directories, model) {\n    const config = SOURCES[source];\n    const key = readSecret(directories, config.secretKey);\n    \n    const response = await fetch(`${url}/embeddings`, {\n        method: 'POST',\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n            input: texts,\n            model: modelName,\n        }),\n    });\n    \n    const vectors = data.data.map(x =\u003e x.embedding);\n    return vectors;\n}\n```\n\n### Supported Providers\n\n| Provider | Source Key | Model Configuration | Batching |\n|----------|-----------|---------------------|----------|\n| OpenAI | `openai` | `text-embedding-3-small/large` | Yes |\n| Cohere | `cohere` | `embed-v4.0` | Yes |\n| TogetherAI | `togetherai` | `m2-bert-80M-32k-retrieval` | Yes |\n| MistralAI | `mistral` | `mistral-embed` | Yes |\n| Google AI | `palm` | `text-embedding-005` | Yes |\n| Vertex AI | `vertexai` | `text-embedding-005` | Yes |\n| NomicAI | `nomicai` | `nomic-embed-text-v1.5` | Yes |\n| Transformers | `transformers` | Local pipeline | Yes |\n| llama.cpp | `llamacpp` | Server model | Yes |\n| Ollama | `ollama` | User-specified tag | No (forced batch size 1) |\n| KoboldCpp | `koboldcpp` | Server model | No (client-side) |\n| WebLLM | `webllm` | Browser model | No (client-side) |\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/vectors.js:22-41](), [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:55]()\n\n## Vector Storage Architecture\n\n### Vectra Integration\n\nSillyTavern uses the `vectra` npm package for local vector storage. Each collection is stored as a separate index.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Collections[\"Collection Types\"]\n        ChatCol[\"Chat Collections\u003cbr/\u003ecollectionId = chatId\"]\n        FileCol[\"File Collections\u003cbr/\u003ecollectionId = 'file_' + hash(url)\"]\n        DBCol[\"Data Bank Collections\u003cbr/\u003ecollectionId = 'file_' + hash(url)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph IndexStructure[\"Index Structure\"]\n        GetIndex[\"getIndex(directories, collectionId,\u003cbr/\u003esource, sourceSettings)\"]\n        VectraIndex[\"vectra.LocalIndex\"]\n        IndexPath[\"DATA_ROOT/vectors/\u003cbr/\u003esource/collectionId/model/\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Operations[\"Vector Operations\"]\n        Upsert[\"upsertItem({vector, metadata})\"]\n        Query[\"queryItems(vector, topK)\"]\n        List[\"listItems()\"]\n        Delete[\"deleteItem(id)\"]\n    end\n    \n    Collections --\u003e GetIndex\n    GetIndex --\u003e VectraIndex\n    VectraIndex --\u003e|Persists to| IndexPath\n    VectraIndex --\u003e Operations\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/vectors.js:266-283](), [src/endpoints/vectors.js:4]()\n\n### Storage Schema\n\nVector items are stored with metadata:\n\n```javascript\n{\n  vector: number[],      // Embedding vector\n  metadata: {\n    hash: number,        // String hash for deduplication\n    text: string,        // Original text\n    index: number        // Order/position information\n  }\n}\n```\n\nPath structure: `DATA_ROOT/vectors/\u003csource\u003e/\u003ccollectionId\u003e/\u003cmodel\u003e/`\n\n- **source**: Embedding provider (e.g., `openai`, `cohere`)\n- **collectionId**: Unique identifier (chatId or file hash)\n- **model**: Model name for scope separation\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/vectors.js:273-283](), [src/endpoints/vectors.js:303]()\n\n## RAG Retrieval Flow\n\n### Query Process\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant UI as Chat UI\n    participant Ext as vectors/index.js\n    participant Server as /api/vector/query\n    participant Provider as Embedding Provider\n    participant Vectra as vectra.LocalIndex\n    \n    UI-\u003e\u003eExt: GENERATION_STARTED event\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: getQueryText(chat, 'chat')\n    Note over Ext: Extract last N messages\u003cbr/\u003eApply summarization if enabled\n    \n    Ext-\u003e\u003eServer: POST /api/vector/query\n    Note over Ext,Server: {collectionId, searchText,\u003cbr/\u003etopK, threshold, source}\n    \n    Server-\u003e\u003eProvider: getVector(searchText, isQuery=true)\n    Provider--\u003e\u003eServer: queryVector[]\n    \n    Server-\u003e\u003eVectra: queryItems(queryVector, topK)\n    Note over Vectra: Cosine similarity search\n    Vectra--\u003e\u003eServer: results with scores\n    \n    Server-\u003e\u003eServer: Filter by threshold\n    Server--\u003e\u003eExt: {hashes[], metadata[]}\n    \n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Match hashes to messages\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Sort by relevance\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Remove from chat array\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Format with template\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eUI: setExtensionPrompt(EXTENSION_PROMPT_TAG)\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:636-719](), [src/endpoints/vectors.js:358-366]()\n\n### Query Text Generation\n\nThe `getQueryText()` function prepares text for similarity search:\n\n1. Extract last N messages (configured by `settings.query`)\n2. Remove file attachments from message text\n3. Apply `substituteParams()` for variable replacement\n4. Optionally summarize with configured source\n5. Collapse newlines and join messages\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:761-780]()\n\n## Chat Message Vectorization\n\n### Synchronization Process\n\nChat messages are vectorized incrementally as the user chats. The `synchronizeChat()` function handles this:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Start[\"synchronizeChat(batchSize)\"] --\u003e Check{enabled_chats?}\n    Check --\u003e|No| ReturnNeg1[\"return -1\"]\n    Check --\u003e|Yes| Wait[\"waitUntilCondition()\u003cbr/\u003e!syncBlocked \u0026\u0026 !is_send_press\"]\n    \n    Wait --\u003e Lock[\"syncBlocked = true\"]\n    Lock --\u003e GetChat[\"Get chat messages\u003cbr/\u003eFilter system messages\"]\n    GetChat --\u003e Hash[\"Hash each message:\u003cbr/\u003egetStringHash(substituteParams(mes))\"]\n    \n    Hash --\u003e GetSaved[\"getSavedHashes(chatId)\"]\n    GetSaved --\u003e Compare[\"Find new messages:\u003cbr/\u003enewVectorItems = messages not in saved\"]\n    \n    Compare --\u003e CheckSum{summarize?}\n    CheckSum --\u003e|Yes| Summarize[\"summarize(newVectorItems,\u003cbr/\u003esummary_source)\"]\n    CheckSum --\u003e|No| Chunk\n    Summarize --\u003e Chunk[\"splitByChunks(items,\u003cbr/\u003emessage_chunk_size)\"]\n    \n    Chunk --\u003e Batch[\"Take first batchSize items\"]\n    Batch --\u003e Insert[\"insertVectorItems(chatId, batch)\"]\n    Insert --\u003e CheckDel{deletedHashes?}\n    \n    CheckDel --\u003e|Yes| Delete[\"deleteVectorItems(chatId, hashes)\"]\n    CheckDel --\u003e|No| Return\n    Delete --\u003e Return[\"return remaining count\"]\n    Return --\u003e Unlock[\"syncBlocked = false\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:328-404]()\n\n### Message Chunking\n\nMessages can be split into chunks for better granularity:\n\n- Configured by `settings.message_chunk_size` (0 to disable)\n- Uses `splitRecursive()` with delimiters: `['\\n\\n', '\\n', ' ', '']`\n- Custom delimiter can be prioritized via `settings.force_chunk_delimiter`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:210-226](), [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:195-203]()\n\n### Automatic Vectorization\n\nThe `ModuleWorkerWrapper` provides debounced automatic synchronization:\n\n```javascript\nconst moduleWorker = new ModuleWorkerWrapper(synchronizeChat);\nconst onChatEvent = debounce(async () =\u003e await moduleWorker.update(), \n                               debounce_timeout.relaxed);\n```\n\nEvents that trigger synchronization:\n- `CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED`\n- `USER_MESSAGE_RENDERED`\n- `CHAT_CHANGED`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:117](), [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:753]()\n\n### Hashing and Deduplication\n\nMessages are hashed to detect changes and avoid duplicate vectorization:\n\n```javascript\nfunction getStringHash(str) {\n    if (hashCache.has(str)) {\n        return hashCache.get(str);\n    }\n    const hash = calculateHash(str);  // From utils.js\n    hashCache.set(str, hash);\n    return hash;\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:416-429]()\n\n## File Vectorization\n\n### File Processing Pipeline\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Start[\"processFiles(chat)\"] --\u003e CheckEnabled{enabled_files?}\n    CheckEnabled --\u003e|No| End[\"return\"]\n    CheckEnabled --\u003e|Yes| DataBank[\"ingestDataBankAttachments()\"]\n    \n    DataBank --\u003e InjectDB[\"injectDataBankChunks(queryText,\u003cbr/\u003ecollectionIds)\"]\n    InjectDB --\u003e IterMsg[\"Iterate chat messages\"]\n    \n    IterMsg --\u003e CheckFiles{message has files?}\n    CheckFiles --\u003e|No| NextMsg[\"Next message\"]\n    CheckFiles --\u003e|Yes| ExtractText[\"Extract file text\u003cbr/\u003efrom message.mes\"]\n    \n    ExtractText --\u003e CheckSize{text.length \u003e\u003cbr/\u003esize_threshold?}\n    CheckSize --\u003e|No| NextMsg\n    CheckSize --\u003e|Yes| RemoveText[\"message.mes =\u003cbr/\u003emessage.mes.substring(fileLength)\"]\n    \n    RemoveText --\u003e GetQuery[\"getQueryText(chat, 'file')\"]\n    GetQuery --\u003e IterFiles[\"For each file in message.extra.files\"]\n    \n    IterFiles --\u003e GetHash[\"collectionId = getFileCollectionId(file.url)\"]\n    GetHash --\u003e CheckVec{Already vectorized?}\n    \n    CheckVec --\u003e|Yes| Retrieve[\"retrieveFileChunks(queryText,\u003cbr/\u003ecollectionId)\"]\n    CheckVec --\u003e|No| Vectorize[\"vectorizeFile(fileText, fileName,\u003cbr/\u003ecollectionId, chunk_size, overlap)\"]\n    \n    Vectorize --\u003e Retrieve\n    Retrieve --\u003e Prepend[\"Prepend chunks to message.mes\"]\n    Prepend --\u003e NextFile{More files?}\n    NextFile --\u003e|Yes| IterFiles\n    NextFile --\u003e|No| NextMsg\n    \n    NextMsg --\u003e MoreMsgs{More messages?}\n    MoreMsgs --\u003e|Yes| CheckFiles\n    MoreMsgs --\u003e|No| End\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:436-497]()\n\n### File Chunking Strategy\n\nThe `vectorizeFile()` function implements sophisticated text chunking:\n\n1. **Optional Translation**: Translate to English if `translate_files` enabled\n2. **Overlap Calculation**: `overlapSize = chunk_size * overlap_percent / 100`\n3. **Recursive Splitting**: Use `splitRecursive()` with delimiters\n4. **Overlap Application**: Call `overlapChunks()` to include context from adjacent chunks\n5. **Batch Processing**: Insert chunks in batches (size depends on provider)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:587-627]()\n\n### Chunk Overlap\n\nThe `overlapChunks()` function adds context from adjacent chunks:\n\n```javascript\nfunction overlapChunks(chunk, index, chunks, overlapSize) {\n    const halfOverlap = Math.floor(overlapSize / 2);\n    const nextChunk = chunks[index + 1];\n    const prevChunk = chunks[index - 1];\n    \n    const nextOverlap = trimToEndSentence(nextChunk?.substring(0, halfOverlap)) || '';\n    const prevOverlap = trimToStartSentence(prevChunk?.substring(prevChunk.length - halfOverlap)) || '';\n    const overlappedChunk = [prevOverlap, chunk, nextOverlap].filter(x =\u003e x).join(' ');\n    \n    return overlappedChunk;\n}\n```\n\nThis ensures chunks maintain context continuity and are trimmed to sentence boundaries.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:732-749]()\n\n### Collection Identification\n\nFiles use a deterministic collection ID based on URL hash:\n\n```javascript\nfunction getFileCollectionId(fileUrl) {\n    return `file_${getStringHash(fileUrl)}`;\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:127-129]()\n\n## Data Bank Integration\n\n### Data Bank Attachment Flow\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Start[\"ingestDataBankAttachments(source?)\"] --\u003e GetFiles[\"getDataBankAttachments(enabled=true)\"]\n    GetFiles --\u003e IterFiles[\"For each file in Data Bank\"]\n    \n    IterFiles --\u003e GetColID[\"collectionId = getFileCollectionId(file.url)\"]\n    GetColID --\u003e CheckHashes[\"getSavedHashes(collectionId)\"]\n    \n    CheckHashes --\u003e HasVectors{hashes.length \u003e 0?}\n    HasVectors --\u003e|Yes| AddToList[\"Add collectionId to list\"]\n    HasVectors --\u003e|No| Download[\"getFileAttachment(file.url)\"]\n    \n    Download --\u003e CheckThreshold{file.size \u003e threshold?}\n    CheckThreshold --\u003e|Yes| ChunkSize[\"chunkSize = chunk_size_db\"]\n    CheckThreshold --\u003e|No| NoChunk[\"chunkSize = -1\u003cbr/\u003e(no chunking)\"]\n    \n    ChunkSize --\u003e Vectorize[\"vectorizeFile(fileText, fileName,\u003cbr/\u003ecollectionId, chunkSize, overlap_db)\"]\n    NoChunk --\u003e Vectorize\n    \n    Vectorize --\u003e AddToList\n    AddToList --\u003e MoreFiles{More files?}\n    MoreFiles --\u003e|Yes| IterFiles\n    MoreFiles --\u003e|No| Return[\"return collectionIds[]\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:504-530]()\n\n### Data Bank Query and Injection\n\nRetrieved chunks from Data Bank files are injected using a separate extension prompt tag:\n\n```javascript\nasync function injectDataBankChunks(queryText, collectionIds) {\n    const queryResults = await queryMultipleCollections(\n        collectionIds, queryText, \n        settings.chunk_count_db, \n        settings.score_threshold\n    );\n    \n    // Aggregate results from all collections\n    let textResult = '';\n    for (const collectionId in queryResults) {\n        const metadata = queryResults[collectionId].metadata\n            ?.filter(x =\u003e x.text)\n            ?.sort((a, b) =\u003e a.index - b.index)\n            ?.map(x =\u003e x.text)\n            ?.filter(onlyUnique) || [];\n        textResult += metadata.join('\\n') + '\\n\\n';\n    }\n    \n    const insertedText = substituteParamsExtended(settings.file_template_db, \n                                                   { text: textResult });\n    setExtensionPrompt(EXTENSION_PROMPT_TAG_DB, insertedText, \n                       settings.file_position_db, settings.file_depth_db, \n                       settings.include_wi, settings.file_depth_role_db);\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:538-560]()\n\n### Multi-Collection Query\n\nThe system supports querying multiple collections simultaneously:\n\n- Client calls `queryMultipleCollections(collectionIds[], queryText, topK, threshold)`\n- Server endpoint: `/api/vector/query-multi`\n- Results are merged and sorted by score across all collections\n- Returns grouped results by collection ID\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:1032-1067](), [src/endpoints/vectors.js:380-411]()\n\n## World Info Vector Activation\n\n### Semantic World Info\n\nWhen `enabled_world_info` is enabled, World Info entries can be activated based on vector similarity:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Start[\"activateWorldInfo(chat)\"] --\u003e GetQuery[\"queryText = getQueryText(chat, 'world-info')\"]\n    GetQuery --\u003e GetEntries[\"entries = getSortedEntries()\"]\n    \n    GetEntries --\u003e FilterEnabled[\"Filter by vector enabled status:\u003cbr/\u003e- If enabled_for_all: exclude ❌\u003cbr/\u003e- Else: only include 🔗\"]\n    \n    FilterEnabled --\u003e Vectorize[\"Vectorize entry contents\u003cbr/\u003e(if not already vectorized)\"]\n    Vectorize --\u003e Compare[\"Calculate similarity scores\u003cbr/\u003ewith queryText\"]\n    \n    Compare --\u003e Sort[\"Sort by score (descending)\"]\n    Sort --\u003e Threshold[\"Filter by score_threshold\"]\n    Threshold --\u003e Limit[\"Take top max_entries\"]\n    \n    Limit --\u003e Activate[\"Activate selected entries\u003cbr/\u003ein World Info system\"]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:1291-1402]()\n\n### Entry Vectorization\n\nWorld Info entries are vectorized on-demand:\n\n1. Each entry gets a unique collection ID\n2. Entry content is combined: `${content} ${displayKeysFilter}`\n3. Stored with metadata including entry UID\n4. Cached to avoid re-vectorization\n\n### Activation Logic\n\nTwo modes controlled by `settings.enabled_for_all`:\n\n| Mode | Enabled For All | Behavior |\n|------|----------------|----------|\n| Opt-out | `true` | All entries except those with ❌ status can be activated |\n| Opt-in | `false` | Only entries with 🔗 status can be activated |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:1291-1402]()\n\n## Configuration Settings\n\n### Settings Object Structure\n\nThe extension maintains a comprehensive settings object:\n\n```javascript\nconst settings = {\n    // Global settings\n    source: 'transformers',              // Embedding provider\n    alt_endpoint_url: '',                // Alternative API endpoint\n    use_alt_endpoint: false,             // Use alternative endpoint\n    include_wi: false,                   // Include in WI scanning\n    \n    // Chat vectorization\n    enabled_chats: false,                // Enable chat RAG\n    template: 'Past events:\\n{{text}}', // Injection template\n    depth: 2,                            // In-chat depth\n    position: extension_prompt_types.IN_PROMPT,\n    protect: 5,                          // Last N messages to protect\n    insert: 3,                           // Number to retrieve\n    query: 2,                            // Query message count\n    message_chunk_size: 400,             // Chunk size (0=disabled)\n    score_threshold: 0.25,               // Similarity threshold\n    \n    // File vectorization\n    enabled_files: false,                // Enable file RAG\n    size_threshold: 10,                  // Min size in KB\n    chunk_size: 5000,                    // Chunk size in chars\n    chunk_count: 2,                      // Chunks to retrieve\n    overlap_percent: 0,                  // Overlap percentage\n    \n    // Data Bank\n    size_threshold_db: 5,\n    chunk_size_db: 2500,\n    chunk_count_db: 5,\n    overlap_percent_db: 0,\n    file_template_db: 'Related information:\\n{{text}}',\n    file_position_db: extension_prompt_types.IN_PROMPT,\n    file_depth_db: 4,\n    file_depth_role_db: extension_prompt_roles.SYSTEM,\n    \n    // World Info\n    enabled_world_info: false,           // Enable vector WI\n    enabled_for_all: false,              // Opt-out vs opt-in\n    max_entries: 5,                      // Max activated entries\n    \n    // Summarization\n    summarize: false,                    // Summarize before vectorizing\n    summarize_sent: false,               // Summarize on send\n    summary_source: 'main',              // 'main'|'extras'|'webllm'\n    summary_prompt: '...',               // Summarization prompt\n};\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:57-115]()\n\n### Provider-Specific Settings\n\nEach provider may require additional configuration:\n\n```javascript\nfunction getVectorsRequestBody(args = {}) {\n    const body = Object.assign({}, args);\n    switch (settings.source) {\n        case 'openai':\n            body.model = extension_settings.vectors.openai_model;\n            break;\n        case 'ollama':\n            body.model = extension_settings.vectors.ollama_model;\n            body.apiUrl = settings.use_alt_endpoint \n                ? settings.alt_endpoint_url \n                : textgenerationwebui_settings.server_urls[textgen_types.OLLAMA];\n            body.keep = !!extension_settings.vectors.ollama_keep;\n            break;\n        // ... other providers\n    }\n    return body;\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:787-845]()\n\n## API Endpoints\n\n### Server Routes\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Purpose | Request Body | Response |\n|----------|--------|---------|--------------|----------|\n| `/api/vector/query` | POST | Query a collection | `{collectionId, searchText, topK, threshold, source, ...}` | `{hashes[], metadata[]}` |\n| `/api/vector/query-multi` | POST | Query multiple collections | `{collectionIds[], searchText, topK, threshold, source, ...}` | `{[collectionId]: {hashes[], metadata[]}}` |\n| `/api/vector/insert` | POST | Insert vector items | `{collectionId, items[], source, ...}` | `200 OK` |\n| `/api/vector/list` | POST | List stored hashes | `{collectionId, source, ...}` | `number[]` (hashes) |\n| `/api/vector/delete` | POST | Delete vector items | `{collectionId, hashes[], source, ...}` | `200 OK` |\n| `/api/vector/purge` | POST | Purge collection | `{collectionId}` | `200 OK` |\n| `/api/vector/purge-all` | POST | Purge all vectors | `{}` | `200 OK` |\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/vectors.js:445-578]()\n\n### Request Flow Example\n\nQuery endpoint implementation:\n\n```javascript\nrouter.post('/query', async (req, res) =\u003e {\n    const collectionId = String(req.body.collectionId);\n    const searchText = String(req.body.searchText);\n    const topK = Number(req.body.topK) || 10;\n    const threshold = Number(req.body.threshold) || 0.0;\n    const source = String(req.body.source) || 'transformers';\n    const sourceSettings = getSourceSettings(source, req);\n    \n    const results = await queryCollection(\n        req.user.directories, \n        collectionId, \n        source, \n        sourceSettings, \n        searchText, \n        topK, \n        threshold\n    );\n    return res.json(results);\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/vectors.js:445-463]()\n\n### Error Handling\n\nThe server includes automatic index corruption recovery:\n\n```javascript\nasync function regenerateCorruptedIndexErrorHandler(req, res, error) {\n    if (error instanceof SyntaxError \u0026\u0026 !req.query.regenerated) {\n        const collectionId = String(req.body.collectionId);\n        const source = String(req.body.source) || 'transformers';\n        const sourceSettings = getSourceSettings(source, req);\n        \n        const index = await getIndex(req.user.directories, collectionId, \n                                     source, sourceSettings);\n        const exists = await index.isIndexCreated();\n        \n        if (exists) {\n            console.warn(`Corrupted index detected, regenerating...`);\n            await index.deleteIndex();\n            return res.redirect(307, req.originalUrl + '?regenerated=true');\n        }\n    }\n    \n    return res.sendStatus(500);\n}\n```\n\nAll endpoints use this handler to catch and recover from corrupted indexes.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/vectors.js:420-441]()\n\n## Slash Commands\n\nThe extension registers several slash commands for user interaction:\n\n| Command | Purpose | Arguments |\n|---------|---------|-----------|\n| `/vectorize-all` | Vectorize all chat messages | None |\n| `/vectorize-collection` | Vectorize specific collection | `(collectionId)` |\n| `/vector-query` | Query vector collection | `collectionId queryText (topK=10)` |\n| `/vector-purge` | Purge chat vectors | None |\n| `/vectorize-files` | Vectorize all files | None |\n| `/purge-vectors` | Purge all vectors | None |\n| `/vector-stats` | Show vector statistics | None |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:1700-1938]()\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n### Batch Processing\n\nThe system uses configurable batch sizes for efficiency:\n\n```javascript\nconst getBatchSize = () =\u003e ['transformers', 'ollama'].includes(settings.source) ? 1 : 5;\n```\n\nTransformers and Ollama are forced to batch size 1 due to provider limitations.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:55]()\n\n### Synchronization Throttling\n\n- Chat synchronization is debounced with `debounce_timeout.relaxed`\n- Manual vectorization shows progress: `processed / total` percentage and ETA\n- Blocks concurrent synchronization with `syncBlocked` flag\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:189](), [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:753]()\n\n### Caching\n\n- **Hash Cache**: Stores computed string hashes to avoid recalculation\n- **Summary Cache**: Stores summarized messages to avoid re-summarization\n- Both use `Map` objects for O(1) lookup\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:409](), [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:119]()\n\n## Integration with Generation Pipeline\n\nThe extension hooks into the generation pipeline via the `rearrangeChat()` function:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant PM as PromptManager\n    participant Ext as vectors/index.js\n    participant Chat as Chat Array\n    participant EP as ExtensionPrompt\n    \n    PM-\u003e\u003eExt: rearrangeChat(chat, contextSize, abort, type)\n    \n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Clear extension prompts\n    \n    alt enabled_files\n        Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: processFiles(chat)\n        Note over Ext: Vectorize and retrieve\u003cbr/\u003efile chunks\n    end\n    \n    alt enabled_world_info\n        Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: activateWorldInfo(chat)\n        Note over Ext: Activate WI entries\u003cbr/\u003eby similarity\n    end\n    \n    alt enabled_chats\n        Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Check chat.length \u003e= protect\n        Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: getQueryText(chat, 'chat')\n        Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: queryCollection(chatId, queryText, insert)\n        Ext-\u003e\u003eChat: Remove queried messages from array\n        Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: getPromptText(queriedMessages)\n        Ext-\u003e\u003eEP: setExtensionPrompt(EXTENSION_PROMPT_TAG)\n    end\n```\n\nThe function is registered globally and called during prompt assembly.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:636-719](), [public/scripts/extensions/vectors/index.js:751]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"3a:T8675,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# User Interaction
