Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document covers command-based interaction systems and automation features in SillyTavern. These systems enable users to extend functionality, automate workflows, and create complex interaction patterns without modifying core code.\n\nFor information about AI generation and prompt assembly, see [AI Integration](#3). For UI components and visual elements, see [User Interface](#4). For data persistence and chat management, see [Data Management](#5).\n\n## Overview\n\nSillyTavern provides a layered interaction system built on three core components:\n\n1. **Slash Commands** - A text-based command language for invoking functionality\n2. **Quick Reply Buttons** - Visual interface elements that execute slash commands\n3. **Auto-execution Framework** - Event-driven automation that triggers commands based on application state\n\nThese systems form a programmable interface layer that sits between the user and the application core, enabling sophisticated automation, custom workflows, and third-party extensions.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    User[\"User Input\"]\n    \n    subgraph InputMethods[\"Input Methods\"]\n        TextInput[\"Text Input\u003cbr/\u003e#send_textarea\"]\n        QRButton[\"Quick Reply Button\u003cbr/\u003eQuickReply.render()\"]\n        AutoExec[\"Auto-execution\u003cbr/\u003eAutoExecuteHandler\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph CommandLayer[\"Command Processing Layer\"]\n        Parser[\"SlashCommandParser\u003cbr/\u003e.parse()\"]\n        Executor[\"SlashCommandExecutor\u003cbr/\u003e.execute()\"]\n        Closure[\"SlashCommandClosure\u003cbr/\u003eScript blocks with scope\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph CoreSystems[\"Core Application Systems\"]\n        ChatGen[\"Chat Generation\u003cbr/\u003eGenerate()\"]\n        CharMgmt[\"Character Management\u003cbr/\u003eselectCharacterById()\"]\n        Settings[\"Settings Management\u003cbr/\u003esaveSettingsDebounced()\"]\n        Extensions[\"Extension System\u003cbr/\u003eeventSource.emit()\"]\n    end\n    \n    User --\u003e TextInput\n    User --\u003e QRButton\n    \n    TextInput --\u003e Parser\n    QRButton --\u003e Parser\n    AutoExec --\u003e Parser\n    \n    Parser --\u003e Executor\n    Executor --\u003e Closure\n    \n    Closure --\u003e ChatGen\n    Closure --\u003e CharMgmt\n    Closure --\u003e Settings\n    Closure --\u003e Extensions\n    \n    Extensions -.-\u003e|Events| AutoExec\n```\n\n**Diagram: User Interaction Flow**\n\nSources: [public/scripts/slash-commands.js:1-100](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:1-305](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandClosure.js:1-50]()\n\n## Slash Command Architecture\n\n### Command Registration and Structure\n\nSlash commands follow the format `/commandname [arguments]` and are registered through the `SlashCommandParser` singleton. Each command is represented by a `SlashCommand` object that defines its name, callback function, arguments, and metadata.\n\n| Component | Class/File | Purpose |\n|-----------|------------|---------|\n| Parser | `SlashCommandParser` | Tokenizes and parses command text |\n| Command Definition | `SlashCommand` | Metadata and execution logic for a command |\n| Executor | `SlashCommandExecutor` | Executes a single command with arguments |\n| Closure | `SlashCommandClosure` | Executes command sequences with scope |\n| Arguments | `SlashCommandArgument` | Typed argument definitions with validation |\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph Registration[\"Command Registration\"]\n        DefineCmd[\"Define SlashCommand\"]\n        AddParser[\"SlashCommandParser\u003cbr/\u003e.addCommandObject()\"]\n        Registry[\"Command Registry\u003cbr/\u003eMap\u0026lt;name, SlashCommand\u0026gt;\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Execution[\"Command Execution\"]\n        Input[\"User Input\u003cbr/\u003e/command arg1 arg2\"]\n        Parse[\"Parser.parse()\"]\n        TokenList[\"Command Tokens\"]\n        ExecChain[\"SlashCommandExecutor[]\"]\n        Execute[\"Execute Chain\"]\n    end\n    \n    DefineCmd --\u003e AddParser\n    AddParser --\u003e Registry\n    \n    Input --\u003e Parse\n    Parse --\u003e TokenList\n    TokenList --\u003e ExecChain\n    Registry -.-\u003e|Lookup| ExecChain\n    ExecChain --\u003e Execute\n```\n\n**Diagram: Slash Command Registration and Execution**\n\nSources: [public/scripts/slash-commands.js:94-102](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommand.js:1-50](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js:1-100]()\n\n### Command Parsing Pipeline\n\nThe parser processes raw text input through multiple stages:\n\n1. **Tokenization** - Split input by commands (identified by `/` prefix)\n2. **Argument Parsing** - Extract named and unnamed arguments\n3. **Pipe Resolution** - Handle pipe operators (`|`) for chaining commands\n4. **Variable Substitution** - Replace `{{variable}}` macros with values\n5. **Executor Creation** - Build `SlashCommandExecutor` objects for each command\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant Parser as SlashCommandParser\n    participant Tokenizer\n    participant ArgParser as ArgumentParser\n    participant VarSub as Variable Substitution\n    participant Executor as SlashCommandExecutor\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eParser: \"/echo msg=Hello {{user}}\"\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eTokenizer: tokenize()\n    Tokenizer--\u003e\u003eParser: Command tokens\n    \n    Parser-\u003e\u003eArgParser: parseArguments()\n    ArgParser--\u003e\u003eParser: {msg: \"Hello {{user}}\"}\n    \n    Parser-\u003e\u003eVarSub: substituteParams()\n    VarSub--\u003e\u003eParser: {msg: \"Hello Alice\"}\n    \n    Parser-\u003e\u003eExecutor: new SlashCommandExecutor()\n    Executor--\u003e\u003eParser: Executor instance\n    \n    Parser--\u003e\u003eUser: SlashCommandClosure\n```\n\n**Diagram: Command Parsing Sequence**\n\nSources: [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js:100-500](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandExecutor.js:1-200]()\n\n### Core Command Categories\n\nSillyTavern provides over 200 built-in slash commands organized into functional categories:\n\n| Category | Example Commands | Purpose |\n|----------|------------------|---------|\n| Generation | `/impersonate`, `/continue`, `/gen` | Control AI text generation |\n| Chat Management | `/delchat`, `/renamechat`, `/newchat` | Manage conversation state |\n| Character Control | `/char`, `/persona`, `/dupe` | Switch and modify characters |\n| API Control | `/api`, `/model`, `/connect` | Configure AI backends |\n| Flow Control | `/if`, `/while`, `/break`, `/return` | Scripting logic |\n| Variables | `/let`, `/var`, `/getvar`, `/setvar` | Variable manipulation |\n| Extension Integration | `/imagine`, `/caption`, `/vectorize` | Invoke extension functionality |\n\nThe command registration pattern in [public/scripts/slash-commands.js:217-1500]() demonstrates how core commands are defined using `SlashCommand.fromProps()` with typed arguments and help documentation.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/slash-commands.js:217-1500]()\n\n## Quick Reply System\n\n### Architecture Overview\n\nThe Quick Reply system is an extension that provides a visual button interface for executing slash commands. It consists of three main layers:\n\n1. **Data Model** - `QuickReply`, `QuickReplySet`, `QuickReplyConfig` classes\n2. **UI Rendering** - `ButtonUi`, `SettingsUi` classes\n3. **Auto-execution** - `AutoExecuteHandler` for event-driven triggers\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph DataModel[\"Data Model Layer\"]\n        QRSet[\"QuickReplySet\u003cbr/\u003eCollection of QRs\"]\n        QR[\"QuickReply\u003cbr/\u003eButton definition\"]\n        QRConfig[\"QuickReplyConfig\u003cbr/\u003eVisibility settings\"]\n        QRLink[\"QuickReplyContextLink\u003cbr/\u003eContext menu entries\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph UILayer[\"UI Rendering Layer\"]\n        ButtonUI[\"ButtonUi\u003cbr/\u003eRenders buttons in chat\"]\n        SettingsUI[\"SettingsUi\u003cbr/\u003eManagement interface\"]\n        EditorUI[\"QR Editor\u003cbr/\u003eCommand editor with syntax\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ExecutionLayer[\"Execution Layer\"]\n        AutoExec[\"AutoExecuteHandler\u003cbr/\u003eEvent-driven execution\"]\n        Execute[\"QuickReply.execute()\u003cbr/\u003eRun slash commands\"]\n        Debug[\"Debug Controller\u003cbr/\u003eBreakpoint support\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Storage[\"Persistence\"]\n        Settings[\"extension_settings\u003cbr/\u003e.quickReplyV2\"]\n        Presets[\"quickReplyPresets\u003cbr/\u003eStored sets\"]\n    end\n    \n    QRSet --\u003e QR\n    QR --\u003e QRLink\n    QRConfig --\u003e QRSet\n    \n    QRSet --\u003e ButtonUI\n    QRSet --\u003e SettingsUI\n    QR --\u003e EditorUI\n    \n    QR --\u003e Execute\n    AutoExec --\u003e Execute\n    Execute --\u003e Debug\n    \n    QRSet --\u003e Presets\n    QRConfig --\u003e Settings\n```\n\n**Diagram: Quick Reply System Architecture**\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:1-305](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:1-100](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/AutoExecuteHandler.js:1-100]()\n\n### Button Rendering and Interaction\n\nQuick Reply buttons are rendered in the chat interface at `#qr--bar` with support for icons, labels, and context menus. The rendering process:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant ButtonUi\n    participant QR as QuickReply\n    participant DOM\n    participant Parser as SlashCommandParser\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eButtonUi: Page Load\n    ButtonUi-\u003e\u003eQR: render()\n    QR-\u003e\u003eDOM: Create button elements\n    DOM--\u003e\u003eUser: Display buttons\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eDOM: Click button\n    DOM-\u003e\u003eQR: click event\n    QR-\u003e\u003eQR: execute()\n    QR-\u003e\u003eParser: parse(this.message)\n    Parser--\u003e\u003eQR: SlashCommandClosure\n    QR-\u003e\u003eParser: Execute closure\n    Parser--\u003e\u003eUser: Command result\n```\n\n**Diagram: Quick Reply Button Interaction**\n\nEach `QuickReply` instance maintains a reference to its DOM element and updates it reactively when properties change. The button structure defined in [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:112-169]() includes:\n\n- Icon element (`.qr--button-icon`) - Optional Font Awesome icon\n- Label element (`.qr--button-label`) - Button text\n- Expander element (`.qr--button-expander`) - Context menu trigger\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:93-169](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/ui/ButtonUi.js:1-200]()\n\n### Quick Reply Sets and Scoping\n\nQuick Replies are organized into sets that can be scoped at three levels:\n\n| Scope | Storage Location | Purpose |\n|-------|------------------|---------|\n| Global | `extension_settings.quickReplyV2.config` | Available across all chats and characters |\n| Chat | `chat_metadata.quickReply` | Specific to current conversation |\n| Character | `extension_settings.quickReplyV2.characterConfigs[avatar]` | Tied to individual character |\n\nThe scoping system in [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:147-174]() determines which sets are visible based on the current context. When a chat loads, the system:\n\n1. Loads global sets from `extension_settings`\n2. Checks for character-specific sets if a character is active\n3. Checks for chat-specific sets if a chat is loaded\n4. Merges all applicable sets for display\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Init[\"Chat Load Event\u003cbr/\u003eCHAT_CHANGED\"]\n    \n    CheckChar[\"Check this_chid\"]\n    LoadGlobal[\"Load Global Config\u003cbr/\u003eextension_settings.quickReplyV2.config\"]\n    LoadChar[\"Load Character Config\u003cbr/\u003echaracterConfigs[avatar]\"]\n    LoadChat[\"Load Chat Config\u003cbr/\u003echat_metadata.quickReply\"]\n    \n    MergeSets[\"Merge Visible Sets\u003cbr/\u003eButtonUi.refresh()\"]\n    RenderButtons[\"Render Buttons\u003cbr/\u003e#qr--bar\"]\n    \n    Init --\u003e LoadGlobal\n    Init --\u003e CheckChar\n    \n    CheckChar --\u003e|Character Active| LoadChar\n    CheckChar --\u003e|No Character| LoadChat\n    \n    LoadChar --\u003e LoadChat\n    \n    LoadGlobal --\u003e MergeSets\n    LoadChar --\u003e MergeSets\n    LoadChat --\u003e MergeSets\n    \n    MergeSets --\u003e RenderButtons\n```\n\n**Diagram: Quick Reply Set Loading and Scoping**\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:147-278]()\n\n## Auto-Execution Framework\n\n### Event-Driven Triggers\n\nThe `AutoExecuteHandler` responds to application events to trigger Quick Replies automatically. This enables sophisticated automation without manual invocation.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph Events[\"Application Events\"]\n        AppReady[\"APP_READY\"]\n        ChatChanged[\"CHAT_CHANGED\"]\n        UserMsg[\"USER_MESSAGE_RENDERED\"]\n        AiMsg[\"CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED\"]\n        MemberDraft[\"GROUP_MEMBER_DRAFTED\"]\n        WIActivated[\"WORLD_INFO_ACTIVATED\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Handler[\"AutoExecuteHandler\"]\n        CheckFlags[\"Check QR Flags\"]\n        Filter[\"Filter Applicable QRs\"]\n        Execute[\"Execute Matching QRs\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph QRFlags[\"QuickReply Execution Flags\"]\n        Startup[\"executeOnStartup\"]\n        ChatChange[\"executeOnChatChange\"]\n        OnUser[\"executeOnUser\"]\n        OnAi[\"executeOnAi\"]\n        OnDraft[\"executeOnGroupMemberDraft\"]\n        OnNewChat[\"executeOnNewChat\"]\n    end\n    \n    AppReady --\u003e CheckFlags\n    ChatChanged --\u003e CheckFlags\n    UserMsg --\u003e CheckFlags\n    AiMsg --\u003e CheckFlags\n    MemberDraft --\u003e CheckFlags\n    WIActivated --\u003e CheckFlags\n    \n    CheckFlags --\u003e Filter\n    Filter --\u003e Startup\n    Filter --\u003e ChatChange\n    Filter --\u003e OnUser\n    Filter --\u003e OnAi\n    Filter --\u003e OnDraft\n    Filter --\u003e OnNewChat\n    \n    Filter --\u003e Execute\n```\n\n**Diagram: Auto-Execution Event Flow**\n\nEach `QuickReply` object has boolean flags that determine when it should auto-execute. The execution conditions defined in [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:41-51]():\n\n- `executeOnStartup` - Run when SillyTavern loads\n- `executeOnUser` - Run after user sends a message\n- `executeOnAi` - Run after AI generates a response\n- `executeOnChatChange` - Run when switching chats or characters\n- `executeOnGroupMemberDraft` - Run when a group member is selected\n- `executeOnNewChat` - Run when creating a new conversation\n- `executeBeforeGeneration` - Run before AI generation starts\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:218-305](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/AutoExecuteHandler.js:1-150]()\n\n### Prevention and Control Flow\n\nThe auto-execution system includes safeguards to prevent infinite loops and unwanted triggers:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Event as Event Trigger\n    participant AutoExec as AutoExecuteHandler\n    participant Stack as preventAutoExecuteStack\n    participant QR as QuickReply\n    \n    Event-\u003e\u003eAutoExec: handleUser()\n    AutoExec-\u003e\u003eStack: Check if prevented\n    \n    alt Prevention Active\n        Stack--\u003e\u003eAutoExec: Blocked\n        AutoExec--\u003e\u003eEvent: Skip execution\n    else Execution Allowed\n        Stack--\u003e\u003eAutoExec: Allowed\n        AutoExec-\u003e\u003eStack: Push true (prevent)\n        AutoExec-\u003e\u003eQR: execute()\n        QR-\u003e\u003eQR: Run slash commands\n        QR--\u003e\u003eAutoExec: Complete\n        AutoExec-\u003e\u003eStack: Pop (restore)\n    end\n```\n\n**Diagram: Auto-Execution Prevention Mechanism**\n\nThe `preventAutoExecuteStack` in [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/AutoExecuteHandler.js:8]() maintains a stack of prevention states. When a Quick Reply executes, it pushes `true` onto the stack to prevent nested auto-executions, then pops the value when complete.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/AutoExecuteHandler.js:1-150]()\n\n## Command Debugging and Development\n\n### Debug Controller and Breakpoints\n\nThe `SlashCommandDebugController` provides breakpoint-based debugging for slash command scripts. This is particularly useful for complex Quick Reply sequences.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph DebugUI[\"Debug Interface\"]\n        EditorModal[\"QR Editor Modal\"]\n        DebugPanel[\"Debug Panel\u003cbr/\u003e#qr--modal-debugController\"]\n        BreakpointToggle[\"Breakpoint Toggles\"]\n        StepControls[\"Step/Continue/Stop\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Execution[\"Debug Execution\"]\n        DebugCtrl[\"SlashCommandDebugController\"]\n        Breakpoint[\"SlashCommandBreakPoint\"]\n        Executor[\"SlashCommandExecutor\"]\n        Closure[\"SlashCommandClosure\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph State[\"Debug State\"]\n        Stack[\"closure.stack\"]\n        StepMode[\"debugController.stepMode\"]\n        Breaking[\"debugController.breaking\"]\n        Variables[\"scope.variables\"]\n    end\n    \n    EditorModal --\u003e DebugPanel\n    DebugPanel --\u003e BreakpointToggle\n    DebugPanel --\u003e StepControls\n    \n    StepControls --\u003e DebugCtrl\n    BreakpointToggle --\u003e Breakpoint\n    \n    DebugCtrl --\u003e Executor\n    Executor --\u003e Breakpoint\n    Breakpoint -.-\u003e|Pause| DebugCtrl\n    \n    DebugCtrl --\u003e Stack\n    DebugCtrl --\u003e StepMode\n    DebugCtrl --\u003e Breaking\n    Closure --\u003e Variables\n    \n    DebugPanel -.-\u003e|Display| Stack\n    DebugPanel -.-\u003e|Display| Variables\n```\n\n**Diagram: Slash Command Debugging Architecture**\n\nThe debugging workflow implemented in [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:570-720]():\n\n1. User opens QR Editor with Ctrl+Click on button\n2. Sets breakpoints by clicking line numbers\n3. Clicks \"Execute \u0026 Debug\" button\n4. Execution pauses at each breakpoint\n5. User can inspect variables, step through commands, or continue\n6. Debug panel shows call stack and variable scope\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:570-720](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandDebugController.js:1-100](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandBreakPoint.js:1-4]()\n\n### QR Editor Features\n\nThe Quick Reply editor provides a full-featured development environment for slash commands:\n\n| Feature | Implementation | Purpose |\n|---------|----------------|---------|\n| Syntax Highlighting | `hljs.highlightElement()` | Visual command recognition |\n| Command Autocomplete | `setSlashCommandAutoComplete()` | IntelliSense for commands |\n| Live Validation | Parser error detection | Immediate feedback on syntax errors |\n| Execution Preview | Execute button in editor | Test commands before saving |\n| Context Menu Support | Nested QR linking | Create command workflows |\n\nThe editor template in [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/html/qrEditor.html:1-200]() includes:\n- Tabbed interface for settings, message, and debug\n- Icon picker for button customization\n- Auto-execution flag checkboxes\n- Context menu configuration for chaining QRs\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/html/qrEditor.html:1-200](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:300-500]()\n\n## Variables, Macros, and Scripting\n\n### Variable System\n\nSillyTavern supports three types of variables for dynamic content:\n\n1. **Local Variables** - `{{getvar::varname}}` scoped to slash command execution\n2. **Global Variables** - `{{getglobalvar::varname}}` persisted across sessions\n3. **Macro Variables** - `{{user}}`, `{{char}}`, etc. built-in substitutions\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph VarTypes[\"Variable Types\"]\n        Local[\"Local Variables\u003cbr/\u003eSlashCommandScope.variables\"]\n        Global[\"Global Variables\u003cbr/\u003eextension_settings.variables.global\"]\n        Macro[\"Macro Variables\u003cbr/\u003eSubstitution rules\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Commands[\"Variable Commands\"]\n        SetVar[\"/setvar key=name value=Alice\"]\n        GetVar[\"/getvar name\"]\n        SetGlobal[\"/setglobalvar key=count value=5\"]\n        GetGlobal[\"/getglobalvar count\"]\n        Let[\"/let x 42\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Substitution[\"Substitution Engine\"]\n        SubParams[\"substituteParams()\"]\n        SubExtended[\"substituteParamsExtended()\"]\n        Resolve[\"Variable Resolution\"]\n    end\n    \n    SetVar --\u003e Local\n    GetVar --\u003e Local\n    Let --\u003e Local\n    \n    SetGlobal --\u003e Global\n    GetGlobal --\u003e Global\n    \n    Local --\u003e Resolve\n    Global --\u003e Resolve\n    Macro --\u003e Resolve\n    \n    Resolve --\u003e SubParams\n    Resolve --\u003e SubExtended\n    \n    SubParams -.-\u003e|Text processing| Output[\"Processed Text\"]\n    SubExtended -.-\u003e|Extended context| Output\n```\n\n**Diagram: Variable System Architecture**\n\nThe variable resolution order in [public/scripts/variables.js:1-500]():\n\n1. Check local scope (`SlashCommandScope.variables`)\n2. Check chat-scoped variables (`chat_metadata.variables`)\n3. Check global variables (`extension_settings.variables.global`)\n4. Check built-in macros (system-defined)\n5. Return empty string if not found\n\nSources: [public/scripts/variables.js:1-500](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js:1-200]()\n\n### Macro Substitution\n\nMacro substitution in [public/script.js:8000-9000]() supports over 50 built-in placeholders:\n\n| Macro | Source | Purpose |\n|-------|--------|---------|\n| `{{user}}` | `name1` | Current user persona name |\n| `{{char}}` | `name2` | Current character name |\n| `{{model}}` | API-specific | Current AI model identifier |\n| `{{lastMessage}}` | `chat[chat.length-1]` | Most recent message |\n| `{{lastUserMessage}}` | Last user message | User's last input |\n| `{{lastCharMessage}}` | Last char message | Character's last response |\n| `{{time}}` | `new Date()` | Current time/date |\n| `{{random::a\\|b\\|c}}` | Random selection | Pick random option |\n| `{{roll:d20}}` | Dice roller | Generate random number |\n\nAdvanced macros support nested evaluation and function-like syntax:\n\n```\n{{pipe::{{getvar::name}}::trim}}\n{{add::{{getvar::count}}::1}}\n```\n\nSources: [public/script.js:8000-9000](), [public/scripts/macros/macro-system.js:1-300]()\n\n### Scripting with Closures\n\n`SlashCommandClosure` represents an executable script block with its own scope. This enables:\n\n- **Conditional Logic** - `/if`, `/else`, `/endif`\n- **Loops** - `/while`, `/times`, `/foreach`\n- **Functions** - `/run` with scoped closures\n- **Error Handling** - Try/catch patterns with `/abort`\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant Parser as SlashCommandParser\n    participant Closure as SlashCommandClosure\n    participant Scope as SlashCommandScope\n    participant Executor as SlashCommandExecutor\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eParser: Parse script with /if\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eClosure: Create closure\n    Closure-\u003e\u003eScope: Create variable scope\n    \n    Closure-\u003e\u003eExecutor: Execute /setvar\n    Executor-\u003e\u003eScope: Store variable\n    \n    Closure-\u003e\u003eExecutor: Execute /if condition\n    Executor-\u003e\u003eScope: Read variables\n    Executor--\u003e\u003eClosure: Condition result\n    \n    alt Condition True\n        Closure-\u003e\u003eExecutor: Execute then-block\n    else Condition False\n        Closure-\u003e\u003eExecutor: Execute else-block\n    end\n    \n    Closure-\u003e\u003eScope: Cleanup scope\n    Closure--\u003e\u003eUser: Result\n```\n\n**Diagram: Closure Execution with Scope**\n\nThe closure system in [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandClosure.js:1-1000]() maintains:\n- Parent/child closure relationships\n- Variable scoping and inheritance\n- Abort controller for cancellation\n- Break controller for loop control\n- Debug controller for stepping\n\nSources: [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandClosure.js:1-1000](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js:1-200]()\n\n## Integration Points\n\n### Event System Integration\n\nUser interaction systems integrate with the core through the `eventSource` event bus:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph CoreEvents[\"Core Event Types\"]\n        AppReady[\"APP_READY\"]\n        ChatChanged[\"CHAT_CHANGED\"]\n        UserSent[\"USER_MESSAGE_RENDERED\"]\n        CharResponse[\"CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED\"]\n        GenStarted[\"GENERATION_STARTED\"]\n        GenEnded[\"GENERATION_ENDED\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Listeners[\"Interaction System Listeners\"]\n        QRAutoExec[\"Quick Reply AutoExecuteHandler\"]\n        SlashExec[\"Slash Command Execution\"]\n        VarUpdate[\"Variable Update Handlers\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Emitters[\"Command-Triggered Events\"]\n        CustomEvent[\"Custom Events\u003cbr/\u003e/trigger-event\"]\n        ExtensionHook[\"Extension Hooks\u003cbr/\u003e/run-extension\"]\n        StateChange[\"State Changes\u003cbr/\u003e/char, /chat\"]\n    end\n    \n    AppReady --\u003e QRAutoExec\n    ChatChanged --\u003e QRAutoExec\n    UserSent --\u003e QRAutoExec\n    CharResponse --\u003e QRAutoExec\n    \n    GenStarted --\u003e SlashExec\n    GenEnded --\u003e SlashExec\n    \n    SlashExec --\u003e CustomEvent\n    SlashExec --\u003e ExtensionHook\n    SlashExec --\u003e StateChange\n    \n    CustomEvent -.-\u003e|Feedback| QRAutoExec\n```\n\n**Diagram: Event System Integration**\n\nCommands can subscribe to events using `eventSource.on()` and emit custom events using `eventSource.emit()`. This pattern is used extensively in [public/scripts/slash-commands.js:218-1500]() for core command implementations.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/slash-commands.js:218-1500](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:218-305]()\n\n### Extension API Surface\n\nExtensions can register slash commands and Quick Reply functionality through well-defined APIs:\n\n| API | Entry Point | Purpose |\n|-----|-------------|---------|\n| Command Registration | `SlashCommandParser.addCommandObject()` | Add new slash commands |\n| Quick Reply API | `quickReplyApi` global | Programmatic QR manipulation |\n| Variable Commands | `/let`, `/setvar`, etc. | Script integration |\n| Context Access | `getContext()` | Access core application state |\n| Event Subscription | `eventSource.on()` | React to application events |\n\nThe Quick Reply API exposed in [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/api/QuickReplyApi.js:1-400]() provides methods for:\n- Creating and modifying Quick Reply sets\n- Executing QRs by name\n- Managing auto-execution settings\n- Querying available commands\n\n```javascript\n// Example extension integration\nglobalThis.executeQuickReplyByName('MyQR', { arg1: 'value' }, { quiet: true });\nquickReplyApi.createQuickReply('MySet', { label: 'Test', message: '/echo Hello' });\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/api/QuickReplyApi.js:1-400](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:188-211]()\n\n### Data Persistence Flow\n\nUser interaction data persists at multiple levels:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    subgraph UserData[\"User Input \u0026 Configuration\"]\n        Commands[\"Slash Commands\"]\n        QRButtons[\"Quick Reply Sets\"]\n        Variables[\"Global Variables\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Storage[\"Storage Locations\"]\n        ExtSettings[\"extension_settings\u003cbr/\u003equickReplyV2\"]\n        ChatMeta[\"chat_metadata\u003cbr/\u003equickReply\"]\n        CharConfig[\"characterConfigs\u003cbr/\u003e[avatar]\"]\n        GlobalVars[\"extension_settings\u003cbr/\u003evariables.global\"]\n        QRPresets[\"Server Storage\u003cbr/\u003equickReplyPresets\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Persistence[\"Persistence Layer\"]\n        SaveSettings[\"saveSettingsDebounced()\"]\n        SaveChat[\"saveChatConditional()\"]\n        SaveMeta[\"saveMetadataDebounced()\"]\n        ServerAPI[\"POST /api/settings/save\"]\n    end\n    \n    Commands --\u003e Variables\n    QRButtons --\u003e ExtSettings\n    QRButtons --\u003e ChatMeta\n    QRButtons --\u003e CharConfig\n    Variables --\u003e GlobalVars\n    \n    ExtSettings --\u003e SaveSettings\n    ChatMeta --\u003e SaveChat\n    CharConfig --\u003e SaveSettings\n    GlobalVars --\u003e SaveSettings\n    \n    SaveSettings --\u003e ServerAPI\n    SaveChat --\u003e ServerAPI\n    SaveMeta --\u003e ServerAPI\n    \n    ServerAPI --\u003e QRPresets\n```\n\n**Diagram: User Interaction Data Persistence**\n\nThe persistence strategy ensures:\n1. Quick Reply sets are saved per-scope (global/chat/character)\n2. Variables persist across sessions\n3. Auto-execution settings are preserved\n4. Debouncing prevents excessive writes\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:59-108](), [public/scripts/extensions.js:100-300](), [public/script.js:5000-5500]()\n\n## Command Execution Lifecycle\n\n### Complete Execution Flow\n\nThe following diagram shows the complete lifecycle of a slash command from user input to result:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant Input as send_textarea\n    participant Parser as SlashCommandParser\n    participant Scope as SlashCommandScope\n    participant Executor as SlashCommandExecutor\n    participant Command as SlashCommand\n    participant Callback\n    participant Core as Core Systems\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eInput: Type \"/echo Hello {{user}}\"\n    Input-\u003e\u003eParser: executeSlashCommands()\n    \n    Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: tokenize()\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: substituteParams()\n    Note over Parser: Replace {{user}} with name1\n    \n    Parser-\u003e\u003eScope: Create execution scope\n    Scope--\u003e\u003eParser: New scope\n    \n    Parser-\u003e\u003eExecutor: Create executor chain\n    Executor-\u003e\u003eParser: Look up command\n    Parser--\u003e\u003eExecutor: SlashCommand instance\n    \n    Parser-\u003e\u003eExecutor: execute()\n    Executor-\u003e\u003eCommand: Validate arguments\n    Command--\u003e\u003eExecutor: Arguments valid\n    \n    Executor-\u003e\u003eCallback: Invoke command.callback()\n    Callback-\u003e\u003eCore: Perform action\n    Core--\u003e\u003eCallback: Action result\n    Callback--\u003e\u003eExecutor: Return value\n    \n    Executor--\u003e\u003eParser: Pipe result\n    Parser--\u003e\u003eInput: Final result\n    Input--\u003e\u003eUser: Display output\n```\n\n**Diagram: Complete Slash Command Execution Lifecycle**\n\nKey phases:\n1. **Tokenization** - Split text into command segments\n2. **Substitution** - Resolve variables and macros\n3. **Scope Creation** - Establish execution context\n4. **Validation** - Check argument types and requirements\n5. **Execution** - Invoke command callback\n6. **Result Handling** - Process return value and pipes\n\nSources: [public/scripts/slash-commands.js:94-500](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js:100-800](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandExecutor.js:1-500]()\n\n### Error Handling and Abortion\n\nThe abort controller pattern enables graceful cancellation:\n\n```mermaid\nstateDiagram-v2\n    [*] --\u003e Parsing: User Input\n    Parsing --\u003e Executing: Valid Command\n    Parsing --\u003e Error: Parse Error\n    \n    Executing --\u003e Running: Command Callback\n    Running --\u003e Success: Complete\n    Running --\u003e Aborted: AbortController.abort()\n    Running --\u003e Error: Exception Thrown\n    \n    Success --\u003e [*]\n    Aborted --\u003e [*]\n    Error --\u003e [*]\n    \n    note right of Aborted\n        SlashCommandAbortController\n        Can be triggered by:\n        - User clicking stop\n        - /abort command\n        - Timeout\n    end note\n```\n\n**Diagram: Command Execution State Machine**\n\nThe `SlashCommandAbortController` in [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandAbortController.js:1-200]() provides:\n- Signal-based cancellation\n- Cleanup callbacks\n- Nested abortion (parent aborts children)\n- Graceful error propagation\n\nSources: [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandAbortController.js:1-200](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandExecutor.js:100-300]()\n\n## Performance and Optimization\n\n### Command Caching and Reuse\n\nThe parser caches parsed command structures to avoid redundant processing:\n\n| Optimization | Implementation | Impact |\n|--------------|----------------|--------|\n| Command Lookup | `Map\u003cstring, SlashCommand\u003e` | O(1) command resolution |\n| Argument Parsing | Cached regex patterns | Faster tokenization |\n| Variable Resolution | Scope inheritance | Reduced lookups |\n| Debounced Saves | `saveSettingsDebounced()` | Fewer I/O operations |\n\n### Auto-Execution Batching\n\nThe `AutoExecuteHandler` batches Quick Reply executions to prevent cascading triggers:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Event[\"Event Fired\u003cbr/\u003eUSER_MESSAGE_RENDERED\"]\n    \n    Collect[\"Collect Matching QRs\u003cbr/\u003eFilter by executeOnUser\"]\n    \n    Batch[\"Batch Execution\u003cbr/\u003eSequential processing\"]\n    \n    Prevent[\"Push Prevention\u003cbr/\u003epreventAutoExecuteStack\"]\n    \n    Execute[\"Execute Each QR\"]\n    \n    Restore[\"Pop Prevention\u003cbr/\u003eRestore state\"]\n    \n    Event --\u003e Collect\n    Collect --\u003e Batch\n    Batch --\u003e Prevent\n    Prevent --\u003e Execute\n    Execute --\u003e Restore\n```\n\n**Diagram: Auto-Execution Batching**\n\nThis prevents infinite loops where:\n- QR1 triggers on user message\n- QR1 generates a message\n- Generated message triggers QR1 again\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/AutoExecuteHandler.js:40-150]()\n\n## Security Considerations\n\n### Input Sanitization\n\nSlash commands use `DOMPurify` for sanitizing user input before display:\n\n```javascript\n// Example from slash-commands.js\nconst sanitizedInput = DOMPurify.sanitize(userInput);\n```\n\n### Scope Isolation\n\nVariable scopes prevent unintended data leakage between command executions. Each `SlashCommandClosure` creates an isolated scope that:\n- Inherits parent scope variables (read-only by default)\n- Creates new local variables\n- Cleans up on completion\n\n### Command Permissions\n\nWhile SillyTavern runs client-side, certain commands are restricted:\n- File system operations require explicit user confirmation\n- API key access is read-only through secure methods\n- Network requests are proxied through the server\n\nSources: [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js:1-200](), [public/scripts/tool-calling.js](public/scripts/tool-calling.js)\n- [src/constants.js](src/constants.js)\n- [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js](src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js)\n- [src/endpoints/openai.js](src/endpoints/openai.js)\n- [src/endpoints/secrets.js](src/endpoints/secrets.js)\n- [src/prompt-converters.js](src/prompt-converters.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe Slash Command System provides a scripting language (STscript) for automating interactions within SillyTavern. It allows users to define custom commands, create macros, control chat flow, manipulate variables, and trigger AI generations programmatically. The system supports complex features including named/unnamed arguments, closures, piped execution, variable scoping, and debugging capabilities.\n\nFor information about variables and macros used within slash commands, see [Variables and Macros System](#7.4). For Quick Reply integration with slash commands, see [Quick Reply System](#7.2). For debugging features, see [Slash Command Debugging and Advanced Features](#7.3).\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\nThe slash command system consists of several interconnected components that handle parsing, execution, and scope management.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Input[\"User Input Text\u003cbr/\u003e/command arg1 arg2\"]\n    Parser[\"SlashCommandParser\u003cbr/\u003epublic/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js\"]\n    Command[\"SlashCommand\u003cbr/\u003eRegistry of commands\"]\n    Executor[\"SlashCommandExecutor\u003cbr/\u003eExecution engine\"]\n    Scope[\"SlashCommandScope\u003cbr/\u003eVariable storage\"]\n    Closure[\"SlashCommandClosure\u003cbr/\u003eCommand blocks\"]\n    \n    Input --\u003e Parser\n    Parser --\u003e |\"parse()\"| Command\n    Command --\u003e |\"commands Map\"| Executor\n    Executor --\u003e |\"execute()\"| Scope\n    Executor --\u003e Closure\n    Closure --\u003e Scope\n    \n    Parser -.-\u003e |\"registers\"| Command\n    Executor --\u003e |\"returns\"| Result[\"SlashCommandClosureResult\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Argument Processing\"\n        NamedArgs[\"SlashCommandNamedArgument\u003cbr/\u003ekey=value pairs\"]\n        UnnamedArgs[\"SlashCommandArgument\u003cbr/\u003epositional values\"]\n    end\n    \n    Command --\u003e NamedArgs\n    Command --\u003e UnnamedArgs\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommand.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandExecutor.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandClosure.js]()\n\n## Command Registration\n\nCommands are registered using the `SlashCommandParser` singleton instance. The parser maintains a central registry of all available commands.\n\n### Registration API\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Registration[\"Command Registration\"]\n    \n    Registration --\u003e Old[\"Deprecated:\u003cbr/\u003eregisterSlashCommand()\"]\n    Registration --\u003e New[\"Recommended:\u003cbr/\u003eSlashCommandParser.addCommandObject()\"]\n    \n    New --\u003e CommandObj[\"SlashCommand.fromProps({\u003cbr/\u003ename, callback,\u003cbr/\u003enamedArgumentList,\u003cbr/\u003eunnamedArgumentList,\u003cbr/\u003ehelpString\u003cbr/\u003e})\"]\n    \n    CommandObj --\u003e Registry[\"parser.commands Map\"]\n    \n    style Old fill:#ffcccc\n    style New fill:#ccffcc\n```\n\n**Command Registration Example:**\n\nThe system provides two methods for registering commands:\n\n1. **Legacy method** (deprecated): `registerSlashCommand(name, callback, aliases, helpString, interruptsGeneration, purgeFromMessage)`\n2. **Modern method** (recommended): `SlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(SlashCommand.fromProps({...}))`\n\nCommands registered in [public/scripts/slash-commands.js:216-3994]() demonstrate the modern approach:\n\n```javascript\nSlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(SlashCommand.fromProps({\n    name: 'impersonate',\n    callback: async function (args, prompt) { ... },\n    aliases: ['imp'],\n    namedArgumentList: [\n        SlashCommandNamedArgument.fromProps({\n            name: 'await',\n            typeList: [ARGUMENT_TYPE.BOOLEAN],\n            defaultValue: 'false',\n        })\n    ],\n    unnamedArgumentList: [\n        SlashCommandArgument.fromProps({\n            description: 'prompt',\n            typeList: [ARGUMENT_TYPE.STRING],\n        })\n    ],\n    helpString: 'Calls an impersonation response...'\n}));\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands.js:93-102](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommand.js]()\n\n## Parser Syntax and Execution Flow\n\nThe parser converts raw text input into executable command structures. It handles various syntactic elements including quotes, escapes, pipes, and closures.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    RawText[\"Raw Input Text\u003cbr/\u003e/command arg1 key=value | /next\"]\n    \n    Lexer[\"Tokenization\u003cbr/\u003eSplit by spaces, quotes, pipes\"]\n    Tokens[\"Token Stream\u003cbr/\u003e['/', 'command', 'arg1', 'key=value', '|', ...]\"]\n    \n    ParsePhase[\"Parse Phase\"]\n    CommandAST[\"Command AST\u003cbr/\u003ename, args, namedArgs\"]\n    \n    ExecutePhase[\"Execution Phase\"]\n    Callback[\"Command Callback\u003cbr/\u003ecallback(namedArgs, ...unnamedArgs)\"]\n    \n    Result[\"Return Value\u003cbr/\u003epiped to next command\"]\n    \n    RawText --\u003e Lexer\n    Lexer --\u003e Tokens\n    Tokens --\u003e ParsePhase\n    ParsePhase --\u003e CommandAST\n    CommandAST --\u003e ExecutePhase\n    ExecutePhase --\u003e Callback\n    Callback --\u003e Result\n    \n    subgraph \"Special Constructs\"\n        Pipes[\"|\u003cbr/\u003ePipe to next command\"]\n        Closures[\"{: commands :}\u003cbr/\u003eCommand blocks\"]\n        Variables[\"{{var}}\u003cbr/\u003eVariable substitution\"]\n        Quotes[\"Quoted strings\u003cbr/\u003epreserve spaces\"]\n    end\n    \n    ParsePhase -.-\u003e Pipes\n    ParsePhase -.-\u003e Closures\n    ParsePhase -.-\u003e Variables\n    ParsePhase -.-\u003e Quotes\n```\n\n### Key Parsing Rules\n\n| Syntax Element | Purpose | Example |\n|---------------|---------|---------|\n| `/command` | Command invocation | `/echo Hello` |\n| `arg1 arg2` | Unnamed arguments | `/add 5 3` |\n| `key=value` | Named arguments | `/gen temperature=0.7` |\n| `\"quoted string\"` | Preserve spaces | `/echo \"hello world\"` |\n| `\\|` or `{{pipe}}` | Pipe to next command | `/getvar x \\| /echo` |\n| `{: ... :}` | Closure (command block) | `/if left=x right=y {:echo yes:}` |\n| `{{variable}}` | Variable substitution | `/echo {{user}}` |\n| `//comment` | Comment (ignored) | `//This is a comment` |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands.js]()\n\n## Argument Types\n\nThe system supports two types of arguments with type validation and enum providers for auto-completion.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Arguments[\"Command Arguments\"]\n    \n    Arguments --\u003e Named[\"Named Arguments\u003cbr/\u003eSlashCommandNamedArgument\"]\n    Arguments --\u003e Unnamed[\"Unnamed Arguments\u003cbr/\u003eSlashCommandArgument\"]\n    \n    Named --\u003e NamedProps[\"Properties:\u003cbr/\u003e- name\u003cbr/\u003e- typeList\u003cbr/\u003e- defaultValue\u003cbr/\u003e- enumList\u003cbr/\u003e- acceptsMultiple\"]\n    \n    Unnamed --\u003e UnnamedProps[\"Properties:\u003cbr/\u003e- description\u003cbr/\u003e- typeList\u003cbr/\u003e- enumList\u003cbr/\u003e- isRequired\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"ARGUMENT_TYPE enum\"\n        STRING[\"STRING\"]\n        NUMBER[\"NUMBER\"]\n        BOOLEAN[\"BOOLEAN\"]\n        CLOSURE[\"CLOSURE\"]\n        VARIABLE_NAME[\"VARIABLE_NAME\"]\n        LIST[\"LIST\"]\n        DICTIONARY[\"DICTIONARY\"]\n    end\n    \n    NamedProps -.-\u003e STRING\n    UnnamedProps -.-\u003e STRING\n```\n\n### Type System\n\nThe `ARGUMENT_TYPE` enum defined in [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandArgument.js:12-20]() includes:\n\n- `STRING`: Text values\n- `NUMBER`: Numeric values  \n- `BOOLEAN`: True/false values\n- `CLOSURE`: Command blocks `{: ... :}`\n- `VARIABLE_NAME`: Variable identifiers\n- `LIST`: Array values\n- `DICTIONARY`: Object values\n\n### Enum Providers\n\nCommands can specify enum providers for auto-completion. The `commonEnumProviders` object in [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandCommonEnumsProvider.js]() provides standard enums:\n\n- `characters()`: Available characters\n- `personas()`: User personas\n- `tags()`: Tag names\n- `groups()`: Group names\n- `boolean()`: True/false values\n- `variables()`: Local/global variables\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandArgument.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandCommonEnumsProvider.js]()\n\n## Command Execution Flow\n\nCommand execution involves multiple stages including scope setup, argument processing, callback invocation, and result handling.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant Parser as SlashCommandParser\n    participant Executor as SlashCommandExecutor\n    participant Scope as SlashCommandScope\n    participant Command as SlashCommand\n    participant Callback as Command Callback\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eParser: executeSlashCommandsWithOptions(text)\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: parse(text)\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eExecutor: execute(closure)\n    \n    Executor-\u003e\u003eScope: Create/inherit scope\n    Scope--\u003e\u003eExecutor: Scope ready\n    \n    loop For each command\n        Executor-\u003e\u003eCommand: Lookup command by name\n        Command--\u003e\u003eExecutor: Command found\n        \n        Executor-\u003e\u003eExecutor: Process named args\n        Executor-\u003e\u003eExecutor: Process unnamed args\n        Executor-\u003e\u003eExecutor: Substitute variables\n        \n        Executor-\u003e\u003eCallback: callback(namedArgs, ...unnamedArgs)\n        Callback-\u003e\u003eCallback: Execute command logic\n        Callback--\u003e\u003eExecutor: Return value\n        \n        alt Has pipe\n            Executor-\u003e\u003eExecutor: Pass result to next command\n        else No pipe\n            Executor-\u003e\u003eExecutor: Store in closure result\n        end\n    end\n    \n    Executor--\u003e\u003eParser: SlashCommandClosureResult\n    Parser--\u003e\u003eUser: Final result\n```\n\n### Execution Context\n\nEach command execution has access to:\n\n1. **Named Arguments** (`args`): Object with named parameters\n2. **Unnamed Arguments** (`...value`): Array of positional parameters\n3. **Scope** (`args._scope`): Variable scope for the command\n4. **Abort Controller** (`args._abortController`): For cancellation\n5. **Parser Flags** (`args._parserFlags`): Parser configuration\n6. **Piped Value**: Result from previous command in pipe chain\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandExecutor.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands.js]()\n\n## Closures and Scopes\n\nClosures allow grouping commands into reusable blocks with isolated variable scopes. They are fundamental to control flow commands like `/if`, `/while`, and `/times`.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    GlobalScope[\"Global Scope\u003cbr/\u003eextension_settings.variables.global\"]\n    LocalScope[\"Local Scope\u003cbr/\u003echat_metadata.variables\"]\n    \n    ParentScope[\"Parent Closure Scope\"]\n    ChildScope[\"Child Closure Scope\"]\n    \n    GlobalScope -.-\u003e |\"fallback\"| LocalScope\n    LocalScope -.-\u003e |\"fallback\"| ParentScope\n    ParentScope --\u003e ChildScope\n    \n    subgraph \"Closure Syntax\"\n        OpenClosure[\"{:\u003cbr/\u003eStart closure\"]\n        Commands[\"command1 |\u003cbr/\u003ecommand2\"]\n        CloseClosure[\":}\u003cbr/\u003eEnd closure\"]\n        \n        OpenClosure --\u003e Commands\n        Commands --\u003e CloseClosure\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"SlashCommandClosure\"\n        ClosureProps[\"Properties:\u003cbr/\u003e- commands\u003cbr/\u003e- scope\u003cbr/\u003e- breakController\u003cbr/\u003e- abortController\"]\n        Methods[\"Methods:\u003cbr/\u003e- execute()\u003cbr/\u003e- getCopy()\u003cbr/\u003e- toString()\"]\n        \n        ClosureProps --\u003e Methods\n    end\n    \n    ChildScope --\u003e ClosureProps\n```\n\n### Scope Hierarchy\n\nScopes follow a hierarchical resolution order defined in [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js:1-177]():\n\n1. **Command Scope**: Variables defined within a closure\n2. **Parent Scopes**: Nested parent closures\n3. **Local Variables**: Chat-specific variables in `chat_metadata.variables`\n4. **Global Variables**: User-wide variables in `extension_settings.variables.global`\n\n### Variable Operations\n\nThe `SlashCommandScope` class provides:\n\n- `letVariable(name, value)`: Declare scoped variable\n- `setVariable(name, value)`: Set variable (searches up scope chain)\n- `getVariable(name)`: Get variable value\n- `existsVariable(name)`: Check if variable exists\n- `getMacro(name)`: Get macro value (like variables but for legacy macros)\n\n**Example Closure Usage:**\n\n```javascript\n/if left=x right=y {: \n    /echo Variable x equals y |\n    /setvar count 1\n:} else={:\n    /echo They don't match\n:}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandClosure.js](), [public/scripts/variables.js]()\n\n## Variable Substitution\n\nVariables are substituted during command execution using the `{{variable}}` syntax. The system supports multiple variable types and sources.\n\n### Variable Resolution Order\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    VarRef[\"{{variable}} reference\"]\n    \n    VarRef --\u003e CheckScope[\"Check Scope\u003cbr/\u003eargs._scope.existsVariable()\"]\n    CheckScope --\u003e |\"Found\"| ScopeVar[\"Return scoped variable\"]\n    CheckScope --\u003e |\"Not found\"| CheckLocal[\"Check Local\u003cbr/\u003echat_metadata.variables\"]\n    \n    CheckLocal --\u003e |\"Found\"| LocalVar[\"Return local variable\"]\n    CheckLocal --\u003e |\"Not found\"| CheckGlobal[\"Check Global\u003cbr/\u003eextension_settings.variables.global\"]\n    \n    CheckGlobal --\u003e |\"Found\"| GlobalVar[\"Return global variable\"]\n    CheckGlobal --\u003e |\"Not found\"| ReturnName[\"Return variable name\u003cbr/\u003e(as literal string)\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Variable Commands\"\n        SetLocal[\"/setvar name value\u003cbr/\u003eSet local variable\"]\n        SetGlobal[\"/setglobalvar name value\u003cbr/\u003eSet global variable\"]\n        GetLocal[\"/getvar name\u003cbr/\u003eGet local variable\"]\n        GetGlobal[\"/getglobalvar name\u003cbr/\u003eGet global variable\"]\n    end\n```\n\n### Variable Types\n\n| Scope | Storage Location | Persistence | Access |\n|-------|-----------------|-------------|--------|\n| **Scoped** | `SlashCommandScope.variables` | Within closure execution | Current closure only |\n| **Local** | `chat_metadata.variables` | Per-chat | Current chat |\n| **Global** | `extension_settings.variables.global` | Cross-chat | All chats |\n\n**Variable Commands:**\n\nLocal variables ([public/scripts/variables.js:22-81]()):\n- `/setvar name value`: Set local variable\n- `/getvar name`: Get local variable\n- `/addvar name value`: Add to local variable\n- `/incvar name`: Increment local variable by 1\n- `/decvar name`: Decrement local variable by 1\n\nGlobal variables ([public/scripts/variables.js:83-134]()):\n- `/setglobalvar name value`: Set global variable\n- `/getglobalvar name`: Get global variable\n- `/addglobalvar name value`: Add to global variable\n- `/incglobalvar name`: Increment global variable\n- `/flushvar name`: Delete variable\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands.js]()\n\n## Built-in Commands\n\nSillyTavern provides an extensive set of built-in commands for controlling chat, characters, generation, and system behavior.\n\n### Command Categories\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Commands[\"Built-in Commands\"]\n    \n    Commands --\u003e Chat[\"Chat Control\u003cbr/\u003e/send, /continue,\u003cbr/\u003e/impersonate,\u003cbr/\u003e/stop, /regenerate\"]\n    Commands --\u003e Character[\"Character Ops\u003cbr/\u003e/char, /dupe,\u003cbr/\u003e/setname\"]\n    Commands --\u003e Settings[\"Settings\u003cbr/\u003e/api, /instruct,\u003cbr/\u003e/context, /preset\"]\n    Commands --\u003e Flow[\"Control Flow\u003cbr/\u003e/if, /while,\u003cbr/\u003e/times, /break\"]\n    Commands --\u003e Variables[\"Variables\u003cbr/\u003e/setvar, /getvar,\u003cbr/\u003e/let, /var\"]\n    Commands --\u003e Generation[\"Generation\u003cbr/\u003e/gen, /genraw,\u003cbr/\u003e/trigger\"]\n    Commands --\u003e System[\"System\u003cbr/\u003e/help, /echo,\u003cbr/\u003e/fuzzy, /json\"]\n    \n    style Chat fill:#e1f5ff\n    style Flow fill:#fff4e1\n    style Variables fill:#ffe1f5\n```\n\n### Essential Commands\n\n**Chat Management:**\n- `/send [message]`: Send a user message\n- `/continue [prompt]`: Continue the last assistant message\n- `/impersonate [prompt]`: Generate as the user\n- `/stop`: Stop ongoing generation\n- `/regenerate`: Regenerate last message\n- `/swipe left|right`: Navigate message swipes\n- `/delchat`: Delete current chat\n- `/renamechat [name]`: Rename current chat\n\n**Character Operations:**\n- `/char [name]`: Switch to character\n- `/dupe`: Duplicate current character\n- `/setname [name]`: Change character name temporarily\n- `/resetcharacter`: Reset character to original state\n\n**Control Flow:**\n- `/if left=x right=y command`: Conditional execution\n- `/while left=x right=y command`: Loop while condition true\n- `/times count command`: Repeat command n times\n- `/break`: Break out of loop\n- `/return [value]`: Return from closure with value\n\n**Settings:**\n- `/api [name]`: Connect to API (openai, claude, kobold, etc.)\n- `/instruct [preset]`: Select instruct mode preset\n- `/context [preset]`: Select context template preset\n- `/preset [name]`: Load generation preset\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands.js:216-3994](), [public/scripts/variables.js]()\n\n## Common Usage Patterns\n\n### Pattern 1: Conditional Logic with Variables\n\n```javascript\n// Check if a flag is set\n/getvar mood | /setvar currentMood\n/if left={{currentMood}} right=happy {:\n    /echo The character is happy!\n:} else={:\n    /echo The character is not happy.\n:}\n```\n\n### Pattern 2: Looping with Counter\n\n```javascript\n// Repeat an action 5 times\n/setvar count 0 |\n/while left={{count}} right=5 rule=lt {:\n    /echo Iteration {{count}} |\n    /addvar count 1\n:}\n```\n\n### Pattern 3: API Switching and Generation\n\n```javascript\n// Switch API and generate\n/api openai |\n/setvar model gpt-4 |\n/gen lock=on What is the meaning of life?\n```\n\n### Pattern 4: Character-specific Automation\n\n```javascript\n// Quick reply that depends on character\n/char-find name=Alice |\n/setvar charId {{pipe}} |\n/if left={{charId}} right= rule=not {:\n    /send Hello Alice!\n:}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands.js](), [public/scripts/variables.js]()\n\n## Error Handling\n\nThe slash command system provides structured error handling and user feedback.\n\n### Error Types\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Errors[\"Error Types\"]\n    \n    Errors --\u003e Parser[\"SlashCommandParserError\u003cbr/\u003eSyntax errors\"]\n    Errors --\u003e Execution[\"SlashCommandExecutionError\u003cbr/\u003eRuntime errors\"]\n    Errors --\u003e Abort[\"SlashCommandAbortController\u003cbr/\u003eUser cancellation\"]\n    Errors --\u003e Break[\"SlashCommandBreakController\u003cbr/\u003eLoop breaks\"]\n    \n    Parser --\u003e ParserCases[\"- Unknown command\u003cbr/\u003e- Invalid syntax\u003cbr/\u003e- Unclosed closure\u003cbr/\u003e- Mismatched quotes\"]\n    Execution --\u003e ExecCases[\"- Callback throws\u003cbr/\u003e- Invalid arguments\u003cbr/\u003e- Type mismatch\"]\n    Abort --\u003e AbortCases[\"- User stops generation\u003cbr/\u003e- Timeout\u003cbr/\u003e- Manual abort\"]\n    Break --\u003e BreakCases[\"- /break command\u003cbr/\u003e- Loop exit\"]\n```\n\n**Error Handling Mechanisms:**\n\n1. **Try-Catch in Callbacks**: Command callbacks can throw errors which are caught by the executor\n2. **Abort Controllers**: Each execution has an `SlashCommandAbortController` in `args._abortController`\n3. **Break Controllers**: Loops use `SlashCommandBreakController` to handle `/break` commands\n4. **Toast Notifications**: Errors are displayed to users via `toastr.error()`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandExecutionError.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandAbortController.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandBreakController.js]()\n\n## Integration with Extensions\n\nExtensions can register custom slash commands to extend functionality. Multiple built-in extensions demonstrate this pattern.\n\n### Extension Registration Example\n\nThe caption extension in [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js:263-285]() registers commands:\n\n```javascript\nSlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(SlashCommand.fromProps({\n    name: 'caption',\n    callback: captionCommandCallback,\n    unnamedArgumentList: [\n        SlashCommandArgument.fromProps({\n            description: 'Image URL or message ID',\n            typeList: [ARGUMENT_TYPE.STRING, ARGUMENT_TYPE.NUMBER],\n        })\n    ],\n    helpString: 'Caption an image'\n}));\n```\n\n### Extension Command Categories\n\n| Extension | Commands | Purpose |\n|-----------|----------|---------|\n| **Caption** | `/caption` | Generate image captions |\n| **Attachments** | `/db-*` commands | Manage file attachments |\n| **Quick Reply** | `/qr-*` commands | Manage quick reply buttons |\n| **Stable Diffusion** | `/sd` | Generate images |\n| **Expressions** | `/emote` | Control character expressions |\n| **World Info** | `/wi-*` commands | Manage lorebook entries |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js](), [public/scripts/extensions/attachments/index.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands.js]()\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n### Parser Caching\n\nThe parser implements command caching to avoid re-parsing identical command strings:\n\n- Parsed closures are cached by content hash\n- Repeat executions reuse parsed structure\n- Cache invalidation on command registry changes\n\n### Execution Optimization\n\n1. **Lazy Evaluation**: Closures not executed until needed (e.g., in `/if` branches)\n2. **Pipe Optimization**: Results passed directly between commands without intermediate storage\n3. **Scope Sharing**: Child scopes reference parent scopes rather than copying\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandClosure.js]()\n\n## Auto-completion System\n\nThe slash command system includes a sophisticated auto-completion engine for user assistance.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Input[\"User Types: /cha\"]\n    \n    AC[\"AutoComplete\u003cbr/\u003epublic/scripts/autocomplete/AutoComplete.js\"]\n    \n    AC --\u003e CommandMatch[\"Match Commands\u003cbr/\u003eFuzzy search in parser.commands\"]\n    AC --\u003e ArgMatch[\"Match Arguments\u003cbr/\u003eCheck current argument type\"]\n    AC --\u003e EnumMatch[\"Match Enums\u003cbr/\u003eCall enumProvider()\"]\n    \n    CommandMatch --\u003e Suggestions[\"Suggestions List\"]\n    ArgMatch --\u003e Suggestions\n    EnumMatch --\u003e Suggestions\n    \n    Suggestions --\u003e UI[\"Dropdown UI\u003cbr/\u003eClick or Tab to complete\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"AUTOCOMPLETE_STATE\"\n        IDLE[\"IDLE: Not showing\"]\n        LOADING[\"LOADING: Fetching enums\"]\n        SHOWING[\"SHOWING: Dropdown visible\"]\n    end\n    \n    UI -.-\u003e SHOWING\n```\n\n**Auto-completion Features:**\n\n- Command name completion with fuzzy matching\n- Argument name completion for named arguments\n- Enum value completion from `enumList` or `enumProvider`\n- Context-aware suggestions based on cursor position\n- Keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Arrow keys)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/autocomplete/AutoComplete.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js](),
