[public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandDebugController.js](public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandDebugController.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe Quick Reply System provides a flexible command automation and button interface that allows users to create customizable buttons for frequently-used slash commands and messages. Quick Replies can be organized into sets, executed automatically based on events, and debugged with integrated breakpoint support.\n\nFor information about the underlying slash command execution system, see [Slash Command System](#7.1). For variable and macro systems, see [Variables, Macros, and Scripting](#7.3).\n\n## Overview\n\nQuick Replies are user-configurable buttons that execute slash commands or send messages. The system supports:\n\n- Visual button interface with icons and labels\n- Hierarchical organization via Quick Reply Sets\n- Scoped configuration (global, chat-specific, character-specific)\n- Event-driven auto-execution on 8+ trigger types\n- Context menus for nested command structures\n- Integrated debugging with breakpoints and step execution\n- Programmatic access through slash commands and JavaScript API\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:1-325]()\n\n## Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Extension[\"Quick Reply Extension\"]\n        Index[\"index.js\u003cbr/\u003eBootstrap \u0026 Event Handlers\"]\n        Settings[\"QuickReplySettings\u003cbr/\u003eConfiguration Manager\"]\n        Api[\"QuickReplyApi\u003cbr/\u003ePublic API\"]\n        \n        subgraph CoreModels[\"Core Models\"]\n            QR[\"QuickReply\u003cbr/\u003eIndividual Button/Command\"]\n            QRSet[\"QuickReplySet\u003cbr/\u003eCollection of QRs\"]\n            QRConfig[\"QuickReplyConfig\u003cbr/\u003eScope Configuration\"]\n            QRLink[\"QuickReplyContextLink\u003cbr/\u003eContext Menu Entry\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph Handlers[\"Handler Layer\"]\n            SlashHandler[\"SlashCommandHandler\u003cbr/\u003eRegisters /qr-* commands\"]\n            AutoExec[\"AutoExecuteHandler\u003cbr/\u003eEvent-based triggers\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph UI[\"UI Components\"]\n            ButtonUi[\"ButtonUi\u003cbr/\u003eRenders QR buttons\"]\n            SettingsUi[\"SettingsUi\u003cbr/\u003eSettings panel\"]\n        end\n    end\n    \n    subgraph SlashSystem[\"Slash Command System\"]\n        Parser[\"SlashCommandParser\"]\n        Executor[\"SlashCommandExecutor\"]\n        Closure[\"SlashCommandClosure\"]\n        DebugCtrl[\"SlashCommandDebugController\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph CoreST[\"SillyTavern Core\"]\n        EventSource[\"eventSource\u003cbr/\u003eEvent Bus\"]\n        ExtSettings[\"extension_settings\"]\n        ChatMeta[\"chat_metadata\"]\n        Characters[\"characters array\"]\n    end\n    \n    Index --\u003e Settings\n    Index --\u003e Api\n    Index --\u003e AutoExec\n    Index --\u003e ButtonUi\n    Index --\u003e SettingsUi\n    \n    Api --\u003e QRSet\n    Api --\u003e QR\n    Api --\u003e SettingsUi\n    \n    Settings --\u003e QRConfig\n    QRConfig --\u003e QRSet\n    QRSet --\u003e QR\n    QR --\u003e QRLink\n    \n    Index --\u003e SlashHandler\n    SlashHandler --\u003e Api\n    \n    AutoExec --\u003e Settings\n    AutoExec --\u003e QR\n    \n    QR --\u003e Closure\n    QR --\u003e DebugCtrl\n    \n    Index --\u003e EventSource\n    Settings --\u003e ExtSettings\n    Settings --\u003e ChatMeta\n    Settings --\u003e Characters\n    \n    SlashHandler --\u003e Parser\n    QR --\u003e Executor\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:1-60](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/api/QuickReplyApi.js:1-40]()\n\n## Configuration Hierarchy\n\nThe Quick Reply System uses a three-tier configuration hierarchy that determines which Quick Reply Sets are active and visible:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Hierarchy[\"Configuration Hierarchy\"]\n        Global[\"Global Config\u003cbr/\u003eQuickReplySettings.config\u003cbr/\u003eScope: 'global'\"]\n        Chat[\"Chat Config\u003cbr/\u003eQuickReplySettings.chatConfig\u003cbr/\u003eScope: 'chat'\"]\n        Char[\"Character Config\u003cbr/\u003eQuickReplySettings.charConfig\u003cbr/\u003eScope: 'character'\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Storage[\"Persistence Layer\"]\n        ExtSettings[\"extension_settings.quickReplyV2\"]\n        ChatMetadata[\"chat_metadata.quickReply\"]\n        CharConfigs[\"settings.characterConfigs[avatar]\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Model[\"Configuration Model\"]\n        Config[\"QuickReplyConfig\"]\n        SetList[\"setList: QuickReplySetLink[]\"]\n        SetLink[\"QuickReplySetLink\u003cbr/\u003e{set, isVisible}\"]\n        Set[\"QuickReplySet\"]\n    end\n    \n    Global --\u003e ExtSettings\n    Chat --\u003e ChatMetadata\n    Char --\u003e CharConfigs\n    \n    Global --\u003e Config\n    Chat --\u003e Config\n    Char --\u003e Config\n    \n    Config --\u003e SetList\n    SetList --\u003e SetLink\n    SetLink --\u003e Set\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:110-174](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReplySettings.js]()\n\n### Configuration Scopes\n\n| Scope | Storage Location | When Active | Use Case |\n|-------|-----------------|-------------|----------|\n| **Global** | `extension_settings.quickReplyV2.config` | Always | Default QR sets available across all chats |\n| **Chat** | `chat_metadata.quickReply` | Current chat only | Chat-specific commands and workflows |\n| **Character** | `settings.characterConfigs[avatar]` | Character selected | Character-specific interactions |\n\nThe `QuickReplyConfig` class manages each scope's active sets via a `setList` array containing `QuickReplySetLink` objects. Each link references a `QuickReplySet` and stores visibility state.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:146-174](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:260-277]()\n\n## Quick Reply Sets\n\nA `QuickReplySet` is a named collection of Quick Reply buttons with shared behavior settings:\n\n### QuickReplySet Structure\n\n| Property | Type | Description |\n|----------|------|-------------|\n| `name` | string | Unique identifier for the set |\n| `qrList` | QuickReply[] | Array of Quick Reply objects |\n| `disableSend` | boolean | Places output in input box instead of sending |\n| `placeBeforeInput` | boolean | Prepends to existing input instead of replacing |\n| `injectInput` | boolean | Appends user input to command automatically |\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    QRSet[\"QuickReplySet\"]\n    QR1[\"QuickReply #1\"]\n    QR2[\"QuickReply #2\"]\n    QRN[\"QuickReply #N\"]\n    \n    QRSet --\u003e|qrList| QR1\n    QRSet --\u003e|qrList| QR2\n    QRSet --\u003e|qrList| QRN\n    \n    QRSet --\u003e|\"disableSend\u003cbr/\u003eplaceBeforeInput\u003cbr/\u003einjectInput\"| Behavior[\"Behavior Settings\"]\n```\n\nSets are stored globally and loaded from server settings at extension initialization. The static `QuickReplySet.list` maintains all available sets.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReplySet.js](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:59-108]()\n\n## Quick Reply Objects\n\nThe `QuickReply` class represents individual command buttons with execution and presentation logic:\n\n### Core Properties\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Display[\"Display Properties\"]\n        Icon[\"icon: string\u003cbr/\u003eFontAwesome class\"]\n        Label[\"label: string\u003cbr/\u003eButton text\"]\n        ShowLabel[\"showLabel: boolean\u003cbr/\u003eShow text with icon\"]\n        Title[\"title: string\u003cbr/\u003eTooltip text\"]\n        Hidden[\"isHidden: boolean\u003cbr/\u003eHidden from UI\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Execution[\"Execution Properties\"]\n        Message[\"message: string\u003cbr/\u003eCommand/text to execute\"]\n        Context[\"contextList: QuickReplyContextLink[]\u003cbr/\u003eContext menu entries\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph AutoExec[\"Auto-Execution Flags\"]\n        Startup[\"executeOnStartup\"]\n        User[\"executeOnUser\"]\n        AI[\"executeOnAi\"]\n        ChatChange[\"executeOnChatChange\"]\n        NewChat[\"executeOnNewChat\"]\n        GroupDraft[\"executeOnGroupMemberDraft\"]\n        BeforeGen[\"executeBeforeGeneration\"]\n        AutoID[\"automationId: string\u003cbr/\u003eWorld Info trigger\"]\n    end\n    \n    QR[\"QuickReply Instance\"]\n    \n    QR --\u003e Display\n    QR --\u003e Execution\n    QR --\u003e AutoExec\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:20-58]()\n\n### Execution Methods\n\nThe `QuickReply` class provides multiple execution entry points:\n\n| Method | Signature | Purpose |\n|--------|-----------|---------|\n| `execute(args, autoExec, chain, options)` | Returns `Promise\u003cSlashCommandClosureResult\u003e` | Main execution with full options |\n| `onExecute()` | Callback set by system | Direct execution trigger |\n| `onDebug(qr)` | Async generator | Debug execution with stepping |\n\nThe `message` property contains STscript code that is parsed and executed through the `SlashCommandClosure` system.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:1155-1264]()\n\n## Auto-Execution System\n\nThe `AutoExecuteHandler` monitors application events and triggers Quick Replies automatically based on their execution flags:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Event as \"Event Source\"\n    participant Index as \"index.js\u003cbr/\u003eEvent Handlers\"\n    participant AutoExec as \"AutoExecuteHandler\"\n    participant QR as \"QuickReply\"\n    \n    Event-\u003e\u003eIndex: CHAT_CHANGED\n    Index-\u003e\u003eAutoExec: handleChatChanged()\n    AutoExec-\u003e\u003eAutoExec: checkExecute()\u003cbr/\u003evalidate enabled \u0026 not blocked\n    AutoExec-\u003e\u003eAutoExec: getCommands('executeOnChatChange')\n    AutoExec-\u003e\u003eAutoExec: collect from global/chat/char configs\n    \n    loop For each matching QR\n        AutoExec-\u003e\u003eAutoExec: push preventAutoExecute to stack\n        AutoExec-\u003e\u003eQR: execute({isAutoExecute: true})\n        QR--\u003e\u003eAutoExec: result\n        AutoExec-\u003e\u003eAutoExec: pop from stack\n    end\n    \n    AutoExec--\u003e\u003eIndex: complete\n```\n\n### Event Triggers\n\n| Event | Trigger Point | Handler | EventSource Event |\n|-------|--------------|---------|-------------------|\n| Startup | App ready | `handleStartup()` | `APP_READY` |\n| User Message | After user sends | `handleUser()` | `USER_MESSAGE_RENDERED` |\n| AI Message | After AI responds | `handleAi()` | `CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED` |\n| Chat Change | Chat loaded | `handleChatChanged()` | `CHAT_CHANGED` |\n| New Chat | Chat created | `handleNewChat()` | `CHAT_CREATED`, `GROUP_CHAT_CREATED` |\n| Group Draft | Member selected | `handleGroupMemberDraft()` | `GROUP_MEMBER_DRAFTED` |\n| Before Generation | Pre-generation | `handleBeforeGeneration()` | `GENERATION_AFTER_COMMANDS` |\n| World Info | WI activated | `handleWIActivation(entries)` | `WORLD_INFO_ACTIVATED` |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:218-324](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/AutoExecuteHandler.js:1-108]()\n\n### Recursive Execution Prevention\n\nThe `preventAutoExecute` flag prevents infinite loops when one Quick Reply triggers another:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Start[\"User Action Triggers QR-A\"]\n    Stack[\"preventAutoExecuteStack\u003cbr/\u003epushed: false\"]\n    ExecA[\"Execute QR-A\u003cbr/\u003epreventAutoExecute = true\"]\n    CheckB[\"QR-B has executeOnUser = true\"]\n    StackTrue[\"preventAutoExecuteStack\u003cbr/\u003epushed: true\"]\n    Block[\"Block QR-B execution\"]\n    \n    Start --\u003e Stack\n    Stack --\u003e ExecA\n    ExecA --\u003e CheckB\n    CheckB --\u003e StackTrue\n    StackTrue --\u003e Block\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/AutoExecuteHandler.js:8-36]()\n\n## Context Menus\n\nQuick Replies can define context menus that display additional Quick Replies on right-click or via the expander button:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    QR[\"QuickReply\u003cbr/\u003e'Main Button'\"]\n    CtxList[\"contextList: QuickReplyContextLink[]\"]\n    Link1[\"QuickReplyContextLink #1\u003cbr/\u003e{set: QRSet-A, isChained: false}\"]\n    Link2[\"QuickReplyContextLink #2\u003cbr/\u003e{set: QRSet-B, isChained: true}\"]\n    \n    SetA[\"QuickReplySet 'QRSet-A'\"]\n    SetB[\"QuickReplySet 'QRSet-B'\"]\n    \n    QR --\u003e CtxList\n    CtxList --\u003e Link1\n    CtxList --\u003e Link2\n    \n    Link1 --\u003e SetA\n    Link2 --\u003e SetB\n    \n    SetA --\u003e|qrList| QRA1[\"QR: Option 1\"]\n    SetA --\u003e|qrList| QRA2[\"QR: Option 2\"]\n    \n    SetB --\u003e|qrList| QRB1[\"QR: Chained 1\"]\n```\n\n### Chaining Behavior\n\nThe `isChained` property controls execution order:\n\n- **Chained (`true`)**: Main QR executes first, then selected context QR\n- **Unchained (`false`)**: Only selected context QR executes\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:86-89](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/ui/ctx/ContextMenu.js]()\n\n## Debugging System\n\nThe Quick Reply editor provides integrated debugging with breakpoints, stepping, and state inspection:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Editor[\"QR Editor (qrEditor.html)\"]\n        Message[\"Message Textarea\u003cbr/\u003ewith syntax highlighting\"]\n        ExecBtn[\"Execute Button\"]\n        PauseBtn[\"Pause Button\"]\n        StopBtn[\"Stop Button\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Debug[\"Debug Controls\"]\n        Resume[\"Resume\u003cbr/\u003eContinue execution\"]\n        Step[\"Step Over\u003cbr/\u003eNext command\"]\n        StepInto[\"Step Into\u003cbr/\u003eEnter closure\"]\n        StepOut[\"Step Out\u003cbr/\u003eExit closure\"]\n        State[\"Debug State Display\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph SlashDebug[\"SlashCommandDebugController\"]\n        Stack[\"stack: SlashCommandClosure[]\"]\n        CmdStack[\"cmdStack: SlashCommandExecutor[]\"]\n        StepStack[\"stepStack: boolean[]\"]\n        IsStepping[\"isStepping: boolean\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph QRDebug[\"QuickReply Debug Methods\"]\n        OnDebug[\"onDebug(qr)\u003cbr/\u003eAsync generator\"]\n        AbortCtrl[\"abortController\"]\n        DebugCtrl[\"debugController\"]\n    end\n    \n    ExecBtn --\u003e QRDebug\n    PauseBtn --\u003e SlashDebug\n    StopBtn --\u003e AbortCtrl\n    \n    Resume --\u003e SlashDebug\n    Step --\u003e SlashDebug\n    StepInto --\u003e SlashDebug\n    StepOut --\u003e SlashDebug\n    \n    QRDebug --\u003e SlashDebug\n    SlashDebug --\u003e State\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:1265-1539](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandDebugController.js:1-84]()\n\n### Breakpoint System\n\nBreakpoints are set by `Ctrl+Alt+Click` or `F9` in the editor. The system uses `SlashCommandBreakPoint` markers in the executor list:\n\n1. Parser creates `SlashCommandBreakPoint` objects at marked lines\n2. During execution, `SlashCommandClosure.executeDirect()` yields at breakpoints\n3. `SlashCommandDebugController` pauses and waits for user action\n4. UI highlights current command and displays variable state\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:876-932](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandBreakPoint.js:1-4]()\n\n### Debug State Display\n\nThe debug state panel shows:\n- Current command being executed\n- Named argument values\n- Unnamed argument values\n- Variable scope state\n- Closure stack depth\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:1417-1539]()\n\n## Slash Command Integration\n\nThe `SlashCommandHandler` registers commands for programmatic Quick Reply management:\n\n### Command Reference\n\n| Command | Arguments | Description |\n|---------|-----------|-------------|\n| `/qr` | `number` | Execute QR by index |\n| `/qr-set` | `name` `visible?` | Toggle global set |\n| `/qr-set-on` | `name` `visible?` | Activate global set |\n| `/qr-set-off` | `name` | Deactivate global set |\n| `/qr-chat-set` | `name` `visible?` | Toggle chat set |\n| `/qr-chat-set-on` | `name` `visible?` | Activate chat set |\n| `/qr-chat-set-off` | `name` | Deactivate chat set |\n| `/qr-set-list` | `type?` | List sets (all/global/chat) |\n| `/qr-list` | `set` | List QRs in set |\n| `/qr-create` | `set=` `label=` `message` | Create new QR |\n| `/qr-update` | `set=` `label=` `newlabel?=` `message?` | Update existing QR |\n| `/qr-delete` | `set=` `label=` | Delete QR |\n| `/qr-get` | `set=` `label=\\|id=` | Get QR properties |\n| `/qr-contextadd` | `set=` `label=` `newset=` `chain?=` | Add context menu entry |\n| `/qr-contextdel` | `set=` `label=` `newset=` | Remove context menu entry |\n| `/qr-contextclear` | `set=` `label=` | Clear all context entries |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/SlashCommandHandler.js:28-437]()\n\n### Enum Providers\n\nThe system provides specialized enum providers for autocomplete:\n\n- `qrSets`: All Quick Reply Set names\n- `qrEntries`: QRs within a specified set (uses `set=` argument)\n- `qrIds`: QR IDs within a specified set\n- `qrExecutables`: All QRs as `SetName.QRLabel` for use with `/run`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/SlashCommandHandler.js:43-78]()\n\n## UI Components\n\n### Button Rendering\n\nThe `ButtonUi` class renders Quick Reply buttons in the chat interface:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    ButtonUi[\"ButtonUi\"]\n    Settings[\"QuickReplySettings\"]\n    Bar[\"#qr--bar\u003cbr/\u003eButton container\"]\n    \n    subgraph Rendering[\"Render Pipeline\"]\n        Refresh[\"refresh()\"]\n        GetConfigs[\"Collect configs\u003cbr/\u003eglobal, chat, char\"]\n        RenderSets[\"Render each set\"]\n        RenderQRs[\"Render QR buttons\"]\n    end\n    \n    ButtonUi --\u003e Settings\n    ButtonUi --\u003e Bar\n    Refresh --\u003e GetConfigs\n    GetConfigs --\u003e RenderSets\n    RenderSets --\u003e RenderQRs\n    \n    RenderQRs --\u003e Button1[\"QR Button 1\"]\n    RenderQRs --\u003e Button2[\"QR Button 2\"]\n    RenderQRs --\u003e ButtonN[\"QR Button N\"]\n```\n\nEach button renders with:\n- Icon (if set) via FontAwesome classes\n- Label text (if no icon or `showLabel` is true)\n- Context menu expander (if `hasContext` is true)\n- Click handler → `QuickReply.execute()`\n- Right-click handler → `ContextMenu.show()`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/ui/ButtonUi.js](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:112-169]()\n\n### Settings UI\n\nThe `SettingsUi` class manages the configuration panel for Quick Reply Sets:\n\n- Set list management (add, remove, reorder)\n- Set-specific settings (disableSend, placeBeforeInput, injectInput)\n- Individual Quick Reply editing\n- Import/export functionality\n- Drag-and-drop reordering via jQuery UI Sortable\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/ui/SettingsUi.js]()\n\n### Quick Reply Editor\n\nThe editor modal (`qrEditor.html`) provides a full-featured editing experience:\n\n| Section | Elements | Purpose |\n|---------|----------|---------|\n| **Labels** | Icon picker, label input, title input | Button appearance |\n| **Message** | Textarea with syntax highlighting | Command editing |\n| **Editor Settings** | Word wrap, tab size, Ctrl+Enter shortcut | Editor preferences |\n| **Context Menu** | Set selector, chaining checkbox | Configure context menu |\n| **Auto-Execute** | 8 trigger checkboxes, automation ID | Event configuration |\n| **Testing** | Execute, pause, stop buttons | Test execution |\n| **Debug Controls** | Resume, step over/into/out, minimize | Debug stepping |\n\nThe editor uses a layered textarea approach for syntax highlighting:\n- Transparent textarea for input (grid position 1/1)\n- Pre-formatted code with Highlight.js for display (grid position 1/1)\n- Synchronized scrolling between layers\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/html/qrEditor.html:1-162](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:390-1154]()\n\n## Data Persistence\n\n### Save Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant QR as \"QuickReply\"\n    participant Set as \"QuickReplySet\"\n    participant Settings as \"QuickReplySettings\"\n    participant Server as \"Server API\"\n    \n    QR-\u003e\u003eQR: updateMessage(value)\n    QR-\u003e\u003eSet: onUpdate()\n    Set-\u003e\u003eSet: save()\n    Set-\u003e\u003eServer: POST /api/settings/save\n    Note over Server: Save to quickReplyPresets\n    \n    Settings-\u003e\u003eSettings: save()\n    Settings-\u003e\u003eServer: POST /api/settings/save\n    Note over Server: Save to extension_settings.quickReplyV2\n    Note over Server: Save to chat_metadata.quickReply\n```\n\n### Storage Structure\n\n| Storage Key | Scope | Contains |\n|-------------|-------|----------|\n| `settings.quickReplyPresets` | Server | Array of `QuickReplySet` objects (migrated from v1 format) |\n| `extension_settings.quickReplyV2` | Client | `QuickReplySettings` with global config |\n| `chat_metadata.quickReply` | Chat | Chat-specific `QuickReplyConfig` |\n| `settings.characterConfigs[avatar]` | Character | Character-specific `QuickReplyConfig` |\n\n### Migration\n\nThe system automatically migrates v1 Quick Reply data to v2 format on load:\n\n- Maps old property names (e.g., `mes` → `message`, `autoExecute_appStartup` → `executeOnStartup`)\n- Converts `quickReplySlots` array to `qrList` with ID assignment\n- Converts `contextMenu` to `contextList` with `QuickReplyContextLink` objects\n- Sets `version = 2` to prevent re-migration\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:59-108](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:110-135]()\n\n## JavaScript API Access\n\nThe `QuickReplyApi` instance is globally accessible:\n\n```javascript\n// Stored in: globalThis.quickReplyApi\n// Also: globalThis.executeQuickReplyByName(name, args, options)\n\n// Example: Execute a Quick Reply\nawait quickReplyApi.executeQuickReply('MySet', 'MyButton', {}, {});\n\n// Example: Create a new Quick Reply\nconst qr = quickReplyApi.createQuickReply('MySet', 'NewButton', {\n    message: '/echo Hello World',\n    executeOnStartup: true,\n    isHidden: false\n});\n\n// Example: Add a set to global config\nquickReplyApi.addGlobalSet('UtilitySet', true);\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/index.js:188-221](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/api/QuickReplyApi.js:23-419]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"3d:Ta15b,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Variables, Macros, and [public/scripts/tool-calling.js](public/scripts/tool-calling.js)\n- [src/constants.js](src/constants.js)\n- [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js](src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js)\n- [src/endpoints/openai.js](src/endpoints/openai.js)\n- [src/endpoints/secrets.js](src/endpoints/secrets.js)\n- [src/prompt-converters.js](src/prompt-converters.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThe Variables, Macros, and Scripting system provides the foundation for automation and dynamic content in SillyTavern. Variables store data with different scopes (scoped, local, global), macros perform text substitution using `{{macro}}` syntax, and `SlashCommandClosure` enables complex scripting with control flow, functions, and state management. Together, these systems form a complete scripting environment for automating interactions, building complex workflows, and creating dynamic responses.\n\nFor information about Slash Commands and control flow, see [Slash Command System](#7.1). For automation patterns using these systems, see [Quick Reply System](#7.2).\n\n---\n\n## Variable Types and Storage Architecture\n\nSillyTavern implements a three-tier variable storage hierarchy with distinct scopes and persistence characteristics.\n\n### Variable Storage Hierarchy\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Variable Resolution Chain\"\n        Lookup[\"Variable Lookup Request\u003cbr/\u003e(resolveVariable)\"]\n        ScopeCheck{\"Exists in\u003cbr/\u003eScope?\"}\n        LocalCheck{\"Exists in\u003cbr/\u003eLocal?\"}\n        GlobalCheck{\"Exists in\u003cbr/\u003eGlobal?\"}\n        ReturnVar[\"Return Variable Value\"]\n        ReturnLiteral[\"Return String Literal\"]\n        \n        Lookup --\u003e ScopeCheck\n        ScopeCheck --\u003e|Yes| ReturnVar\n        ScopeCheck --\u003e|No| LocalCheck\n        LocalCheck --\u003e|Yes| ReturnVar\n        LocalCheck --\u003e|No| GlobalCheck\n        GlobalCheck --\u003e|Yes| ReturnVar\n        GlobalCheck --\u003e|No| ReturnLiteral\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Storage Layers\"\n        ScopeStorage[\"SlashCommandScope\u003cbr/\u003evariables: object\"]\n        LocalStorage[\"chat_metadata.variables\u003cbr/\u003ePer-chat persistence\"]\n        GlobalStorage[\"extension_settings.variables.global\u003cbr/\u003eApplication-wide persistence\"]\n    end\n    \n    ScopeCheck -.-\u003e ScopeStorage\n    LocalCheck -.-\u003e LocalStorage\n    GlobalCheck -.-\u003e GlobalStorage\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:218-232](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js:49-108]()\n\n### Scoped Variables\n\nScoped variables exist within a `SlashCommandScope` and are automatically cleaned up when the scope terminates. They are created with the `/let` command and accessed/modified with the `/var` command.\n\n| Property | Value |\n|----------|-------|\n| Storage | `SlashCommandScope.variables` object |\n| Lifetime | Execution scope duration |\n| Persistence | None (memory only) |\n| Hierarchy | Parent scope traversal via `scope.parent` |\n| Creation | `/let key=value` |\n| Access | `/var key` or `scope.getVariable(key)` |\n\nScoped variables support hierarchical lookup through parent scopes, allowing nested closures and command chains to access variables from outer contexts.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js:4-109](), [public/scripts/variables.js:841-895]()\n\n### Local Variables\n\nLocal variables are chat-scoped and persist to the chat's metadata file. They are stored in `chat_metadata.variables` and are saved when the chat is saved.\n\n| Property | Value |\n|----------|-------|\n| Storage | `chat_metadata.variables` object |\n| Persistence | JSONL chat file |\n| Scope | Current chat only |\n| Functions | `getLocalVariable()`, `setLocalVariable()` |\n| Slash Commands | `/setvar`, `/getvar`, `/addvar`, `/incvar`, `/decvar` |\n| Macros | `{{setvar::}}`, `{{getvar::}}`, `{{incvar::}}`, `{{decvar::}}`, `{{addvar::}}` |\n\nLocal variables are automatically initialized if `chat_metadata.variables` does not exist [public/scripts/variables.js:23-25]().\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:22-81](), [public/scripts/variables.js:136-210]()\n\n### Global Variables\n\nGlobal variables are application-scoped and persist to the extension settings. They are stored in `extension_settings.variables.global` and are available across all chats.\n\n| Property | Value |\n|----------|-------|\n| Storage | `extension_settings.variables.global` object |\n| Persistence | `settings.json` |\n| Scope | Application-wide |\n| Functions | `getGlobalVariable()`, `setGlobalVariable()` |\n| Slash Commands | `/setglobalvar`, `/getglobalvar`, `/addglobalvar`, `/incglobalvar`, `/decglobalvar` |\n| Macros | `{{setglobalvar::}}`, `{{getglobalvar::}}`, `{{incglobalvar::}}`, `{{decglobalvar::}}`, `{{addglobalvar::}}` |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:83-134](), [public/scripts/variables.js:166-210]()\n\n---\n\n## Variable Operations\n\n### Basic Variable Operations\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Get Operations\"\n        GetLocal[\"getLocalVariable(name, args)\"]\n        GetGlobal[\"getGlobalVariable(name, args)\"]\n        GetScope[\"scope.getVariable(key, index)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Set Operations\"\n        SetLocal[\"setLocalVariable(name, value, args)\"]\n        SetGlobal[\"setGlobalVariable(name, value, args)\"]\n        SetScope[\"scope.setVariable(key, value, index, as)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Arithmetic Operations\"\n        Add[\"addLocalVariable(name, value)\u003cbr/\u003eaddGlobalVariable(name, value)\"]\n        Inc[\"incrementLocalVariable(name)\u003cbr/\u003eincrementGlobalVariable(name)\"]\n        Dec[\"decrementLocalVariable(name)\u003cbr/\u003edecrementGlobalVariable(name)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Deletion\"\n        DelLocal[\"deleteLocalVariable(name)\"]\n        DelGlobal[\"deleteGlobalVariable(name)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Persistence\"\n        SaveMeta[\"saveMetadataDebounced()\"]\n        SaveSettings[\"saveSettingsDebounced()\"]\n    end\n    \n    SetLocal --\u003e SaveMeta\n    SetGlobal --\u003e SaveSettings\n    DelLocal --\u003e SaveMeta\n    DelGlobal --\u003e SaveSettings\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:22-134](), [public/scripts/variables.js:196-210](), [public/scripts/variables.js:611-636]()\n\n### Array and Object Indexing\n\nVariables support indexing into arrays and objects using the `index` argument. The system automatically handles JSON parsing and serialization.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Index Operations\"\n        SetWithIndex[\"setLocalVariable(name, value, {index})\"]\n        ParseJSON[\"JSON.parse(currentValue)\"]\n        CheckNumeric{\"index is\u003cbr/\u003enumeric?\"}\n        SetArrayIndex[\"value[numIndex] = convertValueType(value, as)\"]\n        SetObjectIndex[\"value[index] = convertValueType(value, as)\"]\n        Stringify[\"JSON.stringify(value)\"]\n        SaveVariable[\"Store serialized value\"]\n        \n        SetWithIndex --\u003e ParseJSON\n        ParseJSON --\u003e CheckNumeric\n        CheckNumeric --\u003e|Yes| SetArrayIndex\n        CheckNumeric --\u003e|No| SetObjectIndex\n        SetArrayIndex --\u003e Stringify\n        SetObjectIndex --\u003e Stringify\n        Stringify --\u003e SaveVariable\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Type Conversion\"\n        ConvertValue[\"convertValueType(value, as)\"]\n        AsString[\"as='string'\"]\n        AsNumber[\"as='number'\"]\n        AsBoolean[\"as='boolean'\"]\n        AsArray[\"as='array'\"]\n        AsObject[\"as='object'\"]\n        \n        ConvertValue --\u003e AsString\n        ConvertValue --\u003e AsNumber\n        ConvertValue --\u003e AsBoolean\n        ConvertValue --\u003e AsArray\n        ConvertValue --\u003e AsObject\n    end\n```\n\n**Example:**\n```javascript\n// Set an array element\n/setvar key=myArray index=0 as=number 42\n\n// Set an object property\n/setvar key=myObject index=name as=string Alice\n\n// Get an array element\n/getvar key=myArray index=0\n\n// Get an object property\n/getvar key=myObject index=name\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:57-78](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js:59-84]()\n\n### Variable Type Conversion\n\nVariables are automatically converted to appropriate types when retrieved. Numeric strings are converted to numbers, while non-numeric strings remain strings.\n\n| Input | Output Type | Conversion Logic |\n|-------|-------------|------------------|\n| `\"42\"` | `number` | `Number(\"42\") = 42` |\n| `\"hello\"` | `string` | Remains string |\n| `\"\"` | `string` | Empty string |\n| `\"  \"` | `string` | Whitespace string |\n| Array/Object | `string` | JSON serialized |\n\nThe conversion happens at retrieval time [public/scripts/variables.js:45](), [public/scripts/variables.js:102]().\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:45-46](), [public/scripts/variables.js:100-102]()\n\n---\n\n## Macro System\n\nThe macro system performs text substitution using `{{macro}}` syntax. Macros are evaluated during prompt construction, message sending, and various other text processing contexts.\n\n### Macro Evaluation Pipeline\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph MacroEvalFlow[\"Macro Evaluation Flow\"]\n        Input[\"Input String with Macros\"]\n        PreEnv[\"Pre-Environment Macros\u003cbr/\u003eLegacy \u0026lt;USER\u0026gt;, \u0026lt;BOT\u0026gt;\u003cbr/\u003e{{newline}}, {{trim}}\u003cbr/\u003e{{roll}}, Instruct Macros\u003cbr/\u003eVariable Macros\"]\n        EnvMacros[\"Environment Macros\u003cbr/\u003eCustom registered macros\u003cbr/\u003eMacrosParser.registerMacro\"]\n        PostEnv[\"Post-Environment Macros\u003cbr/\u003e{{time}}, {{date}}\u003cbr/\u003e{{lastMessage}}\u003cbr/\u003e{{random}}, {{pick}}\u003cbr/\u003eMath \u0026 utility macros\"]\n        Output[\"Substituted String\"]\n        \n        Input --\u003e PreEnv\n        PreEnv --\u003e EnvMacros\n        EnvMacros --\u003e PostEnv\n        PostEnv --\u003e Output\n    end\n    \n    subgraph EvalFunction[\"evaluateMacros Function\"]\n        EvalFunc[\"evaluateMacros(content, env, postProcessFn)\"]\n        ShortCircuit{\"content.includes\u003cbr/\u003e('{{{')?\"}\n        RegexReplace[\"content.replace(macro.regex, macro.replace)\"]\n        \n        EvalFunc --\u003e ShortCircuit\n        ShortCircuit --\u003e|Yes| RegexReplace\n        ShortCircuit --\u003e|No| Output\n    end\n```\n\n**Substitution Functions:**\n\n| Function | Location | Purpose |\n|----------|----------|---------|\n| `substituteParams(content)` | [script.js]() | Basic macro substitution for character names, etc. |\n| `substituteParamsExtended(content, vars)` | [script.js]() | Advanced substitution with additional variable context |\n| `evaluateMacros(content, env, postProcessFn)` | [macros.js:609-708]() | Main macro evaluation engine |\n\nThe `substituteParams` function is called throughout the codebase for basic text replacement, while `substituteParamsExtended` allows passing additional variables as context (e.g., `{lastChatMessage: chat[chat.length-1].mes}`).\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/macros.js:609-708](), [public/scripts/openai.js:30-31](), [public/scripts/slash-commands.js:72]()\n\n### Built-in Macros\n\n#### Chat Context Macros\n\n| Macro | Description | Returns |\n|-------|-------------|---------|\n| `{{lastMessage}}` | Last message in chat | String |\n| `{{lastMessageId}}` | ID of last message | Number |\n| `{{lastUserMessage}}` | Last message from user | String |\n| `{{lastCharMessage}}` | Last message from character | String |\n| `{{lastSwipeId}}` | 1-based ID of last swipe | Number |\n| `{{currentSwipeId}}` | 1-based ID of current swipe | Number |\n| `{{firstIncludedMessageId}}` | ID of first message in context | Number |\n| `{{firstDisplayedMessageId}}` | ID of first displayed message | Number |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/macros.js:643-650]()\n\n#### Date and Time Macros\n\n| Macro | Description | Example Output |\n|-------|-------------|----------------|\n| `{{time}}` | Current time (localized) | \"2:30 PM\" |\n| `{{date}}` | Current date (localized) | \"January 15, 2024\" |\n| `{{weekday}}` | Current weekday | \"Monday\" |\n| `{{isotime}}` | ISO time format | \"14:30\" |\n| `{{isodate}}` | ISO date format | \"2024-01-15\" |\n| `{{datetimeformat FORMAT}}` | Custom format | `{{datetimeformat YYYY-MM-DD}}` |\n| `{{time_UTC+X}}` | Time in UTC offset | `{{time_UTC-5}}` |\n| `{{timeDiff::TIME1::TIME2}}` | Humanized difference | \"2 hours ago\" |\n| `{{idle_duration}}` | Time since last message | \"5 minutes\" |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/macros.js:653-662](), [public/scripts/macros.js:454-484]()\n\n#### Random and Selection Macros\n\n| Macro | Description | Example |\n|-------|-------------|---------|\n| `{{random::A::B::C}}` | Pick random item (entropy-based) | `{{random::red::blue::green}}` |\n| `{{random::A,B,C}}` | Pick random (comma-separated) | `{{random::apple, banana, cherry}}` |\n| `{{pick::A::B::C}}` | Pick with consistent seed | `{{pick::heads::tails}}` |\n| `{{roll::FORMULA}}` | Dice roll | `{{roll::2d6+3}}` or `{{roll:20}}` |\n\nThe `{{pick}}` macro uses a consistent seed based on chat ID, raw content hash, and position within the content, ensuring the same result for the same context [public/scripts/macros.js:516-543]().\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/macros.js:490-571]()\n\n#### Utility Macros\n\n| Macro | Description | Example |\n|-------|-------------|---------|\n| `{{newline}}` | Insert newline | Text separator |\n| `{{trim}}` | Remove surrounding newlines | Formatting |\n| `{{noop}}` | No operation (removed) | Placeholder |\n| `{{input}}` | Current chatbar text | User input |\n| `{{reverse::TEXT}}` | Reverse string | `{{reverse::hello}}` → \"olleh\" |\n| `{{//COMMENT}}` | Comment (removed) | `{{//This is ignored}}` |\n| `{{maxPrompt}}` | Max context size | `{{maxPrompt}}` → \"8192\" |\n| `{{outlet::KEY}}` | Custom WI outlet prompt | Extension integration |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/macros.js:631-661]()\n\n### Variable Macros\n\nVariable macros enable manipulation of local and global variables directly within text using `{{...}}` syntax.\n\n#### Local Variable Macros\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Local Variable Macros\"\n        SetVar[\"{{setvar::name::value}}\u003cbr/\u003eSet and return empty string\"]\n        GetVar[\"{{getvar::name}}\u003cbr/\u003eGet and return value\"]\n        AddVar[\"{{addvar::name::value}}\u003cbr/\u003eAdd/concatenate and return empty\"]\n        IncVar[\"{{incvar::name}}\u003cbr/\u003eIncrement by 1 and return result\"]\n        DecVar[\"{{decvar::name}}\u003cbr/\u003eDecrement by 1 and return result\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Global Variable Macros\"\n        SetGlobalVar[\"{{setglobalvar::name::value}}\"]\n        GetGlobalVar[\"{{getglobalvar::name}}\"]\n        AddGlobalVar[\"{{addglobalvar::name::value}}\"]\n        IncGlobalVar[\"{{incglobalvar::name}}\"]\n        DecGlobalVar[\"{{decglobalvar::name}}\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Examples:**\n```\n{{setvar::counter::0}}\nCurrent count: {{getvar::counter}}\n{{incvar::counter}}\nNew count: {{getvar::counter}}\n\n{{setglobalvar::username::Alice}}\nHello, {{getglobalvar::username}}!\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:238-261]()\n\n### Custom Macro Registration\n\nExtensions can register custom macros using the `MacrosParser.registerMacro()` method (deprecated) or the new macro engine (when experimental flag is enabled).\n\n```javascript\n// Legacy method (deprecated)\nMacrosParser.registerMacro('myMacro', () =\u003e {\n    return 'My custom value';\n}, 'Description of macro');\n\n// The macro can then be used as {{myMacro}}\n```\n\nWhen the experimental macro engine is enabled, the legacy registration automatically bridges to the new system [public/scripts/macros.js:80-116]().\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/macros.js:182-218](), [public/scripts/macros.js:42-307]()\n\n---\n\n## Slash Commands for Variables\n\n### Variable Management Commands\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Scoped Variable Commands\"\n        Let[\"/let key=value\u003cbr/\u003eDeclare variable in scope\"]\n        Var[\"/var key=value\u003cbr/\u003eSet/get in scope hierarchy\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Local Variable Commands\"\n        SetVar[\"/setvar key=name value\u003cbr/\u003eSet local variable\"]\n        GetVar[\"/getvar name\u003cbr/\u003eGet local variable\"]\n        AddVar[\"/addvar key=name value\u003cbr/\u003eAdd to local variable\"]\n        IncVar[\"/incvar name\u003cbr/\u003eIncrement local variable\"]\n        DecVar[\"/decvar name\u003cbr/\u003eDecrement local variable\"]\n        DelVar[\"/delvar name\u003cbr/\u003eDelete local variable\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Global Variable Commands\"\n        SetGlobalVar[\"/setglobalvar key=name value\"]\n        GetGlobalVar[\"/getglobalvar name\"]\n        AddGlobalVar[\"/addglobalvar key=name value\"]\n        IncGlobalVar[\"/incglobalvar name\"]\n        DecGlobalVar[\"/decglobalvar name\"]\n        DelGlobalVar[\"/delglobalvar name\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"List and Inspect\"\n        ListVar[\"/listvar scope=all|local|global\u003cbr/\u003ereturn=popup-html|json|chat-html\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:922-1071]()\n\n### Command Arguments and Options\n\n#### `/setvar` Command\n\n```\n/setvar key=variableName index=0 as=number value\n```\n\n| Argument | Type | Required | Description |\n|----------|------|----------|-------------|\n| `key` | string | Yes | Variable name |\n| `index` | number/string | No | Array index or object key |\n| `as` | enum | No | Type conversion (`string`, `number`, `boolean`, `array`, `object`) |\n| value (unnamed) | any | Yes | Value to set |\n\n**Returns:** The set variable value\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:965-1011]()\n\n#### `/var` Command (Scoped)\n\n```\n/var key=variableName value\n/var key=variableName  // Get value\n```\n\nThe `/var` command traverses the scope hierarchy to find and modify variables, unlike `/let` which always creates in the current scope [public/scripts/variables.js:873-895]().\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:867-895]()\n\n#### `/listvar` Command\n\n```\n/listvar scope=local return=json\n/listvar scope=global return=popup-html\n/listvar scope=all return=chat-html\n```\n\n| Argument | Type | Options | Default |\n|----------|------|---------|---------|\n| `scope` | enum | `all`, `local`, `global` | `all` |\n| `return` | enum | `popup-html`, `json`, `chat-html`, `popup-text`, `object` | `popup-html` |\n\n**Returns:** JSON list of variables with metadata (key, value, scope)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:263-315](), [public/scripts/variables.js:922-964]()\n\n---\n\n## Control Flow and Boolean Operations\n\n### Boolean Evaluation\n\nThe `evalBoolean()` function evaluates comparison rules between two operands or checks truthiness of a single operand.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Boolean Evaluation Logic\"\n        EvalStart[\"evalBoolean(rule, a, b)\"]\n        CheckB{\"b is\u003cbr/\u003eundefined?\"}\n        TruthyCheck[\"Check if 'a' is truthy\u003cbr/\u003eHandle 'not' rule\"]\n        BothNumeric{\"Both a and b\u003cbr/\u003eare numbers?\"}\n        NumericComp[\"Numeric Comparison\u003cbr/\u003egt, gte, lt, lte, eq, neq\"]\n        StringComp[\"String Comparison\u003cbr/\u003ein, nin, eq, neq\"]\n        ReturnBool[\"Return boolean\"]\n        \n        EvalStart --\u003e CheckB\n        CheckB --\u003e|Yes| TruthyCheck\n        CheckB --\u003e|No| BothNumeric\n        BothNumeric --\u003e|Yes| NumericComp\n        BothNumeric --\u003e|No| StringComp\n        TruthyCheck --\u003e ReturnBool\n        NumericComp --\u003e ReturnBool\n        StringComp --\u003e ReturnBool\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Comparison Rules\"\n        GT[\"gt: a \u003e b\"]\n        GTE[\"gte: a \u003e= b\"]\n        LT[\"lt: a \u003c b\"]\n        LTE[\"lte: a \u003c= b\"]\n        EQ[\"eq: a === b\"]\n        NEQ[\"neq: a !== b\"]\n        IN[\"in: a.includes(b)\"]\n        NIN[\"nin: !a.includes(b)\"]\n        NOT[\"not: !truthy(a)\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Operand Resolution Order:**\n1. Numeric literal\n2. Scope variable\n3. Local variable\n4. Global variable\n5. String literal\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:459-503](), [public/scripts/variables.js:505-580]()\n\n### Control Flow Commands\n\n#### `/if` Command\n\n```\n/if left=value1 right=value2 rule=eq {{\n    // Commands to execute if true\n}} else={{\n    // Commands to execute if false\n}}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:407-434]()\n\n#### `/while` Command\n\n```\n/while left=counter right=10 rule=lt guard=on {{\n    /echo Iteration {{var::counter}}\n    /incvar counter\n}}\n```\n\nThe `guard` parameter limits iterations to `MAX_LOOPS` (100) by default. Set `guard=off` to allow more iterations [public/scripts/variables.js:20](), [public/scripts/variables.js:324]().\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:322-360]()\n\n#### `/times` Command\n\n```\n/times 5 {{\n    /echo Index: {{timesIndex}}\n}}\n```\n\nThe `{{timesIndex}}` macro or `scope.getVariable('timesIndex')` provides the 0-based iteration index [public/scripts/variables.js:389](), [public/scripts/variables.js:393]().\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:368-400]()\n\n---\n\n## Mathematical Operations\n\nSillyTavern provides a comprehensive set of mathematical operations as slash commands.\n\n### Arithmetic Commands\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Basic Arithmetic\"\n        Add[\"/add values\u003cbr/\u003eSum of all values\"]\n        Mul[\"/mul values\u003cbr/\u003eProduct of all values\"]\n        Sub[\"/sub values\u003cbr/\u003eSubtract values sequentially\"]\n        Div[\"/div value1 value2\u003cbr/\u003eDivision\"]\n        Mod[\"/mod value1 value2\u003cbr/\u003eModulo\"]\n        Pow[\"/pow base exponent\u003cbr/\u003eExponentiation\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Statistical\"\n        Min[\"/min values\u003cbr/\u003eMinimum value\"]\n        Max[\"/max values\u003cbr/\u003eMaximum value\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Mathematical Functions\"\n        Sin[\"/sin value\u003cbr/\u003eSine\"]\n        Cos[\"/cos value\u003cbr/\u003eCosine\"]\n        Log[\"/log value\u003cbr/\u003eNatural logarithm\"]\n        Sqrt[\"/sqrt value\u003cbr/\u003eSquare root\"]\n        Abs[\"/abs value\u003cbr/\u003eAbsolute value\"]\n        Round[\"/round value\u003cbr/\u003eRound to integer\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Utility\"\n        Len[\"/len value\u003cbr/\u003eLength of string/array/object\"]\n        Rand[\"/rand from to round=floor|ceil|round\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:696-799]()\n\n### Numeric Series Parsing\n\nThe `parseNumericSeries()` function handles multiple input formats for numeric operations:\n\n| Input Format | Example | Parsed Result |\n|--------------|---------|---------------|\n| Single number | `42` | `[42]` |\n| Space-separated | `\"1 2 3\"` | `[1, 2, 3]` |\n| JSON array | `\"[1,2,3]\"` | `[1, 2, 3]` |\n| Variable names | `\"x y z\"` | Resolved values of x, y, z |\n\nVariables are automatically resolved using `resolveVariable()` before numeric conversion [public/scripts/variables.js:665]().\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:638-669]()\n\n### Operation Examples\n\n```javascript\n// Sum multiple values\n/add 1 2 3 4 5  // Returns \"15\"\n\n// Use variables\n/setvar key=a 10\n/setvar key=b 20\n/add {{getvar::a}} {{getvar::b}}  // Returns \"30\"\n\n// Random number with rounding\n/rand 1 10 round=floor  // Returns integer 1-10\n\n// Length of array\n/setvar key=myArray [\"a\",\"b\",\"c\"]\n/len {{getvar::myArray}}  // Returns \"3\"\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:696-784]()\n\n---\n\n## Array and Object Manipulation\n\n### `/sort` Command\n\nSorts arrays or object keys with optional value-based sorting for objects.\n\n```\n/sort [\"zebra\",\"apple\",\"banana\"]\n// Returns [\"apple\",\"banana\",\"zebra\"]\n\n/sort {\"z\":3,\"a\":1,\"b\":2} keysort=off\n// Returns [\"a\",\"b\",\"z\"] (sorted by values)\n\n/sort {\"z\":3,\"a\":1,\"b\":2} keysort=on\n// Returns [\"a\",\"b\",\"z\"] (sorted by keys)\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:801-833]()\n\n### `/len` Command\n\nReturns the length of strings, arrays, or object key counts.\n\n```javascript\n/len \"hello\"  // Returns 5\n/len [1,2,3]  // Returns 3\n/len {\"a\":1,\"b\":2}  // Returns 2\n```\n\nThe function handles JSON parsing automatically [public/scripts/variables.js:764-784]().\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:764-784]()\n\n---\n\n## Variable Enumeration and Autocomplete\n\nThe system provides enum providers for variable name autocomplete in slash commands.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Variable Enum Provider\"\n        EnumProvider[\"commonEnumProviders.variables(type)\"]\n        ScopeVars[\"Scope Variables\u003cbr/\u003escope.allVariableNames\u003cbr/\u003eIcon: 'S'\"]\n        LocalVars[\"Local Variables\u003cbr/\u003echat_metadata.variables\u003cbr/\u003eIcon: 'L'\"]\n        GlobalVars[\"Global Variables\u003cbr/\u003eextension_settings.variables.global\u003cbr/\u003eIcon: 'G'\"]\n        \n        EnumProvider --\u003e ScopeVars\n        EnumProvider --\u003e LocalVars\n        EnumProvider --\u003e GlobalVars\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Enum Value Types\"\n        VarName[\"SlashCommandEnumValue\u003cbr/\u003ename, null, enumTypes.variable\"]\n        LocalName[\"SlashCommandEnumValue\u003cbr/\u003ename, null, enumTypes.name\"]\n        GlobalName[\"SlashCommandEnumValue\u003cbr/\u003ename, null, enumTypes.macro\"]\n    end\n    \n    ScopeVars --\u003e VarName\n    LocalVars --\u003e LocalName\n    GlobalVars --\u003e GlobalName\n```\n\n**Usage in Command Definitions:**\n\n```javascript\nSlashCommandNamedArgument.fromProps({\n    name: 'key',\n    description: 'variable name',\n    typeList: [ARGUMENT_TYPE.VARIABLE_NAME],\n    enumProvider: commonEnumProviders.variables('local'),\n    forceEnum: false,\n})\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandCommonEnumsProvider.js:145-159](), [public/scripts/variables.js:976]()\n\n---\n\n## SlashCommandClosure and Scripting\n\n`SlashCommandClosure` is the core class enabling scripting capabilities in SillyTavern. Closures encapsulate slash command sequences with their own execution scope, enabling complex scripts with functions, control flow, and state management.\n\n### Closure Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph ClosureStructure[\"SlashCommandClosure Structure\"]\n        Closure[\"SlashCommandClosure\"]\n        ExecutorList[\"executorList: SlashCommandExecutor[]\"]\n        Scope[\"scope: SlashCommandScope\"]\n        AbortCtrl[\"abortController: SlashCommandAbortController\"]\n        BreakCtrl[\"breakController: SlashCommandBreakController\"]\n        \n        Closure --\u003e ExecutorList\n        Closure --\u003e Scope\n        Closure --\u003e AbortCtrl\n        Closure --\u003e BreakCtrl\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ExecutionFlow[\"Execution Flow\"]\n        Execute[\"closure.execute()\"]\n        ExecuteLoop[\"for executor in executorList\"]\n        ExecuteCmd[\"executor.execute(scope)\"]\n        HandleAbort{\"abortController\u003cbr/\u003e.signal.aborted?\"}\n        HandleBreak{\"breakController\u003cbr/\u003e.isActive?\"}\n        Return[\"Return SlashCommandClosureResult\"]\n        \n        Execute --\u003e ExecuteLoop\n        ExecuteLoop --\u003e ExecuteCmd\n        ExecuteCmd --\u003e HandleAbort\n        HandleAbort --\u003e|Yes| Return\n        HandleAbort --\u003e|No| HandleBreak\n        HandleBreak --\u003e|Yes| Return\n        HandleBreak --\u003e|No| ExecuteLoop\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ScopeHierarchy[\"Scope Hierarchy\"]\n        ParentScope[\"parent: SlashCommandScope\"]\n        CurrentScope[\"current: SlashCommandScope\"]\n        ChildScope[\"child: SlashCommandScope\"]\n        \n        ParentScope --\u003e CurrentScope\n        CurrentScope --\u003e ChildScope\n    end\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandClosure.js:1-200](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js:1-109]()\n\n### Closure Execution Model\n\nWhen a closure executes, it:\n1. Inherits the parent scope via `scope.parent`\n2. Executes each `SlashCommandExecutor` in sequence\n3. Maintains a pipe value for command chaining\n4. Checks abort and break controllers after each command\n5. Returns a `SlashCommandClosureResult` containing the final pipe value\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph ClosureExecution[\"Closure Execution Pipeline\"]\n        Start[\"closure.execute()\"]\n        InitPipe[\"Initialize pipe from args.pipe\"]\n        Cmd1[\"executor[0].execute(scope)\"]\n        UpdatePipe1[\"scope.pipe = result\"]\n        Cmd2[\"executor[1].execute(scope)\"]\n        UpdatePipe2[\"scope.pipe = result\"]\n        CmdN[\"executor[n].execute(scope)\"]\n        Result[\"return SlashCommandClosureResult\"]\n        \n        Start --\u003e InitPipe\n        InitPipe --\u003e Cmd1\n        Cmd1 --\u003e UpdatePipe1\n        UpdatePipe1 --\u003e Cmd2\n        Cmd2 --\u003e UpdatePipe2\n        UpdatePipe2 --\u003e CmdN\n        CmdN --\u003e Result\n    end\n```\n\n**Key Methods:**\n\n| Method | Purpose | Returns |\n|--------|---------|---------|\n| `execute(scope)` | Execute all commands in closure | `SlashCommandClosureResult` |\n| `getCopy()` | Create deep copy with new IDs | `SlashCommandClosure` |\n| `toString()` | Serialize to string | `string` |\n| `onProgress(done, total)` | Progress callback | `void` |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandClosure.js:1-1000]()\n\n### Closure Serialization and Deserialization\n\nClosures can be serialized to strings and deserialized back to executable closures, enabling storage and transmission of code.\n\n#### Serialization\n\n```javascript\n/closure-serialize {{\n    /echo This is a closure\n    /setvar key=x 42\n}}\n// Returns the raw text of the closure\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:902-907]()\n\n#### Deserialization\n\n```javascript\n/closure-deserialize /echo Hello | /run\n// Parses string into SlashCommandClosure and executes\n```\n\nThe deserialized closure maintains scope hierarchy through `closure.scope.parent = args._scope` [public/scripts/variables.js:917]().\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:909-919]()\n\n### Closures as Functions\n\nClosures enable functional programming patterns by serving as first-class values that can be passed, stored, and executed.\n\n```javascript\n// Define a closure as a function\n/let myFunction {{\n    /setvar key=result {{pipe}}\n    /mul {{var::result}} 2\n}}\n\n// Execute the function with pipe input\n/echo 5 | /run {{var::myFunction}}\n// Returns: 10\n\n// Pass closures to other commands\n/let transform {{\n    /add {{pipe}} 10\n    /mul {{pipe}} 2\n}}\n/let numbers [1,2,3]\n/foreach list={{var::numbers}} closure={{var::transform}}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandClosure.js:1-1000](), [public/scripts/variables.js:867-895]()\n\n### Debugging Closures\n\nThe `SlashCommandDebugController` enables step-by-step debugging of closure execution with breakpoints.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph DebugFlow[\"Debug Execution Flow\"]\n        Start[\"debugController.start()\"]\n        Breakpoint[\"Hit SlashCommandBreakPoint\"]\n        Pause[\"debugController.pause()\"]\n        UserAction{\"User Action\"}\n        StepInto[\"debugController.stepInto()\"]\n        StepOver[\"debugController.stepOver()\"]\n        StepOut[\"debugController.stepOut()\"]\n        Resume[\"debugController.resume()\"]\n        \n        Start --\u003e Breakpoint\n        Breakpoint --\u003e Pause\n        Pause --\u003e UserAction\n        UserAction --\u003e StepInto\n        UserAction --\u003e StepOver\n        UserAction --\u003e StepOut\n        UserAction --\u003e Resume\n    end\n```\n\nQuick Reply Extension uses this for debugging QR scripts [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:200-400]().\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandDebugController.js:1-100](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:1-600]()\n\n### Practical Scripting Examples\n\n#### Example 1: Conditional Execution with State\n\n```javascript\n// Initialize counter if not exists\n/if left={{getvar::session_count}} rule=not {{\n    /setvar key=session_count 0\n}}\n\n// Increment and check threshold\n/incvar session_count\n/if left={{getvar::session_count}} right=5 rule=gte {{\n    /echo Session limit reached\n    /setvar key=session_count 0\n}} else={{\n    /echo Session {{getvar::session_count}} of 5\n}}\n```\n\n#### Example 2: Iterative Processing with Closures\n\n```javascript\n// Define processing function\n/let processItem {{\n    /setvar key=item {{pipe}}\n    /echo Processing: {{var::item}}\n    /addglobalvar key=processed_count 1\n}}\n\n// Apply to list\n/let items [\"apple\",\"banana\",\"cherry\"]\n/foreach list={{var::items}} closure={{var::processItem}}\n```\n\n#### Example 3: Nested Closures for Complex Logic\n\n```javascript\n/let validator {{\n    /if left={{pipe}} right=0 rule=gt {{\n        /echo Valid\n        /return true\n    }} else={{\n        /echo Invalid\n        /return false\n    }}\n}}\n\n/let processor {{\n    /run {{var::validator}} | /if left={{pipe}} rule=eq right=true {{\n        /echo Processing valid input\n    }}\n}}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:322-434](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandClosure.js:1-1000]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration with Other Systems\n\n### Integration with Slash Commands\n\nVariables, macros, and closures form the foundation of the slash command scripting system:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph CommandExecution[\"Slash Command Execution\"]\n        Parser[\"SlashCommandParser.parse(text)\"]\n        Executor[\"SlashCommandExecutor\"]\n        Command[\"SlashCommand.callback(args, value)\"]\n        Scope[\"SlashCommandScope\"]\n        Closure[\"SlashCommandClosure\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph VariableResolution[\"Variable Resolution in Commands\"]\n        NamedArg[\"Named Argument: key=variableName\"]\n        ResolveVar[\"resolveVariable(name, scope)\"]\n        CheckScope[\"Check scope.variables\"]\n        CheckLocal[\"Check chat_metadata.variables\"]\n        CheckGlobal[\"Check extension_settings.variables.global\"]\n        ReturnValue[\"Return resolved value\"]\n        \n        NamedArg --\u003e ResolveVar\n        ResolveVar --\u003e CheckScope\n        CheckScope --\u003e CheckLocal\n        CheckLocal --\u003e CheckGlobal\n        CheckGlobal --\u003e ReturnValue\n    end\n    \n    subgraph MacroSubstitution[\"Macro Substitution in Commands\"]\n        CommandValue[\"Command value argument\"]\n        SubstituteParams[\"substituteParams(value)\"]\n        EvaluateMacros[\"evaluateMacros(value, env)\"]\n        MacroResult[\"Substituted value\"]\n        \n        CommandValue --\u003e SubstituteParams\n        SubstituteParams --\u003e EvaluateMacros\n        EvaluateMacros --\u003e MacroResult\n    end\n```\n\n**Integration Points:**\n\n| Component | Integration Mechanism | Source Reference |\n|-----------|----------------------|------------------|\n| Named Arguments | `ARGUMENT_TYPE.VARIABLE_NAME` type checking | [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandArgument.js:1-200]() |\n| Pipe Values | `scope.pipe` for command chaining | [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js:18-20]() |\n| Autocomplete | `commonEnumProviders.variables()` enum provider | [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandCommonEnumsProvider.js:145-159]() |\n| Macro Environment | Custom macros via `scope.macroList` | [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js:42-46]() |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:1-15](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js:1-109](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js:1-500]()\n\n### Integration with Quick Reply System\n\nQuick Replies extensively use variables, macros, and closures for automation:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph QRExecution[\"Quick Reply Execution\"]\n        QRButton[\"QuickReply Button Click\"]\n        QRExecute[\"qr.execute(args, preventAutoExecute)\"]\n        ParseClosure[\"SlashCommandParser.parse(qr.message)\"]\n        ExecuteClosure[\"SlashCommandClosure.execute()\"]\n        HandleResult[\"Process SlashCommandClosureResult\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph QRStateManagement[\"State Management with Variables\"]\n        AutoExecCheck[\"Check executeOnUser/AI/etc flags\"]\n        GetState[\"getvar::qr_state\"]\n        UpdateState[\"setvar::qr_state\"]\n        ConditionalExec[\"if/while based on variables\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph QRDebugging[\"QR Debug System\"]\n        DebugMode[\"QuickReply.debugController\"]\n        Breakpoints[\"SlashCommandBreakPoint\"]\n        StepExecution[\"Step through closure\"]\n        InspectVars[\"Inspect scope.variables\"]\n    end\n```\n\nQuick Replies use variables for:\n- **Auto-execution conditions**: Check variable values to determine whether to auto-execute\n- **Dynamic button labels**: Use `{{getvar::}}` macros in labels\n- **State management**: Track execution state across QR invocations\n- **Conditional logic**: Complex scripts with control flow\n- **Debugging**: Step through QR closures with breakpoints\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/QuickReply.js:1-600](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/AutoExecuteHandler.js:1-100](), [public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/src/SlashCommandHandler.js:1-200]()\n\n### Integration with Prompt Assembly\n\nVariables and macros integrate into the prompt assembly pipeline:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph PromptAssembly[\"Prompt Assembly with Variables\"]\n        PromptTemplate[\"Prompt Template with {{macros}}\"]\n        SubstituteParams[\"substituteParams(template)\"]\n        ResolveVars[\"Resolve {{getvar::}} macros\"]\n        EvaluateMacros[\"evaluateMacros()\"]\n        FinalPrompt[\"Final Prompt Text\"]\n        \n        PromptTemplate --\u003e SubstituteParams\n        SubstituteParams --\u003e ResolveVars\n        ResolveVars --\u003e EvaluateMacros\n        EvaluateMacros --\u003e FinalPrompt\n    end\n```\n\nCharacter definitions, system prompts, and chat messages all support macro substitution, enabling dynamic prompt construction based on variable state.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/openai.js:30-31](), [public/scripts/slash-commands.js:72]()\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling and Edge Cases\n\n### Variable Not Found Errors\n\nWhen a scoped variable is accessed but doesn't exist, a `SlashCommandScopeVariableNotFoundError` is thrown [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js:107]().\n\nFor local and global variables, the functions return empty strings or default values rather than throwing:\n```javascript\ngetLocalVariable('nonexistent')  // Returns ''\ngetGlobalVariable('nonexistent')  // Returns ''\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandScope.js:84-108](), [public/scripts/variables.js:45-46]()\n\n### Type Coercion\n\nVariables undergo automatic type coercion:\n- Numeric strings → numbers\n- Empty/whitespace strings → strings\n- Objects/arrays → JSON strings when stored, parsed when accessed with index\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:45](), [public/scripts/variables.js:100-102]()\n\n### Loop Guards\n\nThe `MAX_LOOPS` constant (100) prevents infinite loops in `/while` and `/times` commands. This can be bypassed with `guard=off` [public/scripts/variables.js:20](), [public/scripts/variables.js:324](), [public/scripts/variables.js:384]().\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/variables.js:20](), [public/scripts/variables.js:322-360](), [public/scripts/variables.js:368-400]()\n\n---\n\n## Complete Variable Command Reference\n\n### Local Variable Commands\n\n| Command | Syntax | Returns | Description |\n|---------|--------|---------|-------------|\n| `/setvar` | `key=name [index=i] [as=type] value` | String | Set local variable |\n| `/getvar` | `name [index=i]` | String | Get local variable |\n| `/addvar` | `key=name value` | String | Add to local variable |\n| `/incvar` | `name` | Number | Increment by 1 |\n| `/decvar` | `name` | Number | Decrement by 1 |\n| `/delvar` | `name` | String | Delete local variable |\n\n### Global Variable Commands\n\n| Command | Syntax | Returns | Description |\n|---------|--------|---------|-------------|\n| `/setglobalvar` | `key=name [index=i] [as=type] value` | String | Set global variable |\n| `/getglobalvar` | `name [index=i]` | String | Get global variable |\n| `/addglobalvar` | `key=name value` | String | Add to global variable |\n| `/incglobalvar` | `name` | Number | Increment by 1 |\n| `/decglobalvar` | `name` | Number | Decrement by 1 |\n| `/delglobalvar` | `name` | String | Delete global variable |\n\n### Scoped Variable Commands\n\n| Command | Syntax | Returns | Description |\n|---------|--------|---------|-------------|\n| `/let` | `key=name [value]` or `name [value]` | String | Declare in current scope |\n| `/var` | `key=name [value]` or `name [value]` | String | Get/set in scope hierarchy |\n\n### List and Utility Commands\n\n| Command | Syntax | Returns | Description |\n|---------|--------|---------|-------------|\n| `/listvar` | `[scope=all\\|local\\|global] [return=type]` | Array/HTML | List variables |\n| `/flushvar` | `name` | String | Delete local variable (alias) |\n| `/closure-serialize` | `closure` | String | Serialize closure to string |\n| `/closure-deserialize` | `string` | Closure | Parse string to closure |\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/sprites.js](src/endpoints/sprites.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThe Extension Framework provides a modular architecture for adding functionality to SillyTavern without modifying core code. Extensions can access core APIs, inject prompts into generation requests, register slash commands, subscribe to application events, and maintain their own settings. This document covers the framework architecture, integration points, and built-in extensions.\n\nFor information about specific built-in extensions, see:\n- Image generation: [Image Generation Extensions](#8.2)\n- Character expressions: [Character Expressions System](#8.3)\n- Visual Novel mode: [Visual Novel Mode](#8.4)\n- Image captioning: [Image Captioning and Multimodal AI](#8.5)\n\nFor extension development details, see [Extension Development Guide](#10.2).\n\n---\n\n## System Architecture\n\nThe extension framework sits between the core application and external functionality, providing a well-defined API surface for third-party code to integrate cleanly with SillyTavern's features.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Core[\"Core Application (script.js)\"]\n        AppInit[\"Application Initialization\u003cbr/\u003efirstLoadInit()\"]\n        EventSrc[\"Event System\u003cbr/\u003eeventSource\"]\n        PromptMgr[\"Prompt Assembly\u003cbr/\u003esetupChatCompletionPromptManager()\"]\n        SlashParser[\"Slash Command Parser\u003cbr/\u003eSlashCommandParser\"]\n        ToolMgr[\"Tool Manager\u003cbr/\u003eToolManager\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ExtFramework[\"Extension Framework (extensions.js)\"]\n        ExtInit[\"initExtensions()\"]\n        ExtSettings[\"extension_settings\u003cbr/\u003e{memory: {...}, expressions: {...}}\"]\n        GetContext[\"getContext() API\"]\n        ExtPrompt[\"setExtensionPrompt()\"]\n        ModuleWorker[\"ModuleWorkerWrapper\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph BuiltInExt[\"Built-in Extensions\"]\n        Expressions[\"expressions/\u003cbr/\u003eCharacter sprites \u0026 classification\"]\n        Memory[\"memory/\u003cbr/\u003eChat summarization\"]\n        SD[\"stable-diffusion/\u003cbr/\u003eImage generation\"]\n        QR[\"quick-reply/\u003cbr/\u003eCommand automation\"]\n        Vectors[\"vectors/\u003cbr/\u003eRAG \u0026 embeddings\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ThirdPartyExt[\"Third-Party Extensions\"]\n        Custom[\"Custom extensions in\u003cbr/\u003epublic/scripts/extensions/third-party/\"]\n    end\n    \n    AppInit --\u003e|\"initExtensions()\"| ExtInit\n    ExtInit --\u003e|Load settings| ExtSettings\n    ExtInit --\u003e|Initialize each| BuiltInExt\n    ExtInit --\u003e|Initialize each| ThirdPartyExt\n    \n    BuiltInExt --\u003e|\"getContext()\"| GetContext\n    ThirdPartyExt --\u003e|\"getContext()\"| GetContext\n    \n    GetContext --\u003e|Access| EventSrc\n    GetContext --\u003e|Access| SlashParser\n    GetContext --\u003e|Access| ToolMgr\n    \n    BuiltInExt --\u003e|\"eventSource.on()\"| EventSrc\n    BuiltInExt --\u003e|\"setExtensionPrompt()\"| ExtPrompt\n    BuiltInExt --\u003e|\"SlashCommandParser.addCommand()\"| SlashParser\n    \n    ExtPrompt --\u003e|Inject into| PromptMgr\n
