Lifecycle and Initialization\n\nExtensions are initialized during application startup as part of the bootstrap sequence. The initialization process loads extension settings, instantiates extension modules, and registers their integration points with the core system.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant App as Application Bootstrap\u003cbr/\u003e(firstLoadInit)\n    participant ExtFW as Extension Framework\u003cbr/\u003e(extensions.js)\n    participant ExtMod as Extension Module\u003cbr/\u003e(e.g., expressions/index.js)\n    participant EventSys as Event System\u003cbr/\u003e(eventSource)\n    participant Settings as Extension Settings\u003cbr/\u003e(extension_settings)\n    \n    App-\u003e\u003eExtFW: initExtensions()\n    ExtFW-\u003e\u003eSettings: Load extension_settings from storage\n    ExtFW-\u003e\u003eExtMod: Import extension module\n    ExtMod-\u003e\u003eExtMod: Execute module initialization\n    ExtMod-\u003e\u003eExtFW: getContext() to access APIs\n    ExtMod-\u003e\u003eSettings: Read extension-specific settings\n    ExtMod-\u003e\u003eExtMod: Initialize UI components\n    ExtMod-\u003e\u003eEventSys: Subscribe to events\u003cbr/\u003eeventSource.on(event_types.X, handler)\n    ExtMod-\u003e\u003eExtFW: Register slash commands\n    ExtMod-\u003e\u003eExtFW: Register prompt injections\n    ExtMod--\u003e\u003eExtFW: Initialization complete\n    ExtFW--\u003e\u003eApp: All extensions loaded\n    App-\u003e\u003eEventSys: emit(APP_INITIALIZED)\n    EventSys-\u003e\u003eExtMod: Trigger initialization handlers\n```\n\n**Initialization order:**\n\n1. **Settings load:** Extension settings are loaded from storage at [public/script.js:701]()\n2. **Extension init:** `initExtensions()` is called at [public/script.js:697]()\n3. **Module loading:** Each extension module is imported and executed\n4. **Registration phase:** Extensions register their integration points\n5. **Event notification:** `APP_INITIALIZED` event is emitted at [public/script.js:735]()\n\nExtensions can subscribe to initialization events to perform setup that requires the full application to be loaded:\n\n- `event_types.APP_INITIALIZED` - Core systems ready, but UI may not be rendered\n- `event_types.APP_READY` - Full application ready, UI rendered\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:666-739](), [public/script.js:190]()\n\n---\n\n## Core Extension APIs\n\n### The getContext() Function\n\nThe `getContext()` function is the primary API surface for extensions. It returns an object containing references to core application state and functions, allowing extensions to interact with the system without direct dependencies.\n\n**Key exposed functionality:**\n\n| Category | Properties | Description |\n|----------|-----------|-------------|\n| **Character Data** | `characters`, `this_chid`, `name1`, `name2` | Active character information |\n| **Chat Data** | `chat`, `chat_metadata`, `selected_group` | Current conversation state |\n| **Generation** | `generate()`, `generateRaw()`, `generateQuietPrompt()` | AI generation functions |\n| **UI State** | `chatElement`, `showSwipeButtons()`, `hideSwipeButtons()` | UI manipulation |\n| **Groups** | `groups`, `getGroupMembers()`, `getGroupChat()` | Group chat functionality |\n| **Settings** | `saveSettings()`, `saveChat()`, `extension_settings` | Persistence functions |\n| **Events** | `eventSource` | Event subscription system |\n\n**Usage pattern:**\n\n```javascript\nimport { getContext } from './extensions.js';\n\nexport function myExtensionInit() {\n    const context = getContext();\n    \n    // Access character data\n    const currentChar = context.characters[context.this_chid];\n    \n    // Subscribe to events\n    context.eventSource.on(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, handleMessage);\n    \n    // Modify UI\n    context.chatElement.append(myCustomElement);\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:271](), [public/script.js:289-293]()\n\n### Event System Integration\n\nExtensions integrate with the core event system to react to application state changes. The `eventSource` object provides pub-sub functionality for lifecycle events.\n\n**Common extension events:**\n\n| Event | Trigger | Use Case |\n|-------|---------|----------|\n| `CHAT_CHANGED` | Chat loaded or switched | Reload extension state for new chat |\n| `MESSAGE_RECEIVED` | New message added to chat | Process message content |\n| `CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED` | Character message displayed | Update sprites/expressions |\n| `GENERATION_STARTED` | AI generation begins | Prepare for response |\n| `GENERATION_STOPPED` | AI generation ends | Post-process response |\n| `USER_MESSAGE_RENDERED` | User message displayed | Update UI elements |\n| `GROUP_CHAT_CREATED` | New group chat started | Initialize group-specific state |\n\n**Event subscription pattern:**\n\n```javascript\neventSource.on(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, async (messageIndex) =\u003e {\n    const message = chat[messageIndex];\n    // Process message\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:2-3](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:8]()\n\n### Prompt Injection System\n\nExtensions can inject content into the prompt assembly process using `setExtensionPrompt()`. This allows extensions to provide context, instructions, or data that influences AI generation.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph Extensions\n        Ext1[\"Memory Extension\u003cbr/\u003esetExtensionPrompt('memory', ...)\"]\n        Ext2[\"Vectors Extension\u003cbr/\u003esetExtensionPrompt('vectors', ...)\"]\n        Ext3[\"Custom Extension\u003cbr/\u003esetExtensionPrompt('custom', ...)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph PromptAssembly[\"Prompt Assembly Pipeline\"]\n        Manager[\"PromptManager\u003cbr/\u003esetupChatCompletionPromptManager()\"]\n        ExtPrompts[\"extension_prompts\u003cbr/\u003e{memory: {...}, vectors: {...}}\"]\n        Assembly[\"Assemble Components\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph OutputTypes[\"Prompt Positions\"]\n        InPrompt[\"IN_PROMPT (position: 0)\u003cbr/\u003eMerged into system/char def\"]\n        BeforePrompt[\"BEFORE_PROMPT (position: 2)\u003cbr/\u003eBefore main prompt\"]\n        InChat[\"IN_CHAT (position: 1)\u003cbr/\u003eAs chat messages\"]\n        AtDepth[\"AT_DEPTH\u003cbr/\u003eAt specific depth\"]\n    end\n    \n    Ext1 --\u003e ExtPrompts\n    Ext2 --\u003e ExtPrompts\n    Ext3 --\u003e ExtPrompts\n    \n    Manager --\u003e ExtPrompts\n    ExtPrompts --\u003e Assembly\n    \n    Assembly --\u003e InPrompt\n    Assembly --\u003e BeforePrompt\n    Assembly --\u003e InChat\n    Assembly --\u003e AtDepth\n```\n\n**Extension prompt structure:**\n\n```javascript\nsetExtensionPrompt(\n    identifier,      // Unique string identifier\n    promptText,      // The actual prompt content\n    position,        // extension_prompt_types: IN_PROMPT, IN_CHAT, BEFORE_PROMPT\n    depth,           // Insertion depth (for AT_DEPTH)\n    role            // extension_prompt_roles: SYSTEM, USER, ASSISTANT\n);\n```\n\n**Extension prompt types:**\n\n- `IN_PROMPT` (0): Merged into the main system/character definition\n- `IN_CHAT` (1): Inserted as a message in the chat history\n- `BEFORE_PROMPT` (2): Inserted before the main prompt assembly\n\n**Extension prompt roles:**\n\n- `SYSTEM` (0): System message role\n- `USER` (1): User message role  \n- `ASSISTANT` (2): Assistant message role\n\n**Sources:** [public/script.js:455-469](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:114]()\n\n### Slash Command Registration\n\nExtensions can register custom slash commands using `SlashCommandParser.addCommand()`. This provides a command-line interface for extension functionality.\n\n**Registration pattern:**\n\n```javascript\nSlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(SlashCommand.fromProps({\n    name: 'commandname',\n    callback: myCommandCallback,\n    returns: 'string',\n    helpString: 'Description of what this command does',\n    unnamedArgumentList: [\n        new SlashCommandArgument(\n            'arg1', // name\n            [ARGUMENT_TYPE.STRING], // types\n            true, // required\n            'Description of argument'\n        ),\n    ],\n    namedArgumentList: [\n        SlashCommandNamedArgument.fromProps({\n            name: 'flag',\n            description: 'Optional flag',\n            typeList: [ARGUMENT_TYPE.BOOLEAN],\n            defaultValue: 'false',\n        }),\n    ],\n}));\n```\n\n**Command callback signature:**\n\n```javascript\nasync function myCommandCallback(args, value) {\n    // args: Named arguments object\n    // value: Unnamed argument value\n    return result; // Return value can be used in pipes\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:11-16](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:27-28]()\n\n### Extension Settings Storage\n\nExtension settings are persisted in the `extension_settings` object, which is automatically saved and loaded by the framework.\n\n**Settings structure:**\n\n```javascript\n// Global settings object\nextension_settings = {\n    expressions: {\n        api: 2,\n        translate: false,\n        // ... expression-specific settings\n    },\n    memory: {\n        prompt: \"Summarize...\",\n        position: 0,\n        // ... memory-specific settings\n    },\n    // ... other extensions\n};\n```\n\n**Settings access pattern:**\n\n```javascript\n// Read settings\nconst mySettings = extension_settings.myextension;\n\n// Modify settings\nextension_settings.myextension.someSetting = newValue;\n\n// Trigger save (debounced)\nsaveSettingsDebounced();\n```\n\nSettings are automatically loaded at startup and persisted to the server on changes.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:6](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:2]()\n\n---\n\n## Built-in Extensions Overview\n\nSillyTavern includes several powerful built-in extensions that demonstrate the framework's capabilities:\n\n### Character Expressions Extension\n\n**Location:** `public/scripts/extensions/expressions/`\n\nProvides dynamic character sprite/expression display based on emotional classification of messages.\n\n**Key features:**\n- Multiple classification APIs: Local, Extras, LLM, WebLLM\n- Support for multiple sprites per expression\n- Custom expression definitions\n- Visual Novel mode for group chats\n- Integration with message rendering pipeline\n\n**API integration:**\n- Event: `CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED` for expression updates\n- Slash commands: `/emote`, `/extedit`, `/loadexpressionoverrides`\n- Asset API: `/api/sprites/` for sprite file management\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:1-100]()\n\n### Memory/Summarization Extension\n\n**Location:** `public/scripts/extensions/memory/`\n\nProvides automatic chat summarization to extend effective context length.\n\n**Key features:**\n- Multiple summary sources: Main API, Extras, WebLLM\n- Configurable summary intervals and word counts\n- Template-based summary formatting\n- Summary history with restore capability\n- Prompt injection at configurable positions\n\n**API integration:**\n- `setExtensionPrompt()` for summary injection\n- Event: `CHAT_CHANGED` for summary updates\n- Slash commands: `/summarize`, `/setsummary`, `/memory`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:1-200]()\n\n### Stable Diffusion Extension\n\n**Location:** `public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/`\n\nIntegrates image generation from various Stable Diffusion providers.\n\n**Key features:**\n- 20+ provider backends (AUTOMATIC1111, NovelAI, etc.)\n- Interactive and automatic generation modes\n- Message attachment and character sprite generation\n- Prompt template system with variable substitution\n\n**API integration:**\n- Event: `MESSAGE_RECEIVED` for auto-generation triggers\n- Tool calling API for function-based generation\n- Slash commands: `/imagine`, `/sd`, `/imagine-together`\n\n**Sources:** Referenced in [Diagram 4]() from system architecture overview\n\n### Quick Reply Extension\n\n**Location:** `public/scripts/extensions/quick-reply/`\n\nProvides a meta-programming layer for automating slash command sequences.\n\n**Key features:**\n- QR set management (global, chat, character scopes)\n- Auto-execution system with event triggers\n- Debugging with breakpoints and stepping\n- Context-aware button visibility\n\n**API integration:**\n- `SlashCommandClosure` for script execution\n- Event system for auto-execution triggers\n- Variable system integration\n\n**Sources:** Referenced in [Diagram 4]() from system architecture overview\n\n### Vectors Extension\n\n**Location:** `public/scripts/extensions/vectors/`\n\nImplements Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) using vector embeddings.\n\n**Key features:**\n- Multiple embedding providers (OpenAI, Cohere, etc.)\n- Local vector storage with vectra.LocalIndex\n- File, chat, and World Info vectorization\n- Similarity search and retrieval\n\n**API integration:**\n- Prompt injection for retrieved context\n- Event: `GENERATION_STARTED` for retrieval\n- Slash commands: `/vectorize`, `/vectorquery`\n\n**Sources:** Referenced in [Diagram 4]() from system architecture overview\n\n---\n\n## Extension File Structure\n\nExtensions follow a consistent file structure for organization and discoverability:\n\n```\npublic/scripts/extensions/\n├── extension-name/\n│   ├── index.js              # Main extension code\n│   ├── settings.html         # Settings UI (loaded into panel)\n│   ├── style.css            # Extension-specific styles\n│   ├── templates/           # Handlebars templates\n│   │   └── template.html\n│   └── manifest.json        # Extension metadata (optional)\n│\n├── expressions/\n│   ├── index.js\n│   ├── settings.html\n│   ├── style.css\n│   ├── list-item.html       # Template for expression list items\n│   └── templates/\n│       ├── upload-expression.html\n│       ├── add-custom-expression.html\n│       └── remove-custom-expression.html\n│\n└── memory/\n    ├── index.js\n    ├── settings.html\n    └── style.css\n```\n\n**File conventions:**\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `index.js` | Main extension module, exported functions |\n| `settings.html` | UI fragment for extension settings panel |\n| `style.css` | Extension-specific CSS rules |\n| `templates/*.html` | Handlebars templates for dynamic UI |\n| `manifest.json` | Metadata (name, version, author, etc.) |\n\n**Module exports pattern:**\n\n```javascript\n// index.js\nexport const MODULE_NAME = 'my-extension';\n\n// Extension initialization\nasync function init() {\n    // Setup code\n}\n\n// Make init available if needed by framework\nexport { init };\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:21](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:34]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension API Reference\n\n### Module Worker Integration\n\nExtensions can offload heavy computation to web workers using `ModuleWorkerWrapper`.\n\n```javascript\nimport { ModuleWorkerWrapper } from './extensions.js';\n\nconst worker = new ModuleWorkerWrapper(modules.classify);\nconst result = await worker.run({ text: 'Input text' });\n```\n\n**Available modules:**\n- `modules.classify` - Text classification\n- `modules.caption` - Image captioning\n- `modules.embedding` - Text embeddings\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:5]()\n\n### Extras API Integration\n\nFor extensions using the Extras API backend:\n\n```javascript\nasync function doExtrasFetch(endpoint, args) {\n    const url = getApiUrl();\n    const response = await fetch(`${url}${endpoint}`, {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: getRequestHeaders(),\n        body: JSON.stringify(args),\n    });\n    return await response.json();\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:5]()\n\n### Template Rendering\n\nExtensions can render Handlebars templates:\n\n```javascript\nimport { renderExtensionTemplateAsync } from './extensions.js';\n\nconst html = await renderExtensionTemplateAsync(\n    'extension-name',\n    'template-name',\n    { data: 'values' }\n);\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:5](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:2]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension Integration Patterns\n\n### Prompt Injection Pattern\n\nThe Memory extension demonstrates the standard pattern for prompt injection:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Event[\"Event: GENERATION_STARTED\"]\n    Check[\"Check if summary exists\u003cbr/\u003eand is not frozen\"]\n    Format[\"Format summary with template\u003cbr/\u003eformatMemoryValue()\"]\n    Inject[\"setExtensionPrompt(\u003cbr/\u003e'memory',\u003cbr/\u003eformattedText,\u003cbr/\u003eposition,\u003cbr/\u003edepth,\u003cbr/\u003erole)\"]\n    Generate[\"Prompt assembly includes\u003cbr/\u003eextension prompt\"]\n    \n    Event --\u003e Check\n    Check --\u003e|\"Has summary\"| Format\n    Check --\u003e|\"No summary\"| Skip[\"Skip injection\"]\n    Format --\u003e Inject\n    Inject --\u003e Generate\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:77-89]()\n\n### Event-Driven UI Update Pattern\n\nThe Expressions extension demonstrates reactive UI updates:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Chat as Chat Rendering\n    participant Event as eventSource\n    participant Ext as Expressions Extension\n    participant API as Classification API\n    participant UI as Expression Display\n    \n    Chat-\u003e\u003eEvent: emit(CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED, index)\n    Event-\u003e\u003eExt: Trigger subscribed handler\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Extract message text\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eAPI: Classify emotion (LLM/Local/Extras)\n    API--\u003e\u003eExt: Return expression label\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Select sprite file for expression\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eUI: Update img src with sprite\n    UI--\u003e\u003eChat: Expression displayed\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:1-300]()\n\n### Settings Management Pattern\n\nStandard pattern for extension settings:\n\n1. **Define defaults:** Set default values in settings object\n2. **Load settings:** Read from `extension_settings` at init\n3. **Bind UI:** Connect form inputs to settings values\n4. **Save on change:** Call `saveSettingsDebounced()` when modified\n\n**Example from Memory extension:**\n\n```javascript\nconst defaultSettings = {\n    prompt: defaultPrompt,\n    position: extension_prompt_types.IN_PROMPT,\n    role: extension_prompt_roles.SYSTEM,\n    // ...\n};\n\nfunction loadSettings() {\n    // Merge defaults\n    for (const key of Object.keys(defaultSettings)) {\n        if (extension_settings.memory[key] === undefined) {\n            extension_settings.memory[key] = defaultSettings[key];\n        }\n    }\n    \n    // Bind to UI\n    $('#memory_prompt').val(extension_settings.memory.prompt);\n    $('#memory_prompt').on('input', function() {\n        extension_settings.memory.prompt = $(this).val();\n        saveSettingsDebounced();\n    });\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:108-168]()\n\n---\n\n## Asset Management for Extensions\n\nExtensions that manage files (sprites, audio, etc.) interact with the asset endpoints on the server.\n\n### Sprite Asset Management\n\nThe Expressions extension manages sprite files through dedicated API endpoints:\n\n**Endpoint structure:**\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |\n|----------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/sprites/get` | POST | List sprites for character |\n| `/api/sprites/upload` | POST | Upload new sprite file |\n| `/api/sprites/delete` | POST | Delete sprite file |\n\n**Client-side asset handling:**\n\n```javascript\n// Get sprite list\nconst response = await fetch('/api/sprites/get', {\n    method: 'POST',\n    headers: getRequestHeaders(),\n    body: JSON.stringify({ name: characterName })\n});\nconst sprites = await response.json();\n\n// Upload sprite\nconst formData = new FormData();\nformData.append('name', characterName);\nformData.append('file', fileBlob, filename);\nawait fetch('/api/sprites/upload', {\n    method: 'POST',\n    body: formData\n});\n```\n\n**Server-side sprite storage:**\n\nSprites are stored in character-specific directories:\n```\ndata/[user]/characters/\n└── CharacterName/\n    ├── joy.png\n    ├── anger-1.png\n    ├── sadness.expressive.png\n    └── ... (other expressions)\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/sprites.js:1-150](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:1-500]()\n\n### Generic Asset Storage\n\nFor other extension assets (audio, 3D models, etc.), use the generic asset API:\n\n**Valid asset categories:**\n- `bgm` - Background music\n- `ambient` - Ambient sounds\n- `blip` - UI sound effects\n- `live2d` - Live2D models\n- `vrm` - VRM 3D models\n- `character` - Character-specific assets\n- `temp` - Temporary files\n\n**Storage location:**\n```\ndata/[user]/assets/\n├── bgm/\n├── ambient/\n├── live2d/\n└── ... (other categories)\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/assets.js:14](), [src/endpoints/assets.js:84-96]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension Development Checklist\n\nWhen creating a new extension:\n\n- [ ] Create directory in `public/scripts/extensions/[name]/`\n- [ ] Create `index.js` with `MODULE_NAME` export\n- [ ] Define default settings structure\n- [ ] Create `settings.html` UI fragment\n- [ ] Create `style.css` for extension-specific styles\n- [ ] Register event handlers for relevant lifecycle events\n- [ ] Register slash commands if providing user-facing commands\n- [ ] Implement settings load/save with proper defaults\n- [ ] Handle cleanup in `CHAT_CHANGED` event if maintaining state\n- [ ] Document settings and commands in settings UI\n- [ ] Test with various character types and chat scenarios\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:1-100](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:1-200]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"3f:T7212,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Extension Architecture and APIs\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [public/index.html](public/index.html)\n- [public/script.js](public/script.js)\n- [public/scripts/group-chats.js](public/scripts/group-chats.js)\n- [public/scripts/instruct-mode.js](public/scripts/instruct-mode.js)\n- [public/scripts/power-user.js](public/scripts/power-user.js)\n- [public/scripts/utils.js](public/scripts/utils.js)\n- [public/scripts/world-info.js](public/scripts/world-info.js)\n- [public/style.css](public/style.css)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis document details the extension framework architecture in SillyTavern, including the module registration system, context API, event hooks, and integration mechanisms. Extensions are modular components that enhance core functionality without modifying the main codebase.\n\nFor information about specific built-in extensions (Image Generation, Expressions, Memory, etc.), see [Image Generation Extensions](#8.2), [Character Expressions System](#8.3), [Visual Novel Mode](#8.4), and [Image Captioning and Multimodal AI](#8.5). For command-based interaction systems, see [Slash Command System](#7.1) and [Quick Reply System](#7.2). [public/script.js:190](), [public/script.js:271](), [public/script.js:289-293](), [public/script.js:455-470](), [public/script.js:598](), [public/script.js:697]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension Lifecycle\n\n### Registration and Loading\n\nExtensions are initialized during application startup through the `initExtensions()` function. Each extension must provide a `manifest.json` file containing metadata and an entry point module.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant App as Application Bootstrap\n    participant ExtInit as initExtensions()\n    participant Loader as Module Loader\n    participant Manifest as manifest.json\n    participant ExtModule as Extension Module\n    participant Context as getContext()\n    \n    App-\u003e\u003eExtInit: Initialize extensions\n    ExtInit-\u003e\u003eLoader: Scan extension directories\n    Loader-\u003e\u003eManifest: Load manifest.json\n    Manifest--\u003e\u003eLoader: Return metadata\n    \n    Loader-\u003e\u003eExtModule: Dynamic import(module.js)\n    ExtModule-\u003e\u003eContext: Request context API\n    Context--\u003e\u003eExtModule: Return context object\n    \n    ExtModule-\u003e\u003eExtModule: Register slash commands\n    ExtModule-\u003e\u003eExtModule: Subscribe to events\n    ExtModule-\u003e\u003eExtModule: Initialize UI elements\n    \n    ExtModule--\u003e\u003eExtInit: Initialization complete\n    ExtInit--\u003e\u003eApp: All extensions loaded\n```\n\n**Extension Manifest Structure**:\n\n| Field | Type | Description |\n|-------|------|-------------|\n| `display_name` | string | Human-readable extension name |\n| `loading_order` | number | Load priority (lower = earlier) |\n| `requires` | string[] | Dependencies on other extensions |\n| `optional` | string[] | Optional dependency extensions |\n| `js` | string | Entry point JavaScript file path |\n| `css` | string | Optional CSS file path |\n| `author` | string | Extension author name |\n| `version` | string | Extension version number |\n\n**Sources**: [public/script.js:697](), [public/scripts/extensions.js:190]()\n\n### Settings Persistence\n\nExtension settings are stored in `extension_settings` object and persisted to `public/settings.json` on the server. Each extension typically namespaces its settings under a unique key.\n\n```javascript\n// Example extension settings structure\nextension_settings = {\n    'my-extension': {\n        enabled: true,\n        apiKey: '',\n        customOption: 'value'\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources**: [public/scripts/extensions.js:190]()\n\n---\n\n## The Context API\n\nThe `getContext()` function provides extensions with controlled access to core application state and functionality. This is the primary interface through which extensions interact with SillyTavern.\n\n### Core Context API Surface\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph ContextAPI[\"getContext() API Surface\"]\n        direction TB\n        \n        StateAccess[\"State Access\"]\n        StateAccess --\u003e Characters[\"characters\u003cbr/\u003eCharacter list array\"]\n        StateAccess --\u003e ThisChid[\"this_chid\u003cbr/\u003eSelected character ID\"]\n        StateAccess --\u003e Chat[\"chat\u003cbr/\u003eCurrent chat messages\"]\n        StateAccess --\u003e ChatMeta[\"chat_metadata\u003cbr/\u003eChat metadata object\"]\n        StateAccess --\u003e Groups[\"groups\u003cbr/\u003eGroup chat list\"]\n        StateAccess --\u003e SelGroup[\"selected_group\u003cbr/\u003eActive group ID\"]\n        \n        Functions[\"Core Functions\"]\n        Functions --\u003e Generate[\"Generate()\u003cbr/\u003eTrigger AI generation\"]\n        Functions --\u003e SaveSettings[\"saveSettings()\u003cbr/\u003ePersist configuration\"]\n        Functions --\u003e SaveChat[\"saveChatConditional()\u003cbr/\u003eSave chat messages\"]\n        Functions --\u003e GetChar[\"getCharacters()\u003cbr/\u003eReload character list\"]\n        Functions --\u003e SelectChar[\"selectCharacterById()\u003cbr/\u003eSwitch character\"]\n        Functions --\u003e Reroll[\"rerollSwipe()\u003cbr/\u003eRegenerate response\"]\n        \n        EventAPI[\"Event System\"]\n        EventAPI --\u003e EventSource[\"eventSource\u003cbr/\u003eEvent emitter\"]\n        EventAPI --\u003e EventTypes[\"event_types\u003cbr/\u003eEvent constants\"]\n        \n        Utilities[\"Utility Systems\"]\n        Utilities --\u003e SlashParser[\"SlashCommandParser\"]\n        Utilities --\u003e Tokenizers[\"getTokenCountAsync()\"]\n        Utilities --\u003e Templates[\"renderTemplate()\"]\n        Utilities --\u003e Popup[\"callGenericPopup()\"]\n    end\n    \n    style StateAccess fill:#e1f5ff\n    style Functions fill:#ffe1e1\n    style EventAPI fill:#f0e1ff\n    style Utilities fill:#fff3cd\n```\n\n**Key Context Properties**:\n\n| Category | Property | Type | Description |\n|----------|----------|------|-------------|\n| **State** | `characters` | Character[] | Array of all characters |\n| **State** | `this_chid` | string | Currently selected character index |\n| **State** | `chat` | ChatMessage[] | Current chat message array |\n| **State** | `chat_metadata` | ChatMetadata | Metadata for current chat |\n| **State** | `groups` | Group[] | Array of all group chats |\n| **State** | `selected_group` | string | Currently selected group ID |\n| **State** | `online_status` | string | Current API connection status |\n| **Functions** | `Generate()` | function | Triggers AI message generation |\n| **Functions** | `saveSettings()` | function | Persists application settings |\n| **Functions** | `reloadCurrentChat()` | function | Reloads active chat from disk |\n| **Functions** | `deleteMessage()` | function | Removes message from chat |\n| **Event System** | `eventSource` | EventEmitter | Central event bus |\n| **Event System** | `event_types` | enum | Event type constants |\n\n**Sources**: [public/scripts/st-context.js]() (referenced via [public/script.js:271]()), [public/script.js:289-293]()\n\n---\n\n## Event System Integration\n\nThe event system (`eventSource`) enables extensions to react to application state changes and lifecycle events. Extensions subscribe to events during initialization and can emit custom events.\n\n### Event Subscription Pattern\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Ext as Extension Module\n    participant Context as getContext()\n    participant EventSrc as eventSource\n    participant App as Core Application\n    \n    Ext-\u003e\u003eContext: getContext()\n    Context--\u003e\u003eExt: Return context object\n    \n    Ext-\u003e\u003eEventSrc: eventSource.on(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, handler)\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eEventSrc: eventSource.on(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, handler)\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eEventSrc: eventSource.on(event_types.GENERATION_STARTED, handler)\n    \n    Note over EventSrc: Extension is now listening\n    \n    App-\u003e\u003eEventSrc: emit(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, chatId)\n    EventSrc-\u003e\u003eExt: Invoke handler(chatId)\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Process chat change\n    \n    App-\u003e\u003eEventSrc: emit(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, index)\n    EventSrc-\u003e\u003eExt: Invoke handler(index)\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Process new message\n```\n\n### Common Event Types\n\n| Event Type | Timing | Arguments | Use Case |\n|------------|--------|-----------|----------|\n| `APP_INITIALIZED` | After core app loads | none | Initialize extension UI |\n| `APP_READY` | After all systems ready | none | Perform initial setup |\n| `CHAT_CHANGED` | When active chat switches | `(chatId)` | Update extension state |\n| `MESSAGE_RECEIVED` | After new message added | `(messageIndex)` | Process message content |\n| `CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED` | After message DOM created | `(messageIndex)` | Modify message display |\n| `GENERATION_STARTED` | Before AI generation | `(prompt, options)` | Inject prompts, modify params |\n| `GENERATION_ENDED` | After generation complete | `(text)` | Post-process output |\n| `IMPERSONATE_READY` | Before user impersonation | `(prompt)` | Modify impersonation prompt |\n| `GROUP_MEMBER_DRAFTED` | When group member selected | `(characterId)` | React to activation |\n| `WORLD_INFO_ACTIVATED` | When WI entries trigger | `(entries)` | Track context usage |\n\n**Event Handler Example**:\n\n```javascript\n// Inside extension module\nconst context = getContext();\n\ncontext.eventSource.on(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, (messageIndex) =\u003e {\n    const message = context.chat[messageIndex];\n    console.log('New message received:', message.mes);\n    // Extension logic here\n});\n\ncontext.eventSource.on(event_types.GENERATION_STARTED, (prompt, options) =\u003e {\n    // Inject additional prompts\n    setExtensionPrompt('MY_EXTENSION', 'Custom instruction text', 0);\n});\n```\n\n**Sources**: [public/scripts/events.js]() (referenced via [public/script.js:278]()), [public/script.js:734-738]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension Prompts System\n\nExtensions can inject text into the prompt assembly pipeline through the extension prompts system. This allows dynamic context injection without modifying chat messages.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph PromptTypes[\"Extension Prompt Types\"]\n        InPrompt[\"IN_PROMPT (0)\u003cbr/\u003eInside main context\"]\n        InChat[\"IN_CHAT (1)\u003cbr/\u003eAs chat messages\"]\n        BeforePrompt[\"BEFORE_PROMPT (2)\u003cbr/\u003eBefore all context\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph PromptRoles[\"Extension Prompt Roles\"]\n        System[\"SYSTEM (0)\u003cbr/\u003eSystem message role\"]\n        User[\"USER (1)\u003cbr/\u003eUser message role\"]\n        Assistant[\"ASSISTANT (2)\u003cbr/\u003eAssistant message role\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Registration[\"Registration Flow\"]\n        SetPrompt[\"setExtensionPrompt()\u003cbr/\u003eRegister prompt text\"]\n        PromptObj[\"extension_prompts object\u003cbr/\u003e{ extensionName: {...} }\"]\n        \n        SetPrompt --\u003e PromptObj\n        \n        PromptObj --\u003e Position[\"position: extension_prompt_types\"]\n        PromptObj --\u003e Role[\"role: extension_prompt_roles\"]\n        PromptObj --\u003e Text[\"content: string\"]\n        PromptObj --\u003e Depth[\"depth: number\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Assembly[\"Prompt Assembly\"]\n        PromptMgr[\"PromptManager\u003cbr/\u003eAssembles final prompt\"]\n        ExtCheck[\"shouldWIAddPrompt()\u003cbr/\u003eCheck if prompt should activate\"]\n        \n        PromptMgr --\u003e ExtCheck\n        ExtCheck --\u003e InPrompt\n        ExtCheck --\u003e InChat\n        ExtCheck --\u003e BeforePrompt\n    end\n    \n    Registration --\u003e Assembly\n    InPrompt --\u003e PromptMgr\n    InChat --\u003e PromptMgr\n    BeforePrompt --\u003e PromptMgr\n    System --\u003e PromptMgr\n    User --\u003e PromptMgr\n    Assistant --\u003e PromptMgr\n    \n    style PromptTypes fill:#e1f5ff\n    style PromptRoles fill:#ffe1e1\n    style Registration fill:#f0e1ff\n    style Assembly fill:#fff3cd\n```\n\n### Extension Prompt Configuration\n\n**Prompt Type Enum**:\n```javascript\nextension_prompt_types = {\n    NONE: -1,        // Prompt disabled\n    IN_PROMPT: 0,    // Added to context template\n    IN_CHAT: 1,      // Added as chat message\n    BEFORE_PROMPT: 2 // Added before all context\n}\n```\n\n**Prompt Role Enum**:\n```javascript\nextension_prompt_roles = {\n    SYSTEM: 0,    // System message\n    USER: 1,      // User message\n    ASSISTANT: 2  // Assistant message\n}\n```\n\n**Extension Prompt Object Structure**:\n\n| Field | Type | Description |\n|-------|------|-------------|\n| `position` | number | Prompt injection position (type enum) |\n| `role` | number | Message role (role enum) |\n| `content` | string | Actual prompt text |\n| `depth` | number | Insertion depth for IN_CHAT type |\n\n**Usage Example**:\n\n```javascript\n// Extension registers a prompt\nconst context = getContext();\n\ncontext.setExtensionPrompt(\n    'MY_EXTENSION_KEY',      // Unique identifier\n    'Additional context...',  // Prompt text\n    extension_prompt_types.IN_PROMPT, // Type\n    0,                        // Depth (for IN_CHAT)\n    false,                    // Scan depth\n    extension_prompt_roles.SYSTEM // Role\n);\n\n// Later, extension can update or remove prompt\ncontext.setExtensionPrompt('MY_EXTENSION_KEY', '', -1); // Remove\n```\n\n**Sources**: [public/script.js:455-470](), [public/script.js:598](), [public/scripts/authors-note.js:219]()\n\n---\n\n## Slash Command Registration\n\nExtensions register custom slash commands through the `SlashCommandParser` API, enabling users to invoke extension functionality via text commands.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Ext as Extension Module\n    participant Parser as SlashCommandParser\n    participant Registry as Command Registry\n    participant User as User Input\n    participant Executor as Command Executor\n    \n    Ext-\u003e\u003eParser: registerSlashCommand()\n    Note over Ext,Parser: name, callback, aliases,\u003cbr/\u003ehelpText, arguments\n    \n    Parser-\u003e\u003eRegistry: Add command definition\n    Registry-\u003e\u003eRegistry: Store SlashCommand object\n    \n    Note over Registry: Extension commands registered\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eParser: Enter \"/mycommand arg1 arg2\"\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eParser: Parse command string\n    Parser-\u003e\u003eRegistry: Lookup command\n    Registry--\u003e\u003eParser: Return SlashCommand\n    \n    Parser-\u003e\u003eExecutor: Execute with arguments\n    Executor-\u003e\u003eExt: Invoke callback(args)\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Process command\n    Ext--\u003e\u003eUser: Return result or output\n```\n\n### Slash Command Definition\n\n**SlashCommand Constructor**:\n\n```javascript\n// From extension module\nSlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(SlashCommand.fromProps({\n    name: 'mycommand',\n    callback: (args, value) =\u003e {\n        // Command implementation\n        return 'Command executed';\n    },\n    aliases: ['mycmd'],\n    helpString: 'Description of what command does',\n    returns: 'string',\n    namedArgumentList: [\n        SlashCommandNamedArgument.fromProps({\n            name: 'option',\n            description: 'Option description',\n            typeList: [ARGUMENT_TYPE.STRING],\n            defaultValue: 'default',\n            enumProvider: () =\u003e ['choice1', 'choice2']\n        })\n    ],\n    unnamedArgumentList: [\n        SlashCommandArgument.fromProps({\n            description: 'Unnamed arg description',\n            typeList: [ARGUMENT_TYPE.NUMBER],\n            isRequired: true\n        })\n    ]\n}));\n```\n\n**Command Argument Types**:\n\n| Type | Description | Example |\n|------|-------------|---------|\n| `ARGUMENT_TYPE.STRING` | Text string | \"hello world\" |\n| `ARGUMENT_TYPE.NUMBER` | Numeric value | 42 |\n| `ARGUMENT_TYPE.BOOLEAN` | Boolean flag | true/false |\n| `ARGUMENT_TYPE.VARIABLE_NAME` | Variable reference | {{var}} |\n| `ARGUMENT_TYPE.CLOSURE` | Code block | `{:command:}` |\n| `ARGUMENT_TYPE.LIST` | Array of values | [\"a\", \"b\", \"c\"] |\n\n**Sources**: [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js]() (referenced via [public/script.js:191]()), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommand.js:56](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandArgument.js:16]()\n\n---\n\n## Tool Calling API\n\nExtensions can register functions that AI models can invoke through the tool calling API. This enables dynamic function execution based on AI decisions.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Registration[\"Tool Registration\"]\n        RegTool[\"registerFunctionTool()\u003cbr/\u003eToolManager API\"]\n        ToolDef[\"Tool Definition Object\"]\n        \n        RegTool --\u003e ToolName[\"name: string\"]\n        RegTool --\u003e ToolDesc[\"description: string\"]\n        RegTool --\u003e ToolParams[\"parameters: JSONSchema\"]\n        RegTool --\u003e ToolFn[\"action: (args) =\u003e result\"]\n        \n        ToolDef --\u003e ToolName\n        ToolDef --\u003e ToolDesc\n        ToolDef --\u003e ToolParams\n        ToolDef --\u003e ToolFn\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ToolExecution[\"Tool Calling Flow\"]\n        AIGen[\"AI Generation Request\"]\n        ToolSupport[\"Model supports tools?\"]\n        ToolInclude[\"Include tool definitions\"]\n        AIResponse[\"AI chooses tool\"]\n        ToolInvoke[\"Invoke registered function\"]\n        ToolResult[\"Return result to AI\"]\n        \n        AIGen --\u003e ToolSupport\n        ToolSupport --\u003e|Yes| ToolInclude\n        ToolSupport --\u003e|No| Skip[\"Skip tools\"]\n        ToolInclude --\u003e AIResponse\n        AIResponse --\u003e ToolInvoke\n        ToolInvoke --\u003e ToolResult\n        ToolResult --\u003e AIGen\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ToolFormats[\"Provider Formats\"]\n        OpenAIFormat[\"OpenAI Functions/Tools\"]\n        ClaudeFormat[\"Claude Tools\"]\n        GoogleFormat[\"Google Function Calling\"]\n        \n        ToolInclude --\u003e OpenAIFormat\n        ToolInclude --\u003e ClaudeFormat\n        ToolInclude --\u003e GoogleFormat\n    end\n    \n    Registration --\u003e ToolExecution\n    \n    style Registration fill:#e1f5ff\n    style ToolExecution fill:#ffe1e1\n    style ToolFormats fill:#fff3cd\n```\n\n### Tool Registration Pattern\n\n**Tool Definition Structure**:\n\n```javascript\n// Inside extension module\nconst context = getContext();\n\n// Register a tool\ncontext.registerFunctionTool({\n    name: 'get_weather',\n    description: 'Get current weather for a location',\n    parameters: {\n        type: 'object',\n        properties: {\n            location: {\n                type: 'string',\n                description: 'City name or coordinates'\n            },\n            units: {\n                type: 'string',\n                enum: ['celsius', 'fahrenheit'],\n                description: 'Temperature units'\n            }\n        },\n        required: ['location']\n    },\n    action: async (args) =\u003e {\n        // Tool implementation\n        const { location, units = 'celsius' } = args;\n        const weather = await fetchWeatherData(location, units);\n        return JSON.stringify(weather);\n    }\n});\n```\n\n**Tool Invocation Flow**:\n\n1. Extension registers tool definition during initialization\n2. `ToolManager` collects all registered tools\n3. When generating, tools are included in request if model supports them\n4. AI response includes tool call with arguments\n5. `ToolManager` invokes corresponding action function\n6. Result is formatted and sent back to AI\n\n**Sources**: [public/scripts/tool-calling.js:265]() (referenced via [public/script.js:265]()), [public/script.js:699]()\n\n---\n\n## Generation Interceptors\n\nExtensions can intercept and modify generation parameters before requests are sent to AI providers through generation interceptors.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User as User Action\n    participant Gen as Generate()\n    participant Interceptors as runGenerationInterceptors()\n    participant Ext1 as Extension 1\n    participant Ext2 as Extension 2\n    participant API as AI API Request\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eGen: Trigger generation\n    Gen-\u003e\u003eGen: Assemble prompt\n    Gen-\u003e\u003eGen: Prepare parameters\n    \n    Gen-\u003e\u003eInterceptors: Pass prompt \u0026 params\n    \n    Interceptors-\u003e\u003eExt1: Call interceptor function\n    Ext1-\u003e\u003eExt1: Modify prompt/params\n    Ext1--\u003e\u003eInterceptors: Return modified\n    \n    Interceptors-\u003e\u003eExt2: Call interceptor function\n    Ext2-\u003e\u003eExt2: Modify prompt/params\n    Ext2--\u003e\u003eInterceptors: Return modified\n    \n    Interceptors--\u003e\u003eGen: Return final prompt \u0026 params\n    Gen-\u003e\u003eAPI: Send modified request\n    API--\u003e\u003eGen: Return response\n    Gen--\u003e\u003eUser: Display result\n```\n\n**Interceptor Registration**:\n\n```javascript\n// Extension registers interceptor\nconst context = getContext();\n\ncontext.registerGenerationInterceptor((prompt, params) =\u003e {\n    // Modify prompt\n    prompt = 'Additional instruction:\\n' + prompt;\n    \n    // Modify parameters\n    params.temperature = Math.min(params.temperature, 0.7);\n    params.top_p = 0.9;\n    \n    // Return modified values\n    return { prompt, params };\n});\n```\n\n**Use Cases**:\n- Adding system-level instructions to all generations\n- Enforcing parameter constraints\n- Logging generation requests\n- A/B testing different prompt formulations\n\n**Sources**: [public/scripts/extensions.js:190]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension Settings Management\n\nExtensions persist configuration through the `extension_settings` object, which is saved to the server and synchronized across sessions.\n\n### Settings Storage Pattern\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph ExtModule[\"Extension Module\"]\n        InitSettings[\"Initialize default settings\"]\n        ReadSettings[\"Read from extension_settings\"]\n        UpdateSettings[\"Update settings in memory\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph SettingsObject[\"extension_settings Object\"]\n        ExtKey[\"extension_settings['my-extension']\"]\n        ExtData[\"{ enabled: true, ... }\"]\n        ExtKey --\u003e ExtData\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Persistence[\"Persistence Layer\"]\n        SaveDebounced[\"saveSettingsDebounced()\"]\n        SettingsAPI[\"/api/settings/save\"]\n        SettingsFile[\"public/settings.json\"]\n        \n        SaveDebounced --\u003e SettingsAPI\n        SettingsAPI --\u003e SettingsFile\n    end\n    \n    subgraph UIIntegration[\"UI Integration\"]\n        ExtPanel[\"Extension Settings Panel\"]\n        Inputs[\"Input Elements\"]\n        EventHandlers[\"Change Handlers\"]\n        \n        ExtPanel --\u003e Inputs\n        Inputs --\u003e EventHandlers\n    end\n    \n    InitSettings --\u003e ReadSettings\n    ReadSettings --\u003e SettingsObject\n    UpdateSettings --\u003e SettingsObject\n    SettingsObject --\u003e SaveDebounced\n    \n    EventHandlers --\u003e UpdateSettings\n    ReadSettings --\u003e ExtPanel\n    \n    style ExtModule fill:#e1f5ff\n    style SettingsObject fill:#ffe1e1\n    style Persistence fill:#f0e1ff\n    style UIIntegration fill:#fff3cd\n```\n\n**Settings Management Example**:\n\n```javascript\n// Extension initialization\nconst context = getContext();\nconst extensionName = 'my-extension';\n\n// Define default settings\nconst defaultSettings = {\n    enabled: false,\n    apiKey: '',\n    temperature: 0.7,\n    maxTokens: 100\n};\n\n// Initialize settings if not present\nif (!context.extensionSettings[extensionName]) {\n    context.extensionSettings[extensionName] = defaultSettings;\n}\n\n// Read settings\nconst settings = context.extensionSettings[extensionName];\n\n// Update setting\nsettings.enabled = true;\ncontext.saveSettingsDebounced();\n\n// Settings UI in HTML\n$('#my_extension_enabled').on('change', function() {\n    const enabled = $(this).prop('checked');\n    context.extensionSettings[extensionName].enabled = enabled;\n    context.saveSettingsDebounced();\n});\n```\n\n**Settings Best Practices**:\n- Always define default values\n- Validate user input before storing\n- Use debounced save to avoid excessive writes\n- Namespace settings under unique extension key\n- Document setting purposes in manifest\n\n**Sources**: [public/scripts/extensions.js:190](), [public/script.js:441]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension Integration Patterns\n\nExtensions integrate with core functionality through several well-defined patterns, each serving specific use cases.\n\n### Message Rendering Hook Pattern\n\nUsed by extensions that need to modify or annotate message display.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Core as Core Chat Display\n    participant Event as eventSource\n    participant Expressions as Expressions Extension\n    participant SD as Stable Diffusion Extension\n    \n    Core-\u003e\u003eCore: Add message to DOM\n    Core-\u003e\u003eEvent: emit(CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED, index)\n    \n    Event-\u003e\u003eExpressions: Message rendered event\n    Expressions-\u003e\u003eExpressions: Classify emotion\n    Expressions-\u003e\u003eExpressions: Update sprite display\n    Expressions-\u003e\u003eCore: Modify message DOM\n    \n    Event-\u003e\u003eSD: Message rendered event\n    SD-\u003e\u003eSD: Check for image triggers\n    SD-\u003e\u003eSD: Generate image if triggered\n    SD-\u003e\u003eCore: Append image to message\n```\n\n**Implementation Example**:\n\n```javascript\ncontext.eventSource.on(event_types.CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED, (messageIndex) =\u003e {\n    const message = context.chat[messageIndex];\n    const messageElement = $(`#chat [mesid=\"${messageIndex}\"]`);\n    \n    // Extension-specific processing\n    if (shouldProcessMessage(message)) {\n        const annotation = createAnnotation(message);\n        messageElement.find('.mes_text').append(annotation);\n    }\n});\n```\n\n**Sources**: [public/script.js:278]()\n\n### Chat Change Hook Pattern\n\nUsed by extensions that need to react to chat switching or loading.\n\n```javascript\ncontext.eventSource.on(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, (chatId) =\u003e {\n    // Load extension-specific data for this chat\n    loadExtensionData(chatId);\n    \n    // Update UI to reflect new chat\n    updateExtensionUI();\n    \n    // Reset extension state\n    resetExtensionState();\n});\n```\n\n### Generation Hook Pattern\n\nUsed by extensions that inject prompts or modify generation parameters.\n\n```javascript\ncontext.eventSource.on(event_types.GENERATION_STARTED, (prompt, options) =\u003e {\n    // Inject additional context\n    if (shouldInjectContext()) {\n        context.setExtensionPrompt(\n            'MY_EXTENSION',\n            getAdditionalContext(),\n            extension_prompt_types.IN_PROMPT,\n            0,\n            false,\n            extension_prompt_roles.SYSTEM\n        );\n    }\n    \n    // Modify generation parameters\n    options.temperature = adjustTemperature(options.temperature);\n});\n```\n\n**Sources**: [public/script.js:455-470](), [public/script.js:598]()\n\n---\n\n## Built-In Extension Examples\n\nThe following table summarizes how core extensions utilize the extension framework:\n\n| Extension | Key Integration Points | Events Used | APIs Used |\n|-----------|----------------------|-------------|-----------|\n| **Expressions** | Message rendering, emotion classification | `CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED`, `USER_MESSAGE_RENDERED` | getContext(), sprite APIs |\n| **Memory** | Chat summarization, prompt injection | `GENERATION_STARTED`, `CHAT_CHANGED` | setExtensionPrompt(), API proxies |\n| **Stable Diffusion** | Image generation on triggers | `MESSAGE_RECEIVED`, `GENERATION_ENDED` | Tool calling, API proxies |\n| **Quick Reply** | Command automation, auto-execution | Multiple trigger events | SlashCommandParser, eventSource |\n| **Vectors** | RAG retrieval, embedding generation | `GENERATION_STARTED`, `MESSAGE_RECEIVED` | setExtensionPrompt(), vector APIs |\n\n**Sources**: [public/scripts/extensions.js:190]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension Development Checklist\n\nWhen developing a new extension:\n\n1. **Manifest**: Create `manifest.json` with metadata and entry point\n2. **Module Structure**: Export initialization function that accepts context\n3. **Context API**: Call `getContext()` to access core functionality\n4. **Settings**: Initialize default settings in `extension_settings`\n5. **Event Subscriptions**: Subscribe to relevant lifecycle events\n6. **Slash Commands**: Register custom commands if needed\n7. **Extension Prompts**: Register prompt injections if needed\n8. **UI Integration**: Add settings panel HTML to extension directory\n9. **Cleanup**: Implement proper cleanup on disable/unload\n10. **Documentation**: Document settings, commands, and usage\n\n**Sources**: [public/scripts/extensions.js:190](), [public/script.js:697-698]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"40:T83ed,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Image Generation Extensions\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js](public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/settings.html](public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/settings.html)\n- [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js](src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThis page documents the Image Generation extension system, which integrates 20+ image generation providers (Stable Diffusion, DALL-E, Flux, and others) into SillyTavern. The extension provides multiple generation modes, trigger mechanisms, and a unified abstraction layer for diverse image generation APIs.\n\nFor general extension framework documentation, see [Extension Architecture and APIs](#8.1). For character sprite systems, see [Character Expressions System](#8.3). For vision/multimodal capabilities, see [Image Captioning and Multimodal AI](#8.5).\n\n## Overview\n\nThe Image Generation extension (`stable-diffusion`) is implemented as a client-side module ([public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js]()) with corresponding server-side API endpoints ([src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js]()). It abstracts 20+ different image generation providers behind a unified interface, allowing users to generate images through multiple trigger mechanisms with context-aware prompt assembly.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Client[\"Client-Side Extension\"]\n        SDIndex[\"index.js\u003cbr/\u003e(Main Extension)\"]\n        Settings[\"settings.html\u003cbr/\u003e(UI Configuration)\"]\n        TriggerSys[\"Trigger System\"]\n        PromptAssembly[\"Prompt Assembly\"]\n        ProviderConfig[\"Provider Configuration\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Server[\"Server-Side API\"]\n        MainRouter[\"/api/sd/*\u003cbr/\u003e(Main Router)\"]\n        ProviderRouters[\"Provider-Specific Routers\"]\n        Auto[\"/api/sd/generate\u003cbr/\u003e(AUTOMATIC1111)\"]\n        Comfy[\"/api/sd/comfy/*\u003cbr/\u003e(ComfyUI)\"]\n        Novel[\"NovelAI Integration\"]\n        OpenAI[\"OpenAI Integration\"]\n        Others[\"17+ Other Providers\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph External[\"External Services\"]\n        A1111[\"AUTOMATIC1111\u003cbr/\u003eSD.Next\u003cbr/\u003eDrawThings\"]\n        ComfyUI[\"ComfyUI\u003cbr/\u003eStandard/RunPod\"]\n        CloudAPIs[\"OpenAI\u003cbr/\u003eNovelAI\u003cbr/\u003eStability AI\u003cbr/\u003eTogetherAI\u003cbr/\u003eetc.\"]\n    end\n    \n    SDIndex --\u003e|\"HTTP Requests\"| MainRouter\n    TriggerSys --\u003e|\"Initiates\"| PromptAssembly\n    PromptAssembly --\u003e|\"Generates\"| SDIndex\n    ProviderConfig --\u003e|\"Configures\"| SDIndex\n    \n    MainRouter --\u003e ProviderRouters\n    ProviderRouters --\u003e Auto\n    ProviderRouters --\u003e Comfy\n    ProviderRouters --\u003e Novel\n    ProviderRouters --\u003e OpenAI\n    ProviderRouters --\u003e Others\n    \n    Auto --\u003e A1111\n    Comfy --\u003e ComfyUI\n    Novel --\u003e CloudAPIs\n    OpenAI --\u003e CloudAPIs\n    Others --\u003e CloudAPIs\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:1-100](), [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:1-50]()\n\n## Supported Providers\n\nThe extension supports 20+ image generation providers through a unified abstraction layer. Each provider is identified by a source constant in the `sources` object.\n\n| Provider | Source ID | Type | Configuration |\n|----------|-----------|------|---------------|\n| AUTOMATIC1111 | `auto` | Local/Remote | URL + Auth |\n| SD.Next (vladmandic) | `vlad` | Local/Remote | URL + Auth |\n| stable-diffusion.cpp | `sdcpp` | Local | URL |\n| ComfyUI | `comfy` | Local/Remote/Serverless | URL + Workflow |\n| DrawThings | `drawthings` | Local | URL + Auth |\n| NovelAI | `novel` | Cloud API | API Key (from NAI settings) |\n| OpenAI (DALL-E) | `openai` | Cloud API | API Key (from OpenAI settings) |\n| Stability AI | `stability` | Cloud API | API Key |\n| AI Horde | `horde` | Distributed | API Key (from Horde settings) |\n| TogetherAI | `togetherai` | Cloud API | API Key |\n| Pollinations | `pollinations` | Cloud API | API Key |\n| HuggingFace | `huggingface` | Cloud API | API Key |\n| BFL (Black Forest Labs) | `bfl` | Cloud API | API Key |\n| FAL.AI | `falai` | Cloud API | API Key |\n| Chutes | `chutes` | Cloud API | API Key |\n| Electron Hub | `electronhub` | Cloud API | API Key |\n| NanoGPT | `nanogpt` | Cloud API | API Key |\n| xAI (Grok) | `xai` | Cloud API | API Key |\n| Google AI | `google` | Cloud API | API Key (from Google settings) |\n| Z.AI | `zai` | Cloud API | API Key |\n| AI/ML API | `aimlapi` | Cloud API | API Key |\n| OpenRouter | `openrouter` | Cloud API | API Key |\n| Extras API | `extras` | Legacy | Deprecated |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:78-102](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/settings.html:43-67]()\n\n## Generation Modes\n\nThe extension provides multiple generation modes, each with a specific purpose and prompt template. Generation modes are defined in the `generationMode` enumeration.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph Modes[\"Generation Modes\"]\n        TOOL[\"-2: TOOL\u003cbr/\u003e(Function Tool)\"]\n        MESSAGE[\"-1: MESSAGE\u003cbr/\u003e(Chat Template)\"]\n        CHARACTER[\"0: CHARACTER\u003cbr/\u003e(Full Body)\"]\n        USER[\"1: USER\u003cbr/\u003e(User Portrait)\"]\n        SCENARIO[\"2: SCENARIO\u003cbr/\u003e(Scene Description)\"]\n        RAW_LAST[\"3: RAW_LAST\u003cbr/\u003e(Verbatim Message)\"]\n        NOW[\"4: NOW\u003cbr/\u003e(Visual Details)\"]\n        FACE[\"5: FACE\u003cbr/\u003e(Close-up Portrait)\"]\n        FREE[\"6: FREE\u003cbr/\u003e(User-Specified)\"]\n        BACKGROUND[\"7: BACKGROUND\u003cbr/\u003e(Scene Background)\"]\n        CHAR_MM[\"8: CHARACTER_MULTIMODAL\"]\n        USER_MM[\"9: USER_MULTIMODAL\"]\n        FACE_MM[\"10: FACE_MULTIMODAL\"]\n        FREE_EXT[\"11: FREE_EXTENDED\u003cbr/\u003e(LLM-Extended)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Templates[\"Prompt Templates\"]\n        ToolTemplate[\"Tool description\"]\n        MessageTemplate[\"Chat insertion\"]\n        CharTemplate[\"Full body portrait,\u003cbr/\u003edetailed description\"]\n        FaceTemplate[\"Close-up facial portrait,\u003cbr/\u003efacial features\"]\n        MMTemplate[\"Multimodal caption\u003cbr/\u003efrom avatar\"]\n    end\n    \n    TOOL --\u003e ToolTemplate\n    MESSAGE --\u003e MessageTemplate\n    CHARACTER --\u003e CharTemplate\n    USER --\u003e CharTemplate\n    FACE --\u003e FaceTemplate\n    CHAR_MM --\u003e MMTemplate\n    USER_MM --\u003e MMTemplate\n    FACE_MM --\u003e MMTemplate\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:117-154](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:178-222]()\n\n### Mode Descriptions\n\n- **TOOL (-2)**: Used when function tool calling is enabled; describes what the tool generates\n- **MESSAGE (-1)**: Template for inserting generated images into chat messages\n- **CHARACTER (0)**: Generates a full-body portrait of the current character using LLM description\n- **USER (1)**: Generates a full-body portrait of the user persona\n- **SCENARIO (2)**: Generates a scene depicting the current story context\n- **RAW_LAST (3)**: Uses the last chat message verbatim as the prompt\n- **NOW (4)**: Generates visual details from the most recent message\n- **FACE (5)**: Generates a close-up facial portrait of the character\n- **FREE (6)**: User-specified subject (from interactive mode or commands)\n- **BACKGROUND (7)**: Generates scene background/environment\n- **CHARACTER_MULTIMODAL (8)**: Uses multimodal captioning on character avatar\n- **USER_MULTIMODAL (9)**: Uses multimodal captioning on user avatar\n- **FACE_MULTIMODAL (10)**: Uses multimodal captioning for face portrait\n- **FREE_EXTENDED (11)**: User subject extended with LLM description\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:140-154]()\n\n## Trigger Mechanisms\n\nImages can be generated through four distinct initiator types, each with different visibility settings and use cases.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Initiators[\"Trigger Initiators\"]\n        Command[\"command\u003cbr/\u003e(Slash Commands)\"]\n        Action[\"action\u003cbr/\u003e(UI Button/Wand)\"]\n        Interactive[\"interactive\u003cbr/\u003e(Message Detection)\"]\n        Wand[\"wand\u003cbr/\u003e(Extension Menu)\"]\n        Swipe[\"swipe\u003cbr/\u003e(Swipe Handler)\"]\n        Tool[\"tool\u003cbr/\u003e(Function Calling)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Visibility[\"Visibility Controls\"]\n        CmdVis[\"sd_command_visible\"]\n        WandVis[\"sd_wand_visible\"]\n        InterVis[\"sd_interactive_visible\"]\n        ToolVis[\"sd_tool_visible\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Functions[\"Implementation\"]\n        GeneratePicture[\"generatePicture(initiator, args, subject, message)\"]\n        ProcessTriggers[\"processTriggers(chat, _, abort, type)\"]\n        SlashSD[\"/sd slash command\"]\n        RegisterTool[\"registerFunctionTool()\"]\n    end\n    \n    Command --\u003e CmdVis\n    Wand --\u003e WandVis\n    Interactive --\u003e InterVis\n    Tool --\u003e ToolVis\n    \n    Command --\u003e SlashSD\n    Interactive --\u003e ProcessTriggers\n    Tool --\u003e RegisterTool\n    \n    SlashSD --\u003e GeneratePicture\n    ProcessTriggers --\u003e GeneratePicture\n    RegisterTool --\u003e GeneratePicture\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:108-115](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:372-438]()\n\n### Interactive Mode\n\nInteractive mode automatically detects user intents to generate images based on message patterns. The trigger detection uses a regex pattern to identify generation requests.\n\n**Activation Pattern:**\n```javascript\nactivationRegex: /\\b(send|mail|imagine|generate|make|create|draw|paint|render|show)\\b.{0,10}\\b(pic|picture|image|drawing|painting|photo|photograph)\\b(?:\\s+of)?(?:\\s+(?:a|an|the|this|that|those|your)?\\s+)?(.+)/i\n```\n\n**Special Case Keywords:**\n\n| Subject | Detected Keywords | Maps To Mode |\n|---------|------------------|--------------|\n| Character | `you`, `yourself` | CHARACTER (0) |\n| User | `me`, `myself` | USER (1) |\n| Scenario | `story`, `scenario`, `whole story` | SCENARIO (2) |\n| Last Message | `last message` | NOW (4) |\n| Face | `face`, `portrait`, `selfie` | FACE (5) |\n| Background | `background`, `scene`, `scenery` | BACKGROUND (7) |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:166-176](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:379-437]()\n\n### Function Tool Integration\n\nWhen enabled (`extension_settings.sd.function_tool`), the extension registers as a function tool that AI models can invoke to generate images.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant LLM as \"AI Model\"\n    participant ToolMgr as \"ToolManager\"\n    participant SDExt as \"SD Extension\"\n    participant Server as \"Server API\"\n    participant Provider as \"Image Provider\"\n    \n    LLM-\u003e\u003eToolMgr: Call generate_image tool\n    ToolMgr-\u003e\u003eSDExt: Invoke registered tool\n    SDExt-\u003e\u003eSDExt: Extract prompt from args\n    SDExt-\u003e\u003eServer: POST /api/sd/{provider}/generate\n    Server-\u003e\u003eProvider: Generate image\n    Provider--\u003e\u003eServer: Return image data\n    Server--\u003e\u003eSDExt: Base64 image\n    SDExt-\u003e\u003eSDExt: Format as chat message\n    SDExt--\u003e\u003eToolMgr: Return message\n    ToolMgr--\u003e\u003eLLM: Tool result\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:384-386](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:3273-3330]()\n\n## Client-Side Architecture\n\nThe main extension module manages configuration, prompt assembly, provider selection, and result handling.\n\n### Key Components\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Core[\"Core Functions\"]\n        GeneratePicture[\"generatePicture(initiator, args, subject, message)\"]\n        GetPrompt[\"getPrompt(generationMode, message, trigger, ...)\"]\n        SendGenerationRequest[\"sendGenerationRequest(prompt, negativePrompt, ...)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Settings[\"Settings Management\"]\n        LoadSettings[\"loadSettings()\"]\n        SaveSettings[\"saveSettingsDebounced()\"]\n        ExtSettings[\"extension_settings.sd\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph PromptSystem[\"Prompt System\"]\n        PromptTemplates[\"promptTemplates{}\"]\n        CombinePrefixes[\"combinePrefixes(str1, str2, macro)\"]\n        GetCharPrefix[\"getCharacterPrefix()\"]\n        RefinePrompt[\"refinePrompt(prompt, isNegative)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Providers[\"Provider Handling\"]\n        ToggleControls[\"toggleSourceControls()\"]\n        OnSourceChange[\"onSourceChange()\"]\n        LoadOptions[\"loadSettingOptions()\"]\n    end\n    \n    GeneratePicture --\u003e GetPrompt\n    GetPrompt --\u003e PromptTemplates\n    GetPrompt --\u003e GetCharPrefix\n    GeneratePicture --\u003e RefinePrompt\n    GeneratePicture --\u003e SendGenerationRequest\n    \n    LoadSettings --\u003e ExtSettings\n    ExtSettings --\u003e ToggleControls\n    OnSourceChange --\u003e LoadOptions\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:1400-1700](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:466-584]()\n\n### Extension Registration\n\nThe extension integrates with the SillyTavern extension framework through standard hooks:\n\n**Module Name:** `MODULE_NAME = 'sd'`\n\n**Event Listeners:**\n- `CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED`: Triggers on message swipe if enabled\n- `CHAT_CHANGED`: Updates character-specific prompts\n- `USER_MESSAGE_RENDERED`: Not used directly\n- `GENERATION_STARTED`: Processes interactive mode triggers via `processTriggers()`\n\n**Slash Commands:**\n- `/sd` - Main image generation command with multiple subcommands\n- `/sd-settings` - Opens SD settings panel\n- `/comfyui-workflow` - Sets ComfyUI workflow\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:66-68](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:3740-3900]()\n\n## Server-Side Architecture\n\nThe server provides a routing layer that dispatches requests to provider-specific handlers, normalizes responses, and manages API keys.\n\n### Main Router Structure\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph MainRouter[\"/api/sd Router\"]\n        Ping[\"/ping\u003cbr/\u003e(AUTOMATIC1111/SD.Next)\"]\n        Generate[\"/generate\u003cbr/\u003e(AUTOMATIC1111)\"]\n        Models[\"/models\u003cbr/\u003e(Get available models)\"]\n        Samplers[\"/samplers\u003cbr/\u003e(Get samplers)\"]\n        Schedulers[\"/schedulers\u003cbr/\u003e(Get schedulers)\"]\n        Upscalers[\"/upscalers\u003cbr/\u003e(Get upscalers)\"]\n        Vaes[\"/vaes\u003cbr/\u003e(Get VAE models)\"]\n        GetModel[\"/get-model\"]\n        SetModel[\"/set-model\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph SubRouters[\"Provider Sub-Routers\"]\n        ComfyRouter[\"/comfy/*\"]\n        ComfyRunPodRouter[\"/comfyrunpod/*\"]\n        TogetherRouter[\"/together/*\"]\n        SdcppRouter[\"/sdcpp/*\"]\n        DrawthingsRouter[\"/drawthings/*\"]\n        PollinationsRouter[\"/pollinations/*\"]\n        StabilityRouter[\"/stability/*\"]\n        HFRouter[\"/huggingface/*\"]\n        ChutesRouter[\"/chutes/*\"]\n        ElectronhubRouter[\"/electronhub/*\"]\n        NanoGPTRouter[\"/nanogpt/*\"]\n        BFLRouter[\"/bfl/*\"]\n        FalaiRouter[\"/falai/*\"]\n        XAIRouter[\"/xai/*\"]\n        AimlapiRouter[\"/aimlapi/*\"]\n        ZAIRouter[\"/zai/*\"]\n    end\n    \n    MainRouter --\u003e SubRouters\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:30-2029]()\n\n### Provider Adapter Pattern\n\nEach provider implements a consistent interface while handling provider-specific quirks:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph AdapterInterface[\"Common Interface\"]\n        Ping[\"POST /ping\"]\n        Models[\"POST /models\"]\n        Samplers[\"POST /samplers\"]\n        Generate[\"POST /generate\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Implementations[\"Provider Implementations\"]\n        Auto[\"AUTOMATIC1111\u003cbr/\u003eStandard SD API\"]\n        Comfy[\"ComfyUI\u003cbr/\u003eWorkflow-based\"]\n        Novel[\"NovelAI\u003cbr/\u003eProprietary API\"]\n        OpenAI[\"OpenAI\u003cbr/\u003eDALL-E API\"]\n        Cloud[\"20+ Cloud APIs\u003cbr/\u003eVarious formats\"]\n    end\n    \n    AdapterInterface --\u003e Auto\n    AdapterInterface --\u003e Comfy\n    AdapterInterface --\u003e Novel\n    AdapterInterface --\u003e OpenAI\n    AdapterInterface --\u003e Cloud\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:32-217]() (AUTOMATIC1111), [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:387-634]() (ComfyUI), [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:738-831]() (TogetherAI)\n\n### Example: ComfyUI Router\n\nComfyUI uses a unique workflow-based approach where users define custom node graphs:\n\n**Endpoints:**\n- `POST /api/sd/comfy/ping`: Health check\n- `POST /api/sd/comfy/samplers`: Get available samplers\n- `POST /api/sd/comfy/models`: Get checkpoint models, UNets, and GGUFs\n- `POST /api/sd/comfy/schedulers`: Get available schedulers\n- `POST /api/sd/comfy/vaes`: Get VAE models\n- `POST /api/sd/comfy/workflows`: List workflow files\n- `POST /api/sd/comfy/workflow`: Load workflow JSON\n- `POST /api/sd/comfy/save-workflow`: Save workflow\n- `POST /api/sd/comfy/delete-workflow`: Delete workflow\n- `POST /api/sd/comfy/rename-workflow`: Rename workflow\n- `POST /api/sd/comfy/generate`: Execute workflow and poll for results\n\n**Generation Flow:**\n1. Submit workflow to `/prompt` endpoint\n2. Receive `prompt_id`\n3. Poll `/history` endpoint until job completes\n4. Extract output image from history data\n5. Fetch image from `/view` endpoint\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:387-634]()\n\n### Example: Cloud API Providers\n\nCloud providers typically follow simpler REST patterns:\n\n**Stability AI Example:**\n```javascript\n// Request to https://api.stability.ai/v2beta/stable-image/generate/{model}\nFormData: {\n    prompt: \"...\",\n    negative_prompt: \"...\",\n    aspect_ratio: \"16:9\",\n    output_format: \"png\",\n    style_preset: \"anime\"\n}\n// Returns raw image buffer\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:1066-1124]()\n\n**BFL (Black Forest Labs) Example:**\n```javascript\n// 1. Submit job\nPOST https://api.bfl.ml/v1/{model}\nBody: { prompt, steps, guidance, width, height, seed }\n\n// 2. Poll status\nGET https://api.bfl.ml/v1/get_result?id={id}\n// Wait until status === \"Ready\"\n\n// 3. Fetch image from result.sample URL\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:1458-1570]()\n\n## Prompt Assembly Pipeline\n\nThe prompt assembly system constructs generation prompts from multiple sources, applies templates, and performs substitutions.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Start[\"generatePicture()\"] --\u003e Mode[\"Determine generationMode\"]\n    \n    Mode --\u003e GetPrompt[\"getPrompt(mode, message, trigger, quiet)\"]\n    \n    GetPrompt --\u003e Template[\"Load template from promptTemplates[mode]\"]\n    Template --\u003e SubParams[\"substituteParams(template)\"]\n    SubParams --\u003e LLMGen[\"generateQuietPrompt(template)\u003cbr/\u003e(if mode requires LLM)\"]\n    \n    LLMGen --\u003e ProcessResp[\"Process LLM response\"]\n    ProcessResp --\u003e MinimalCheck{minimal_prompt_processing?}\n    MinimalCheck --\u003e|Yes| SkipClean[\"Skip cleaning\"]\n    MinimalCheck --\u003e|No| CleanResp[\"Clean response\u003cbr/\u003e(remove quotes, prefixes)\"]\n    \n    SkipClean --\u003e Multimodal\n    CleanResp --\u003e Multimodal\n    \n    Multimodal{multimodal_captioning\u003cbr/\u003eenabled?}\n    Multimodal --\u003e|Yes| Caption[\"getMultimodalCaption(avatar)\"]\n    Multimodal --\u003e|No| CombinePrefix\n    \n    Caption --\u003e CombinePrefix[\"combinePrefixes(base, character, macro)\"]\n    CombinePrefix --\u003e FinalPrompt[\"Final Prompt\"]\n    \n    FinalPrompt --\u003e Refine{refine_mode?}\n    Refine --\u003e|Yes| UserEdit[\"Show popup for user editing\"]\n    Refine --\u003e|No| Send[\"sendGenerationRequest()\"]\n    UserEdit --\u003e Send\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:1400-1700](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:1950-2150]()\n\n### Prompt Template System\n\nTemplates are stored in `extension_settings.sd.prompts` and can be customized per mode. Macros are replaced during processing:\n\n**Available Macros:**\n- `{{char}}`: Current character name\n- `{{user}}`: User name\n- `{{charPrefix}}`: \"full body portrait\" or \"close-up portrait\" based on mode\n- `{prompt}`: Insertion point for character-specific prompt in prefix\n- Standard SillyTavern macros via `substituteParams()`\n\n**Template Example (CHARACTER mode):**\n```javascript\npromptTemplates[generationMode.CHARACTER] = \n    'In the next response I want you to provide only a detailed comma-delimited ' +\n    'list of keywords and phrases which describe {{char}}. The list must include ' +\n    'all of the following items in this order: name, species and race, gender, ' +\n    'age, clothing, occupation, physical features and appearances. Do not include ' +\n    'descriptions of non-visual qualities such as personality, movements, scents, ' +\n    'mental traits, or anything which could not be seen in a still photograph. ' +\n    'Do not write in full sentences. Prefix your description with the phrase ' +\n    '\\'full body portrait,\\''\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:178-222]()\n\n### Prefix Combination\n\nPrompts are assembled from multiple layers:\n\n1. **Common Prompt Prefix** (`extension_settings.sd.prompt_prefix`): Base style tags\n2. **Character-Specific Prompt** (`extension_settings.sd.character_prompts[key]`): Character appearance\n3. **Generated Prompt**: LLM-generated or user-specified subject\n4. **Negative Prompt**: Common + character-specific negative prompts\n\nThe `combinePrefixes()` function merges these with comma separation and supports macro replacement:\n\n```javascript\nfunction combinePrefixes(str1, str2, macro = '') {\n    const process = (s) =\u003e s.trim().replace(/^,|,$/g, '').trim();\n    \n    if (!str2) return str1;\n    \n    str1 = process(str1);\n    str2 = process(str2);\n    \n    // If macro exists in str1, replace it; otherwise append\n    const result = macro \u0026\u0026 str1.includes(macro) \n        ? str1.replace(macro, str2) \n        : `${str1}, ${str2},`;\n    \n    return process(result);\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:919-940]()\n\n### Multimodal Captioning\n\nWhen `extension_settings.sd.multimodal_captioning` is enabled, the extension uses vision models to generate descriptions from character/user avatars instead of LLM text generation:\n\n1. Load avatar image (character or user)\n2. Call `getMultimodalCaption(base64Image, prompt)` from shared extension utilities\n3. Use captioning result as the prompt base\n4. Apply normal prefix combination\n\nThis requires a multimodal-capable AI backend. The feature is used automatically for `_MULTIMODAL` generation modes (8, 9, 10).\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:2090-2140](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:47]()\n\n## Configuration System\n\nSettings are stored in `extension_settings.sd` and persisted via `saveSettingsDebounced()`.\n\n### Default Settings Structure\n\n```javascript\nconst defaultSettings = {\n    source: sources.extras,\n    \n    // Generation parameters\n    scale: 7,              // CFG scale\n    steps: 20,             // Sampling steps\n    width: 512,\n    height: 512,\n    seed: -1,              // -1 = random\n    sampler: 'DDIM',\n    scheduler: 'normal',\n    model: '',\n    vae: '',\n    \n    // Prompts\n    prompt_prefix: defaultPrefix,\n    negative_prompt: defaultNegative,\n    prompts: promptTemplates,\n    character_prompts: {},          // Per-character prompts\n    character_negative_prompts: {}, // Per-character negative\n    \n    // Feature flags\n    refine_mode: false,\n    interactive_mode: false,\n    multimodal_captioning: false,\n    snap: false,\n    free_extend: false,\n    function_tool: false,\n    minimal_prompt_processing: false,\n    \n    // Provider URLs\n    auto_url: 'http://localhost:7860',\n    auto_auth: '',\n    vlad_url: 'http://localhost:7860',\n    comfy_url: 'http://127.0.0.1:8188',\n    // ... more provider configs\n    \n    // Visibility controls\n    wand_visible: false,\n    command_visible: false,\n    interactive_visible: false,\n    tool_visible: false,\n    \n    // Provider-specific settings\n    horde_nsfw: false,\n    novel_anlas_guard: false,\n    restore_faces: false,\n    enable_hr: false,\n    // ... many more\n};\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:237-367]()\n\n### Character-Specific Prompts\n\nCharacter prompts are keyed by character filename and can optionally be stored within character card data:\n\n**Storage Locations:**\n1. `extension_settings.sd.character_prompts[key]` (user-specific)\n2. `extension_settings.sd.character_negative_prompts[key]` (user-specific)\n3. `characters[id].data.extensions.sd_character_prompt` (shared with card)\n\nWhen `sd_character_prompt_share` is checked, prompts are written to the character card's extension data, allowing them to be shared when exporting the character.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:831-890](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:3570-3650]()\n\n### Style Presets\n\nUsers can save combinations of prompt prefix and negative prompt as named styles:\n\n```javascript\nextension_settings.sd.styles = [\n    {\n        name: 'Default',\n        prefix: 'best quality, absurdres, aesthetic,',\n        negative: 'lowres, bad anatomy, bad hands, text, error, ...'\n    },\n    // ... user-created styles\n];\n```\n\nThe active style is selected via `extension_settings.sd.style` and applied through the UI.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:227-233](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:679-807]()\n\n## Resolution Presets\n\nThe extension provides predefined resolution options optimized for different providers and aspect ratios:\n\n```javascript\nconst resolutionOptions = {\n    // Standard SD\n    sd_res_512x512: { width: 512, height: 512, name: '512x512 (1:1)' },\n    sd_res_512x768: { width: 512, height: 768, name: '512x768 (2:3, portrait)' },\n    sd_res_768x512: { width: 768, height: 512, name: '768x512 (3:2, landscape)' },\n    \n    // SDXL optimized\n    sd_res_1024x1024: { width: 1024, height: 1024, name: '1024x1024 (SDXL)' },\n    sd_res_1152x896: { width: 1152, height: 896, name: '1152x896 (9:7, SDXL)' },\n    // ... many more SDXL options\n    \n    // DALL-E optimized\n    sd_res_1024x1792: { width: 1024, height: 1792, name: '1024x1792 (4:7, DALL-E)' },\n    sd_res_1792x1024: { width: 1792, height: 1024, name: '1792x1024 (7:4, DALL-E)' },\n    \n    // Provider-specific\n    // ... Z.AI, ChatGPT, and other sizes\n};\n```\n\nWhen `snap` mode is enabled, requested resolutions are automatically adjusted to the nearest preset while preserving aspect ratio and pixel count.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:1019-1050]()\n\n## Request Flow\n\nThe complete flow from user action to displayed image:\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant UI as \"Extension UI\"\n    participant Generate as \"generatePicture()\"\n    participant Prompt as \"getPrompt()\"\n    participant LLM as \"generateQuietPrompt()\"\n    participant Request as \"sendGenerationRequest()\"\n    participant Server as \"/api/sd/{provider}\"\n    participant Provider as \"Image Provider\"\n    participant Display as \"appendMediaToMessage()\"\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eUI: Trigger generation\u003cbr/\u003e(button/command/interactive)\n    UI-\u003e\u003eGenerate: Call with mode \u0026 subject\n    Generate-\u003e\u003ePrompt: Get prompt for mode\n    \n    alt LLM-based mode\n        Prompt-\u003e\u003eLLM: Send template to AI\n        LLM--\u003e\u003ePrompt: Return description\n    else
