Free/Raw mode\n        Prompt-\u003e\u003ePrompt: Use subject directly\n    end\n    \n    alt Multimodal mode\n        Prompt-\u003e\u003ePrompt: Call getMultimodalCaption()\n    end\n    \n    Prompt--\u003e\u003eGenerate: Final prompt text\n    \n    alt Refine mode enabled\n        Generate-\u003e\u003eUser: Show edit dialog\n        User--\u003e\u003eGenerate: Confirm/edit prompt\n    end\n    \n    Generate-\u003e\u003eRequest: Send with all params\n    Request-\u003e\u003eServer: POST /api/sd/{provider}/generate\n    Server-\u003e\u003eProvider: Provider-specific API call\n    \n    alt Async provider (BFL, FAL.AI, etc)\n        loop Poll for completion\n            Server-\u003e\u003eProvider: Check status\n            Provider--\u003e\u003eServer: Status response\n        end\n    end\n    \n    Provider--\u003e\u003eServer: Image data (base64/URL)\n    Server--\u003e\u003eRequest: Normalized response\n    Request--\u003e\u003eGenerate: { format, data }\n    \n    Generate-\u003e\u003eDisplay: Append to chat message\n    Display--\u003e\u003eUser: Show generated image\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:1400-1700](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:2560-2900]()\n\n## Provider-Specific Features\n\nDifferent providers support different features, controlled through UI visibility and parameter availability:\n\n### AUTOMATIC1111 / SD.Next / DrawThings\n- Full control over samplers, schedulers, CFG, steps\n- Hires. Fix with upscalers and denoising strength\n- VAE selection\n- Restore Faces\n- ADetailer (face refinement)\n- CLIP Skip\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:32-352](), [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:354-385](), [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:900-992]()\n\n### ComfyUI\n- Custom workflow system (JSON-based node graphs)\n- Workflow management (save, load, rename, delete)\n- Support for standard servers and RunPod serverless\n- Model support: Checkpoints, UNets, GGUF models\n- Sampler and scheduler selection\n- VAE selection\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:387-634](), [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:636-736]()\n\n### NovelAI\n- SMEA and SMEA DYN samplers\n- Decrisper option\n- Variety+ boost\n- Anlas cost guard (prevents spending)\n- Subscription status check\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:324-329](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:1143-1172]()\n\n### OpenAI (DALL-E)\n- Style selection (vivid/natural)\n- Quality selection (standard/HD)\n- Fixed resolution options (1024x1024, 1792x1024, 1024x1792)\n- No sampler/steps control\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:331-335]()\n\n### Stability AI\n- Style presets (anime, photographic, cinematic, etc.)\n- Fixed models (ultra, core, sd3)\n- Automatic aspect ratio handling\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:1066-1124]()\n\n### Video Generation (Z.AI, Google AI, OpenAI Sora)\nSome providers support video generation in addition to images:\n\n- **Z.AI**: `cogvideox` model with duration/quality/aspect_ratio\n- **Google AI**: Veo model with duration (4/6/8 seconds)\n- **OpenAI**: Sora-2 models with duration selection\n\nVideos are handled through the same pipeline but return `format: 'mp4'` and `video` field instead of `image`.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:1912-2011](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:365-366]()\n\n## Integration Points\n\n### Event-Driven Triggers\n\nThe extension hooks into the application event system:\n\n```javascript\neventSource.on(event_types.GENERATION_STARTED, processTriggers);\neventSource.on(event_types.CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED, onMessageRendered);\neventSource.on(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, onChatChanged);\n```\n\n**`processTriggers(chat, _, abort, type)`:**\n- Called before AI generation starts\n- Scans last user message for interactive mode patterns\n- Aborts generation and triggers image generation if pattern matches\n\n**`onMessageRendered(messageId)`:**\n- Called when character swipes are rendered\n- Can trigger regeneration of images for swipes\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:379-438](), [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:3740-3760]()\n\n### Slash Command System\n\nThe `/sd` command provides extensive subcommand functionality:\n\n**Main Command:**\n```\n/sd (aspect=\"character\"|\"background\"|\"ui-bg\"|...) \n    (character=\"charName\") \n    (prompt=\"...\")\n    (negative=\"...\")\n    – Generate image with specified parameters\n```\n\n**Subcommands:**\n- `/sd-settings` - Opens SD settings panel\n- `/comfyui-workflow workflow=\"name.json\"` - Switch ComfyUI workflow\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:3817-3975]()\n\n### Macro System\n\nThe extension registers macros for use in prompts and other contexts:\n\n```javascript\nmacros.register(MacroCategory.SD, 'sdmodel', () =\u003e extension_settings.sd.model);\nmacros.register(MacroCategory.SD, 'sdsource', () =\u003e extension_settings.sd.source);\nmacros.register(MacroCategory.SD, 'sdwidth', () =\u003e extension_settings.sd.width);\nmacros.register(MacroCategory.SD, 'sdheight', () =\u003e extension_settings.sd.height);\nmacros.register(MacroCategory.SD, 'sdsteps', () =\u003e extension_settings.sd.steps);\nmacros.register(MacroCategory.SD, 'sdscale', () =\u003e extension_settings.sd.scale);\nmacros.register(MacroCategory.SD, 'sdseed', () =\u003e extension_settings.sd.seed);\n```\n\nThese can be used as `{{sdmodel}}`, `{{sdwidth}}`, etc. in any macro-enabled context.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:3702-3738]()\n\n### UI Buttons\n\nThe extension adds buttons to the UI in multiple locations:\n\n1. **Extensions Menu (Wand Button)**: Quick access to generate with saved presets\n2. **Message Actions**: Generate button on messages (when enabled)\n3. **Send Form**: Generate button in chat input area (when enabled)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:3490-3570]()\n\n## Error Handling and Status\n\nThe extension provides visual feedback during generation:\n\n### Generation Toast\n\n```javascript\nlet activeGenerations = 0;\nlet generationToast = null;\n\nfunction showGenerationToast() {\n    activeGenerations++;\n    if (!generationToast) {\n        generationToast = toastr.info('Generating image...', '', { \n            timeOut: 0, \n            extendedTimeOut: 0 \n        });\n    }\n}\n\nfunction hideGenerationToast() {\n    activeGenerations--;\n    if (activeGenerations \u003c= 0 \u0026\u0026 generationToast) {\n        toastr.clear(generationToast);\n        generationToast = null;\n        activeGenerations = 0;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/stable-diffusion/index.js:74-76]()\n\n### Cancellation Support\n\nGenerations can be cancelled through:\n1. Socket disconnect (client closes connection)\n2. Stop button (triggers `CUSTOM_STOP_EVENT`)\n3. Server-side interrupt endpoints (for AUTOMATIC1111, ComfyUI)\n\nWhen cancelled, the server sends interrupt requests to the provider if supported.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:317-326](), [src/endpoints/stable-diffusion.js:571-577]()\n\n---\n\nThis documentation covers the image generation extension system comprehensively. For information about using generated images with character expressions, see [Character Expressions System](#8.3). For vision model integration that analyzes images, see [Image Captioning and Multimodal AI](#8.5)."])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"41:T616e,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Character Expressions System\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/add-custom-expression.html](public/scripts/extensions/expressions/add-custom-expression.html)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js](public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/list-item.html](public/scripts/extensions/expressions/list-item.html)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/remove-custom-expression.html](public/scripts/extensions/expressions/remove-custom-expression.html)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/settings.html](public/scripts/extensions/expressions/settings.html)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/style.css](public/scripts/extensions/expressions/style.css)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/templates/upload-expression.html](public/scripts/extensions/expressions/templates/upload-expression.html)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js](public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/memory/settings.html](public/scripts/extensions/memory/settings.html)\n- [public/scripts/extensions/memory/style.css](public/scripts/extensions/memory/style.css)\n- [src/endpoints/assets.js](src/endpoints/assets.js)\n- [src/endpoints/backends/kobold.js](src/endpoints/backends/kobold.js)\n- [src/endpoints/files.js](src/endpoints/files.js)\n- [src/endpoints/sprites.js](src/endpoints/sprites.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nThe Character Expressions System automatically classifies the emotional state of character messages and displays corresponding character sprites (images). The system supports multiple classification APIs, manages sprite assets, and provides two display modes: single-character expression display and Visual Novel mode for group chats with multiple character sprites positioned on screen.\n\nFor information about Visual Novel mode's multi-character sprite positioning and layering, see [Visual Novel Mode](#8.4). For image generation capabilities, see [Image Generation Extensions](#8.2). For multimodal AI vision features, see [Image Captioning and Multimodal AI](#8.5).\n\n---\n\n## System Architecture Overview\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Client[\"Client-Side (Expression Extension)\"]\n        ModuleWorker[\"moduleWorker()\u003cbr/\u003eEvent Handler\"]\n        Classifier[\"getExpressionLabel()\u003cbr/\u003eClassification Logic\"]\n        SpriteCache[\"spriteCache\u003cbr/\u003eIn-Memory Asset Cache\"]\n        DisplayManager[\"sendExpressionCall()\u003cbr/\u003eDisplay Coordinator\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ClassificationAPIs[\"Classification APIs\"]\n        LocalAPI[\"Local BERT\u003cbr/\u003etransformers.js\"]\n        ExtrasAPI[\"Extras API\u003cbr/\u003e/api/classify\"]\n        LLMAPI[\"Main LLM API\u003cbr/\u003egenerateRaw()\"]\n        WebLLMAPI[\"WebLLM Extension\u003cbr/\u003egenerateWebLlmChatPrompt()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Display[\"Display Systems\"]\n        SingleMode[\"Single Character\u003cbr/\u003e#expression-wrapper\"]\n        VNMode[\"Visual Novel Mode\u003cbr/\u003e#visual-novel-wrapper\u003cbr/\u003eupdateVisualNovelMode()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Server[\"Server-Side Endpoints\"]\n        SpritesGet[\"/api/sprites/get\u003cbr/\u003eList sprites\"]\n        SpritesUpload[\"/api/sprites/upload\u003cbr/\u003eUpload sprite\"]\n        SpritesDelete[\"/api/sprites/delete\u003cbr/\u003eDelete sprite\"]\n        SpritesZip[\"/api/sprites/upload-zip\u003cbr/\u003eBulk upload\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Storage[\"File System\"]\n        CharFolder[\"DATA_ROOT/characters/\u003cbr/\u003e{character_name}/\"]\n        SpriteFiles[\"{expression}.png\u003cbr/\u003e{expression}-1.png\u003cbr/\u003e{expression}.variant.png\"]\n        Subfolders[\"{character_name}/\u003cbr/\u003e{subfolder}/\"]\n    end\n    \n    ModuleWorker --\u003e|\"CHAT_CHANGED event\"| Classifier\n    Classifier --\u003e ClassificationAPIs\n    Classifier --\u003e|\"expression label\"| DisplayManager\n    \n    DisplayManager --\u003e SingleMode\n    DisplayManager --\u003e VNMode\n    \n    ModuleWorker --\u003e|\"validateImages()\"| SpritesGet\n    SpritesGet --\u003e CharFolder\n    CharFolder --\u003e SpriteFiles\n    CharFolder --\u003e Subfolders\n    \n    SpritesUpload --\u003e CharFolder\n    SpritesDelete --\u003e CharFolder\n    SpritesZip --\u003e CharFolder\n    \n    SpriteCache --\u003e|\"cache miss\"| SpritesGet\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:1-1031]()\n\n---\n\n## Expression Classification System\n\n### Classification Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Chat as Chat System\n    participant Worker as moduleWorker()\n    participant Classifier as getExpressionLabel()\n    participant API as Classification API\n    participant Display as sendExpressionCall()\n    \n    Chat-\u003e\u003eWorker: CHAT_CHANGED event\n    Worker-\u003e\u003eWorker: Check if message changed\n    Worker-\u003e\u003eWorker: Throttle during streaming\n    \n    Worker-\u003e\u003eClassifier: classify(messageText)\n    Classifier-\u003e\u003eClassifier: sampleClassifyText()\u003cbr/\u003ePreprocess text\n    \n    alt Local Classifier\n        Classifier-\u003e\u003eAPI: classifyText() via transformers.js\n    else Extras API\n        Classifier-\u003e\u003eAPI: POST /api/classify\n    else LLM API\n        Classifier-\u003e\u003eAPI: generateRaw() with prompt\n    else WebLLM\n        Classifier-\u003e\u003eAPI: generateWebLlmChatPrompt()\n    end\n    \n    API--\u003e\u003eClassifier: emotion label\n    Classifier-\u003e\u003eClassifier: Fuzzy match to valid labels\n    Classifier--\u003e\u003eWorker: expression (e.g., \"joy\")\n    \n    Worker-\u003e\u003eDisplay: sendExpressionCall(folder, expression)\n    Display-\u003e\u003eDisplay: Update sprite display\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:478-612](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:896-1031]()\n\n### Classification APIs\n\nThe system supports four classification methods configured via `extension_settings.expressions.api`:\n\n| API Type | Enum Value | Description | Requirements |\n|----------|-----------|-------------|--------------|\n| **Local** | `EXPRESSION_API.local` (0) | Uses transformers.js BERT model in browser | No external service needed |\n| **Extras** | `EXPRESSION_API.extras` (1) | Legacy classification via Extras API | `modules.includes('classify')` |\n| **LLM** | `EXPRESSION_API.llm` (2) | Uses main LLM API with custom prompt | Active LLM connection |\n| **WebLLM** | `EXPRESSION_API.webllm` (3) | Uses WebLLM extension for local inference | WebLLM extension installed |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:80-87]()\n\n### Text Preprocessing\n\nThe `sampleClassifyText()` function preprocesses message text before classification:\n\n1. **Macro substitution** via `substituteParams()`\n2. **Character filtering** - removes asterisks and quotes\n3. **Text sampling** - for non-LLM APIs:\n   - If text \u003c 500 characters: trim to end of sentence\n   - If text ≥ 500 characters: take first 250 + last 250 characters, trim to sentence boundaries\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:898-921]()\n\n### LLM Classification Prompt\n\nWhen using LLM or WebLLM classification, the system uses a configurable prompt stored in `extension_settings.expressions.llmPrompt` (default: `DEFAULT_LLM_PROMPT`):\n\n```\nIgnore previous instructions. Classify the emotion of the last message. \nOutput just one word, e.g. \"joy\" or \"anger\". \nChoose only one of the following labels: {{labels}}\n```\n\nThe prompt supports:\n- **`{{labels}}` macro** - substituted with available expression labels\n- **JSON schema** if supported via `isJsonSchemaSupported()`\n- **Fuzzy matching** - response is matched to valid labels using Fuse.js if exact match fails\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:43-73](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:928-965]()\n\n### Prompt Strategy Types\n\nThe `extension_settings.expressions.promptType` setting controls context handling:\n\n| Strategy | Value | Behavior |\n|----------|-------|----------|\n| **Raw** | `PROMPT_TYPE.raw` | Sends only the last message text with minimal context |\n| **Full** | `PROMPT_TYPE.full` | Uses full chat context via `generateRaw()` or `generateQuietPrompt()` |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:89-93]()\n\n---\n\n## Sprite Asset Management\n\n### File Storage Structure\n\nSprites are stored in the file system following this hierarchy:\n\n```\nDATA_ROOT/\n└── characters/\n    ├── {character_name}/\n    │   ├── joy.png                    # Base expression\n    │   ├── joy-1.png                  # Additional variant (dash separator)\n    │   ├── joy.happy.png              # Additional variant (dot separator)\n    │   ├── anger.png\n    │   ├── sadness.png\n    │   └── {subfolder}/               # Expression override subfolder\n    │       ├── joy.png\n    │       └── anger.png\n    └── {character_name_2}/\n        └── ...\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/sprites.js:11-38](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/settings.html:97-109]()\n\n### Sprite Naming Convention\n\nSprite filenames follow a specific pattern extracted via regex in the server endpoint:\n\n```javascript\n// Pattern: {expression}[-.]{optional_suffix}.{extension}\n// Examples: joy.png, joy-1.png, joy.expressive.png\n\nconst fileName = path.parse(pathToSprite).name.toLowerCase();\nconst label = fileName.match(/^(.+?)(?:[-\\\\.].*?)?$/)?.[1] ?? fileName;\n```\n\nThe system extracts the **expression label** (e.g., `joy`) from the filename prefix before the first dash or dot separator.\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/sprites.js:135-138]()\n\n### Expression Types\n\nThe system categorizes sprites into three types:\n\n```typescript\ntype ExpressionImage = {\n    expression: string;           // Label (e.g., \"joy\")\n    isCustom: boolean;           // User-defined vs default\n    fileName: string;            // Full filename with extension\n    title: string;               // Display title\n    imageSrc: string;            // URL path\n    type: 'success' | 'additional' | 'failure';\n}\n```\n\n| Type | Meaning | Visual Indicator |\n|------|---------|------------------|\n| `success` | First/primary sprite for expression | Green label |\n| `additional` | Multiple sprites for same expression | Olive green label with ➕ icon |\n| `failure` | Missing sprite (placeholder) | Red label |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:24-37](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/style.css:172-191]()\n\n### Multiple Sprites Per Expression\n\nWhen `extension_settings.expressions.allowMultiple` is enabled, the system supports multiple sprite files per expression label. Selection behavior:\n\n- **Random selection** from available variants\n- **Re-roll option** - `extension_settings.expressions.rerollOnSameExpression` forces new sprite even if expression unchanged\n- **Deterministic fallback** - if multiple sprites disabled, uses first matching sprite\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:1109-1159]()\n\n### Sprite Cache Architecture\n\nThe client maintains an in-memory cache:\n\n```javascript\n/** @type {{[characterKey: string]: Expression[]}} */\nlet spriteCache = {};\n```\n\nCache invalidation occurs when:\n- Character switched via `lastCharacter` tracking\n- API connection state changes (offline ↔ online)\n- Sprite upload/delete operations\n- Expression override modified\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:95-104]()\n\n---\n\n## Display Modes\n\n### Mode Selection Logic\n\n```javascript\nfunction isVisualNovelMode() {\n    return Boolean(!isMobile() \u0026\u0026 power_user.waifuMode \u0026\u0026 getContext().groupId);\n}\n```\n\nVisual Novel mode activates when:\n1. Not on mobile device\n2. `power_user.waifuMode` enabled\n3. Currently in group chat (`context.groupId` exists)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:123-125]()\n\n### Single Character Mode\n\nDisplays one sprite in `#expression-wrapper` container positioned absolutely on screen.\n\n**Display Update Flow:**\n```javascript\nfunction setExpression(spriteFolderName, expression, options) {\n    // 1. Choose sprite file for expression\n    const spriteFile = chooseSpriteForExpression(folder, expression);\n    \n    // 2. Update image with animation\n    await setImage(imgElement, spriteFile.imageSrc);\n    \n    // 3. Track last expression\n    lastExpression[spriteFolderName] = expression;\n}\n```\n\n**Animation:** Uses clone-and-fade technique for smooth transitions (200ms duration).\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:1161-1240](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:388-476]()\n\n### Visual Novel Mode\n\nDisplays multiple character sprites simultaneously in `#visual-novel-wrapper`. Each character gets a separate `.expression-holder` container.\n\n**Key Functions:**\n\n| Function | Purpose |\n|----------|---------|\n| `updateVisualNovelMode()` | Main coordinator - removes inactive, sets sprites, updates layers |\n| `visualNovelRemoveInactive()` | Fades out and removes characters no longer in group |\n| `visualNovelSetCharacterSprites()` | Creates/updates sprite containers for each group member |\n| `visualNovelUpdateLayers()` | Calculates z-index and horizontal positioning |\n\n**Positioning Algorithm:**\n\n1. Sort characters by recent message activity (most recent = front layer)\n2. Calculate total width of all sprites\n3. If total width \u003e container width, apply proportional overlap\n4. Center the sprite array horizontally\n5. Animate positions with 500ms jQuery animation (unless `power_user.reduced_motion`)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:135-380]()\n\n---\n\n## Expression Override System\n\n### Override Configuration\n\nUsers can specify custom sprite folders per character via `extension_settings.expressionOverrides`:\n\n```javascript\nconst expressionOverride = extension_settings.expressionOverrides.find(\n    e =\u003e e.name == avatarFileName\n);\n\nif (expressionOverride \u0026\u0026 expressionOverride.path) {\n    spriteFolderName = expressionOverride.path;\n}\n```\n\n**Override Formats:**\n\n| Input Format | Resolved Path | Description |\n|--------------|---------------|-------------|\n| `Bob` | `characters/Bob/` | Character root folder |\n| `Bob/formal` | `characters/Bob/formal/` | Subfolder for character |\n| `/formal` | `characters/{current_char}/formal/` | Subfolder (relative to current char) |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:614-645]()\n\n### Override Management UI\n\nThe override system provides:\n- **Text input** - `#expression_override` for folder path\n- **Submit button** - `#expression_override_button` applies override\n- **Cleanup button** - `#expression_override_cleanup_button` removes all overrides\n- **Slash command** - `/setspritefolder` for programmatic control\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/settings.html:80-95]()\n\n---\n\n## Server API Endpoints\n\n### GET /api/sprites/get\n\nRetrieves list of sprites for a character.\n\n**Request:**\n```javascript\n{ name: string }  // Character name or \"name/subfolder\"\n```\n\n**Response:**\n```javascript\n[\n    {\n        label: string,      // Expression label (e.g., \"joy\")\n        path: string        // URL path with cache-busting timestamp\n    },\n    ...\n]\n```\n\n**Implementation Details:**\n- Filters files by image MIME type\n- Extracts label from filename via regex pattern\n- Adds modification timestamp for cache invalidation\n- Supports subfolder paths with `/` separator\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/sprites.js:118-151]()\n\n### POST /api/sprites/upload\n\nUploads a single sprite image.\n\n**Request Body:**\n```javascript\n{\n    name: string,       // Character name or \"name/subfolder\"\n    label: string,      // Expression label\n    spriteName: string, // Optional custom filename (without extension)\n    file: File          // Multipart file upload\n}\n```\n\n**Behavior:**\n1. Creates sprites folder if doesn't exist\n2. Removes existing sprite with same `spriteName`\n3. Copies uploaded file to `{spriteName}.{extension}`\n4. Deletes temporary upload file\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/sprites.js:240-290]()\n\n### POST /api/sprites/delete\n\nDeletes a sprite image.\n\n**Request Body:**\n```javascript\n{\n    name: string,       // Character name or \"name/subfolder\"\n    label: string,      // Expression label (legacy)\n    spriteName: string  // Filename without extension\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/sprites.js:153-185]()\n\n### POST /api/sprites/upload-zip\n\nBulk upload sprites from ZIP archive.\n\n**Request:**\n```javascript\n{\n    name: string,  // Character name or \"name/subfolder\"\n    file: File     // ZIP file\n}\n```\n\n**Implementation:**\n1. Extracts image buffers using `getImageBuffers()`\n2. Overwrites existing sprites with matching names\n3. Writes all new sprites atomically\n4. Deletes ZIP file after extraction\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/sprites.js:187-238]()\n\n---\n\n## RisuAI Sprite Import\n\nThe system automatically imports embedded sprites from RisuAI character cards during character import.\n\n```javascript\nexport function importRisuSprites(directories, data) {\n    const risuData = data?.data?.extensions?.risuai;\n    let images = [];\n    \n    // Combine additionalAssets and emotions arrays\n    if (Array.isArray(risuData.additionalAssets)) {\n        images = images.concat(risuData.additionalAssets);\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(risuData.emotions)) {\n        images = images.concat(risuData.emotions);\n    }\n    \n    // Write base64 sprites to disk as {label}.png\n    // Remove from character data to avoid duplication\n}\n```\n\n**Process:**\n1. Detects `data.extensions.risuai` in character JSON\n2. Extracts base64-encoded sprites from `additionalAssets` and `emotions` arrays\n3. Writes sprites to `characters/{name}/{label}.png`\n4. Removes sprite data from character JSON to prevent duplication\n5. Skips sprites that already exist\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/sprites.js:48-114]()\n\n---\n\n## Slash Commands\n\n### /setsprite\n\nSets the current character's expression or sprite file.\n\n**Syntax:**\n```\n/setsprite (type=expression|sprite) \u003csearch_term\u003e\n```\n\n**Parameters:**\n- `type` - Search mode: `expression` (fuzzy match labels) or `sprite` (fuzzy match filenames)\n- `search_term` - Expression label or sprite filename to search for\n- Special value: `#reset` - resets sprite to default\n\n**Behavior:**\n1. Validates sprites via `validateImages()`\n2. Performs fuzzy search using `performFuzzySearch()` with Fuse.js\n3. Calls `sendExpressionCall()` with matched expression/sprite\n4. Returns matched expression label\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:728-792]()\n\n### /setspritefolder\n\nSets custom sprite folder override for current character.\n\n**Syntax:**\n```\n/setspritefolder (name=\u003ccharacter\u003e) \u003cfolder_path\u003e\n```\n\n**Parameters:**\n- `name` - Optional character name override (defaults to last message character)\n- `folder_path` - Can be full path or relative with `/` prefix\n\n**Examples:**\n```\n/setspritefolder Bob/formal          # Full path\n/setspritefolder /formal             # Relative to current character\n/setspritefolder \"\"                  # Clear override\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:677-698]()\n\n### /classify\n\nManually classifies text using the selected expression API.\n\n**Syntax:**\n```\n/classify (api=local|extras|llm|webllm) (filter=true|false) (prompt=\u003ccustom\u003e) \u003ctext\u003e\n```\n\n**Parameters:**\n- `api` - Override classification API\n- `filter` - Whether to filter to available expressions only\n- `prompt` - Custom classification prompt (for LLM/WebLLM)\n- `text` - Text to classify (if empty, summarizes current chat)\n\n**Returns:** Expression label string\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:700-726]()\n\n### /uploadsprite\n\nUploads a sprite from URL or data URI.\n\n**Syntax:**\n```\n/uploadsprite (name=\u003ccharacter\u003e) label=\u003cexpression\u003e (folder=\u003cpath\u003e) (spriteName=\u003cname\u003e) \u003cimage_url\u003e\n```\n\n**Parameters:**\n- `name` - Character name (defaults to last message character)\n- `label` - **Required** - Expression label (must contain letters)\n- `folder` - Optional sprite folder override\n- `spriteName` - Optional custom filename (defaults to label)\n- `image_url` - URL to fetch image from\n\n**Validation:**\n- Label must contain at least one letter\n- Sprite name must follow expression naming pattern via `validateExpressionSpriteName()`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:840-888]()\n\n---\n\n## Fallback Expression System\n\n### Default Expressions\n\nThe system provides 28 default expression labels based on GoEmotions dataset:\n\n```javascript\nconst DEFAULT_EXPRESSIONS = [\n    'admiration', 'amusement', 'anger', 'annoyance', 'approval', \n    'caring', 'confusion', 'curiosity', 'desire', 'disappointment',\n    'disapproval', 'disgust', 'embarrassment', 'excitement', 'fear',\n    'gratitude', 'grief', 'joy', 'love', 'nervousness',\n    'optimism', 'pride', 'realization', 'relief', 'remorse',\n    'sadness', 'surprise', 'neutral'\n];\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:44-73]()\n\n### Fallback Options\n\nWhen no matching sprite exists, the system uses `extension_settings.expressions.fallback_expression`:\n\n| Option | Value | Behavior |\n|--------|-------|----------|\n| **Specific expression** | Expression label | Uses specified expression sprite |\n| **No fallback** | `#none` | Hides sprite display |\n| **Emoji fallback** | `#emoji` | *(Future feature - not implemented)* |\n\n**Default fallback:** `\"joy\"` (`DEFAULT_FALLBACK_EXPRESSION`)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:42](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:75-77]()\n\n### Custom Expressions\n\nUsers can define custom expressions beyond the default set via UI:\n\n1. **Add custom** - `#expression_custom_add` button\n2. **Remove custom** - `#expression_custom_remove` button\n3. **Validation** - Must contain only letters, numbers, dashes, underscores\n4. **Storage** - Persisted in `extension_settings.expressions.custom`\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/settings.html:64-72]()\n\n---\n\n## Event Integration\n\n### Event Listeners\n\nThe expression system registers these event handlers:\n\n```javascript\neventSource.on(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, () =\u003e {\n    moduleWorker();  // Classify latest message\n});\n\neventSource.on(event_types.GROUP_UPDATED, () =\u003e {\n    validateImages(folder, true);\n    updateVisualNovelModeDebounced();\n});\n\neventSource.on(event_types.WORLDINFO_ACTIVATED, () =\u003e {\n    if (isVisualNovelMode()) {\n        updateVisualNovelModeDebounced();\n    }\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:1660-1700]()\n\n### Throttling and Debouncing\n\nTo prevent excessive API calls during streaming:\n\n```javascript\nconst UPDATE_INTERVAL = 2000;              // Normal update interval\nconst STREAMING_UPDATE_INTERVAL = 10000;   // Throttle during streaming\n\nif (!context.groupId \u0026\u0026 context.streamingProcessor \u0026\u0026 !context.streamingProcessor.isFinished) {\n    const timeSinceLastUpdate = Date.now() - lastServerResponseTime;\n    if (timeSinceLastUpdate \u003c STREAMING_UPDATE_INTERVAL) {\n        return;  // Skip update\n    }\n}\n```\n\nVisual Novel mode updates are debounced using `debounce_timeout.quick`.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:40-41](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:574-583]()\n\n---\n\n## Translation Support\n\nThe system supports translating message text to English before classification via `extension_settings.expressions.translate`:\n\n```javascript\nif (extension_settings.expressions.translate) {\n    text = await translate(text);  // Uses Chat Translation extension\n}\n```\n\nThis relies on the Chat Translation extension's selected API and improves classification accuracy for non-English chats.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:1000-1003](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/settings.html:9-12]()\n\n---\n\n## Configuration Settings\n\n### Extension Settings Structure\n\n```javascript\nextension_settings.expressions = {\n    api: EXPRESSION_API,              // Classification API selection\n    llmPrompt: string,                // LLM classification prompt\n    promptType: PROMPT_TYPE,          // Raw vs Full context\n    fallback_expression: string,      // Default expression\n    showDefault: boolean,             // Show default expressions in UI\n    translate: boolean,               // Translate before classification\n    allowMultiple: boolean,           // Multiple sprites per expression\n    rerollOnSameExpression: boolean,  // Re-roll on same expression\n    filterAvailable: boolean,         // Filter labels to available sprites\n    custom: string[],                 // User-defined expression labels\n};\n\nextension_settings.expressionOverrides = [\n    { name: string, path: string },   // Character → folder mapping\n    ...\n];\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:1524-1614]()\n\n---\n\n## Macros and Variables\n\n### Available Macros\n\nThe expression system exposes the `{{lastExpression}}` macro:\n\n```javascript\n/** @type {{[characterName: string]: string}} */\nexport let lastExpression = {};\n```\n\nUsage in prompts or scripts:\n```\nThe character looks {{lastExpression}}.\n```\n\nThis tracks the most recent expression label set for each character.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:104]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"42:T4ccc,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Visual Novel Mode\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003eRelevant source files\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe following files were used as context for generating this wiki page:\n\n- [src/endpoints/sprites.js](src/endpoints/sprites.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nVisual Novel Mode is a presentation feature within the Expressions extension that displays multiple character sprites simultaneously during group chats, arranged horizontally across the screen in a visual novel-style layout. This mode automatically manages sprite positioning, layering, and transitions for all active group members.\n\nFor information about individual character expressions and sprite classification, see [Character Expressions System](#8.3). For group chat message generation, see [Group Chats and Multi-Character Interactions](#9).\n\n---\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nVisual Novel Mode transforms the single-character expression display into a multi-character sprite arrangement suitable for group conversations. The system:\n\n- Displays sprites for all active group members simultaneously\n- Dynamically positions sprites to fit within the viewport\n- Manages z-index layering based on message recency\n- Handles sprite transitions with smooth animations\n- Automatically removes sprites for disabled group members\n- Preserves user-customized sprite positions via drag-and-drop\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:123-151]()\n\n---\n\n## Activation Conditions\n\nVisual Novel Mode activates when three conditions are met simultaneously:\n\n```javascript\nfunction isVisualNovelMode() {\n    return Boolean(!isMobile() \u0026\u0026 power_user.waifuMode \u0026\u0026 getContext().groupId);\n}\n```\n\n| Condition | Requirement | Reason |\n|-----------|-------------|--------|\n| `!isMobile()` | Not on mobile device | Limited screen space on mobile |\n| `power_user.waifuMode` | Waifu Mode enabled | User preference toggle |\n| `getContext().groupId` | Active group chat | VN mode requires multiple characters |\n\nWhen these conditions are not met, the system falls back to single-character expression display using `#expression-wrapper`.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:123-125](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:487-504]()\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph Activation[\"Activation Layer\"]\n        moduleWorker[\"moduleWorker()\"]\n        isVNMode[\"isVisualNovelMode()\"]\n        modeCheck{\"VN Mode Active?\"}\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Display[\"Display Containers\"]\n        vnWrapper[\"#visual-novel-wrapper\"]\n        exprWrapper[\"#expression-wrapper\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Update[\"Update Pipeline\"]\n        updateVN[\"updateVisualNovelMode()\"]\n        removeInactive[\"visualNovelRemoveInactive()\"]\n        setSprites[\"visualNovelSetCharacterSprites()\"]\n        updateLayers[\"visualNovelUpdateLayers()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Rendering[\"Sprite Rendering\"]\n        exprHolder[\".expression-holder elements\"]\n        imgElements[\"img.expression elements\"]\n        setImage[\"setImage()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph Data[\"Data Sources\"]\n        groupMembers[\"group.members array\"]\n        spriteCache[\"spriteCache object\"]\n        chatHistory[\"context.chat array\"]\n    end\n    \n    moduleWorker --\u003e isVNMode\n    isVNMode --\u003e modeCheck\n    modeCheck --\u003e|\"true\"| vnWrapper\n    modeCheck --\u003e|\"false\"| exprWrapper\n    \n    vnWrapper --\u003e updateVN\n    updateVN --\u003e removeInactive\n    removeInactive --\u003e setSprites\n    setSprites --\u003e updateLayers\n    \n    setSprites --\u003e exprHolder\n    exprHolder --\u003e imgElements\n    imgElements --\u003e setImage\n    \n    groupMembers --\u003e setSprites\n    spriteCache --\u003e setSprites\n    chatHistory --\u003e updateLayers\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:478-612](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:123-151]()\n\n---\n\n## Core Components\n\n### Visual Novel Container\n\nThe `#visual-novel-wrapper` element serves as the root container for all character sprites in VN mode:\n\n```css\n#visual-novel-wrapper {\n    display: flex;\n    height: calc(100vh - var(--topBarBlockSize));\n    width: 100vw;\n    overflow: hidden;\n}\n```\n\nUnlike the single-character `#expression-wrapper`, the VN wrapper uses flexbox to arrange multiple character containers horizontally.\n\n### Expression Holder Elements\n\nEach character in the group receives a `.expression-holder` element:\n\n```html\n\u003cdiv id=\"expression-{avatar}\" \n     class=\"expression-holder\" \n     data-avatar=\"{avatar}\"\u003e\n    \u003cdiv class=\"drag-grabber\" id=\"expression-{avatar}header\"\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n    \u003cimg class=\"expression\" src=\"{sprite.imageSrc}\" /\u003e\n\u003c/div\u003e\n```\n\nThese containers:\n- Are cloned from the `#expression-holder` template [line 235-239]()\n- Support drag-and-drop repositioning via `dragElement()` [line 240]()\n- Store the character's avatar identifier in `data-avatar` attribute\n- Contain a single `img.expression` element for the sprite\n\n### Sprite Cache Structure\n\nThe `spriteCache` object stores sprite metadata per character:\n\n```javascript\nspriteCache = {\n    \"CharacterName\": [\n        {\n            label: \"joy\",\n            files: [\n                {\n                    expression: \"joy\",\n                    fileName: \"joy.png\",\n                    imageSrc: \"/characters/CharacterName/joy.png\",\n                    title: \"Joy\",\n                    type: \"success\"\n                }\n            ]\n        }\n    ]\n}\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:99](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/style.css:13-18](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/style.css:36-52]()\n\n---\n\n## Update Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant MW as moduleWorker()\n    participant UVN as updateVisualNovelMode()\n    participant VRI as visualNovelRemoveInactive()\n    participant VSCS as visualNovelSetCharacterSprites()\n    participant VUL as visualNovelUpdateLayers()\n    participant SI as setImage()\n    \n    MW-\u003e\u003eUVN: Trigger update\n    UVN-\u003e\u003eVRI: Remove inactive members\n    \n    loop For each .expression-holder\n        VRI-\u003e\u003eVRI: Check if member in group\n        alt Member disabled or removed\n            VRI-\u003e\u003eVRI: fadeOut() and remove()\n        end\n    end\n    \n    UVN-\u003e\u003eVSCS: Set sprites for all members\n    \n    loop For each group.members\n        VSCS-\u003e\u003eVSCS: Check if disabled\n        alt Sprite exists in cache\n            VSCS-\u003e\u003eVSCS: Find existing holder\n            VSCS-\u003e\u003eSI: Update sprite image\n        else No holder exists\n            VSCS-\u003e\u003eVSCS: Clone template\n            VSCS-\u003e\u003eVSCS: Append to wrapper\n            VSCS-\u003e\u003eVSCS: fadeIn() animation\n        end\n    end\n    \n    UVN-\u003e\u003eVUL: Calculate positions\n    UVL-\u003e\u003eVUL: Sort by recent messages\n    UVL-\u003e\u003eVUL: Calculate horizontal layout\n    \n    alt Sprites fit in viewport\n        VUL-\u003e\u003eVUL: Center all sprites\n    else Sprites overflow\n        VUL-\u003e\u003eVUL: Calculate overlap amounts\n    end\n    \n    loop For each sprite\n        VUL-\u003e\u003eVUL: Set z-index by recency\n        VUL-\u003e\u003eVUL: Animate to position\n    end\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:135-151](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:153-178](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:188-265](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:284-380]()\n\n---\n\n## Positioning System\n\nThe `visualNovelUpdateLayers()` function implements a dynamic horizontal layout algorithm that adapts to viewport constraints.\n\n### Layout Algorithm\n\n1. **Sort sprites** by character order in `group.members` [line 289-304]()\n2. **Measure sprite widths** after images load [line 319-330]()\n3. **Calculate total width** and pivotal center point [line 316-333]()\n4. **Determine positioning strategy**:\n\n```javascript\nconst pivotalPoint = containerWidth * 0.5;\nlet currentPosition = pivotalPoint - (totalWidth / 2);\n\nif (totalWidth \u003e containerWidth) {\n    // Overflow: calculate proportional overlaps\n    let totalOverlap = totalWidth - containerWidth;\n    let overlaps = imagesWidth.map(width =\u003e \n        (width / totalWidthWithoutWidest) * totalOverlap\n    );\n    imagesWidth = imagesWidth.map((width, index) =\u003e \n        width - overlaps[index]\n    );\n    currentPosition = 0;\n}\n```\n\n### Centering vs. Overlapping\n\n| Condition | Strategy | Implementation |\n|-----------|----------|----------------|\n| `totalWidth \u003c= containerWidth` | Center all sprites | Start from center, space naturally |\n| `totalWidth \u003e containerWidth` | Proportional overlap | Reduce spacing based on sprite width ratios |\n\nThe overlap calculation excludes the widest sprite to prevent excessive compression of large sprites.\n\n### User-Positioned Sprites\n\nSprites with manual positions are skipped during automatic layout:\n\n```javascript\nif (element.data('dragged') || \n    (power_user.movingUIState[elementID] \u0026\u0026 \n     Object.keys(power_user.movingUIState[elementID]).length \u003e 0)) {\n    loadMovingUIState();\n    return; // Skip auto-positioning\n}\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:284-380](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:316-377]()\n\n---\n\n## Layering System\n\nZ-index values are assigned based on message recency to create visual depth.\n\n### Layer Calculation\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    A[\"context.chat messages\"] --\u003e B[\"Extract original_avatar\"]\n    B --\u003e C[\"Reverse and filter unique\"]\n    C --\u003e D[\"recentMessages array\"]\n    D --\u003e E[\"Compare with group.members\"]\n    E --\u003e F[\"Generate layerIndices\"]\n    \n    F --\u003e G{\"Message exists?\"}\n    G --\u003e|\"Yes\"| H[\"Higher z-index (front)\"]\n    G --\u003e|\"No\"| I[\"Lower z-index (back)\"]\n    \n    H --\u003e J[\"Apply to .expression-holder\"]\n    I --\u003e J\n```\n\n### Sorting Logic\n\nCharacters are sorted by three criteria in priority order:\n\n1. **Recent message index**: Characters with recent messages appear in front\n2. **No recent message**: Characters without recent messages\n3. **Group member order**: Fallback to original group member order\n\n```javascript\nconst layerIndices = filteredMembers.slice().sort((a, b) =\u003e {\n    const aRecentIndex = recentMessages.indexOf(a);\n    const bRecentIndex = recentMessages.indexOf(b);\n    \n    if (aRecentIndex !== -1 \u0026\u0026 bRecentIndex !== -1) {\n        return bRecentIndex - aRecentIndex; // Most recent = highest\n    } else if (aRecentIndex !== -1) {\n        return 1; // Has message = higher\n    } else if (bRecentIndex !== -1) {\n        return -1; // No message = lower\n    } else {\n        return aFilteredIndex - bFilteredIndex; // Member order\n    }\n});\n```\n\nThis creates a natural visual hierarchy where recently-speaking characters appear prominent.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:284-304](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:358-360]()\n\n---\n\n## Expression Changes and Animation\n\nThe `setImage()` function handles sprite transitions with crossfade animations to create smooth visual updates.\n\n### Transition Process\n\n```mermaid\nstateDiagram-v2\n    [*] --\u003e CheckChange: setImage(img, path)\n    CheckChange --\u003e NoChange: Same path\n    CheckChange --\u003e Animate: Different path\n    NoChange --\u003e [*]\n    \n    Animate --\u003e Clone: Create expressionClone\n    Clone --\u003e Position: Set opacity 0\n    Position --\u003e FadeIn: animate({opacity: 1})\n    FadeIn --\u003e FadeOut: Original fades out\n    FadeOut --\u003e Replace: Remove original\n    Replace --\u003e Cleanup: Clone becomes new original\n    Cleanup --\u003e [*]\n```\n\n### Animation Implementation\n\nThe crossfade prevents jarring sprite swaps:\n\n```javascript\nconst expressionClone = img.clone();\nexpressionClone.addClass('expression-clone');\nexpressionClone.css({ opacity: 0 });\nexpressionClone.attr('src', path);\nexpressionClone.appendTo(img.parent());\n\nconst duration = 200;\nimg.addClass('expression-animating');\nexpressionClone.addClass('expression-animating');\n\n// Fade in clone\nexpressionClone.animate({ opacity: 1 }, duration)\n    .promise()\n    .done(function() {\n        // Fade out original\n        img.animate({ opacity: 0 }, duration);\n        img.remove();\n        expressionClone.removeClass('expression-animating');\n    });\n```\n\n### Container Sizing\n\nTo prevent layout shift during transitions, the parent `.expression-holder` receives minimum dimensions:\n\n```javascript\nconst imgWidth = img.width();\nconst imgHeight = img.height();\nconst expressionHolder = img.parent();\nexpressionHolder.css('min-width', imgWidth \u003e 100 ? imgWidth : 100);\nexpressionHolder.css('min-height', imgHeight \u003e 100 ? imgHeight : 100);\n```\n\nThese are reset after the transition completes [line 452-453]().\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:388-476](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:407-460]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration with Group Chats\n\nVisual Novel Mode responds to group chat events and member changes.\n\n### Event Triggers\n\n| Event | Handler | Action |\n|-------|---------|--------|\n| `CHAT_CHANGED` | `moduleWorker()` | Re-evaluate VN mode state |\n| `MESSAGE_RECEIVED` | `moduleWorker()` | Update sprites for message sender |\n| `GROUP_UPDATED` | `updateVisualNovelMode()` | Refresh all sprites |\n\n### Member State Management\n\nThe `visualNovelSetCharacterSprites()` function handles three member states:\n\n```javascript\nfor (const avatar of group.members) {\n    const isDisabled = group.disabled_members.includes(avatar);\n    \n    if (isDisabled \u0026\u0026 hideMutedSprites) {\n        continue; // Skip completely\n    }\n    \n    const character = context.characters.find(x =\u003e x.avatar == avatar);\n    if (!character) {\n        continue; // Character not loaded\n    }\n    \n    // Proceed with sprite display\n}\n```\n\n### Expression Selection\n\nIn VN mode, expressions are determined per character:\n\n1. If `expression` parameter provided: use that expression for all\n2. Otherwise: call `getLastMessageSprite(avatar)` for each character\n3. Fallback: use `chooseSpriteForExpression()` with available sprites\n\n```javascript\nif (!originalExpression \u0026\u0026 spriteCache[memberSpriteFolderName].length \u003e 0) {\n    expression = await getLastMessageSprite(avatar);\n}\n\nconst spriteFile = chooseSpriteForExpression(\n    memberSpriteFolderName, \n    expression, \n    { prevExpressionSrc: prevExpressionSrc }\n);\n```\n\nThis allows each character to display their contextually-appropriate expression independently.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:188-265](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:196-201](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:220-224](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:8]()\n\n---\n\n## State Persistence\n\nVisual Novel Mode respects manual sprite positioning through the `movingUIState` system.\n\n### Drag-and-Drop State\n\nWhen users manually reposition sprites via `dragElement()`:\n\n```javascript\nif (element.data('dragged') || \n    (power_user.movingUIState[elementID] \u0026\u0026 \n     Object.keys(power_user.movingUIState[elementID]).length \u003e 0)) {\n    loadMovingUIState(); // Restore manual position\n    return; // Skip automatic positioning\n}\n```\n\nManual positions are stored in `power_user.movingUIState` with keys like `expression-{avatar}` and persist across sessions.\n\n### Reduced Motion Support\n\nAnimation behavior adapts to accessibility preferences:\n\n```javascript\nif (power_user.reduced_motion) {\n    element.css('left', currentPosition + 'px');\n    requestAnimationFrame(() =\u003e resolve());\n} else {\n    element.animate({ left: currentPosition + 'px' }, 500, resolve);\n}\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:348-372](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:363-371]()\n\n---\n\n## CSS Styling and Layout\n\nThe visual presentation is controlled by specific CSS rules.\n\n### Container Styles\n\n```css\n#visual-novel-wrapper {\n    display: flex;\n    height: calc(100vh - var(--topBarBlockSize));\n    width: 100vw;\n    overflow: hidden;\n}\n\n#visual-novel-wrapper .expression-holder {\n    width: max-content;\n    display: flex;\n    left: unset;\n    right: unset;\n}\n```\n\nThe `overflow: hidden` prevents sprites from extending beyond viewport boundaries during overlap calculations.\n\n### Sprite Image Styles\n\n```css\nimg.expression {\n    min-width: 100px;\n    min-height: 100px;\n    max-height: 90vh;\n    max-width: 90vh;\n    object-fit: contain;\n    vertical-align: bottom;\n}\n```\n\nThe `max-height: 90vh` constraint ensures sprites remain visible even on shorter displays.\n\n### Hidden State\n\nSprites without valid files receive the `.hidden` class:\n\n```css\n#visual-novel-wrapper .hidden {\n    display: none !important;\n    visibility: hidden !important;\n}\n```\n\nThis is applied when `chooseSpriteForExpression()` returns null [line 233, 241]().\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/style.css:13-30](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/style.css:65-75]()\n\n---\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n### Image Loading Synchronization\n\nThe positioning algorithm waits for all images to load before calculating dimensions:\n\n```javascript\nfor (const image of images) {\n    if (image instanceof HTMLImageElement \u0026\u0026 !image.complete) {\n        await new Promise(resolve =\u003e \n            image.addEventListener('load', resolve, { once: true })\n        );\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThis prevents layout shifts from dimension changes after positioning.\n\n### Debounced Updates\n\nUpdates are debounced to prevent excessive recalculations:\n\n```javascript\nconst updateVisualNovelModeDebounced = debounce(\n    forceUpdateVisualNovelMode, \n    debounce_timeout.quick\n);\n```\n\nThe `debounce_timeout.quick` constant prevents updates from firing more frequently than necessary.\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:322-326](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:133]()\n\n---\n\n## Server Integration\n\nVisual Novel Mode uses the same sprite retrieval endpoints as single-character mode.\n\n### Sprite Loading Endpoint\n\nSprites are fetched via `GET /api/sprites/get`:\n\n```javascript\nspriteCache[memberSpriteFolderName] = await getSpritesList(memberSpriteFolderName);\n```\n\nThe endpoint returns sprite metadata including paths, labels, and modification times. See [src/endpoints/sprites.js:118-151]() for implementation.\n\n### Character Directory Structure\n\nSprites are stored in the character directory:\n\n```\ncharacters/\n  ├── CharacterName/\n  │   ├── joy.png\n  │   ├── anger.png\n  │   └── subfolder/\n  │       ├── joy.png\n  │       └── sad.png\n```\n\nThe `getSpritesPath()` function resolves both root-level and subfolder sprite locations [src/endpoints/sprites.js:18-38]().\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:214-216](), [src/endpoints/sprites.js:118-151](), [src/endpoints/sprites.js:18-38]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"43:T5c89,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Image Captioning and Multimodal [src/endpoints/sprites.js](src/endpoints/sprites.js)\n- [src/prompt-converters.js](src/prompt-converters.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document covers SillyTavern's image captioning system and multimodal AI capabilities, which enable users to send images, videos, and audio to AI models, either for automatic captioning or as context in conversations. For information about image generation, see [Image Generation Extensions](#8.2). For character expressions using images, see [Character Expressions System](#8.3).\n\n## Overview\n\nSillyTavern provides two primary multimodal capabilities:\n\n1. **Image Captioning**: Automatically generate text descriptions of images/videos using various AI services\n2. **Multimodal Chat**: Send visual and audio content directly to vision-capable language models as part of the conversation\n\nThe captioning extension (`caption`) handles both use cases through a unified interface, supporting multiple backends including local models, API services, and vision-capable LLMs.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js:1-1000]()\n\n## System Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph UserInterface[\"User Interface\"]\n        SendPic[\"#send_picture Button\"]\n        FileInput[\"File Upload Input\"]\n        CaptionBtn[\"Caption Existing Image\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph CaptionExtension[\"Caption Extension Module\"]\n        GetCaption[\"getCaptionForFile()\"]\n        DoRequest[\"doCaptionRequest()\"]\n        WrapTemplate[\"wrapCaptionTemplate()\"]\n        SendMsg[\"sendCaptionedMessage()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph CaptionSources[\"Caption Source Handlers\"]\n        Local[\"captionLocal()\"]\n        Extras[\"captionExtras()\"]\n        Horde[\"captionHorde()\"]\n        Multimodal[\"captionMultimodal()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ServerAPI[\"Server API Layer\"]\n        CaptionEndpoint[\"/api/openai/caption-image\"]\n        ExtrasEndpoint[\"/api/extra/caption\"]\n        HordeEndpoint[\"/api/horde/caption-image\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ExternalServices[\"External Services\"]\n        VisionAPIs[\"Vision-Capable LLMs\u003cbr/\u003e(GPT-4V, Claude 3.5, Gemini, etc.)\"]\n        ExtrasAPI[\"Extras API\u003cbr/\u003e(BLIP, GIT, etc.)\"]\n        HordeAPI[\"AI Horde Workers\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph ChatSystem[\"Chat Message System\"]\n        MediaAttachment[\"MediaAttachment Object\"]\n        ChatMessage[\"chat[] Message Entry\"]\n        MessageRender[\"Message Rendering\"]\n    end\n    \n Extras\n    DoRequest --\u003e Horde\n    DoRequest --\u003e Multimodal\n    \n    Local -.-\u003e CaptionEndpoint\n    Extras --\u003e ExtrasEndpoint\n    Horde --\u003e HordeEndpoint\n    Multimodal --\u003e CaptionEndpoint\n    \n    CaptionEndpoint --\u003e VisionAPIs\n    ExtrasEndpoint --\u003e ExtrasAPI\n    HordeEndpoint --\u003e HordeAPI\n    \n    GetCaption --\u003e WrapTemplate\n    WrapTemplate --\u003e SendMsg\n    SendMsg --\u003e MediaAttachment\n    MediaAttachment --\u003e ChatMessage\n    ChatMessage --\u003e MessageRender\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js:1-800](), [src/endpoints/openai.js:15-400]()\n\n## Caption Sources\n\nThe caption extension supports four different sources, configured via `extension_settings.caption.source`:\n\n### Source Comparison Table\n\n| Source | Description | Requirements | Use Case |\n|--------|-------------|--------------|----------|\n| `multimodal` | Vision-capable LLMs | API key for supported service | High-quality, context-aware captions |\n| `local` | Local transformers.js models | Modern browser, no API key | Privacy-focused, offline captioning |\n| `extras` | SillyTavern Extras API | Running Extras instance | Legacy support, BLIP/GIT models |\n| `horde` | AI Horde distributed network | Horde API key (optional) | Free, community-powered |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js:223-237]()\n\n### Multimodal Source Configuration\n\nThe multimodal source supports 20+ vision APIs configured via:\n- `extension_settings.caption.multimodal_api` - API provider (e.g., 'openai', 'anthropic', 'google')\n- `extension_settings.caption.multimodal_model` - Model selection (e.g., 'gpt-4o', 'claude-opus-4-6')\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph MultimodalAPIs[\"Supported Multimodal APIs\"]\n        OpenAI[\"openai\u003cbr/\u003eGPT-4V series\"]\n        Anthropic[\"anthropic\u003cbr/\u003eClaude 3/4 series\"]\n        Google[\"google/vertexai\u003cbr/\u003eGemini series\"]\n        Groq[\"groq\u003cbr/\u003eLlama 4 Vision\"]\n        Ollama[\"ollama\u003cbr/\u003eLocal vision models\"]\n        Custom[\"custom\u003cbr/\u003eOpenAI-compatible\"]\n    end\n    \n    CaptionMultimodal[\"captionMultimodal()\"] --\u003e GetMultimodal[\"getMultimodalCaption()\"]\n    GetMultimodal --\u003e BuildRequest[\"Build Request Body\"]\n    BuildRequest --\u003e APISelect{API Type}\n    \n    APISelect --\u003e|openai| OpenAI\n    APISelect --\u003e|anthropic| Anthropic\n    APISelect --\u003e|google| Google\n    APISelect --\u003e|groq| Groq\n    APISelect --\u003e|ollama| Ollama\n    APISelect --\u003e|custom| Custom\n [public/scripts/extensions/caption/settings.html:16-42]()\n\n## Caption Generation Flow\n\n### End-to-End Caption Request Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant UI as Send Picture Button\n    participant Ext as Caption Extension\n    participant Shared as shared.js\n    participant Server as Express Server\n    participant API as External Vision API\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eUI: Click send_picture\n    UI-\u003e\u003eUI: Open file dialog\n    User-\u003e\u003eUI: Select image file\n    UI-\u003e\u003eExt: getCaptionForFile(file, prompt)\n    \n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Convert file to base64\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Check caption source\n    \n    alt Source: multimodal\n        Ext-\u003e\u003eShared: getMultimodalCaption(base64, prompt)\n        Shared-\u003e\u003eShared: Compress/convert image if needed\n        Shared-\u003e\u003eShared: Build request body\n        Shared-\u003e\u003eServer: POST /api/openai/caption-image\n        Server-\u003e\u003eServer: Read API key from secrets\n        Server-\u003e\u003eAPI: Forward multimodal request\n        API--\u003e\u003eServer: Vision API response\n        Server--\u003e\u003eShared: { caption: \"...\" }\n        Shared--\u003e\u003eExt: caption string\n    end\n    \n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: wrapCaptionTemplate(caption)\n    Note over Ext: Apply template:\u003cbr/\u003e\"[{{user}} sends {{char}} a picture\u003cbr/\u003ethat contains: {{caption}}]\"\n    \n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: sendCaptionedMessage(caption, image)\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Create MediaAttachment object\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Create ChatMessage with media\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eUI: Add message to chat\n    UI-\u003e\u003eUser: Display captioned message\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js:120-235](), [public/scripts/extensions/shared.js:16-150]()\n\n## Media Attachment System\n\n### MediaAttachment Structure\n\nMedia content in SillyTavern is represented by the `MediaAttachment` interface:\n\n```typescript\n// As defined in message.extra.media array\n{\n  url: string,              // Data URL or asset path\n  type: MEDIA_TYPE,         // IMAGE, VIDEO, or AUDIO\n  title?: string,           // Optional caption/title\n  captioned?: boolean,      // Whether this was auto-captioned\n  source?: MEDIA_SOURCE,    // Origin of the media\n  append_title?: boolean,   // Append title to message text\n}\n```\n\nMedia attachments are stored in the `extra.media` array of chat messages:\n\n```javascript\n// Message structure with media\n{\n  mes: \"Message text\",\n  extra: {\n    media: [MediaAttachment],      // Array of media items\n    media_display: MEDIA_DISPLAY,  // GALLERY, LIST, etc.\n    media_index: number,            // Currently displayed index\n    inline_image: boolean,          // Show inline vs. gallery\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/constants.js:1-200](), [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js:180-215]()\n\n### Captioning Existing Messages\n\nThe `/editcaption` slash command allows captioning images already in the chat:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Command[\"/editcaption\"] --\u003e ParseArgs[\"Parse Arguments\u003cbr/\u003emesId, mediaIndex\"]\n    ParseArgs --\u003e GetMsg[\"Retrieve chat[mesId]\"]\n Handling\n\nDifferent chat completion APIs require different formats for multimodal content. The server-side prompt converters handle this:\n\n### Image Format Conversion Matrix\n\n| API | Image Format | Location in Prompt |\n|-----|-------------|-------------------|\n| OpenAI | `{type:'image_url', image_url:{url:'data:...'}}` | message.content array |\n| Claude | `{type:'image', source:{type:'base64', media_type, data}}` | message.content array |\n| Google | `{inlineData:{mimeType, data}}` | parts array |\n| Groq | `{type:'image_url', image_url:{url:'data:...'}}` | message.content array |\n| Cohere | `{type:'image', source:{type:'url', url:'data:...'}}` | message array |\n\n**Sources:** [src/prompt-converters.js:1-1200](), [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:1-2000]()\n\n### Claude Image Conversion\n\nThe `convertClaudeMessages` function transforms OpenAI-style image content to Claude's format:\n\n```javascript\n// Input: OpenAI format\n{\n  type: 'image_url',\n  image_url: { url: 'data:image/jpeg;base64,...' }\n}\n\n// Output: Claude format\n{\n  type: 'image',\n  source: {\n    type: 'base64',\n    media_type: 'image/jpeg',\n    data: '...'  // base64 without prefix\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [src/prompt-converters.js:270-295]()\n\n### Google Gemini Conversion\n\nThe `convertGooglePrompt` function handles Gemini's unique structure:\n\n```javascript\n// Gemini structure\n{\n  contents: [\n [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js:700-850]()\n\n## Server-Side Caption Endpoint\n\n### Request/Response Format\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant Route as /api/openai/caption-image\n    participant Secrets as Secret Manager\n    participant API as External Vision API\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eRoute: POST with body\n    Note over Client,Route: {\u003cbr/\u003e  image: base64,\u003cbr/\u003e  prompt: string,\u003cbr/\u003e  api: 'openai',\u003cbr/\u003e  model: 'gpt-4o',\u003cbr/\u003e  reverse_proxy?: url,\u003cbr/\u003e  proxy_password?: 'claude-opus-4-6',\n  messages: [{\n    role: 'user',\n    content: [\n      { type: 'text', text: prompt },\n      { type: 'image', source: { type: 'base64', ... } }\n    ]\n  }],\n  max_tokens: supported for select multimodal APIs:\n\n```javascript\nfunction isVideoCaptioningAvailable() {\n  const api = extension_settings.caption.multimodal_api;\n  const model = extension_settings.caption.multimodal_model;\n  \n  // Google Gemini supports video\n  if (api === 'google' || api === 'vertexai') {\n    return /^gemini-/i.test(model);\n  }\n  \n  return false;\n}\n```\n\nVideos are handled identically to images, but with `MEDIA_TYPE.VIDEO` and appropriate MIME types (e.g., `video/mp4`, `video/webm`).\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/extensions/caption/index.js:550-600]()\n\n### Audio Transcription Support\n\nAudio attachments can be sent to compatible models:\n\n```javascript\n// Audio support detection\nfunction isAudioInliningSupported() {\n  if (main_api !== 'openai') return false;\n  \n  const source = oai_settings.chat_completion_source;\n  const model = getChatCompletionModel();\n  \n  // Google Gemini 2.0+ supports audio\n  if (source === 'makersuite' || source === 'vertexai') {\n    return /^gemini-2\\./i.test(model);\n  }\n  \n  return false;\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:**
