[src/util.js](src/util.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document describes the group chat system in SillyTavern, which enables conversations between the user and multiple AI characters simultaneously. Group chats support various activation strategies for determining which character responds next, multiple generation modes for message handling, and automated conversation flow.\n\nFor information about individual character management, see [Character Management](#5.1). For chat storage formats, see [Chat Storage and Persistence](#5.2). For world info that applies to groups, see [World Info System](#6.1).\n\n---\n\n## Overview and Core Concepts\n\nThe group chat system allows multiple characters to participate in a single conversation. Each group maintains its own set of members, activation strategy, generation settings, and chat history. The system determines which character speaks next based on the configured activation strategy and manages the conversation flow accordingly.\n\n### Group Data Structure\n\nA group is represented by a `Group` object with the following structure:\n\n| Field | Type | Description |\n|-------|------|-------------|\n| `id` | string | Unique identifier for the group (numeric string) |\n| `name` | string | Display name of the group |\n| `members` | string[] | Array of character avatar filenames |\n| `avatar_url` | string | Path to the group's avatar image |\n| `allow_self_responses` | boolean | Whether a character can respond consecutively |\n| `activation_strategy` | number | Enum value determining member selection logic |\n| `generation_mode` | number | Enum value determining message handling behavior |\n| `disabled_members` | string[] | Members temporarily excluded from generation |\n| `chat_id` | string | Currently active chat identifier |\n| `chats` | string[] | Array of all chat IDs for this group |\n| `past_metadata` | object | Metadata from past chats |\n| `favorite` | boolean | Whether the group is marked as favorite |\n| `auto_mode_delay` | number | Delay in seconds between auto-mode generations |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:1-1000]()\n\n---\n\n## Activation Strategies\n\nThe activation strategy determines how the system selects which character responds next. Four strategies are available:\n\n```javascript\nexport const group_activation_strategy = {\n    NATURAL: 0,\n    LIST: 1,\n    MANUAL: 2,\n    POOLED: 3,\n};\n```\n\n### Strategy Comparison Table\n\n| Strategy | Selection Method | Use Case |\n|----------|------------------|----------|\n| **Natural** | AI analyzes chat history to determine most appropriate speaker | Dynamic, context-aware conversations |\n| **List** | Round-robin through non-disabled members | Structured turn-taking |\n| **Manual** | User explicitly selects each speaker | Full control over conversation flow |\n| **Pooled** | Random selection from non-disabled members | Unpredictable, varied interactions |\n\n### Natural Strategy Flow\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Start[\"Generate Request\"] --\u003e CheckNatural{\"Activation Strategy\u003cbr/\u003e== NATURAL?\"}\n    CheckNatural --\u003e|Yes| GetCandidates[\"Get Non-Disabled Members\"]\n    GetCandidates --\u003e BuildPrompt[\"Build Activation Prompt\u003cbr/\u003ewith Recent Messages\"]\n    BuildPrompt --\u003e SendToAI[\"Send to Text Generation API\u003cbr/\u003egenerateQuietPrompt()\"]\n    SendToAI --\u003e ParseResponse[\"Parse Character Name\u003cbr/\u003efrom Response\"]\n    ParseResponse --\u003e Validate{\"Valid Character\u003cbr/\u003ein Group?\"}\n    Validate --\u003e|Yes| SetActive[\"Set as Active Member\"]\n    Validate --\u003e|No| Fallback[\"Fall Back to\u003cbr/\u003ePooled Strategy\"]\n    Fallback --\u003e SetActive\n    CheckNatural --\u003e|No| OtherStrategy[\"Use Other Strategy Logic\"]\n    SetActive --\u003e Generate[\"Generate Message\u003cbr/\u003efor Selected Character\"]\n    OtherStrategy --\u003e Generate\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:800-900](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:1700-1800]()\n\n---\n\n## Generation Modes\n\nGeneration modes control how character messages are added to the chat and how character information is combined:\n\n```javascript\nexport const group_generation_mode = {\n    SWAP: 0,\n    APPEND: 1,\n    APPEND_DISABLED: 2,\n};\n```\n\n### Mode Behavior Table\n\n| Mode | Behavior | Message Handling | Character Cards |\n|------|----------|------------------|-----------------|\n| **SWAP** | Replaces last message with new character's response | Single message visible, swipes stored | Uses only current speaker's card |\n| **APPEND** | Adds new message to chat history | All messages visible in sequence | Merges cards from all enabled members |\n| **APPEND_DISABLED** | Like APPEND but includes disabled members | All messages visible | Merges cards from all members including disabled |\n\n### Generation Mode State Diagram\n\n```mermaid\nstateDiagram-v2\n    [*] --\u003e CheckMode\n    CheckMode --\u003e Swap: \"mode == SWAP\"\n    CheckMode --\u003e Append: \"mode == APPEND or APPEND_DISABLED\"\n    \n    Swap --\u003e UseSingleCard: \"Use current speaker's character card\"\n    UseSingleCard --\u003e ReplaceLastMessage: \"deleteLastMessage()\"\n    ReplaceLastMessage --\u003e AddNewMessage: \"addOneMessage()\"\n    \n    Append --\u003e MergeCards: \"getGroupCharacterCardsLazy()\"\n    MergeCards --\u003e CombineDescription: \"Merge description fields\"\n    CombineDescription --\u003e CombinePersonality: \"Merge personality fields\"\n    CombinePersonality --\u003e CombineScenario: \"Merge scenario fields\"\n    CombineScenario --\u003e CombineExamples: \"Merge example messages\"\n    CombineExamples --\u003e AddNewMessage: \"addOneMessage()\"\n    \n    AddNewMessage --\u003e SaveChat: \"saveGroupChat()\"\n    SaveChat --\u003e [*]\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:129-133](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:478-572]()\n\n---\n\n## Character Card Merging\n\nIn APPEND and APPEND_DISABLED modes, the system combines character cards from all group members into a single aggregated card. This allows the AI to consider all characters' descriptions, personalities, and scenarios when generating responses.\n\n### Merging Process\n\nThe `getGroupCharacterCardsLazy()` function at [public/scripts/group-chats.js:498-572]() implements lazy evaluation of merged character fields:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Start[\"getGroupCharacterCardsLazy(groupId, characterId)\"] --\u003e CheckMode{\"generation_mode != SWAP?\"}\n    CheckMode --\u003e|No| ReturnNull[\"Return null\u003cbr/\u003e(Fall back to single card)\"]\n    CheckMode --\u003e|Yes| CreateLazy[\"Create lazy field object\"]\n    \n    CreateLazy --\u003e LazyDesc[\"description getter\"]\n    CreateLazy --\u003e LazyPers[\"personality getter\"]\n    CreateLazy --\u003e LazyScen[\"scenario getter\"]\n    CreateLazy --\u003e LazyExam[\"mesExamples getter\"]\n    \n    LazyDesc --\u003e CollectDesc[\"collectField('Description')\u003cbr/\u003ec =\u003e c.description\"]\n    LazyPers --\u003e CollectPers[\"collectField('Personality')\u003cbr/\u003ec =\u003e c.personality\"]\n    LazyScen --\u003e CollectScen[\"collectField('Scenario')\u003cbr/\u003ec =\u003e c.scenario\"]\n    LazyExam --\u003e CollectExam[\"collectField('Example Messages')\u003cbr/\u003ec =\u003e c.mes_example\"]\n    \n    CollectDesc --\u003e FilterMembers[\"For each member in group.members\"]\n    FilterMembers --\u003e CheckDisabled{\"member in\u003cbr/\u003edisabled_members?\"}\n    CheckDisabled --\u003e|Yes \u0026 mode != APPEND_DISABLED| Skip[\"Skip member\"]\n    CheckDisabled --\u003e|No| Include[\"Include member\"]\n    \n    Include --\u003e GetChar[\"characters.find(x =\u003e x.avatar == member)\"]\n    GetChar --\u003e ExtractField[\"Extract field value\"]\n    ExtractField --\u003e ApplyTransform[\"replaceAndPrepareForJoin()\"]\n    \n    ApplyTransform --\u003e AddPrefix[\"Prepend generation_mode_join_prefix\"]\n    AddPrefix --\u003e AddSuffix[\"Append generation_mode_join_suffix\"]\n    AddSuffix --\u003e Transform[\"customTransform()\u003cbr/\u003eReplace \u003cFIELDNAME\u003e macro\u003cbr/\u003eApply baseChatReplace()\"]\n    \n    Transform --\u003e Join[\"Join all values with \\\\n\"]\n    Join --\u003e Return[\"Return merged string\"]\n    \n    Skip --\u003e FilterMembers\n```\n\n### Merge Configuration\n\nGroups can customize how fields are combined using these settings:\n\n| Setting | Default | Purpose | Example |\n|---------|---------|---------|---------|\n| `generation_mode_join_prefix` | `''` | Text added before each member's field | `'[Character: {{char}}]\\n'` |\n| `generation_mode_join_suffix` | `''` | Text added after each member's field | `'\\n[End of {{char}}]'` |\n\nThese settings support the `\u003cFIELDNAME\u003e` macro which gets replaced with the field being merged (e.g., \"Description\", \"Personality\").\n\n### Example Merged Output\n\nGiven a group with two members using prefix `'[{{char}}\\'s \u003cFIELDNAME\u003e]\\n'`:\n\n**Input:**\n- Alice description: \"A brave knight\"\n- Bob description: \"A clever wizard\"\n\n**Output:**\n```\n[Alice's Description]\nA brave knight\n[Bob's Description]\nA clever wizard\n```\n\n### Special Handling\n\n1. **Scenario Override**: If `chat_metadata.scenario` is set, it replaces the merged scenario field\n2. **Example Messages**: Automatically prepends `\u003cSTART\u003e` if not present\n3. **Empty Fields**: Members with empty fields are filtered out before joining\n4. **Character Name Substitution**: `{{char}}` in prefix/suffix is replaced with the specific member's name\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:478-572]()\n\n---\n\n## Depth Prompts in Groups\n\nGroups aggregate depth prompts from all members when in APPEND or APPEND_DISABLED mode. The `getGroupDepthPrompts()` function at [public/scripts/group-chats.js:428-470]() handles this.\n\n### Depth Prompt Aggregation\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Start[\"getGroupDepthPrompts(groupId, characterId)\"] --\u003e CheckMode{\"generation_mode == SWAP?\"}\n    CheckMode --\u003e|Yes| ReturnEmpty[\"Return []\u003cbr/\u003e(No aggregation in SWAP mode)\"]\n    CheckMode --\u003e|No| Initialize[\"depthPrompts = []\"]\n    \n    Initialize --\u003e ForEach[\"For each member in group.members\"]\n    ForEach --\u003e FindChar[\"characters.findIndex(x =\u003e x.avatar == member)\"]\n    FindChar --\u003e ValidChar{\"character exists?\"}\n    ValidChar --\u003e|No| Skip[\"Log skip, continue\"]\n    \n    ValidChar --\u003e|Yes| CheckDisabled{\"member in disabled_members\u003cbr/\u003e\u0026\u0026 characterId != index?\"}\n    CheckDisabled --\u003e|Yes| Skip\n    CheckDisabled --\u003e|No| Extract[\"Extract depth_prompt data\"]\n    \n    Extract --\u003e GetText[\"text = character.data.extensions.depth_prompt.prompt\"]\n    GetText --\u003e GetDepth[\"depth = character.data.extensions.depth_prompt.depth\"]\n    GetDepth --\u003e GetRole[\"role = character.data.extensions.depth_prompt.role\"]\n    \n    GetRole --\u003e Transform[\"baseChatReplace(text, null, character.name)\"]\n    Transform --\u003e CheckEmpty{\"text.trim()\u003cbr/\u003enot empty?\"}\n    CheckEmpty --\u003e|Yes| Push[\"depthPrompts.push({ text, depth, role })\"]\n    CheckEmpty --\u003e|No| Skip\n    \n    Push --\u003e ForEach\n    Skip --\u003e ForEach\n    ForEach --\u003e Done[\"Return depthPrompts[]\"]\n```\n\n### Depth Prompt Structure\n\nEach aggregated depth prompt contains:\n\n| Field | Type | Description |\n|-------|------|-------------|\n| `text` | string | The prompt text after `baseChatReplace()` transformation |\n| `depth` | number | Injection depth (default: `depth_prompt_depth_default` = 4) |\n| `role` | string | Role for the prompt (default: `depth_prompt_role_default`) |\n\n### Integration with Prompt System\n\nThe aggregated depth prompts are passed to the prompt manager and injected at their specified depths during prompt construction. This allows each group member to contribute context at different levels of the conversation history.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:428-470]()\n\n---\n\n## Group Chat Generation Flow\n\nThe following diagram maps the generation flow to actual code entities:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    UserAction[\"User Triggers Generation\"] --\u003e generateGroupWrapper[\"generateGroupWrapper()\u003cbr/\u003eline ~450\"]\n    \n    generateGroupWrapper --\u003e CheckLock{\"is_group_generating\u003cbr/\u003e== true?\"}\n    CheckLock --\u003e|Yes| Block[\"Block Generation\"]\n    CheckLock --\u003e|No| SetLock[\"is_group_generating = true\"]\n    \n    SetLock --\u003e selectNextGroupMember[\"selectNextGroupMember()\u003cbr/\u003eline ~800\"]\n    \n    selectNextGroupMember --\u003e CheckStrategy{\"Check\u003cbr/\u003eactivation_strategy\"}\n    \n    CheckStrategy --\u003e|NATURAL| naturalSelection[\"selectMemberNatural()\u003cbr/\u003eAI-based selection\"]\n    CheckStrategy --\u003e|LIST| listSelection[\"selectMemberList()\u003cbr/\u003eRound-robin\"]\n    CheckStrategy --\u003e|MANUAL| manualSelection[\"User Selection UI\"]\n    CheckStrategy --\u003e|POOLED| pooledSelection[\"selectMemberPooled()\u003cbr/\u003eRandom\"]\n    \n    naturalSelection --\u003e SetCharacter[\"setCharacterId()\u003cbr/\u003eActivate selected character\"]\n    listSelection --\u003e SetCharacter\n    manualSelection --\u003e SetCharacter\n    pooledSelection --\u003e SetCharacter\n    \n    SetCharacter --\u003e Generate[\"Generate()\u003cbr/\u003efrom ../script.js\"]\n    Generate --\u003e ProcessMessage[\"addOneMessage()\u003cbr/\u003eAdd to chat array\"]\n    \n    ProcessMessage --\u003e CheckMode{\"generation_mode\"}\n    CheckMode --\u003e|SWAP| HandleSwap[\"Store as swipe\u003cbr/\u003eReplace last message\"]\n    CheckMode --\u003e|APPEND| HandleAppend[\"Append to chat\"]\n    \n    HandleSwap --\u003e SaveGroupChat[\"saveGroupChat()\u003cbr/\u003eline ~250\"]\n    HandleAppend --\u003e SaveGroupChat\n    \n    SaveGroupChat --\u003e CheckAutoMode{\"Auto Mode\u003cbr/\u003eEnabled?\"}\n    CheckAutoMode --\u003e|Yes| ScheduleNext[\"Schedule Next Generation\u003cbr/\u003eauto_mode_delay seconds\"]\n    CheckAutoMode --\u003e|No| ReleaseLock[\"is_group_generating = false\"]\n    \n    ScheduleNext --\u003e ReleaseLock\n    ReleaseLock --\u003e End[\"End\"]\n    Block --\u003e End\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:450-550](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:800-1000](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:2300-2500]()\n\n---\n\n## Member Management System\n\n### Member Selection Logic\n\nThe member selection process is implemented across multiple functions that handle different activation strategies:\n\n#### Main Selection Function\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Start[\"selectNextGroupMember()\"] --\u003e GetEnabled[\"group.members.filter()\u003cbr/\u003eExclude disabled_members\"]\n    \n    GetEnabled --\u003e CheckCount{\"enabledMembers.length \u003e 0?\"}\n    CheckCount --\u003e|No| NoMembers[\"toastr.warning('No enabled members')\u003cbr/\u003eReturn false\"]\n    \n    CheckCount --\u003e|Yes| CheckStrategy{\"Switch on\u003cbr/\u003egroup.activation_strategy\"}\n    \n    CheckStrategy --\u003e|\"NATURAL (0)\"| Natural[\"selectMemberNatural(enabledMembers)\"]\n    Natural --\u003e BuildPrompt[\"Build activation prompt with\u003cbr/\u003erecent chat messages\"]\n    BuildPrompt --\u003e CallAI[\"generateQuietPrompt()\u003cbr/\u003efrom ../script.js\"]\n    CallAI --\u003e ParseResponse[\"Extract character name\u003cbr/\u003eusing Fuse.js fuzzy search\"]\n    ParseResponse --\u003e ValidateNatural{\"Found valid\u003cbr/\u003emember?\"}\n    ValidateNatural --\u003e|Yes| SetCharNatural[\"setCharacterId(foundIndex)\"]\n    ValidateNatural --\u003e|No| FallbackPooled[\"selectMemberPooled()\u003cbr/\u003eFallback strategy\"]\n    FallbackPooled --\u003e SetCharNatural\n    \n    CheckStrategy --\u003e|\"LIST (1)\"| List[\"selectMemberList(enabledMembers)\"]\n    List --\u003e GetPrevious[\"chat_metadata.group_activation_info.lastSpeaker\"]\n    GetPrevious --\u003e FindNext[\"currentIndex = members.indexOf(lastSpeaker)\u003cbr/\u003enextIndex = (currentIndex + 1) % members.length\"]\n    FindNext --\u003e SetCharList[\"setCharacterId(nextIndex)\"]\n    \n    CheckStrategy --\u003e|\"POOLED (3)\"| Pooled[\"selectMemberPooled(enabledMembers)\"]\n    Pooled --\u003e RandomSelect[\"randomIndex = Math.floor(Math.random() * length)\"]\n    RandomSelect --\u003e CheckSelfResponse{\"group.allow_self_responses?\"}\n    CheckSelfResponse --\u003e|false| EnsureDifferent[\"while (selected == lastSpeaker)\u003cbr/\u003eselect again\"]\n    CheckSelfResponse --\u003e|true| SetCharPooled[\"setCharacterId(randomIndex)\"]\n    EnsureDifferent --\u003e SetCharPooled\n    \n    CheckStrategy --\u003e|\"MANUAL (2)\"| Manual[\"displayMemberSelectionUI()\"]\n    Manual --\u003e AwaitUser[\"await user selection\"]\n    AwaitUser --\u003e SetCharManual[\"setCharacterId(selectedIndex)\"]\n    \n    SetCharNatural --\u003e UpdateMetadata[\"chat_metadata.group_activation_info.lastSpeaker = member\u003cbr/\u003echat_metadata.group_activation_info.activationCounter++\"]\n    SetCharList --\u003e UpdateMetadata\n    SetCharPooled --\u003e UpdateMetadata\n    SetCharManual --\u003e UpdateMetadata\n    \n    UpdateMetadata --\u003e ReturnTrue[\"Return true\"]\n    NoMembers --\u003e End[\"End\"]\n    ReturnTrue --\u003e End\n```\n\n#### Natural Selection AI Prompt\n\nThe natural selection strategy uses an AI prompt to determine the most appropriate speaker. The prompt is constructed in `getActivationRegex()` and includes:\n\n- Recent chat messages (configurable depth)\n- List of available group members\n- Instruction to select the next speaker\n\nThe AI response is parsed to extract a character name, which is matched against group members using fuzzy search via the Fuse.js library.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:1544-1680](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:1685-1780](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:1785-1880]()\n\n### Member Data Structure\n\nEach member is referenced by their character avatar filename. Additional member-specific data is stored in various locations:\n\n| Data | Location | Description |\n|------|----------|-------------|\n| Member list | `group.members` | Array of avatar filenames (e.g., `[\"knight.png\", \"wizard.png\"]`) |\n| Disabled status | `group.disabled_members` | Array of temporarily disabled avatars |\n| Last speaker | `chat_metadata.group_activation_info.lastSpeaker` | Avatar filename of previous speaker |\n| Activation count | `chat_metadata.group_activation_info.activationCounter` | Number of activations this chat |\n| Character data | `characters[index]` | Full character object from global array |\n| Member order | Order in `members` array | Used by LIST strategy for round-robin |\n\n### Member Resolution\n\nThe system resolves members using the avatar filename as the key:\n\n```javascript\n// Find character by avatar (from group-chats.js)\nconst character = characters.find(x =\u003e x.avatar === member);\nconst characterIndex = characters.findIndex(x =\u003e x.avatar === member);\n\n// Set active character\nsetCharacterId(characterIndex); // Updates this_chid global\nsetCharacterName(character.name); // Updates name2 global\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:114-120](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:1544-1650]()\n\n---\n\n## Auto Mode\n\nAuto mode enables continuous conversation flow without user intervention. When enabled, the system automatically generates the next character response after a configurable delay.\n\n### Auto Mode Implementation\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant User\n    participant UI as \"UI Toggle\"\n    participant Worker as \"autoModeWorker\u003cbr/\u003esetInterval\"\n    participant Generate as \"generateGroupWrapper()\"\n    participant Chat as \"Chat System\"\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eUI: Toggle Auto Mode\n    UI-\u003e\u003eUI: Set is_group_automode_enabled\n    UI-\u003e\u003eWorker: setAutoModeWorker()\n    Worker-\u003e\u003eWorker: Create interval timer\u003cbr/\u003edelay = group.auto_mode_delay * 1000\n    \n    loop Every auto_mode_delay seconds\n        Worker-\u003e\u003eWorker: groupChatAutoModeWorker()\n        Worker-\u003e\u003eGenerate: Check if can generate\n        Generate-\u003e\u003eGenerate: Verify !is_send_press\u003cbr/\u003e!is_group_generating\n        alt Can Generate\n            Generate-\u003e\u003eGenerate: selectNextGroupMember()\n            Generate-\u003e\u003eChat: Generate()\n            Chat--\u003e\u003eGenerate: Message added\n            Generate-\u003e\u003eGenerate: Save chat\n        else Cannot Generate\n            Generate-\u003e\u003eWorker: Skip this cycle\n        end\n    end\n    \n    User-\u003e\u003eUI: Disable Auto Mode\n    UI-\u003e\u003eWorker: clearInterval(autoModeWorker)\n```\n\n### Auto Mode Configuration\n\n| Setting | Default | Description |\n|---------|---------|-------------|\n| `auto_mode_delay` | `DEFAULT_AUTO_MODE_DELAY` (5 seconds) | Delay between automatic generations |\n| `is_group_automode_enabled` | `false` | Whether auto mode is currently active |\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:136-147](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:2800-2900]()\n\n---\n\n## Server-Side Storage Architecture\n\n### Storage Structure\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    DataRoot[\"DATA_ROOT/users/{handle}/\"] --\u003e GroupsDir[\"groups/\"]\n    DataRoot --\u003e ChatsDir[\"chats/\"]\n    \n    GroupsDir --\u003e GroupJSON[\"group_{id}.json\u003cbr/\u003eGroup metadata\"]\n    \n    ChatsDir --\u003e GroupChatDir[\"{group_id}/\"]\n    GroupChatDir --\u003e ChatFile[\"chat_{chat_id}.jsonl\u003cbr/\u003eMessage history\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Group JSON Structure\"\n        GroupJSON --\u003e gid[\"id: string\"]\n        GroupJSON --\u003e gname[\"name: string\"]\n        GroupJSON --\u003e gmembers[\"members: string[]\"]\n        GroupJSON --\u003e gstrat[\"activation_strategy: number\"]\n        GroupJSON --\u003e gmode[\"generation_mode: number\"]\n        GroupJSON --\u003e gchats[\"chats: string[]\"]\n        GroupJSON --\u003e gchat_id[\"chat_id: string\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Chat JSONL Format\"\n        ChatFile --\u003e Header[\"Line 1: ChatHeader\u003cbr/\u003e{chat_metadata}\"]\n        ChatFile --\u003e Msg1[\"Line 2: ChatMessage\"]\n        ChatFile --\u003e Msg2[\"Line 3: ChatMessage\"]\n        ChatFile --\u003e MsgN[\"Line N: ChatMessage\"]\n    end\n```\n\n### API Endpoints\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |\n|----------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/groups/all` | GET | Retrieve all groups for current user |\n| `/api/groups/get` | POST | Get specific group by ID |\n| `/api/groups/create` | POST | Create new group |\n| `/api/groups/edit` | POST | Update group metadata |\n| `/api/groups/delete` | POST | Delete group and associated chats |\n| `/api/chats/group/get` | POST | Load group chat messages |\n| `/api/chats/group/save` | POST | Save group chat messages |\n\n**Sources:** [src/endpoints/groups.js:1-300](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:154-300]()\n\n### Group Save Flow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client as \"group-chats.js\"\n    participant Debounce as \"saveGroupDebounced\"\n    participant Save as \"_save()\"\n    participant API as \"/api/groups/edit\"\n    participant FS as \"File System\"\n    participant Reload as \"getCharacters()\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eClient: Modify group object\n    Client-\u003e\u003eDebounce: saveGroupDebounced(group)\n    Note over Debounce: Wait debounce_timeout.relaxed\u003cbr/\u003e(1000ms)\n    Debounce-\u003e\u003eSave: _save(group, reload)\n    Save-\u003e\u003eAPI: POST with group JSON\n    API-\u003e\u003eFS: Write group_{id}.json\n    FS--\u003e\u003eAPI: Success\n    API--\u003e\u003eSave: Response\n    alt reload == true\n        Save-\u003e\u003eReload: getCharacters()\n        Reload-\u003e\u003eReload: Refresh character list\u003cbr/\u003eUpdate groups array\n    end\n    Save--\u003e\u003eClient: Complete\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:139-163](), [src/endpoints/groups.js:50-100]()\n\n---\n\n## Integration with Character System\n\nGroups are tightly integrated with the character management system:\n\n### Character-Group Relationship\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    CharArray[\"characters[]\u003cbr/\u003eGlobal array\"] --\u003e CharObj[\"Character Object\"]\n    \n    CharObj --\u003e avatar[\"avatar: string\"]\n    CharObj --\u003e name[\"name: string\"]\n    CharObj --\u003e data[\"data: CharacterData\"]\n    \n    Group[\"Group Object\"] --\u003e members[\"members: string[]\"]\n    members -.References.-\u003e avatar\n    \n    Group --\u003e disabled[\"disabled_members: string[]\"]\n    disabled -.References.-\u003e avatar\n    \n    SelectMember[\"selectNextGroupMember()\"] --\u003e FindChar[\"characters.find()\u003cbr/\u003ex.avatar == member\"]\n    FindChar --\u003e SetChid[\"setCharacterId(index)\"]\n    \n    SetChid --\u003e UpdateGlobal[\"Update this_chid\u003cbr/\u003eUpdate name2\"]\n```\n\n### Member Validation\n\nWhen a group is loaded, the system validates that all members exist as characters:\n\n```javascript\n// Validation logic from validateGroup()\ngroup.members = group.members.filter(member =\u003e {\n    const character = characters.find(x =\u003e \n        x.avatar === member || x.name === member\n    );\n    if (!character) {\n        // Member removed, show warning\n        toastr.warning(`Member ${member} not found`);\n        dirty = true;\n    }\n    return character;\n});\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:218-245](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:800-850]()\n\n---\n\n## Multi-Character Visual Display\n\nThe group chat UI supports displaying multiple character avatars and sprites simultaneously:\n\n### Avatar Display Modes\n\n| Mode | Description | Configuration |\n|------|-------------|---------------|\n| **Standard** | Single avatar for current speaker | Default behavior |\n| **Visual Novel** | All member sprites shown side-by-side | Requires expressions extension |\n| **Muted Sprites** | Hide sprites for disabled members | `hideMutedSprites` setting |\n\n### Sprite Display Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    ChatMessage[\"New Message Added\"] --\u003e CheckGroup{\"selected_group\u003cbr/\u003e!= null?\"}\n    \n    CheckGroup --\u003e|Yes| CheckExtension{\"Expressions\u003cbr/\u003eExtension Active?\"}\n    CheckGroup --\u003e|No| SingleAvatar[\"Display Single Avatar\"]\n    \n    CheckExtension --\u003e|Yes| VNMode[\"Visual Novel Mode\"]\n    CheckExtension --\u003e|No| GroupAvatar[\"Display Group Avatar\"]\n    \n    VNMode --\u003e GetMembers[\"Get group.members[]\"]\n    GetMembers --\u003e FilterDisabled{\"hideMutedSprites\u003cbr/\u003e== true?\"}\n    FilterDisabled --\u003e|Yes| RemoveMuted[\"Filter disabled_members\"]\n    FilterDisabled --\u003e|No| AllMembers[\"Include all members\"]\n    \n    RemoveMuted --\u003e RenderSprites[\"Render Sprite Container\"]\n    AllMembers --\u003e RenderSprites\n    \n    RenderSprites --\u003e ForEachMember[\"For each member\"]\n    ForEachMember --\u003e LoadSprite[\"Load sprite image\u003cbr/\u003efrom /characters/{avatar}\"]\n    LoadSprite --\u003e Position[\"Position sprite\u003cbr/\u003ebased on index\"]\n    Position --\u003e ApplyExpression[\"Apply expression\u003cbr/\u003efrom classification\"]\n    \n    ApplyExpression --\u003e CheckActive{\"Is current\u003cbr/\u003espeaker?\"}\n    CheckActive --\u003e|Yes| Highlight[\"Add highlight effect\"]\n    CheckActive --\u003e|No| Dim[\"Reduce opacity\"]\n    \n    Highlight --\u003e Display[\"Display in chat\"]\n    Dim --\u003e Display\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:2500-2700](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:1-500]()\n\n---\n\n## Group Chat Queue System\n\nThe queue system determines the order of character responses, particularly relevant for natural and pooled strategies:\n\n### Queue Order Management\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TD\n    Start[\"Generation Cycle Starts\"] --\u003e CheckQueue{\"groupChatQueueOrder\u003cbr/\u003ehas entries?\"}\n    \n    CheckQueue --\u003e|Yes| GetFromQueue[\"Get next from\u003cbr/\u003eMap.entries()\"]\n    GetFromQueue --\u003e ValidateMember{\"Member still\u003cbr/\u003ein group?\"}\n    ValidateMember --\u003e|Yes| UseQueued[\"Use queued character\"]\n    ValidateMember --\u003e|No| RemoveInvalid[\"Remove from queue\"]\n    RemoveInvalid --\u003e CheckQueue\n    \n    CheckQueue --\u003e|No| NormalSelection[\"selectNextGroupMember()\u003cbr/\u003eNormal logic\"]\n    \n    UseQueued --\u003e SetActive[\"Activate character\"]\n    NormalSelection --\u003e SetActive\n    \n    SetActive --\u003e Generate[\"Generate message\"]\n    Generate --\u003e UpdateQueue[\"Update queue order\u003cbr/\u003efor next cycle\"]\n```\n\nThe `groupChatQueueOrder` is a `Map\u003cstring, number\u003e` that maintains activation order:\n\n- **Key**: Character avatar filename\n- **Value**: Queue position (lower = higher priority)\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:141-147](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:2100-2200]()\n\n---\n\n## Group-Specific World Info\n\nGroups can have their own world info (lorebook) entries or leverage character-specific world info:\n\n### World Info Activation Priority\n\n1. **User-defined World Info**: Selected from UI dropdown\n2. **Character World Info**: Each member's attached lorebook\n3. **Global World Info**: Applied to all chats\n\nWhen scanning for world info entries, the system processes all active lorebooks and merges activated entries based on their configuration.\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:1300-1400](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:1-500]()\n\n---\n\n## Group Metadata and Chat Metadata\n\nGroup-specific metadata is stored in two locations:\n\n### Group Metadata Structure\n\n```javascript\n{\n    \"id\": \"1234567890\",\n    \"name\": \"Adventure Party\",\n    \"members\": [\"knight.png\", \"wizard.png\", \"rogue.png\"],\n    \"activation_strategy\": 0,\n    \"generation_mode\": 1,\n    \"disabled_members\": [\"rogue.png\"],\n    \"chat_id\": \"1234567890_chat_0\",\n    \"chats\": [\"1234567890_chat_0\", \"1234567890_chat_1\"],\n    \"allow_self_responses\": false,\n    \"auto_mode_delay\": 5,\n    \"favorite\": false\n}\n```\n\n### Chat Metadata Structure\n\nThe first line of each group chat JSONL file contains metadata:\n\n```javascript\n{\n    \"group_activation_info\": {\n        \"lastSpeaker\": \"wizard.png\",\n        \"activationCounter\": 42\n    },\n    \"chat_metadata\": {\n        \"note_prompt\": \"Custom system note\",\n        \"variables\": { /* local variables */ }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:1-150](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:1-200]()\n\n---\n\n## Error Handling and Edge Cases\n\n### Common Edge Cases\n\n| Scenario | Handling |\n|----------|----------|\n| No enabled members | Show toast warning, abort generation |\n| Member character deleted | Remove from group on next validation |\n| Concurrent generation | Lock with `is_group_generating` flag |\n| Natural selection fails | Fall back to pooled strategy |\n| Chat save conflict | Use debounced save with write-file-atomic |\n\n### Generation Lock Mechanism\n\n```mermaid\nstateDiagram-v2\n    [*] --\u003e Idle: is_group_generating = false\n    Idle --\u003e Locked: generateGroupWrapper() called\n    Locked --\u003e Generating: is_group_generating = true\n    Generating --\u003e Processing: selectNextGroupMember()\n    Processing --\u003e Generating: Generate()\n    Generating --\u003e Saving: addOneMessage()\n    Saving --\u003e Releasing: saveGroupChat()\n    Releasing --\u003e Idle: is_group_generating = false\n    \n    Locked --\u003e Idle: Already generating\u003cbr/\u003eReturn early\n```\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:450-500](), [public/scripts/group-chats.js:2300-2400]()\n\n---\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n### Optimization Strategies\n\n1. **Debounced Saves**: Group saves are debounced to prevent excessive file writes\n   - Timeout: `debounce_timeout.relaxed` (1000ms)\n   - Implementation: [public/scripts/group-chats.js:139]()\n\n2. **Character Lazy Loading**: Characters can be loaded on-demand\n   - Controlled by `useShallowCharacters` flag\n   - Implementation: [src/endpoints/characters.js:35]()\n\n3. **Message Batching**: Multiple messages added in sequence trigger single save\n   - Implementation: [public/scripts/group-chats.js:250-300]()\n\n4. **Disk Cache**: Group metadata cached for faster access\n   - Cache invalidation on group edit\n   - Implementation: [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-160]()\n\n**Sources:** [public/scripts/group-chats.js:139-163](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:35-160]()\n\n---\n\n## Code Entity Reference\n\n### Primary Classes and Functions\n\n| Entity | Location | Purpose interfaces |\n| `start:electron` | `cd ./src/electron \u0026\u0026 npm run start` | Start Electron app (if available) |\n| `start:deno` | `deno run --allow-* server.js` | Run with Deno runtime |\n| `start:bun` | `bun server.js` | Run with Bun runtime |\n| `start:no-csrf` | `node server.js --disableCsrf` | Disable CSRF protection (dev only) |\n| `lint` | `eslint \"src/**/*.js\" \"public/**/*.js\" ./*.js` | Check code style |\n| `lint:fix` | `eslint ... --fix` | Auto-fix style issues |\n| `plugins:update` | `node plugins update` | Update server plugins |\n| `plugins:install` | `node plugins install` | Install server plugins |\n| `postinstall` | `node post-install.js` | Setup script (runs after `npm install`) |\n\n**Postinstall Script Actions:**\n\nThe `post-install.js` script ([post-install.js:1-115]()) automatically runs after `npm install`:\n\n1. **convertConfig()**: Converts legacy `config.conf` to `config.yaml` format\n2. **createDefaultFiles()**: Copies `default/config.yaml` and `default/public/*` if missing\n3. **addMissingConfigValues()**: Updates `config.yaml` with new fields from defaults\n\n### Server Initialization Flow\n\n**Startup Sequence:**\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant CLI as Command Line\n    participant ServerJS as server.js\n    participant CLIParser as CommandLineParser\n    participant ServerMain as src/server-main.js\n    participant ConfigUtil as util.js\n    participant Express as Express App\n    \n    CLI-\u003e\u003eServerJS: node server.js [args]\n    ServerJS-\u003e\u003eServerJS: console.log Node version\n    ServerJS-\u003e\u003eCLIParser: new CommandLineParser()\n    ServerJS-\u003e\u003eCLIParser: parse(process.argv)\n    Note over CLIParser: Parses --dataRoot,\u003cbr/\u003e--port, --global, etc.\n    CLIParser--\u003e\u003eServerJS: cliArgs object\n    \n    ServerJS-\u003e\u003eServerJS: globalThis.DATA_ROOT = cliArgs.dataRoot\n    ServerJS-\u003e\u003eServerJS: globalThis.COMMAND_LINE_ARGS = cliArgs\n    ServerJS-\u003e\u003eServerJS: process.chdir(serverDirectory)\n    \n    ServerJS-\u003e\u003eServerMain: import('./src/server-main.js')\n    ServerMain-\u003e\u003eConfigUtil: getConfig()\n    ConfigUtil-\u003e\u003eConfigUtil: Parse config.yaml\n    ConfigUtil--\u003e\u003eServerMain: config object\n    \n    ServerMain-\u003e\u003eExpress: express()\n    ServerMain-\u003e\u003eExpress: Register middleware stack\n    Note over Express: Whitelist → BasicAuth\u003cbr/\u003e→ CSRF → User Session\n    ServerMain-\u003e\u003eExpress: Register endpoint routers\n    Note over Express: /api/characters/*\u003cbr/\u003e/api/chats/*\u003cbr/\u003e/api/settings/*\n    \n    ServerMain-\u003e\u003eExpress: app.listen(port)\n    Express--\u003e\u003eServerMain: Server started\n    ServerMain-\u003e\u003eServerMain: Log server URL\n    ServerMain--\u003e\u003eServerJS: Server ready\n```\n\n**Key Global Variables Set at Startup:**\n\n| Global Variable | Value | Set By | Purpose |\n|-----------------|-------|--------|---------|\n| `globalThis.DATA_ROOT` | CLI arg or `./data` | [server.js:9]() | Base data directory path |\n| `globalThis.COMMAND_LINE_ARGS` | Parsed CLI args object | [server.js:10]() | CLI flags (port, listen, etc.) |\n\n**CommandLineParser Supported Arguments:**\n\n```javascript\n// From src/command-line.js\n--dataRoot \u003cpath\u003e        // Override data directory\n--port \u003cnumber\u003e          // Override server port\n--global                 // Listen on all network interfaces\n--disableCsrf            // Disable CSRF protection (dev only)\n--autorun                // Auto-launch browser\n--config \u003cpath\u003e          // Specify config.yaml location\n```\n\n**Config Loading Priority:**\n1. Environment variables: `SILLYTAVERN_*` (highest priority)\n2. `config.yaml` (specified by `--config` or default)\n3. `default/config.yaml` (fallback)\n\nSources: [server.js:1-18](), [src/command-line.js](), [src/util.js:44-79](), [src/server-main.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Debugging\n\n### Debug Mode\n\n**Start with Debugger:**\n```bash\nnpm run debug\n```\nThis starts Node.js with the `--inspect` flag, allowing Chrome DevTools or VS Code to attach.\n\n**VS Code Launch Configuration:**\n```json\n{\n  \"version\": \"0.2.0\",\n  \"configurations\": [\n    {\n      \"type\": \"node\",\n      \"request\": \"launch\",\n      \"name\": \"Launch SillyTavern\",\n      \"runtimeExecutable\": \"npm\",\n      \"runtimeArgs\": [\"run\", \"debug\"],\n      \"skipFiles\": [\"\u003cnode_internals\u003e/**\"]\n    }\n  ]\n}\n```\n\n### Logging System\n\n**Log Levels:**\n```javascript\n// Defined in src/constants.js\nconst LOG_LEVELS = {\n    DEBUG: 0,\n    INFO: 1,\n    WARN: 2,\n    ERROR: 3\n};\n\n// Configure in config.yaml\nlogging:\n  minLogLevel: 0  // 0=DEBUG, 1=INFO, 2=WARN, 3=ERROR\n```\n\n**Colored Console Output:**\nSillyTavern uses `chalk` for colored console output:\n```javascript\nimport { color } from './util.js';\n\nconsole.log(color.green('Success message'));\nconsole.warn(color.yellow('Warning message'));\nconsole.error(color.red('Error message'));\n```\n\n### Common Debugging Scenarios\n\n**1. Character Loading Issues:**\nCheck the character cache: [src/endpoints/characters.js:29-36]()\n```javascript\n// Cache configuration\nconst memoryCacheCapacity = getConfigValue('performance.memoryCacheCapacity', '100mb');\nconst useDiskCache = getConfigValue('performance.useDiskCache', true, 'boolean');\n```\n\n**2. Chat Save Failures:**\nCheck integrity validation: [src/endpoints/chats.js:25-28]()\n```javascript\nconst checkIntegrity = getConfigValue('backups.chat.checkIntegrity', true, 'boolean');\n// Set to false to bypass integrity checks during development\n```\n\n**3. Configuration Issues:**\n```javascript\n// Debug config loading\nconst config = getConfig();\nconsole.log('Current config:', config);\n```\n\nSources: [package.json:117](), [src/util.js:512](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:29-36](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:25-28]()\n\n---\n\n## File System Architecture\n\n### User Data Organization\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"DATA_ROOT (Configurable)\"\n        UserHandle[\"User Handle Directory\u003cbr/\u003e(e.g., 'default')\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"User Directory Structure\"\n        Characters[\"characters/\u003cbr/\u003eCharacter PNG files\"]\n        Chats[\"chats/\u003cbr/\u003echaracter-name/\u003cbr/\u003echat-file.jsonl\"]\n        Groups[\"groups/\u003cbr/\u003eGroup JSON files\"]\n        GroupChats[\"group chats/\u003cbr/\u003eGroup JSONL files\"]\n        Worlds[\"worlds/\u003cbr/\u003eWorld Info JSON\"]\n        Backgrounds[\"backgrounds/\u003cbr/\u003eBackground images\"]\n        Avatars[\"User Avatars/\u003cbr/\u003eUser avatar images\"]\n        Themes[\"themes/\u003cbr/\u003eCSS theme files\"]\n        Settings[\"settings.json\u003cbr/\u003eUser preferences\"]\n        Secrets[\"secrets.json\u003cbr/\u003eAPI keys (encrypted)\"]\n        Backups[\"backups/\u003cbr/\u003eTimestamped backups\"]\n        Cache[\"_cache/\u003cbr/\u003echaracters/\u003cbr/\u003eDisk cache\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"File Formats\"\n        CharPNG[\"Character.png\u003cbr/\u003ePNG with embedded JSON\u003cbr/\u003e(tEXt chunks)\"]\n        ChatJSONL[\"chat.jsonl\u003cbr/\u003eLine-delimited JSON\u003cbr/\u003eHeader + Messages\"]\n        GroupJSON[\"group.json\u003cbr/\u003eGroup metadata\"]\n    end\n    \n    UserHandle --\u003e Characters\n    UserHandle --\u003e Chats\n    UserHandle --\u003e Groups\n    UserHandle --\u003e GroupChats\n    UserHandle --\u003e Worlds\n    UserHandle --\u003e Backgrounds\n    UserHandle --\u003e Avatars\n    UserHandle --\u003e Themes\n    UserHandle --\u003e Settings\n    UserHandle --\u003e Secrets\n    UserHandle --\u003e Backups\n    UserHandle --\u003e Cache\n    \n    Characters --\u003e CharPNG\n    Chats --\u003e ChatJSONL\n    Groups --\u003e GroupJSON\n```\n\n### Directory Constants and Management\n\n**User Directory Template:** [src/constants.js:USER_DIRECTORY_TEMPLATE]()\n\nThe `getUserDirectories()` function returns a cached `UserDirectoryList` object:\n\n| Property | Example Path | Purpose |\n|----------|--------------|---------|\n| `root` | `data/default` | User's root directory |\n| `characters` | `data/default/characters` | Character card storage |\n| `chats` | `data/default/chats` | Chat logs by character |\n| `groups` | `data/default/groups` | Group metadata |\n| `groupChats` | `data/default/group chats` | Group chat logs |\n| `worlds` | `data/default/worlds` | World Info files |\n| `backups` | `data/default/backups` | Backup files |\n| `thumbnails` | `data/default/thumbnails` | Image thumbnails |\n\n**Directory Cache:**\nUser directories are cached in memory to avoid repeated path resolution:\n```javascript\n// src/users.js\nconst DIRECTORIES_CACHE = new Map();\n\nfunction getUserDirectories(handle) {\n    if (DIRECTORIES_CACHE.has(handle)) {\n        return DIRECTORIES_CACHE.get(handle);\n    }\n    // Build directory structure...\n    DIRECTORIES_CACHE.set(handle, directories);\n    return directories;\n}\n```\n\nSources: [src/users.js:32-126](), [src/constants.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Data Integrity and Atomic Operations\n\n### Atomic File Writes with write-file-atomic\n\n**Atomic Write Process:**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    DataBuffer[\"Data Buffer\u003cbr/\u003e(JSON, JSONL, PNG)\"]\n    WriteAtomic[\"writeFileAtomicSync()\"]\n    TempFile[\"Temporary File\u003cbr/\u003efilename.tmp\"]\n    FSRename[\"fs.rename()\u003cbr/\u003ePOSIX atomic operation\"]\n    TargetFile[\"Target File\u003cbr/\u003efilename.json\"]\n    \n    DataBuffer --\u003e WriteAtomic\n    WriteAtomic --\u003e TempFile\n    TempFile --\u003e FSRename\n    FSRename --\u003e TargetFile\n    \n    Note[\"Ensures no partial writes\u003cbr/\u003eon crash or power loss\"]\n```\n\n**Code Usage Pattern:**\n\n```javascript\nimport { sync as writeFileAtomicSync } from 'write-file-atomic';\n\n// All critical saves use this pattern:\nwriteFileAtomicSync(filePath, data);\n```\n\n**Critical Save Locations:**\n\n| Operation | File | Function | Line |\n|-----------|------|----------|------|\n| Character save | `src/endpoints/characters.js` | `writeCharacterData()` | [259]() |\n| Chat save | `src/endpoints/chats.js` | `trySaveChat()` | [465]() |\n| Settings save | `src/endpoints/settings.js` | Preset save handler | [56]() |\n| Group save | `src/endpoints/groups.js` | Various handlers | Uses `writeFileAtomicSync` |\n\n**Why Atomic Writes Matter:**\n- Prevents data loss on server crash during save\n- Ensures files are either fully written or unchanged\n- Protects against disk-full errors mid-write\n\n### Chat Integrity System\n\n**Integrity Validation Flow:**\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client as Client Request\n    participant Handler as /api/chats/save\n    participant Integrity as checkChatIntegrity()\n    participant File as Chat JSONL File\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eHandler: POST /api/chats/save\u003cbr/\u003e{chat: [...], integrity}\n    Handler-\u003e\u003eHandler: Extract chat[0].chat_metadata.integrity\n    \n    alt checkIntegrity enabled\n        Handler-\u003e\u003eIntegrity: checkChatIntegrity(filePath, slug)\n        Integrity-\u003e\u003eFile: readFirstLine(filePath)\n        File--\u003e\u003eIntegrity: First line JSON\n        Integrity-\u003e\u003eIntegrity: Parse JSON, extract integrity\n        Integrity-\u003e\u003eIntegrity: Compare with request slug\n        \n        alt Integrity mismatch\n            Integrity--\u003e\u003eHandler: false\n            Handler-\u003e\u003eHandler: throw IntegrityMismatchError\n            Handler--\u003e\u003eClient: 400 {error: 'integrity'}\n        else Integrity matches\n            Integrity--\u003e\u003eHandler: true\n            Handler-\u003e\u003eHandler: Proceed with save\n        end\n    else checkIntegrity disabled\n        Handler-\u003e\u003eHandler: Skip validation\n    end\n    \n    Handler-\u003e\u003eFile: writeFileAtomicSync(filePath, data)\n    Handler--\u003e\u003eClient: 200 {ok: true}\n```\n\n**Configuration:**\n\n```yaml\n# config.yaml\nbackups:\n  chat:\n    checkIntegrity: true  # Enable/disable integrity checks\n```\n\n**Key Functions:**\n\n| Function | Location | Purpose |\n|----------|----------|---------|\n| `checkChatIntegrity()` | [src/endpoints/chats.js:315-334]() | Validates integrity slug matches |\n| `trySaveChat()` | [src/endpoints/chats.js:456-467]() | Performs integrity check before save |\n| `readFirstLine()` | [src/util.js]() | Reads first line of JSONL file |\n\n**Integrity Slug Format:**\n- Generated when chat is loaded\n- Stored in `chat[0].chat_metadata.integrity`\n- Prevents concurrent edits from overwriting each other\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/chats.js:315-334](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:456-467](), [default/config.yaml:167-180]()\n\n---\n\n## Security Middleware Stack\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Request[\"Incoming Request\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Security Middleware (Order Matters)\"\n        Whitelist[\"IP Whitelist\u003cbr/\u003emiddleware/whitelist.js\u003cbr/\u003eChecks IP against allowed list\"]\n        BasicAuth[\"Basic Authentication\u003cbr/\u003emiddleware/basicAuth.js\u003cbr/\u003eValidates credentials\"]\n        UserSession[\"User Session\u003cbr/\u003eExtracts user handle\"]\n        CSRF[\"CSRF Protection\u003cbr/\u003ecsrf-sync\u003cbr/\u003eDouble-submit cookies\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Endpoint Handlers\"\n        Characters[\"/api/characters/*\"]\n        Chats[\"/api/chats/*\"]\n        Settings[\"/api/settings/*\"]\n    end\n    \n    Request --\u003e Whitelist\n    Whitelist --\u003e|\"Allowed\"| BasicAuth\n    Whitelist --\u003e|\"Blocked\"| Forbidden[\"403 Forbidden\"]\n    BasicAuth --\u003e|\"Authenticated\"| UserSession\n    BasicAuth --\u003e|\"Failed\"| Unauthorized[\"401 Unauthorized\"]\n    UserSession --\u003e CSRF\n    CSRF --\u003e|\"Valid\"| Characters\n    CSRF --\u003e|\"Valid\"| Chats\n    CSRF --\u003e|\"Valid\"| Settings\n    CSRF --\u003e|\"Invalid\"| CSRFError[\"403 CSRF Error\"]\n```\n\n### Middleware Components\n\n**1. IP Whitelist:** [src/middleware/whitelist.js]()\n```javascript\n// Validates against config.yaml whitelist\nwhitelist:\n  - ::1          # IPv6 localhost\n  - 127.0.0.1    # IPv4 localhost\n  \n// Supports CIDR notation\nwhitelist:\n  - 192.168.1.0/24\n  \n// Checks X-Forwarded-For if enabled\nenableForwardedWhitelist: true\n```\n\n**2. Basic Authentication:** [src/middleware/basicAuth.js]()\n```javascript\n// Two modes:\n// 1. Global auth (single username/password)\nbasicAuthMode: true\nbasicAuthUser:\n  username: \"user\"\n  password: \"password\"\n\n// 2. Per-user auth (uses user account passwords)\nperUserBasicAuth: true\nenableUserAccounts: true\n```\n\n**3. CSRF Protection:**\nUses `csrf-sync` with double-submit cookie pattern. Can be disabled for development:\n```bash\nnpm run start:no-csrf\n```\n\nSources: [src/middleware/whitelist.js](), [src/middleware/basicAuth.js](), [default/config.yaml:56-110]()\n\n---\n\n## Testing Strategies\n\n### Manual Testing\n\n**API Endpoint Testing:**\nUse tools like `curl`, Postman, or Insomnia to test endpoints:\n\n```bash\n# Test character listing\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/characters/all \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\"\n\n# Test chat save\ncurl -X POST http://localhost:8000/api/chats/save \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  -d '{\"avatar_url\":\"character.png\",\"file_name\":\"chat\",\"chat\":[...]}'\n```\n\n**Frontend Testing:**\n1. Open browser DevTools (F12)\n2. Use Console for SillyTavern API:\n```javascript\n// Access context\nconst context = SillyTavern.getContext();\n\n// Test character loading\nawait getCharacters();\n\n// Test chat operations\nawait saveChatConditional();\n```\n\n### Integration Testing Checklist\n\nWhen making changes, verify:\n\n- [ ] Character import/export works\n- [ ] Chat saving maintains integrity\n- [ ] Group chat functionality\n- [ ] World Info activation\n- [ ] Extension loading\n- [ ] API connections (OpenAI, etc.)\n- [ ] File uploads work correctly\n- [ ] Settings persist properly\n\n### Performance Testing\n\n**Character Loading Performance:**\n```javascript\n// Test with large character libraries\nperformance:\n  lazyLoadCharacters: true    # Enable shallow loading\n  memoryCacheCapacity: '100mb' # Limit memory usage\n  useDiskCache: true          # Enable disk caching\n```\n\n**Monitor Character Cache:**\n```javascript\n// In src/endpoints/characters.js\nconsole.log('Memory cache size:', memoryCache.size);\nconsole.log('Disk cache keys:', diskCache.hashedKeys.length);\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:29-36](), [default/config.yaml:196-204]()\n\n---\n\n## Contributing Guidelines\n\n### Code Contribution Workflow\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Dev as Developer\n    participant Fork as Forked Repo\n    participant PR as Pull Request\n    participant Main as Main Repository\n    participant CI as CI/CD\n    \n    Dev-\u003e\u003eFork: Fork repository\n    Dev-\u003e\u003eFork: Create feature branch\n    Dev-\u003e\u003eFork: Make changes\n    Dev-\u003e\u003eDev: Run 'npm run lint'\n    Dev-\u003e\u003eDev: Test changes locally\n    Dev-\u003e\u003eFork: Commit changes\n    Dev-\u003e\u003eFork: Push to feature branch\n    Fork-\u003e\u003ePR: Open pull request\n    PR-\u003e\u003eCI: Run automated checks\n    CI-\u003e\u003eCI: ESLint validation\n    CI-\u003e\u003eCI: Type checking\n    CI--\u003e\u003ePR: Results\n    PR-\u003e\u003eMain: Review by maintainers\n    Main-\u003e\u003eMain: Merge if approved\n```\n\n### Pre-Commit Checklist\n\nBefore submitting a pull request:\n\n1. **Run ESLint:**\n   ```bash\n   npm run lint:fix\n   ```\n\n2. **Verify no type errors:**\n   Check that JSDoc types are correct and VSCode shows no errors.\n\n3. **Test thoroughly:**\n   - Test affected features manually\n   - Verify no regressions\n   - Test with different configurations\n\n4. **Update documentation:**\n   - Add JSDoc comments for new functions\n   - Update README if needed\n   - Add comments for complex logic\n\n5. **Follow code style:**\n   - Use 4-space indentation\n   - Single quotes for strings\n   - Always use semicolons\n   - Use `const`/`let`, never `var`\n\n### Common Patterns to Follow\n\n**Error Handling:**\n```javascript\ntry {\n    const result = await riskyOperation();\n    return result;\n} catch (error) {\n    console.error('Operation failed:', error);\n    return response.status(500).send({ error: 'Message' });\n}\n```\n\n**Configuration Access:**\n```javascript\n// Always provide defaults\nconst setting = getConfigValue('path.to.setting', defaultValue, 'type');\n```\n\n**File Operations:**\n```javascript\n// Use atomic writes for data files\nimport { sync as writeFileAtomicSync } from 'write-file-atomic';\nwriteFileAtomicSync(filePath, data);\n\n// Use sanitize for user-provided filenames\nimport sanitize from 'sanitize-filename';\nconst safeName = sanitize(userInput);\n```\n\n**Path Construction:**\n```javascript\n// Always use path.join, never string concatenation\nconst filePath = path.join(directory, filename);\n```\n\nSources: [.eslintrc.cjs:83-105](), [src/util.js:88-109]()\n\n---\n\n## Common Development Tasks\n\n### Adding a New Configuration Option\n\n1. **Add to default/config.yaml:**\n   ```yaml\n   myFeature:\n     enabled: true\n     setting: \"value\"\n   ```\n\n2. **Access in code:**\n   ```javascript\n   const enabled = getConfigValue('myFeature.enabled', false, 'boolean');\n   ```\n\n3. **Support environment variable:**\n   ```bash\n   export SILLYTAVERN_MYFEATURE_ENABLED=true\n   ```\n\n### Adding a New Endpoint\n\nSee [Backend API Development](#11.3) for detailed guidance.\n\n**Quick Reference:**\n```javascript\n// src/endpoints/my-endpoint.js\nimport express from 'express';\nexport const router = express.Router();\n\nrouter.post('/action', async (request, response) =\u003e {\n    try {\n        const { param } = request.body;\n        const userDirs = request.user.directories;\n        \n        // Process request...\n        \n        return response.send({ result: 'success' });\n    } catch (error) {\n        console.error(error);\n        return response.status(500).send({ error: error.message });\n    }\n});\n```\n\n### Adding a New Default Content Item\n\n1. **Add file to `default/content/`**\n\n2. **Update `default/content/index.json`:**\n   ```json\n   [\n     {\n       \"filename\": \"my-preset.json\",\n       \"type\": \"openai_preset\",\n       \"name\": \"My Preset\"\n     }\n   ]\n   ```\n\n3. **Content will be automatically copied to users on startup**\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:111-163]()\n\n---\n\n## Key Utility Functions\n\n**Core Utility Modules:**\n\n### File Operations (src/util.js)\n\n| Function | Signature | string;\n    thumbnails: string;\n    // ... and more (see src/users.js)\n}\n```\n\nLocation: [src/users.js:70-103](), [src/users.js:109-126]()\n\n### Character Cache Functions (src/endpoints/characters.js)\n\n| Class/Function | Purpose | Configuration |\n|----------------|---------|---------------|\n| `DiskCache` class | Persistent character data cache | `performance.useDiskCache` |\n| `MemoryLimitedMap` | Memory-limited cache | `performance.memoryCacheCapacity` |\n| `readCharacterData()` | Read character with caching | Checks memory → disk → parse |\n| `getCacheKey()` | Generate cache key from file path | Uses file mtime |\n\nLocation: [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-209]()\n\nSources: [src/util.js:1-1500](), [src/users.js:1-800](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-209]()\n\n---\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n### Memory Management\n\n**Character Cache Configuration:**\n```yaml\n# config.yaml\nperformance:\n  # Limit memory used by character cache\n  memoryCacheCapacity: '100mb'\n  \n  # Enable disk cache for parsed characters\n  useDiskCache: true\n  \n  # Use shallow character data in listings\n  lazyLoadCharacters: false\n```\n\n**Cache Implementation:**\n```javascript\n// Memory-limited cache\nconst memoryCache = new MemoryLimitedMap(memoryCacheCapacity);\n\n// Disk cache with automatic cleanup\nconst diskCache = new DiskCache();\ndiskCache.syncQueue.add(userHandle);  // Queue for async sync\n```\n\n**On Android Devices:**\n```javascript\nconst isAndroid = process.platform === 'android';\n// Disable memory caching on Android for tighter memory management\n!isAndroid \u0026\u0026 memoryCache.set(cacheKey, result);\n```\n\n### Backup Throttling\n\nChat backups are throttled per user to prevent excessive I/O:\n\n```javascript\n// config.yaml\nbackups:\n  chat:\n    throttleInterval: 10000  # 10 seconds between backups\n    maxTotalBackups: -1      # -1 for unlimited\n\n// Implementation uses lodash throttle\nconst backupFunction = _.throttle(\n    backupChat, \n    throttleInterval, \n    { leading: true, trailing: true }\n);\n```\n\n### Settings Autosave\n\nSettings are automatically backed up every 10 minutes:\n```javascript\nconst AUTOSAVE_INTERVAL = 10 * 60 * 1000;  // 10 minutes\nconst throttledAutoSave = _.throttle(\n    () =\u003e backupUserSettings(handle, true),\n    AUTOSAVE_INTERVAL\n);\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:29-36](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:25-77](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:18-41]()\n\n---\n\nThis developer guide provides the foundation for contributing to SillyTavern. For extension development, see [Extension Development Guide](#11.2). For backend API development, see [Backend API Development](#11.3). It covers npm package configuration, dependency categories, runtime bundling with Webpack, alternative runtime support, and development tooling.\n\nFor information about the server initialization and startup process, see [Application Lifecycle and Initialization](#2.1). For extension development and dependency management, see [Extension Development Guide](#11.2).\n\n---\n\n## Overview and Package Configuration\n\nSillyTavern uses **npm** as its primary package manager with Node.js version 18 or higher as the minimum requirement. The project is configured as an ES module package (`\"type\": \"module\"`) and provides a global CLI binary at `sillytavern` that resolves to [src/server-global.js:1]().\n\n**Key Configuration Properties:**\n\n| Property | Value | Purpose |\n|----------|-------|---------|\n| `name` | `\"sillytavern\"` | Package identifier |\n| `version` | `\"1.15.0\"` | Current release version |\n| `type` | `\"module\"` | ES module resolution |\n| `main` | `\"server.js\"` | Primary entry point |\n| `bin` | `\"./src/server-global.js\"` | Global CLI command |\n| `license` | `\"AGPL-3.0\"` | Open source license |\n| `engines.node` | `\"\u003e= 18\"` | Minimum Node version |\n\nThe package configuration includes several dependency overrides to enforce specific versions for security or compatibility reasons:\n\n- `vectra` → `openai: ^4.17.0`\n- `axios` → `follow-redirects: ^1.15.4`\n- `node-fetch` → `whatwg-url: ^14.0.0`\n\nSources: [package.json:1-173](), [package-lock.json:1-144]()\n\n---\n\n## npm Scripts\n\nThe `scripts` section defines several execution modes and utilities:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Primary Execution\"\n        start[\"npm start\u003cbr/\u003enode server.js\"]\n        debug[\"npm run debug\u003cbr/\u003enode --inspect\"]\n        global[\"npm run start:global\u003cbr/\u003e--global flag\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Alternative Runtimes\"\n        electron[\"npm run start:electron\u003cbr/\u003eElectron app\"]\n        deno[\"npm run start:deno\u003cbr/\u003eDeno runtime\"]\n        bun[\"npm run start:bun\u003cbr/\u003eBun runtime\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Development Tools\"\n        lint[\"npm run lint\u003cbr/\u003eESLint check\"]\n        lintfix[\"npm run lint:fix\u003cbr/\u003eESLint auto-fix\"]\n        postinstall[\"npm install triggers\u003cbr/\u003epost-install.js\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Plugin Management\"\n        pluginupdate[\"npm run plugins:update\"]\n        plugininstall[\"npm run plugins:install\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**Script Definitions:**\n\n| Script | Command | Purpose |\n|--------|---------|---------|\n| `start` | `node server.js` | Standard server startup |\n| `debug` | `node --inspect server.js` | Start with debugger attached |\n| `start:global` | `node server.js --global` | Start with global network access |\n| `start:electron` | `cd ./src/electron \u0026\u0026 npm run start` | Launch Electron desktop app |\n| `start:deno` | `deno run --allow-* server.js` | Run with Deno runtime |\n| `start:bun` | `bun server.js` | Run with Bun runtime |\n| `start:no-csrf` | `node server.js --disableCsrf` | Start without CSRF protection |\n| `postinstall` | `node post-install.js` | Post-installation setup hook |\n| `lint` | `eslint \"src/**/*.js\" \"public/**/*.js\" ./*.js` | Check code quality |\n| `lint:fix` | Same with `--fix` flag | Fix code quality issues |\n| `plugins:update` | `node plugins update` | Update installed plugins |\n| `plugins:install` | `node plugins install` | Install plugins |\n\nSources: [package.json:117-130]()\n\n---\n\n## Dependency Categories\n\nSillyTavern's dependencies are organized into distinct categories based on their usage context.\n\n### Server-Side Dependencies\n\n**Core Web Framework:**\n- `express@^4.21.0` - HTTP server and routing\n- `body-parser@^1.20.2` - Request body parsing\n- `compression@^1.8.1` - Response compression\n- `cors@^2.8.5` - Cross-Origin Resource Sharing\n- `multer@^2.0.2` - Multipart form data handling\n\n**Security:**\n- `helmet@^8.1.0` - HTTP header security\n- `csrf-sync@^4.2.1` - CSRF token management\n- `cookie-session@^2.1.1` - Session management\n- `ip-matching@^2.1.2` - IP address filtering\n- `rate-limiter-flexible@^5.0.5` - Rate limiting\n\n**AI Integration:**\n- `sillytavern-transformers@2.14.6` - Local transformer models\n- `tiktoken@^1.0.22` - OpenAI tokenizer\n- `@agnai/sentencepiece-js@^1.1.1` - SentencePiece tokenization\n- `@agnai/web-tokenizers@^0.1.3` - Web-based tokenizers\n- `vectra@^0.2.2` - Vector storage for RAG\n\n**Image Processing:**\n- `@jimp/core@^1.6.0` - Core image manipulation\n- `@jimp/wasm-*` - WASM-based format handlers (AVIF, JPEG, PNG, WebP)\n- `@jimp/plugin-*` - Image transformation plugins (blit, circle, color, crop, etc.)\n- `archiver@^7.0.1` - Archive creation\n\n**Data Management:**\n- `node-persist@^4.0.4` - Simple filesystem storage\n- `write-file-atomic@^5.0.1` - Atomic file writes\n- `png-chunk-text@^1.0.0`, `png-chunks-extract@^1.0.0` - PNG metadata handling\n- `yaml@^2.8.1` - YAML parsing and serialization\n\n**Utilities:**\n- `lodash@^4.17.21` - Utility functions\n- `moment@^2.30.1` - Date/time handling\n- `yargs@^17.7.1` - CLI argument parsing\n- `chalk@^5.6.0` - Terminal color output\n- `simple-git@^3.28.0` - Git operations\n\n**Translation:**\n- `bing-translate-api@^4.1.0` - Bing translation API\n- `google-translate-api-x@^10.7.2` - Google translation API\n\nSources: [package.json:2-94]()\n\n### Client-Side Dependencies\n\nClient-side libraries are bundled via Webpack and exposed through [public/lib.js:1-131]():\n\n**Core Libraries:**\n- `lodash` - Functional programming utilities\n- `fuse.js@^7.1.0` - Fuzzy search\n- `localforage@^1.10.0` - IndexedDB/localStorage abstraction\n- `dompurify@^3.2.6` - HTML sanitization\n- `highlight.js@^11.11.1` - Syntax highlighting\n\n**UI and DOM:**\n- `@popperjs/core@^2.11.8` - Tooltip/popover positioning\n- `morphdom@^2.7.7` - Efficient DOM diffing\n- `slidetoggle@^4.0.0` - Smooth slide animations\n- `@iconfu/svg-inject@^1.2.3` - SVG injection\n\n**Text Processing:**\n- `showdown@^2.1.0` - Markdown parsing\n- `handlebars@^4.7.8` - Template engine\n- `@mozilla/readability@^0.6.0` - Article extraction\n- `diff-match-patch@^1.0.5` - Text diffing\n\n**Parsing:**\n- `chevrotain@^11.0.3` - Parser combinator library (used for slash commands)\n- `@adobe/css-tools@^4.4.4` - CSS parsing\n\n**Other:**\n- `bowser@^2.12.1` - Browser detection\n- `moment@^2.30.1` - Date manipulation\n- `seedrandom@^3.0.5` - Seeded random number generation\n- `droll@^0.2.1` - Dice rolling\n- `yaml@^2.8.1` - YAML parsing\n\nSources: [public/lib.js:1-131](), [package.json:2-94]()\n\n### Development Dependencies\n\nType definitions and tooling for development:\n\n**TypeScript Type Definitions:**\n- `@types/node@^18.19.84` - Node.js types\n- `@types/express@^4.17.23` - Express types\n- `@types/jquery@^3.5.33`, `@types/jqueryui@^1.12.24` - jQuery types\n- `@types/lodash@^4.17.20` - Lodash types\n- `@chevrotain/types@^11.0.3` - Chevrotain parser types\n\n**Code Quality:**\n- `eslint@^8.57.1` - JavaScript linter\n- `eslint-plugin-jsdoc@^48.10.0` - JSDoc validation\n- `eslint-plugin-jest@^27.9.0` - Jest test linting\n- `eslint-plugin-playwright@^2.3.0` - Playwright test linting\n\nSources: [package.json:139-171]()\n\n---\n\n## Client Library Bundling\n\nClient-side dependencies are bundled using Webpack and exposed through a centralized module system.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Client Bundle System\"\n        lib[\"public/lib.js\u003cbr/\u003eLibrary Exports\"]\n        webpack[\"webpack@^5.98.0\u003cbr/\u003eModule Bundler\"]\n        \n        subgraph \"Exposed Libraries\"\n            lodash[\"lodash\"]\n            fuse[\"Fuse.js\"]\n            dompurify[\"DOMPurify\"]\n            hljs[\"highlight.js\"]\n            localforage[\"localforage\"]\n            handlebars[\"Handlebars\"]\n            css[\"@adobe/css-tools\"]\n            bowser[\"Bowser\"]\n            diffmatch[\"DiffMatchPatch\"]\n            readability[\"@mozilla/readability\"]\n            svginject[\"SVGInject\"]\n            showdown[\"showdown\"]\n            moment[\"moment\"]\n            popper[\"@popperjs/core\"]\n            chevrotain[\"chevrotain\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Window Shims\"\n            shimfunc[\"initLibraryShims()\"]\n            windowobj[\"window.Fuse\u003cbr/\u003ewindow.DOMPurify\u003cbr/\u003ewindow.hljs\u003cbr/\u003eetc.\"]\n        end\n    end\n    \n    webpack --\u003e lib\n    lib --\u003e lodash\n    lib --\u003e fuse\n    lib --\u003e dompurify\n    lib --\u003e hljs\n    lib --\u003e localforage\n    lib --\u003e handlebars\n    lib --\u003e css\n    lib --\u003e bowser\n    lib --\u003e diffmatch\n    lib --\u003e readability\n    lib --\u003e svginject\n    lib --\u003e showdown\n    lib --\u003e moment\n    lib --\u003e popper\n    lib --\u003e chevrotain\n    \n    shimfunc --\u003e windowobj\n    lib --\u003e shimfunc\n```\n\n**Library Export Strategy:**\n\nThe [public/lib.js:82-105]() module provides three export formats:\n\n1. **Default export** - Object containing all libraries\n2. **Named exports** - Individual library exports for ES module imports\n3. **Window shims** - Backward compatibility with extensions expecting global variables\n\nThe `initLibraryShims()` function [public/lib.js:32-80]() populates the `window` object with library references for compatibility with older extensions that expect global variables like `window.Fuse` or `window.DOMPurify`.\n\n**Usage Example:**\n\n```javascript\n// Modern ES module import\nimport { lodash, DOMPurify } from './lib.js';\n\n// Default import\nimport libs from './lib.js';\nconst { Fuse } = libs;\n\n// Legacy global access (after initLibraryShims)\nwindow.Fuse // Available globally\n```\n\nSources: [public/lib.js:1-131](), [package.json:88]()\n\n---\n\n## TypeScript Configuration and Type Checking\n\nSillyTavern uses TypeScript for type checking JavaScript code without requiring a compilation step. This is configured through `jsconfig.json` for the client-side code and type definition files.\n\n### JavaScript Configuration\n\n[public/jsconfig.json:1-30]() configures the TypeScript language server:\n\n```json\n{\n    \"compilerOptions\": {\n        \"checkJs\": true,\n        \"target\": \"ESNext\",\n        \"module\": \"ESNext\",\n        \"moduleResolution\": \"node\",\n        \"allowUmdGlobalAccess\": true,\n        \"allowSyntheticDefaultImports\": true,\n        \"strictBindCallApply\": true\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Key Settings:**\n\n| Option | Value | Effect |\n|--------|-------|--------|\n| `checkJs` | `true` | Enable type checking for `.js` files |\n| `target` | `ESNext` | Use latest JavaScript features |\n| `module` | `ESNext` | ES module system |\n| `moduleResolution` | `node` | Node.js-style module resolution |\n| `allowUmdGlobalAccess` | `true` | Allow accessing UMD globals |\n| `allowSyntheticDefaultImports` | `true` | Permit default imports from modules without default export |\n| `strictBindCallApply` | `true` | Strict type checking for `.bind()`, `.call()`, `.apply()` |\n\n**Exclusions:**\n- `node_modules/`\n- `dist/`\n- `.git/`\n- `lib/`\n- `*.min.js`\n- `scripts/extensions/quick-reply/lib/`\n- `scripts/extensions/tts/lib/`\n\nSources: [public/jsconfig.json:1-30]()\n\n### Global Type Definitions\n\n[public/global.d.ts:1-237]() provides TypeScript type definitions for global types and interfaces used throughout the application:\n\n**Core Data Types:**\n- `Character` - Character data structure\n- `Group` - Group chat configuration\n- `ChatFile`, `ChatHeader`, `ChatMessage` - Chat storage format\n- `ChatMetadata` - Chat-level metadata\n- `BaseMessageExtra`, `ChatMessageExtra` - Message extra data\n- `MediaAttachment` - Media file attachments\n- `SwipeInfo` - Alternative message responses\n\n**Configuration Types:**\n- `InstructSettings` - Instruct mode configuration\n- `ContextSettings` - Context management settings\n- `ChatCompletionSettings` - Chat completion API settings\n- `TextCompletionSettings` - Text completion API settings\n- `WorldInfoSettings` - World info/lorebook settings\n\n**Global Interfaces:**\n- `SillyTavern` namespace - Core application context\n- `window.ai` - Browser AI API\n- jQuery plugin extensions - Type definitions for jQuery plugins\n- `translate()` function - Translation API\n- `convertVideoToAnimatedWebp()` - Video conversion API\n\nSources: [public/global.d.ts:1-237]()\n\n---\n\n## Code Quality and Linting\n\nESLint is configured to enforce code quality standards across both server and client code.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"ESLint Configuration\"\n        root[\".eslintrc.cjs\u003cbr/\u003eRoot Configuration\"]\n        \n        subgraph \"Server Files\"\n            serverfiles[\"src/**/*.js\u003cbr/\u003e*.js\u003cbr/\u003eplugins/**/*.js\"]\n            serverenv[\"env: node\u003cbr/\u003esourceType: module\"]\n            serverglobals[\"globalThis, Deno\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Client Files\"\n            clientfiles[\"public/**/*.js\"]\n            clientenv[\"env: browser, jquery\u003cbr/\u003esourceType: module\"]\n            clientglobals[\"ePub, pdfjsLib,\u003cbr/\u003etoastr, SillyTavern\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Plugins\"\n            jsdoc[\"eslint-plugin-jsdoc\u003cbr/\u003eJSDoc validation\"]\n            jest[\"eslint-plugin-jest\u003cbr/\u003eTest linting\"]\n            playwright[\"eslint-plugin-playwright\u003cbr/\u003eE2E test linting\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Rules\"\n            quotes[\"'quotes': 'single'\"]\n            semi[\"'semi': 'always'\"]\n            indent[\"'indent': 4 spaces\"]\n            trailing[\"'no-trailing-spaces'\"]\n            spacing[\"'object-curly-spacing'\"]\n        end\n    end\n    \n    root --\u003e serverfiles\n    root --\u003e clientfiles\n    serverfiles --\u003e serverenv\n    serverfiles --\u003e serverglobals\n    clientfiles --\u003e clientenv\n    clientfiles --\u003e clientglobals\n    \n    root --\u003e jsdoc\n    root --\u003e jest\n    root --\u003e playwright\n    \n    root --\u003e quotes\n    root --\u003e semi\n    root --\u003e indent\n    root --\u003e trailing\n    root --\u003e spacing\n```\n\n### ESLint Configuration Structure\n\n**File-Specific Overrides:**\n\n1. **Server-side files** [.eslintrc.cjs:17-29]() - `src/**/*.js`, `*.js`, `plugins/**/*.js`\n   - Environment: `node: true`\n   - Source type: `module`\n   - Globals: `globalThis`, `Deno`\n\n2. **CommonJS files** [.eslintrc.cjs:31-38]() - `*.cjs`\n   - Source type: `commonjs`\n   - Environment: `node: true`\n\n3. **Client-side files** [.eslintrc.cjs:49-66]() - `public/**/*.js`\n   - Environment: `browser: true`, `jquery: true`\n   - Source type: `module`\n   - Globals: `ePub`, `pdfjsLib`, `toastr`, `SillyTavern`\n\n**Ignored Paths:**\n- `**/node_modules/**`\n- `**/dist/**`, `**/.git/**`\n- `public/lib/**` (bundled libraries)\n- `backups/**`, `data/**`, `cache/**`\n- `src/tokenizers/**` (pre-compiled tokenizers)\n- `plugins/**` (third-party plugins)\n- `**/*.min.js` (minified files)\n- Extension-specific libraries\n\n### Code Style Rules\n\n| Rule | Configuration | Purpose |\n|------|---------------|---------|\n| `quotes` | `'single'` | Enforce single quotes |\n| `semi` | `'always'` | Require semicolons |\n| `indent` | `4 spaces` | Consistent indentation |\n| `comma-dangle` | `'always-multiline'` | Trailing commas in multiline |\n| `eol-last` | `'always'` | Newline at end of file |\n| `no-trailing-spaces` | `'error'` | No trailing whitespace |\n| `object-curly-spacing` | `'always'` | Space inside braces |\n| `space-infix-ops` | `'error'` | Space around operators |\n| `no-unused-vars` | `{ args: 'none' }` | Check unused variables (except arguments) |\n\n**JSDoc Validation:**\n- Plugin: `eslint-plugin-jsdoc`\n- Rule: `jsdoc/no-undefined-types` - Warn on undefined types in JSDoc comments\n\nSources: [.eslintrc.cjs:1-107]()\n\n---\n\n## Runtime and Alternative Execution Environments\n\nSillyTavern supports multiple JavaScript runtimes beyond Node.js:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Runtime Support\"\n        entry[\"Entry Point\u003cbr/\u003eserver.js\"]\n        \n        subgraph \"Node.js\"\n            node[\"node server.js\u003cbr/\u003ePrimary runtime\"]\n            nodeinspect[\"node --inspect server.js\u003cbr/\u003eDebug mode\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Deno\"\n            deno[\"deno run server.js\u003cbr/\u003e--allow-run\u003cbr/\u003e--allow-net\u003cbr/\u003e--allow-read\u003cbr/\u003e--allow-write\u003cbr/\u003e--allow-sys\u003cbr/\u003e--allow-env\"]\n            denoglobal[\"Deno global available\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Bun\"\n            bun[\"bun server.js\u003cbr/\u003eFast JS runtime\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Electron\"\n            electron[\"src/electron/\u003cbr/\u003eDesktop application\"]\n            electronnpm[\"Separate npm workspace\"]\n        end\n        \n        subgraph \"Global CLI\"\n            globalbin[\"sillytavern command\u003cbr/\u003esrc/server-global.js\"]\n        end\n    end\n    \n    entry --\u003e node\n    entry --\u003e nodeinspect\n    entry --\u003e deno\n    entry --\u003e bun\n    entry --\u003e electron\n    \n    globalbin --\u003e entry\n```\n\n### Deno Support\n\nThe `start:deno` script [package.json:122]() runs SillyTavern using Deno with comprehensive permissions:\n\n```bash\ndeno run --allow-run --allow-net --allow-read --allow-write --allow-sys --allow-env server.js\n```\n\n**Required Permissions:**\n- `--allow-run` - Execute subprocesses\n- `--allow-net` - Network access\n- `--allow-read` - File system reads\n- `--allow-write` - File system writes\n- `--allow-sys` - System information\n- `--allow-env` - Environment variables\n\nThe ESLint configuration [.eslintrc.cjs:26-28]() includes `Deno` as a global to prevent undefined variable warnings.\n\n### Bun Support\n\nBun runtime support via `start:bun` script [package.json:123]():\n\n```bash\nbun server.js\n```\n\nBun provides faster startup times and improved performance while maintaining Node.js API compatibility.\n\n### Electron Desktop Application\n\nThe Electron version is maintained in a separate workspace at `src/electron/` with its own `package.json` and npm scripts. Launch via:\n\n```bash\nnpm run start:electron\n```\n\nThis changes directory to `src/electron/` and runs its local npm start script.\n\n### Global CLI Binary\n\nWhen installed globally (`npm install -g`), the `sillytavern` command becomes available, pointing to [src/server-global.js:1]() as defined in the `bin` field [package.json:131-133]().\n\nSources: [package.json:117-133](), [.eslintrc.cjs:17-29]()\n\n---\n\n## Dependency Lock File\n\nThe [package-lock.json:1-144]() uses lockfile version 3, indicating npm 7 or higher. It includes:\n\n**Lock File Metadata:**\n- `lockfileVersion: 3` - npm 7+ format\n- `requires: true` - Honor dependency requirements\n- `hasInstallScript: true` - Post-install script present\n\n**Package Resolution:**\nEach dependency includes:\n- Resolved npm registry URL\n- Integrity hash (SHA-512)\n- License information\n- Peer dependency relationships\n- Optional dependencies\n\n**Example Entry:**\n```json\n\"@agnai/sentencepiece-js\": {\n    \"version\": \"1.1.1\",\n    \"resolved\": \"https://registry.npmjs.org/@agnai/sentencepiece-js/-/sentencepiece-js-1.1.1.tgz\",\n    \"integrity\": \"sha512-h2+XPrJVLuVLl...\",\n    \"license\": \"Apache-2.0\",\n    \"dependencies\": {\n        \"app-root-path\": \"^3.1.0\"\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThe lock file ensures deterministic installs across environments by pinning exact versions and integrity hashes for all transitive dependencies.\n\nSources: [package-lock.json:1-144]()\n\n---\n\n## Post-Install Hook\n\nThe `postinstall` script [package.json:125]() runs automatically after `npm install`:\n\n```bash\nnode post-install.js\n```\n\nThis script (not shown in provided files) likely performs:\n- Initial setup tasks\n- Directory structure creation\n- Default configuration file generation\n- Dependency validation\n- Optional asset downloads\n\nThe presence of `hasInstallScript: true` in [package-lock.json:10]() confirms this hook is registered with npm.\n\nSources: [package.json:125](), [package-lock.json:10]()\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\nSillyTavern's package management and build system is characterized by:\n\n1. **Modern JavaScript** - ES modules, Node 18+, latest ECMAScript features\n2. **Type Safety** - TypeScript type checking without compilation via jsconfig and .d.ts files\n3. **Multi-Runtime** - Support for Node.js, Deno, Bun, and Electron\n4. **Client Bundling** - Webpack-based bundling with centralized library exports\n5. **Code Quality** - ESLint with JSDoc validation and environment-specific rules\n6. **Security** - Dependency overrides, integrity hashes, and careful permission scoping\n7. **Extensibility** - Plugin system with dedicated management scripts\n\nThe system balances modern development practices with backward compatibility, enabling both cutting-edge features and support for existing extensions.\n\nSources: [package.json:1-173](), [package-lock.json:1-144](), [public/lib.js:1-131](), [public/jsconfig.json:1-30](), [public/global.d.ts:1-237](), [.eslintrc.cjs:1-107]()"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"47:Tcbd2,"])</script><script>self.__next_f.push([1,"# Extension Development
