[src/endpoints/sprites.js](src/endpoints/sprites.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis document provides a comprehensive guide for developing extensions for SillyTavern. It covers the extension architecture, available APIs, integration points, and best practices for creating custom functionality that extends the core application.\n\n**Scope**: This guide focuses on client-side extension development. For information about adding server-side API endpoints, see [Backend API Development](#10.3). For information about the core extension framework architecture, see [Extension Architecture and APIs](#8.1).\n\n---\n\n## Extension System Overview\n\nExtensions in SillyTavern are client-side JavaScript modules that can hook into the application lifecycle, inject prompts, register commands, and add UI elements. Extensions are loaded from the `public/scripts/extensions/` directory and initialized during application startup.\n\n**Core Components**:\n- **Extension Manager**: Handles extension loading and lifecycle ([public/scripts/extensions.js]())\n- **Context API**: Provides access to core functionality via `getContext()`\n- **Event System**: Enables reactive behavior through `eventSource`\n- **Prompt Injection**: Allows extensions to add content to AI prompts\n- **Slash Commands**: Enables custom command registration\n- **Settings System**: Manages extension-specific configuration\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions.js](), [public/script.js:1-330](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:1-50](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:1-40]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension File Structure\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    ExtDir[\"public/scripts/extensions/\"]\n    \n    ExtDir --\u003e ExtFolder[\"extension-name/\"]\n    ExtFolder --\u003e IndexJS[\"index.js\u003cbr/\u003e(Main entry point)\"]\n    ExtFolder --\u003e SettingsHTML[\"settings.html\u003cbr/\u003e(UI template)\"]\n    ExtFolder --\u003e StyleCSS[\"style.css\u003cbr/\u003e(Styles)\"]\n    ExtFolder --\u003e Templates[\"templates/\u003cbr/\u003e(Handlebars templates)\"]\n    \n    IndexJS --\u003e ModuleName[\"MODULE_NAME constant\"]\n    IndexJS --\u003e InitFunc[\"Initialization code\"]\n    IndexJS --\u003e EventHandlers[\"Event handlers\"]\n    IndexJS --\u003e SlashCmds[\"Slash command registration\"]\n    \n    SettingsHTML --\u003e InlineDrawer[\"inline-drawer structure\"]\n    SettingsHTML --\u003e Controls[\"Form controls\"]\n    \n    Templates --\u003e TemplateFiles[\"*.html files\"]\n```\n\n**Standard Files**:\n| File | Purpose | Required |\n|------|---------|----------|\n| `index.js` | Main extension code, exported as ES module | Yes |\n| `settings.html` | Settings UI (loaded into Extensions panel) | No |\n| `style.css` | Extension-specific styles | No |\n| `templates/*.html` | Handlebars templates for dynamic UI | No |\n\n**Directory Structure Example**:\n```\npublic/scripts/extensions/\n└── my-extension/\n    ├── index.js\n    ├── settings.html\n    ├── style.css\n    └── templates/\n        └── popup.html\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/settings.html](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/settings.html]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension Entry Point and Initialization\n\n### Basic Extension Structure\n\nEvery extension must export a `MODULE_NAME` constant and initialize during the application lifecycle:\n\n```javascript\n// index.js\nexport const MODULE_NAME = 'my-extension';\n\nimport { getContext, extension_settings, saveSettingsDebounced } from '../../extensions.js';\nimport { eventSource, event_types } from '../../../script.js';\n\n// Extension-specific settings\nconst defaultSettings = {\n    enabled: false,\n    apiKey: '',\n};\n\n// Load extension settings\nfunction loadSettings() {\n    // Initialize settings if not present\n    if (!extension_settings[MODULE_NAME]) {\n        extension_settings[MODULE_NAME] = defaultSettings;\n    }\n    \n    // Sync UI with settings\n    $('#my_extension_enabled').prop('checked', extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].enabled);\n}\n\n// Initialize extension\nasync function init() {\n    loadSettings();\n    \n    // Register event handlers\n    eventSource.on(event_types.APP_READY, onAppReady);\n    eventSource.on(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, onMessageReceived);\n    \n    console.log('My Extension initialized');\n}\n\n// Extension lifecycle hooks\nasync function onAppReady() {\n    console.log('App is ready');\n}\n\nasync function onMessageReceived(messageIndex) {\n    console.log('Message received:', messageIndex);\n}\n\n// Auto-initialize when module is imported\ninit();\n```\n\n### Extension Loading Sequence\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Boot as Application Bootstrap\n    participant ExtMgr as Extension Manager\n    participant Ext as Extension Module\n    participant Events as Event System\n    \n    Boot-\u003e\u003eExtMgr: initExtensions()\n    ExtMgr-\u003e\u003eExt: Import module\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: Execute top-level code\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eExt: init()\n    Ext-\u003e\u003eEvents: Register event handlers\n    \n    Boot-\u003e\u003eEvents: emit(APP_INITIALIZED)\n    Events-\u003e\u003eExt: Call registered handlers\n    \n    Boot-\u003e\u003eEvents: emit(APP_READY)\n    Events-\u003e\u003eExt: Extension fully active\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions.js](), [public/script.js:666-738](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:1-150](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:1-170]()\n\n---\n\n## The `getContext()` API\n\nThe `getContext()` function provides access to core application state and functionality. It returns an object containing key components of the SillyTavern runtime.\n\n### Context API Surface\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    GetContext[\"getContext()\"]\n    \n    GetContext --\u003e Characters[\"characters\u003cbr/\u003e(Character array)\"]\n    GetContext --\u003e Groups[\"groups\u003cbr/\u003e(Group array)\"]\n    GetContext --\u003e Chat[\"chat\u003cbr/\u003e(Current chat messages)\"]\n    GetContext --\u003e ChatMeta[\"chat_metadata\u003cbr/\u003e(Chat metadata)\"]\n    GetContext --\u003e Name1[\"name1\u003cbr/\u003e(User name)\"]\n    GetContext --\u003e Name2[\"name2\u003cbr/\u003e(Character name)\"]\n    GetContext --\u003e Chid[\"characterId\u003cbr/\u003e(Current character ID)\"]\n    GetContext --\u003e GroupId[\"groupId\u003cbr/\u003e(Current group ID)\"]\n    GetContext --\u003e OnlineStatus[\"onlineStatus\u003cbr/\u003e(API connection status)\"]\n    GetContext --\u003e MaxContext[\"maxContext\u003cbr/\u003e(Context size)\"]\n    \n    GetContext --\u003e Methods[\"Methods\"]\n    Methods --\u003e Generate[\"generate()\u003cbr/\u003e(Trigger generation)\"]\n    Methods --\u003e GenerateRaw[\"generateRaw()\u003cbr/\u003e(Raw API call)\"]\n    Methods --\u003e SaveChat[\"saveChat()\u003cbr/\u003e(Save current chat)\"]\n    Methods --\u003e AddMessage[\"addOneMessage()\u003cbr/\u003e(Add message to UI)\"]\n    Methods --\u003e GetTokenCount[\"getTokenCountAsync()\u003cbr/\u003e(Count tokens)\"]\n    Methods --\u003e SetCharacter[\"setCharacterId()\u003cbr/\u003e(Switch character)\"]\n```\n\n### Key Context Properties\n\n| Property | Type | Description |\n|----------|------|-------------|\n| `characters` | `Character[]` | Array of all loaded characters |\n| `groups` | `Group[]` | Array of all character groups |\n| `chat` | `ChatMessage[]` | Current chat message array |\n| `chat_metadata` | `ChatMetadata` | Metadata for current chat |\n| `characterId` | `number|undefined` | Index of current character |\n| `groupId` | `string|null` | ID of current group |\n| `name1` | `string` | Current user name |\n| `name2` | `string` | Current character name |\n| `onlineStatus` | `string` | API connection status |\n| `maxContext` | `number` | Maximum context size |\n\n### Context Usage Example\n\n```javascript\nimport { getContext } from '../../extensions.js';\n\nasync function processCurrentChat() {\n    const context = getContext();\n    \n    // Access current state\n    const characterName = context.name2;\n    const messages = context.chat;\n    const currentCharacter = context.characters[context.characterId];\n    \n    // Use methods\n    await context.saveChat();\n    const tokenCount = await context.getTokenCountAsync('test message');\n    \n    console.log(`Processing ${messages.length} messages for ${characterName}`);\n}\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/st-context.js](), [public/script.js:290-293](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:3-5](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:2]()\n\n---\n\n## Event System Integration\n\nThe event system allows extensions to react to application lifecycle events and state changes. Events are emitted via `eventSource` and can be subscribed to using the `.on()` method.\n\n### Event Types and Usage\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Application Events\"\n        AppInit[\"APP_INITIALIZED\"]\n        AppReady[\"APP_READY\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Chat Events\"\n        ChatChanged[\"CHAT_CHANGED\"]\n        MsgReceived[\"MESSAGE_RECEIVED\"]\n        MsgRendered[\"CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED\"]\n        UserMsgRendered[\"USER_MESSAGE_RENDERED\"]\n        GenStarted[\"GENERATION_STARTED\"]\n        GenEnded[\"GENERATION_ENDED\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Character Events\"\n        CharSelected[\"CHARACTER_SELECTED\"]\n        CharEdited[\"CHARACTER_EDITED\"]\n        CharPageLoaded[\"CHARACTER_PAGE_LOADED\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Group Events\"\n        GroupSelected[\"GROUP_SELECTED\"]\n        GroupUpdated[\"GROUP_UPDATED\"]\n        GroupChatCreated[\"GROUP_CHAT_CREATED\"]\n    end\n    \n    AppInit --\u003e Extension[\"Extension Handlers\"]\n    AppReady --\u003e Extension\n    ChatChanged --\u003e Extension\n    MsgReceived --\u003e Extension\n    GenStarted --\u003e Extension\n    CharSelected --\u003e Extension\n    GroupSelected --\u003e Extension\n```\n\n### Common Event Patterns\n\n**1. Application Lifecycle**:\n```javascript\nimport { eventSource, event_types } from '../../../script.js';\n\n// Wait for app to be fully initialized\neventSource.on(event_types.APP_READY, async () =\u003e {\n    console.log('Application ready, safe to access all systems');\n    await performSetup();\n});\n```\n\n**2. Message Events**:\n```javascript\n// React to new messages\neventSource.on(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, async (messageIndex) =\u003e {\n    const context = getContext();\n    const message = context.chat[messageIndex];\n    \n    if (!message.is_user) {\n        await processCharacterMessage(message);\n    }\n});\n\n// React when message is rendered in UI\neventSource.on(event_types.CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED, async (messageIndex) =\u003e {\n    // Update UI elements for this message\n    updateMessageDisplay(messageIndex);\n});\n```\n\n**3. Generation Events**:\n```javascript\n// Before generation starts\neventSource.on(event_types.GENERATION_STARTED, async (type, data, signal) =\u003e {\n    console.log('Generation starting:', type);\n    // type: 'normal', 'continue', 'regenerate', etc.\n});\n\n// After generation completes\neventSource.on(event_types.GENERATION_ENDED, async (messageIndex) =\u003e {\n    console.log('Generation complete for message:', messageIndex);\n    await analyzeGeneratedMessage(messageIndex);\n});\n```\n\n**4. Character Selection Events**:\n```javascript\neventSource.on(event_types.CHARACTER_SELECTED, async (characterId) =\u003e {\n    const context = getContext();\n    const character = context.characters[characterId];\n    console.log('Character selected:', character.name);\n});\n\neventSource.on(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, async (currentChatId) =\u003e {\n    console.log('Chat changed to:', currentChatId);\n    await loadExtensionDataForChat(currentChatId);\n});\n```\n\n### Event Reference Table\n\n| Event Type | Parameters | When Fired |\n|------------|-----------|------------|\n| `APP_INITIALIZED` | none | Core systems loaded, before UI ready |\n| `APP_READY` | none | Application fully ready for interaction |\n| `CHAT_CHANGED` | `chatId: string` | Active chat changes (character switch, chat load) |\n| `MESSAGE_RECEIVED` | `messageIndex: number` | New message added to chat array |\n| `CHARACTER_MESSAGE_RENDERED` | `messageIndex: number` | Character message rendered in DOM |\n| `USER_MESSAGE_RENDERED` | `messageIndex: number` | User message rendered in DOM |\n| `GENERATION_STARTED` | `type: string, data: object, signal: AbortSignal` | AI generation begins |\n| `GENERATION_ENDED` | `messageIndex: number` | AI generation completes |\n| `CHARACTER_SELECTED` | `characterId: number` | Character selected (not group) |\n| `GROUP_SELECTED` | `groupId: string` | Group selected |\n| `CHARACTER_EDITED` | `characterId: number` | Character data modified |\n\nSources: [public/scripts/events.js](), [public/script.js:278](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:126-134](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:7-9]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension Prompts\n\nExtensions can inject content into AI prompts through the `setExtensionPrompt()` function. This allows extensions to add context, instructions, or data that influences AI responses.\n\n### Prompt Injection System\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Extension[\"Extension\"]\n    SetPrompt[\"setExtensionPrompt()\"]\n    PromptMgr[\"PromptManager\"]\n    \n    Extension --\u003e|\"setExtensionPrompt(MODULE_NAME, text, position, depth)\"| SetPrompt\n    SetPrompt --\u003e ExtensionPrompts[\"extension_prompts object\"]\n    \n    ExtensionPrompts --\u003e PromptMgr\n    \n    PromptMgr --\u003e InPrompt[\"IN_PROMPT\u003cbr/\u003e(In character card)\"]\n    PromptMgr --\u003e BeforePrompt[\"BEFORE_PROMPT\u003cbr/\u003e(Before character)\"]\n    PromptMgr --\u003e InChat[\"IN_CHAT\u003cbr/\u003e(At depth in chat)\"]\n    \n    InPrompt --\u003e FinalPrompt[\"Final Prompt Assembly\"]\n    BeforePrompt --\u003e FinalPrompt\n    InChat --\u003e FinalPrompt\n```\n\n### Prompt Position Types\n\n```javascript\nimport { extension_prompt_types, setExtensionPrompt } from '../../../script.js';\n\n// Available position types\nextension_prompt_types = {\n    NONE: -1,           // Don't inject\n    IN_PROMPT: 0,       // Inside character card area\n    IN_CHAT: 1,         // At specified depth in chat history\n    BEFORE_PROMPT: 2,   // Before the character card\n};\n```\n\n### Setting Extension Prompts\n\n```javascript\nimport { extension_prompt_types, extension_prompt_roles, setExtensionPrompt } from '../../../script.js';\n\nconst MODULE_NAME = 'my-extension';\n\nfunction updateExtensionPrompt(content) {\n    // Set prompt with specific position and depth\n    setExtensionPrompt(\n        MODULE_NAME,                           // Unique identifier\n        content,                               // Prompt text/content\n        extension_prompt_types.IN_CHAT,        // Where to inject\n        2,                                     // Depth (for IN_CHAT)\n        false,                                 // Scan for World Info\n        extension_prompt_roles.SYSTEM          // Role (system/user/assistant)\n    );\n}\n\n// Example: Inject summary\nfunction injectMemorySummary(summary) {\n    const formatted = `[Memory: ${summary}]`;\n    setExtensionPrompt(\n        MODULE_NAME,\n        formatted,\n        extension_prompt_types.IN_PROMPT,\n        0,\n        true  // Enable WI scanning on this prompt\n    );\n}\n\n// Remove extension prompt\nfunction removeExtensionPrompt() {\n    setExtensionPrompt(MODULE_NAME, '', extension_prompt_types.NONE, 0);\n}\n```\n\n### Prompt Role Types\n\n```javascript\nimport { extension_prompt_roles } from '../../../script.js';\n\nextension_prompt_roles = {\n    SYSTEM: 0,      // System message role\n    USER: 1,        // User message role  \n    ASSISTANT: 2,   // Assistant message role\n};\n```\n\n### Advanced Prompt Configuration\n\nExtensions can configure prompts with additional metadata:\n\n```javascript\n// Store prompt configuration in extension settings\nextension_settings[MODULE_NAME] = {\n    prompt_text: 'Instructions for the AI',\n    prompt_position: extension_prompt_types.IN_CHAT,\n    prompt_depth: 3,\n    prompt_role: extension_prompt_roles.SYSTEM,\n    scan_world_info: true,\n};\n\n// Apply configured prompt\nfunction applyConfiguredPrompt() {\n    const config = extension_settings[MODULE_NAME];\n    setExtensionPrompt(\n        MODULE_NAME,\n        config.prompt_text,\n        config.prompt_position,\n        config.prompt_depth,\n        config.scan_world_info,\n        config.prompt_role\n    );\n}\n```\n\nSources: [public/script.js:453-470](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:75-90](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js](), [public/scripts/authors-note.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Slash Command Registration\n\nExtensions can register custom slash commands that users can execute in the chat input or through Quick Reply buttons.\n\n### Slash Command System Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Parser[\"SlashCommandParser.addCommandObject()\"]\n    \n    SlashCmd[\"SlashCommand\"]\n    SlashCmd --\u003e Name[\"name\"]\n    SlashCmd --\u003e Callback[\"callback function\"]\n    SlashCmd --\u003e Returns[\"returns value\"]\n    SlashCmd --\u003e NamedArgs[\"namedArgumentList\"]\n    SlashCmd --\u003e UnnamedArgs[\"unnamedArgumentList\"]\n    SlashCmd --\u003e HelpString[\"helpString\"]\n    SlashCmd --\u003e Aliases[\"aliases\"]\n    \n    Parser --\u003e SlashCmd\n    \n    User[\"User types /command\"] --\u003e Execute[\"SlashCommandParser.parse()\"]\n    Execute --\u003e Callback\n    Callback --\u003e Return[\"Return value or output\"]\n```\n\n### Basic Command Registration\n\n```javascript\nimport { SlashCommandParser } from '../../slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js';\nimport { SlashCommand } from '../../slash-commands/SlashCommand.js';\n\nconst MODULE_NAME = 'my-extension';\n\nSlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(SlashCommand.fromProps({\n    name: 'mycommand',\n    callback: async (args, value) =\u003e {\n        console.log('Command executed with:', args, value);\n        return 'Command result';\n    },\n    helpString: 'Description of what this command does',\n}));\n```\n\n### Commands with Arguments\n\n```javascript\nimport { ARGUMENT_TYPE, SlashCommandArgument, SlashCommandNamedArgument } from '../../slash-commands/SlashCommandArgument.js';\n\n// Command with named arguments\nSlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(SlashCommand.fromProps({\n    name: 'summarize',\n    callback: async (args) =\u003e {\n        const words = args.words || 200;\n        const force = args.force || false;\n        \n        return await generateSummary(words, force);\n    },\n    namedArgumentList: [\n        SlashCommandNamedArgument.fromProps({\n            name: 'words',\n            description: 'Target word count',\n            typeList: [ARGUMENT_TYPE.NUMBER],\n            defaultValue: '200',\n        }),\n        SlashCommandNamedArgument.fromProps({\n            name: 'force',\n            description: 'Force regeneration',\n            typeList: [ARGUMENT_TYPE.BOOLEAN],\n            defaultValue: 'false',\n        }),\n    ],\n    helpString: 'Generate a summary of the chat',\n}));\n\n// Usage: /summarize words=300 force=true\n```\n\n### Commands with Unnamed Arguments\n\n```javascript\nimport { ARGUMENT_TYPE, SlashCommandArgument } from '../../slash-commands/SlashCommandArgument.js';\n\nSlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(SlashCommand.fromProps({\n    name: 'emote',\n    callback: async (args, value) =\u003e {\n        // value is the unnamed argument\n        const expression = value || args.expression;\n        await setExpression(expression);\n        return expression;\n    },\n    unnamedArgumentList: [\n        SlashCommandArgument.fromProps({\n            description: 'Expression name',\n            typeList: [ARGUMENT_TYPE.STRING],\n            isRequired: true,\n        }),\n    ],\n    namedArgumentList: [\n        SlashCommandNamedArgument.fromProps({\n            name: 'expression',\n            description: 'Expression to set',\n            typeList: [ARGUMENT_TYPE.STRING],\n        }),\n    ],\n    helpString: 'Set character expression',\n}));\n\n// Usage: /emote happy\n// Or: /emote expression=sad\n```\n\n### Enum Arguments with Autocomplete\n\n```javascript\nimport { SlashCommandEnumValue, enumTypes } from '../../slash-commands/SlashCommandEnumValue.js';\nimport { commonEnumProviders } from '../../slash-commands/SlashCommandCommonEnumsProvider.js';\n\n// Custom enum provider\nfunction getExpressionEnumProvider() {\n    return () =\u003e {\n        const expressions = getAvailableExpressions();\n        return expressions.map(expr =\u003e new SlashCommandEnumValue(expr, null, enumTypes.enum, enumTypes.name));\n    };\n}\n\nSlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(SlashCommand.fromProps({\n    name: 'setexpression',\n    callback: async (args, value) =\u003e {\n        return await setExpression(value);\n    },\n    unnamedArgumentList: [\n        SlashCommandArgument.fromProps({\n            description: 'Expression to set',\n            typeList: [ARGUMENT_TYPE.STRING],\n            isRequired: true,\n            enumProvider: getExpressionEnumProvider(),\n        }),\n    ],\n    helpString: 'Set character expression with autocomplete',\n}));\n```\n\n### Return Values and Piping\n\nCommands can return values that can be piped to other commands:\n\n```javascript\nSlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(SlashCommand.fromProps({\n    name: 'getmessage',\n    callback: async (args, value) =\u003e {\n        const context = getContext();\n        const index = parseInt(value) || context.chat.length - 1;\n        return context.chat[index]?.mes || '';\n    },\n    returns: 'message text',\n    unnamedArgumentList: [\n        SlashCommandArgument.fromProps({\n            description: 'Message index',\n            typeList: [ARGUMENT_TYPE.NUMBER],\n        }),\n    ],\n    helpString: 'Get message text by index',\n}));\n\n// Can be used in pipes: /getmessage 5 | /echo\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommand.js](), [public/scripts/slash-commands/SlashCommandArgument.js](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:900-1000](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:500-650]()\n\n---\n\n## Extension Settings and UI\n\nExtensions integrate their settings UI through HTML templates and manage configuration through the `extension_settings` object.\n\n### Settings System Flow\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    SettingsHTML[\"settings.html\"]\n    ExtSettings[\"extension_settings object\"]\n    SaveDebounce[\"saveSettingsDebounced()\"]\n    ServerSave[\"POST /api/settings/save\"]\n    \n    SettingsHTML --\u003e InlineDrawer[\"Inline Drawer Structure\"]\n    InlineDrawer --\u003e Controls[\"Form Controls\"]\n    \n    Controls --\u003e|User input| UpdateSettings[\"Update extension_settings\"]\n    UpdateSettings --\u003e SaveDebounce\n    SaveDebounce --\u003e ServerSave\n    ServerSave --\u003e SettingsJSON[\"settings.json on disk\"]\n    \n    LoadSettings[\"loadSettings()\"] --\u003e ExtSettings\n    ExtSettings --\u003e SyncUI[\"Sync UI controls\"]\n```\n\n### Settings HTML Template Structure\n\nExtensions should use the inline-drawer pattern for collapsible settings sections:\n\n```html\n\u003c!-- settings.html --\u003e\n\u003cdiv class=\"my_extension_settings\"\u003e\n    \u003cdiv class=\"inline-drawer\"\u003e\n        \u003cdiv class=\"inline-drawer-toggle inline-drawer-header\"\u003e\n            \u003cb data-i18n=\"My Extension\"\u003eMy Extension\u003c/b\u003e\n            \u003cdiv class=\"inline-drawer-icon fa-solid fa-circle-chevron-down down\"\u003e\u003c/div\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n        \n        \u003cdiv class=\"inline-drawer-content\"\u003e\n            \u003c!-- Extension controls go here --\u003e\n            \n            \u003clabel for=\"my_ext_enabled\" class=\"checkbox_label\"\u003e\n                \u003cinput id=\"my_ext_enabled\" type=\"checkbox\"\u003e\n                \u003cspan data-i18n=\"Enable Feature\"\u003eEnable Feature\u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003c/label\u003e\n            \n            \u003cdiv class=\"range-block\"\u003e\n                \u003cdiv class=\"range-block-title\" data-i18n=\"Setting Value\"\u003e\n                    Setting Value\n                \u003c/div\u003e\n                \u003cdiv class=\"range-block-range-and-counter\"\u003e\n                    \u003cdiv class=\"range-block-range\"\u003e\n                        \u003cinput type=\"range\" id=\"my_ext_value\" min=\"0\" max=\"100\" step=\"1\"\u003e\n                    \u003c/div\u003e\n                    \u003cdiv class=\"range-block-counter\"\u003e\n                        \u003cinput type=\"number\" min=\"0\" max=\"100\" step=\"1\" \n                               data-for=\"my_ext_value\" id=\"my_ext_value_counter\"\u003e\n                    \u003c/div\u003e\n                \u003c/div\u003e\n            \u003c/div\u003e\n            \n            \u003clabel for=\"my_ext_text\" data-i18n=\"Text Setting\"\u003eText Setting\u003c/label\u003e\n            \u003ctextarea id=\"my_ext_text\" class=\"text_pole textarea_compact\" rows=\"3\"\u003e\u003c/textarea\u003e\n            \n            \u003cselect id=\"my_ext_mode\" class=\"text_pole\"\u003e\n                \u003coption value=\"mode1\" data-i18n=\"Mode 1\"\u003eMode 1\u003c/option\u003e\n                \u003coption value=\"mode2\" data-i18n=\"Mode 2\"\u003eMode 2\u003c/option\u003e\n            \u003c/select\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n    \u003c/div\u003e\n\u003c/div\u003e\n```\n\n### Settings Management in JavaScript\n\n```javascript\nimport { extension_settings, saveSettingsDebounced } from '../../extensions.js';\n\nconst MODULE_NAME = 'my-extension';\n\n// Default settings structure\nconst defaultSettings = {\n    enabled: false,\n    value: 50,\n    text: 'Default text',\n    mode: 'mode1',\n};\n\nfunction loadSettings() {\n    // Initialize settings if not present\n    if (!extension_settings[MODULE_NAME]) {\n        extension_settings[MODULE_NAME] = structuredClone(defaultSettings);\n    }\n    \n    // Ensure all default keys exist (for migration)\n    for (const key in defaultSettings) {\n        if (extension_settings[MODULE_NAME][key] === undefined) {\n            extension_settings[MODULE_NAME][key] = defaultSettings[key];\n        }\n    }\n    \n    // Sync UI with settings\n    $('#my_ext_enabled').prop('checked', extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].enabled);\n    $('#my_ext_value').val(extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].value).trigger('input');\n    $('#my_ext_text').val(extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].text);\n    $('#my_ext_mode').val(extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].mode);\n}\n\nfunction setupEventListeners() {\n    // Checkbox\n    $('#my_ext_enabled').on('input', function() {\n        extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].enabled = !!$(this).prop('checked');\n        saveSettingsDebounced();\n        onSettingChanged();\n    });\n    \n    // Range slider\n    $('#my_ext_value').on('input', function() {\n        extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].value = Number($(this).val());\n        $('#my_ext_value_counter').val($(this).val());\n        saveSettingsDebounced();\n    });\n    \n    // Text area\n    $('#my_ext_text').on('input', function() {\n        extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].text = String($(this).val());\n        saveSettingsDebounced();\n    });\n    \n    // Select dropdown\n    $('#my_ext_mode').on('change', function() {\n        extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].mode = String($(this).val());\n        saveSettingsDebounced();\n    });\n}\n\nfunction onSettingChanged() {\n    // React to setting changes\n    console.log('Settings updated:', extension_settings[MODULE_NAME]);\n}\n```\n\n### Dynamic Templates with Handlebars\n\nFor complex UI components, use Handlebars templates:\n\n```javascript\nimport { renderExtensionTemplateAsync } from '../../extensions.js';\n\nasync function renderCustomUI(data) {\n    // Renders template from public/scripts/extensions/my-extension/templates/widget.html\n    const html = await renderExtensionTemplateAsync(\n        MODULE_NAME,\n        'widget',\n        data\n    );\n    \n    $('#container').html(html);\n}\n\n// Example usage\nawait renderCustomUI({\n    title: 'Widget Title',\n    items: ['Item 1', 'Item 2', 'Item 3'],\n    enabled: true,\n});\n```\n\n**Template file** (`templates/widget.html`):\n```handlebars\n\u003cdiv class=\"widget\"\u003e\n    \u003ch3\u003e{{title}}\u003c/h3\u003e\n    {{#if enabled}}\n        \u003cul\u003e\n            {{#each items}}\n                \u003cli\u003e{{this}}\u003c/li\u003e\n            {{/each}}\n        \u003c/ul\u003e\n    {{else}}\n        \u003cp\u003eWidget disabled\u003c/p\u003e\n    {{/if}}\n\u003c/div\u003e\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/settings.html](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/settings.html](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:140-170](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:141-170]()\n\n---\n\n## Asset Management\n\nExtensions can store and serve assets (images, audio, models) through the asset API endpoints.\n\n### Asset Storage Structure\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    UserDir[\"user/directories/assets/\"]\n    \n    UserDir --\u003e BGM[\"bgm/\u003cbr/\u003e(Background music)\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Ambient[\"ambient/\u003cbr/\u003e(Ambient sounds)\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Blip[\"blip/\u003cbr/\u003e(UI sounds)\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Live2D[\"live2d/\u003cbr/\u003e(Live2D models)\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e VRM[\"vrm/\u003cbr/\u003e(VRM models)\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Character[\"character/\u003cbr/\u003e(Character assets)\"]\n    UserDir --\u003e Temp[\"temp/\u003cbr/\u003e(Temporary files)\"]\n    \n    Character --\u003e Sprites[\"character-name/\u003cbr/\u003e(Expression sprites)\"]\n```\n\n### Asset API Endpoints\n\n| Endpoint | Method | Purpose |\n|----------|--------|---------|\n| `/api/assets/get` | POST | List all assets by category |\n| `/api/assets/upload` | POST | Upload new asset file |\n| `/api/assets/delete` | POST | Delete asset file |\n| `/api/assets/rename` | POST | Rename asset file |\n| `/api/sprites/get` | POST | Get character sprites |\n| `/api/sprites/upload` | POST | Upload character sprite |\n| `/api/sprites/delete` | POST | Delete character sprite |\n\n### Working with Character Sprites\n\n```javascript\nimport { getRequestHeaders } from '../../../script.js';\n\nconst MODULE_NAME = 'expressions';\n\n/**\n * Get list of sprites for a character\n */\nasync function getCharacterSprites(characterName) {\n    const response = await fetch('/api/sprites/get', {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: getRequestHeaders(),\n        body: JSON.stringify({ name: characterName }),\n    });\n    \n    if (!response.ok) {\n        return [];\n    }\n    \n    const sprites = await response.json();\n    return sprites; // Array of { label, path }\n}\n\n/**\n * Upload a new sprite\n */\nasync function uploadSprite(characterName, expression, imageFile) {\n    const formData = new FormData();\n    formData.append('name', characterName);\n    formData.append('label', expression);\n    formData.append('avatar', imageFile);\n    \n    const response = await fetch('/api/sprites/upload', {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: getRequestHeaders({ omitContentType: true }),\n        body: formData,\n    });\n    \n    return response.ok;\n}\n\n/**\n * Delete a sprite\n */\nasync function deleteSprite(characterName, spritePath) {\n    const response = await fetch('/api/sprites/delete', {\n        method: 'POST',\n        headers: getRequestHeaders(),\n        body: JSON.stringify({\n            name: characterName,\n            file: spritePath,\n        }),\n    });\n    \n    return response.ok;\n}\n```\n\n### File Upload Validation\n\nAsset filenames must be validated to prevent security issues:\n\n```javascript\n/**\n * Validate asset filename\n */\nfunction validateAssetFileName(fileName) {\n    // Only alphanumeric, underscore, dash, dot\n    if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9_\\-.]+$/.test(fileName)) {\n        return { error: true, message: 'Invalid characters in filename' };\n    }\n    \n    // Check for unsafe extensions\n    const ext = fileName.split('.').pop().toLowerCase();\n    const unsafeExtensions = ['exe', 'bat', 'cmd', 'sh', 'js', 'mjs'];\n    if (unsafeExtensions.includes(ext)) {\n        return { error: true, message: 'Forbidden file extension' };\n    }\n    \n    // No leading dot\n    if (fileName.startsWith('.')) {\n        return { error: true, message: 'Filename cannot start with dot' };\n    }\n    \n    return { error: false };\n}\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/assets.js](), [src/endpoints/sprites.js](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:95-122]()\n\n---\n\n## Chat Metadata Storage\n\nExtensions can store data associated with specific chats using the chat metadata system.\n\n### Metadata Storage Architecture\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    ChatMeta[\"chat_metadata object\"]\n    \n    ChatMeta --\u003e ExtData[\"Extension-specific data\"]\n    ChatMeta --\u003e WIState[\"world_info state\"]\n    ChatMeta --\u003e Integrity[\"integrity UUID\"]\n    ChatMeta --\u003e Note[\"note data\"]\n    \n    ExtData --\u003e ExtKey1[\"extension_name_1: {...}\"]\n    ExtData --\u003e ExtKey2[\"extension_name_2: {...}\"]\n    \n    SaveMeta[\"updateChatMetadata()\"] --\u003e ChatMeta\n    ChatMeta --\u003e ChatFile[\"chat.jsonl file\"]\n    \n    LoadChat[\"Load Chat\"] --\u003e ReadMeta[\"Read metadata header\"]\n    ReadMeta --\u003e ChatMeta\n```\n\n### Storing Extension Data in Chat Metadata\n\n```javascript\nimport { chat_metadata, updateChatMetadata } from '../../../script.js';\n\nconst MODULE_NAME = 'my-extension';\n\n/**\n * Initialize extension metadata for current chat\n */\nfunction initChatMetadata() {\n    if (!chat_metadata[MODULE_NAME]) {\n        chat_metadata[MODULE_NAME] = {\n            version: 1,\n            data: {},\n            lastUpdated: Date.now(),\n        };\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * Store data in chat metadata\n */\nfunction setExtensionData(key, value) {\n    initChatMetadata();\n    chat_metadata[MODULE_NAME].data[key] = value;\n    chat_metadata[MODULE_NAME].lastUpdated = Date.now();\n    \n    // Trigger save\n    updateChatMetadata(chat_metadata);\n}\n\n/**\n * Retrieve data from chat metadata\n */\nfunction getExtensionData(key, defaultValue = null) {\n    initChatMetadata();\n    return chat_metadata[MODULE_NAME].data[key] ?? defaultValue;\n}\n\n/**\n * Example: Store processing state\n */\nasync function onMessageReceived(messageIndex) {\n    const context = getContext();\n    const message = context.chat[messageIndex];\n    \n    // Process message\n    const result = await processMessage(message);\n    \n    // Store result in metadata\n    setExtensionData(`message_${messageIndex}_result`, result);\n}\n```\n\n### Metadata Best Practices\n\n**Namespace your data**: Always use your `MODULE_NAME` as the top-level key to avoid conflicts:\n```javascript\nchat_metadata[MODULE_NAME] = { /* your data */ };\n```\n\n**Version your schema**: Include a version field for migration:\n```javascript\nchat_metadata[MODULE_NAME] = {\n    version: 2,\n    // ... data\n};\n\n// Migration example\nif (!chat_metadata[MODULE_NAME]) {\n    chat_metadata[MODULE_NAME] = { version: 2 };\n} else if (chat_metadata[MODULE_NAME].version === 1) {\n    migrateFromV1ToV2(chat_metadata[MODULE_NAME]);\n    chat_metadata[MODULE_NAME].version = 2;\n}\n```\n\n**Clean up on chat change**: Listen for `CHAT_CHANGED` events to reset transient state:\n```javascript\neventSource.on(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, () =\u003e {\n    // Reset any extension state that's chat-specific\n    extensionState = {};\n});\n```\n\nSources: [public/script.js:424-425](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js](), [public/scripts/world-info.js:1-100]()\n\n---\n\n## Working with External APIs\n\nExtensions often need to communicate with external services for classification, generation, or other processing tasks.\n\n### API Integration Patterns\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Extension[\"Extension Code\"]\n    \n    Extension --\u003e ExtrasAPI[\"Extras API\u003cbr/\u003e(doExtrasFetch)\"]\n    Extension --\u003e MainAPI[\"Main API\u003cbr/\u003e(generateQuietPrompt)\"]\n    Extension --\u003e CustomAPI[\"Custom API\u003cbr/\u003e(fetch)\"]\n    \n    ExtrasAPI --\u003e ExtrasServer[\"http://localhost:5100\"]\n    MainAPI --\u003e ConfiguredLLM[\"Configured LLM Provider\"]\n    CustomAPI --\u003e ExternalService[\"External Service\"]\n    \n    ExtrasServer --\u003e Classification[\"classify\"]\n    ExtrasServer --\u003e Summarize[\"summarize\"]\n    ExtrasServer --\u003e Embedding[\"embedding\"]\n    \n    ConfiguredLLM --\u003e OpenAI[\"OpenAI\"]\n    ConfiguredLLM --\u003e Claude[\"Claude\"]\n    ConfiguredLLM --\u003e Local[\"Local (text-gen-webui)\"]\n```\n\n### Using Extras API\n\nThe Extras API provides specialized endpoints for common tasks:\n\n```javascript\nimport { doExtrasFetch, getApiUrl, modules } from '../../extensions.js';\n\nconst MODULE_NAME = 'my-extension';\n\n/**\n * Check if Extras API is available\n */\nfunction isExtrasAvailable() {\n    return modules.includes('classify');\n}\n\n/**\n * Classify text using Extras API\n */\nasync function classifyText(text) {\n    if (!isExtrasAvailable()) {\n        console.warn('Extras API not available');\n        return null;\n    }\n    \n    try {\n        const url = new URL(getApiUrl());\n        url.pathname = '/api/classify';\n        \n        const response = await doExtrasFetch(url, {\n            method: 'POST',\n            headers: {\n                'Content-Type': 'application/json',\n                'Bypass-Tunnel-Reminder': 'bypass',\n            },\n            body: JSON.stringify({\n                text: text,\n            }),\n        });\n        \n        if (!response.ok) {\n            throw new Error(`Classification failed: ${response.status}`);\n        }\n        \n        const result = await response.json();\n        return result.classification;\n    } catch (error) {\n        console.error('Classification error:', error);\n        return null;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Using Main API for Generation\n\nExtensions can use the configured LLM to generate text:\n\n```javascript\nimport { generateQuietPrompt } from '../../../script.js';\n\n/**\n * Generate text using the main API\n */\nasync function generateWithMainAPI(prompt, options = {}) {\n    try {\n        const result = await generateQuietPrompt(prompt, false, false, options);\n        return result;\n    } catch (error) {\n        console.error('Generation error:', error);\n        return '';\n    }\n}\n\n/**\n * Example: Generate a summary\n */\nasync function generateSummary(messages, targetWords = 200) {\n    const chatText = messages\n        .filter(m =\u003e !m.is_system)\n        .map(m =\u003e m.mes)\n        .join('\\n');\n    \n    const prompt = `Summarize the following conversation in approximately ${targetWords} words:\\n\\n${chatText}\\n\\nSummary:`;\n    \n    const summary = await generateWithMainAPI(prompt, {\n        max_tokens: Math.ceil(targetWords * 1.5),\n        temperature: 0.5,\n    });\n    \n    return summary.trim();\n}\n```\n\n### Custom API Integration\n\nFor external services, use standard `fetch()`:\n\n```javascript\n/**\n * Call custom external API\n */\nasync function callCustomAPI(endpoint, data) {\n    try {\n        const response = await fetch(endpoint, {\n            method: 'POST',\n            headers: {\n                'Content-Type': 'application/json',\n                'Authorization': `Bearer ${getAPIKey()}`,\n            },\n            body: JSON.stringify(data),\n        });\n        \n        if (!response.ok) {\n            throw new Error(`API error: ${response.status}`);\n        }\n        \n        return await response.json();\n    } catch (error) {\n        console.error('Custom API error:', error);\n        throw error;\n    }\n}\n\nfunction getAPIKey() {\n    return extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].api_key || '';\n}\n```\n\n### Handling API Timeouts and Aborts\n\n```javascript\n/**\n * Make API call with timeout\n */\nasync function fetchWithTimeout(url, options = {}, timeoutMs = 30000) {\n    const controller = new AbortController();\n    const timeout = setTimeout(() =\u003e controller.abort(), timeoutMs);\n    \n    try {\n        const response = await fetch(url, {\n            ...options,\n            signal: controller.signal,\n        });\n        clearTimeout(timeout);\n        return response;\n    } catch (error) {\n        clearTimeout(timeout);\n        if (error.name === 'AbortError') {\n            console.error('Request timed out');\n        }\n        throw error;\n    }\n}\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions.js](), [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js:400-500](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js:200-350]()\n\n---\n\n## Best Practices and Common Patterns\n\n### 1. Initialization Pattern\n\n```javascript\nconst MODULE_NAME = 'my-extension';\nlet isInitialized = false;\n\nasync function init() {\n    if (isInitialized) return;\n    \n    loadSettings();\n    setupEventListeners();\n    registerSlashCommands();\n    \n    // Wait for app to be ready before doing heavy work\n    eventSource.on(event_types.APP_READY, async () =\u003e {\n        await onAppReady();\n    });\n    \n    isInitialized = true;\n    console.log(`${MODULE_NAME} initialized`);\n}\n\n// Auto-initialize\ninit();\n```\n\n### 2. Debouncing and Performance\n\n```javascript\nimport { debounce } from '../../utils.js';\nimport { debounce_timeout } from '../../constants.js';\n\n// Debounce expensive operations\nconst updateUIDebounced = debounce(() =\u003e {\n    updateUI();\n}, debounce_timeout.quick); // 100ms\n\nconst saveDataDebounced = debounce(() =\u003e {\n    saveData();\n}, debounce_timeout.relaxed); // 1000ms\n\n// Use in event handlers\neventSource.on(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, () =\u003e {\n    updateUIDebounced();\n});\n```\n\n### 3. Error Handling\n\n```javascript\nasync function safeOperation() {\n    try {\n        const result = await riskyOperation();\n        return result;\n    } catch (error) {\n        console.error(`[${MODULE_NAME}] Operation failed:`, error);\n        toastr.error(`Extension error: ${error.message}`, MODULE_NAME);\n        return null;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### 4. State Management\n\n```javascript\n// Extension-scoped state (not persisted)\nlet extensionState = {\n    isProcessing: false,\n    cache: new Map(),\n    tempData: null,\n};\n\n// Reset state on chat change\neventSource.on(event_types.CHAT_CHANGED, () =\u003e {\n    extensionState.cache.clear();\n    extensionState.tempData = null;\n});\n\n// Persistent state (saved to settings)\nfunction saveState() {\n    extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].state = {\n        lastUpdate: Date.now(),\n        version: 1,\n    };\n    saveSettingsDebounced();\n}\n```\n\n### 5. Conditional Feature Activation\n\n```javascript\nfunction shouldActivateFeature() {\n    const settings = extension_settings[MODULE_NAME];\n    \n    // Check if enabled\n    if (!settings.enabled) return false;\n    \n    // Check API availability\n    if (settings.useExtras \u0026\u0026 !modules.includes('classify')) {\n        console.warn('Extras API required but not available');\n        return false;\n    }\n    \n    // Check context\n    const context = getContext();\n    if (!context.characterId \u0026\u0026 !context.groupId) {\n        return false; // No character/group selected\n    }\n    \n    return true;\n}\n\neventSource.on(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, async (index) =\u003e {\n    if (!shouldActivateFeature()) return;\n    \n    await processMessage(index);\n});\n```\n\n### 6. Internationalization\n\n```javascript\n// Use data-i18n attributes in HTML\n// \u003cspan data-i18n=\"setting_label\"\u003eSetting Label\u003c/span\u003e\n\n// In JavaScript, use the t`` template tag\nimport { t } from '../../i18n.js';\n\nfunction showNotification(count) {\n    const message = t`Processed ${count} messages`;\n    toastr.info(message, MODULE_NAME);\n}\n```\n\n### 7. Memory Management\n\n```javascript\n// Clean up resources\nfunction cleanup() {\n    // Remove event listeners\n    eventSource.removeListener(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, onMessageReceived);\n    \n    // Clear caches\n    spriteCache = {};\n    \n    // Abort ongoing requests\n    if (abortController) {\n        abortController.abort();\n        abortController = null;\n    }\n}\n\n// Clean up on extension disable\neventSource.on(event_types.EXTENSION_DISABLED, (extensionName) =\u003e {\n    if (extensionName === MODULE_NAME) {\n        cleanup();\n    }\n});\n```\n\nSources: [public/scripts/extensions/expressions/index.js](), [public/scripts/extensions/memory/index.js](), [public/scripts/utils.js]()\n\n---\n\n## Complete Extension Example\n\nHere's a complete minimal extension that demonstrates key concepts:\n\n**File: `public/scripts/extensions/example/index.js`**\n\n```javascript\nimport { getContext, extension_settings, saveSettingsDebounced } from '../../extensions.js';\nimport { eventSource, event_types, setExtensionPrompt, extension_prompt_types } from '../../../script.js';\nimport { SlashCommandParser } from '../../slash-commands/SlashCommandParser.js';\nimport { SlashCommand } from '../../slash-commands/SlashCommand.js';\nimport { debounce } from '../../utils.js';\nimport { debounce_timeout } from '../../constants.js';\n\nexport const MODULE_NAME = 'example';\n\nconst defaultSettings = {\n    enabled: false,\n    auto_process: true,\n    threshold: 50,\n};\n\nlet isProcessing = false;\n\nfunction loadSettings() {\n    if (!extension_settings[MODULE_NAME]) {\n        extension_settings[MODULE_NAME] = structuredClone(defaultSettings);\n    }\n    \n    $('#example_enabled').prop('checked', extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].enabled);\n    $('#example_auto').prop('checked', extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].auto_process);\n    $('#example_threshold').val(extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].threshold).trigger('input');\n}\n\nfunction setupEventListeners() {\n    $('#example_enabled').on('input', function() {\n        extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].enabled = !!$(this).prop('checked');\n        saveSettingsDebounced();\n    });\n    \n    $('#example_auto').on('input', function() {\n        extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].auto_process = !!$(this).prop('checked');\n        saveSettingsDebounced();\n    });\n    \n    $('#example_threshold').on('input', function() {\n        extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].threshold = Number($(this).val());\n        $('#example_threshold_counter').val($(this).val());\n        saveSettingsDebounced();\n    });\n    \n    $('#example_process_now').on('click', async () =\u003e {\n        await processCurrentChat();\n    });\n}\n\nasync function processCurrentChat() {\n    if (isProcessing) {\n        toastr.warning('Processing already in progress');\n        return;\n    }\n    \n    const context = getContext();\n    if (!context.chat || context.chat.length === 0) {\n        toastr.warning('No chat to process');\n        return;\n    }\n    \n    isProcessing = true;\n    \n    try {\n        console.log(`[${MODULE_NAME}] Processing chat with ${context.chat.length} messages`);\n        \n        const result = await performProcessing(context.chat);\n        \n        // Inject result as extension prompt\n        setExtensionPrompt(\n            MODULE_NAME,\n            `[Processing Result: ${result}]`,\n            extension_prompt_types.IN_PROMPT,\n            0\n        );\n        \n        toastr.success('Processing complete', MODULE_NAME);\n    } catch (error) {\n        console.error(`[${MODULE_NAME}] Processing error:`, error);\n        toastr.error(`Processing failed: ${error.message}`, MODULE_NAME);\n    } finally {\n        isProcessing = false;\n    }\n}\n\nasync function performProcessing(messages) {\n    // Simulate processing\n    await new Promise(resolve =\u003e setTimeout(resolve, 1000));\n    \n    const messageCount = messages.filter(m =\u003e !m.is_system).length;\n    return `Processed ${messageCount} messages`;\n}\n\nfunction registerSlashCommands() {\n    SlashCommandParser.addCommandObject(SlashCommand.fromProps({\n        name: 'example',\n        callback: async (args, value) =\u003e {\n            await processCurrentChat();\n            return 'Processing initiated';\n        },\n        helpString: 'Process the current chat',\n    }));\n}\n\nasync function onAppReady() {\n    console.log(`[${MODULE_NAME}] App ready, extension active`);\n}\n\nasync function onMessageReceived(messageIndex) {\n    if (!extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].enabled) return;\n    if (!extension_settings[MODULE_NAME].auto_process) return;\n    \n    const context = getContext();\n    const message = context.chat[messageIndex];\n    \n    console.log(`[${MODULE_NAME}] New message:`, message.mes?.substring(0, 50));\n}\n\nasync function init() {\n    loadSettings();\n    setupEventListeners();\n    registerSlashCommands();\n    \n    eventSource.on(event_types.APP_READY, onAppReady);\n    eventSource.on(event_types.MESSAGE_RECEIVED, onMessageReceived);\n    \n    console.log(`[${MODULE_NAME}] initialized`);\n}\n\n// Auto-initialize\ninit();\n```\n\n**File: `public/scripts/extensions/example/settings.html`**\n\n```html\n\u003cdiv class=\"example_settings\"\u003e\n    \u003cdiv Now\"\u003eProcess Now\u003c/span\u003e\n            \u003c/div\u003e\n        \u003c/div\u003e\n    \u003c/div\u003e\n\u003c/div\u003e\n```\n\nThis example demonstrates:\n- Settings management with easy filtering:\n\n```javascript\nconsole.log(`[${MODULE_NAME}] Extension initialized`);\nconsole.debug(`[${MODULE_NAME}] Processing state:`, state);\nconsole.warn(`[${MODULE_NAME}] API not available`);\nconsole.error(`[${MODULE_NAME}] Operation failed:`, error);\n```\n\n### Debugging Event Flow\n\n```javascript\n// Log all events for debugging\nObject.values(event_types).forEach(eventType =\u003e {\n    eventSource.on(eventType, (...args) =\u003e {\n        console.debug(`[${MODULE_NAME}] Event:`, eventType, args);\n    });\n});\n```\n\n### Performance Monitoring\n\n```javascript\nasync function monitoredOperation(name, fn) {\n    const start = performance.now();\n    try {\n        const result = await fn();\n        const duration = performance.now() - start;\n        console.log(`[${MODULE_NAME}] ${name} took ${duration.toFixed(2)}ms`);\n        return result;\n    } catch (error) {\n        const duration = performance.now() - start;\n        console.error(`[${MODULE_NAME}] ${name} failed after ${duration.toFixed(2)}ms`, error);\n        throw error;\n    }\n}\n\n// Usage\nawait monitoredOperation('processChat', () =\u003e processCurrentChat());\n```\n\n### Common Issues\n\n| Issue | Solution |\n|-------|----------|\n| Settings not persisting | Ensure `saveSettingsDebounced()` is called after changes |\n| Events not firing | Check event subscription happens before `APP_READY` |\n| UI not updating | Verify jQuery selectors match HTML element IDs |\n| Extension not loading | Check console for import errors, verify file paths |\n| Prompts not injecting | Confirm `setExtensionPrompt()` called with valid position |\n\nSources:
