[src/endpoints/settings.js](src/endpoints/settings.js)\n- [src/middleware/basicAuth.js](src/middleware/basicAuth.js)\n- [src/middleware/whitelist.js](src/middleware/whitelist.js)\n- [src/prompt-converters.js](src/prompt-converters.js)\n- [src/users.js](src/users.js)\n- [src/util.js](src/util.js)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n\nThis page covers creating server-side endpoints and extending the SillyTavern backend. For information about AI provider integration specifically, see the chat completion and text generation sections in [3]() and [3.1](). For client-side extension development, see [10.2]().\n\n## Purpose and Scope\n\nBackend API development in SillyTavern involves creating Express.js endpoints that handle HTTP requests, manage file operations, integrate with user directories, and communicate with external services. This guide covers the architectural patterns, middleware systems, file handling conventions, and best practices for extending the server-side functionality.\n\n## Server Architecture\n\n### Application Initialization\n\nThe SillyTavern server follows a bootstrap pattern starting from the entry point:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Entry[\"server.js\"]\n    CLI[\"CommandLineParser\u003cbr/\u003ecliArgs.dataRoot\u003cbr/\u003ecliArgs.port\"]\n    Globals[\"globalThis.DATA_ROOT\u003cbr/\u003eglobalThis.COMMAND_LINE_ARGS\"]\n    Import[\"import('./src/server-main.js')\"]\n    \n    Config[\"getConfig()\u003cbr/\u003eRead config.yaml\u003cbr/\u003eCACHED_CONFIG\"]\n    ConfigPath[\"setConfigFilePath()\u003cbr/\u003eCONFIG_PATH\"]\n    EnvOverride[\"keyToEnv()\u003cbr/\u003eSILLYTAVERN_* env vars\"]\n    \n    Entry --\u003e CLI\n    CLI --\u003e Globals\n    Globals --\u003e Import\n    Import --\u003e ConfigPath\n    ConfigPath --\u003e Config\n    Config --\u003e EnvOverride\n```\n\n**Application Entry Point Flow**\n\nSources: [server.js:1-17](), [src/util.js:29-79]()\n\nThe server initialization begins at [server.js:1-17](), which instantiates `CommandLineParser` [server.js:8]() to parse arguments like `--dataRoot` and `--port`. These values are stored in `globalThis.DATA_ROOT` [server.js:9]() and `globalThis.COMMAND_LINE_ARGS` [server.js:10](). The main server module is dynamically imported [server.js:14]().\n\nConfiguration is managed through [src/util.js:29-79](). The `getConfig()` function [src/util.js:55-79]() reads and parses `config.yaml`, caching the result in `CACHED_CONFIG` [src/util.js:29](). The config path is set via `setConfigFilePath()` [src/util.js:44-49](). Environment variables override config values using the `SILLYTAVERN_*` prefix [src/util.js:38](), converted via `keyToEnv()` [src/util.js:38]().\n\n### Express Routing Structure\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Middleware Stack\"\n        CORS[\"cors()\u003cbr/\u003econfig.cors settings\"]\n        Session[\"express-session\u003cbr/\u003ecookie-secret.txt\"]\n        Whitelist[\"getWhitelistMiddleware()\u003cbr/\u003econfig.whitelist\"]\n        BasicAuth[\"basicAuthMiddleware\u003cbr/\u003econfig.basicAuthMode\"]\n        UserCtx[\"setUserMiddleware()\u003cbr/\u003ereq.user.profile\u003cbr/\u003ereq.user.directories\"]\n        CSRF[\"csrfProtection\u003cbr/\u003econfig.disableCsrfProtection\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Endpoint Routers (express.Router)\"\n        Chars[\"characters.router\u003cbr/\u003ePOST /create, /edit, /delete\u003cbr/\u003ePOST /import, /export\"]\n        Chats[\"chats.router\u003cbr/\u003ePOST /save, /get, /rename\u003cbr/\u003ePOST /delete, /export\"]\n        Settings[\"settings.router\u003cbr/\u003ePOST /save, /get\u003cbr/\u003ePOST /make-snapshot, /restore-snapshot\"]\n        Content[\"content-manager.router\u003cbr/\u003ePOST /import, /download\"]\n        Presets[\"presets.router\u003cbr/\u003ePOST /save, /delete, /restore\"]\n        Groups[\"groups.router\u003cbr/\u003ePOST /all, /create, /edit, /delete\"]\n        Secrets[\"secrets.router\u003cbr/\u003ePOST /read, /write, /find\"]\n    end\n    \n    CORS --\u003e Session\n    Session --\u003e Whitelist\n    Whitelist --\u003e BasicAuth\n    BasicAuth --\u003e UserCtx\n    UserCtx --\u003e CSRF\n    \n    CSRF --\u003e Chars\n    CSRF --\u003e Chats\n    CSRF --\u003e Settings\n    CSRF --\u003e Content\n    CSRF --\u003e Presets\n    CSRF --\u003e Groups\n    CSRF --\u003e Secrets\n```\n\n**Express Router Mounting**\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:1-27](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:432](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:200](), [src/endpoints/groups.js:12](), [src/middleware/whitelist.js:101](), [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:13]()\n\nEach endpoint file exports a router via `express.Router()` [src/endpoints/chats.js:432](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:200](), [src/endpoints/groups.js:12](). The middleware chain is applied globally: CORS (configured via `config.cors`), session management (using `cookie-secret.txt` [src/users.js:38]()), whitelist [src/middleware/whitelist.js:101-148]() (checks IP against `config.whitelist`), basic auth [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:13-54]() (optional, `config.basicAuthMode`), user context injection via `setUserMiddleware()` [src/users.js:710-799]() (sets `req.user`), and CSRF protection (unless `config.disableCsrfProtection` is true).\n\n## Creating New Endpoints\n\n### Basic Endpoint Structure\n\nA typical endpoint module follows this pattern:\n\n```javascript\n// src/endpoints/my-endpoint.js\nimport express from 'express';\nimport path from 'node:path';\nimport fs from 'node:fs';\nimport sanitize from 'sanitize-filename';\nimport { sync as writeFileAtomicSync } from 'write-file-atomic';\n\n// Import middleware\nimport validateAvatarUrlMiddleware from '../middleware/validateFileName.js';\n\n// Import utilities\nimport { getConfigValue, tryParse, humanizedDateTime } from '../util.js';\n\n// Create and export router\nexport const router = express.Router();\n\n// Define routes\nrouter.post('/action', validateAvatarUrlMiddleware, async (request, response) =\u003e {\n    try {\n        // Validate input\n        if (!request.body.requiredField) {\n            return response.sendStatus(400);\n        }\n        \n        // Access user directories (injected by setUserMiddleware)\n        const directories = request.user.directories;\n        const handle = request.user.profile.handle;\n        \n        // Build safe file path\n        const fileName = sanitize(request.body.name);\n        const filePath = path.join(directories.root, fileName);\n        \n        // Perform file operation\n        const data = JSON.stringify(request.body.data, null, 4);\n        writeFileAtomicSync(filePath, data, 'utf8');\n        \n        // Send response\n        return response.json({ ok: true });\n    } catch (error) {\n        console.error('Error in /action:', error);\n        return response.status(500).send({ error: error.message });\n    }\n});\n```\n\n**Key Imports and Patterns**\n\n| Import | Purpose | Usage Example |\n|--------|---------|---------------|\n| `express` | Router creation | `express.Router()` |\n| `sanitize-filename` | Path safety | `sanitize(userInput)` |\n| `write-file-atomic` | Safe writes | `writeFileAtomicSync(path, data)` |\n| `validateAvatarUrlMiddleware` | Input validation | Applied to routes |\n| `tryParse()` from util | Safe JSON parse | `tryParse(jsonString)` |\n| `humanizedDateTime()` from util | Timestamps | `humanizedDateTime()` |\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:1-27](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:1-24](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:1-11](), [src/util.js:540-564]()\n\n### Registering a New Router\n\nOnce you create a router module, it must be mounted in the main server application. Based on the existing endpoint structure, routers are mounted with path prefixes:\n\n```javascript\n// In src/server-main.js or main Express app initialization\nimport { router as charactersRouter } from './endpoints/characters.js';\nimport { router as chatsRouter } from './endpoints/chats.js';\nimport { router as settingsRouter } from './endpoints/settings.js';\nimport { router as myRouter } from './endpoints/my-endpoint.js';\n\n// Mount routers with path prefixes\napp.use('/api/characters', charactersRouter);\napp.use('/api/chats', chatsRouter);\napp.use('/api/settings', settingsRouter);\napp.use('/api/my-endpoint', myRouter);  // Your new router\n```\n\n**Router Export Pattern**\n\nEach endpoint file exports its router:\n\n```javascript\n// At the end of src/endpoints/my-endpoint.js\nexport const router = express.Router();\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:1-27](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:432](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:200](), [src/endpoints/groups.js:12]()\n\nAll endpoint modules follow this pattern: create router with `express.Router()`, define routes on the router, and export it as a named export `router`.\n\n### Request Flow Diagram\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant Express\n    participant Whitelist as \"getWhitelistMiddleware()\"\n    participant Auth as \"basicAuthMiddleware\"\n    participant User as \"setUserMiddleware()\"\n    participant CSRF as \"csrfProtection\"\n    participant Validator as \"validateFileName\"\n    participant Handler as \"router.post()\"\n    participant FS as \"fs/fsPromises\"\n    \n    Client-\u003e\u003eExpress: \"POST /api/endpoint\"\n    Express-\u003e\u003eWhitelist: \"isIPInWhitelist()\"\n    Whitelist-\u003e\u003eAuth: \"IP authorized\"\n    Auth-\u003e\u003eUser: \"Verify credentials\"\n    User-\u003e\u003eCSRF: \"getUserDirectories()\"\n    CSRF-\u003e\u003eValidator: \"verifyToken()\"\n    Validator-\u003e\u003eHandler: \"sanitize(filename)\"\n    Handler-\u003e\u003eFS: \"readFile()/writeFileAtomicSync()\"\n    FS--\u003e\u003eHandler: \"Buffer/data\"\n    Handler--\u003e\u003eClient: \"response.json()\"\n```\n\n**Request Processing Flow**\n\nSources: [src/middleware/whitelist.js:101-148](), [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:13-54](), [src/users.js:636-687](), [src/users.js:710-799]()\n\nRequests pass through multiple middleware layers before reaching endpoint handlers:\n\n1. **Whitelist Middleware** [src/middleware/whitelist.js:112-147](): Checks client IP using `isIPInWhitelist()` [src/middleware/whitelist.js:123-125](). Supports forwarded IPs via `getForwardedIp()` [src/middleware/whitelist.js:55-73]() which reads `X-Real-IP` or `X-Forwarded-For` headers. If enabled, Docker host IPs are automatically whitelisted via `addDockerHostsToWhitelist()` [src/middleware/whitelist.js:79-95]().\n\n2. **Basic Auth Middleware** [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:13-54](): When `basicAuthMode` is enabled, validates credentials. Supports per-user auth via `perUserBasicAuth` [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:34](), checking passwords with `getPasswordHash()` [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:47](). Credential format is `Basic {base64(username:password)}` [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:35-38]().\n\n3. **User Context Middleware** [src/users.js:710-799](): Loads user profile and injects `request.user` object. Calls `getUserDirectories()` [src/users.js:636-687]() to populate directory paths. Uses `DIRECTORIES_CACHE` [src/users.js:36]() for performance.\n\n## User Directory System Integration\n\n### Directory Structure Access\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"request.user.directories (UserDirectoryList)\"\n        Root[\"root = DATA_ROOT/users/{handle}\"]\n        \n        Root --\u003e Chars[\"characters\u003cbr/\u003ePNG with embedded JSON\"]\n        Root --\u003e Chats[\"chats/{character}/*.jsonl\u003cbr/\u003eLine-delimited chat logs\"]\n        Root --\u003e GroupChats[\"groupChats/*.jsonl\u003cbr/\u003eGroup chat logs\"]\n        Root --\u003e Groups[\"groups/*.json\u003cbr/\u003eGroup metadata\"]\n        Root --\u003e Worlds[\"worlds/*.json\u003cbr/\u003eWorld Info files\"]\n        Root --\u003e Avatars[\"avatars/\u003cbr/\u003eUser avatar images\"]\n        Root --\u003e UserImages[\"userImages/\u003cbr/\u003eUploaded images\"]\n        Root --\u003e BG[\"backgrounds/\u003cbr/\u003eBackground images\"]\n        Root --\u003e Assets[\"assets/\u003cbr/\u003eSprite/audio files\"]\n        Root --\u003e NovelAI[\"novelAI_Settings/\u003cbr/\u003eNovelAI presets\"]\n        Root --\u003e OpenAI[\"openAI_Settings/\u003cbr/\u003eOpenAI presets\"]\n        Root --\u003e KoboldAI[\"koboldAI_Settings/\u003cbr/\u003eKoboldAI presets\"]\n        Root --\u003e TextGen[\"textGen_Settings/\u003cbr/\u003eText gen presets\"]\n        Root --\u003e Instruct[\"instruct/\u003cbr/\u003eInstruct templates\"]\n        Root --\u003e Context[\"context/\u003cbr/\u003eContext templates\"]\n        Root --\u003e Sysprompt[\"sysprompt/\u003cbr/\u003eSystem prompts\"]\n        Root --\u003e Reasoning[\"reasoning/\u003cbr/\u003eReasoning templates\"]\n        Root --\u003e QR[\"quickreplies/\u003cbr/\u003eQuick reply sets\"]\n        Root --\u003e Ext[\"extensions/\u003cbr/\u003eThird-party extensions\"]\n        Root --\u003e Themes[\"themes/\u003cbr/\u003eUI themes\"]\n        Root --\u003e MovingUI[\"movingUI/\u003cbr/\u003eUI layout presets\"]\n        Root --\u003e Workflows[\"comfyWorkflows/\u003cbr/\u003eComfyUI workflows\"]\n        Root --\u003e Thumbs[\"thumbnails/\u003cbr/\u003ethumbnailsBg/\u003cbr/\u003ethumbnailsAvatar/\u003cbr/\u003ethumbnailsPersona/\"]\n        Root --\u003e Backups[\"backups/\u003cbr/\u003eAuto-backups\"]\n        Root --\u003e Vectors[\"vectors/\u003cbr/\u003eEmbedding storage\"]\n        Root --\u003e Files[\"files/\u003cbr/\u003eUploaded files\"]\n    end\n```\n\n**User Directory Hierarchy**\n\nSources: [src/users.js:71-103](), [src/users.js:636-687](), [src/constants.js:4-40]()\n\nThe `getUserDirectories()` function [src/users.js:636-687]() returns a `UserDirectoryList` typedef [src/users.js:71-103]() containing all user-specific directory paths. Paths are constructed from `DATA_ROOT`, the user's handle, and directory names from `USER_DIRECTORY_TEMPLATE` [src/constants.js:4-40](). The result is cached in `DIRECTORIES_CACHE` [src/users.js:36]() using the user handle as key for performance optimization.\n\n### Accessing User Context\n\nThe `request.user` object is injected by `setUserMiddleware()` and provides access to user information and file system paths:\n\n```javascript\n// User profile information (User typedef)\nconst handle = request.user.profile.handle;     // User's short handle (string)\nconst name = request.user.profile.name;         // Display name (string)\nconst isAdmin = request.user.profile.admin;     // Admin flag (boolean)\nconst enabled = request.user.profile.enabled;   // Account status (boolean)\nconst created = request.user.profile.created;   // Creation timestamp (number)\nconst password = request.user.profile.password; // Scrypt hash (string)\nconst salt = request.user.profile.salt;         // Salt for hashing (string)\n\n// Directory paths (UserDirectoryList typedef)\nconst charactersDir = request.user.directories.characters;  // Character PNG files\nconst chatsDir = request.user.directories.chats;            // Chat JSONL files\nconst worldsDir = request.user.directories.worlds;          // World Info JSON\nconst backupsDir = request.user.directories.backups;        // Backup files\nconst secretsPath = path.join(request.user.directories.root, 'secrets.json');\nconst settingsPath = path.join(request.user.directories.root, 'settings.json');\n\n// Full directory object\nconst directories = request.user.directories;  // All 30+ directory paths\n```\n\n**User Object Structure**\n\n| Property | Type | Description | Source |\n|----------|------|-------------|--------|\n| `request.user.profile` | `User` | User account metadata | [src/users.js:49-57]() |\n| `request.user.directories` | `UserDirectoryList` | File system paths | [src/users.js:71-103]() |\n\nSources: [src/users.js:49-68](), [src/users.js:71-103](), [src/users.js:710-799]()\n\nThe `request.user` object is populated by `setUserMiddleware()` [src/users.js:710-799](). The `profile` property follows the `User` typedef [src/users.js:49-57]() containing account information. The `directories` property follows the `UserDirectoryList` typedef [src/users.js:71-103]() with paths to all content directories. Always use `request.user.directories` for file paths rather than manual construction to ensure multi-user support and proper isolation.\n\n## File Operations and Persistence\n\n### Character Card Operations\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Read Operations\"\n        ReadFile[\"readCharacterData()\u003cbr/\u003ewith caching\"]\n        MemCache[\"MemoryLimitedMap\u003cbr/\u003e100MB default\"]\n        DiskCache[\"DiskCache\u003cbr/\u003enode-persist\"]\n        Parse[\"parse()\u003cbr/\u003eExtract from PNG\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Write Operations\"\n        WriteFile[\"writeCharacterData()\"]\n        ImageProc[\"Resize/crop avatar\u003cbr/\u003eAVATAR_WIDTH x AVATAR_HEIGHT\"]\n        WritePNG[\"write()\u003cbr/\u003eEmbed JSON in PNG\"]\n        AtomicWrite[\"writeFileAtomicSync()\"]\n    end\n    \n    ReadFile --\u003e MemCache\n    MemCache --\u003e|Miss| DiskCache\n    DiskCache --\u003e|Miss| Parse\n    \n    WriteFile --\u003e ImageProc\n    ImageProc --\u003e WritePNG\n    WritePNG --\u003e AtomicWrite\n```\n\n**Character File Read/Write Pattern**\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:29-36](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-159](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:166-209](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:220-265]()\n\nCharacter data uses a three-tier caching system:\n\n1. **Memory Cache** [src/endpoints/characters.js:29-31](): `MemoryLimitedMap` with capacity from `getConfigValue('performance.memoryCacheCapacity', '100mb')` [src/endpoints/characters.js:30](). Uses LRU eviction. Disabled on Android via `isAndroid` check [src/endpoints/characters.js:33]().\n\n2. **Disk Cache** [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-159](): `DiskCache` class wraps `node-persist` [src/endpoints/characters.js:51](). Stores parsed character data at `DATA_ROOT/_cache/characters/` [src/endpoints/characters.js:69](). Syncs every `SYNC_INTERVAL` (5 minutes) [src/endpoints/characters.js:49]() via `#syncCacheEntries()` [src/endpoints/characters.js:84-97](). The `verify()` method [src/endpoints/characters.js:126-150]() prunes invalid entries.\n\n3. **File System** [src/endpoints/characters.js:198](): Direct parsing via `parse()` from `character-card-parser.js`.\n\n`readCharacterData()` [src/endpoints/characters.js:181-209]() checks caches in order. Cache keys use `getCacheKey()` [src/endpoints/characters.js:166-173]() which includes `stat.mtimeMs` to detect file changes.\n\n`writeCharacterData()` [src/endpoints/characters.js:220-265]() processes images with Jimp [src/endpoints/characters.js:239-251](), embeds JSON via `write()` [src/endpoints/characters.js:256](), uses `writeFileAtomicSync()` [src/endpoints/characters.js:259](), and invalidates caches [src/endpoints/characters.js:222-233]().\n\n### Chat File Operations\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Chat Persistence\"\n        ChatData[\"Chat messages array\"]\n        Serialize[\"JSON.stringify() per message\"]\n        JSONL[\"Join with newlines\u003cbr/\u003eJSONL format\"]\n        FilePath[\"chats/{character}/{chatname}.jsonl\"]\n        Atomic[\"writeFileAtomicSync()\"]\n        Backup[\"Throttled backup\u003cbr/\u003e10 second interval\"]\n    end\n    \n    ChatData --\u003e Serialize\n    Serialize --\u003e JSONL\n    JSONL --\u003e FilePath\n    FilePath --\u003e Atomic\n    Atomic --\u003e Backup\n    \n    subgraph \"Integrity Check\"\n        IntSlug[\"chat_metadata.integrity\"]\n        FirstLine[\"Read first line\"]\n        Compare[\"Compare integrity values\"]\n    end\n    \n    FilePath --\u003e IntSlug\n    Atomic --\u003e FirstLine\n    FirstLine --\u003e Compare\n```\n\n**Chat Save and Backup Pattern**\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/chats.js:25-77](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:315-334](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:441-467]()\n\nChat files use JSONL format (JSON Lines) where each line is a message object. The save flow:\n\n1. **Serialization** [src/endpoints/chats.js:457](): `chatData.map(m =\u003e JSON.stringify(m)).join('\\n')`\n\n2. **Integrity Check** [src/endpoints/chats.js:459-464](): Optional check controlled by `checkIntegrity` config [src/endpoints/chats.js:28](). Compares `chatData[0].chat_metadata.integrity` slug against first line of existing file via `checkChatIntegrity()` [src/endpoints/chats.js:315-334](). Throws `IntegrityMismatchError` [src/endpoints/chats.js:435-445]() on mismatch to prevent concurrent write conflicts.\n\n3. **Atomic Write** [src/endpoints/chats.js:465](): `tryWriteFileSync(filePath, jsonlData)`\n\n4. **Throttled Backup** [src/endpoints/chats.js:466](): Calls `getBackupFunction(handle)` [src/endpoints/chats.js:72-77]() which returns a per-user throttled version of `backupChat()` [src/endpoints/chats.js:40-60](). Throttle interval from `getConfigValue('backups.chat.throttleInterval', 10_000)` [src/endpoints/chats.js:27](). Backups stored as `{backupsDir}/chat_{name}_{timestamp}.jsonl` [src/endpoints/chats.js:49](). Old backups pruned via `removeOldBackups()` [src/endpoints/chats.js:52]().\n\n**Backup Configuration**\n\n| Config Key | Default | Purpose |\n|------------|---------|---------|\n| `backups.chat.enabled` | `true` | Enable chat backups |\n| `backups.chat.throttleInterval` | `10000` | Throttle interval (ms) |\n| `backups.chat.maxTotalBackups` | `-1` | Total backup limit (-1 = unlimited) |\n| `backups.common.numberOfBackups` | `50` | Backups per chat |\n\n### Atomic File Writing\n\nAll file writes must use `writeFileAtomicSync()` to prevent corruption from crashes or concurrent access:\n\n```javascript\nimport { sync as writeFileAtomicSync } from 'write-file-atomic';\n\n// Write JSON data (pretty-printed)\nconst data = { name: 'example', values: [1, 2, 3] };\nwriteFileAtomicSync(filePath, JSON.stringify(data, null, 4), 'utf8');\n\n// Write binary data (Buffer)\nwriteFileAtomicSync(imagePath, imageBuffer);\n\n// Write plain text\nwriteFileAtomicSync(logPath, logString, 'utf8');\n```\n\n**Atomic Write Mechanism**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Data[\"Data to write\"]\n    Temp[\"Temp file\u003cbr/\u003e{filename}.tmp\"]\n    Rename[\"fs.renameSync()\u003cbr/\u003eAtomic operation\"]\n    Final[\"Final file\u003cbr/\u003e{filename}\"]\n    \n    Data --\u003e Temp\n    Temp --\u003e Rename\n    Rename --\u003e Final\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:8](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:8](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:6](), [src/endpoints/groups.js:7]()\n\nThe `write-file-atomic` library [src/endpoints/characters.js:8]() writes to a temporary file first, then atomically renames it. This prevents corruption if the process crashes mid-write. Used throughout the codebase for critical files:\n\n- Character cards: [src/endpoints/characters.js:259]()\n- Chat logs: [src/endpoints/chats.js:465]() via `tryWriteFileSync()`\n- Settings: [src/endpoints/settings.js:205]()\n- Groups: [src/endpoints/groups.js:187](), [src/endpoints/groups.js:200]()\n\nNever use `fs.writeFileSync()` for important data. Always use `writeFileAtomicSync()` or the util wrapper `tryWriteFileSync()` [src/util.js:1218-1227]().\n\n## Error Handling and Validation\n\n### Input Validation Patterns\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    Input[\"Request Input\"]\n    \n    subgraph \"Validation Middleware\"\n        FileVal[\"validateFileName\u003cbr/\u003eCheck for path traversal\"]\n        Sanitize[\"sanitize-filename\u003cbr/\u003eRemove dangerous chars\"]\n        TypeCheck[\"Type validation\u003cbr/\u003etypeof checks\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Handler Validation\"\n        Exists[\"fs.existsSync()\u003cbr/\u003eCheck file exists\"]\n        Perms[\"Check user permissions\"]\n        Format[\"Validate data format\"]\n    end\n    \n    Error[\"Return 400/403/404\"]\n    Success[\"Process request\"]\n    \n    Input --\u003e FileVal\n    FileVal --\u003e Sanitize\n    Sanitize --\u003e TypeCheck\n    TypeCheck --\u003e Exists\n    Exists --\u003e Perms\n    Perms --\u003e Format\n    Format --\u003e Success\n    \n    FileVal --\u003e|Invalid| Error\n    Exists --\u003e|Not found| Error\n    Perms --\u003e|Denied| Error\n    Format --\u003e|Invalid| Error\n```\n\n**Input Validation Flow**\n\nSources: [src/middleware/validateFileName.js:1-69](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:742-744](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:441-463]()\n\nInput validation follows a layered approach. The `validateFileName` middleware [src/middleware/validateFileName.js]() checks for path traversal attacks and validates filename patterns. It can be applied globally or per-route using `getFileNameValidationFunction()` [src/middleware/validateFileName.js:55-69]() which creates a validator for a specific request body field.\n\nAll filenames should be sanitized using the `sanitize-filename` library [src/endpoints/characters.js:743](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:447]() before any file operations. Type checking with `typeof` prevents type coercion attacks [src/endpoints/chats.js:465-467](), and existence checks with `fs.existsSync()` prevent operations on non-existent files [src/endpoints/chats.js:540-545]().\n\n### Error Response Pattern\n\n```javascript\nrouter.post('/endpoint', async (request, response) =\u003e {\n    try {\n        // Validate inputs\n        if (!request.body.requiredField) {\n            return response.sendStatus(400);  // Bad request\n        }\n        \n        // Check file existence\n        const filePath = path.join(request.user.directories.root, 'file.json');\n        if (!fs.existsSync(filePath)) {\n            return response.sendStatus(404);  // Not found\n        }\n        \n        // Check permissions (if applicable)\n        if (!userHasPermission) {\n            return response.sendStatus(403);  // Forbidden\n        }\n        \n        // Perform operation\n        const result = await operation();\n        \n        // Success response\n        return response.json({ result });\n        \n    } catch (error) {\n        console.error('Error in endpoint:', error);\n        return response.status(500).send({ error: error.message });\n    }\n});\n```\n\n**HTTP Status Code Conventions**\n\n| Status Code | Meaning | When to Use |\n|-------------|---------|-------------|\n| 200 | OK | Successful GET/operation |\n| 400 | Bad Request | Invalid/missing parameters |\n| 403 | Forbidden | User lacks permission |\n| 404 | Not Found | Resource doesn't exist |\n| 500 | Internal Server Error | Unexpected server error |\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/chats.js:450-471](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:1349-1369](), [src/endpoints/settings.js:202-212]()\n\nError handling follows HTTP status code conventions: 400 for bad requests [src/endpoints/chats.js:462](), 403 for forbidden operations, 404 for not found [src/endpoints/chats.js:584-586](), and 500 for server errors [src/endpoints/settings.js:210](). Always log errors with `console.error()` before returning error responses [src/endpoints/chats.js:469]().\n\n## Configuration and Utilities\n\n### Reading Configuration Values\n\nThe configuration system supports hierarchical YAML config with environment variable overrides:\n\n```javascript\nimport { getConfigValue, getConfig, keyToEnv } from '../util.js';\n\n// Read with default value\nconst enabled = getConfigValue('feature.enabled', false, 'boolean');\n\n// Read with type conversion\nconst maxSize = getConfigValue('feature.maxSize', 100, 'number');\n\n// Read nested objects\nconst settings = getConfigValue('feature.settings', { default: 'value' });\n\n// Read array values\nconst whitelist = getConfigValue('whitelist', ['127.0.0.1']);\n\n// Environment variable override\n// SILLYTAVERN_FEATURE_ENABLED=true overrides config.yaml\nconst envKey = keyToEnv('feature.enabled'); // Returns 'SILLYTAVERN_FEATURE_ENABLED'\n\n// Get entire config object (cached)\nconst config = getConfig();  // Returns parsed config.yaml\n```\n\n**Configuration Resolution Order**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Check[\"getConfigValue(key, default, type)\"]\n    EnvKey[\"keyToEnv(key)\u003cbr/\u003eSILLYTAVERN_*\"]\n    EnvVar[\"process.env[envKey]\"]\n    YAML[\"getConfig()\u003cbr/\u003eCACHED_CONFIG\"]\n    LodashGet[\"_.get(config, key)\"]\n    DefaultVal[\"Return default\"]\n    TypeConv[\"Type conversion:\u003cbr/\u003etoBoolean()\u003cbr/\u003eparseFloat()\"]\n    \n    Check --\u003e EnvKey\n    EnvKey --\u003e EnvVar\n    EnvVar --\u003e|\"Set\"| TypeConv\n    EnvVar --\u003e|\"Not Set\"| YAML\n    YAML --\u003e LodashGet\n    LodashGet --\u003e|\"Found\"| TypeConv\n    LodashGet --\u003e|\"Not Found\"| DefaultVal\n    DefaultVal --\u003e TypeConv\n    TypeConv --\u003e Return[\"Return value\"]\n```\n\n**Configuration Functions**\n\n| Function | Purpose | Returns | Location |\n|----------|---------|---------|----------|\n| `getConfig()` | Load entire config | `object` | [src/util.js:55-79]() |\n| `getConfigValue(key, default, type)` | Get single value | `any` | [src/util.js:88-109]() |\n| `keyToEnv(key)` | Convert to env var name | `string` | [src/util.js:38]() |\n| `setConfigFilePath(path)` | Set config location | `void` | [src/util.js:44-49]() |\n\nSources: [src/util.js:29](), [src/util.js:38](), [src/util.js:44-79](), [src/util.js:88-109]()\n\nConfiguration is loaded from `config.yaml` [src/util.js:71]() via `getConfig()` [src/util.js:55-79]()] and cached in `CACHED_CONFIG` [src/util.js:29](). The path is set via `setConfigFilePath()` [src/util.js:44-49]() during server initialization.\n\n`getConfigValue()` [src/util.js:88-109]() first checks environment variables using `keyToEnv()` [src/util.js:38]() which converts dot notation to `SILLYTAVERN_KEY_SUBKEY` format [src/util.js:38](). Falls back to YAML via `_.get(config, key, defaultValue)` [src/util.js:97](). Supports type conversion: `'number'` via `parseFloat()` [src/util.js:103](), `'boolean'` via `toBoolean()` [src/util.js:105]().\n\n**Example Usages**\n\n```javascript\n// From chats.js\nconst isBackupEnabled = getConfigValue('backups.chat.enabled', true, 'boolean');\nconst throttleInterval = getConfigValue('backups.chat.throttleInterval', 10_000, 'number');\n\n// From characters.js\nconst memoryCacheCapacity = getConfigValue('performance.memoryCacheCapacity', '100mb');\nconst useDiskCache = getConfigValue('performance.useDiskCache', true, 'boolean');\n\n// From content-manager.js\nconst skipContentCheck = getConfigValue('skipContentCheck', false, 'boolean');\n```\n\n### Common Utility Functions\n\n| Function | Purpose | Example | Returns |\n|----------|---------|---------|---------|\n| `sanitize()` | Safe filename | `sanitize(userInput)` | `string` |\n| `tryParse()` | Safe JSON parse | `tryParse(jsonString)` | `object \\| undefined` |\n| `humanizedDateTime()` | Formatted timestamp | `humanizedDateTime()` | `string` |\n| `generateTimestamp()` | Compact timestamp | `generateTimestamp()` | `string` |\n| `removeOldBackups()` | Prune backups | `removeOldBackups(dir, prefix, limit)` | `void` |\n| `getImages()` | List image files | `getImages(directoryPath, sortBy, type)` | `string[]` |\n| `formatBytes()` | Human-readable size | `formatBytes(1024)` | `'1 KB'` |\n| `clientRelativePath()` | Client-accessible path | `clientRelativePath(root, fullPath)` | `string` |\n| `uuidv4()` | Generate UUID | `uuidv4()` | `string` |\n| `delay()` | Async delay | `await delay(1000)` | `Promise\u003cvoid\u003e` |\n| `deepMerge()` | Merge objects | `deepMerge(target, source)` | `object` |\n\nSources: [src/util.js:558-564](), [src/util.js:540-556](), [src/util.js:615-625](), [src/util.js:633-650](), [src/util.js:659-692](), [src/util.js:198-200](), [src/util.js:573-579](), [src/util.js:518-533](), [src/util.js:174-176](), [src/util.js:494-510]()\n\nThe `util.js` module provides essential helper functions. `tryParse()` [src/util.js:558-564]() safely parses JSON without throwing errors. `humanizedDateTime()` [src/util.js:540-556]() generates human-readable timestamps in the format `YYYY-MM-DD@HHhMMmSSsMSms`. `removeOldBackups()` [src/util.js:633-650]() prunes old backup files based on configuration limits. `getImages()` [src/util.js:659-692]() lists image files with optional sorting and media type filtering. `clientRelativePath()` [src/util.js:573-579]() converts server paths to client-accessible URLs by stripping the data root prefix and normalizing separators.\n\n## Content Management Patterns\n\n### Default Content Seeding\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Content Index\"\n        ScaffoldIdx[\"scaffold/index.json\u003cbr/\u003eUser scaffold content\"]\n        ContentIdx[\"content/index.json\u003cbr/\u003eDefault content\"]\n        Combined[\"Combined index\u003cbr/\u003egetContentIndex()\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"User Directories\"\n        UserRoot[\"users/{handle}/\"]\n        ContentLog[\"content.log\u003cbr/\u003eTracks seeded content\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Seeding Process\"\n        Check[\"checkForNewContent()\"]\n        Compare[\"Compare with content.log\"]\n        Copy[\"Copy missing files\"]\n        UpdateLog[\"Append to content.log\"]\n    end\n    \n    ScaffoldIdx --\u003e Combined\n    ContentIdx --\u003e Combined\n    Combined --\u003e Check\n    Check --\u003e Compare\n    ContentLog --\u003e Compare\n    Compare --\u003e Copy\n    Copy --\u003e UserRoot\n    Copy --\u003e UpdateLog\n    UpdateLog --\u003e ContentLog\n```\n\n**Content Seeding System**\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:171-197](), [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:111-163](), [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:203-229]()\n\nThe content management system seeds default content to user directories on first use. `checkForNewContent()` [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:171-197]() reads content indices from both `scaffold/` (user-specific template) and `content/` (default assets) directories [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:203-229](). \n\nFor each user, `seedContentForUser()` [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:111-163]() compares content filenames against `content.log` [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:119]() in the user's root directory. Files not in the log are copied to their target directories based on content type [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:139-144](), and the log is updated [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:161](). This prevents re-seeding deleted content while allowing new additions.\n\n### Content Download Integration\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    subgraph \"Download Sources\"\n        Chub[\"Chub.ai\u003cbr/\u003eCharacters \u0026 Lorebooks\"]\n        Pyg[\"Pygmalion.chat\u003cbr/\u003eCharacters\"]\n        Generic[\"Generic URL\u003cbr/\u003eWhitelisted domains\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Parser Functions\"\n        ParseChub[\"downloadChubCharacter()\u003cbr/\u003edownloadChubLorebook()\"]\n        ParsePyg[\"downloadPygmalionCharacter()\"]\n        ParseURL[\"Generic fetch\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Output\"\n        Buffer[\"{ buffer, fileName, fileType }\"]\n        Import[\"Import to user directory\"]\n    end\n    \n    Chub --\u003e ParseChub\n    Pyg --\u003e ParsePyg\n    Generic --\u003e ParseURL\n    \n    ParseChub --\u003e Buffer\n    ParsePyg --\u003e Buffer\n    ParseURL --\u003e Buffer\n    \n    Buffer --\u003e Import\n```\n\n**Content Download and Import Pattern**\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:370-428](), [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:436-479](), [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:198-201]()\n\nContent can be downloaded from external sources. `downloadChubCharacter()` [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:370-428]() fetches character metadata from the Chub API, constructs a Tavern Card V2 object [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:388-408](), downloads the avatar image, and embeds the JSON into a PNG using `write()` [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:424](). \n\n`downloadPygmalionCharacter()` [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:436-479]() follows a similar pattern for Pygmalion.chat. Generic URL downloads check against the whitelist [src/endpoints/content-manager.js:22]() configured in `config.yaml` [default/config.yaml:198-203](). All download functions return a standardized object with buffer, filename, and MIME type for consistent handling.\n\n## Middleware Development\n\n### Creating Custom Middleware\n\n```javascript\n// Example: Custom validation middleware\nexport default function customMiddleware(options) {\n    return async function(request, response, next) {\n        try {\n            // Validation logic\n            if (!isValid(request)) {\n                return response.status(400).send({ error: 'Invalid request' });\n            }\n            \n            // Modify request object\n            request.customData = await loadData();\n            \n            // Continue to next middleware\n            next();\n        } catch (error) {\n            console.error('Middleware error:', error);\n            return response.status(500).send({ error: error.message });\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n// Apply middleware\nrouter.post('/endpoint', customMiddleware({ option: 'value' }), handler);\n```\n\nSources: [src/middleware/whitelist.js:101-148](), [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:13-53](), [src/middleware/validateFileName.js:55-69]()\n\nMiddleware functions follow the Express pattern: `(request, response, next) =\u003e {}`. The whitelist middleware [src/middleware/whitelist.js:101-148]() demonstrates factory pattern middleware that returns a configured function. It checks IP addresses [src/middleware/whitelist.js:123-129]() and calls `next()` on success [src/middleware/whitelist.js:146]() or sends an error response [src/middleware/whitelist.js:144]().\n\nThe `basicAuth` middleware [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:13-53]() shows authentication patterns with configurable per-user auth [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:34]() and credential validation [src/middleware/basicAuth.js:39-50](). The `validateFileName` factory [src/middleware/validateFileName.js:55-69]() creates field-specific validators for request bodies.\n\n### Request Context Injection\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Req as \"Request\"\n    participant Session as \"Session Middleware\"\n    participant UserMW as \"User Middleware\"\n    participant Handler as \"Route Handler\"\n    \n    Req-\u003e\u003eSession: Load session cookie\n    Session-\u003e\u003eSession: Decrypt session data\n    Session-\u003e\u003eReq: Set req.session\n    Req-\u003e\u003eUserMW: Continue\n    UserMW-\u003e\u003eUserMW: Load user profile\u003cbr/\u003efrom handle\n    UserMW-\u003e\u003eUserMW: Get directories\u003cbr/\u003egetUserDirectories()\n    UserMW-\u003e\u003eReq: Set req.user.profile\u003cbr/\u003eSet req.user.directories\n    Req-\u003e\u003eHandler: Fully contextualized request\n```\n\n**User Context Middleware Pattern**\n\nSources: [src/users.js:636-687](), [src/users.js:710-799]()\n\nUser context is injected into requests after session management. The user middleware retrieves the user handle from the session, loads the full user profile [src/users.js:710-744](), and attaches directory paths via `getUserDirectories()` [src/users.js:636-687](). This ensures every authenticated request has access to `request.user.profile` (user metadata) and `request.user.directories` (file system paths).\n\nThe directory cache [src/users.js:36]() improves performance by avoiding repeated path construction. This pattern allows endpoint handlers to focus on business logic without managing authentication or path resolution.\n\n## Integrating AI Providers\n\n### Chat Completions Backend Architecture\n\nThe chat completions backend at [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js]() is the central integration point for AI providers. It routes requests based on `chat_completion_source` and handles format conversion.\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Client Layer\"\n        ClientReq[\"POST /api/backends/chat-completions\"]\n        ReqBody[\"request.body:\u003cbr/\u003e- messages[]\u003cbr/\u003e- model\u003cbr/\u003e- temperature\u003cbr/\u003e- chat_completion_source\u003cbr/\u003e- stream\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Router Function\"\n        Route[\"router.post('/chat-completions')\"]\n        Switch[\"switch(chat_completion_source)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Provider Handlers\"\n        Claude[\"sendClaudeRequest()\u003cbr/\u003e- convertClaudeMessages()\u003cbr/\u003e- API_CLAUDE\"]\n        Google[\"sendMakerSuiteRequest()\u003cbr/\u003e- convertGooglePrompt()\u003cbr/\u003e- API_MAKERSUITE/API_VERTEX_AI\"]\n        OpenAI[\"sendOpenAIRequest()\u003cbr/\u003e- Direct message passing\u003cbr/\u003e- API_OPENAI\"]\n        Mistral[\"sendMistralRequest()\u003cbr/\u003e- convertMistralMessages()\u003cbr/\u003e- API_MISTRAL\"]\n        Cohere[\"sendCohereRequest()\u003cbr/\u003e- convertCohereMessages()\u003cbr/\u003e- API_COHERE_V2\"]\n        Custom[\"sendCustomRequest()\u003cbr/\u003e- Custom URL handling\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Utilities\"\n        Secrets[\"readSecret(directories, key)\"]\n        Convert[\"prompt-converters.js:\u003cbr/\u003e- convertClaudeMessages()\u003cbr/\u003e- convertGooglePrompt()\u003cbr/\u003e- postProcessPrompt()\"]\n        Stream[\"forwardFetchResponse()\"]\n    end\n    \n    ClientReq --\u003e Route\n    Route --\u003e Switch\n    Switch --\u003e Claude\n    Switch --\u003e Google\n    Switch --\u003e OpenAI\n    Switch --\u003e Mistral\n    Switch --\u003e Cohere\n    Switch --\u003e Custom\n    \n    Claude --\u003e Secrets\n    Google --\u003e Secrets\n    OpenAI --\u003e Secrets\n    \n    Claude --\u003e Convert\n    Google --\u003e Convert\n    Mistral --\u003e Convert\n    Cohere --\u003e Convert\n    \n    Claude --\u003e Stream\n    Google --\u003e Stream\n    OpenAI --\u003e Stream\n```\n\n**Chat Completions Request Routing**\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:1-1200](), [src/prompt-converters.js:1-600]()\n\nThe main router [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js]() contains provider-specific handler functions:\n\n- `sendClaudeRequest()` [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:205-385]() - Handles Claude API integration with prompt caching, thinking budgets, and tool calling\n- `sendMakerSuiteRequest()` [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:392-696]() - Google AI Studio and Vertex AI with safety settings and thinking configs\n- `sendOpenAIRequest()` - OpenAI-compatible endpoints with reasoning effort and function calling\n- `sendMistralRequest()` - MistralAI with message conversion\n- `sendCohereRequest()` - Cohere chat API with custom message format\n\nEach handler performs these steps:\n\n1. **Authentication**: Retrieve API key via `readSecret(request.user.directories, SECRET_KEYS.*)` [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:207]()\n2. **Message Conversion**: Use provider-specific converters from [src/prompt-converters.js]():\n   - `convertClaudeMessages()` [src/prompt-converters.js:198-360]() - Converts to Anthropic format with system prompt extraction\n   - `convertGooglePrompt()` [src/prompt-converters.js:362-520]() - Formats for Gemini with safety settings\n   - `convertMistralMessages()` [src/prompt-converters.js:522-580]() - Handles Mistral-specific formatting\n3. **Request Building**: Construct provider-specific request body with API-specific parameters\n4. **Streaming**: Use `forwardFetchResponse()` [src/util.js:531-567]() for SSE streaming or handle complete responses\n\n### Adding a New Provider\n\nTo add a new AI provider, follow this pattern:\n\n```javascript\n// In src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js\nasync function sendNewProviderRequest(request, response) {\n    const apiUrl = new URL(request.body.reverse_proxy || API_NEWPROVIDER).toString();\n    const apiKey = request.body.reverse_proxy \n        ? request.body.proxy_password \n        : readSecret(request.user.directories, SECRET_KEYS.NEWPROVIDER);\n    \n    if (!apiKey) {\n        console.warn('NewProvider API key is missing.');\n        return response.status(400).send({ error: true });\n    }\n    \n    try {\n        const controller = new AbortController();\n        request.socket.removeAllListeners('close');\n        request.socket.on('close', () =\u003e controller.abort());\n        \n        // Convert messages to provider format\n        const convertedPrompt = convertNewProviderMessages(\n            request.body.messages,\n            request.body.use_sysprompt,\n            getPromptNames(request)\n        );\n        \n        const requestBody = {\n            messages: convertedPrompt.messages,\n            model: request.body.model,\n            max_tokens: request.body.max_tokens,\n            temperature: request.body.temperature,\n            stream: request.body.stream,\n        };\n        \n        const generateResponse = await fetch(apiUrl + '/v1/chat/completions', {\n            method: 'POST',\n            signal: controller.signal,\n            body: JSON.stringify(requestBody),\n            headers: {\n                'Content-Type': 'application/json',\n                'Authorization': `Bearer ${apiKey}`,\n            },\n        });\n        \n        if (request.body.stream) {\n            forwardFetchResponse(generateResponse, response);\n        } else {\n            const json = await generateResponse.json();\n            return response.send(json);\n        }\n    } catch (error) {\n        console.error('Error communicating with NewProvider:', error);\n        if (!response.headersSent) {\n            return response.status(500).send({ error: true });\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Add to router switch\nrouter.post('/chat-completions', async (request, response) =\u003e {\n    const source = request.body.chat_completion_source;\n    switch (source) {\n        case 'newprovider':\n            return await sendNewProviderRequest(request, response);\n        // ... other cases\n    }\n});\n```\n\n**Provider Integration Checklist**\n\n| Step | Action | Files |\n|------|--------|-------|\n| 1. Add API constant | Define `API_NEWPROVIDER` URL | [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js:66-89]() |\n| 2. Create handler | Implement `sendNewProviderRequest()` | [src/endpoints/backends/chat-completions.js]() |\n| 5. format\u003cbr/\u003e- Map message roles\"]\n        PostProc[\"postProcessPrompt()\u003cbr/\u003e- Merge consecutive messages\u003cbr/\u003e- Ensure role alternation\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph manages API keys and sensitive configuration per user:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Secrets Storage\"\n        File[\"users/{handle}/secrets.json\"]\n        Keys[\"SECRET_KEYS constants:\u003cbr/\u003e- OPENAI\u003cbr/\u003e- CLAUDE\u003cbr/\u003e- MAKERSUITE\u003cbr/\u003e- MISTRALAI\u003cbr/\u003e- etc.\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"API Functions\"\n /api/secrets/write\"]\n        FindEP[\"POST /api/secrets/find\"]\n    end\n    \n    Keys --\u003e Read\n    File --\u003e Read\n    File --\u003e Write\n    File --\u003e State\n    \n    Read --\u003e ReadEP\n    Write --\u003e WriteEP\n    State --\u003e FindEP\n```\n\n**Secrets Management Functions**\n\n| Function | Purpose | Returns | Location |\n|----------|---------|---------|----------|\n| `readSecret(directories, key)` | Read single secret value | `string \\| undefined` | [src/endpoints/secrets.js:94-102]() |\n| `writeSecret(directories, key, value)` | Write secret atomically | `void` | [src/endpoints/secrets.js:104-109]() |\n| `readSecretState(directories)` | Get which keys exist | `Record\u003cstring, boolean\u003e` | [src/endpoints/secrets.js:80-92]() |\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/secrets.js:9-109]()\n\nSecrets are stored in `users/{handle}/secrets.json` [src/endpoints/secrets.js:8](). The `readSecret()` function [src/endpoints/secrets.js:94-102]() reads and parses the JSON file, returning the value for a specific key. `writeSecret()` [src/endpoints/secrets.js:104-109]() updates individual keys atomically using `writeFileAtomicSync()`. `readSecretState()` [src/endpoints/secrets.js:80-92]() returns which keys exist without exposing values, used for UI state management.\n\n**Secret Keys Definition**\n\nAdd new provider keys to `SECRET_KEYS` object [src/endpoints/secrets.js:9-80](). Example:\n\n```javascript\nexport const SECRET_KEYS = {\n    OPENAI: 'api_key_openai',\n    CLAUDE: 'api_key_claude',\n    MAKERSUITE: 'api_key_makersuite',\n    NEWPROVIDER: 'api_key_newprovider',  // Add your key here\n    // ... other keys\n};\n```\n\n**Security Note**: Never return secrets in API responses unless explicitly requested and `allowKeysExposure` is true [default/config.yaml:216](). The `/api/secrets/read` endpoint checks this configuration before exposing values.\n\n## Performance Considerations\n\n### Caching Strategies\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Character Caching\"\n        Req[\"readCharacterData()\"]\n        MemCheck[\"memoryCache.get()\"]\n        DiskCheck[\"diskCache.instance().getItem()\"]\n        FileRead[\"parse(inputFile)\"]\n    end\n    \n    subgraph \"Cache Management\"\n        MemEvict[\"MemoryLimitedMap\u003cbr/\u003eLRU eviction\"]\n        DiskSync[\"syncCacheEntries()\u003cbr/\u003eEvery 5 minutes\"]\n        InvalidateOnWrite[\"memoryCache.delete()\u003cbr/\u003ediskCache.syncQueue.add()\"]\n    end\n    \n    Req --\u003e MemCheck\n    MemCheck --\u003e|\"Hit\"| Return[\"Return cached data\"]\n    MemCheck --\u003e|\"Miss\"| DiskCheck\n    DiskCheck --\u003e|\"Hit\"| MemStore[\"memoryCache.set()\"]\n    DiskCheck --\u003e|\"Miss\"| FileRead\n    FileRead --\u003e MemStore\n    MemStore --\u003e DiskStore[\"cache.setItem()\"]\n    DiskStore --\u003e Return\n    \n    MemCheck --\u003e MemEvict\n    DiskCheck --\u003e DiskSync\n    FileRead --\u003e InvalidateOnWrite\n```\n\n**Multi-Tier Caching Architecture**\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/characters.js:29-36](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-159](), [src/endpoints/characters.js:181-209]()\n\nThe character system uses three caching tiers for performance:\n\n1. **Memory Cache** [src/endpoints/characters.js:29-31](): A `MemoryLimitedMap` with configurable capacity (default 100MB via `memoryCacheCapacity` [src/endpoints/characters.js:30]()) provides fastest access. Uses LRU eviction when capacity is exceeded. Disabled on Android to conserve memory [src/endpoints/characters.js:33]().\n\n2. **Disk Cache** [src/endpoints/characters.js:38-159](): Uses `node-persist` via the `DiskCache` class [src/endpoints/characters.js:38]() to store parsed character data. Syncs asynchronously every 5 minutes via `SYNC_INTERVAL` [src/endpoints/characters.js:49](). Automatically prunes invalid entries during `verify()` [src/endpoints/characters.js:126-150]().\n\n3. **File System** [src/endpoints/characters.js:198](): Direct PNG parsing via `parse()` is the slowest but always authoritative. Cache keys include file modification time via `getCacheKey()` [src/endpoints/characters.js:166-173]() to detect changes.\n\nCache configuration is controlled by `performance` settings [default/config.yaml:174-182](): `memoryCacheCapacity` [default/config.yaml:180](), `useDiskCache` [default/config.yaml:182](), and `lazyLoadCharacters` [default/config.yaml:177]().\n\n### Throttling and Debouncing\n\n```javascript\nimport _ from 'lodash';\n\n// Throttle: Execute at most once per interval\nconst AUTOSAVE_INTERVAL = 10 * 60 * 1000; // 10 minutes\nconst throttledSave = _.throttle(\n    () =\u003e backupUserSettings(handle, true),\n    AUTOSAVE_INTERVAL,\n    { leading: true, trailing: true }\n);\n\n// Store throttled functions per user\nconst AUTOSAVE_FUNCTIONS = new Map();\n\nfunction triggerAutoSave(handle) {\n    if (!AUTOSAVE_FUNCTIONS.has(handle)) {\n        const throttled = _.throttle(\n            () =\u003e backupUserSettings(handle, true),\n            AUTOSAVE_INTERVAL\n        );\n        AUTOSAVE_FUNCTIONS.set(handle, throttled);\n    }\n    AUTOSAVE_FUNCTIONS.get(handle)();\n}\n```\n\n**Throttling Patterns**\n\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n    Call1[\"triggerAutoSave()\"]\n    Call2[\"triggerAutoSave()\"]\n    Call3[\"triggerAutoSave()\"]\n    \n    Map[\"AUTOSAVE_FUNCTIONS\u003cbr/\u003eMap\u0026lt;handle, throttled\u0026gt;\"]\n    Throttle[\"_.throttle()\"]\n    Execute[\"backupUserSettings()\"]\n    \n    Call1 --\u003e Map\n    Call2 --\u003e Map\n    Call3 --\u003e Map\n    Map --\u003e Throttle\n    Throttle --\u003e|\"Once per interval\"| Execute\n```\n\nSources: [src/endpoints/settings.js:18-41](), [src/endpoints/chats.js:27-77]()\n\nUse lodash's `throttle()` for rate-limiting operations like autosaves. The settings autosave [src/endpoints/settings.js:33]() throttles to once per 10 minutes [src/endpoints/settings.js:18]() via `triggerAutoSave()` [src/endpoints/settings.js:31-41](). Each user gets their own throttled function stored in `AUTOSAVE_FUNCTIONS` map [src/endpoints/settings.js:24]().\n\nChat backups are throttled per user via `getBackupFunction()` [src/endpoints/chats.js:72-77]() with configurable intervals via `throttleInterval` [src/endpoints/chats.js:27](). The backups use `_.throttle()` with `{ leading: true, trailing: true }` [src/endpoints/chats.js:74]() to ensure the first call executes immediately and the last call in a burst also executes after the interval.\n\n## Best Practices Summary\n\n### File Operations Checklist\n\n- ✅ Always use `sanitize()` on user-provided filenames\n- ✅ Use `writeFileAtomicSync()` for critical writes\n- ✅ Check file existence with `fs.existsSync()` before operations\n- ✅ Use `request.user.directories` for all path construction\n- ✅ Handle both successful and error cases explicitly\n- ✅ Log errors with `console.error()` before returning error responses\n\n### Security Checklist\n\n- ✅ Validate all user inputs with middleware or manual checks\n- ✅ Never trust client-provided paths directly\n- ✅ Use path sanitization to prevent directory traversal\n- ✅ Check file permissions and user access rights\n- ✅ Use CSRF protection for state-changing operations\n- ✅ Store secrets in `secrets.json`, never in regular settings\n\n### Performance Checklist\n\n- ✅ Use caching for expensive operations (parsing, I/O)\n- ✅ Implement cache invalidation on writes\n- ✅ Throttle/debounce frequent operations (backups, saves)\n- ✅ Use streaming for large file transfers\n- ✅ Avoid synchronous file operations in request handlers when possible\n- ✅ Consider lazy loading for optional data\n\nSources:
